PN CN107646548-A
TI Edible fungus culture greenhouse environment control system has action execution unit which completes corresponding action operation based on received action instruction to realize real-time control of environment of greenhouse.
AU WANG Y
   WU X
   LIU J
   WANG T
   YANG Y
AE JIANGSU TENESUN CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201811626U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a data acquisition unit that detects (S1) the temperature, the humidity and the carbon dioxide concentration in a current shed in real time, and transmits the real-time detection value to the data receiving end of a data processing unit. The data processing unit sends (S2) the corresponding action command to an action execution unit according to the data calculation and processing result. The action execution unit completes (S3) the corresponding action operation based on received action instruction to realize the real-time control of the environment of the greenhouse.
   USE - Edible fungus culture greenhouse environment control system.
   ADVANTAGE - The control of edible fungus culture greenhouse environment is performed efficiently.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an edible fungus culture greenhouse environment control method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the edible fungus culture greenhouse environment control system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for detecting the temperature, the humidity and the carbon dioxide concentration in current shed in real time, and transmitting the real-time detection value to the data receiving end of a data processing unit (S1)
   Step for sending the corresponding action command to action execution unit according to the data calculation and processing result (S2)
   Step for completing the corresponding action operation based on received action instruction to realize the real-time control of the environment of the greenhouse (S3)
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); T01 (Digital Computers); T06 (Process and Machine Control)

PT P
PN CN111036199-A
TI Use of rutile titanium oxide supported catalyst comprising ruthenium and/or rhodium as noble metals in hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide.
AU WANG X
   LI X
   LI L
   LIN J
   QIAO B
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202034713C
AB    NOVELTY - Use of rutile titanium oxide supported catalyst in hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide is claimed. The catalyst comprises ruthenium and/or rhodium as active component loaded on catalyst carrier comprising rutile titanium dioxide.
   USE - Use of rutile titanium oxide in hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide for producing methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The rutile titanium oxide has simple preparation method, high selectivity and stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The loading amount of active component preferably is 1-3 wt.% of carrier mass. The volume ratio of hydrogen/carbon dioxide is 0.1-10 (preferably 3-10). The volume content of balance gas in raw material gas is 0-30% (preferably 10-20%). Preferred Process: The preparation of catalyst by excessive impregnation method involves taking precious metal precursor solution in container, adding rutile titanium oxide to container with stirring at room temperature, transferring to water bath, continue stirring until drying at 373-423 K (preferably 398K), performing reduction treatment, and obtaining target catalyst. Preferred Condition: The temperature of water bath is controlled to be 323-353K. The drying time is 6-24 hours. The reducing atmosphere is 10-100 vol.% hydrogen/helium. The reducing temperature is 473-873 K for 1-8 hours. The temperature range of hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide is 273-773K. The space velocity of feed gas for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide is GHSV preferably is 48000-72000 mL hour-1.g cat-1. Preferred Component: The raw material is composed of reaction gas and balance gas. The reaction gas is hydrogen and carbon dioxide. The balance gas is chosen from one or more of nitrogen or inert gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The precious metal ruthenium precursor is chosen from one or more of ruthenium trichloride, acetylacetone ruthenium and nitrosyl ruthenium nitrate. The precursor of rhodium is chosen from rhodium trichloride, rhodium nitrate, and triphenylphosphine rhodium chloride. Preferred Composition: The concentration of precious metal ruthenium precursor solution is 0.32-32 mg.mL-1. The concentration of precious metal rhodium precursor solution is 0.98-98 mg.mL-1. The mass ratio of ruthenium and rhodium is (3:1)-(1:1).
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E10-J02D1; E11-D02; J04-E04B; J04-E11; N02-E01; N02-E02; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-B02
IP B01J-023/46; B01J-035/00; B01J-037/08; B01J-037/18; C07C-001/12; C07C-009/04
PD CN111036199-A   21 Apr 2020   B01J-023/46   202039   Pages: 16   Chinese
AD CN111036199-A    CN11182899    11 Oct 2018
PI CN11182899    11 Oct 2018
CP CN111036199-A
      CN103157466-A   CHINESE ACAD SCI DALIAN CHEM PHYS INST (CACP)   CONG Y, HUANG Y, LIU S, WANG X, ZHANG T

      JINGHUA XU ET AL.: "Influence of pretreatment temperature on catalytic performance of rutile TiO2-supported ruthenium catalyst in CO2 methanation", JOURNAL OF CATALYSIS,relevantClaims[1-10],relevantPassages[227-237]

PT P
PN CN111560002-A
TI Catalyst used for preparing cyclic carbonate compounds using carbon dioxide and propylene oxide derivatives as raw materials, comprises ionic liquid containing metaborate ion.
AU HU J
   WANG Z
   DENG T
   CHEN S
   GUO Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
GA 202083028B
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises an ionic liquid containing metaborate ion.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate compounds using carbon dioxide and propylene oxide derivatives as raw materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst enables the preparation of cyclic carbonate compounds without any external organic solvents and lower carbon dioxide pressure, and is easy to synthesize by simple and environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst is ionic liquid 1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene metaboric acid, where its anion is metaborate ion and its cation is protonated 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undece-7-en (DBUH+). The propylene oxide derivative comprises 2-chloromethyl-oxirane, 2-bromomethyl-oxirane and 2-phenoxymethyl-oxirane. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the catalyst and the propylene oxide derivative is 0.1:1-1:1. Preferred Method: The preparation method of the catalyst involves performing synthesis reaction of 1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene and equivalent amount of metaboric acid under uniform stirring. Preferred Condition: The pressure of the catalytic reaction of the catalyst is 0.1 MPa. The temperature of the catalytic reaction of the catalyst is 30-50 degrees C. The catalytic reaction time of the catalyst is 4-6 hours.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E07-A04; E11-B01; E11-W; J04-E04; N01-D01; N05-D; N06-C04; N07-F04
IP B01J-031/02; C07D-317/36

      ZHEN-ZHEN YANG ET AL.: "Lewis Basic Ionic Liquids-Catalyzed Conversion of Carbon Dioxide to Cyclic Carbonates", ADVANCED SYNTHESIS & CATALYSIS,relevantClaims[1-8],relevantPassages[2233-2240]

PT P
PN CN115031250-A
TI Thermal power generation energy-saving            carbon-reducing method, involves pre-processing flue            gas, removing discharged flue gas of power plant            boiler, decomposing waste gas by waste heat, recycling            waste gas and feeding waste gas into decomposition            mixing generation furnace to decompose.
AU SONG Y
AE SHANXI CHANGYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B99338
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pre-processing the flue                gas, removing the discharged flue gas of the power                plant boiler (S1). The high-temperature waste gas                is pressurized (S2) by using an induced draft fan.                The high-temperature waste gas is sent to a                decomposition position. The waste gas is decomposed                and mixed (S3). The waste gas is introduced into                each decomposed and mixed fuel generating furnace                through a waste gas operation pipeline. The waste                gas is sprayed into the furnace through a flow                control gate valve. The fuel is discharged in the                decomposed and mixed fuel generating furnace. The                waste gas is washed and reduced (S4). The waste gas                is sheared by using particles. The waste gas is                decomposed by the waste heat. The waste gas is                recycled (S5) and the waste gas is feed into the                decomposition mixing generation furnace to                decompose.
   USE - Thermal power generation energy-saving                carbon-reducing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables converting and decomposing                carbon dioxide to reduce carbon displacement,                reducing carbon emission and saving energy, such                that the carbon dioxide is reduced to carbon                dioxide and fuel gasification to form water coal                gas as circulating carbon dioxide reducing gas, and                hence solving problem that the traditional thermal                power generation process in background technology                generates large amount of carbon dioxide, causing                pollution to the environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a thermal power generation energy-saving                carbon-reducing method. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
   S1Step for pre-processing the flue gas,                removing the discharged flue gas of the power plant                boiler
   S2Step for pressurizing high-temperature                waste gas by using an induced draft fan and sending                high-temperature waste gas to a decomposition                position.
   S3Step for decomposing and mixing waste                gas
   S4Step for washing and reducing waste                gas
   S5Step for recycling waste gas and the                feeding waste gas into the decomposition mixing                generation furnace to decompose.
DC Q72 (Steam generation (F22)); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23)); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)
MC Q72-U16; Q72-B07; Q72-A99; Q72-A02; Q73-U27; Q73-U20; Q73-T07; Q73-T02; Q73-A05; X11-A08; X11-U01A

A                lower outlet of a first gas-liquid separating tank                is connected with a low temperature channel inlet                of the fourth heat regenerator such that high                temperature channel side carbon dioxide working                substance of the fourth heat regenerator is cooled.                An outlet of the second gas-liquid separating tank                is connected with an inlet of a high pressure level                compressor and a low pressure level compressor. The                low temperature channel outlet of the fourth heat                regenerator provides low temperature and low                pressure gas-liquid two-phase carbon dioxide                working medium entering into a low pressure                circulating barrel through a throttling action of s                second expansion valve.

   ADVANTAGE - The system is high in coupling, realizes low                pressure stage compression process in the low                pressure level compressor and the high                pressure-level compressor, which can realize                dual-stage compression in series and single- stage                compression in parallel, thus improving flexibility                of transcritical compression process and make the                system to reach a best working condition. The                system realizes multi-stage heat recovery and                multi-mode injection, which can improve energy                utilization efficiency and reduce ice energy                consumption of ice and resource waste and carbon                emission. The system comprises a heat regenerator                set to save power consumption of an additional                heating system and shares condensation heat load of                a refrigeration system, which reduces energy                consumption.

PN CN114570754-A
TI Method for treating volatile organic polluted soil with low boiling point by carbon dioxide, involves providing grid with two temperature sensors and two organic material concentration sensors, where data line of sensor is allowed to pass through sealing cover.

   XIA W
   SHAO G
   LIU S
   ZHONG Y
   CAI G
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 202278151T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing in-situ exploration on a pre-processing field. An isolation barrel is inserted along a square grid into polluted soil, where inserting depth of isolation barrel exceeds depth of pre-treatment soil for 30-50 50 cm. A sealing cover is provided. An end of an air injection pipe is inserted into the polluted soil mixed with a mixed material by a penetration device. A temperature sensor and an organic matter concentration sensor is inserted in preprocessing soil. A sensor number is set according to a grid section area. The grid is provided with two temperature sensors and two organic material concentration sensors. A data line of the sensor is allowed to pass through a sealing cover, and is connected with a data monitoring device.
   USE - Method for treating volatile organic polluted soil with low boiling point by carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses low carbon gel material magnesium oxide, realizes the carbon dioxide in the volatile organic polluted soil repairing application, has low energy consumption, high processing efficiency and so on. The method is in situ processing, without digging and transporting soil, also without special soil pyrolysis device, greatly reducing the processing cost, and improving the processing efficiency. The treatment process is controllable. The device is simple and the integrity is good; field pre-treatment, material preparation, field treatment and soil repairing and other processes are connected in turn, the gas injection and air extraction are combined, the technique operation is simple. The material for processing volatile organic pollutant is mainly active magnesium oxide (MgO), active MgO calcining temperature is low, easy to absorb carbon dioxide, has obvious low carbon environment-friendly efficiency.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for            improving material replacement efficiency, has strut            connected below working box, first pipeline is            connected with compressor, and second pipeline is            connected with turbine box and stirring chamber and is            arranged with valve.

GA 2022649731
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a strut (11) connected below a                working box (1) that is provided with a stirring                chamber. A blade is fixedly connected with a first                rotating shaft. A turbine box (15) is connected                with an outer side of the working box. A compressor                (16) is fixedly connected with an outer side of the                working box. The working box and the compressor are                jointly connected with a separation mechanism for                separating impurities. A first pipeline (17) is                connected with the compressor. A second pipeline                (18) is connected with the turbine box and the                stirring chamber. A valve (19) is arranged with the                second pipeline. A side of the working box is                provided with a slag discharging port (35). A                feeding port (22) is connected with a material                distributing cabin.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device                for improving material replacement efficiency
   ADVANTAGE - The device is capable of improving material                replacement efficiency, crushing the material by                the blade, at the same time, stirring the                supercritical carbon dioxide and material, such                that it is fully mixed, thus the extraction is more                sufficient. By using the process of separating and                mechanism, mechanism accelerating extracting,                reducing the time of extraction, improving the                efficiency of extraction. The impurity is pushed                the impurity to the slag outlet, convenient for                uniformly processing, pumping the gaseous carbon                dioxide, re-using, and reducing the production                cost. The airbag controls the adding quality of the                material to be suitable for extracting the                different solute.

PN CN114134521-A
TI Through flow field membrane reactor for electro-catalytic carbon dioxide reduction, comprises cathode end plate, cathode gas chamber plate, and cathode through flow field plate.
AU LIU D
   LIU B
   YANG K
   DUAN J
   LI Q
   MA L
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 2022443890
AB    NOVELTY - The reactor has a cathode end plate connected with an outer circuit. A cathode gas chamber is connected with carbon dioxide gas and a product inlet and an outlet pipeline. An anode electrolyte chamber is fixed with the inlet of the cathode and the outlet of the product. A porous polymer membrane structure separates anode oxygen and discharges cathode oxygen through a flow field structure. The cathode diffuses carbon dioxide through the flow field plate structure to allow CO2 gas to pass through a gas diffusion layer. A microporous layer diffuses to a cathodic catalytic layer. The electrolyte accumulated in the cathodic catalyst layer is discharged from the cathoric chamber. The flow of the anode and anode product is discharged in time through the anodic electrolyte and the anod product.
   USE - The through flow field membrane reactor is useful for electro-catalysis carbon dioxide reduction, where the carbon dioxide is coal, petroleum and natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The through flow field plate structure of the reactor realizes the fast separation of the anode oxygen, it solves the problem that anode electrolyte is penetrated by the cathode gas diffusion electrode catalytic layer caused by oxygen pressure, at the same time, it is good for discharging cathode water flooding problem. The reactor structure can adopt cheap porous polymer membrane to replace expensive ion polymer membrane, it realizes good CO2 electrolytic stability and high C2H4 Faraday efficiency. The porous polymer diaphragm is made of strong acid-resistant hydrophilic porous polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) film, the aperture range of the PTFE film comprises 0.1-5 m m.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structural diagram of a through-flow field membrane reactor.
   Cathode end plate (1)
   Cathode gas chamber (2)
   Flow field plate (3)
   Cathode gas diffusion electrode (4)
   Porous polymer membrane (5)
   Anode electrolyte chamber (6)
   Anode electrolyte chamber (7)
   Cathode gas diffusion electrode (8)
   Porous oxygen evolution anode (9)
   Gasket (10)
   Bolt (11)
   Gas inlet pipeline (12)
   Gas discharging pipeline (13)
   Inlet line (15)

PT P
PN CN113913866-A
TI Preparing metal organic framework loaded uranium            catalyst useful in carbon dioxide electrocatalytic            reduction, comprises e.g. dispersing zeolitic            imidazolate framework-8 powder in organic solvent,            adding uranyl nitrate aqueous solution, centrifuging,            separating, drying, calcining, and cooling.
AU DONG Y
   REN Y
   ZHOU L
   YANG F
   HE R
   ZHU W
   CHEN T
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 202215312Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal organic framework loaded                uranium catalyst comprises (i) dispersing                zinc-zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 (ZIF-8)                powder in an organic solvent, and ultrasonically                mixing to obtain a uniform solution, and (ii)                adding uranyl nitrate aqueous solution to the                uniform solution for ultrasonically mixing to                obtain a mixture, stirring the mixture,                centrifuging and separating, vacuum drying,                calcining the vacuum-dried material in an inert gas                atmosphere, and cooling to obtain the metal-organic                framework-loaded uranium catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing metal                organic framework loaded uranium catalyst, which is                useful in carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction                (claimed), nuclear fuel production, nuclear power                plant operation, nuclear facility retirement and                other processes for generating large amount of                uranium-containing nuclear waste liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has a high Faraday efficiency of                the carbon monoxide product; exhibits a                considerable activity; reaches the geometric                current density about 200 mA/cm2 under the                over-potential of -1.69 V vs reversible hydrogen                electrode, and geometric current densities about                150 mA/cm2 along with the change of the total                current applied; exhibits a maximum 95% for carbon                monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of the metal organic framework loaded uranium                catalyst in carbon dioxide electrocatalytic                reduction, comprising passing the circulating                electrolyte into a cathode chamber and an anode                chamber of a flow cell electrolysis cell, making                metal organic framework loaded uranium catalyst                into a working electrode, using a                polytetrafluoroethylene gasket to clamp the working                electrode, Nafion                (Tetrafluoroethylene-perfluoro-3,6-dioxa-4-methyl-7-                octenesulfonic acid copolymer) 115 proton exchange                membrane and nickel foam, taking silver/silver                chloride as the reference electrode and platinum                wire electrode as the counter electrode, feeding                the electrolyte with carbon dioxide to achieve                saturation, and applying a voltage to promote the                reduction reaction of carbon dioxide on the surface                of the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inert gas                  comprises argon. The organic solvent comprises                  n-hexane. The electrolyte comprises 1 mol/l                  potassium hydroxide solution. Preferred Method:                  The ZIF-8 powder is prepared by dissolving 0.558                  g zinc nitrate hexahydrate in 15 ml methanol,                  adding 15 ml methanol containing 0.616 g                  2-methylimidazole, ultrasonically processing at                  room temperature for 10 minutes, statically                  growing at 35degreesC for 12 hours, centrifuging                  the precipitate, washing with methanol for three                  times, and vacuum drying at 65degreesC for                  overnight. The process of making the                  metal-organic framework loaded uranium catalyst                  into a working electrode comprises dispersing                  0.5-1 mg metal-organic framework loaded uranium                  catalyst in 500 mul ethanol, 500 mul deionized                  water and 20 mul Nafion                  (Tetrafluoroethylene-perfluoro-3,6-dioxa-4-methyl-7-                  octenesulfonic acid copolymer) solution,                  ultrasonically treating for 30 minutes to obtain                  evenly distributed catalyst ink, drop-casting                  onto carbon fiber paper with a polyimide film on                  the back, and drying at 50-60degreesC to obtain a                  working electrode.

PN WO2019016765-A1
TI Manufacture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen for e.g. hydrogen fuel cells, involves decomposing hydrocarbon by introducing hydrocarbon feed stream into decomposition reactor, collecting hydrogen product, and producing carbon monoxide gas.
AU VISWANATH V K
   RAVON U
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201909628N
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of hydrogen and carbon monoxide involves (a) decomposing a hydrocarbon by (i) introducing a gaseous light hydrocarbon feed stream into a decomposition reactor having a hydrocarbon decomposition catalyst under non-oxidizing conditions, (ii) contacting gaseous light hydrocarbon feed stream with the decomposition catalyst at 500-800 degrees C, forming hydrogen and a carbonized catalyst, and (iii) collecting hydrogen product, and (b) contacting the carbonized decomposition catalyst with carbon dioxide gas, producing carbon monoxide gas and regenerating the hydrocarbon decomposition catalyst.
   USE - Manufacture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen used for hydro-treating processes in petroleum industry and for hydrogen fuel cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient manufacture of carbon monoxide and carbon with reduced emission of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) single reactor system, which consists of at least one inlet which is configurable as a gaseous light hydrocarbon feed inlet or carbon dioxide feed inlet, at least one outlet which is configurable as hydrogen outlet or carbon monoxide outlet, and a decomposition catalyst in the reactor. The reactor is arranged for hydrocarbon decomposition producing hydrogen or catalyst regeneration producing carbon monoxide; and
   (2) two reactor system, which consists of a methane decomposition reactor having a feed source inlet, a decomposition catalyst arranged to catalyze the production of hydrogen, a carbonized decomposition catalyst outlet, and a fresh catalyst inlet, a carbon monoxide generating reactor including a carbon dioxide inlet, a carbonized catalyst inlet, and a fresh catalyst outlet. The carbon monoxide generating unit is connected to the decomposition reactor and configured to receive carbonized decomposition catalyst from the decomposition reactor and to provide fresh catalyst to the decomposition reactor. The carbonized decomposition catalyst outlet is connected to a carbon monoxide generating reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a double bed fluidized reactor system.
   Catalyst regeneration reactor (104)
   Feed stream (106)
   Carbonized catalyst stream (108)
   Hydrogen stream (110)
   Carbon dioxide stream (114)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The decomposition catalyst is a metal based catalyst, and comprises two or more metals from 7-12 periodic table, preferably nickel. The decomposition catalyst is hollow zeolite catalyst, pyrochlore catalyst, spinal catalyst, and/or olivine catalyst. The catalyst comprises metallic, bimetallic, or trimetallic nanoparticles.The nanoparticles are nickel nanoparticles encapsulated in a hollow silicate-1 support.The gaseous light hydrocarbon feed stream comprises methane. Preferred Apparatus: The reactor is cycled from the hydrocarbon decomposition configuration to the carbon monoxide generation configuration.

PN CN109172552-A
TI Plant derived medicine useful for preventing and treating male prostatic cancer, comprises lycopene raw materials, excipient and plant nutrient element selenium extract.
AU QIAN L
AE ZHEJIANG GRESS BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201908413A
AB    NOVELTY - Plant derived medicine comprises 2-8 pts. wt. lycopene raw materials, 1-20 pts. wt. excipient and 0.5-5 pts. wt. plant nutrient element selenium extract. The excipient comprises starch, dextrin, lactose, microcrystalline cellulose, pregelatinized starch, sodium carboxymethyl starch, magnesium stearate, silicon dioxide, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, methyl cellulose, ethyl cellulose, crospovidone, povidone, croscarmellose sodium, coating powder or their combinations.
   USE - The plant derived medicine is useful for preventing and treating male prostatic cancer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for extracting lycopene raw material involves (a) grinding and filtering the raw material to obtain a stock solution, (b) vacuum freeze drying the stock solution, (c) crushing and sieving the dried material to obtain a coarse powder, (d) adding coarse powder and hexane into an extraction tank to form a homogeneous mixing system, so that the pigment in the raw material is dissolved, (e) collecting carbon dioxide out of the cylinder and liquefying through the gas purifier into the liquefaction tank, (f) feeding the liquefied carbon dioxide into the extraction cylinder by the high pressure piston pump through preheater and purifier and boosting to form a supercritical fluid, (g) introducing the mixed substance obtained in step (d) into the extraction cylinder, (h) feeding supercritical carbon dioxide into the extraction cylinder to dissolve the extract and (i) feeding the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the extract is dissolved into a separation system to be decompressed and separating the extract from the carbon dioxide; and
   (2) method for extracting plant nutrient element selenium extract, involves (a) crushing the plant, (b) mixing the crushed plants with water to obtain a mixture, (c) adding a cell wall decomposing enzyme to the mixture, (d) uniformly mixing, (e) leaving the mixture, (f) subjecting the mixture to solid phase liquid centrifugation, (g) performing liquid phase evaporation concentration and (h) obtaining a plant nutrient element selenium extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In step (d), the ratio of the coarse powder and hexane is 1:2. The step (e) involves liquefying at 0-5 degrees C.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PN CN114622953-A
TI Method for reducing coal mine gangue and carbon            dioxide mining rock-covering isolation grouting filling            emission involves determining key layer position in            rock and water guide crack zone, filling closed cranny            cavity under key layer through ground drilling.
AU XU J
   XUAN D
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 202282662G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a key layer                position in a rock and a water guide crack zone                based on formation lithology and thickness. A                protective layer with a certain thickness is left                above the water guiding crack zone. A key layer                above the protective layer is selected as a                grouting key layer. A bottom surface of a rock                layer of the grouting Key layer and a top surface                of the rock layer below the grout key layer form a                grout and filling mining fissure space. Multiple                adjacent working surfaces are arranged in the                mining area. Each working surface is arranged with                multiple grouting holes along the pushing                direction. The closed cranny cavity under key layer                is filled through ground drilling. A grouting                filling system and preparing grouting filler slurry                are established. A coal gangue is conveyed to a                crusher for crushing by a conveyor.
   USE - Method for reducing coal mine gangue and                carbon dioxide mining rock-covering isolation                grouting filling emission-reducing method in coal                mine filling and sequestration field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the solid waste rock generated                in the coal mine area and the discharged carbon                dioxide as the mining fissure grouting filling                material, treating the waste rock floor space                accumulation problem, using the closed space of the                mining-fissure to improve the carbon dioxide                sealing effect. The method solves the problem of                surface subsidence caused by coal mining, and uses                the characteristic of low fineness to solve the                problem that waste rock powder is not easy to pump.                The carbon dioxide and calcium oxide reaction is                not only limited in the stirring process but also                in the slurry conveying pumping process and in the                mining space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                coal mine gangue and carbon dioxide mining                overlying rock isolation grouting filling and                emission reduction.
   Crusher (1)
   Ball mill (2)
   Stirring tank (3)

PN CN112588088-A; CN112588088-B
TI Device for suppressing corrosion in process of membrane separation and capture of carbon dioxide, has second membrane separator and fourth membrane separator that configures air outlet, where second and fourth separator have gas outlet.
AU HUANG H
   MAO S
   GUO B
   CHEN X
   WANG D
   YE N
   JI Y
   YANG J
AE SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021359868
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a two-stage compressor (3) whose inlet that is connected with a first membrane separator (4) and a third membrane separator (6). The outlet is connected with a second membrane separator (5) and a fourth membrane separator (7). The two-stage compressor adds a bypass and a valve, and control the flue gas to bypass a secondary compressor (2) or pass through the secondary compressor. The two-stage compressor and the first-stage membrane separator are connected in series in the case of passing, and located between two membrane separators of the same stage. The second-stage membrane separator and the fourth-stage membrane separator have two gas outlets and pass through valves, and emits carbon dioxide product gas. The other is connected to the first-stage compressor through a pipeline. The second membrane separator and the fourth membrane separator configures air outlet and the outlets used are different. The content of carbon dioxide in the raw gas is 5%-60%.
   USE - Device for suppressing corrosion in the process of membrane separation and capture of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the corrosion problem of the flue gas to the compressor in the traditional membrane separation process. The step of supplementing process water in the system is reduced, so as to reduce the discharge of the process waste water. The collection of the waste water is avoided, and is environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for suppressing corrosion of carbon dioxide capture process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for suppressing corrosion in process of membrane separation and capture of carbon dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

TI Experimental method for influencing factors and            changing laws of liquid carbon dioxide (CO-2) phase            change jet impact force, involves adjusting initial            pressure of CO-2, distance and angle from jet nozzle to            impact stress sensor.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing (S1) liquid                carbon dioxide phase change jet experimental                system. An experiment plan is made (S2). The                experiment preparation is made (S3). The                preparation of high-pressure liquid carbon dioxide                is made (S4). A liquid CO2 jet is formed (S5), and                the impact factors are tested and laws of the                liquid CO2 phase change jet are changed. The                initial pressure of CO2, the distance from the jet                nozzle to the impact stress sensor, the angle                between the jet nozzle and the impact stress sensor                are adjusted (S6). The                relationship between the impact force of the liquid                CO2 phase change jet and the initial pressure,                distance, angle, and time parameters are                analyzed.

PT P
PN CN109776261-A; CN109776261-B
TI Direct production of alcohol from coke oven gas involves purifying coke oven gas and extracting hydrogen, reforming, mixing coke oven gas and carbon dioxide, mixing synthesis gas and hydrogen extracted, and synthesizing crude alcohol.
AU ZHAO L
   LIU P
   DU J
   GAO Z
   ZHANG W
   SHI L
   CHANG J
   SI R
AE XINDI ENERGY ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIND-Non-standard)
GA 201948278D
AB    NOVELTY - Direct production of alcohol from coke oven gas involves (1) obtaining coke oven gas by purifying coke oven gas, and extracting hydrogen from coke oven gas by separating, (2) reforming coke oven gas and hydrogen dioxide after in carbon dioxide dry reforming unit, and mixing coke oven gas and carbon dioxide in a volume ratio of 0.8-5:1 preferably, 1-3:1 to obtain synthesis gas, (3) and mixing obtained synthesis gas and hydrogen separated and purified in (1) in a volume ratio of 0.8-5:1 preferably, 1-3:1, and introducing into alcohol synthesis apparatus to carry out synthesis reaction, and purifying crude alcohol by separating.
   USE - Direct production of alcohol from coke oven gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces methanol, ethanol or other alcohols by adjusting hydrogen-carbon ratio and does not introduce additional hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system used for directly producing alcohol from coke oven gas, which comprises desulfurization purification device, PSA (pressure swing adsorption) separation hydrogen extraction device, methane carbon dioxide dry reforming device, syngas direct alcohol production device, product separation device and alcohol rectification device. The desulfurization device has coke oven gas inlet and outlet. The outlet of desulfurization purification device is connected with PSA separation hydrogen extraction device. The separated hydrogen outlet of PSA separation hydrogen extraction device is connected to hydrogen inlet of syngas direct alcohol production device. The coke oven tail gas outlet after hydrogen extraction is connected to methane inlet of methane carbon dioxide dry reforming unit. The outlet of methane carbon dioxide dry reforming unit is connected to syngas inlet of syngas direct alcohol making unit, and outlet of syngas direct alcohol making unit is connected to product separation unit. The crude alcohol outlet of product separation unit is connected to alcohol rectification unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The unreacted gas outlet of product separation unit is coupled to fuel inlet of syngas direct alcohol unit and/or methane carbon dioxide dry reform unit. The flue gas outlet of methane carbon dioxide dry reforming unit is connected to wet decarburization device and carbon dioxide storage tank.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst used for reforming reaction of coke oven gas and carbon dioxide is one of rhodium-based catalyst, nickel-based catalyst, ruthenium-based catalyst or copper-based catalyst. The catalyst used in synthesis gas to produce ethanol is supported noble metal catalyst or non-precious metal catalyst. The catalyst used in synthesis gas to produce methanol is copper-based catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The coke oven gas comprises 55-60 vol.% hydrogen, 23-27 vol.% methane, 5-8 vol.% carbon monoxide, 2-4 vol.% 2C or more unsaturated hydrocarbon, 1.5-3 vol.% carbon dioxide, 0.3-0.8 vol.% oxygen, 3-7 vol.% nitrogen, and 200-500 mg/N3 sulfide. The purified coke oven gas comprises 55-60 vol.% hydrogen. The hydrogen-carbon ratio is 2.5-3. The sulfur content of purified coke oven gas is 0-50 mg/Nm3. The coke oven gas after hydrogen extraction comprises 60-70 vol.% preferably, 65-70 vol.% methane, and balance carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, 2C hydrocarbons and above and nitrogen. The reformed syngas comprises 10-50% hydrogen, 10-50% carbon monoxide, 0-20% carbon dioxide, and less amount of methane and nitrogen. The ratio of hydrogen-carbon in syngas is 0.01-3:1 preferably, 0.1-1:1. Preferred Process: The purification process involves dust removing and desulfurizing. The hydrogen is extracted by PSA separation carried out at a hydrogen extraction pressure of 1-3 MPa. The reforming is carried out at a reaction temperature of 700-900 degrees C and pressure of 1.5-3 MPa under presence of reforming catalyst. The synthesis reaction is carried out at a temperature of 200-300 degrees C and pressure of 2-5 MPa. The hydrogen-carbon ratio after mixing is 2-3:1 preferably, 2.0-2.5:1 when synthesis gas mixed with hydrogen produces methanol as product. The hydrogen-carbon ratio after mixing is 1.4-2.6:1 preferably, 1.5-2.2:1 when synthesis gas mixed with hydrogen produces ethanol as product. Preferred Component: The alcohol is ethanol or methanol. The separation process is carried out by product separation device by separating synthesis reaction product into liquid phase and gas phase by condensation, returning gas phase to reactor inlet to be mixed with feed gas for reaction, and introducing liquid phase to product separation device, separating alcohol through fraction cutting tower having top pressure of 0.01-0.02 MPa, and condensing overhead steam at 15-40 degrees C, and passing condensate to alcohol refining unit, intermediate refining by methanol or ethanol rectification device, obtaining high-purity ethanol product by vacuum distilling in ethanol refining tower having overhead pressure of 0.01-0.1 MPa and condensing overhead steam at 56-65 degrees C, entering into reflux tank, refining ethanol product at outlet of lower side of ethanol refining tower having recovery temperature of 100-150 degrees C and obtaining high purity methanol product by rectifying in methanol rectification tower at a production temperature of 60-65 degrees C and pressure of 0.11-0.15 MPa. The obtained ethanol product has purity of 99-99.9 wt.%. The obtained methanol product has purity of 98-99.9 wt.%.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN109761594-A
TI Calcium-containing cobalt-free carbon            dioxide-resistant two-phase mixed conductor oxygen            permeable membrane material used for processing oxygen            transmission.
AU LUO H
   WANG S
   XIE Z
   SHI L
   HE Y
   YAN D
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 201946445S
AB    NOVELTY - Calcium-containing cobalt-free carbon                dioxide-resistant two-phase mixed conductor oxygen                permeable membrane material has Xwt.%Ce0.9Pr0.1O2-                delta fluorite oxide phase (100-x) wt.%                Pr0.6Ca0.4FeO3- delta . The perovskite oxide phase                has 0 less than x less than 100.
   USE - Calcium-containing cobalt-free carbon                dioxide-resistant two-phase mixed conductor oxygen                permeable membrane material used for processing                oxygen transmission (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium-containing cobalt-free carbon                dioxide-resistant two-phase mixed conductor oxygen                permeable membrane material is very dense, no                impurity phase is formed, and has high oxygen                permeability at 1000 degrees C temperature, and can                stably operate for a long time withstand carbon                dioxide. In the high temperature separation of                oxygen, oxyfuel combustion, fuel cells and other                fields have a very large application                prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the calcium-containing cobalt-free                carbon dioxide-resistant two-phase mixed conductor                oxygen permeable membrane material , which                involves:
   (A) sequentially weighing the corresponding                nitric acid salt and citric acid, dissolving in                water while ethylene glycol added dropwise into the                beaker, where the ratio of amount of metal ion and                citric acid in 1:2 ratio, and amount of ethylene                glycol (ml) approximately 0.3 times weight of                citric acid (g);
   (B) placing the beaker on a magnetic stirrer                at 90 degrees C temperature, and evaporating the                water until the solution become viscous gel, drying                in an oven at 150 degrees C temperature, and                grinding into a powder;
   (C) then heating at 600 degrees C temperature                for 8 hours to remove the organic matter in the                powder to obtain a precursor powder;
   (D) continue preheating the precursor powder                in a 950 degrees C temperature muffle furnace for                10 hours to obtain a two-phase oxygen permeable                membrane material powder; and
   (E) tabletting the double-phase oxygen                permeable membrane material powder under a pressure                of 8 MPa, and sintering at 1400 degrees C                temperature for 5 hours to obtain a dense oxygen                permeable membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Conditions: Calcium-containing                  cobalt-free carbon dioxide-resistant two-phase                  mixed conductor oxygen permeable membrane                  material preparation involves heating at 4                  degrees C temperature per minute, incubating at                  250 degrees C temperature for 30 minutes,                  incubating at 400 degrees C temperature for 30                  minutes, incubating at 600 degrees C temperature                  for 480 minutes, and natural cooling. The                  temperature is raised by 4 degrees C temperature                  per minute, and the temperature is kept at 950                  degrees C temperature for 600 minutes, and it is                  naturally cooled.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

PT P
PN JP2019041641-A; JP6849563-B2
TI Carbon-dioxide application device for supplying carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas into agricultural greenhouse, has adjustment mechanism that suppresses increase pressure in storage tank after operation of device is stopped.
AU YUDATE M
AE FUTABA SANGYO KK (FUTS-C)
GA 2019261860
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a liquid storage tank that is configured to store liquid and to allow flue gas to pass through the liquid. A supply flow path (15) is configured to supply the combustion exhaust gas having passed through the liquid to the downstream side of the liquid storage tank. A combustion device (2) is provided in the supply flow path for guiding the combustion exhaust gas having passed through the liquid to the downstream side of the liquid storage tank. An adjustment mechanism is adapted for adjusting the pressure in the liquid storage tank. The adjustment mechanism is configured to suppress an increase in pressure in the liquid storage tank after the operation of the device is stopped. Blocking mechanism is configured to shut off the flow of gas from the device into the liquid storage tank as the adjusting mechanism.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide application device for supplying carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas into agricultural greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The discharge of the liquid from the drainage mechanism is suppressed, since the pressure increase in the liquid storage tank is suppressed after the operation of the device is stopped by the adjustment mechanism, thus the liquid level drop in the liquid storage tank can be suppressed. The device easily and reliably suppresses an increase in pressure in the liquid storage tank after shutdown of the device without increasing the number of components constituting the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An open flow passage (9) is configured to open the gas in the supply flow passage to the atmosphere as the adjustment mechanism. The adjustment mechanism includes a control unit (7) is configured to gradually stop the device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon-dioxide application device.
   Carbon-dioxide application device (1)
   Combustion device (2)
   Control unit (7)

PN CN109380027-A
TI Planting site with solar energy illuminating, has electric heating converter which is arranged inside scaffold, and electrothermal converter which is electrically connected to controller.
AU ZHAO Y
AE SICHUAN ZHONGZIXING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2019208825
AB    NOVELTY - The planting site has a scaffold (1) and a plastic film (2). The scaffold is semi-cylindrical, and the two semi-circular faces of the scaffold are sealed to the edge of the plastic film by a thermal insulation board. One of the semi-circular faces is provided with a door (13) for accessing the planting site. Several illumination lamps (5) are uniformly arranged on a crossbar (4), and an environmental detector (9) is fixedly mounted adjacent to the crossbar, the illumination lamp and the environment detector is electrically connected to the controller. The scaffold is internally provided with a light intensity sensor (6) and a carbon dioxide generator (7), and the light intensity sensor and the carbon dioxide generator are electrically connected to the controller. An electric heating converter (8) is arranged inside the scaffold and the electrothermal converter is electrically connected to the controller.
   USE - Planting site with solar energy illuminating.
   ADVANTAGE - The light intensity sensor senses the intensity of the light and transmits the signal to the controller to control the illumination lamp, and simultaneously controls the winch, and automatically adjusts the distance between the illumination lamp and the plant through the traction rope to adjust the light intensity. The ambient detector detects the temperature and the concentration of the carbon dioxide to control the conditions required for the electric heating converter to provide heat and the carbon dioxide generator to generate carbon dioxide to reach plant growth. The use of the solar power generation device for self-generating for use in the planting site improves the environmentally-friendly performance of the entire planting site.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a planting site with solar energy illuminating.

TI Adsorbent for adsorption unit for use in removing carbon dioxide from gas mixture containing carbon dioxide comprises alumina, carbonate compound, and alkali metals in which adsorbent has predetermined carbonate to alumina intensity ratio.
AU LAU G C
   SORENSEN E M
   CASTEEL W J J
   TAYLOR F W
   GOLDEN T C
   QUINN R
AE AIR PROD & CHEM INC (AIRP-C)
GA 201874353X
AB    NOVELTY - An adsorbent for use in removing carbon dioxide (CO2) from gas mixture containing CO2, comprises alumina, carbonate compound, and greater than or equal to 1 alkali metals in which total amount of alkali metals in adsorbent is 0.9-10 wt.% and adsorbent has carbonate to alumina intensity ratio (R) of less than or equal to 0.0150 as determined by Fourier Transform infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy of crushed sample of adsorbent, and carbonate to alumina intensity ratio is ratio of peak absorbance intensity for carbonate (Alc), to peak absorbance intensity for alumina (Ala) where Ala is observed at FTIR wavenumber of 420-520 cm-1, and Alc is observed at FTIR wavenumber of 1300-1400 cm-1.
   USE - An adsorbent for adsorption unit for use in removing carbon dioxide from gas mixture containing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Adsorbent has better carbon dioxide removal performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) making adsorbent particles for use in removing CO2 from gas mixture containing CO2 which involves washing alumina materials which consists of greater than or equal to 1 alkali metals and carbonate compound with water; and drying washed alumina materials to form adsorbent particles where washed and dried adsorbent particles have carbonate to alumina intensity ratio, R2, which is smaller than carbonate to alumina intensity ratio, R1, of alumina compound which consists of greater than or equal to 1 of alkali metal ions and carbonate ions before washing, and carbonate to alumina intensity ratio is as determined by Fourier Transform infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy of crushed sample of washed and dried adsorbent particles and crushed sample of alumina materials containing greater than or equal to 1 alkali metals and carbonate compound before washing;
   (2) removing CO2 from gas mixture containing CO2 which involves passing gas mixture containing CO2 into bed containing adsorbent or particles and withdrawing CO2-depleted gas from bed; and
   (3) adsorption unit comprising bed containing adsorbent or particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Total amount of alkali metals in adsorbent is 1-8 wt.%. Amount of alumina in adsorbent is 90-99 wt.%. Alkali metals are sodium (Na) and/or potassium (K). Amount of carbonate is lower than amount of carbonate necessary for compensating the charge of total amount of alkali metals. Preferred Parameters: Surface area of adsorbent is 220-400 m2/g. Value of carbonate to alumina intensity ratio is 0.003-0.015. Preferred Process: Alumina materials which contain greater than or equal to 1 alkali metals and carbonate compound are washed sufficiently to produce adsorbent particles having carbonate to alumina intensity ratio, R2, of less than or equal to 0.015. Alumina materials which contain greater than or equal to 1 alkali metals and carbonate compound are washed with water until alumina materials have pH in solution of less than or equal to 9.5 forming washed alumina materials where pH in solution is determined by measuring the pH of equilibrated 2 L solution of deionized water containing 100 g washed alumina materials. Alumina materials which contain greater than or equal to 1 alkali metals and carbonate compound are washed with water containing less than or equal to 100 ppm total dissolved solids.

AB    NOVELTY - Stabilizing surface of metallic lithium                comprises (i) firstly soaking the metal lithium in                the fluorine-containing ester solvent, reacting for                a certain time at a certain temperature to obtain                primary modified metal lithium; (ii) placing the                lithium metal primary modified into reaction                kettle, introducing carbon dioxide gas with certain                pressure and vacuumizing the reaction kettle; and                (iii) reacting the metal lithium and carbon dioxide                in the reaction kettle at a certain temperature for                a period of time, and evacuating the carbon                dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for stabilizing surface                of metallic lithium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical, improves                stability to air and is suitable for industrial                production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metallic lithium                  is in the form of a strip, powder, block and                  other forms. The time for which metallic                  lithium is immersed in a fluorine-containing                  ester solvent is 4-24 hours. Step of using calcination to obtain MgO-Mg(OH)2 nano-composites comprises using an aqueous solution of polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) as a polymer base to obtain the MgO-Mg(OH)2 nano-composites. Step of using calcination to obtain MgO-Mg(OH)2 nano-composites comprises calcination at 300degrees Celsius, preferably 500degrees Celsius. Aging the MgO-Mg(OH)2 nano-composites comprises aging the MgO-Mg(OH)2 nano-composites for a multi-month time duration, preferably 3-6 months.Preferred Component: Nano MCHs comprise nanofibers.

AB    NOVELTY - Process for reducing carbon-dioxide emission                of combustion exhaust gas involves (i) preparing                caustic soda by using a natural sodium carbonate                aqueous solution prepared by dissolving purified                powders of sodium carbonate mineral, forming                caustic soda aqueous solution and a calcium                carbonate precipitate by causticization with slaked                lime and performing solid-liquid separation to                obtain a synthetic caustic soda aqueous solution,                and (ii) performing carbon-dioxide emission                reduction treatment process by contacting caustic                soda aqueous solution and purified combustion                exhaust gas. In the step (i), the calcium carbonate                precipitate obtained by solid-liquid separation of                the caustic soda aqueous solution and calcium                carbonate precipitate produced in the                causticization reaction are washed with water and                dried to obtain light calcium carbonate. The                alkaline waste water generated in the washing                treatment is recycled to the aqueous solution                preparation step of the sodium carbonate                mineral.
   USE - Process for reducing carbon-dioxide emission                of combustion exhaust gas e.g. waste gas after                desulfurization, denitration, and dust-removal                process from fuel oil boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, efficient and                economical reduction of carbon-dioxide emission of                combustion exhaust gas. The method does not require                special equipment. The method is capable of                producing sodium-carbonate products and sodium                bicarbonate products with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for reducing carbon-dioxide emission                of combustion exhaust gas using a caustic soda                aqueous solution as a carbon-dioxide absorbing                liquid, involves (i) preparing caustic soda by                using a natural sodium carbonate aqueous solution                prepared by dissolving purified powders of sodium                carbonate mineral comprising 99.2% or more sodium                carbonate component, forming caustic soda aqueous                solution and a calcium carbonate precipitate by                causticization with slaked lime and performing                solid-liquid separation to obtain a synthetic                caustic soda aqueous solution, and (ii) performing                carbon-dioxide emission reduction treatment process                by contacting caustic soda aqueous solution and                purified combustion exhaust gas into gas-liquid                countercurrent such that the carbon-dioxide in the                exhaust gas is absorbed by the caustic soda aqueous                solution and fixed as sodium carbonate for                reduction. In the step (i), the calcium carbonate                precipitate obtained by solid-liquid separation of                the caustic soda aqueous solution and calcium                carbonate precipitate produced in the                causticization reaction are washed with water and                dried to obtain light calcium carbonate. The                alkaline waste water generated in the washing                treatment is recycled to the aqueous solution                preparation step of the sodium carbonate mineral.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for                reducing carbon-dioxide emission of combustion                exhaust gas.

PN ES2673492-A1; ES2673492-B2
TI Polysulfone asymmetric membrane for sequestering carbon dioxide from atmosphere, comprises chemically homogeneous structure of polysulfone or its derivative comprising first porous surface region interconnected with second transport region.
AU VALLS R
   NOGALSKA A
   BARTOSZ T
AE UNIV ROVIRA I VIRGILI (UYRO-Non-standard)
   CENT TECNOLOGIC QUIMICA CATALUNYA (TECN-Non-standard)
GA 2018544111
AB    NOVELTY - Polysulfone asymmetric membrane for sequestering carbon dioxide from the atmosphere, comprises a chemically homogeneous structure of polysulfone or a polysulfone derivative comprising a first porous surface region interconnected with a second transport region including macropores, where the structure comprises magnetic nanoparticles at a concentration of 0.01-35 wt.% with respect to the total weight of the membrane, and the magnetic nanoparticles are mostly distributed in the first surface region.
   USE - The membrane is useful for capturing carbon dioxide from the air in the atmosphere (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane, at pressure and at room temperature, is capable of sequestering carbon dioxide from the air of the atmosphere at a flow rate of greater than or equal to 0.1-1.2 moles of carbon dioxide per m2/hour of the membrane (claimed) without the need to create a partial pressure difference between both faces of the membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the membrane, based on the technology of precipitation by inversion of phases, comprising (a) dissolving a polysulfone polymer or polysulfone derivative at a concentration of 2-30% wt. in an organic solvent with respect to the weight of the organic solvent, stirring the resulting polymer solution, allowing the polymeric solution to stand for degassing, degassing the polymer solution, adding magnetic nanoparticles at a concentration of 0.01-35 wt.% with respect to the weight of the polymer solution and stirring to obtain a mixture of polymeric solution and magnetic nanoparticles, pouring the obtained mixture into a support for this purpose, molding it with the use of a film rod and then submerging it in a coagulation bath comprising a solvent, so that the precipitation of the polymer takes place on the support to form the membrane that comprises magnetic nanoparticles, and removing from the bath, drying and separating the membrane from the support; and
   (2) a system of absorption of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere for transport or subsequent reuse of carbon dioxide, comprising a carbon dioxide absorbing solution, and the membrane, where the first porous surface region defines an outer surface or face of the membrane and is arranged in contact with the air of the atmosphere, and the second transport region including macropores defines another outer surface or opposite face of the membrane and is associated with the carbon dioxide absorbing solution, so that, at pressure and room temperature, the carbon dioxide of the air in the atmosphere is adsorbed spontaneously on the outer surface of the membrane of the surface region, diffused through the membrane through the transport region to the other outer surface or opposite face of the membrane and absorbed in the absorbent solution for transport or subsequent reuse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The carbonic anhydrase enzyme is mostly distributed in the first surface region. The RUBISCO enzyme is mostly distributed in the second transport region.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polysulfone or polysulfone derivative has an average molecular weight of 20000-75000 g/mol. The membrane presents an asymmetry comprised of 3-15%, and has been formed by the technology of precipitation by inversion of phases. The absorbent solution is an alkaline aqueous solution with a pH of 8-9. The system is capable of transporting carbon dioxide at a flow rate of greater than or equal to 0.1-1.2 moles of carbon dioxide per m2/hour of the membrane. The magnetic nanoparticles are functionalized with amino groups. The first porous surface region includes pores of average pore size of 1.5-7 mu m, and the second transport region includes macropores of average surface area of 300-1300 mu m2. The membrane is flat and has a thickness of 70-180 mu m. The structure comprises at least one enzyme comprising ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase-oxygenase (RUBISCO) or a carbonic anhydrase. Preferred Method: The method comprises incubating the membrane in a previously prepared suspension including at least one enzyme comprising RUBISCO or carbonic anhydrase at a concentration of 0.025-1.2 mg/mg of magnetic nanoparticle functionalized with amino groups in the membrane.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN109652477-A
TI Increasing conversion rate in middle and later periods of glutamic acid fermentation by adding inositol and dimethylformamide in fermentation process using ultrasonic treatment.
AU XU C
   CHENG S
   WANG X
   CAO B
AE XU C (XUCC-Individual)
GA 201939352R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for increasing the conversion rate in middle and later periods of glutamic acid fermentation involves adding inositol and dimethylformamide in the fermentation process using ultrasonic treatment.
   USE - Method for increasing the conversion rate in middle and later periods of glutamic acid fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines carbon dioxide fixation, cell penetration and low-strength ultrasonic treatment, and improves the conversion rate and yield of glutamic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves (i) inoculating Corynebacterium glutamicum into seed culture medium, culturing at 32 degrees C for 12-24 hours at a speed of 100 rpm to obtain C.glutamicum seed solution, (ii) inoculating C.glutamicum seed solution into a fermentation tank containing 8-10% fermentation culture medium, fermenting for 48 hours, dividing the fermentation into two stages, initially fermenting at 32 degrees C for 12 hours having ventilation amount of 0.3-0.5 vvm, then fermenting at 32 degrees C for 36 hours having ventilation amount of 0.4-0.6 vvm, starting the second stage by adding inositol and dimethylformamide to the fermentation culture medium, performing ultrasonic treatment at the same time, controlling pH to 5 by automatic flow addition of aqueous ammonia, controlling the residual sugar to not less than 1.0% by adding 100 g/l glucose solution, and collecting the fermentation liquor after completing the second stage. Preferred Conditions: The ultrasonic treatment is carried out at a frequency of 28 kHz and power density of 100 W/l for 1 hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The method comprises 0.6-0.8 %mass inositol and 0.3-0.4 %mass dimethylformamide. The C.glutamicum seed culture medium comprises 6 %mass glucose, 3 %mass corn steep liquor, 0.2 %mass monopotassium phosphate, 0.02 %mass magnesium sulfate heptahydrate and 0.01 %mass manganese sulfate monohydrate. The fermentation tank culture medium comprises 12 %mass glucose, 3 %mass corn steep liquor, 0.5 %mass urea, 0.1 %mass monopotassium phosphate, 0.02 %mass ferrous sulfate heptahydrate, 0.02 %mass magnesium sulfate heptahydrate and 0.02 %mass magnesium sulfate heptahydrate.

PN CN109134943-A; CN109134943-B
TI Preparing pressure-sensitive            cellulose/multi-walled carbon nanotubes/thermoplastic            polyurethane composite foaming material involves            cellulose/multi-walled carbon nanotubes conductive            aerogel, casting thermoplastic polyurethane.
AU FANG W
   FEI Y
   WEI X
   CHEN F
   FEI Z
   FAN P
   HAN J
   YANG J
   ZHONG M
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201905489F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pressure-sensitive                cellulose/multi-walled carbon                nanotubes/thermoplastic polyurethane composite                foaming material involves cellulose/multi-walled                carbon nanotubes conductive aerogel, casting                thermoplastic polyurethane by solution into gas gel                and using supercritical carbon dioxide foaming to                obtain pressure-sensitive cellulose/multi-walled                carbon nanotubes/thermoplastic polyurethane                composite foaming material.
   USE - Method for preparing pressure-sensitive                cellulose/multi-walled carbon                nanotubes/thermoplastic polyurethane composite                foaming material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The                  cellulose/multi-walled carbon nanotubes                  conductive aerogel is prepared by adding sodium                  hydroxide and thiourea, dissolving in deionized                  water to prepare aqueous solution, where ratio of                  water, sodium hydroxide, thiourea is                  80-90:8-10:4-5, dispersing multi-walled carbon                  nanotubes in sodium hydroxide aqueous solution in                  ratio 1:1, processing ultrasonically for 5                  minutes to form uniform dispersion, and                  pre-cooling to (-)8 degrees C, adding 3-7 g                  cellulose in pre-cooled mixed solution to 2000                  recolutions per minute and stirring for 10                  minutes to form uniformly dispersed cellulose                  sol. The gel is poured into mold and put in 60                  degrees C oven, crosslinking reaction for 48                  hours to obtain cellulose/multi-walled carbon                  nanotubes hydrogel. The hydrogel is placed in                  large amount of de-ionized water dialysis until                  neutral, freezed and dried hydrogel to obtain                  cellulose/multi-walled carbon nanotubes                  conductive aerogel. The composite material is                  placed into high pressure kettle, introduced                  carbon dioxide and foamed kettle. The pressure is                  maintained at 13.8 mega pascal. The temperature                  is kept at 100 degrees C and absorbed for 12                  hours. The mixture is put in ice water bath                  cooled and formed to obtain                  cellulose/multi-walled carbon                  nanotubes/thermoplastic polyurethane foaming                  material.

   KIM W

   LEE J
   JUNG H M
   CHOI H S
AE UNIV SUNMOON IND COOP FOUND (UYSU-Non-standard)
GA 2022938227
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has an electrolytic cell divided                into a positive electrode part (102) and a negative                electrode part . The positive and negative                electrode parts are partitioned by a cation                exchange membrane (103). A washing water production                part (200) generates sodium hydroxide aqueous                solution. An air supply unit containing carbon                dioxide is installed on a surface of a washing                water production part. A hypochlorous acid water                storage unit (300) is installed at one surface of                the positive electrode unit. A gas diffusion                barrier is provided for suppressing a phenomenon of                suspended and discharging the carbon dioxide. The                washing water production part reacts with the                sodium hydroxide generated in the negative                electrode part in which the carbon dioxide supplied                from the air supply unit containing the carbon                dioxide reacts to produce sodium carbonate and                sodium hydrogen carbonate.
   USE - The apparatus is useful for producing                hypochlorous acid water.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                generating washing water by generating hypochlorous                acid and removing carbon dioxide, comprising (a)                supplying aqueous solution of sodium chloride to                the positive electrode part of the electrolyzer of                the washing water generating device through the                generation of hypochlorous acid and the removal of                carbon dioxide, (b) generating hypochlorous acid                from chloride ions in the positive electrode part                by applying voltage to the electrolytic cell, and                generating sodium hydroxide from sodium ions in the                negative electrode part, (c) supplying the aqueous                sodium hydroxide solution produced in the negative                electrode part to the washing water production part                so that the carbon dioxide supplied from the carbon                dioxide supply pipe reacts with sodium hydroxide                generated in the negative electrode part to produce                sodium carbonate and sodium hydrogen carbonate, and                (d) supplying the hypochlorous acid aqueous                solution generated in the positive electrode part                to the hypochlorous acid water storage part, where                the cation exchange membrane is made of sulfonated                poly arylene ether sulfone, sulfonated polyether                ether ketone, polybenzimidazole and/or sulfonated                polysulfone, the positive electrode includes                platinum, ruthenium oxide, iridium oxide, and/or                composite oxide of ruthenium and iridium and/or                noble metal-based metal, and the negative electrode                includes titanium, carbon plating, nickel alloy,                stainless steel, hastelloy, and a gas diffusion                electrode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the apparatus for generating hypochlorous acid                water and washing water.

   Cation exchange membrane (103)

   Hypochlorous acid water storage unit                (300)
