TI Method for purifying high-purity carbon dioxide gas, involves designing gas recovery loop between two or more sets of symmetrically operating beds, and refluxing portion of product gas to feed end of adsorbent bed to replace impure gas in gas phase.
AU CHEN Y
   LIU A
   HAN D
   SHI H
   WANG J
   FAN H
   SONG S
   YAN J
   SHEN G
   WANG C
   GU X
   XIE D
   JIA J
   SHEN X
AE SHANGHAI SUISHAN IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   STATE GRID SHANGHAI COMPREHENSIVE ENERGY (SGCC-C)
   SUISHAN NINGBO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUIS-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI HERONG ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI HERONG ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021945647
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adopting pressure swing adsorption technology based on equilibrium adsorption mechanism of carbon dioxide adsorbent, and obtaining high-purity carbon dioxide from the adsorption phase under lower pressure. The gas phase components in the bed are replaced by circulating feed of higher-purity carbon dioxide product gas, and a mixture of higher-purity carbon dioxide and water vapor is obtained through desorption after the bed is saturated with adsorption. A gas recovery loop is designed between two or more sets of symmetrically operating beds to minimize the loss of target gas and increase the recovery rate. A portion of the exhaust gas generated is recirculated in the adsorption separation process to the symmetrical separation bed. A portion of the product gas is refluxed to the feed end of the adsorbent bed to replace the impure gas in the gas phase.
   USE - Method for purifying high-purity carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas recovery loop is designed between two or more sets of symmetrically operating beds to return a portion of the waste gas generated in the adsorption separation process to the symmetrical separation bed to improve the recovery rate of the system. A portion of the product gas is refluxed to the feed end of the adsorbent bed to replace the impure gas in the gas phase. The method can purify and obtain 60-99.9% carbon dioxide gas from the carbon dioxide mixed gas containing about 15%. The purification system makes the purity as high as possible, which can greatly reduce the operating pressure of liquefaction, and the selection of 6.5MPa liquefaction takes into account the effects of low-temperature refrigeration and high-pressure compression. A portion of the exhaust gas generated is recirculated in the adsorption separation process to the symmetrical separation bed, so as to facilitate the pressure increase process of the symmetrical separation bed, reduce the power consumption of the desorption process, and improve the recovery rate of the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a high-purity carbon dioxide gas purification device, which has the feed gas inlet end of the pressure swing adsorption device compositely filled with a water adsorbent to remove impurity gases such as moisture and total hydrocarbons contained in the mixed gas, the water adsorbent is one of activated alumina and silica gel molecular sieves for adsorbing water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a high-purity carbon dioxide gas purification device.
   Control valves (01A,01B,02A,02B,03A,03B,04A,04B)
   Adsorption components (101A,101B)
   Boosting device (B01,C01)
   Product gas buffer tank (PVO1)
   Cooling medium heat exchanger (TC01)

AU FUJITA K
   CUI Z Z
   MATSUO H
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 202255558S
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of 2-oxazolidinone (I) involves cyclizing propargylamine (II) with carbon dioxide in the presence of a catalyst containing silica as an active ingredient.
   USE - Manufacture of 2-oxazolidinone used as intermediates for medical and agricultural chemicals such as antibiotics and furaltadone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of 2-oxazolidinone using economical catalyst that can be reused.

   or R2+R3 = ring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silica is mesoporous silica. The catalyst comprises only silica. Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises silica-coated magnetic nanoparticles comprising magnetic nanoparticles and silica coating the magnetic nanoparticles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The propargylamines and carbon dioxide are reacted at 120-140 degrees C. Preferred Process: The method involves cyclizing propargylamine (II) with carbon dioxide in the presence of catalyst containing silica as active ingredient, and without containing catalytic metals and catalytic organics.

   WANG Y
   LI C
   LI Y
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2021C8508W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of bismuth-based material involves                preparing bismuth phosphate and subjecting to                electrochemical reduction method to form                nano-bismuth-based material.
   USE - Preparation of bismuth-based material used as                catalyst in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide (all claimed) into formic acid in aqueous                electrolyte.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared bismuth-based material has high                oxygen atom insertion amount, high energy                utilization rate and excellent catalytic stability,                and can ensure the Faraday efficiency of formic                acid in process of electrocatalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation involves                  hydrothermally reacting bismuth source precursor                  and phosphorus source precursor in molar ratio of                  1:1-1:5, preferably 1:1 in solvent to obtain                  bismuth phosphate, dispersing bismuth phosphate                  and 5 wt.% perfluorosulfonic acid-PTFE copolymer                  solution in ethanol, ultrasonically processing to                  obtain working electrode, performing                  electrochemical reduction method by using working                  electrode, silver/silver chloride reference                  electrode and platinum electrode as counter                  electrode to obtain bismuth-based material                  nanosheets or nanoparticles.Preferred Properties: The bismuth phosphate                  is columnar particles, and has length of 200 nm                  to 2 mum, diameter of 100 nm to 1 mum, and                  monoclinic crystal structure. When shape of the                  bismuth-based material is a nanosheet, the                  nanosheet has length of 1-10 mum, and thickness                  of 5-15 nm. When shape of the bismuth-based                  material is nanoparticles, the particle size of                  the nanoparticles is 50-150 nm.Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  phosphorous source precursor solution is                  0.02-0.12 mol/L, preferably 0.022 mol/L or 0.044                  mol/L, and bismuth source precursor solution is                  0.5-1 mol/L, preferably 0.67 mol/L. The                  mass-volume ratio of the bismuth phosphate to the                  perfluorosulfonic acid-polytetrafluoroethylene                  copolymer solution is 0.1-2 mg/muL, preferably                  0.25 mg/muL. The mass-volume ratio of the bismuth                  phosphate to the ethanol is 1-50 mg/mL,                  preferably 10 mg/mL. The amount of oxygen atoms                  embedded on the surface of the bismuth-based                  material is 35-60%, preferably 50-60%.Preferred Components: The bismuth source                  precursor is one or more of bismuth                  ethylenediaminetetraacetate, bismuth nitrate and                  bismuth chloride, preferably bismuth nitrate                  pentahydrate. The phosphorus source precursor is                  sodium phosphate dodecahydrate. The solvent is                  ethylene glycol and/or water. The solution in                  reference electrode is 3 M potassium chloride                  solution.

AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen production method involves sequentially subjecting biomass to high-temperature steam catalytic gasification at greater than or equal to 700 degrees C, medium-temperature adsorption enhanced steam reforming at 600-700 degrees C, low-temperature water-gas shift reaction and carbon dioxide adsorption reaction at 300-500 degrees C to obtain high purity hydrogen.
   USE - Hydrogen production method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines catalysis with the in-situ adsorption of carbon dioxide to significantly enhance the hydrogen production process of biomass gasification and by controlling the reaction temperature of each stage to produce high hydrogen concentration and yield.

The catalyst used for medium temperature adsorption enhanced steam reforming process is a nickel based catalyst. The catalyst for low-temperature water-gas shift reaction and carbon dioxide adsorption reaction is nickel-based catalyst, an iron-based catalyst, a cobalt-molybdenum catalyst, a copper-zinc catalyst or supported noble metal catalyst. Calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent is used in medium temperature adsorption enhanced steam reforming process. The calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises nano-calcium oxide, quicklime, calcined dolomite, modified calcium oxide or supported calcium-based absorbent.

AU QIAO J
   PENG L

   LIU J
   GUO J
   WANG X
   LIU Y
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2019349803
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon nitride loaded copper/tin alloy material comprises loading carbon material loaded with carbon nitride as working electrode under three-electrode system, preparing plating solution comprising copper chloride and tin chloride, and co-depositing at negative potential.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon nitride loaded copper/tin alloy material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple preparation method, easy operation, and low cost, is environment-friendly, adsorbs carbon dioxide molecules, inhibits competitive hydrogen evolution reaction in the electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, and solves the problem of low carbon dioxide reduction current density.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon nitride is prepared by putting carbon material in urea solution, sonicating for 0.5-3 hours, drying for 1-5 hours at 50-80 degrees C, and calcining at 350-750 degrees C for 3-8 hours. The electroplating solution is prepared by taking trisodium citrate, urea, stannous chloride and copper chloride, dissolving in deionized water, stirring, adjusting pH to 3-10, adding sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, stirring, and sonicating.

PT P
PN CN108641807-A
TI Preparation of fragrant sesame oil involves frying sesame, crushing, subjecting obtained sesame powder to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under specified extraction pressure and temperature and purifying obtained sesame oil.
AU OUYANG D
AE ANHUI LAOOUMOFANG FOOD TRADE CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018828868
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of fragrant sesame oil involves (1) frying sesame and crushing 130-150 mesh to obtain sesame powder, (2) subjecting the obtained sesame powder to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and controlling extraction pressure of 25-35 MPa, extraction temperature at 30-40 degrees C for 150-200 minutes and controlling carbon dioxide flow rate of 18-25 L/hour to obtain sesame oil, and (3) purifying the obtained sesame oil.
   USE - Preparation of fragrant sesame oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of fragrant sesame oil ensuring high quality, increased purity and improved taste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adsorbent comprises activated clay, montmorillonite and activated carbon at weight ratio of (8-10):(3-5):1. The content of adsorbent is 0.2-0.5 wt.% of sesame oil.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction uses ethanol as entraining agent. Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of ethanol is 20-30% of carbon dioxide flow rate. Preferred Process: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction specifically involves using two separation kettles, controlling pressure of separation kettle (A) at 7-9 MPa and temperature at 38-42 degrees C, and controlling pressure of separation kettle (B) at 3-5 MPa and temperature at 32-36 degrees C. The step (1) involves frying sesame at 50-60 degrees C for 8-10 minutes, stirring frying at 130-150 degrees C for 3-5 minutes and frying at 70-80 degrees C for 10-15 minutes. The method for removing impurities involves adding adsorbent to sesame oil, stirring for 30-40 minutes and filtering.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A01; D10-A02
IP C11B-001/04; C11B-001/10; C11B-003/00; C11B-003/10

TI Edible oil dispensing equipment, has metal container whose inner portion is moved between inner surface and outer surface of bag with gas mixture, where gas mixture comprises nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
AU STAJERSKI J
AE SCHOTHELT SP ZOO SPK (SCHO-Non-standard)
GA 201884647P
AB    NOVELTY - The equipment has a metal container closed from a top portion and provided with a valve on which a cap is attached with a spray nozzle. An inner portion of the metal container is attached with the valve that is integrated into a bag filled with edible oil. The inner portion of the metal container is moved between an inner surface and an outer surface of the bag with gas mixture. The gas mixture comprises nitrogen and carbon dioxide, where amount of the carbon dioxide is 15-35% of total mixture.
   USE - Edible oil dispensing equipment.

PT P
PN RU2656490-C1
TI Method of producing recoverable carbon dioxide absorber, involves mixing zirconium hydroxide with polymer binder and extruded.
AU YARKINA L A
   BULAEV N A
   FERAPONTOV YU A
   POSTERNAK N V
AE ROSKHIMZASHCHITA CORP STOCK CO (ROSK-Non-standard)
GA 201855672F
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to a process for the production of a regenerable carbon dioxide absorber. Zirconium hydroxide is mixed with the polymer binder and extruded. As a binder, polymers from a number of polysulfones in a solvent of tetrahydrofuran are used. After molding, the resulting granules are dried in vacuum at a residual pressure of not more than 5 mm Hg. and temperatures from 65 to 85 degrees C until the solvent is completely removed.
   USE - Chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention makes it possible to increase the water resistance and strength of the absorber granules during long-term operation while maintaining high rates of dynamic activity for carbon dioxide.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN FI128819-B1; WO2021110524-A1; CA3155563-A1; KR2022072865-A; CN114729268-A; EP4069803-A1
TI Method for upgrading bio-based material has            bio-based fresh feed material and an optional diluent            to a hydrotreatment.
AU OJALA A
   MYLLYOJA J
   MAKRONEN J
   VAN DE VELDE R
   JAMIESON J
   MAKKONEN J
   VANDEVELDE R
AE NESTE OYJ (NESO-C)
   NESTE CORP (NESO-C)
   NESTE OYJ (NESO-C)
GA 202102795N
AB    NOVELTY - Upgrading (m1) a bio-based material comprises:                e.g. (A) providing a bio-based fresh feed material                of bio-renewable oils and/or fats having 2-60 wt.%                a glycerol; (B) subjecting a hydrotreatment feed                comprising the bio-based fresh feed material and an                optional diluent to a hydrotreatment comprising HDO                in the presence of a sulfided metal catalyst and                hydrogen to provide a hydrotreated effluent; (C)                subjecting the hydrotreated effluent to gas-liquid                separation so as to provide a gaseous hydrotreated                material comprising hydrogen, bio-propane, water,                hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide or carbon monoxide                and a liquid hydrotreated material comprising                paraffinic hydrocarbons; and (D) subjecting the                liquid hydrotreated material to fractionation,                after an optional second hydrotreatment and                recovering at least diesel and/or paraffin range                paraffinic hydrocarbon material.
   USE - The method is useful for upgrading a bio-based                material e.g. a liquefied bio-propane composition                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and effective; requires                less space; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Upgrading (m2) a bio-based material comprises:                (A) providing a bio-based fresh feed material of                bio-renewable oils and/or fats having 2-60 wt.% a                glycerol; (B) subjecting a hydrotreatment feed                comprising the bio-based fresh feed material and an                optional diluent to a hydrotreatment comprising HDO                in the presence of a sulfided metal catalyst and                hydrogen to provide a hydrotreated effluent, where                the hydrotreatment feed comprises 10-10000 wt.-ppm                sulfur-containing compound calculated as elemental                sulfur; (C) subjecting the hydrotreated effluent to                gas-liquid separation so as to provide a gaseous                hydrotreated material comprising hydrogen,                bio-propane, water, hydrogen sulfide, carbon                dioxide or carbon monoxide and a liquid                hydrotreated material comprising paraffinic                hydrocarbons; (D) subjecting the liquid                hydrotreated material to fractionation, after an                optional second hydrotreatment and recovering at                least diesel and/or paraffin range paraffinic                hydrocarbon material; (E) subjecting the gaseous                hydrotreated material to a purification step for                removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide to                obtain a hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide                depleted gaseous stream; (F) subjecting the                hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide depleted                gaseous stream to hydrogen recovering and drying to                obtain dried hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and                hydrogen depleted gaseous stream; and (G)                fractionating the dried hydrogen sulfide, carbon                dioxide and hydrogen depleted gaseous stream to                recover a bio-propane gas composition and                optionally compressing the bio-propane gas                composition to obtain a liquefied bio-propane                composition. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a liquefied bio-propane composition having                a vapor pressure of 1200-1500 kPa at 40 degrees C                and a density of 495-520 kg/m3 at 15 degrees C and                comprising at least 94 wt.% bio-propane, at most                2000 wt.-ppm carbon dioxide, at most 1000 wt.-ppm                carbon monoxide, at most 15 wt.-ppm                sulfur-containing compounds, calculated as                elemental sulfur, at most 1500 wt.-ppm unsaturated                hydrocarbons, at most 5.5 wt.% hydrocarbons having                more than 3 carbon atoms (C3+ hydrocarbons), where                at most 1.4 wt.% (relative to the liquefied                bio-propane composition) are hydrocarbons having                greater than or equal to 5 carbon atoms and                optionally at most 1500 wt.-ppm, preferably at most                500 wt.- ppm propylene;
   (2) upgrading (m1) a bio-based material                comprising (A)-(G) as per se, (H) subjecting at                least part of the bio-propane gas composition                and/or the liquefied bio-propane composition to a                conversion comprising catalytic dehydrogenation to                obtain a dehydrogenation effluent comprising bio                propylene and (I) recovering and optionally                purifying a bio-propylene composition from the                dehydrogenation effluent; and
   (3) a biopolymer composition obtainable by the                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The bio-based fresh                  feed material has at least 3 wt.%, preferably at                  least 7 wt.% glycerol or at least 8 wt.%                  glycerol; and/or the bio-based fresh feed                  material has less than or equal to 55 wt.%,                  preferably less than or equal to 50 wt.% or less                  than or equal to 45 wt.% or less than or equal to                  40 wt.% or less than or equal to 35 wt.% or less                  than or equal to 30 wt.% or less than or equal to                  25 wt.% or less than or equal to 20 wt.% the                  glycerol; and/or the bio-based fresh feed                  material has 4-50 wt.%, preferably 6-40 wt.% or                  7-30 wt.% the glycerol. The bio propane gas                  composition and/or the liquefied bio-propane                  composition has at least 90-97 wt.% a propane.                  Preferred Components: The hydrotreatment feed                  comprises 10-1000 wt.-ppm, preferably 10-300                  wt.-ppm sulfur-containing compound calculated as                  elemental sulfur. Preferred Method: In the method                  (m1): The method comprises in step (D),                  subjecting the liquid hydrotreated material to                  fractionation after a second hydrotreatment                  comprising hydroisomerization and recovering at                  least diesel and/or paraffin range iso-paraffinic                  hydrocarbon material. The step (A) comprises a                  step (A') of pre-treating bio-renewable oil(s)                  and/or fat(s) for reducing contaminants in the                  oil(s) and/or fat(s) to produce the bio-based                  fresh feed material. The purification step (E)                  further comprises a step (E') of recovering the                  hydrogen sulfide removed from the gaseous                  hydrotreated material and recycling the recovered                  hydrogen sulfide to the hydrotreatment feed. The                  method further comprises: a step of subjecting at                  least part of the bio-propane gas composition                  and/or the liquefied bio-propane composition to a                  conversion comprising catalytic dehydrogenation                  to obtain a dehydrogenation effluent, followed by                  recovering at least bio-propylene in the                  dehydrogenation effluent to obtain, after                  optional purification, a bio-propylene                  composition; and (co)polymerizing at least                  bio-propylene of the bio-propylene composition                  and/or at least a derivative of bio-propylene of                  the bio-propylene composition, optionally                  together with other (co)monomer(s) to produce a                  bio-polymer. In the method (M2) the liquefied                  bio-propane composition is produced by the                  method. The method further comprises:                  derivatization of at least part of the                  bio-propylene composition to obtain at least one                  bio-propylene derivative, preferably at least one                  bio-propylene derivative comprising                  bio-(meth)acrylic acid, bio-acrylonitrile,                  bio-acrolein or bio-propylene oxide;                  (co)polymerizing a mixture comprising at least                  part of the recovered bio propylene composition                  and/or its derivative(s) and optionally                  co-monomer(s) and/or additive(s) to obtain a                  biopolymer composition.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. PT P
PN US2020206680-A1
TI Installation contains source of biogas, main compressor, first and second purification stage comprising membrane selective for carbon dioxide over methane, and high recovery module comprising third purification stage.
AU MITARITEN M J
AE AIR LIQUIDE ADVANCED TECHNOLOGIES US LLC (AIRL-C)
GA 2020592074
AB    NOVELTY - Installation comprises: a source of a biogas; a main compressor (10), an inlet of which is in fluid communicating with the source; a first purification stage (35) comprising at least one membrane selective for carbon dioxide over methane, where an inlet of the first purification stage is in fluid communication with an outlet of the main compressor; a second purification stage (50) comprising at least one membrane selective for carbon dioxide over methane, where an inlet of the second purification stage is in fluid communication with a retentate outlet of the first purification stage; and a high recovery module comprising a third purification stage comprising at least one membrane in parallel or in series that are selective for carbon dioxide over methane and a secondary compressor in fluid communication between a permeate outlet of the first purification stage and an inlet of the third purification stage.
   USE - The installation is useful for: producing biomethane containing at least 94% methane; and increasing a recovery of methane in biomethane product gas produced by an existing installation for producing biomethane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The installation improves recoveries of biogas methane in the biomethane product gas and removes impurities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Installation comprises: a source of a biogas; a main compressor (10), an inlet of which is in fluid communicating with the source; a first purification stage (35) comprising at least one membrane selective for carbon dioxide over methane, where an inlet of the first purification stage is in fluid communication with an outlet of the main compressor; a second purification stage (50) comprising at least one membrane selective for carbon dioxide over methane, where an inlet of the second purification stage is in fluid communication with a retentate outlet of the first purification stage; and a high recovery module comprising a third purification stage comprising at least one membrane in parallel or in series that are selective for carbon dioxide over methane and a secondary compressor in fluid communication between a permeate outlet of the first purification stage and an inlet of the third purification stage, where a retentate outlet of the third purification stage is in fluid communication with the first purification stage inlet so as to allow a retentate stream (40) from the third purification stage to be recycled to the first purification stage. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for increasing a recovery of methane in biomethane product gas produced by an existing installation for producing biomethane comprising an inlet of the main compressor being in fluid communication downstream of the source, an inlet of the first purification stage being in fluid communication downstream of an outlet of the main compressor, an inlet of the second purification stage being in fluid communication downstream of a retentate outlet of the first purification stage, a permeate outlet of the second purification stage being in fluid communication with the inlet of the main compressor, comprising (a) providing a high recovery module comprising a secondary compressor and a third purification stage comprising at least one membrane in series or in parallel that are selective for carbon dioxide over methane, (b) placing an inlet of the secondary compressor in downstream flow communication with a permeate outlet of the first purification stage, (c) placing an inlet of the third purification stage in downstream flow communication with an outlet of the secondary compressor, and (d) placing a retentate outlet of the third purification stage in fluid communication with the inlet of the first purification stage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a biomethane production containing a high recovery module and hydrogen sulfide removal media.
   Main compressor (10)
   First purification stage (35)
   Retentate stream (40)
   Permeate stream (45)
   Second purification stage (50)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: A permeate outlet of the second purification stage is in fluid communication with the main compressor inlet so as to allow a permeate stream (45) from the second purification stage to be recycled to the main compressor. The installation further comprises: a dehydration unit in fluid communication between the main compressor outlet and the first purification stage inlet i.e. adapted and configured to remove water from a combination stream received from the compressor outlet prior to the combination stream is fed to the first purification stage inlet; a hydrogen sulfide (H2S) removal unit comprising a H2S scavenger media in fluid communication between the main compressor and the first purification stage, where the H2S removal unit is adapted and configured to remove H2S from a combination stream received from the compressor; and a H2S removal unit comprising a H2S scavenger media in fluid communication downstream of a retentate outlet of the second stage purification unit, where the H2S unit is adapted and configured to remove H2S from a second stage retentate stream produced by the second purification stage. The dehydration unit is a condenser (15) or dehydration media.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The membrane of the first purification stage comprises a porous polymeric substrate and at least one separation layer, where the polymeric substrate comprises polyimides, polysulfones, and/or polyether ether ketones. The polymeric substrate is a polyether ether ketone. The separation layer: is made of a copolymer or block polymer of formula (I); is made of repeating units of monomers of formula (Ia) or (Ib); and is made of a copolymer or block polymer of tetramethylene oxide, propylene oxide, and/or ethylene oxide.PA = aliphatic polyamide having 6 or 12 carbon atoms;PE = either poly(ethylene oxide) or poly (tetramethylene oxide); andn, s, y, x = not defined.The membrane of the first purification stage: is in the form of flat films or many hollow fibers; is selectivity for H2S, CO6+ hydrocarbons, carbon dioxide, siloxane and water over methane. The stream of the first gas mixture has a pressure drop of less than 50 psi from the feed gas. Each of the membranes of the second stage is made of cellulose acetate, a polysulfone, a polyimide, and/or polyamide. The membrane of the third purification stage: is the same as the membrane of the first purification stage; and is the same as the membrane of the second purification stage. The high recovery module further comprises: a fourth purification stage comprising at least one membrane selective for carbon dioxide over methane; an inlet of the fourth purification being in downstream flow communication with a retentate outlet of the third purification stage; a permeate outlet of the second purification stage is in fluid communication with the main compressor inlet so as to allow a permeate stream from the second purification stage to be recycled to the main compressor; and a retentate outlet of the fourth purification stage is in fluid communication with the first purification stage inlet so as to allow a retentate stream from the fourth purification stage to be recycled to the first purification stage.

PN KR1882813-B1
TI Converting methane and carbon dioxide into syngas using plasma triple reforming process system comprising plasma triple reforming reactor, heat exchanger, cyclone,bag filter, pressure gage compressor and gaseous product separator.
AU CHO W
   LEE J
   YU H
AE BIO FRIENDS INC (BIOF-Non-standard)
GA 2018609011
AB    NOVELTY - Converting methane and carbon dioxide in syngas using a plasma triple reforming process system comprising a plasma triple reforming reactor, a heat exchanger, a cyclone, a bag filter, a pressure gage compressor and a gaseous product separator, is claimed. The methane and carbon dioxide is generated from biogas or landfill gas. The plasma triple reforming process system is provided with e.g. DC-RF hybrid plasma torch to convert methane as thermal source, provided with DC non-transferred torch as low temperature plasma torch located at upper side, and an inductively coupled RF torch located at lower side, a first injection port for injecting air including nitrogen gas and oxygen gas, connected to DC-RF hybrid plasma torch, a second injection port for injecting the raw material gas including methane and carbon dioxide, connected to produce from biogas or landfill gas in the DC-RF hybrid plasma torch, and a plasma reaction unit.
   USE - The method is useful to convert methane and carbon dioxide in syngas for producing hydrogen, methanol and dimethyl ether.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting methane and carbon dioxide in syngas using a plasma triple reforming process system comprising a plasma triple reforming reactor, a heat exchanger, a cyclone, a bag filter, a pressure gage compressor and a gaseous product separator, is claimed. The methane and carbon dioxide is generated from biogas or landfill gas. The plasma triple reforming process system is provided with a DC-RF hybrid plasma torch (100) to convert methane as a thermal source, provided with a DC non-transferred torch as low temperature plasma torch located at upper side, and an inductively coupled RF torch located at lower side, a first injection port for injecting air including nitrogen gas and oxygen gas, connected to the DC-RF hybrid plasma torch, a second injection port for injecting the raw material gas including methane and carbon dioxide, connected to produce from biogas or landfill gas in the DC-RF hybrid plasma torch, a plasma reaction unit (200) disposed at a rear end of the DC-RF hybrid plasma torch unit and homogeneous reaction region, a catalytic reacting unit (300) provided at a rear end of the plasma reaction unit and including a catalyst, and a discharge port (400) for discharging the synthesis gas generated through the catalytic reaction unit. The gas-decomposed gases are transferred to the catalytic reacting unit and can react on the catalytic reaction unit by the plasma reaction in the plasma reacting unit. The gas mixture discharged from the outlet of the plasma triple reforming reactor is passed through a heat exchanger, passed through a cyclone and a back filter, and collected. The collected gas mixture is transferred to a gaseous product separator through a compression gauge compressor and separated into meteorological material. The gaseous product separation unit separates synthesis gas into the synthesis gas and the unreacted gaseous raw material by adsorbing the synthesis gas in an adsorbent made of zeolite, activated carbon, silica gel or alumina. The un-reacted gaseous feedstock is recycled to the plasma triple reforming reactor. The biogas is generated in a sewage treatment plant, a food waste digestion gas or an animal manure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the plasma triple reforming process system (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   DC-RF hybrid plasma torch (100)
   Cooling water pipe (130)
   Plasma reaction unit (200)
   Catalytic reaction unit (300)
   Discharge port (400)

TI Aluminum-cobalt composite catalyst for            photothermal carbon dioxide methanation, comprises            alumina as carrier and cobalt uniformly dispersed on            carrier in form of zero-valent cobalt in preset loading            amount.
AU JIA H
   CHEN X
AE INST URBAN ENVIRONMENT CHINESE ACAD SCI (CUBN-C)
GA 202154806Q
AB    NOVELTY - An aluminum-cobalt composite catalyst                comprises alumina as carrier and cobalt uniformly                dispersed on the carrier in the form of zero-valent                cobalt in the loading amount of 5-50 wt.%.
   USE - Aluminum-cobalt composite catalyst is used for                photothermal carbon dioxide methanation                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst comprises uniformly                dispersed cobalt, uses the strong combination of                cobalt-based organic metal framework and alumina to                maintain the shape, enables efficient conversion of                carbon dioxide to methane, has strong light                absorption under full spectrum, converts absorbed                light energy into heat energy without using                external heater, and improves methane yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of the aluminum-cobalt                composite catalyst, which involves preparing a                precursor of alumina and cobalt-based metal organic                framework, calcining the precursor, and reducing;                and
   (2) photothermal catalytic carbon dioxide                methanation, which involves making the composite                catalyst absorb light energy of visible light,                infrared light, and/or UV light, and converting the                absorbed light energy into heat energy to catalyze                carbon dioxide methanation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The composite catalyst                  has a specific surface area of 75-150 m2/g, a                  pore volume of 0.6-0.9 cm3/g, an average pore                  diameter of 15-30 nm, and an average particle                  diameter of 800-2500 nm. Preferred Method: The                  catalyst preparation involves preparing a mixed                  solution using cobalt salt, alumina, methanol,                  2-methylimidazole, and cobalt-based metal organic                  framework by ultrasonic dispersion and stirring,                  stirring and aging the precursor mixed solution                  for 2-24 hours, filtering, drying at 60-100                  degrees C for 1-24 hours to obtain a precursor                  alumina-ZIF-67, calcining the precursor in air at                  250-600 degrees C for 2-10 hours, and reducing at                  300-600 degrees C for 2-10 hours. Preferred                  Components: A cobalt-aluminum spinel structure is                  formed by reinforced cobalt-aluminum interaction.                  The cobalt salt is cobalt nitrate, cobalt                  chloride, and/or cobalt acetate. Alumina is nano                  alumina, preferably crystalline form of gamma                  -alumina. Preferred Composition: The usage amount                  of alumina is 1-35 wt.% with respect to cobalt                  salt. The mass ratio of cobalt salt and                  2-methylimidazole is 0.5-1.2:1. The ratio (mL/g)                  of methanol and cobalt salt is (8-20):1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compounds: The imidazole compound                  is an alkyl imidazole or an aryl imidazole. The                  raw material further comprises methanol.

PT P
PN CN110368945-A; CN110368945-B
TI Enhancing carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation            reaction involves preparing structured catalyst by            supporting active component able to hydrogenate carbon            dioxide, placing structured catalyst in feed gas, and            turning on power source.
AU YAN C
   DOU L
   LI X
   ZHANG D
   ZHANG J
   XIAO L
AE INST ELECTRICAL ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CADG-C)
GA 201990356V
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing carbon dioxide catalytic                hydrogenation reaction involves preparing a                structured catalyst by supporting an active                component having a catalytic ability to hydrogenate                carbon dioxide (CO2) on a porous conductive                skeleton. The structured catalyst is connected to                the circuit to conduct current. The structured                catalyst is placed in a feed gas containing CO2 and                hydrogen, and turning on a power source to apply a                current to the circuit. The Joule heat generated by                the current on the structured catalyst can heat the                structured catalyst to a temperature at which a CO2                hydrogenation reaction can be carried out, and CO2                and hydrogen are reacted on the structured                catalyst.
   USE - Method for enhancing carbon dioxide catalytic                hydrogenation reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves applying electric current                to the structured catalyst of the conductive                skeleton to achieve electric heating, so that the                catalyst is more uniformly heated, that results in                improving the activity of catalyst and ability to                resist sulfur poisoning, that further results in                significantly improving the conversion performance                of CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The material of the                  porous conductive skeleton is selected from a                  metal, a carbon material, and/or a semiconductor.                  The structural form of the porous conductive                  skeleton adopts a foam structure, a honeycomb                  structure or a fiber structure. The active                  ingredient is selected from metallic nickel,                  cobalt, iron, copper, rhodium, ruthenium,                  palladium, and zinc. Preferred Process: The                  method for preparing the structured catalyst                  comprises placing nickel foam in an acid for                  ultrasonic cleaning, and after repeated washing                  with deionized water and absolute ethanol,                  performing first drying to obtain a conductive                  skeleton. Nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate,                  aluminum nitrate nonahydrate and urea are                  dissolved in deionized water to obtain a mixed                  solution. The conductive skeleton is immersed in                  the mixed solution, and a reaction is performed                  to obtain a reaction product. The reaction                  product is sequentially subjected to a second                  drying and calcination to obtain the structured                  catalyst. Preferred Conditions: The reaction has                  a reaction temperature of 200 to 350 degrees C.                  The loading amount of the active ingredient in                  the structured catalyst is 0.01 to 30 wt.%. The                  raw material gas contains CO2 and hydrogen in a                  ratio of 1: (3-5). The porous electrically                  conductive skeleton is a metal foam, preferably                  nickel foam. The structured catalyst is a                  hydrothermal in situ growth method, and the                  active ingredient is metallic nickel. The acid                  comprises hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid or                  nitric acid, the concentration of the acid is 4-8                  mole/L, and the ultrasonic cleaning time is 3-7                  minute. The first drying temperature is 50-80                  degrees C, the time is 2-4 hour, the reaction                  temperature is 100-120 degrees C, and the time is                  6-10 hour. The second drying temperature is                  performed at 50-80 degrees C for 2-4 hour. The                  calcination is performed at 400-600 degrees C for                  2-4 hour. The calcination is carried out in a                  hydrogen/nitrogen (H2/N2) mixed gas, and the                  volume ratio of H2 to N2 in the H2/N2 mixed gas                  is 1: (5-15). The obtained structured catalyst is                  naturally cooled to room temperature.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN109232781-A; CN109232781-B
TI Preparing nitrogen-containing porous organic polymer comprises e.g. synthesizing nitrogen containing porous organic polymer by nitrogen containing monomer and 1,4-dichloro-benzyl or vinyl benzyl chloride under catalysis of lewis acid.
AU LIU F
   MI J
   PAN S
   CAO Y
   JIANG L
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 201913311P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-containing porous organic polymer comprises synthesizing nitrogen containing porous organic polymer through alkylation by crosslinking induced by nitrogencontaining monomer and 1,4-dichloro-benzyl or vinyl benzyl chloride under the catalysis of the lewis acid condition.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nitrogen-containing porous organic polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen-containing polymer: has stable nitrogen-containing active site, large proportion of mesoporous macropores, unique carbon dioxide enrichment, and high carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity, excellent application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of nitrogen-containing porous organic for selective adsorption of carbon dioxide comprising carbon dioxide selective adsorption temperature is 0 degrees C and 25 degrees C loading adsorbent was 0.1 g, carrying out the pretreatment under vacuum conditions before adsorption at 160 degrees C for 12 hours, and carrying out the adsorption of carbon dioxide and nitrogen in Dewar at 0 degrees C and 25 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen-containing monomer is para-phenylene diamine, 4,4'-bipyridine, hexamethylene tetramine or 4-vinyl pyridine. The nitrogen-containing monomer is one of p-phenylenediamine, 4,4'-bipyridine, hexamethylenetetramine or 4-vinylpyridine. The nitrogen-containing monomer and 1,4-p-dichlorobenzyl are combined to form a nitrogen-containing porous organic polymer by alkylation under Lewis acid catalyzed conditions, Preferred Method: The first a certain mass of 1,4-pair Dichlorobenzyl is dissolved in 1,2-dichloroethane, vigorously stirred at 0 degrees C for 30-120 seconds, and a Lewis acid catalyst is added to the mixture. The nitrogen-containing monomer is then added to the mixture and heat treated in a nitrogen atmosphere at elevated temperature for a period of time, suction filtration, a solid is formed and washed with hot anhydrous ethanol for 3-5 times to remove residual reactants and Lewis acid catalyst, the crude product is immersed in isopropylamine, treated at room temperature for 6-12 hours for activation, and vacuum dried to obtain A nitrogen-containing porous organic polymer, designated N-POP-x, where x is a nitrogen-containing monomer. The mass of the 1,4-p-dichlorobenzyl is 0.5-2 g, the mass of the nitrogen-containing monomer is 0.5-1.5 g, and the volume of the 1,2-dichloroethane is 15-30 ml. The nitrogen-containing monomer and vinylbenzyl chloride are combined to form a nitrogen-containing porous organic polymer by alkylation under Lewis acid-catalyzed conditions. The specific steps comprises a certain amount of 1,4-p-dichlorobenzyl is dissolved in 1,2-dichloroethane, an azobisisobutyronitrile AIBN initiator is added, and then vigorously stirred at 0 degrees C 30-120 seconds, and a Lewis acid catalyst is added to the mixture. The nitrogen-containing monomer is then added to the mixture and heat treated in a nitrogen atmosphere at elevated temperature for a period of time, suction filtration, a solid is formed and washed with hot anhydrous ethanol for 3-5 times to remove residual reactants and Lewis acid catalyst, the crude product is immersed in isopropylamine, treated at room temperature for 6-12 hours for activation, and vacuum dried to obtain A nitrogen-containing porous organic polymer, designated N-POP-x, where x is a nitrogen-containing monomer. The vinylbenzyl chloride has a volume of 0.5-1.5 ml, an initiator mass of 0.05 g, a nitrogen-containing monomer mass of 0.5-1.5 g, and a volume of 15-30 ml of 1,2-dichloroethane. The Lewis acid catalyst is tin tetrachloride or Titanium tetrachloride added in an amount of 2-5 ml. The heat treatment temperature is 60-90 degrees C, and the heat treatment time is 2-6 hours. The temperature and time for vacuum drying were 60-120 degrees C and 6-24 hours.

AU OKU T
   SUEHIRO M
AE NISSAN MOTOR CO LTD (NSMO-C)
   RENAULT SAS (RENA-C)

PN PL431645-A1
TI Oxygen and carbon dioxide mixture feeding method for internal combustion engine involves supplying gas to intake manifold and then to combustion chamber of engine after gas pressure is reduced to predetermined value.
AU KASZKOWIAK J
AE UNIV TECHNOLOGICZNO-PRZYRODNICZY IM JANA (UYTE-Non-standard)
GA 202159119P
AB    NOVELTY - The oxygen and carbon dioxide mixture feeding method involves supplying gas gas to the intake manifold and/or the combustion chamber of an engine. The gas is a mixture of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the proportion of 21% oxygen and 79% carbon dioxide measured in a volumetric manner. The gas is compressed in a pressure cylinder to a pressure of 10 megapascals (MPa) by a reducer. The gas is expanded in an intermediate tank to a pressure of 0.3-0.5 MPa. The gas pressure is reduced by the reducer to a value of 0.1-0.12 MPa. The gas is supplied to the intake manifold and then to the combustion chamber of the engine.
   USE - Oxygen and carbon dioxide mixture feeding method for internal combustion engine powered by gasoline, diesel and other liquid fuels and gases e.g. liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), compressed natural gas (CNG), hydrogen gas (H2).

PN CN112237852-A
TI Block polyetheramide mixed substrate film filled with bionic material bio-ZIF used in carbon dioxide separation, is prepared by using bio-ZIF and block copolymer of polyamide and polyether blocks.
AU LI X
   HUANG L
   DING S
   LV X
   WEI Z
AE UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
GA 202109675C
AB    NOVELTY - Block polyetheramide mixed substrate film filled with bionic material bio-ZIF is prepared by using bio-ZIF and block polyetheramide Pebax (RTM: Block copolymer of polyamide and polyether blocks), where the thickness of mixed substrate film is 100-130 mu m.
   USE - The block polyetheramide mixed substrate film filled with biomimetic material bio-ZIF is useful in separation of carbon dioxide/methane mixed gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The substrate film is economical and prepared by simple process, has controllable reaction, adopts easy-to-obtain raw materials, ensures mild conditions, exhibits synergistic promotion effect on carbon dioxide, has carbon dioxide affinity and sieving effect, and strengthens the transfer of carbon dioxide in membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing block polyetheramide mixed matrix film filled with the biomimetic material bio-ZIF, involving preparing mixed substrate film by incorporating bio-ZIF as filler to block polyetheramide Pebax (RTM: Block copolymer of polyamide and polyether blocks) substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In mixed substrate film, the concentration of bio-ZIF is 4-16 %mass, and the concentration of block polyetheramide Pebax (RTM: Block copolymer of polyamide and polyether blocks) is 84-96 %mass. In mixed substrate film, the mass ratio of bio-ZIF and block polyetheramide Pebax (RTM: Block copolymer of polyamide and polyether blocks) is 4:96, 8:92, 12:88 or 16:84. The molar ratio of 2-methylimidazole, zinc nitrate and 3-amino-1,2,4-triazole is 4:1:1.49. Preferred Method: The bio-ZIF is prepared by uniformly mixing 2-methylimidazole and zinc nitrate, and reacting to obtain ZIF-8, reacting obtained ZIF-8 with 3-amino-1,2,4-triazole to obtain bio-ZIF. The bio-ZIF preparation involves dissolving 2-methylimidazole and zinc nitrate separately, uniformly mixing, ultrasonically reacting, collecting the precipitate by centrifugation and washing after leaving to obtain ZIF-8, drying obtained ZIF-8 and dispersing in methanol to obtain ZIF-8 suspension, adding 3-amino-1,2,4-triazole to ZIF-8 suspension, reacting with stirring condition under water bath, and collecting the precipitate by centrifugation, washing and drying to obtain bio-ZIF. The method involves incorporating bio-ZIF as filler to block polyetheramide Pebax (RTM: Block copolymer of polyamide and polyether blocks) by physically blending bio-ZIF and block polyetheramide Pebax (RTM: Block copolymer of polyamide and polyether blocks), uniformly stirring at room temperature to obtain casting solution, casting and drying obtained casting solution to obtain block polyetheramide mixed substrate film filled with biomimetic material bio-ZIF.

TI Temperature control device for planting eucommia bark, has external display screen, electromagnetic valve, and air exchange fan that are electrically connected with power supply line system.
AU ZHANG J
   WANG T
AE NANZHAO JINLEI AGRIC CULTURE IND PARK (NANZ-Non-standard)
GA 202278996M
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model model relates to the technical field of temperature control device, especially claims a temperature control device for planting eucommia bark, comprising a combustion furnace fixed outside the greenhouse and a smoke conveying pipe fixed on the top part of the greenhouse, the lower surface of the smoke conveying pipe is fixed with several radiating coil pipes at equal intervals, the upper part of the smoke conveying pipe is provided with a smoke discharging device connected with the radiating coil; the upper part of the smoke discharging device is fixed with an electromagnetic valve, the upper part of the electromagnetic valve is fixed with a smoke discharging pipe passing through the ceiling of the greenhouse. The beneficial effects are as follows: the heat energy carried by the smoke can be quickly radiated in the greenhouse through multiple radiating coil pipes; the heat can be fully used, the heat energy waste is reduced; at the same time, the radiating coil is suspended; it can avoid the scald plant and the person passing through the lower part; the carbon dioxide gas can be sent into the greenhouse through the rotatable smoke gas discharging pipe, it can increase the carbon dioxide concentration, at the same time, it can uniformly disperse the carbon dioxide, it avoids the local area carbon dioxide concentration is too high.

PN CN114262041-A
TI Method for supercritical water oxidation tail gas recovery comprises e.g. oxidizing the organic sewage in the reactor to generate water, carbon dioxide and salt, separating the gas-liquid mixture after depressurizing the gas-liquid mixture, and recovering the obtained pure water by filtration.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHU W
   JIANG Y
   JIANG P
   JIN J
AE HUNAN HANHUA JINGDIAN CLEAN ENERGY TECHN (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2022514213
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (a) transporting supercritical organic sewage and oxygen into the supercritical water oxidation reactor, and completely oxidizing the organic sewage in the reactor to generate water, carbon dioxide and salt, and discharging the salt, (b) flowing the high temperature and high pressure reaction vapor phase into the reaction vapor phase-organic sewage raw material heat exchanger to recover heat to obtain the high pressure reaction vapor phase, (c) obtaining a gas-liquid mixture after heat-exchanging the high-pressure reaction vapor phase with boiler water, separating the gas-liquid mixture after depressurizing the gas-liquid mixture, and recovering the obtained pure water by filtration, and (d) adsorbing the vapor phase after gas-liquid separation, recovering the carbon dioxide obtained after desorption, pressurizing other purified gas and enters the reaction system for reuse.
   USE - Method for supercritical water oxidation tail gas recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the operating cost by recovering oxygen and reaction heat; and recovers carbon dioxide gas, which greatly reduces carbon emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a supercritical water oxidation tail gas recovery system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method for supercritical water oxidation tail gas recovery (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Tower adsorption saturation (3)
   Boiler water inlet (33)
   Vapour phase outlet (41)

PN WO2022044039-A1; IN202011036464-A
TI Dual functional photocatalytic device for            photocatalytic conversion of carbon dioxide, and water,            comprises visible-light absorbing semiconductor chosen            from earth abundant transition metals, and metal oxides            alone arranged in pores of wide band gap semiconductor            and integrated with co-catalyst.
AU SALGAONKAR K N
   GOPINATH C S
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
   COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 2022398142
AB    NOVELTY - A dual functional photocatalytic device                comprises visible-light absorbing semiconductor                (VAS) arranged in the pores of wide band gap                semiconductor called photoanode and integrated with                a co-catalyst in thin film form. The visible-light                absorbing semiconductor (VAS) and co-catalyst are                chosen from earth abundant 3d or 4d transition                metals, metal oxides alone or their combinations,                and the wide gap semiconductor are chosen form                titanium dioxide and zinc oxide.
   USE - Dual functional photocatalytic device used for                photocatalytic co-conversion of carbon dioxide, and                water into value added products under direct                sunlight (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dual functional photocatalytic device                produces high yield value added chemicals such as                methanol and formaldehyde.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) fabrication of the photoanode device,                which involves assembling visible light absorbing                semiconductor from their ionic components and                depositing directly inside the pores of the wide                band gap semiconductor; and
   (2) process for the photocatalytic                co-conversion of a mixture of carbon dioxide and                water to value added products in batch mode, which                involves injecting carbon dioxide into water to                maximum saturation level and placing a                photocatalytic device in a quartz reactor at 1-60                degrees C under light source which is selected from                UV-Visible light, visible light and direct                sunlight.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The visible-light                  absorbing semiconductor (VAS) is selected from                  bismuth vanadate, cadmium sulfide, and lead                  sulfide, and is in the mesopores of the wide band                  gap semiconductor in the form of quantum dots                  (QDs). The co-catalyst is selected from nano                  palladium, platinum, gold, silver, nickel,                  cobalt, cuprous oxide, nickel-copper alloy,                  titanium, silicon or zinc, nickel oxide,                  manganese oxide, iron oxides, nickel-iron alloy                  loaded 0.5 theta pt doped nickel-iron alloy, 0.5                  theta platinum doped nickel copper alloy, and 0.5                  theta pt doped palladium, nickel-iron alloy or                  platinum coated nickel-copper alloy. Preferred                  Properties: The device shows stable activity for                  50 hours while maintaining product yield and                  selectivity. The conversion efficiency of carbon                  dioxide to value added products is 35-55%.                  Preferred Process: The method involves                  continuously flowing carbon dioxide through water                  with photocatalytic device in a quartz                  reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The value added                  product is selected from methanol and                  formaldehyde. Preferred Process: The method                  involves generating desired value-added products                  selected from methanol and formaldehyde.

AU MA J
   CHEN N

AE BAODING SIBEISI ELECTRICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BAOD-Non-standard)
GA 202231181X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a wind generating set cabin passive superfine dry powder automatic fire extinguishing device, comprising a fixing frame and four first bolts, four first bolt through respectively thread passes through the four corners of the top end of the fixing frame inner cavity, the inner part of the fixing frame is provided with a fire extinguishing system, the fire extinguishing system comprises a pressurizing mechanism, a fire extinguishing mechanism and a lifting mechanism. The utility model is provided with a PLC controller, the electromagnetic valve of the second conduit is controlled by the PLC controller in an open state, at the same time, the booster pump starts to work, the booster pump is pressurized to the inner part of the carbon dioxide storage tank through the first conduit, so that the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank is discharged from the second conduit to the dry powder storage barrel in time; at the same time, the carbon dioxide will be pressurized by the dry powder, the dry powder in the dry powder storage barrel is discharged to the spray head through the discharge pipe and the high temperature resistant telescopic hose, and the dry powder is sprayed out by the spray head to extinguish the fire, The whole fire extinguishing process is full automatic operation.

PT P
PN US2022011022-A1; WO2022010749-A1; DK202170359-A; US11421918-B2
TI Refrigeration system i.e. trans-critical carbon            dioxide refrigeration system, has rotary pressure            exchanger fluidly coupled to low pressure branch and            high pressure branch, where exchanger receives            refrigerant at high pressure from branch.
AU THATTE A M
AE ENERGY RECOVERY INC (ENER-Non-standard)
   ENERGY RECOVERY INC (ENER-Non-standard)
   ENERGY RECOVERY INC (ENER-Non-standard)
GA 202207375C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a rotary pressure exchanger                fluidly coupled to a low pressure branch and a high                pressure branch. The exchanger receives a                refrigerant e.g. carbon dioxide, at high and low                pressures from the branches, respectively, and                exchanges pressure between the refrigerants. An                exiting stream from the exchanger comprises the                refrigerant at high pressure in a supercritical                state or a subcritical state, and another exiting                stream comprises the low pressure refrigerant in a                liquid state or two-phase mixture of liquid and                vapor. A gas cooler or a condenser is disposed                along the branches.
   USE - Refrigeration system i.e. trans-critical                carbon dioxide refrigeration system.
   ADVANTAGE - The efficiency of refrigeration systems can be                increased, so that the cost of operating the                refrigeration equipment can be reduced and the                availability of the system can be improved, while                helping to reduce global warming. The pressure of                the refrigerant at high pressure is increased to a                sufficiently high temperature to enable rejection                of heat to the surrounding hotter environment by                increasing pressure ratio across the compressor                even higher, thus creating an even larger                electricity demand by the compressor and in turn                increasing the electricity costs per unit cooling                performed. The refrigerant temperature at the inlet                of the chiller needs to be increased to sufficient                high temperature in hotter climates.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a phase diagram of carbon                dioxide.
   Phase diagram (2)

PN CN113868819-A
TI Sewage processing network design based undetermined parameter and double-target method, involves minimizing total cost and carbon dioxide emissions of wastewater treatment system associated with hydraulic fracturing operation.
AU JIN H
   LING L
   QI W
   JIANG H
   DAI X
   SUN J
   WANG Q
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG CITY COLLEGE (UZCC-C)
GA 202211160W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves designing a sewage treatment network. A robust optimization model is established based on an undetermined parameter. Data set, a model parameter and a decision parameter are obtained. The robust optimization model is established. A risk constraint is calculated with the un-determined parameter. A waste water treatment network model is optimized based on a double-target model. A double target parameter is converted into a cumulative distribution function of standard normal distribution by multiple iterations. A sewage treatment system optimization control model is constructed. The total cost and carbon dioxide emissions of the wastewater treatment system associated with hydraulic fracturing operation are minimized.
   USE - Sewage processing network design based undetermined parameter and double-target method in an industrial sewage treatment field based on hydraulic fracturing operation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables expanding the single target mathematical model into a double-target mathematical model based on risk restriction so as to reduce carbon dioxide emission generated in the fracturing operation and transportation process, and reducing the uncertainty parameter problem in the device selection, and establishing different optimization model according to the undetermined parameter type, and minimizing the total cost and carbon dioxide emissions of the sewage treatment network associated with the hydraulic fracturing operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a sewage processing network design based undetermined parameter and double-target method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   MEI X

AE BAOTOU HEZHONG ALLOY CO LTD (BAOT-Non-standard)
GA 2022133590
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a fireproof and explosion-proof structure for crushing high magnesium alloy, comprising a crusher and a discharging channel set on the crusher, wherein the top part of the crusher is provided with a feeding port; the middle upper section of the discharging channel is provided with an air nozzle, the inert gas is supplied to the crusher through the air nozzle, not only carbon dioxide; The carbon dioxide is introduced into the crusher at the position of the discharge hole of the crusher, and the setting in this position is of the technical significance: in the crushing process, if there is no inward blowing air flow in the discharging port, a large amount of dust will not only overflow from the feeding port, but also overflow from the discharging passage, the dust will seriously pollute the working environment in the factory, and the carbon dioxide is blown to the crusher by the discharging passage, the dust is discharged from the feeding port (the high point) of the crusher; The invention is provided with carbon dioxide blowing to the crusher in the discharging channel, the light does not have the explosion-proof, anti-combustion effect, at the same time, it has the effect of introducing dust, improving the environment of the field.

   WEI G
   HAO J
AE UNIV XIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYXS-C)
GA 202025842D
AB    NOVELTY - Liquid carbon dioxide (CO2) phase change                hydraulic cutting device comprises a liquid CO2                phase changer comprising a phase change tank, a                heater and a constant pressure valve. The heater is                set in a first chamber to vaporize the liquid CO2                in a first chamber into high-pressure CO2 gas. The                high-pressure CO2 gas breaks through the constant                pressure valve and enters a second chamber provided                with a gaseous CO2 gas outlet. When the                high-pressure CO2 gas enters a third chamber                through the second chamber, a first piston performs                work and drives a second piston for driving the                water in a fourth chamber to form high-pressure                water. The abrasive tank is provided with an                abrasive. A discharge port of the abrasive tank is                connected to a nozzle, where the port is filled                with liquid CO2. When the high-pressure water in                the fourth chamber enters the abrasive tank, the                abrasive is mixed with the high-pressure water and                forms a high-speed abrasive jet through the                nozzle.
   USE - Used as liquid carbon dioxide phase change                hydraulic cutting device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: reduces the labor intensity of                manual demolition of the closed wall; is portable                and convenient for underground operations; and                ensures safe use in underground explosive                environments.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the liquid                CO2 phase change hydraulic cutting device.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables removing oxygen with increased fuel consumption.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves utilizing composite and dense electrochemical membrane, where the electrochemical membrane consists of first allow melting points of first phase below 600 degrees C, eutectic mixtures, and single or mixture of two or more alkaline earth carbonates selected from calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, compound (La Ni(0.6) Fe(0.4)O3), or a metal selected from aluminum, titanium, nickel, copper, zinc, tin, ion and silicon or an alloy mixture, or a carbon based nanomaterial, both sides surface of second phase is decorated by particles of metal selected from copper, platinum, palladium, rhodium, titanium, ruthenium, iridium, ion, zinc, tin, gold, calcium, lanthanum, magnesium, manganese, cobalt , chromium, vanadium, gadolinium, gallium, germanium, cerium, zirconium, silver, molybdenum, niobium, aluminum, indium, antimony, barium, strontium, caesium, ruthenium, sodium, potassium, lithium, yttrium and tungsten, or alloy mixtures, or oxide compounds, or carbide compounds, or nitrate compounds, the weight ratio between 0-2% and particle diameter from 1-100 mu m, and the thickness of the membrane varies between 0.5-10mm, exhibiting flat, or toothed, or corrugated, or tubular with circular or triangular or square or other polygonal cross-section profile, and second phase is solid and refractory at temperatures up to 1000 degrees C and exhibits 30-70% porosity, 10-3 -107 S/cm electronic conductivity and oxygen ionic conductivity below 10-3 S/cm.

The gas supplied to the absorption space is poor gas with reduced oxygen concentration, or rich gas with increased oxygen concentration and increased carbon dioxide concentration. The source gas is air. Preferred Properties: The permeation rate of oxygen and/or carbon dioxide of the oxygen separation membrane or carbon dioxide separation membrane are 10-10000 GPU. The selectivity of oxygen to nitrogen or carbon dioxide to nitrogen is more than 1 and 1000 or less.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprising                (1) grinding the natural biological material in the                ball mill to powder, then sieving; (2) mixing the                polyvinyl chloride and tetrahydrofuran in the                reactor, then mechanically stirring; (3) adding the                natural biological material, covalent organic                framework material and activated carbon into the                mixture of polyvinyl chloride and tetrahydrofuran;                mechanically stirring at room temperature; mixing                the mixture uniformly; (4) coating the mixture                obtained in the step (3) by dip coating method,                rotary coating method or grinding method on the dry                glass plate to prepare the nano-functional                composite material; (5) drying the nano composite                material; soaking the dried nano composite material                into the distilled water for solid processing; and                (6) soaking the solid nano-composite material into                the acid radical alkaline salt ion solution for                fully soaking; repeatedly washing the                nano-composite material for many times, so that the                nano-functional composite material is completely                loaded and saturated; then washing the nano                composite material with de-ionized water, drying by                drying box, drying and placing to obtain the                product.

The covalent organic                  framework material is all anion exchange covalent                  organic framework. The concentration of the acid                  radical alkaline salt ion solution is: 0.1-1.5 M;                  the soaking time is 1-12 hours; the washing time                  is 4-5 times; the drying temperature is:                  20-50degrees Celsius; the drying time is: 1-2                  hours; the acid radical alkaline salt ion                  solution is sodium carbonate solution, sodium                  phosphate solution, sodium sulfite solution,                  sodium sulfate solution, potassium carbonate                  solution, potassium phosphate solution, potassium                  sulfite solution and potassium sulfate solution.                  Preferred Method: In the step (4), the thickness                  of the prepared nano-composite material is                  10-1000 microns. PN CN113592194-A
TI Method for establishing prediction model of carbon            dioxide (CO2) throughput effect, involves stopping            training and neural network model after training as CO2            throughput effect prediction model if loss function            value of neural network model in preset error            range.
AU KONG D
   SONG H
   LIU Y
   ZHU W
   SONG T
   YUE M
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 2021C7178Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining influence factor                parameter data set and target factor data set of                carbon dioxide huff and spitting development as                sample data set, where the influence factor                parameters includes well-stewing time, crack                distance, water saturation, stratum pressure,                porosity, permeability, crack length, injection                amount and single layer thickness. The target                factor parameter includes oil change rate. The                sample data group is divided into a training group                and a test group. A neural network model is                constructed. The weight of neurons in the neural                network models is set. An activation function and                an optimizer are set. The training group is                inputted into the network model to train. The loss                function value of the neural model is greater than                the preset error range. The training and the neural                network model is stopped after training as the                carbon dioxide (CO2) throughput effect prediction                model if the loss function value of the neural                network model in the preset error range.
   USE - Method for establishing a prediction model of                carbon dioxide throughput effect in an oil field                development technology field for a country oil                exploration and development with low permeability,                bad oil storage property, low single well capacity,                generally using carbon dioxide drive and other gas                drive mode to exploit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be applied to the fast                prediction of the oil change rate of the well area                under the different influence factor parameters to                be CO2 huff and puff development, and controls the                loss function value of the model in the error range                by modifying the hyper-parameter, so as to obtain                the trained model.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) carbon dioxide (CO2) throughput effect                evaluation method; and
   (2) carbon dioxide (CO2) throughput effect                evaluation device.
