PN RU2714306-C1
TI Method of producing biodiesel fuel and installation for its implementation has a vegetable oil with supercritical alcohol in volume ratio.
AU TERTYCHNAYA T N
   SHEVTSOV S A
   TKACH V V
   SERDYUKOVA N A
AE UNIV VORON STATE AGRARIAN (UYVO-Non-standard)
GA 202020015N
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to a method of producing biodiesel fuel and can be used in oil and fat, fuel and other industries. Method of producing biodiesel fuel involves re-esterification of vegetable oil with supercritical alcohol in volume ratio of 1:101:15 at temperature of 250280C, pressure 15 MPa, condensation of excess alcohol vapor at temperature of 6080C, extraction of obtained reaction mixture with carbon dioxide in supercritical conditions at temperature 240260C, pressure 15 MPa, cooling the obtained biodisel mixture to temperature of 2030C, separation of glycerine from the obtained biodisel mixture in the centrifugal force field, separation of carbon dioxide vapors from the biodisil mixture by gas-liquid separation, compression of carbon dioxide vapor to 15 MPa and condensation at temperature minus 40C, heating of liquefied carbon dioxide to supercritical temperature with return to extraction in closed cycle mode. Plant for producing biodiesel fuel comprises a re-esterification reactor with a coil and a blade mixer; supercritical fluid CO2-extractor; dish-shaped separator for separation of reaction products into biodiesel mixture and glycerine, gas-liquid separator for separation of biodiesel fuel from carbon dioxide and additionally equipped with settler for separation of water from biodiesel fuel; as well as two-stage compressor, evaporator of refrigerating unit, reservoir for liquefied carbon dioxide, a high pressure pump and a recuperative heat exchanger for heating liquefied dioxide, arranged in series in a carbon dioxide return line; plant is equipped with steam ejector heat pump for preparation of heat carriers of different temperature potential.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention allows to reduce specific heat and power consumption during processing of vegetable oil into pure biodiesel fuel and to increase environmental safety of the whole production cycle.
2 cl, 1 dwg, 1 ex

AB    NOVELTY - The electrolysis cell (1A) has anode unit (10)                that includes an anode to oxidize water or                hydroxide ions and thus produce oxygen and an anode                solution flow path to supply an anode solution to                the anode. A cathode unit (20) includes a cathode                to reduce carbon dioxide and thus produce a carbon                compound. A cathode solution flow path (21) supply                a cathode solution to the cathode, and a gas flow                path supply carbon dioxide to the cathode. A                separator (30) separates the anode unit and the                cathode unit, in which the anode has a first                surface in contact with the separator, and a second                surface faces the anode solution flow path so that                the anode solution is in contact with the                anode.

Provides the lands along the anode solution                flow path and the carbon dioxide gas flow path                which makes it possible to increase a contact area                between the anode and a first flow path plate                constituting the anode solution flow path and a                contact area between the cathode and a third flow                path plate constituting the carbon dioxide gas flow                path.

PN CN107915863-A
TI Energy-saving and environmentally-friendly twin-screw extrusion method for producing reclaimed rubber, involves crushing waste rubber, grinding, conveying into twin-screw extruder, injecting supercritical carbon dioxide and extruding.
AU CHEN W
   SUN M
   XU Z
AE MAANSHAN ZHONGCHUANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTE (MAAN-Non-standard)
GA 201831475U
AB    NOVELTY - The twin-screw extrusion method for producing reclaimed rubber, involves (i) crushing waste rubber and grinding to obtain rubber powder, (ii) conveying rubber powder to twin-screw extruder, (iii) injecting supercritical carbon dioxide in extrusion section of twin-screw extruder where supercritical carbon dioxide has critical temperature of 31.26 degrees C, critical pressure of 7.4 MPa, density close to that of liquid and viscosity close to that of gas, and performing twin-screw mechanical shearing, achieving rapid desulfurization of waste rubber, and extruding.
   USE - Energy-saving and environmentally-friendly twin-screw extrusion method for producing reclaimed rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces reclaimed rubber with excellent appearance and less odor, removes decomposition products and achieves the functions of purification and exhaust, effectively inhibits the problem of exhaust gas and pollution, and achieves excellent regeneration effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The twin-screw extrusion method for producing reclaimed rubber, involves (i) crushing waste rubber and grinding by a rubber front crusher to obtain rubber powder, (ii) conveying rubber powder to a twin-screw extruder, (iii) injecting supercritical carbon dioxide in the extrusion section of twin-screw extruder where the supercritical carbon dioxide has critical temperature of 31.26 degrees C, critical pressure of 7.4 MPa, carbon dioxide changes in nature under supercritical conditions and has density close to that of liquid and viscosity close to that of gas and diffusion coefficient of 100 times that of a liquid so that supercritical carbon dioxide has amazing ability to dissolve and swells vulcanized rubber, and performing twin-screw mechanical shearing to selectively break C-sulfur bond and sulfur-sulfur bond, achieving rapid desulfurization of waste rubber, and extruding to obtain reclaimed rubber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The particle size of rubber powder in step (i) is 20-30 mesh. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the 20-30 mesh powder is delivered to the twin-screw extruder delivery section through a doser. The twin-screw extruder has a long screw design with an aspect ratio of 12-14.

The operation of the burner and the fan                are controlled by the fan. The carbon dioxide                concentration obtaining device is used for                conveying air to the burner. The fan is provided                with an air inlet and an air outlet. Operation of                the fan is controlled according to the                concentration parameter value. Determination is                made to check wether carbon dioxide is greater than                a first set parameter value.

The method allows the gas valve                to regulate the proportion of the fuel gas and air                entering a combustor by controlling the opening                degree of a fuel gas proportional valve, thus                improving the combustion efficiency of the                burner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also aloes included                for:

   n = 17-24.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of                  carbonyl group of                  1,3-diallyl-1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-trione and                  1,3,5,7-tetramethylcyclotetrasiloxane active                  hydrogen is 1-2:1, preferably 1.25:1. The                  catalyst activation temperature is 80-90 degrees                  Celsius; preferably the silicon-hydrogen reaction                  temperature is the same as the catalyst                  activation temperature. The dosage of the                  catalyst chloroplatinic acid is 25-60 parts per                  million, preferably 35-50 parts per million. The                  weight average molecular weight of the modified                  siloxane polymer is 8,000-11,000.Preferred Method: The silicon-hydrogen                  reaction time is 2-20 hours, preferably 4-8                  hours. When in use, the modified siloxane polymer                  is formulated into solution having concentration                  of 1-2 wt.%; no cosolvent is needed. The prepared                  modified siloxane polymer can be directly used as                  supercritical carbon dioxide thickener without                  post-purification treatment.

AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises entering flue gas into                the multi-level circulating absorption tower,                setting a pre-washing device at the front part,                removing impurity and cooling, entering the 1-n-1                level carbon dioxide absorption system (CO2),                n-level water washing system, where n is not less                than 3, entering CO2 desorption tower through the                barren rich liquid heat exchanger, discharging                barren liquid into the barren CO2 desorbed tower                and entering barren rich liquid heat exchange,                entering carbon dioxide concentration device,                regenerating carbon dioxide regeneration gas into                CO2 concentration device and entering the                gas-liquid separator through the cooler the                intelligent control system respectively with the                1-n-1 level CO2 absorption system. The n-stage                water washing system is serially connected in                series in the multi-stage circulating absorption                tower from bottom to top.

AU YAO P
   XU P
AE APEX GRAPHENE TECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN CO LTD (APEX-Non-standard)
GA 202130091D
AB    (CN212712759-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for industrial mass production of porous graphene powder with high purity; The device comprises: a storage groove for setting a liquid polyimide; a preparation cylinder; an ultrasonic atomizer connected with the upper part of the preparation cylinder, and connected with the storage tank, for atomizing the liquid polyimide into a plurality of fog particles; a plurality of carbon dioxide laser irradiator for carbon dioxide laser irradiation for the plurality of mist particles, so it can provide a new high-purity porous graphene powder mass production device, and reaches excellent production economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for industrial mass production of porous graphene powder.
EA    NOVELTY - Preparing industrial mass production porous graphene powder comprises (i) taking the liquid polyimide in an ultrasonic atomizer to perform ultrasonic oscillation, so as to atomize the atomized particles into fog particles; (ii) making the misty particles of the polyimide enter into a preparation cylinder, and performing carbon dioxide laser irradiation in the preparation cylinder; and (iii) irradiating fog particle of polyimide by carbon dioxide laser, forming high purity porous graphene powder, and falling on bottom of the preparation cylinder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing industrial mass production porous graphene powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a new high-purity porous graphene powder mass production method, and reaches excellent production economic benefit.

PN CN111378101-A; CN111378101-B
TI Preparing biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate ternary copolymer involves adding monomer phthalic anhydride, propylene oxide, catalyst tetra-n-butylammonium chloride and triethylboron into high-pressure reaction kettle.
AU MENG Y
   LIANG J
   XIAO M
   WANG S
   YE S
   HAN D
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2020650300
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate ternary copolymer involves adding monomer phthalic anhydride, propylene oxide, catalyst tetra-n-butylammonium chloride and triethylboron into high-pressure reaction kettle, filling third monomer carbon dioxide, and heating it to perform ternary opening cyclic copolymerization to obtain reacted material. The reacted material us subjected to precipitation, drying and granulation to obtain final purified product. The sequence structure of ternary copolymer includes random and block, and is represented by (I).
   USE - Method for preparing a biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate ternary copolymer for synthesizing modified propylene carbonate biodegradable material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate ternary copolymer, which has different sequence structures including random, block and other structures to improve glass transition temperature and thermal stability of propylene carbonate, uses phthalic anhydride modified propylene carbonate that has extremely high light transmittance, high melt viscosity and good foaming performance, and expands application range of propylene carbonate material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate ternary copolymer involves adding monomer phthalic anhydride, propylene oxide, catalyst tetra-n-butylammonium chloride and triethylboron into high-pressure reaction kettle, filling third monomer carbon dioxide, and heating it to perform ternary opening cyclic copolymerization to obtain reacted material. The reacted material us subjected to precipitation, drying and granulation to obtain final purified product. The sequence structure of ternary copolymer includes random and block, and is represented by formula (I), where a greater than or equal to 1, b greater than or equal to 1, c greater than or equal to 0, and a, b, and c are all integers..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The content of polyester in polymer is 10-40%, and content of polyether in polymer is less than 10%. The molar ratio of tetra-n-butylammonium chloride and triethylboron is 1:2-8. The molar ratio of tetra-n-butylammonium chloride and phthalic anhydride is 1:50-2000. The molar ratio of propylene oxide and phthalic anhydride is 1:2-30. The carbon dioxide pressure of ring-opening copolymerization reaction is 0.1-3MPa, and reaction temperature is 40-100 degrees C for 4-15 hours.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)

TI Plant useful for synthesizing ammonia and reducing volatile hydrocarbons, comprises a reformer, a carbon monoxide converter, a carbon dioxide scrubber unit with regeneration, a methanation unit and an ammonia synthesis unit.
AU MIELKE B
   NOLKER K
   NOELKER K
AE THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
GA 202003731X
AB    NOVELTY - Plant comprises a reformer (1), a carbon monoxide converter (2), a carbon dioxide scrubber unit with regeneration (3), a methanation unit (4) and an ammonia synthesis unit (5). The carbon dioxide scrubber unit is connected with regeneration having at least one fired auxiliary steam boiler.
   USE - The plant and the method are useful for synthesizing ammonia and reducing volatile hydrocarbons (VOC and HAP) (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids VOC and HAP emissions from synthesis gas processing plants, and synthesizes ammonia, which has reduced emission of volatile, environmentally and health-damaging hydrocarbons (VOCs and HAPs).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for synthesizing ammonia, comprising (a) introducing an alkane-containing gas into a reformer and receiving a first synthesis gas mixture, (b) introducing the first synthesis gas mixture into a carbon monoxide converter and obtaining a second synthesis gas mixture, (c) introducing the second synthesis gas mixture into a carbon dioxide scrubber unit with regeneration and receiving a third synthesis gas mixture and a carbon dioxide containing exhaust gas, (d) introducing the third synthesis gas mixture into a methanation unit and obtaining a fourth synthesis gas mixture and (e) introducing the fourth synthesis gas mixture into an ammonia synthesis unit and receiving ammonia, where the carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas from the step (c) is introduced in completely or partially into a fired auxiliary steam boiler and obtaining a low or free exhaust gas from volatile hydrocarbons (volatile organic compound (VOC) and hazardous air pollutant (HAP)).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the flow diagram of a plant.
   Reformer (1)
   Carbon monoxide (2)
   Carbon dioxide scrubber unit (3)
   Methanation unit (4)
   Ammonia synthesis unit (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The fired auxiliary steam boiler is connected to an exhaust air device. The reformer comprises a steam reformer with or without a secondary reformer and/or an autothermal reformer. An air separation plant is included and/or the nitrogen is provided from the process air that is charged (burned) in a secondary reformer. The carbon monoxide converter, the carbon dioxide scrubber unit with regeneration, the methanation unit and ammonia synthesis unit are connected in the process direction and in the row of reformers. The carbon dioxide scrubber unit with regeneration has an additional connection to at least one fired auxiliary steam boiler. The fired auxiliary steam boiler has supply lines for air and supply lines for fuel. The air supply lines are connected to the carbon dioxide scrubber unit with regeneration. The fired auxiliary steam boiler has a capacity of 10-200 tons of steam per hour. The reformer comprises a steam reformer with or without a secondary reformer and/or an autothermal reformer. Preferred Method: The ammonia and a large part of the carbon dioxide -containing exhaust gas or volatile hydrocarbons (VOC and HAP) poor or free exhaust gas are converted to urea in a urea plant. The fired auxiliary steam boiler is operated at 170-550 degrees C under 5-150 bar on the side of the steam generation.

AU DANG Z
   XI G
   ZHONG L
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2019572048
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a solid oxide fuel cell sub-system and an ultra-critical carbon dioxide circulating sub-system that are connected with each other. An integrated fuel cell and ultra-critical carbon dioxide cycle distributed energy supply system unit utilizes tail gas of the solid oxide fuel cell subsystem as a stable high-temperature heat source and a low-temperature heat source of the ultra-critical carbon dioxide circulating sub-system. The solid oxide fuel cell sub-system is provided with an air compressor (1), a fuel compressor (15), a mixer (14), a pre-reformer (13), a solid oxide fuel cell (10), an inverter (11), a combustion chamber (9), an air high-temperature regenerator (12) and an air low-temperature regenerator (3).

   ADVANTAGE - The system has compact structure, and occupies small floor area, and realizes energy utilization function, improves energy utilization rate of the system for about 70.93%, and reaches comprehensive energy utilization rate of the system for about 89.76%.

PN WO2018226856-A2; US2019016905-A1; WO2018226856-A3; US10793728-B2
TI Enhancing integration of binder material in hydrophobic coating by placing diatomaceous earth into vessel, introducing carbon dioxide to partially infiltrate interstitial voids of diatomaceous earth, adding binder material, and mixing.
AU BOHNERT G
   MESSMAN J
AE HONEYWELL FEDERAL MFG & TECHNOLOGIES LLC (HONE-C)
   HONEYWELL FEDERAL MFG & TECHNOLOGIES LLC (HONE-C)
GA 201899597L
AB    NOVELTY - Integration of a binder material in a hydrophobic coating is enhanced by placing (112) diatomaceous earth (10) into a vessel (12); introducing (114) carbon dioxide (14) into the vessel to partially infiltrate interstitial voids of the diatomaceous earth; adding (116) binder material (16) to the vessel; and mixing the diatomaceous earth, the carbon dioxide, and the binder material to form the hydrophobic coating.
   USE - The method is useful for enhancing integration of a binder material in a hydrophobic coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The coating formulations are more robust and may strongly adhere to substrates to which they are applied. The void-filling fluid in the voids of the superhydrophobic material can repel the binder to preserve a volume of void-filling fluid within the voids of the superhydrophobic material. Consequently, the resulting coating formulation can comprise more binder relative to present hydrophobic coatings.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) superhydrophobic coating comprising superhydrophobic diatomaceous earth, carbon dioxide, and greater than or equal to 1 binder; and
   (2) enhancing integration of binder material in superhydrophobic coating which involves placing superhydrophobic material into a vessel, introducing void-filling fluid into vessel to partially infiltrate interstitial voids of superhydrophobic material to form a saturated superhydrophobic material, adding binder material to the vessel, and mixing saturated superhydrophobic material and binder material to form superhydrophobic coating.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the process for making a superhydrophobic coating, in which liquid, gaseous, or supercritical carbon dioxide is used to enhance the integration of a binder material in the superhydrophobic coating.
   Diatomaceous earth (10)
   Vessel (12)
   Carbon dioxide (14)
   Binder material (16)
   Process for enhancing integration of binder material in superhydrophobic coating (110)
   Placing diatomaceous earth into a vessel (112)
   Introducing carbon dioxide in vessel (114)
   Adding binder material to vessel (116)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Carbon dioxide comprises supercritical carbon dioxide. Diatomaceous earth comprises superhydrophobic diatomaceous earth. Void-filling fluid comprises supercritical fluid. Void-filling fluid comprises carbon dioxide or hydrocarbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Hydrophobic coating comprises 5-20 wt.% binder material based on total weight of hydrophobic coating. Superhydrophobic coating comprises 5-20 wt.%, preferably 10-20 wt.% based on the total weight of the superhydrophobic coating. Superhydrophobic coating comprises greater than or equal to 60 wt.% superhydrophobic diatomaceous earth based on total weight of superhydrophobic coating. Preferred Processes: The method further includes applying hydrophobic coating onto a substrate to form a coated substrate. Applying comprises spraying hydrophobic coating onto the substrate, where residual carbon dioxide in the vessel is used as a propellant for the spraying.

TI Method for producing enriched methane gas, involves operating gas separation system under conditions in-which carbon dioxide permeability of first gas separation membrane unit is higher than dioxide permeability of second membrane unit.
AU FUKUDA N
   NAKAMURA T
AE UBE IND LTD (UBEI-C)
GA 201894763D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves supplying a feed gas mixture to a first gas separation membrane unit (11) through a feed gas mixture supply line (16), and taking out enriched methane gas from a retentate gas discharge port (13b) of a second gas separation membrane unit (12). A gas separation system (10) is operated under conditions in-which carbon dioxide permeability of the second gas separation membrane unit is higher than carbon dioxide permeability of a third gas separation membrane unit (13) and the carbon dioxide/methane selectivity of the third gas separation membrane unit is higher than the carbon dioxide/methane selectivity of the second gas separation membrane unit, where a ratio of the carbon dioxide permeability in the second gas separation membrane unit to the carbon dioxide permeability in the first gas separation membrane unit in operation is 1.2 or greater.
   USE - Method for producing enriched methane gas using a multi-stage gas separation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing a total membrane area in the system or a number of membrane modules while maintaining predetermined gas purity and gas recovery rate, so that initial system cost can be reduced and space saving can be achieved, while maintaining a high target-gas recovery rate. The method enables providing gas separation membrane modules so as to easily adjust the membrane area by changing the number of gas separation membrane modules.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a gas separation system.
   Gas separation system (10)
   Gas separation membrane units (11, 12, 13)
   Retentate gas discharge port (13b)
   Retentate gas discharge line (14)
   Feed gas mixture supply line (16)

PN WO2018075933-A1; CN109862962-A; IN201917015597-A; EP3528941-A1; US2019262762-A1; JP2019535494-W; MX2019004625-A1
TI Sorbent structure for capturing carbon dioxide            from e.g. power plants, comprises continuous component            comprising sorbent material and carbon, and has            temperature controlled by conduction of electrical            current through component.
AU GADKAREE K P
   PATIL M D
   PIEROTTI K D
AE CORNING INC (CORG-C)
   CORNING INC (CORG-C)
   CORNING INC (CORG-C)
   CORNING INC (CORG-C)
GA 2018321453
AB    NOVELTY - A sorbent structure (10a) comprises a                continuous component (14a) in the form of a                flow-through substrate (14) comprising at least one                cell defined by at least one porous wall. The                continuous component comprises a sorbent material                and 5-40 wt.% carbon. The carbon is dispersed                within the component. The temperature of the                sorbent structure can be controlled by conduction                of an electrical current through the                component.
   USE - Sorbent structure used for capturing carbon                dioxide (claimed). Uses include but are not limited                to power plants, oil refinement industry, steel and                metal alloy fabrication industry, and cement                processing industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The sorbent structure has excellent                electrically resistive heating capacity. The                sorbent structure is manufactured by an economical                method when compared with the existing sorbent                structure having carbon configured in the activated                form. The structure efficiently captures carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of sorbent structure, which                involves forming a mixture of the carbon precursor                and the sorbent material into the shape of the                flow-through substrate, drying the mixture, and                carbonizing the carbon precursor in the mixture to                define the continuous component in the form of the                flow-through substrate; and
   (2) method of using the sorbent structure for                capturing carbon dioxide, which involves providing                the sorbent structure, and contacting the sorbent                structure with a gas comprising carbon dioxide. The                contacting is conducted to capture a portion of                carbon dioxide within the sorbent structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective, schematic                view of carbon dioxide removal and regeneration                system with the sorbent structure.
   Sorbent structure (10a)
   Flow-through substrate (14)
   Continuous component (14a)
   Leads (40)
   Electrical power supply (48)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The component                  preferably comprises 10-25 wt.% carbon. Preferred                  Components: The carbon is an activated carbon                  and/or a non-activated carbon, preferably a                  non-activated carbon. The sorbent material is                  continuous within the component. The sorbent                  material comprises a zeolite and/or a                  metal-organic framework. The carbon is derived                  from a synthetic carbon precursor and/or an                  organic carbon precursor. The continuous                  component further comprises a carbon black. The                  continuous component is derived from a green                  component that comprises the carbon material, the                  sorbent material, and at least one of a phenolic                  resin, a methylcellulose, a plasticizer, or an                  inorganic filler. The sorbent structure comprises                  the sorbent material as a coating on the                  continuous component. Preferred Properties: The                  component has an electrical resistance of 5-15 (                  Omega ). The sorbent structure has a specific                  surface area of 400-600 m2/g, preferably 100-200                  m2/g as measured by a BET adsorption method.                  Preferred Process: The method further involves                  activating the carbon in the continuous                  component, after the carbonizing step, applying                  the sorbent material as a coating onto the                  substrate, and firing the coating, and applying a                  current to the sorbent structure to heat the                  sorbent structure such that the portion of the                  carbon dioxide within the sorbent structure is                  removed from the sorbent structure by desorption.                  The forming, drying and carbonizing steps are                  conducted such that the sorbent material is                  continuous within the component. The forming step                  involves extruding the mixture into the green                  component of the shape of the flow-through                  substrate. The applying step involves                  wash-coating the sorbent coating into the                  substrate at a loading of 100-300 g/L. The                  applying step is conducted to heat the sorbent                  structure to a temperature of 50 degrees C or                  above. Preferred Condition: The gas is at a                  temperature of up to 80 degrees C.

GA 2021607055

PN CN111995509-A
TI Preparation of gingerol by pretreating ginger,            ultra-microcrushing, adding complex enzyme for            enzymolysis, extracting by pulse electric field after            inactivating, extracting by supercritical carbon            dioxide, and separating to obtain gingerol.
AU LI X
   MENG J
   LI D
   QIN H
AE QIANJIN PHARM CO LTD ZHUZHOU (QJPM-C)
GA 2020C1313Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of gingerol comprises pretreating                ginger, ultra-microcrushing, adding complex enzyme                for enzymolysis, extracting by pulse electric field                after inactivating, extracting by supercritical                carbon dioxide, and separating to obtain                gingerol.
   USE - The method is for preparation of                gingerol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method keeps the high activity and                stability of gingerol and can greatly improve the                yield and content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically                  comprises selecting ginger, peeling, slicing,                  drying and ultra-microcrushing to obtain ginger                  ultra-micropowder; complex enzyme enzymolysis by                  adding the ginger ultra-micropowder into buffer                  solution, adjusting the pH at 4-6, adding                  cellulase, pectinase and glucoamylase at                  1:1-2:1-2 mass ratio, performing enzymolysis,                  inactivating, and centrifuging to obtain ginger                  residue and enzymolysis liquid; pulse electric                  field extracting by adding the ginger residue                  into ethanol solution, pulse electric field                  oscillation extracting, centrifuging, and                  separating to obtain gingerol crude extract; and                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction by                  putting the gingerol crude extract and                  enzymolysis liquid in a supercritical carbon                  dioxide extraction kettle, setting the pressure                  at 20-50 MPa and temperature at 35-60 degrees C                  to extract, collecting the extract, centrifuging,                  and separating to obtain gingerol. The particle                  diameter D50 of the ginger ultra-micropowder is                  less than 50 mu m. The enzymolysis is at 35-50                  degrees C for 1-2 hours. The weight volume ratio                  of ginger ultra-micropowder to the buffer                  solution is 1:1-5 g/L. The mass volume ratio of                  the complex enzyme to the buffer solution is                  60-100 mg/L. The pulse electric field has field                  intensity of 1-10 kv/cm, pulse time of 10-50                  times, pulse width of 100-1000 mu s and pulse                  frequency of 0.1-10 Hz. The mass volume ratio of                  ginger residue to ethanol solution is 1:15-30                  g/ml. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 20-50                  L/hour.

   QIAO Y
   WU J
AE SHANGHAI LVSHENG IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201847462A

   USE - The method is useful for color protection and/or retarding yellowing of broccoli (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces degradation of chlorophyll in broccoli; and extends freshness of broccoli to 14-28 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The volume ratio of carbon dioxide in the sealing system is 5-10%, and temperature of the sealing system is -2 degrees C to 0 degrees C. The method comprises (i) pre-processing broccoli at -2 degrees C to 4 degrees C, (preferably -2 degrees C to 0 degrees C) for 20-30 hours (preferably 24 hours) , where the humidity is 90-98%, (preferably 90-95%), and (ii) placing the broccoli in the sealing system, and filling with 2-20% carbon dioxide, 2-20% oxygen and 70-90% protective gas. The broccoli is packaged in a polyethylene wrap film. Preferred Components: The sealing system further comprises oxygen and/or protective gas. The protective gas comprises nitrogen, helium gas, neon gas and/or argon gas (preferably nitrogen). The volume ratio of the protective gas in the sealing system is 70-90%, (preferably 80-90%).

PT P
PN EP3330380-A1; WO2018104143-A1; CN110036115-A; KR2019092494-A; IN201917026487-A; SG11201904898-A1; EP3548628-A1; US2019338324-A1; VN66366-A; BR112019011512-A2; ID201907647-A; US10920255-B2; EP3548628-B1
TI Producing L-amino acid involves reacting mixture            comprising aldehyde, carbon dioxide, decarboxylase, its            corresponding cofactor and one donor amino acid and            aminotransferase and/or nicotinamide adenine            dinucleotide.
AU SKERRA A
   EISOLDT L
   SQUEIRA A
AE EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
   UNIV TECH MUENCHEN (MUEN-C)
   EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 201844221W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing an L-amino acid involves reacting a                mixture comprising an aldehyde, carbon dioxide, a                decarboxylase, its corresponding cofactor and one                donor amino acid and an aminotransferase and/or                nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NADH), ammonia                and/or an ammonium salt and an amino acid                dehydrogenase to form the L-amino acid or a                salt.
   USE - Method for producing an L-amino acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The L-amino acid to                  be formed is selected from the group consisting                  of L-alanine, L-valine, L-methionine, L-leucine,                  L-isoleucine and L-phenylalanine. The L-amino                  acid to be formed is L-methionine and the                  aldehyde is 3-(methylthio)-propanal (methional).                  The cofactor comprises thiamine pyrophosphate.                  The decarboxylase is selected from the group                  consisting of pyruvate decarboxylase PDC1, which                  originates from Saccharomyces cerevisiae,                  phenylpyruvate decarboxylase ARO10, which                  originates from Saccharomyces cerevisiae, and                  branched chain decarboxylase KdcA, which                  originates from Lactococcus lactis, as well as                  mutants and variants of these decarboxylase                  having decarboxylase activity. The                  aminotransferase is selected from the group                  consisting of methionine aminotransferase YbdL,                  which originates from Escherichia coli, and                  aromatic aminotransferase Aro8, which originates                  from Saccharomyces cerevisiae, as well as mutants                  and variants of these aminotransferases having                  aminotransferase activity. The amino acid                  dehydrogenase is selected from the group                  consisting of leucine dehydrogenase LeuDH, which                  originates from Bacillus sphaericus, and                  phenylalanine dehydrogenase PheDH, which                  originates from Thermoactinomyces intermedius, as                  well as well as mutants and variants of these                  amino acid dehydrogenases having amino acid                  dehydrogenase activity. The donor amino acid is                  different from the L-amino acid to be formed and                  at least one L-amino acid is selected from the                  group consisting of L-glutamine, L-glutamate,                  L-alanine, L-phenylalanine, L-tyrosine,                  L-leucine, L-isoleucine, L-histidine and                  L-tryptophan. The carbon dioxide is applied to                  the mixture at a pressure from 10-7400 kilo                  Pascal. The mixture further comprises formic acid                  or a salt and a formate dehydrogenase. The                  formate dehydrogenase is selected from the group                  consisting of formate dehydrogenase from                  Pseudomonas and formate dehydrogenase from                  Candida as well as mutants and variants of these                  formate dehydrogenases having formate                  dehydrogenase activity.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable examples given.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.)

PN CN111333029-A
TI Producing carbon nanotubes by reforming methane            and carbon dioxide comprises e.g. crushing iron ore and            reduced iron, sieving and placing the sieved iron ore            and reduced iron in rotary furnace reactor and            heating.
AU WANG Y
   FAN H
   ZHU P
   LIU J
   LV X
   ZHANG T
   LUO Y
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202061168M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbon nanotubes by reforming                methane and carbon dioxide comprises e.g. taking                iron ore and reduced iron, then crushing and                sieving and placing the sieved iron ore and reduced                iron in different chambers connected but separated                in a rotary furnace reactor under nitrogen                atmosphere, heating the cavity system of reduced                iron, injecting premixed gas of methane and carbon                dioxide into the rotary kiln reactor to starting                the rotary kiln reactor, rotating at a speed of                0-10 revolutions/minute, maintaining the premixed                gas, send methane carbon dioxide reforming reaction                and generating iron-based carbon nanotubes on the                reduced iron to obtain reformed carbon monoxide and                hydrogen gas, start the heating device at the                cavity where the iron ore is placed, heating the                cavity system where the iron ore and open the valve                on the partition, open the valve on the partition,                passing carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas into the                cavity, start the rotary furnace reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbon                nanotubes by reforming methane and carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing carbon nanotubes by reforming                methane and carbon dioxide comprises (i) taking                iron ore and reduced iron, then crushing and                sieving and placing the sieved iron ore and reduced                iron in different chambers connected but separated                in a rotary furnace reactor under nitrogen                atmosphere, (ii) heating the cavity system of                reduced iron placed in step (i) at 800-1000 degrees                C and maintaining the temperature for 1-10 hours                after reaching the final temperature, injecting                premixed gas of methane and carbon dioxide into the                rotary kiln reactor to starting the rotary kiln                reactor, rotating at a speed of 0-10                revolutions/minute, maintaining the premixed gas                for 1-10 hours, send methane carbon dioxide                reforming reaction and generating iron-based carbon                nanotubes on the reduced iron to obtain reformed                carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas, (iii) start the                heating device at the cavity where the iron ore is                placed, heating the cavity system where the iron                ore at 700-800 degrees C and open the valve on the                partition, open the valve on the partition, passing                carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas obtained in the                step (ii) into the cavity where the iron ore is                located, and keeping for 1-10 hours, start the                rotary furnace reactor, rotating at a speed of 0-10                revolutions/minute to complete the reduction of                iron ore and recovering reduced iron, and mixing                unreacted carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas with the                steam and carbon dioxide after the reaction as                exhaust gas, (iv) refluxing reduced iron obtained                in step (iii) into step (i) as a raw material to                participating in the methane carbon dioxide                reforming reaction, (v) separating and purifying                the tail gas obtained in step (iii), mixing carbon                monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the tail                with methane reflux, and repeatedly participates                the mixed gas in the reaction process of step (i),                (vi) pickling and purifying iron-based carbon                nanotubes obtained in step (v) to obtain pure                carbon nanotubes and carbon monoxide and hydrogen                gas, and (vii) pickling obtained all iron-based                carbon nanotubes, purifying and drying to obtain                pure carbon nanotubes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i): the                  components of iron ore are iron(III) oxide and                  iron(II,III) oxide, and the mass fraction of iron                  ore is 1000 pts. wt., the composition of reduced                  iron is iron element, the content of iron element                  is greater than 98 wt.%, the mass fraction of                  reduced iron is 0-100 pts. wt., and the particle                  size of crushed and sieved iron ore and reduced                  iron is 0.1-10 mm. Preferred Method: In step                  (ii): the heating rate is 2-10 degrees C/minute,                  the volume ratio between methane and carbon                  dioxide is 1:0.1-1, and the space velocity of the                  premixed gas is 1000-30000 h-1. In step (iii):                  the heating rate is 2-10 degrees C/minute, the                  total space velocity of carbon monoxide and                  hydrogen is 0-5000 h-1, and the volume ratio                  between carbon monoxide and hydrogen is 1:1. In                  step (iv): the amount of reduced iron is 10-50                  wt% of the total reduced iron content. The                  separation and purification of the tail gas in                  step (v) comprises removing water vapor using dry                  calcium chloride. In step (vi) and (vii): the                  pickling of the iron-based carbon nanotubes is                  carried out using 4 mol/l dilute nitric acid for                  purification.

PN CN108514888-A; CN108514888-B
TI Preparing polyacid intercalated hydrotalcite photocatalytic material, comprises e.g. preparing hydrotalcite by reacting metal nitrates and de-carbon dioxide aqueous solution and sodium hydroxide and de-carbon dioxide aqueous solution.
AU SONG H
   CAI Y
   HE J
   AN Z
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201874920T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyacid intercalated hydrotalcite photocatalytic material, comprises (i) dissolving weighed divalent metal M nitrate and trivalent metal N nitrate in de-carbon dioxide water to obtain a first solution, dissolving sodium hydroxide in de-carbon dioxide water to obtain a second solution, then adding two solutions to a four-necked bottle, stirring, reacting, precipitating, aging, washing, suction filtering, drying and grinding to obtain layered hydrotalcite (MxN-NO3-layered double hydroxides (LDHs)) and (ii) and adding weighed layered hydrotalcite prepared in the step (i) to a three-necked bottle containing formamide, stirring to form a sol, dissolving weighed phosphotungstic acid (PW) in de-carbon dioxide water, adjusting pH of phosphotungstic acid solution to neutral and then dropping into a three-necked bottle, stirring, aging, centrifugal washing, vacuum drying and grinding to obtain phosphotungstic acid intercalated hydrotalcite (ZnxAl-PW-LDHs).
   USE - The polyacid intercalated hydrotalcite photocatalytic material is useful as a photocatalyst for photocatalytic desulfurization (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly; has low cost; can be performed under mild reaction conditions; utilizes simple reaction equipment; and produces photocatalyst which can be reused, is unchanged and easy to recycle, and has a desulfurization rate of 95.3%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polyacid intercalated hydrotalcite photocatalytic material, comprises (i) dissolving weighed divalent metal M nitrate and trivalent metal N nitrate in de-carbon dioxide water to obtain a first solution, dissolving sodium hydroxide in de-carbon dioxide water to obtain a second solution, then simultaneously adding the two solutions to a four-necked bottle under nitrogen protection, stirring and reacting, precipitating, aging at 60-100 degrees C for many hours, washing with carbon dioxide water, suction filtering, drying and grinding to obtain layered hydrotalcite (MxN-NO3-layered double hydroxides (LDHs)) and (ii) weighing layered hydrotalcite prepared in the step (i) and then adding to a three-necked bottle containing formamide, stirring at room temperature for many hours under nitrogen protection to form a sol, dissolving weighed phosphotungstic acid (PW) in de-carbon dioxide water, adjusting pH of the phosphotungstic acid solution to neutral under nitrogen protection and then dropping into a three-necked bottle, stirring at room temperature for many hours to ensure the reaction is completed, aging at 60-100 degrees C for many hours, centrifugal washing with carbon dioxide water, vacuum drying, and grinding to obtain phosphotungstic acid intercalated hydrotalcite (ZnxAl-PW-LDHs). In the step (ii), the concentration of layered hydrotalcite in the sol is 0.5-3 g/l, the mass ratio of the phosphotungstic acid/layered hydrotalcite is not greater than 6:1, and the phosphotungstic acid solution is 1.2 g of phosphotungstic acid corresponds to 30-50 ml of de-carbon dioxide.
   x = not defined.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) photocatalytic fuel deep desulfurization system comprising extracting acetonitrile as an extractant by adding the phosphotungstic acid intercalated hydrotalcite as a photocatalytic oxidation catalyst and air as an oxidant to form sulfur product; and
   (2) photocatalytic desulfurization using the photocatalytic fuel deep desulfurization system, comprising adding the photocatalyst to a system containing desulfurization, introducing air, and adding acetonitrile as an extractant, and reacting for many hours under irradiation with an ultraviolet lamp.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of phosphotungstic acid/layered hydrotalcite is 2-4:1.The phosphotungstic acid solution corresponds to 40 ml of de-carbon dioxide water per 1.2 g of phosphotungstic acid. The valence metals M nitrates include zinc nitrate, magnesium nitrate. The trivalent metal N nitrates include aluminum nitrate. the volume ratio of acetonitrile: system to be desulfurized is 1:1-1:1.5. The system to be desulfurized is an oil system, and the sulfur substance contained in the system to be desulfurized is benzothiophene , dibenzothiophene or 4,6-dimethyldibenzothiophene.

AB    NOVELTY - Recycling and regenerating lithium carbonate                from lithium iron phosphate waste involves adding                an impurity removing agent to the waste                lithium-containing solution, and adjusting the pH                of the solution to 4-8 to generate a precipitate in                the mixed solution, filtering and concentrating the                filtrate to obtain an alkaline lithium solution,                introducing the alkaline lithium solution into a                carbonization tower, passing excessive carbon                dioxide, reacting to obtain lithium bicarbonate                mixed solution, discharging excessive carbon                dioxide gas from the top of the carbonization                tower, circulating into the carbonization tower                through the gas inlet pipe, filtering the lithium                bicarbonate mixed solution to obtain lithium                bicarbonate solution, heating and decomposing to                generate solid precipitate, filtering, washing and                drying the solid precipitate to obtain lithium                carbonate. The filtrate obtained after filtering                solid precipitate is repeatedly used in the                lithium-containing solution.
   USE - Recycling and regenerating lithium carbonate                from lithium iron phosphate waste obtained from                lithium iron phosphate power battery used in                electric vehicle and energy storage                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses industrial waste gas carbon                dioxide to recycle lithium carbonate in the waste                lithium-containing solution, ensures effective                recovery of lithium, and solves the problem of                greenhouse gas caused by carbon dioxide emission.                The recovered filtrate can be recovered again so,                improves the recycling efficiency of the lithium                metal element, and provides high purity high and                ensures recycling efficiency of the recycled and                regenerated lithium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The impurity removing                  agent is at least one of ammonia gas, sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and ammonium                  hydroxide.Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  lithium ions in the alkaline lithium solution is                  20-60 g/L. PN US2021363473-A1
TI Generating energy and storing energy for subsequent use, by providing carbon dioxide, electromethanogenesis of carbon dioxide into fuel gas, and storing fuel gas for subsequent use.
AU CHANDRASEKARAN S
   WORSLEY M A
   BAKER S E
   KNIPE J M
AE LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
GA 2021D8769D
AB    NOVELTY - Generating energy and storing the energy for subsequent use, involves providing carbon dioxide, electromethanogenesis of the carbon dioxide into a fuel gas, and storing the fuel gas for subsequent use.
   USE - The method is useful for generating energy and storing the energy for subsequent use (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus, systems, and methods provide a scalable, production module for microbial methanogenesis of methane gas from carbon dioxide (CO2). The use of graphene aerogels for microbial electromethanogenesis allows the current density to be maximized due to the ability to control material pore size (and thus biologically accessible surface area) over 4 orders of magnitude (1 nm to 10 mum) and conductivity from 1 to 100's of S/cm. The use of enzymes to mediate charge transfer can increase current density, since nanometer scale enzymes can access more electrode surface area than significantly larger micron scale whole microbes. Further, using enzymes rather than microbes for the critical charge transfer step allows a wider range of process conditions, e.g. temperatures and pH, which can be used to increase catalytic activity and CO2 solubility. Separating the charge transfer from the methanogenesis step to a chemical intermediate production step also allows a wider range of microbial species that can be used for methanogenesis; and processes for microbial methane production from syngas have been optimized and established for industrial biogas production. Three dimensional (3D) printing th reactors maximizes volumetric productivity by both optimally utilizing 3D space and reducing diffusion limitations. Further, 3D printed reactors allow for modular and flow-through designs, positioning the technology for scale-up and commercialization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The electromethanogenesis of the carbon dioxide into a fuel gas involves electromethanogenesis of the carbon dioxide into methane gas or syngas. Storing the fuel gas for subsequent use involves using the fuel gas for power generation.

AU SCHUETZLE R
   SCHUETZLE D
AE INFINIUM TECHNOLOGY LLC (INFI-Non-standard)
   CARBON RECYCLING SOLUTIONS LLC (CARB-Non-standard)
   INFINIUM TECHNOLOGY LLC (INFI-Non-standard)
GA 2021C5993W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves producing hydrogen and                oxygen from the electrolysis of water. The hydrogen                in combination with carbon dioxide is introduced                into a first catalytic reactor that uses a first                catalyst (105). The output from the first catalytic                reactor is used as syngas. The first catalyst is                used as a transition metal solid-solution catalyst.                The syngas is introduced into a second catalytic                reactor that uses a second catalyst (107) to                produce tail gas (109), water (111) and liquid fuel                (113). The tail gas from the second catalytic                reactor is introduced to a tail gas conversion                system that utilizes oxygen from the electrolyzer                to produce additional syngas.
   USE - Method for conversion of carbon dioxide into                liquid fuel such as premium kerosene, diesel and                jet fuels, and gasoline blendstocks.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is durable, efficient and                maintains a relatively constant level of fuel                productivity over long periods of time without                requiring catalyst re-activation or replacement.                The process employs two enhanced catalysts that                function efficiently in series at similar                pressures, simplifying the overall process of                producing fuels from non-petroleum feedstocks. The                catalysts and processes can efficiently and                economically convert CO2 and beta mixtures directly                to liquid fuels in two main steps.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for the production of a liquid fuel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic process flow                diagram for the improved catalysts for the direct                production of liquid fuels from carbon                dioxide.
   Catalyst (105)
   Heat exchanger (106)
   Second catalyst (107)
   Tail gas (109)
   Water (111)
   Liquid fuel (113)

AU REN J
   HUANG H
   ZOU X
   GUO Z
AE HUADIAN ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (HUAD-C)
GA 202151384N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining flue gas carbon dioxide emission rate. Flue gas carbon dioxide cumulative emission is calculated using the flue gas carbon dioxide emission rate. Cumulative carbon allowance surplus is calculated using the flue gas carbon dioxide cumulative emission. Electricity carbon emission intensity and historical heating carbon emission intensity are determined to obtain predicted carbon emission. Cumulative carbon allowance surplus and carbon allowance of the thermal power plant are determined using the predicted carbon emission. Carbon emissions and the carbon allowance of the thermal power plant is adjusted in real time so that the carbon emissions of the thermal power plant is equal to preset carbon allowance.
   USE - Method for analyzing the surplus and deficiency of carbon allowances for thermal power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables detecting flue gas carbon dioxide emission rate, calculating the accumulated carbon quota of the heat-engine plant in real time, predicting deviation of carbon emission and carbon quota in the final actual heat-engine plant and adjusting the carbon emission and the carbon quota of the heat-engine plant in time according to the deviation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a system for analyzing the surplus and deficiency of carbon allowances for thermal power plants; and
   (2) a device for analyzing the surplus and deficiency of carbon allowances for thermal power plants; and
   (3) a computer readable storage medium comprises a set of instructions for analyzing the surplus and deficiency of carbon allowances for thermal power plants.

AU ZANICHELLI L
   COLMEGNA G
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 2020901710
AB    NOVELTY - Performing suppression of soot formation involves adding gaseous carbon dioxide to the hydrocarbon-containing gaseous feed before entry into the reactor.
   USE - Method for performing suppression of soot formation during the partial oxidation of a hydrocarbon-containing gaseous feed, in the presence of steam and in an ATR reactor or in a POX reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform Method for the suppression of soot formation during the partial oxidation of a hydrocarbon-containing gaseous feed that increases the steam to carbon ratio and/or the oxygen to carbon ratio to inhibit soot formation, decreases the consumption of steam or oxygen, which are valuable source materials, and reduces the soot formation and operate under more advantageous conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for determining a minimum steam to carbon ratio required for soot-free operation in a process of partial oxidation of a hydrocarbon-containing gaseous feed, where process of partial oxidation is carried out in the presence of steam in an ATR reactor or in a POX reactor, adding gaseous feed with carbon dioxide before entry into the reactor, determining minimum steam to carbon ratio for soot-free operation in the presence of added carbon dioxide; and
   (2) a method for controlling a partial oxidation process in an ATR or POX reactor, which involves adding carbon dioxide to a hydrocarbon-containing gaseous feed of the process in the presence of steam and determining a minimum steam to carbon ratio for soot free operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ratio mCO2/mC of moles of added carbon dioxide over moles of carbon contained in the feed prior to addition of CO2 is 0.25. The ratio mCO2/mC is in the range 0.25 to 2 and preferably 0.25 to 1. The CO2 is added to the hydrocarbon-containing gaseous feed before or after addition of steam. The (S/C)lim and (S/C)lim,CO2 are determined with reference to operation, where the concentration in volume of the soot in the effluent of the partial oxidation process is not greater than 5 parts per billion.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   LIU Y

   FANG T

GA 2020666180
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves opening the windows for                ventilation, and dynamically controlling the window                opening based on the real-time monitoring of the                indoor carbon dioxide concentration, if that the                indoor carbon dioxide concentration exceeds the                carbon dioxide concentration value and the outdoor                environment meets the window opening conditions.                The operating mode is adjusted according to the                real-time monitored indoor temperature and indoor                humidity during the window opening period during                the window opening period. The indoor temperature                and indoor humidity are kept within the rated                range. The window is closed and the air                conditioning system operates in a preset mode, if                that the indoor carbon dioxide concentration is                lower than the predetermined carbon dioxide                concentration value or the outdoor environment does                not meet the window opening condition.

GA 2020257932
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises poly (methyl methacrylate) (PMMA) sheet, micro phase-change induced cracking device, multiple foil resistance strain gauges, high-speed camera, and digital image processing system super dynamic strain test system. The PMMA sheet has opposite first and second sides, and a blast hole is drilled through middle, first side and second side surfaces are prefabricated with non-through structure surface on side of blast hole. The second side surface is sprayed with speckles. The part protruding from blast hole is used for supercritical carbon dioxide phase change gas-dynamic loading on PMMA sheet. The high-speed camera records image of second side. The test system is electrically obtains cracking strain and cracking time of crack. The digital image processing system obtains displacement field on surface of PMMA sheet and strain field, compares cracking strain and cracking time of crack, and analyzes crack propagation direction, propagation length and propagation speed.

PN CN110711582-A; CN110711582-B
TI Preparing catalyst used for catalyzing methanation            reaction of carbon dioxide, by dispersing nickel salt            into polar solvent, dispersing carrier titanium dioxide            into alkali solution, mixing obtained solutions, aging,            drying and roasting.
AU SUN S
   CHEN Q
   ZHANG F
   GAO J
   HAN Y
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202010851Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing catalyst, involves (i)                uniformly dispersing nickel salt into a polar                solvent to obtain solution (a) with the                concentration of 0.007-0.1 mol/l, uniformly                dispersing the carrier titanium dioxide into an                alkali solution to obtain solution (b), uniformly                mixing solution (a) with solution (b), aging at                room temperature for 12-24 hours, and washing to                neutral, or uniformly dispersing nickel ions into a                polar solvent to obtain solution (a) with the                concentration of 0.007-0.1 mol/l, uniformly                dispersing the carrier titanium dioxide into a                polar solvent to obtain solution (b), and uniformly                mixing solution (a) with solution (b), and (ii)                drying at 70-90 degrees C for 12-24 hours and                roasting the dried product at 350-450 degrees C for                3-4 hours to obtain a catalyst with titanium                dioxide as a carrier.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing catalyst                for catalyzing a methanation reaction of carbon                dioxide and/or a reverse water gas shift reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst achieves selective change by                adjusting solution environment, exhibits excellent                low-temperature catalytic performance in carbon                dioxide hydrogenation reaction, has methane                selectivity of 99% in carbon dioxide methanation                reaction and excellent stability, and generates 99%                of carbon monoxide with high selectivity in                backwater gas reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a use of                the catalyst in catalyzing a methanation reaction                of carbon dioxide and/or a reverse water gas shift                reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel salt is a                  mixture of nickel nitrate, nickel acetate, nickel                  chloride and/or their hydrates. The carrier                  titanium dioxide is anatase and/or rutile. The                  alkali comprises sodium carbonate solution,                  sodium bicarbonate solution, aqueous ammonia,                  ammonium bicarbonate solution or ammonium                  carbonate solution. Preferred Composition: The                  concentration of carrier titanium dioxide in                  solution (b) is 0.3-4 mol/l. The alkali has a                  concentration of 0.1-0.5 mol/l.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of washing is                  carried out using 15-45 vol.% ethanol solution.                  The roasting the dried product is carried out at                  a heating rate of 2-5 degrees C/minute. The                  application of the catalyst is carried out by                  reducing the temperature at 400-450 degrees C for                  3-5 hours under a reducing atmosphere to obtain                  nickel doped titanium dioxide catalyst, before                  the catalyst is used. Preferred Components: The                  polar solvent comprises deionized water, 1-4C                  monohydric alcohol and/or 2-4C dihydric alcohol.                  The reducing atmosphere is hydrogen, carbon                  monoxide, a mixed gas of hydrogen and an inert                  gas, or a mixed gas of carbon monoxide and an                  inert gas. Preferred Composition: The volume                  ratio of solution (a) and solution (b)                  (0.4-7):1.

PN CN110484930-A; CN110484930-B
TI Preparing electrode used for producing formic acid comprises dissolving composite material of zinc cadmium sulfide solid solution, cobalt phosphide nanowires, multi-walled carbon nanotube in ethylene glycol, and impregnating nickel foam.
AU ZHANG Y
   LONG Y
   YUAN C
   ZHU Y
   LU C
   WU J
   DING C
   WANG W
   WANG Q
   CONG Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG GONGSHANG (UYZG-C)
GA 2019A0373W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing electrode useful for producing formic acid by reduction of carbon dioxide comprises dissolving the composite material of zinc cadmium sulfide (Zn0.5Cd0.5S) solid solution and cobalt phosphide nanowires, multi-walled carbon nanotubes and surfactants in ethylene glycol, impregnating nickel foam in the above solution, and drying to obtain foamed nickel electrode loaded with zinc cadmium sulfide solid solution, cobalt phosphide nanowires, and multi-walled carbon nanotubes.
   USE - The method is useful for producing formic acid by reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines zinc cadmium sulfide solid solution semiconductor with cobalt phosphide nanowires having a rigid one-dimensional nanostructure, thus it has excellent metal conductivity, which improves its activity for reducing carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for foamed nickel electrode, prepared by the above mentioned method; and #use of foamed nickel electrode loaded with zinc cadmium sulfide solid solution, cobalt phosphide nanowires and multi-walled carbon nanotubes for producing formic acid by reduction of carbon dioxide comprising using carbonate solution as an electrolyte, and foamed nickel electrode loaded with zinc cadmium sulfide solid solution, cobalt phosphide nanowires and multi-walled carbon nanotubes as an electrode, placing the electrode in the electrolyte, placing the dielectric baffle horizontally above the electrolyte, where the area between the electrode and dielectric baffle is the discharge area, and using dielectric barrier discharge plasma to catalyze the reduction of carbon dioxide to produce formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of composite material of zinc cadmium sulfide solid solution and cobalt phosphide nanowires to multi-walled carbon nanotubes is 0.5-1:1. The mass ratio of zinc cadmium sulfide solid solution to cobalt phosphide nanowires is 5-15:1. The distance between dielectric baffle and surface of the electrolyte is 2-8 mm, and dielectric barrier discharge uses a pulse voltage of 10-50 V and a pulse frequency of 1-10 kHz. Preferred Method: The method comprises dispersing zinc cadmium sulfide solid solution in water, ultrasonically treating to obtain suspension, adding cobalt phosphide nanowires under stirring, mixing uniformly, centrifuging, and drying to obtain composite material of zinc cadmium sulfide solid solution and cobalt phosphide nanowires. The method comprises dissolving zinc acetate, cadmium acetate and thioacetamide in water, adding sodium hydroxide aqueous solution under stirring, performing solvent thermal reaction at 170-190 degrees C for 16-32 hours, washing and drying the obtained precipitate to obtain zinc cadmium sulfide solid solution, where the molar ratio of zinc acetate, cadmium acetate, thioacetamide, and sodium hydroxide is 1:1:2-3:5-10. The method comprises (i) dissolving cobalt chloride and urea in a molar ratio of 1:0.8-1.2 in water, performing solvent thermal reaction at 95-105 degrees C for 6-18 hours, washing, drying, and roasting the obtained precipitate at 350-450 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain cobalt oxide nanowires, and (ii) dividing sodium hypophosphite and cobalt oxide nanowires obtained in step (i) in a molar ratio of 10-20:1 into two containers, and heating the two containers at 250-300 degrees C for 1-3 hours under the protection of same nitrogen atmosphere to obtain cobalt phosphide nanowires.

PN CN108003349-A; CN108003349-B
TI Preparation of siloxane polymer supercritical            carbon dioxide fracturing thickener involves            hydrosilylating chloroplatinic acid, aromatic amide            group-terminated side chain-modified siloxane polymer            and hydrogen-containing siloxane.
AU WANG Y
   LI Q
   YIN Z
   LI Y
   TANG L
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2018375014
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of siloxane polymer supercritical                carbon dioxide thickener involves adding a solution                of chloroplatinic acid and aromatic amide                group-terminated side chain-modified siloxane                polymer in an organic solvent under an inert gas                atmosphere, subjecting to a hydrosilylation                reaction with a hydrogen-containing siloxane,                filtering solid impurities, and distilling the                organic solvent. The aminopropyl-terminated vinyl                side chain-modified polydimethylsiloxane is                obtained by hydrolyzing aminopropylalkoxysilane                under inert gas atmosphere, dissolving the                hydrolyzate in toluene, separating to remove water,                distilling toluene, adding                tetramethyltetravinylcyclotetrasiloxane and other                cyclosiloxane, performing ring-opening                polymerization under reflux in a base, removing the                base, washing with saturated brine, and drying. The                base is chosen from tetramethylammonium hydroxide,                potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, and iron                hydroxide.
   USE - Preparation of siloxane polymer supercritical                carbon dioxide fracturing thickener.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of siloxane                polymer supercritical carbon dioxide thickener with                high synthesis efficiency, and excellent thickening                effect. The siloxane polymer supercritical carbon                dioxide thickener effectively reduces pollution of                groundwater and surface water in formation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The aromatic amide group-terminated side                chain-modified siloxane polymer is obtained by                reacting the aminopropyl-terminated vinyl side                chain-modified polydimethylsiloxane with an                aromatic acid chloride in the presence of an acid                and 4-dimethylaminopyridine catalyst under an inert                gas atmosphere, separating, purifying, and removing                the solvent. The other cyclosiloxane is chosen from                octamethylcyclotetrasiloxane,                octaphenylcyclotetrasiloxane,                tetramethyltetraphenylcyclotetrasiloxane,                hexamethylcyclotrisiloxane, and                trimethyltriphenylcyclotrisiloxane. The aromatic                acid chloride is chosen from benzoyl chloride,                phenylacetyl chloride, dibenzoyl chloride,                p-toluenesulfonyl chloride, hydrazino-2-carbonyl                chloride, and hydrazino-8-methyl-2-carbonyl                chloride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The inert gas is                  nitrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The                  aminopropylalkoxysilane is chosen from                  aminopropyldiethoxymethylsilane,                  aminopropyldiethoxyethylsilane,                  aminopropyldimethoxymethylsilane,                  aminopropyldimethoxyethylsilane,                  aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, and                  aminopropyltriethoxysilane. The organic solvent                  is chosen from tetrahydrofuran, petroleum ether,                  toluene, and ethyl acetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The                  hydrogen-containing siloxane is chosen from                  tetramethyldihydrodisiloxane,                  heptamethyltrisiloxane, dimethoxysilane,                  1,3-2H-hexamethyltrisiloxane, diethoxysilane, and                  polydimethylsiloxane having number average                  molecular weight of 90-200.

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of granular aerogel (1) involves                mixing a precursor (2) with a synthesis solvent (3)                and a hydrolyzing agent e.g. water, and optionally                a catalyst (4) to obtain a gel, granulating the gel                product to obtain granules, contacting the granules                with the synthesis solvent and the hydrolyzing                agent, washing the granules by adding a washing                solvent (preferably, hydrolyzing agent) and                optionally catalyst, drying the granules in order                to extract synthesis solvent and/or washing with                excess of supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - Manufacture of granular aerogel                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical                manufacture of granular aerogel with high quality,                high porosity, high specific surface area of 1000                m2/g or less, low thermal conductivity, and high                optical transmission of 90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of granular aerogel involves                mixing a precursor with a synthesis solvent and a                hydrolyzing agent e.g. water, and optionally a                catalyst to obtain a gel, granulating the gel                product to obtain granules, contacting the granules                with the synthesis solvent and the hydrolyzing                agent, washing the granules by adding a washing                solvent (preferably, hydrolyzing agent) and                optionally catalyst, drying the granules in order                to extract synthesis solvent and/or washing with                excess of supercritical carbon dioxide. The                granulation, contacting, washing and drying                processes are performed under a pressure of above                the critical point of carbon dioxide. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a granular                aerogel-manufacturing apparatus.
