TI Removing carbon dioxide in natural gas at normal            temperature comprises entering of natural gas to            stabilize pressure, absorbing carbon dioxide by            decarburization solvent solution, performing            decarburization gas-liquid separating, carrying out gas            stripping desorption and recycling barren            solution.
AU CHEN X
   PAN T
   LIU J
AE LINHE CLIMATE TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (LINH-Non-standard)
GA 202246461L
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide in natural gas at                normal temperature comprises (1) entering of the                natural gas into the raw material gas buffer tank                to stabilize the pressure, (2) absorbing the carbon                dioxide in the natural gas by the decarburization                solvent solution, performing decarburization                gas-liquid separating the decarburization purifying                gas to obtain the gas enters the subsequent                process, separating the decarburization pregnant                solution, (3) absorbing the carbon dioxide                decarburization pregnant solution, leading out from                the bottom of the decarburization absorption tower,                (4) entering of the decarburization pregnant                solution in the secondary flash evaporator to the                desorption regeneration tower, carrying out the gas                stripping desorption at normal temperature and                normal pressure and (5) desorbing the lean solution                obtained by desorption in the regeneration tower                into the decarburization circulation tank and                recycling the barren solution.
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon                dioxide in natural gas at normal temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The decarburized liquid: has high carbon load;                does not need steam for rich liquid analysis and                has low energy consumption, low operating cost,                short technological process, low investment and                high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing carbon dioxide in natural gas at                normal temperature comprises (1) entering of the                natural gas into the raw material gas buffer tank                to stabilize the pressure, where the power device                pressurizes and exchanges heat with the heat                exchange device, the natural gas pressure entering                the decarburization absorption tower is 3-5 MPa,                the temperature is 35-55degrees Celsius, (2)                entering of natural gas into the lower part of the                decarburization absorption tower, where the                decarburization solvent solution of natural gas and                from up to down in the decarburization absorption                tower is that the barren solution contact in the                rotor packing layer of the decarburization                absorption tower, absorbing the carbon dioxide in                the natural gas by the decarburization solvent                solution, discharging the un-absorbed                decarburization purifying gas from the                decarburization absorption tower, entering a                decarburization gas-liquid separator, performing                decarburization gas-liquid separating the                decarburization purifying gas to obtain the gas                enters the subsequent process, separating the                decarburization pregnant solution into the                desorption regeneration tower, (3) absorbing the                carbon dioxide decarburization pregnant solution,                leading out from the bottom of the decarburization                absorption tower, entering into second-stage flash                evaporator to flash, where the first level flash                steam pressure is 2.8-3 MPa, separating the carried                liquid drop by the first level flash gas, vacuum                extracting to 2.6 MPa to remove pressure fuel gas                buffer tank, sending to the desorption section of                the desorption regeneration tower after being                reduced pressure to be used as gas lift, (4)                entering of the decarburization pregnant solution                in the secondary flash evaporator to the desorption                regeneration tower, carrying out the gas stripping                desorption at normal temperature and normal                pressure, desorbing carbon dioxide gas from the                liquid phase at normal temperature and normal                pressure desorption and (5) desorbing the lean                solution obtained by desorption in the regeneration                tower into the decarburization circulation tank and                exchanging heat by the heat exchanger and conveying                the barren solution from the barren solution pump                to the decarburization absorption tower to be                recycled.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method                of removing carbon dioxide in natural gas at normal                temperature. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The decarburization                  solvent solution comprises a polar solvent and an                  activator, the polar solvent is propylene                  carbonate aqueous solution accounting for 90%,                  the activator accounting for 10%, the activator                  composition is piperazine 80%, and carbon dioxide                  promotes absorption 8-10% corrosion inhibitor,                  4-6% corrosion inhibitor, 4-6% methane absorption                  inhibitor. The concentration of the propylene                  carbonate aqueous solution is 40-50% and has good                  absorption and desorption capacity for carbon                  dioxide gas. The decarbonization efficiency                  reaches 99.5%, and the solvent is a physical                  solvent, non-toxic, non-corrosive, and                  biodegradable.

TI Assessing vascular compliance in subjects with multiple sclerosis using sequential gas delivery involves e.g. during inspiration by subject with multiple sclerosis, delivering first gas to subject.
AU FISHER J A
   DUFFIN J
   SOBCZYK O
   MIKULIS D
AE THORNHILL SCI INC (THOR-Non-standard)
   THORNHILL SCI INC (THOR-Non-standard)
GA 202120543G
AB    NOVELTY - Assessing vascular compliance in subjects with multiple sclerosis using sequential gas delivery comprises: during an inspiration by a subject with multiple sclerosis, delivering a first gas to the subject, where the first gas contains a concentration of oxygen to meet a respiratory need of the subject and to target an end-tidal partial concentration of oxygen, the first gas further contains a concentration of carbon dioxide to target an end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide and thus provide a vasoactive stimulus to the subject to assess vascular compliance with regard to multiple sclerosis-related cognition of the subject; and during the same inspiration and after delivering the first gas to the subject, delivering a second gas to the subject, where the second gas contains a concentration of carbon dioxide equal to the target end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide or, if at a resting state, equilibrated with an arterial partial pressure of carbon dioxide of the subject.
   USE - The method is useful for assessing vascular compliance in subjects with multiple sclerosis using sequential gas delivery, for delivery of a gas-containing carbon dioxide, to test for neurovascular uncoupling in a subject with multiple sclerosis, and to determine vascular compliance in a subject with multiple sclerosis (all claimed).
No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides vasoactive stimulus to a subject with multiple sclerosis to assess vascular compliance with regard to MS-related cognition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Assessing vascular compliance in subjects with multiple sclerosis using sequential gas delivery comprises: during an inspiration by a subject with multiple sclerosis, delivering a first gas to the subject, where the first gas provided in a volume i.e. less than a tidal volume minus an anatomical dead space, the first gas contains a concentration of oxygen to meet a respiratory need of the subject and to target an end-tidal partial concentration of oxygen, the first gas further contains a concentration of carbon dioxide to target an end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide and thus provide a vasoactive stimulus to the subject to assess vascular compliance with regard to multiple sclerosis-related cognition of the subject; and during the same inspiration and after delivering the first gas to the subject, delivering a second gas to the subject, where the second gas contains a concentration of carbon dioxide equal to the target end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide or, if at a resting state, equilibrated with an arterial partial pressure of carbon dioxide of the subject.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for providing a vasoactive stimulus to a subject with multiple sclerosis to assess vascular compliance with regard to multiple sclerosis-related cognition of the subject, comprising (a) a gas delivery device, and (b) a processor (36) connected to the gas delivery device, where the processor is configured to during an inspiration by a subject with multiple sclerosis, control the gas delivery device to deliver a first gas to the subject; and during the same inspiration and after delivering the first gas to the subject, control the gas delivery device to deliver a second gas to the subject.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of sequential gas delivery device.
   Expiratory reservoir (18)
   Inspiratory reservoir (20)
   Processor (36)
   Gas blender (38)
   Sensor (50)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: delivering the first and second gases during repeated inspirations by the subject; targeting a first end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide for a first series of repeated inspirations by the subject, when the first end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide is reached, measuring blood flow in the subject's brain, targeting a second end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide for a second series of repeated inspirations by the subject, when the second end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide is reached, measuring blood flow in the subject's brain, and calculating a cardiovascular response using a difference between the blood flow measured at the first and second end-tidal concentrations of carbon dioxide; selecting the first end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide to induce normocapnia in the subject, and selecting the second end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide to induce hypercapnia in the subject; measuring blood flow using one or both of cerebral blood flow imaging and blood oxygen level dependent signal; measuring blood flow in many layers of the subject's cerebral cortex; and calculating the vascular compliance using the cardiovascular response. The second gas includes rebreathed gas exhaled by the subject during an expiration prior to the inspiration.Preferred Components: The apparatus further comprises: a sensor (50) connected to the processor; the processor further configured to control the gas delivery device to target first end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide for a first series of repeated inspirations by the subject; when the first end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide is reached, control the sensor to measure blood flow in the subject's brain; control the gas delivery device to target a second end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide for a second series of repeated inspirations by the subject; when the second end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide is reached, control the sensor measure blood flow in the subject's brain; and calculate a cardiovascular response using the difference between the blood flow measured at the first and second end-tidal concentrations of carbon dioxide. The processor further configured to control the gas delivery device to deliver the first and second gases during repeated inspirations by the subject. The processor further configured to select the first end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide to induce normocapnia in the subject; and select the second end-tidal concentration of carbon dioxide to induce hypercapnia in the subject. The processor further configured to control the sensor to measure blood flow using one or both of cerebral blood flow imaging and blood oxygen level dependent signal. The processor further configured to control the sensor to measure blood flow in many layers of the subject's cerebral cortex. The processor further configured to calculate vascular compliance using the cardiovascular response.
   ACTIVITY - Neuroprotective.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

TI Production of fuel e.g. ethanol, involves            partially purifying biogas comprising methane and            carbon dioxide, removing hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon            dioxide to produce purified biogas, and producing fuel            from methane from purified biogas.
AU FOODY P J
   FOODY B
   DECHMAN J
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
   IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
   IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 2020065694
AB    NOVELTY - Production of fuel from biogas involves (a)                partially purifying (20) a biogas (A) comprising                methane and carbon dioxide from a biogas source (A)                using stationary purification system, removing                hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon dioxide from the                biogas (A) to produce a partially purified biogas                (A) having a heating value of less than 950                BTU/scf, (b) partially purifying (20) a biogas (B)                comprising methane and carbon dioxide from a biogas                source (B) using a other stationary purification                system (B), removing hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon                dioxide from the biogas (B) to produce a partially                purified biogas (B) having a heating value of less                than 950 BTU/scf, (c) feeding partially purified                biogas (A) and (B) into vessels (A) and (B), (d)                transporting (40) the vessels (A) and (B)                containing the purified biogas (A) and (B) to a                destination using vehicles, (e) removing the                purified biogas and reducing the pressure, and (f)                producing (50) the fuel from methane derived from                the removed biogas.
   USE - Production of fuel e.g. liquid transportation                fuel such as ethanol and hydrogen (all claimed),                methanol, gasoline, diesel, and dimethyl ether from                biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of fuel without                upgrading the biogas from renewable natural gas.                The components removed using a stationary system                that can remove the components more efficiently                and/or economically than a mobile biogas upgrading                or purification system. The method utilizes the                simple biogas upgrading system, such as simple                membrane system in which methane is lost in an                off-gas which can be combusted to provide energy                for compression, and providing the additional                value-added products such as carbon dioxide which                can be recycled within the biogas plant, by feeding                the gas into an anaerobic digester. The stationary                purification system allows the partial purification                system to be readily available on-site to at least                partially purify the raw biogas as it is                produced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of fuel from biogas involves (a)                partially purifying a biogas (A) comprising methane                and carbon dioxide from a biogas source (A) using a                stationary purification system, removing hydrogen                sulfide and/or carbon dioxide from the biogas (A)                to produce a partially purified biogas (A) having a                heating value of less than 950 BTU/scf, (b)                partially purifying a biogas (B) comprising methane                and carbon dioxide from a biogas source (B) using a                other stationary purification system (B), removing                hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon dioxide from the                biogas (B) to produce a partially purified biogas                (B) having a heating value of less than 950                BTU/scf, (c) feeding partially purified biogas (A)                and (B) into vessels (A) and (B), such that each                partially purified biogas is pressurized to at                least 1500 psig, (d) transporting the vessel (A)                containing the partially purified biogas (A) and                the vessel (B) containing the partially purified                biogas (B) to a destination using one or more                vehicles, (e) removing the partially purified                biogas from each vessel transported in step (d) and                reducing the pressure, and (f) producing the fuel                from methane derived from the partially purified                biogas removed in step (e).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of production                of fuel from biogas.
   Obtaining raw biogas (10)
   Partial purifying of raw biogas (20)
   Transporting purified gas (40)
   Producing fuel using partially purified                biogas (50)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The methane reformer is                  a steam methane reformer, an autothermal                  reformer, a dry methane reformer, and/or a                  partial oxidation reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The partially                  purified biogases removed in step (e) comprises                  8% or less carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (f) further                  involves feeding a gas stream comprising methane                  from the partially purified biogas (A) and/or                  partially purified biogas (B) into a methane                  reformer to produce syngas comprising carbon                  monoxide and hydrogen. The production of fuel                  further involves purifying the partially purified                  biogas removed in step (e), and producing the                  renewable natural gas from the partially purified                  biogas. The destination is a biogas upgrading                  facility, the method involves (i) producing                  liquefied renewable natural gas from the                  partially purified biogases, (ii) injecting the                  renewable natural gas produced in step (i) into a                  natural gas distribution system, and (iii)                  delivering the liquefied renewable natural gas to                  a fuel production facility through the natural                  gas distribution system. The step (f) further                  involves feeding a gas stream comprising the                  renewable natural gas delivered in step (iii)                  into a methane reformer to produce syngas, and                  gasifying the liquefied gas, feeding the gas                  stream comprising carbon monoxide, and carbon                  dioxide and hydrogen in the molar ratio of                  2:1-4:1, from the syngas to a gas fermentation to                  produce a fermentation product in which the fuel                  comprising the fermentation product comprising                  ethanol, and subjecting the syngas to a water gas                  shift reaction to produce a shifted gas, removing                  carbon dioxide from the shifted gas to produce a                  stream-enriched in hydrogen comprising at least                  80% hydrogen, incorporating hydrogen from the                  steam-enriched in hydrogen into a crude                  oil-derived liquid hydrocarbon to produce the                  liquid transportation fuel. The step (b) involves                  removing less than 75% carbon dioxide from each                  of the biogases.

PT P
PN CN108753445-A
TI Extracting walnut oil by breaking walnut, putting walnut kernels into hot water, cooling and removing peel, followed by baking or frying, crushing, putting into extraction kettle, extracting and then introducing into oil-liquid separator.
AU ZHU Q
AE SHAANXI HAIYUAN ECOLOGICAL AGRIC CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2018905942
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting walnut oil by supercritical carbon dioxide involves: (1) breaking the walnut, selecting walnut kernels, putting the kernels into hot water, cooling and removing the peel; (2) baking or frying the peeled walnut kernels (3) crushing the walnut kernels after step (2); (4) putting the crushed walnut kernels into an extraction kettle, introducing an extraction medium carbon dioxide into the kettle, extracting at 40-45 degrees C under pressure of 36-40 MPa for 2-3 hours, then starting pressure relief and gradually reducing the pressure to normal pressure in the pressure relief process to generate a mixture of defatted walnut kernels, liquid carbon dioxide and walnut oil; (5) introducing the product obtained in step (4) into an oil-liquid separator and separating walnut oil from the separator; and (6) introducing the walnut oil prepared in step (5) into a decolorizing oil tank, followed by adding active carbon, decolorizing under stirring and then filtering to remove impurities.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting walnut oil by supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is capable of simply extracting the walnut oil by supercritical carbon dioxide with reduced energy consumption in a safe and environmentally-friendly manner; prevents various nutrients in the pressing process from being damaged compared with the traditional process; and does not cause harm to human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting walnut oil by supercritical carbon dioxide involves: (1) breaking the walnut through a shell breaking machine, separating shell kernels, selecting the walnut kernels, putting the walnut kernels into hot water at 86 degrees C, discharging the hot water after soaking for 8 minutes, cooling the walnut kernels by using cold water, and removing the peel; (2) baking the peeled walnut kernels at 50-60 degrees C for 3-5 hours, or putting the peeled walnut kernels into a frying pan and frying at 95-105 degrees C for 25-30 minutes; (3) putting the walnut kernels after baking or frying in step (2) into a crusher and crushing to fineness of 30-50 mesh; (4) putting the crushed walnut kernels into an extraction kettle, measuring an extraction medium carbon dioxide by a flowmeter and then introducing the carbon dioxide into the kettle, controlling the supercritical extraction temperature at 40-45 degrees C and the supercritical extraction pressure at 36-40 MPa, where the flow of carbon dioxide is 300-550 l/hour, and the extraction time is 2-3 hours, then starting pressure relief, and gradually reducing the pressure to normal pressure in the pressure relief process to generate a mixture of defatted walnut kernels, liquid carbon dioxide and walnut oil; (5) introducing the defatted walnut kernels, liquid carbon dioxide and walnut oil obtained in step (4) into an oil-liquid separator, controlling the separation pressure at 15-20 MPa and the separation temperature at 50-70 degrees C, and separating walnut oil from the oil-liquid separator; and (6) introducing the walnut oil prepared in step (5) into a decolorizing oil tank, maintaining the vacuum degree of the decolorizing oil tank at 550-600 mm mercury column, maintaining the oil temperature at 85-95 degrees C, adding 0.6 wt.% active carbon to the walnut oil, decolorizing for 30 minutes under stirring and then filtering to remove impurities.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-K01B; D03-Q; D10-A01; D10-A02
IP A23D-009/02; A23J-001/14; C11B-001/04; C11B-001/10; C11B-003/00; C11B-003/10; C11B-003/16
PD CN108753445-A   06 Nov 2018   C11B-001/10   201901   Pages: 5   Chinese

PN WO2022189318-A1
TI Purification of cyclic carbonate e.g. propylene            carbonate and ethylene carbonate used as polar aprotic            solvent, involves adding alkylene oxide and carbon            dioxide, separating mixture into e.g. polyether            carbonate polyol, and obtaining raw mixture containing            cyclic carbonate and purified product.
AU BAUSA J
   TRAVING M
   HOFMANN J
   BRAUN S
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2022B6676G
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of cyclic carbonate involves (A)                adding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide to a                hydrogen-functional starter substance in the                presence of a catalyst, resulting in a mixture                containing polyether carbonate polyol and cyclic                carbonate, (B) separating the mixture resulting                from the step (A) into polyether carbonate polyol                and cyclic carbonate containing impurities, (C)                supplying the cyclic carbonate containing                impurities from the step (B) to a rectification                column (c1), equipped with at least one feature                chosen from structured packing, packed bed of                fillers, or trays, and subsequently separating a                portion of the impurities from the top of the                rectification column (c1) and obtaining a raw                mixture containing cyclic carbonate, (D) supplying                the raw mixture containing cyclic carbonate                obtained in the step (C) to a rectification column                (c2), equipped with at least one feature chosen                from structured packing, packed bed of fillers, or                trays.
   USE - Purification of cyclic carbonate e.g.                propylene carbonate and ethylene carbonate used as                polar aprotic solvent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables purification of cyclic                carbonate with high purity, reduced the proportion                of low-boiling and high-boiling by-products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of cyclic carbonate involves (A)                adding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide to a                hydrogen-functional starter substance in the                presence of a catalyst, resulting in a mixture                containing polyether carbonate polyol and cyclic                carbonate, (B) separating the mixture resulting                from the step (A) into polyether carbonate polyol                and cyclic carbonate containing impurities, (C)                supplying the cyclic carbonate containing                impurities from the step (B) to a rectification                column (c1), equipped with at least one feature                chosen from structured packing, packed bed of                fillers, or trays, and subsequently separating a                portion of the impurities from the top of the                rectification column (c1) and obtaining a raw                mixture containing cyclic carbonate, (D) supplying                the raw mixture containing cyclic carbonate                obtained in the step (C) to a rectification column                (c2), equipped with at least one feature chosen                from structured packing, packed bed of fillers, or                trays, and subsequently separating another portion                of the impurities as a sump in the rectification                column (c2) and obtaining the pure cyclic carbonate                product, where (D-1) a bottom temperature is                130-170&#176; C prevailing in (D) and/or (D-2)                the pure cyclic carbonate product is removed from                the rectification column (c2) via a side                draw.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step (C), the                  bottom temperature is 155-180&#176; C, and a                  pressure is 5-500 mbar. In the step (D-l), the                  pressure is 5-80 mbar and the bottom temperature                  is 140-160&#176; C. In the step (D-2), the                  bottom temperature is 120-180&#176; C,                  preferably 140-160&#176; C. The step (A) is                  performed by (a) placing a subset of                  hydrogen-functional starter substance and/or a                  suspending agent which does not containing                  hydrogen-functional groups in a reactor,                  optionally together with a catalyst, (b)                  optionally activating a DMC catalyst, adding a                  portion (based on the total amount of alkylene                  oxide used in the activation and                  copolymerization) of alkylene oxide to the                  mixture resulting from the step (a), this                  addition of a portion of alkylene oxide                  optionally can take place in the presence of                  carbon dioxide, and in which the temperature peak                  occurring as a result of the following exothermic                  chemical reaction and/or a drop in pressure in                  the reactor is awaited in each case, and in which                  the step (b) for activation can also take place                  several times, (g) metering the                  hydrogen-functional starter substance, alkylene                  oxide and the suspending agent which does not                  contain hydrogen-functional groups, and/or carbon                  dioxide into the reactor during the reaction, (d)                  transferring the reaction mixture continuously                  removed in the step (g) to an after-reactor in                  which the content of free alkylene oxide in the                  reaction mixture is reduced by way of an                  after-reaction. In the step (B), the cyclic                  carbonate containing impurities are separated by                  a thin film evaporator, falling film evaporator,                  and/or stripping column from the mixture                  resulting from the step (A). The steps (C) and                  (D) are carried out in a main column with a side                  rectification column, each equipped with                  structured packing, a random packing or trays,                  the purified cyclic carbonate is removed from the                  top of the side column or a dividing wall column                  equipped with structured packing, a random                  packing or trays, the purified cyclic carbonate                  is removed via a side draw.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cyclic carbonate                  is cyclic propylene carbonate, cyclic ethylene                  carbonate and/or mixture.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)

PT P
PN CN114507362-A
TI Preparing hydrophobically modified            polyethyleneimine carbon dioxide adduct microspheres            used as polyurethane foam blowing agent for            polyurethane foaming, involves mixing the            hydrophobically modified polyethyleneimine and the            solvent in storage tank in a certain proportion, and            stirring uniformly.
AU LI Z
   XIE X
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2022722713
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrophobically modified                polyethyleneimine carbon dioxide (CO2) adduct                microspheres involves mixing the hydrophobically                modified polyethyleneimine and the solvent in a                storage tank in a certain proportion, and stirring                uniformly to obtain a solution that can absorb CO2.                The weight of the solvent is 100 pts. wt., and the                weight of the hydrophobically modified                polyethyleneimine is 0.1-300 pts. wt. The                CO2-absorbing solution is sprayed into the                microsphere synthesis reactor after entering the                sprayer through a pressurized pump, and the CO2gas                volume concentration in the reactor is not less                than 30%. The temperature in the reactor is kept in                the range of 40-70&#8451;, and the mist                droplets absorb CO2in the reactor. The solvent                volatilizes to form hydrophobically modified                polyethyleneimine CO2adduct microspheres suspended                in the airflow.
   USE - Method for preparing hydrophobically modified                polyethyleneimine CO2 adduct microspheres used as                polyurethane foam blowing agent for polyurethane                foaming (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, can                preparation size, controllable grain diameter,                outer layer is hydrophobic graft chain core is                polyethylene imine CO2 adduct of core-shell                structure, and can be stably dispersed in the                polyurethane white material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hydrophobically modified                polyethyleneimine carbon dioxide (CO2) adduct                microspheres involves mixing the hydrophobically                modified polyethyleneimine and the solvent in a                storage tank in a certain proportion, and stirring                uniformly to obtain a solution that can absorb CO2.                The weight of the solvent is 100 pts. wt., and the                weight of the hydrophobically modified                polyethyleneimine is 0.1-300 pts. wt. The                CO2-absorbing solution is sprayed into the                microsphere synthesis reactor after entering the                sprayer through a pressurized pump, and the CO2 gas                volume concentration in the reactor is not less                than 30%. The temperature in the reactor is kept in                the range of 40-70&#8451;, and the mist                droplets absorb CO2 in the reactor. The solvent                volatilizes to form hydrophobically modified                polyethyleneimine CO2 adduct microspheres suspended                in the airflow. The gas-solid separation is                accomplished by at least one of sedimentation,                filtration, electrostatic removal and cyclone                separation in the air stream containing the                hydrophobically modified polyethyleneimine CO2                adduct microspheres. The obtained solid is                hydrophobically modified polyethyleneimine CO2                adduct microspheres. The obtained gas recovers the                solvent through the condenser, and the remaining                CO2 gas is returned to the microsphere synthesis                reactor as CO2 raw material to participate in the                synthesis of hydrophobically modified                polyethyleneimine CO2 adduct microspheres                again.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hydrophobic chain                  contains repeating unit of polypropylene oxide,                  polyoxetane, polytetrahydrofuran or polysiloxane,                  preferably the hydrophobic chain contains                  trimethylsilyl, more preferably the hydrophobic                  chain has a hydrocarbon group having 1-22 carbon                  atoms. The solvent contains diethyl ether,                  dichloromethane, methyl tert-butyl ether, or                  acetone. The nebulizer includes a pressure                  nebulizer, an ultrasonic nebulizer, or a rotating                  disc nebulizer. The polyethyleneimine comprises                  linear polyethyleneimine, branched                  polyethyleneimine or hyperbranched                  polyethyleneimine.

Testing.); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)

PN CN113908659-A
TI Carbon dioxide sealing device used in waste gas            discharge in lime kiln production lime process, has            purification and liquefaction equipment whose bottom            end is fixed with sealing equipment, and sealing device            fixedly clamped on side of top of bottom frame away            from dust removal assembly.
AU DING D
   ZHANG Y
   YOU S
   XIAO D
   ZHANG J
   LI Y
AE ANHUI HWASU CORP (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202215900U
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a dust removal assembly (2)                fixedly installed on the top side of the bottom                frame (1). A dust collecting frame (3) is fixedly                installed at the bottom end of the dust removal                assembly. A degassing assembly (4) is fixedly                installed in the top middle of the bottom frame. A                circulation assembly (5) is arranged between the                dust removal assembly and the degassing assembly. A                first pump frame (6) is fixedly installed between                the dust removal assembly and the degassing                assembly by bolts. A second pump frame (7) is                fixedly installed on the side of the degassing                assembly away from the first pump frame by bolts. A                purification and liquefaction device (8) is fixedly                installed at one end of the second pump frame away                from the degassing assembly. The bottom end of the                purification and liquefaction equipment is fixedly                installed with a sealing equipment (9). The sealing                device is fixedly clamped on the side of the top of                the bottom frame away from the dust removal                assembly.
   USE - Carbon dioxide sealing device used in waste                gas discharge in lime kiln production lime                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can effectively clean the dust                attached on the dust removing net frame, so as to                improve the processing effect of the subsequent                dust removal net frame to the smoke dust, thus                improving the sealing degree of carbon dioxide. The                device is provided with a dust removing component                and matched with the driving motor, thus filtering                and removing dust for the flue gas, and hence                improving the dust removal effect of a whole device                to smoke dust. The circulating component is set                between the degassing component and the                dust-removing component, so that the heat in the                smoke can be conducted to a spiral pipe through the                spiral pipe, thus heating the composite alkali                solution at the bottom of the air removing outer                frame through the heat, and improving the                absorption reaction efficiency of the gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a sealing                method of carbon-di-oxide in waste gas discharge in                lime kiln production lime process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view showing                the structure of a CO2 sealing device in waste gas                discharge in lime kiln production lime                process.

   Degassing component (4)
   Circulation component (5)
   First pump frame (6)
   Second pump frame (7)
   Purifying liquefying device (8)
   Sealing Equipment (9)
   Driving motor (10)

PT P
PN CN113563200-A; CN113563200-B
TI Preparation of linear amine involves selective            hydrogen amine methylating olefin in mixture of olefin,            carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide, hydrogen and amine            in presence of nitrogen heterocyclic carbene metal            coordination polymer as catalyst.
AU ZHENG Q
   QIAN C
   TU T
AE ZHUHAI FUDAN INNOVATION INST (ZHUH-Non-standard)
   UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
GA 2021C6528U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of a linear amine (I) involves                mixing an olefin (II), carbon monoxide or carbon                dioxide, hydrogen and an amine (III) in a solvent                comprising ether, alcohol, and benzene for                selective hydrogen amine methylation of the olefin                in presence of a nitrogen heterocyclic carbene                metal coordination polymer (IV) as a catalyst at                80-200degrees Celsius for 4-24 hours. The molar                ratio of catalyst and amine is 10000-100.
   USE - Preparation of linear amine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces linear amine using cheap                and easily-available olefin and amine as starting                materials with reaction selectivity as high as                95/5, high amine yield of 98%, and high purity and                without complicated post-treatment and by simple                filtration. The catalyst used for producing the                linear amine can be recycled more than ten                times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of a linear amine of formula (I)                involves mixing an olefin of formula (II), carbon                monoxide or carbon dioxide, hydrogen and an amine                of formula (III) in a solvent comprising ether,                alcohol, and benzene for selective hydrogen amine                methylation of the olefin in presence of a nitrogen                heterocyclic carbene metal coordination polymer of                formula (IV) as a catalyst at 80-200degrees Celsius                for 4-24 hours. The molar ratio of catalyst and                amine is 10000-100.
   R1' = 6-10C aryl, 5-8C linear alkyl, or 5-6C                cycloalkyl (all optionally substituted by group                (A)); Ar' = 6-10C aryl or 4-16C heteroaryl (both                optionally substituted at ortho-meta-para positions                by group (A)); R2 = 1-4C linear alkyl or 5-6C                cycloalkyl; A = halo, 3-6C alkyl, 4C ester, 5C                carbonyl, 5C alkyl ether, 4C hydroxy, or 1-2C                alkoxy; M = transition metals of group VIIIB such                as Rh, Co, Ir, Ru, Pd, Fe, Ni, and/or Mn; linker =                group chosen from groups of formulae (a1)-(a5);                V',W' = N or CH; X,Y' = O, N-G, CH, -CH=CH-, -CC-,                0-8C alkyl, p-phenyl, m-phenyl, o-phenyl,                1,4-divinylphenyl, 1,4-diethynylphenyl, biphenyl,                1,4-methylenephenyl or 9,10-anthracenyl; G =                protecting group such as 1-12C linear alkyl, 5-7C                cycloalkyl, benzyl, aryl, tert-butoxycarbonyl,                benzoyl or benzyl chloroformate; Z = halide,                tetrafluoroborate, hexafluorophosphate,                hexafluoroantimonate, acetate, nitrate, or sulfate;                L = auxiliary ligand chosen from halo, carbonyl,                phenyl, cyclooctadienyl, hydroxy, water, carbonate,                acetate, acetylacetone anion, or phosphine ligand;                R1 = 1-12C linear alkyl, 5-7C cycloalkyl, benzyl,                or aryl; R2' = 1-10C linear alkyl or 3-10C                cycloalkyl; m = 0 or natural number; and n = 2 or                more.

PN CN112552145-A
TI Collecting, concentrating and enriching volatile aroma and alcohol during wine fermentation comprises e.g. passing processed volatile steam of fermentation broth in fermentation tank into condenser, condensing and discharging carbon dioxide.
AU DANG G
   CHEN X
   MENG L
   LIU Z
   LU P
   CHENG C
   WANG X
   CHEN Z
   ZHANG R
   TAN M
   DANG X
AE XINJIANG CITIC GUOAN WINE CO LTD (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 202134320P
AB    NOVELTY - Collecting, concentrating and enriching volatile aroma and alcohol during wine fermentation comprises e.g. (i) passing the processed volatile steam of the fermentation broth in the fermentation tank into the condenser, condensing, discharging carbon dioxide, condensing alcohol, aroma components and water vapor into liquid and collecting; and (ii) passing the collected liquid into the upstream side of the membrane in the membrane module in liquid form, forming the vapor partial pressure difference of the components in the upstream and downstream of the membrane by means of vacuuming and condensation on the downstream side of membrane, penetrating the ingredients e.g. as alcohol and aroma in the raw materials through the membrane to the downstream side of the membrane, vaporizing into steam in a vacuum environment, passing the permeated steam into the condenser under suction of vacuum unit, forming a concentrated liquid into the product tank, circulating and separating the retentate.
   USE - The method is useful for collecting, concentrating and enriching volatile aroma and alcohol during wine fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can adopt condensation and collection first and separation and enrichment technology by pervaporation membrane and realize the recovery and preservation of alcohol and aroma substances which is convenient for production, deployment and use; improves product quality; and reduces production process losses and emission of volatile organic compounds into the air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Collecting, concentrating and enriching volatile aroma and alcohol during wine fermentation comprises (i) passing the processed volatile steam of the fermentation broth in the fermentation tank into the condenser, condensing, discharging carbon dioxide, condensing alcohol, aroma components and water vapor into liquid and collecting; and (ii) passing the collected liquid into the upstream side of the membrane in the membrane module in liquid form, forming the vapor partial pressure difference of the components in the upstream and downstream of the membrane by means of vacuuming and condensation on the downstream side of the membrane, penetrating the ingredients e.g. as alcohol and aroma in the raw materials through the membrane to the downstream side of the membrane, vaporizing into steam in a vacuum environment, passing the permeated steam into the condenser under the suction of the vacuum unit, forming a concentrated liquid into the product tank after condensation, circulating and separating the retentate on the upstream side of the membrane until the alcohol content of the collected liquid meets the requirements
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of collecting, concentrating and enriching volatile aroma and alcohol during wine fermentation. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the 1KL fermentation broth is equipped with a condensation area of 1m2. In the step (i), the condensing temperature of the condenser is -1 to 5 degrees C. In the step (ii), the method comprises pumping the collected liquid for preheating before entering the membrane module, and setting the temperature at 40-45 degrees C. In the step (ii), the method comprises using downstream side of the membrane adopts a vacuum and condensation method, where the vacuum degree of the vacuum is 4000-8000 Pa. In the step (ii), the method comprises using the downstream side of the membrane which adopts the method of vacuuming and condensation, where the condensation temperature of condensation is -10 to-15 degrees C. In the step (ii), the method comprises, circulating the retentate on the upstream side of the membrane and separating until the volume ratio of the collected liquid alcohol is reduced to 0-0.5%.

PT P
PN CN112053855-A; CN112053855-B
TI Electrode material based on multi-walled carbon nanotube-carbonized wood hybrid scaffold comprises carbonized wood chips formed after carbonization and activation of Chinese fir chips, and tracheid structure.
AU LUO Y
   WANG X
   WU W
   DENG Y
   ZHOU C
   WANG Z
   LI Z
   ZHUO C
   CHEN D
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH FORESTRY & TECHNOLOGY (UCSF-C)
GA 2020C47161
AB    NOVELTY - An electrode material based on multi-walled carbon nanotube-carbonized wood hybrid scaffold comprises carbonized wood chips formed after carbonization and activation of Chinese fir chips, and tracheid structure is formed on the carbonized wood chips. The inner wall of the tracheid structure is anchored and grown with multi-walled carbon nanotubes in situ by chemical vapor deposition to form multi-walled carbon nanotube-carbonized wood scaffold on the carbonized wood chip, and coating cobalt(II) hydroxide nanosheet on the multi-wall carbon nanotube via electrochemical deposition.
   USE - The electrode material based on multi-walled carbon nanotube-carbonized wood hybrid scaffold.
   ADVANTAGE - The super capacitor prepared by electrode material has high energy storage performance, comprising a surface capacitance of the 3.31Fcm-2, energy density of the 0.9 Wh-2, the power density of the 70m Wcm-2 and the capacitance retention rate after charging and discharging at the current density of the 60m-2 is 97.8% after charging.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the:
   (1) preparation of electrode material based on multi-walled carbon nanotube-carbonized wood hybrid scaffold comprising taking Chinese fir slices, air drying, cutting, placing in hot air drying box, carbonizing for 4-8 hours under argon gas protection at 800-1200 degrees C for 4-8 hours, activating in the carbon dioxide and argon mixed gas flow for 8-12 hours, cutting or polishing to form AWC electrode, activating at 650-850 degrees C with argon gas flow rate is 3 times the carbon dioxide flow rate, carbon dioxide flow rate of 60-80 sccm, removing impurities, soaking AWC electrode in nickel(II) nitrate aqueous solution for 10-20 minutes, removing water in a drying box, using hydrogen gas as the reducing gas at flow rate of 20-40 sccm, ethylene as the carbon source at flow rate of 80-100 sccm, and argon gas as the protective gas at flow rate of 300-500 sccm, performing chemical vapor deposition for 3-10 minutes, obtaining multi-walled carbon nanotube-carbonized wood scaffold, subjecting to hydrophilic treatment, immersing in a solution containing 0.1 mol/L cobaltous nitrate hexahydrate, using carbonized wood chips containing form multi-walled carbon nanotube-carbonized wood scaffolds as working electrodes, platinum electrodes as counter electrodes, and saturated calomel electrodes as reference electrodes, and performing electrochemical deposition for 1-9 hours, and drying; and
   (2) supercapacitor comprising carbonized wood chips electrode material based on carbon nanotubes and manganese dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Methods: The carbonization consists of drying in a blast drying oven at 250 degrees C for 3-9 hours for pre-carbonization, placing in tube furnace under the protection of argon gas, and carbonizing at 1000 degrees C for 3-9 hours. The activation is carried out for 8-12 hours under carbon dioxide gas flowing atmosphere at 750 degrees C and carbon dioxide gas flow rate of 60-100 sccm. The chemical vapor deposition is carried out by soaking the activated carbonized wood chips in 0.25 mol/L nickel(II) nitrate aqueous solution at 80-100 degrees C for 15 minutes, removing water, using hydrogen gas as the reducing gas at flow rate of 20-40 sccm, ethylene as the carbon source at flow rate of 80-100 sccm, and argon gas as the protective gas at flow rate of 300-500 sccm, and performing chemical vapor deposition for 3-10 minutes. The electrochemical deposition is carried out using solution containing 0.1 mol/L cobaltous nitrate hexahydrate at - 0.9 V relative to the saturated calomel electrode, and electrodepositing cobalt(II) hydroxide using three-electrode system using carbonized wood chips containing form multi-walled carbon nanotube-carbonized wood scaffolds as working electrodes, platinum electrodes as counter electrodes, and saturated calomel electrodes as reference electrodes. The multi-walled carbon nanotube-carbonized wood scaffold comprises soaking carbonized wood chips in 0.25mol/L nickel(II) nitrate solution at 90 degrees C for 15 minutes, air drying, putting into tube furnace, pumping argon for more than 30 minutes to exhaust the air, raising temperature to 740 degrees C at heating rate of 10 degrees C/minute, and introducing ethylene and hydrogen gas for 5 minutes. The washing is carried out using 6 wt.% hydrochloric acid for more than 15 minutes, and then with deionized water for more than 3 times until the washing liquid becomes clear, and blast drying at 60-100 degrees C.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

PN CN111905752-A
TI Catalytically synthesizing dimethyl carbonate with            ultrafine cobalt-cerium bimetallic nano-catalyst by            dissolving cobalt salt and cerium salt in water,            stirring, centrifuging, and using ultrafine            cobalt-cerium bimetal oxide nanocatalyst.
AU LIU Z
   SUN Y
   HE Z
   WANG K
   WANG Z
   WANG W
   YANG Y
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
   UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 2020B40458
AB    NOVELTY - Method for catalytically synthesizing dimethyl                carbonate with ultrafine cobalt-cerium bimetallic                nano-catalyst involves (a) according to the                stoichiometric ratio of cobaltaceriumboxygenx,                where the ratio of a to b is 1/8-8/1, dissolving                the cobalt salt and cerium salt in water, slowly                dripping the resulting solution into the lye at                50-100 degrees C under stirring conditions, and                keep stirring for 12-24 hours, leaving still and                aging for 1-5 hours, centrifuging, washing, and                drying at 80-140 degrees C, heating at 550-800                degrees C at a heating rate of 2-10 degrees                C/minute, roasting at a constant temperature for                1-5 hours to obtain a particle size of 1-20 nm                ultrafine cobalt-cerium bimetallic nano-catalyst,                the lye is a mixed solution of sodium hydroxide or                potassium hydroxide and ammonia, (b) using the                ultrafine cobalt-cerium bimetal oxide nanocatalyst                of step (a) to catalyze the carbonylation reaction                of methanol and carbon dioxide in the presence of a                dehydrating agent to prepare dimethyl                carbonate.
   USE - The method is used for catalytically                synthesizing dimethyl carbonate with ultrafine                cobalt-cerium bimetallic nano-catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in operation, cheap and                readily available raw materials and catalysts, has                less catalyst usage, easy separation, reusable, and                suitable for large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for catalytically synthesizing dimethyl                carbonate with ultrafine cobalt-cerium bimetallic                nano-catalyst involves (a) according to the                stoichiometric ratio of cobaltaceriumboxygenx,                where the ratio of a to b is 1/8-8/1, dissolving                the cobalt salt and cerium salt in water, slowly                dripping the resulting solution into the lye at                50-100 degrees C under stirring conditions, and                keep stirring for 12-24 hours, leaving and aging                for 1-5 hours, centrifuging, washing, and drying at                80-140 degrees C, heating at 550-800 degrees C at a                heating rate of 2-10 degrees C/minute, roasting at                a constant temperature for 1-5 hours to obtain a                particle size of 1-20 nm ultrafine cobalt-cerium                bimetallic nano-catalyst, the lye is a mixed                solution of sodium hydroxide and ammonia or                potassium hydroxide and ammonia, and the                concentration of the alkali is 0.01-0.5 mol/l, (b)                using the ultrafine cobalt-cerium bimetal oxide                nanocatalyst of step (a) to catalyze the                carbonylation reaction of methanol and carbon                dioxide in the presence of a dehydrating agent to                prepare dimethyl carbonate, the reaction                temperature is controlled at 100-160 degrees C for                4-16 hours, the carbon dioxide pressure is 1-5 MPa,                and the dehydrating agent is                dicyclohexylcarbodiimide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of a to b in                  step (a) is 1/8-1/2. The cobalt salt in step (a)                  comprises cobalt nitrate, cobalt acetate, cobalt                  chloride, and/or cobalt acetylacetonate. The                  cerium salt comprises cerium nitrate, cerium                  acetate, cerium chloride, and/or cerium                  acetylacetonate. Preferred Method: The cobalt                  salt and cerium salt in step (a) are fully                  dissolved in water, where the resulting solution                  is slowly dripped into the lye at 60-90 degrees C                  under stirring conditions, and keep stirring for                  20-24 hours, leaving for aging for 2-4 hours,                  centrifuging, washing, and drying at 100-120                  degrees C. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio                  of the methanol to the ultrafine cobalt-cerium                  bimetal oxide nanocatalyst and the dehydrating                  agent in step (b), is 1:0.01:1-10.05:5. Preferred                  Conditions: The temperature in step (a) is                  increased to 600-700 degrees C at a heating rate                  of 5-10 degrees C/minute, and the temperature is                  roasted for 3-5 hours. The reaction temperature                  in step (b) is 120-140 degrees C, the reaction                  time is 10-12 hours, and the carbon dioxide                  pressure is 3-5 MPa.

TI Operating coke oven involves inserting            combustion-removing injecting the gas in spray nozzle            for carbon adhering in coke oven chamber into            carbonization chamber having oxygen and correlation            with carbon dioxide of combustion exhaust gas.

PN CN111066578-A
TI Performing industrial cultivation of Grifola frondosa involves taking miscellaneous wood chips, bran, cottonseed hulls, rice bran, and calcium carbonate, and preparing cultivation bag culture medium.
AU HUANG Q
   LIU Y
   XU X
   ZHOU W
AE SHAOGUAN STARWAY BIOTECH CO LTD (SHAO-Non-standard)
   XICHONG XINGHE BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (XICH-Non-standard)
GA 202038170A
AB    NOVELTY - Performing industrial cultivation of Grifola frondosa involves taking 60.0 wt.% miscellaneous wood chips, 20.0 wt.% bran, 10.0 wt.% cottonseed hulls, 8.0 wt.% rice bran, and 2.0 wt.% calcium carbonate, and preparing cultivation bag culture medium. According to each bag of 620g to 820g wet material, the cultivation bag is made into a cultivation bag, which is sealed with a collar and a plastic cover, and the moisture content of the wet material is less than or equal to 65 wt.%. After inoculation of the cultivation bag, it is moved to the culture rack of the bacteria room, and the culture condition of the bacteria is controlled at room temperature 20 to 22 degrees C. The relative humidity of the air is controlled at 60 to 75 wt.%, the concentration of carbon dioxide is controlled at 2500 to 3500 ppm, and the cultivation is completed for 40 to 45 days to complete the germ.
   USE - Method for performing industrial cultivation of Grifola frondosa.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform industrial cultivation of Grifola frondosa, which improves the production efficiency of the Grifola frondosa, saves the mechanical or manual digging of material scraching links, and improves the speed of scraping operations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The plastic film of the cultivation bag is cut and peeled off to form an equilateral triangle scraching area with a side length of 4 to 5 cm, so that the mycelium and the cultivation material are in direct contact with the air. After completion of the above sterilization process, the cultivation bag is moved into the mushroom outlet room, and the room temperature of the mushroom outlet room is controlled at 18 to 20 degrees C.The relative humidity of the air is controlled at 90 to 95 wt.%, and the carbon dioxide concentration is controlled at 700 to 1000ppm. The scattering light source of 200 to 300 Lx is turned on for light source induction, and induce the formation of primordium from 10 to 14d. The cultivation bag is moved into the growth room for mushroom cultivation. The room temperature of the growth room is controlled at 19 to 21 degrees C, and the relative humidity of the air is controlled at 90 to 95 wt.%. The carbon dioxide concentration is controlled at 700 to 1000 ppm, using a scattering light source of 200 to 300 Lx to irradiate for 12 to 18 h / d, and culture for 10 to 14 d to complete growth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Condition: The cultivation bag is a polypropylene plastic bag.

TI Production of substantially two-dimensional sheet of transition metal carbide, transition metal nitride, or transition metal carbonitride used in supercapacitor or topological insulator involves chemically etching three-dimensional material.
AU PERSSON P
   NAESLUND L
AE PERSSON P (PERS-Individual)
   NAESLUND L (NAES-Individual)
GA 202004503K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves synthesizing a substantially two-dimensional sheet represented as Mn+1XnTs by chemically etching so to remove A atoms from three-dimensional material with Mn+1Xn, where M constitutes transition metal, A-group element, X constitutes C and/or N, n=1, 2, 3 or higher, and Ts constitutes a surface termination of the substantially two-dimensional sheet; placing the substantially two-dimensional sheet in an enclosed chamber, preferably in a vacuum chamber; introducing a first gas into the chamber, the first gas consisting of hydrogen gas and optionally inert gasses for desorbing oxygen from the surface of the substantially two-dimensional sheet by reaction with hydrogen to form water molecules and removing water molecules from the chamber for obtaining substantially two-dimensional sheet of a transition metal carbide, transition metal nitride, or transition metal carbonitride.
   USE - Method for production of a substantially two-dimensional sheet of transition metal carbide, transition metal nitride, or transition metal carbonitride used in supercapacitor or in a topological insulator or stacked assembly for capturing and/or storing carbon dioxide or for removing carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide containing gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare two-dimensional sheet with higher reactivity so that it can efficiently adsorb atoms from for example surrounding gasses in a controllable manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves synthesizing a substantially two-dimensional sheet represented as Mn+1XnTs by chemically etching so as to remove A atoms from a three-dimensional material with Mn+1Xn, where M constitutes at least one transition metal, at least one A-group element, X constitutes C and/or N, n=1, 2, 3 or higher, and Ts constitutes a surface termination of the substantially two-dimensional sheet; placing the substantially two-dimensional sheet in an enclosed chamber, preferably in a vacuum chamber; introducing a first gas into the chamber, the first gas consisting of hydrogen gas and optionally one or more inert gasses for desorbing oxygen from the surface of the substantially two-dimensional sheet by reaction with hydrogen to form water molecules and removing water molecules from the chamber for obtaining a substantially two-dimensional sheet of a transition metal carbide, transition metal nitride, or transition metal carbonitride, the substantially two- dimensional sheet having a reduced amount of oxygen in the surface termination Ts. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) substantially two-dimensional sheet of transition metal carbide, transition metal nitride, or transition metal carbonitride;a
   (2) stacked assembly; and
   (3) a method for capturing, removing, and/or storing COx from a COx containing gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The second gas comprises or consists of nitrogen, ammonia, hydrazine, amidogen, hydrogen cyanide, or methylamine. The second gas comprises or consists of carbon dioxide. The COx containing gas comprises biogas.

TI Producing calcium sulfate whisker, by cooling ammonia vapor waste liquid, separating, precipitating, filtering, concentrating introducing carbon dioxide kiln gas, carbonizing, concentrating, heating, mixing, filtering, washing and drying.
AU LI K
   ZHANG R
   SUN L
   ZHANG Q
   WAN L
   DAO R
AE QINGHAI ADVENT ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5775B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing calcium sulfate whisker, by (i) cooling ammonia vapor waste liquid and separating to obtain to-be-treated liquid, (ii) precipitating the to-be-treated liquid, filtering and concentrating the filtrate to obtain clear liquid, (iii) introducing carbon dioxide kiln gas in the clear liquid, adjusting the pH value, and carrying out carbonization reaction to obtain salt solution containing calcium carbonate, (iv) concentrating calcium carbonate-containing salt solution to obtain clear liquid and heating, (v) heating the saturated solution of sodium sulfate, and (vi) mixing the clear liquid with the saturated solution of sodium sulfate, maintaining the temperature, stirring to obtain a suspension, cooling and filtering, and (vii) washing the obtained filter cake and drying to obtain the product, where the aspect ratio of the calcium sulfate dihydrate crystal whisker is 30-50, and using ammonia vapor waste liquid and gypsum produced by anhydrous mirabilite.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium sulfate whisker.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for recovery and reuse of steamed ammonia waste liquid, and expands the production mode of calcium sulfate whiskers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing calcium sulfate whisker, involves (i) cooling ammonia vapor waste liquid and separating to obtain a to-be-treated liquid with a solid content of less than 5 g/l, (ii) precipitating the to-be-treated liquid, filtering and concentrating the filtrate to obtain a clear liquid with a solid content of less than 100 mg/l, (iii) introducing carbon dioxide kiln gas in the clear liquid of step (ii), adjusting the pH value to 6-9, and carrying out carbonization reaction to obtain a salt solution containing calcium carbonate, (iv) concentrating calcium carbonate-containing salt solution to obtain a clear liquid with a solid content of less than 100 mg/l, and heating the clear liquid at 94-100 degrees C, (v) heating the saturated solution of sodium sulfate to 80-94 degrees C, and (vi) mixing the clear liquid with the saturated solution of sodium sulfate, maintaining the temperature at 90-95 degrees C for 4-6 hours and stirring at 150 rpm to obtain a suspension, cooling to 60-70 degrees C and filtering, and (vii) washing the obtained filter cake and drying to obtain the product, where the aspect ratio of the calcium sulfate dihydrate crystal whisker is 30-50 and using ammonia vapor waste liquid and gypsum produced by anhydrous mirabilite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the steamed ammonia waste liquid to anhydrous thenardite is 50: 7. In step (iii), the concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide kiln gas is 40 vol.%, and the volume ratio of the carbon dioxide to the ammonia waste liquid is (1.1-1.2):1. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the steamed ammonia waste liquid is cooled to 40 degrees C. In step (iii), the carbonization reaction time is 1 hour. In step (iv), the clear solution is heated at 94 degrees C. In step (v), the saturated sodium sulfate solution is heated at 80 degrees C. In step (v), the saturated sodium sulfate is prepared by dissolving anhydrous sodium sulfate with a purity of 90% per 200 kg in 410 kg water and filtering. In step (vii), the water washing method uses 80 degrees C water with a mass ratio of 1:1 to the filter cake for washing. In step (vii), the drying is carried out at 80 degrees C for 12 hours. In step (vii), washing the filter cake with water, removing sodium chloride residues on the filter cake to obtain washing water containing sodium chloride, mixing the washed water obtained in step (vi) with clear liquid containing residual calcium sulfate calcium sulfate whiskers, and carrying out enrichment, where the obtained clear liquid is a salt solution with the concentration of sodium chloride of 5-6 % mass.

PN CN109536800-A
TI High deformation magnesium-zinc-copper alloy            comprises magnesium, zinc, copper and impurity,            prepared by e.g. preheating the magnesium ingot, zinc            ingot and copper plate, and casting.
AU WANG X
   WANG S
   ZHANG Y
   WANG Q
   JIANG X
   WU Y
   WANG G
   SI W
   WANG F
   YANG Y
AE SHANDONG YINGUANG YUYUAN LIGHT METAL PRE (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201930996S
AB    NOVELTY - High deformation magnesium-zinc-copper alloy                comprises 93-97.7 wt.% magnesium, 2-5 wt.% zinc,                0.3-1 wt.% copper and less than 1 wt.% impurity.                The high deformation magnesium-zinc-copper alloy is                prepared by (i) preheating the magnesium ingot,                zinc ingot and copper plate, (ii) heating preheated                magnesium ingot to molten state in a crucible, and                injecting carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride                mixed protective gas into the crucible during                heating and melting, then melting the magnesium                ingot completely, and allowing to stand, and (iii)                heating, adding preheated zinc ingot and copper                plate while stirring and refining, degassing with                argon to remove surface scum, allowing to stand,                heating and casting to obtain slab, where the flow                rate of argon gas is 12 l/minute, and (iv)                processing the obtained slab to obtain extruded                blank, placing slab in a heating furnace for                preheating to obtain high deformation                magnesium-zinc-copper alloy.
   USE - Used as high deformation magnesium-zinc-copper                alloy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High deformation magnesium-zinc-copper alloy                comprises 93-97.7 wt.% magnesium, 2-5 wt.% zinc,                0.3-1 wt.% copper and less than 1 wt.% impurity.                The high deformation magnesium-zinc-copper alloy is                prepared by (i) preheating the magnesium ingot,                zinc ingot and copper plate at 150-200 degrees C,                (ii) heating preheated magnesium ingot to molten                state in a crucible, and injecting carbon dioxide                and sulfur hexafluoride mixed protective gas into                the crucible during heating and melting, then                melting the magnesium ingot completely, and                allowing to stand for 30-50, where the volume ratio                between carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride is                99:1, and (iii) adding preheated zinc ingot and                copper plate while stirring, then completely                melting, then heating at 710-730 degrees C and                refining by manual stirring for 10-15 minutes,                degassing with argon for 10-12 minutes to remove                surface scum, allowing to stand for 1-2 hours and                then heating at 680-720 degrees C, and casting                alloy cast liquid in a mold preheated at 180-250                degrees C to obtain slab, where the flow rate of                argon gas is 12 l/minute, and (iv) processing the                obtained slab and then setting temperature of the                extrusion tube at 380-420 degrees C, performing                extrusion to obtain blank, then controlling                extrusion speed at 5-10 mm/second and placing blank                in a heating furnace and then preheating at 410-450                degrees C and insulating for 1-2 hours to obtain                high deformation magnesium-zinc-copper alloy, where                the heating temperature of the extrusion die is                390-430 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN108609638-A
TI Producing aluminum acid-produced special fertilizer used for Chinese cabbage, by mixing crushed potassium feldspar with phosphogypsum, additive and modifier, grinding clinker, carrying out solid-liquid separation and adding carbon dioxide.
AU LI J
   SU X
   XIE Y
   ZENG Y
   LU F
   LUO X
   ZHOU D
   CHEN X
   GAO G
AE GUIZHOU GUIFU ECOLOGICAL FERTILIZER IND (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
   GUIZHOU KEXIN CHEM & METALLURGY CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201880431A
AB    NOVELTY - The method for producing aluminum acid-produced special fertilizer involves (a) crushing potassium feldspar through 150-180 mesh, mixing potassium feldspar with phosphogypsum, additive and modifier to obtain a raw material, transferring raw material to a kiln and calcining to obtain clinker, (b) grinding the clinker, dissolving and carrying out solid-liquid separation, (c) adding carbon dioxide gas to liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until white precipitate is not generated, filtering out, washing, drying to obtain aluminum hydroxide and calcining aluminum hydroxide to obtain aluminum oxide, (d) floating the filter residue obtained by solid-liquid separation to select a sulfide, calcining sulfide and transferring flue gas to a sulfuric acid preparation system to obtain sulfuric acid, and (e) adding residual filter residues to stirrer, adding urea, fishbone dust, borax, zinc sulfate, manganese sulfate and cobalt nitrate, air-drying and granulating.
   USE - Method is useful for producing aluminum acid-produced special fertilizer, which is useful for Chinese cabbage.
   ADVANTAGE - The aluminum acid-produced special fertilizer has high aluminum recovery rate and purity, and exhibits small damage action on soil acidity and alkalinity of cabbage planting land.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for producing aluminum acid-produced special fertilizer involves (a) crushing potassium feldspar through 150-180 mesh, mixing potassium feldspar with phosphogypsum, additive and modifier to obtain a raw material, transferring the raw material to a kiln and roasting to obtain clinker, where the weight ratio of phosphogypsum to potassium feldspar in the raw material is 1:0.6-1.3, the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide and alumina+iron oxide contained in the raw materials is 1:1 and the mixing ratio of the modifier is 13-21 wt.%, (b) grinding the clinker, dissolving and carrying out solid-liquid separation, (c) adding carbon dioxide gas to the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until white precipitate is not generated, filtering out the white precipitate, washing, drying to obtain aluminum hydroxide and calcining aluminum hydroxide to obtain aluminum oxide, (d) floating the filter residue obtained by solid-liquid separation to select a sulfide, calcining sulfide and transferring flue gas to a sulfuric acid preparation system to obtain sulfuric acid, and (e) adding 60-80 pts. wt. residual filter residues to a stirrer, adding 40-60 pts. wt. urea, 60-70 pts. wt. fishbone meal, 3-7 pts. wt. borax, 1-4 pts. wt. zinc sulfate, 1-3 pts. wt. manganese sulfate and 1-2 pts. wt. cobalt nitrate to the stirrer, stirring, air-drying, granulating the mixture to obtain special fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The potassium feldspar comprises 10-18 %mass potassium oxide, 16-20 %mass alumina and 60-70 %mass silica. The fertilizer comprises 70 pts. wt. residual filter, 50 pts. wt. urea, 65 pts. wt. fishbone meal, 5 pts. wt. borax, 2.5 pts. wt. zinc sulfate, 2 pts. wt. manganese sulfate and 1.5 pts. wt. cobalt nitrate. Preferred Components: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. Preferred Conditions: In the step (a), the calcining step is carried out at 800-1200 degrees C for 2-5 hours. In the step (c), the calcining step is carried out at 800-900 degrees C for 2-4 hours. In the step (d), the calcining step is carried out at 800-1000 degrees C for 1-3 hours. In the step (e), the stirring step is carried out for 5-10 minutes. Preferred Device: The kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.)
MC C04-B04E; C05-A01; C05-A03; C05-B02C; C05-C04; C05-C05; C05-C06; C10-A13C; C14-T
IP C01B-017/79; C01F-007/04; C01F-007/06; C01F-007/08; C01F-007/14; C05G-001/00
