PT P
PN EP3361318-A1; US2018231901-A1; JP2018132758-A; US10409180-B2
TI Production of toner used in color laser printer,            involves producing dispersion using resin fine            particles soluble in chloroform and comprising resins            having partial structures, organic solvent, and resin,            and removing organic solvent.
AU KOJI S
   SUDA S
   KOSAKI Y
   FUKUYA S
   KINUMATSU T
   AOKI K
   MORI T
   WATANABE S
   TOYOIZUMI N
   TANI A
   NAKAHAMA K
   FUKUTANI S
   OZAKI Y
AE CANON KK (CANO-C)
   CANON KK (CANO-C)
   CANON KK (CANO-C)
GA 201863296E
AB    NOVELTY - Production of toner comprising toner particles                involves mixing resin fine particles, an organic                solvent, and a resin (R) with a dispersion medium                comprising carbon dioxide to prepare a dispersion                in which a droplet comprising the resin (R) is                dispersed in the dispersion medium and a surface of                the droplet is covered with the resin fine                particles, and removing the organic solvent                contained in the droplet and dispersion medium by                flowing carbon dioxide under pressurization through                the dispersion. The resin fine particles comprise                one or more resins having partial structures                (A)-(C), and are soluble in chloroform. A resin (A)                constituted of only the partial structure (A), and                a resin (B) constituted of only the partial                structure (B) are soluble in the organic solvent. A                resin (C) constituted of only the partial structure                (C) is insoluble in the organic solvent.
   USE - Production of toner used in color laser                printer for forming images.
   ADVANTAGE - The method stably produces toner having sharp                particle size distribution and excellent                environmental stability, using resin fine particles                having small particle diameter and excellent                solvent resistance, and exhibiting excellent                affinity for carbon dioxide and resin                solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The resin fine particles have absolute value                of difference between solubility parameter of the                resin (R) and solubility parameter of the partial                structure (A) of 2.0 or less, and difference                between solubility parameter of the partial                structure (A) and solubility parameter of the                partial structure (B) of 3.0 or more. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) production of resin particles, which                involves mixing the resin fine particles, organic                solvent, and resin (R) with the dispersion medium                to prepare the dispersion;
   (2) resin fine particles, which comprise a                resin (X) having the partial structures (A)-(C), or                comprise resin (Y) having the partial structures                (A) and (C), and a resin (Z) having the partial                structures (B) and (C). The organic solvent is at                least one chosen from acetone, ethyl acetate,                methyl ethyl ketone, tetrahydrofuran, and                2-phenylethyl alcohol; and
   (3) production of resin fine particles, which                involves dissolving the resin (X) in the organic                solvent and precipitating the resin (X) in water or                organic solvent to microparticulate the resin (X),                or mixing and dissolving the resins (Y) and (Z) in                the organic solvent, and precipitating the resins                (Y) and (Z) in water or organic solvent to                microparticulate the resins (Y) and (Z).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a toner                production apparatus.
   Cylinder (B1)
   Filter (F1)
   Valve (V1)
   Granulation tank (t1)
   Solvent recovery tank (t2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The resin fine                  particles contain 10.0-40.0 %mass partial                  structure (A). The partial structure (B) has an                  organopolysiloxane structure of formula (I). The                  resin fine particles comprise 5.0-30.0 %mass                  silicon atom originating with the                  organopolysiloxane structure. The partial                  structure (C) comprises a crystalline resin. The                  fraction of the resin fine particles soluble in                  the organic solvent is less than 35.0                  %mass.R1,R2 = 1-3C alkyl, preferably                  methyl;andn = 2-150.Preferred Properties: The partial structure                  (B) has chain length (LB) of 18000-120000 pm. The                  ratio of chain length of the partial structure                  (A) and chain length (LB) is 1.6-4.5. Preferred                  Process: Production of resin fine particles                  further involves obtaining the resin (Y) by                  radical polymerization of a monomer having the                  partial structure (C) using a macroinitiator                  having the partial structure (A), and a                  functional group which generates a free radical,                  or obtaining the resin (Y) by radical                  polymerization of a monomer having the partial                  structure (A) using a macroinitiator having the                  partial structure (C) and a functional group                  which generates a free radical, and obtaining the                  resin (Z) by radical polymerization of a monomer                  having the partial structure (C) using a                  macroinitiator having partial structure (B), and                  a functional group which generates a free                  radical, or obtaining the resin (Z) by radical                  polymerization of a monomer having the partial                  structure (B) using a macroinitiator having the                  partial structure (C), and a functional group                  which generates a free radical.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); G08 (Electrography, electrophotography and magnetography (G03G).); P84 (Other photographic (G03D-H).); S06 (Electrophotography and Photography)
MC A06-A00E4; A08-S02; A11-A03; A12-L05C2; G06-G05A; G06-G08E; P84-A02; P84-T50D; S06-E04A1; S06-E04D; S06-K01A; S06-K06C; S06-K99C

PT P
PN CN110589765-A
TI Preparing synthesis gas with different proportions            using natural gas, comprises carrying out crude            desulfurization, pressurizing, mixing desulfurized            natural gas, absorbing carbon dioxide, dehydrating            decarburization process and vaccumizing.
AU XIE D
   SHI Y
   PAN Z
   QIAN J
   PEI Z
   ZHANG H
   GUAN C
   QIN X
AE SINOPEC NANJING ENG CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2019A7614L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing synthesis gas with different                proportions using natural gas, comprises (i)                carrying out crude desulfurization of the                desulfurization tower, passing a part of the                natural gas from the plant boundary area directly                to the radiant section of the double reformer for                fuel; (ii) pressurizing the pressure of raw                material carbon dioxide having atmospheric pressure                by carbon dioxide compressor; (iii) mixing the                desulfurized natural gas with steam from the drum                and carbon dioxide, entering the preheater of the                conversion raw material in the convection section                of the double reformer; (iv) passing the flue gas                leaving the radiation section of the reformer                through the heat exchange coils in the convection                section to recover the heat of the flue gas; (v)                absorbing carbon dioxide in the process gas in a                carbon washing tower; and (vi) dehydrating                decarburization process gas by a TSA dryer and                vaccumizing by a syngas compressor.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing synthesis                gas with different proportions using natural                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the adjustment of the                ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide of the                synthesis gas 1.0-3.0 and the type and scale of the                target product is adjusted according to market                changes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing synthesis gas with different                proportions using natural gas, comprises (i)                carrying out crude desulfurization of the                desulfurization tower, passing a part of the                natural gas from the plant boundary area directly                to the radiant section of the double reformer for                fuel, mixing another part of natural gas with                hydrogen, entering the natural gas compressor                pressurization, preheating the desulfurization raw                material preheater in the convection section of the                double reforming furnace, entering the fixed-bed                hydrogenation reactor, converting all                sulfur-containing compounds into hydrogen sulfide                under the action of a hydrogenation catalyst,                passing the gas from the hydrogenation reactor in                the zinc oxide desulfurization reactor and                performing refined desulfurization to obtain                natural gas, where zinc oxide reacts with hydrogen                sulfide to form zinc sulfide; (ii) pressurizing the                pressure of raw material carbon dioxide having                atmospheric pressure by carbon dioxide compressor                and desulfurizing through carbon dioxide                desulfurization tank, carrying out refined                desulfurization and feeding into the raw gas                system; (iii) mixing the desulfurized natural gas                with steam from the drum and carbon dioxide,                entering the preheater of the conversion raw                material in the convection section of the double                reformer, heating, entering the furnace tube of the                radiant section of the double reforming furnace,                carrying out double reforming reaction and                producing the main components of the process gas                hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide,                passing combustion air through the blower,                preheating by the combustion air preheater I,                heating the combustion air preheater II passed to                the convection section of the reformer to the                required temperature and passing to the burner in                the radiant section of the reformer; (iv) passing                the flue gas leaving the radiation section of the                reformer through the heat exchange coils in the                convection section to recover the heat of the flue                gas, heat exchanging the flue gas, cooling and                discharging from the chimney to the atmosphere                through the induced fan, recovering the process gas                generated in the radiant section of the double                reforming furnace by the waste heat boiler for                steam production in the drum, passing the process                gas through the process gas cooler to fall to the                normal operating temperature of the downstream                decarburization unit, exchanging desalinated water                and process condensate for heat and mass transfer                by steam in the deaerator, removing soluble gas                impurities and pumping to the drum by the boiler                feed water; (v) absorbing carbon dioxide in the                process gas in a carbon washing tower using an                active MDEA (methyl glycol amine) solution to                reduce carbon dioxide content in the synthesis gas,                regenerating the absorbed rich liquid in a solvent                regeneration tower, mixing the high-concentration                carbon dioxide gas with extra carbon dioxide and                returning to the inlet of the reformer through                carbon dioxide compressor; and (vi) dehydrating                decarburization process gas by a TSA dryer and                vaccumizing by a syngas compressor using syngas                (hydrogen + carbon monoxide) as a product out of                bounds according to a certain proportion to meet                the needs of downstream equipment products. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system for                preparing different proportions of synthesis gas                using natural gas, comprising a double reforming                furnace radiation section and a double reforming                furnace convection section. The radiation section                of the double reforming furnace includes a furnace                tube and a burner. A rapid evaporator, a conversion                raw material preheater, a steam superheater, a                desulfurization raw material preheater and                combustion air preheater II are provided in the                convection section of the double reforming furnace                in order from left to right. The natural gas output                pipeline is connected to the desulfurization tower.                One output end of the desulfurization tower is                connected to the radiation section of the double                reformer. The other output end is connected with                the output end of the hydrogen pipeline in order of                a natural gas compressor, a desulfurization raw                material preheater, a fixed-bed hydrogenation                reactor and a zinc oxide desulfurization reactor.                The carbon dioxide output pipe is connected to the                carbon dioxide desulfurization tank after passing                through the carbon dioxide compressor. An output                end of the steam drum is connected to the input end                of the steam superheater. The output end of the                carbon dioxide desulfurization tank at the output                end of the zinc oxide desulfurization reactor and                the output end of the steam superheater are                collected and connected to the furnace tube in the                radiation section of the dual integral converter                through the conversion material preheater. The                output end at the top of the radiation section of                the dual integral converter passes through the                waste heat boiler process gas cooler and the carbon                wash tower in turn. The output end at the top of                the carbon washing tower is connected to the syngas                compressor through a TSA dryer in turn.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  pressure of natural gas from outside the device                  boundary is 0.1-3.0 MPaG, the natural gas                  pressure is 0.3-0.5 MPaG, the molar ratio of the                  natural gas and hydrogen flow is 8:1-15:1, the                  molar ratio of natural gas and hydrogen flow is                  10:1-12:1, the natural gas and hydrogen are                  pressurized by a natural gas compressor to                  2.0-3.5 MPaG, the natural gas and hydrogen are                  boosted to 2.5-3.2 MPaG by a natural gas                  compressor, the pressurized raw material gas is                  preheated by a desulfurization raw material                  preheater to a temperature of 250-450 degrees C,                  preferably 280-400 degrees C. In the step (ii),                  the carbon dioxide is pressurized by the                  compressor to 2.5-4.5 MPaG, preferably 2.8-4.0                  MPaG. In the step (iii), the molar ratio of                  natural gas, steam and carbon dioxide is                  1:1:0.6-1: 3:1.5, preferably 1: 2.8:1.1, the raw                  material gas is heated to a temperature of                  500-650 degrees C through a preheater of                  converted raw materials, preferably 550-600                  degrees C, the pressure required for the                  conversion reaction is 0.1-3.5 MPaG, preferably                  0.15-3.0 MPaG, the temperature required for the                  conversion reaction is 750-1000 degrees C,                  preferably 800-950 degrees C, the temperature of                  the air preheated by the combustion air preheater                  I is 50-100 degrees C, preferably 60-80 degrees C                  and the temperature of the air heated by the                  combustion air preheater II in the convection                  section of the reforming furnace is 200-400                  degrees C, preferably 250-350 degrees C. In the                  step (iv), cooling the process gas by the waste                  heat boiler and the process gas cooler to 30-100                  degrees C, preferably 35-50 degrees C and the                  temperature of the flue gas after heat exchange                  and cooling to 110-250 degrees C, preferably                  120-150 degrees C. In the step (v), absorbing                  carbon dioxide in the process gas by alpha -MDEA                  solution and carbon dioxide content is less than                  30 ppm. Preferred Components: In the step (iv),                  the convection section of the reformer includes a                  heat exchange equipment comprising rapid                  evaporator, conversion raw material preheater,                  steam superheater, desulfurization raw material                  preheater, combustion air preheater II, the rapid                  evaporator is used to produce saturated steam in                  the drum, the saturated steam generated by the                  steam drum passes through the steam superheater                  in the convection section to generate a part of                  the steam required as the raw gas after the                  superheated steam is generated and a part of it                  is used for preheating the air by the combustion                  air preheater I. In the step (vi), the moisture                  content of the syngas dehydrated by the TSA dryer                  is less than 1 ppm, the pressure of the                  compressed synthesis gas is 2.0-4.0 MPaG,                  preferably, 2.5-3.5 MPaG and the hydrogen/carbon                  monoxide ratio of the synthesis gas (hydrogen +                  carbon monoxide) as a product out-of-bounds is                  1.0-3.0.

PN US2018154300-A1; US10315150-B2
TI Method for recovering carbon dioxide (CO2),            involves using pressurized CO2 vapor stream to melt            solid CO2 within melting zone of rotating            freezer/melter.
AU HUNTINGTON R A
   DENTON R D
   DHANUKA S K
AE HUNTINGTON R A (HUNT-Individual)
   DENTON R D (DENT-Individual)
   DHANUKA S K (DHAN-Individual)
   EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
GA 201845001Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving a gas mixture                from a power plant. The gas mixture comprises CO2                (120), and the CO2 separation system comprises a                rotating freezer/melter having a freezing zone, a                melting zone and a rotor. The solid CO2 captured on                the rotor is melted while the rotor is in the                melting zone, thus forming liquid CO2. The liquid                CO2 is flowed through the rotor while the rotor is                in the melting zone. The remaining gas mixture out                of the freezing zone and into the CO2 separation                device is routed. The residual CO2 is recovered                from the remaining gas mixture using the CO2                separation device. The residual CO2 is pressurized                using the compressor to produce a pressurized CO2                vapor stream. The pressurized CO2 vapor stream is                used to melt the solid CO2 within the melting zone                of the rotating freezer/melter.
   USE - Method for recovering carbon dioxide                (CO2).
   ADVANTAGE - The higher combined cycle efficiencies result                from synergistic utilization of a combination of                the gas turbine with the steam turbine. The                combination produces electricity more efficiently                than either the gas turbine or steam turbine alone.                The sealing both the freezing zone and the melting                zone of the rotating freezer/melter prevents gases                or liquids from flowing from the freezing zone to                the melting zone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a system                for power generation and carbon dioxide (CO2)                recovery.
   Power generation and carbon dioxide recovery                System (100)
   Oxidant (102)
   Fuel (104)
   Power plant (106)
   CO2 (120)
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN WO2021245120-A1; TW202204498-A
TI Reactive composition used to make polyisocyanurate (PIR) comprises e.g. isocyanate composition of isocyanate compound, PIR promoting catalyst, physical blowing agent with lambda gas and carbon dioxide scavenging compound of epoxy compound.
AU JONCHERAY T J
   VANDENBROECK J
   GEUMEZ G J
AE HUNTSMAN INT LLC (HUTM-C)
GA 2021E0321U
AB    NOVELTY - Reactive composition comprises at least (a) an isocyanate composition comprising at least one isocyanate compound, and (b) an isocyanate-reactive composition comprising at least one isocyanate-reactive compound, and (c) at least one polyisocyanurate (PIR) promoting catalyst, and (d) at least one physical blowing agent with a lambda gas of less than or equal to 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C, and (e) at least one carbon dioxide scavenging compound comprising at least one epoxy compound having an equivalent weight lower than 300 g/mol, and (f) optionally a catalyst promoting epoxy reaction with carbon dioxide having the amount of isocyanate-reactive compounds (b) in the composition is at least 10 wt.% calculated on the total weight of the composition, or at least more than the amount of epoxy compounds and the molar amount of epoxy compounds in the composition is at least 7.8 times higher than the molar amount of carbon dioxide formed by the water present in the composition after reaction with isocyanates.
   USE - The composition is useful for making a PIR, comprising foam at an isocyanate index of at least 120, which is used as thermal insulator having construction thermal insulation foam, appliance thermal insulation foam or pipe insulation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: provides stabilized PIR that has a foam density of less than 45 kg/m3, preferably greater than 45 and a stabilized thermal conductivity of less than 20 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C, preferably 14-20 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C, or preferably less than 25 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C, preferably 14-25 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C; and provides semi-rigid and rigid PIR that has significantly improved long term insulation values when used under diffusion tight conditions and significantly improved thermal insulation properties maintained over the average economic lifetime of the foam thus making use of blowing agents having low lambda gas values ( less than or equal to 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C) in combination with a predetermined amount of carbon dioxide scavengers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) making a PIR comprising insulation foam, comprising combining and/or mixing the ingredients of the reactive composition at an isocyanate index of at least 120, preferably at least 250; and
   (2) a stabilized PIR comprising insulation foam made using the process, where the wt.% of carbon dioxide in the stabilized aged foam is 0-2 wt.%, preferably 0-0.5 wt.%, calculated on the total weight of the stabilized aged foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst used for promoting epoxy reaction with carbon dioxide comprising ammonium salts, more preferably tetrabutylammonium bromide and/or tetrabutylammonium iodide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises at least 10 wt.%, preferably at least 20 wt.% isocyanate-reactive compounds. The composition further comprises beside the blowing agents having a lambda gas value of less than or equal to 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C additional blowing agents having a lambda gas value of greater than 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C and the ratio of blowing agents having a lambda gas value of less than or equal to 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C to the additional blowing agents is in the weight ratio 95/5-5/95 calculated on the total weight of all blowing agents. Preferred Components: The molar amount of epoxy compounds in the reactive composition is preferably at least 10 times, more preferably at least 15 times higher than the molar amount of carbon dioxide formed by the water present in the reactive composition after reaction with isocyanates. The maximum amount of all epoxy compounds in the composition is less than 25 wt.%, preferably less than 20 wt.% calculated on the total weight of the composition. The epoxy compound comprises epoxy compounds having equivalent weight lower than 300 g/mol, preferably lower than 250 g/mol, more preferably lower than 200 g/mol and the epoxy compound used is liquid at 20 degrees C. The physical blowing agent having a lambda gas value of less than or equal to 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C comprises: hydrofluoroolefin blowing agent and/or hydrochlorofluoroolefin blowing agent and/or hydrocarbon blowing agent having cyclopentane and its mixtures; or chlorofluorocarbons and/or hydrofluorocarbons and/or hydrochlorofluorocarbons. The polyisocyanate compounds comprise a toluene diisocyanate, a methylene diphenyl diisocyanate and/or a polyisocyanate composition comprising a methylene diphenyl diisocyanate. The isocyanate reactive compounds comprise polyols and polyol mixtures having average hydroxy numbers of 50-1000, preferably 150-700 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and hydroxy functionalities of 2-8, especially 3-8. The blowing agent is present in an amount of 1-60 pts. wt., preferably 2-45 pts. wt. per 100 pts. wt. isocyanate reactive compounds. Preferred Process: The process further includes: sealing the foam with a gas diffusion tight sealing, where at least 50%, preferably 90-100% of the foam surfaces are covered with the gas diffusion tight sealing, the gas diffusion tight sealing comprises metal foils having aluminum foil or metal multilayers comprising aluminum foil and/or gas barrier polymer layers having ethylene vinyl alcohol copolymer, polyvinyl alcohol and its copolymers, polyvinylidene chloride, polyamide, polyethylene terephthalate, polyketones and/or polyacrilonitriles and/or a thermoplastic polymer having polyethylene and/or polypropylene; and after sealing the foam a step of aging the foam, where the aging step includes keeping the foam at a given temperature above room temperature until a stable low ~ value is obtained, preferably at a temperature of 25-100 degrees C, more preferably less than 1 day.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of: 80.16 pts. wt. Stepanpol PS 2352 (RTM: aromatic polyester polyol with hydroxy value of 240 mg potassium hydroxide/g); 16 pts. wt. tris(chloroisopropyl) phosphate; 0.1 pts. wt. pentamethyldiethylenetriamine; 1.36 pts. wt. PT P
PN US2021363032-A1; WO2021236641-A1; US11247919-B2
TI Treating sour water comprises e.g. converting hydrosulfide ions into sulfate ions in anodic chamber, generating carbon dioxide in chamber, reacting carbon dioxide with hydroxide ions in chamber and discharging anodic chamber solution.
AU RAYNEL G R J
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2021D4902R
AB    NOVELTY - Treating sour water (102) comprises: providing sour water comprising hydrosulfide ions and a carbon-containing compound to an anodic chamber of an electrolyzer vessel, where the electrolyzer vessel comprises the anodic chamber and a cathodic chamber separated by a diaphragm (110); generating an electric potential between an anode in the anodic chamber and a cathode (108) in cathodic chamber via an electric power source; converting the hydrosulfide ions into sulfate ions in anodic chamber via an oxido half-reaction of a first oxido-reduction reaction; generating carbon dioxide in the anodic chamber via an oxido half-reaction of a second oxido-reduction reaction associated with the carbon-containing compound; reacting the carbon dioxide with hydroxide ions in anodic chamber to generate bicarbonate ions; and discharging an anodic chamber solution from the electrolyzer vessel from anodic chamber as a discharged anodic-chamber solution comprising the sulfate ions and bicarbonate ions.
   USE - The method is useful for treating sour water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: facilitate the sequential transformation of hydrosulfide ions into sulfite ions; utilizes typical nanofiltration membrane that exhibit a rejection rate of bisulfate and sulfate of 97%; and utilizes reducing the pH below 5 that greatly decreases the rejection rate of bisulfate and sulfate, which results in greater amounts of the bisulfates and sulfates passed through the membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a sour-water treatment system (100) comprising an electrolyzer vessel to receive electricity from an electric power source to generate an electric potential between an anode and a cathode in the electrolyzer vessel, where the electrolyzer vessel comprises an anodic chamber to receive sour water comprising hydrosulfide ions and a carbon-containing compound, the anodic chamber comprises the anode to convert the hydrosulfide ions into sulfate ions via an oxido half-reaction of a first oxido-reduction reaction and generate carbon dioxide via an oxido half-reaction of a second oxido-reduction reaction associated with the carbon-containing compound, the carbon dioxide to react with hydroxide ions to generate bicarbonate ions and a separation system comprising membranes to remove the bicarbonate ions and water from a discharged anodic-chamber solution discharged from the anodic chamber to give a processed discharged anodic-chamber solution comprising a greater concentration of sulfate ions than in the discharged anodic-chamber solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates diagram of a sour-water treatment system.
   Sour-water treatment system (100)
   Sour water (102)
   Diaphragm electrolyzer (104)
   Cathode (108)
   Diaphragm (110)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises: adding a metal hydroxide to the anodic chamber to maintain pH of at least 7.5 in the electrolyzer vessel, where the diaphragm comprises a cation-exchange diaphragm, and the sour water comprises ammonium hydrosulfide; removing bicarbonate ions and water from the discharged anodic-chamber solution to give a processed discharged anodic-chamber solution having a greater concentration of sulfate ions than in the discharged anodic-chamber solution; adding sulfate ions to the processed discharged anodic-chamber solution; providing the processed discharged anodic-chamber solution to the cathodic chamber of the electrolyzer vessel; converting the sulfate ions into sulfite ions in the cathodic chamber via a reduction half-reaction of the first oxido-reduction reaction; and discharging a cathodic chamber solution comprising the sulfite ions as a discharged cathodic-chamber solution; removing water from the discharged cathodic-chamber solution to give a slurry comprising solid metal sulfite; and filtering the solid metal sulfite from the slurry giving filtrate comprising sulfite ions; and reacting hydrogen cyanide with hydroxide ions in the anodic chamber to give cyanide ions, where the carbon-containing compound comprises the hydrogen cyanide. The adding the sulfate ions comprise: adding sodium sulfate to the processed discharged anodic-chamber solution; and adding sulfate ions discharged from a second sour-water treatment system comprising a second electrolyzer vessel without a diaphragm or membrane. The removing the bicarbonate ions comprises removing the bicarbonate ions from the discharged anodic-chamber solution as a first permeate in a first nanofiltration system comprising membranes giving a first retentate comprising the sulfate ions. The removing the water comprises removing the water as a second permeate from the first retentate via a second nanofiltration system comprising membranes giving a second retentate comprising the processed discharged anodic-chamber solution. The discharged cathodic-chamber solution comprises sodium sulfite comprising the sulfite ions, where the sodium sulfite dissolved in the discharged cathodic-chamber solution. The second oxido-reduction reaction comprises an oxido-reduction reaction of the carbon-containing compound. The second oxido-reduction reaction comprises an oxido-reaction of an ion from the carbon-containing compound.Preferred Components: The metal hydroxide comprises sodium hydroxide, where the discharged anodic-chamber solution comprises sodium sulfate comprising the sulfate ions, and the discharged anodic-chamber solution comprises sodium bicarbonate comprising the bicarbonate ions. The solid metal sulfite comprises solid sodium sulfite, and the filtrate comprising sulfite ions comprises ammonium sulfite. The carbon-containing compound comprises a phenolic compound. The carbon-containing compound comprises hydrogen cyanide, and the ion is a cyanide ion. The carbon-containing compound comprises a first carbon-containing compound that is hydrogen cyanide and a second carbon-containing compound that is a phenolic compound. The system comprises: a flow control valve to add a metal hydroxide to the anodic chamber to maintain pH of at least 7.5 in the electrolyzer vessel, where the sour water comprises hydrogen sulfide and/or ammonium hydrosulfide; the energy source; and a vessel to add sulfate ions to the processed discharged anodic-chamber solution; a second sour-water treatment system to add sulfate ions to the processed discharged anodic-chamber solution, where the second sour-water treatment system comprises a second electrolyzer vessel different than the electrolyzer vessel; a crystallizer vessel to remove water from a discharged cathodic-chamber solution comprising the sulfite ions discharged from the cathodic chamber to give a slurry comprising solid metal sulfite; and a filter to remove the solid metal sulfite from the slurry giving filtrate comprising sulfite ions. The electrolyzer vessel comprises a cathodic chamber to receive the processed discharged anodic-chamber solution, and the cathodic chamber comprises a cathode to convert the sulfate ions into sulfite ions via a reduction half-reaction of the first oxido-reduction reaction. The electrolyzer vessel comprises a diaphragm separating the anodic chamber from the cathodic chamber, and the cathodic chamber to discharge a cathodic chamber solution as a discharged cathodic-chamber solution comprising the sulfite ions comprising sodium sulfite dissolved in the discharged cathodic-chamber solution. The separation system comprises: a first nanofiltration system comprising membranes to remove the bicarbonate ions as a first permeate from the discharged anodic-chamber solution giving a first retentate comprising the sulfate ions; and a second nanofiltration system comprising membranes to remove the water as a second permeate from the first retentate giving a second retentate comprising the processed discharged anodic-chamber solution. The carbon-containing compound comprises a first carbon-containing compound that is hydrogen cyanide and a second carbon-containing compound that is a phenolic compound. The second oxido-reduction reaction comprises: an oxido-reduction reaction of the first carbon-containing compound that is a phenolic compound; and an oxido-reaction of a cyanide ion from the second carbon-containing compound that is hydrogen cyanide.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN113648975-A
TI Ionic liquid/natural porous inorganic mineral composite material used in carbon dioxide capture, comprises traditional ionic liquid or eutectic solvent DES, natural porous inorganic minerals and gas adsorbing materials.
AU LI X
   SHEN J
   JIANG H
   SUN W
   XIA G
   LIN S
   WU X
   ZHANG X
   ZHENG W
   FANG D
   XU G
AE UNIV YANCHENG TEACHERS (UAET-C)
GA 2021D6645H
AB    NOVELTY - An ionic liquid/natural porous inorganic mineral composite material comprises traditional ionic liquid or eutectic solvent DES as gas trapping and confinement materials, natural porous inorganic minerals as supporting materials and gas adsorbing materials. The ionic liquids are composed of organic cations and anions, and DES is a mixture of hydrogen bond acceptors and hydrogen bond donors. The natural porous inorganic minerals includes perlite, volcanic stone, sepiolite, diatomite, zeolite, montmorillonite, attapulgite and/or expanded vermiculite. The loading amount of ionic liquid or eutectic solvent is 1-25% of the mass of natural porous inorganic minerals.
   USE - Ionic liquid/natural porous inorganic mineral composite material used in carbon dioxide capture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product is suitable for carbon peaking and carbon neutralization. It uses low-cost and easy to obtain raw materials, has simple preparation process, has low equipment requirements. The prepared YCTC2 composite material has a significant improvement in the effect of other carbon dioxide gas adsorption materials reported publicly, and can be used for technical carbon fixation. The composite material is easy to recycle, reusable, environment-friendly, and suitable for industrialization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An ionic liquid/natural porous inorganic mineral composite material comprises traditional ionic liquid or eutectic solvent DES as gas trapping and confinement materials, natural porous inorganic minerals as supporting materials and gas adsorbing materials. The ionic liquids are composed of organic cations and anions, and DES is a mixture of hydrogen bond acceptors and hydrogen bond donors. The natural porous inorganic minerals includes perlite, volcanic stone, sepiolite, diatomite, zeolite, montmorillonite, attapulgite and/or expanded vermiculite. The composite material YCTC2 is prepared by sequentially adding substance A (providing organic cations, or hydrogen bond acceptor for DES), 95% ethanol and porous inorganic minerals into the reactor, stirring for 1-2 hours, drying, adding substance B (providing anion, or hydrogen bond donor for DES) and 95% ethanol, stirring for 1-2 hours, and drying via vacuum drying at 100degrees Celsius for 2 hours, or natural or sun-drying. The loading amount of ionic liquid or eutectic solvent is 1-25% of the mass of natural porous inorganic minerals.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for application method for absorbing carbon dioxide comprising putting ionic liquid/natural porous inorganic mineral composite YCTC2 into the gas absorption tank, passing carbon dioxide, absorbing under 0.001-3 bar, preferably 0.1-3 bar at 20-100degrees Celsius, preferably 20-40degrees Celsius, and calculating carbon dioxide absorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Methods: The application method for absorbing carbon further includes regeneratng absorbent after absorption by putting composite material YCTC-2 that has absorbed carbon dioxide in a sealed tank with a pressure of 0.005 bar, and the residual amount of desorption is 0.02-0.05 mmol/g, desorbing at 60-120 degrees Celsius, preferably 70-80degrees Celsius, and regenerating for 0.1-3.0 hours, preferably 1.0 hour.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN111517910-A
TI System useful for preparing low-carbon olefins            from syngas in one step, comprises raw gas pretreatment            unit, reaction unit, deoiling and oxygenated            hydrocarbon unit, carbon dioxide removal unit, drying            unit and adsorption separation unit.
AU HU G
   LIU Y
   YANG Y
   HE G
   WANG J
   ZHENG Z
   SUN Y
   LV J
   LI H
   LI X
   JING X
   WEI X
   GAO W
   LIU Z
AE SHAANXI YANCHANG PETROLEUM GROUP CO LTD (SXYP-C)
GA 202078691J
AB    NOVELTY - A system comprises raw gas pretreatment units,                a reaction unit, a deoiling and oxygenated                hydrocarbon unit, a carbon dioxide removal unit, a                drying unit and an adsorption separation unit,                which are connected in sequence. The system further                comprises a heat extraction unit connected to the                reaction unit. The syngas produced by the                adsorption separation unit is further passed into                the reaction unit. The raw gas pretreatment unit is                a pressure swing adsorption device or a membrane                separation device. The reaction unit is a reactor.                The deoiling and oxygenated hydrocarbon unit and                the carbon dioxide removal unit are all scrubbing                towers. The drying unit is a drying tank. The                adsorption separation unit is a pressure swing                adsorption device.
   USE - The system is useful for preparing low-carbon                olefins from syngas in one step.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reasonable steps, produces                low-carbon olefins from syngas gas in one step,                omits the syngas-to-methanol device, and reduces                equipment investment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the low-carbon olefins from syngas in                one step, which involves: (i) adjusting feed gas                (S1) by a feed gas pretreatment unit to adjust the                ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide to obtain                syngas (S2), where the hydrogen/carbon monoxide                molar ratio in the syngas (S2) is 1-5; (ii) passing                the syngas (S2) through the reaction unit to                generate 2-5C light olefins, 1-10C hydrocarbon                products and unconverted syngas-product mixture                (S3) under the action of the catalyst in the                reaction unit; (iii) subjecting the syngas-product                mixture (S3) to deoiling and oxygenated hydrocarbon                units, and removing heavy hydrocarbons and                oxygenated hydrocarbons by washing to obtain a                syngas-product mixture (S4); (iv) subjecting the                syngas-product mixture (S4) to the carbon dioxide                removal unit to remove carbon dioxide to obtain a                syngas-product mixture (S5), where the mass                concentration of carbon dioxide in the                syngas-product mixture (S5) is less than 1 ppm; (v)                drying and dehydrating the syngas-product mixture                (S5) by the drying unit to obtain a dehydrated                syngas-product mixture (S6); and (vi) after                dehydration is completed, separating the                syngas-product mixture (S6) from 2-4C mixed                hydrocarbons and unconverted syngas through the                adsorption separation unit to obtain syngas (S7)                and crude product gas (S8), where the syngas (S7)                contains carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and the                crude product gas (S8) contains ethylene, propylene                and butane, and entering the crude product gas (S8)                to a downstream olefin separation unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The raw gas pretreatment                  unit is a membrane separation device. The                  reaction unit is a fixed bed reactor (preferred),                  a fluidized bed reactor or a slurry bed reactor.                  The deoiling and oxygenated hydrocarbon unit is a                  scrubbing tower with a built-in absorption                  liquid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The syngas (S2)                  further includes syngas (S7). The feed gas (S1)                  contains carbon monoxide and hydrogen components,                  and preferably contains carbon dioxide, methane,                  argon and nitrogen. The molar ratio of                  hydrogen/carbon monoxide in the syngas gas (S2)                  is 2-3. Preferred Conditions: The reaction                  conditions of the reaction unit are a reaction                  temperature of 300-500 degrees C, preferably                  380-460 degrees C, a reaction pressure of 2-9                  MPaG, preferably 3.5-6 MPaG, and a catalyst bed                  volumetric space velocity of 2000-10000 hour-1,                  preferably 3000-8000 hour-1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for removing                  carbon dioxide is alcohol amine method, alkaline                  washing method and/or physical solvent method,                  where the alkaline solution used in the alkaline                  washing method is sodium hydroxide solution                  and/or potassium hydroxide solution, the solution                  concentration is 5-40 wt.%, preferably 5-20 wt.%.                  Preferred Components: The oxygenated hydrocarbons                  include methanol, dimethyl ether, ethanol,                  propionaldehyde and/or acetone. The absorption                  liquid is desalinated water. The alcohol amine                  solution used in the alcohol amine method                  includes monoethanolamine, diethanolamine,                  triethanolamine, N-methyldiethanolamine,                  diisopropanolamine and/or diglycolamine. The                  absorbent used in the physical solvent method is                  methanol, N-methylpyrrolidone, polyethylene                  glycol dimethyl ether, propylene carbonate,                  tri-n-butyl phosphate, polyethylene glycol methyl                  isopropyl ether and/or formylmorphine                  derivative.

The heated refrigerant in the intermediate heat                exchange device is configured to heat the tap water                at the second heat exchange device. The refrigerant                in the intermediate heat exchange device is                circulated inside the intermediate heat exchange                device.
   USE - New type heat pump water heater.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a vertical section whose top end is connected with a drilling platform. A bottom end of the vertical section is located in an overlying stratum (7) of a natural gas hydrate reservoir. The horizontal section extends horizontally in the natural air reservoir, where a second end portion of the horizontal section communicates with an inner portion of an air storage layer. A production well is provided with a sleeve. The sleeve is sleeved outside the production pipe. A first drilling hole is provided on a pipe wall of the sleeve at a position opposite to a position of the first drilling holes. A carbon dioxide injection pipe is embedded in the production well. The carbon dioxide gas injection pipe passes through a second drilling hole. The first drillinghole extends into a carbon dioxide (CO2) gas injection well.
   USE - Natural gas hydrate exploitation system for combined carbon dioxide gas storage, uses include but are not limited to oil, natural gas, water, and gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The exploitation system not only realizes the exploitation of the natural gas hydrate reservoir, but also realizes the embedding of the carbon dioxide gas, which is good for the recycling of the gas and the safety of the reservoir. The mixed gas is uniformly diffused in the overlying stratum, and the mixed gas diffusion downward effectively improves the internal temperature of the hydrocarbon reservoir and natural gas migration channel. It effectively promotes the decomposition of hydrocarbon in the reservoir, avoids the hydrate blocking of the migration channel caused by natural gas and formation water in the mining process, and reduces the exploitation risk of hydrate, laying the foundation for the commercial exploitation of hydrocarbons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the natural gas hydrate exploitation method of combined CO gas storage.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Amino bisphenol tetradentate trivalent                chromium metal complex i.e.                2-(2-hydroxybenzylamino)phenol-chromium compound of                formula (I) is new.
   R1 = sterically hindered substituent,                preferably tert-butyl group or tert-amyl group;                and
   R2 = general alkyl substituent, preferably                methyl group and tert-butyl group.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. preparation of (I);
   2. regeneration of the amino bisphenol                tetradentate trivalent chromium metal complex                comprising dissolving modified amino bisphenol                tetradentate chromium complex that has been exposed                to air for a long time in tetrahydrofuran solvent,                and stirring for 0.5 hours in a nitrogen                atmosphere, and removing the tetrahydrofuran                solvent under reduced pressure to obtain                regenerated aminobisphenol tetradentate chromium                complex;
   3. use of (I) comprising taking (I) as a                catalyst, and bis(triphenylphosphine)iminium                chloride, bis(triphenylphosphoranylidene)ammonium                azide (PPNN3), and phenylpyridine N-oxide as                co-catalysts, and catalytic copolymerization of                carbon dioxide and epoxy cyclohexane, the molar                ratio of catalyst to epoxy cyclohexane is                1:500-1000, the molar ratio between catalyst and                cocatalyst is 1:1, the reaction conditions during                the copolymerization is 0&#8451; and 4 MPa                carbon dioxide pressure; and
   4. use of amino bisphenol tetradentate                trivalent chromium complex for catalyzing the                copolymerization of carbon dioxide and alkylene                oxide, comprising (I) as a catalyst, and                bis(triphenylphosphine)iminium chloride,                bis(triphenylphosphoranylidene)ammonium azide                (PPNN3), and phenylpyridine N-oxide as                co-catalysts, and catalyzing the copolymerization                of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide, the molar                ratio of catalyst to propylene oxide is 1:500-1000,                the molar ratio between catalyst and cocatalyst is                1:1, and the reaction conditions during the                copolymerization is normal temperature                25&#8451; and carbon dioxide pressure of 4                MPa.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) reacting phenol, formaldehyde                  and diamine compound using water as solvent to                  obtain aminobisphenol tetradentate ligand, (ii)                  stirring ligand and sodium hydride in                  tetrahydrofuran under the conditions of nitrogen                  and -78&#8451; for five minutes, then                  naturally heating to room temperature, and                  reacting for one hour to obtain ligand precursor,                  and (iii) stirring ligand precursor and                  chromium(III) chloride tetrahydrofuran complex in                  tetrahydrofuran for 5 minutes, heating to normal                  temperature, stirring for 18 hours, then removing                  the solvent under reduced pressure, extracting                  with toluene, filtering through celite, and                  removing the solvent in the filtrate by reduced                  pressure, washing the solid with hexane,                  filtering and drying to obtain final                  product.

The coking device utilizes pure                oxygen, and air and/or carbon dioxide to obtain                combustion-supporting gas for coking production. The                gas processing unit separates mixed gas to obtain                synthesis gas.

   ADVANTAGE - The flow adjusting unit adjusts the                concentration of oxygen in the                combustion-supporting gas to have oxygen                concentration greater than that in air, thus                realizing oxygen-enriched combustion and reducing                generation of nitrogen oxide in coke oven flue gas.                The carbon dioxide is enriched in waste gas after                combustion, which is good for capturing and using                carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide and water vapor                in the coke oven flue gas are processed by gas                activation unit and gas processing unit to obtain                synthesis gas, where the synthesis gas can be used                as chemical raw material, thus reducing emission of                carbon dioxide, changing waste into valuable                components, and realizing low carbon emission                reduction and green production.

TI Freezing fresh foods in refrigerating equipment involves cleaning fresh food and removing water from food surface, subjecting clean food to pre-cooling in high-pressure sample container, and injecting container with liquid carbon dioxide.
AU SUN D
   LI D
   ZHU Z
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2018150391
AB    NOVELTY - Freezing fresh foods involves cleaning the fresh food and removing water from the food surface. The clean food are subjected to pre-cooling in high-pressure sample container, which is then injected with liquid carbon dioxide and pressure is increased to 50-210 megapascal, and maintained. The liquid carbon dioxide is returned to the storage tank after the completion of pressure maintenance treatment. The fresh food in the high pressure sample container is taken out and stored in a cold storage room for long-term storage.
   USE - Method for freezing fresh foods in refrigerating equipment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to freeze fresh foods, which slows down the oxidation rate of unsaturated fatty acid in fresh meat, reduces the degree of contamination during thawing process, allows better maintenance of the quality of fresh foods.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for refrigerating equipment for freezing fresh foods, comprises refrigeration temperature control system, high pressure freezer and liquid carbon dioxide storage tank, where high pressure freezer includes chamber with two open ends, outer side of the chamber is provided with a jacket, refrigerant chamber is formed between the inner wall of the jacket and outer wall of the chamber, end of the opening of chamber is provided with a movable first plug, second open end of the chamber is provided with second plug, and second plug is provided with liquid carbon dioxide inlet set with the interior of the chamber;liquid carbon dioxide storage tank includes a tank body and jacket disposed on the outer side of the tank body, where top of the tank body is provided with opening, and opening passes through pipeline and second plug, and liquid carbon dioxide inlet on the head is connected, and the pipeline is a pressurized liquid carbon dioxide pipeline and pump is connected to the inlet of refrigerant chamber and refrigerating device with circulation pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a refrigerating equipment
   Refrigeration temperature control system (1)
   Pump (2)
   Cooling medium cavity (4)
   Second plug (7)
   Pressure gage (16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the cold storage is less than or equal to -18 degrees C, temperature after the liquid carbon dioxide is pre-cooled and maintained to 0 to -20 degrees C, and temperature in the sample container after pre-cooling temperature is maintained between 0 to -20 degrees C and pressure-retaining treatment is performed for 10-20 minutes. Preferred Components: The fresh food is selected from fish, shrimp, crab, shellfish, chicken, pork, beef, duck, goose, rabbit, mutton, or dog meat.

TI Upgrading biogas from anaerobic fermentation of            biological material, by adding hydrogen gas to            fermentation, subjecting biogas to desulfurization, and            desulfurized biogas to sorption, and regenerating            calcium oxide in endothermic reaction.
AU RAAHEIM A
   MEYER J
   ANDRESEN B
   DI GUILIO N
   ULLEBERG O
   RAHEIM A
   GIULIO N D
   MEIER J
   ANDERSON B
   RHEIM A N
   DI GIULIO N
AE ZEG POWER AS (ZEGP-Non-standard)
   ZEG POWER AS (ZEGP-Non-standard)
   ZEG POWER AS (ZEGP-Non-standard)
   ZEG POWER CO LTD (ZEGP-Non-standard)
   ZEG POWER AS (ZEGP-Non-standard)
GA 201805619Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for upgrading biogas from anaerobic                fermentation of biological material, involves                adding hydrogen gas to the fermentation step to                enhance the methane:carbon dioxide ratio in the raw                biogas produced, and subjecting the raw biogas to a                step of desulfurization to the extent, and further                subjecting at least a part of the desulfurized raw                biogas to a step of sorption enhanced reforming                without separation of carbon dioxide, using calcium                oxide as an absorbent to capture carbon dioxide                from the raw biogas, and regenerating calcium oxide                in an endothermic reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for upgrading biogas from                anaerobic fermentation of biological material                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method upgrades biogas from anaerobic                fermentation in a cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for upgrading biogas (13) from                anaerobic fermentation of biological material under                production of energy rich gases chosen from methane                and hydrogen (15) or their combinations, involves                adding hydrogen gas to the fermentation step to                enhance the methane:carbon dioxide ratio in the raw                biogas produced, and subjecting the raw biogas to a                step of desulfurization to the extent, and further                subjecting at least a part of the desulfurized raw                biogas to a step of sorption enhanced reforming                without separation of carbon dioxide, using calcium                oxide as an absorbent to capture carbon dioxide                from the raw biogas as well as carbon dioxide                released in the reforming reaction, and                regenerating calcium oxide in an endothermic                reaction directly or indirectly using at least                partially provided heat, thus producing                substantially pure hydrogen and carbon dioxide. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device (60),                which comprises a digester (50), a device for                desulfurization, and a reformer unit (70,80) where                the reforming unit is arranged downstream of the                digester and the device for reforming according to                sorption enhanced reforming principle based on                calcium-looping is inherently integrated with                carbon capture and subsequent purification of the                hydrogen with a hydrogen purification unit                (100).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                device.
   Biogas (13)
   Water (14)
   Hydrogen (15)
   Digester (50)
   Device (60)
   Reformer unit (70,80)
   Hydrogen purification unit (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The step of                  desulfurization is conducted upstream of the step                  of sorption enhanced reforming. The partial flow                  of desulfurized raw biogas is subjected to                  treatment in a carbon dioxide separation unit,                  thus providing one discharge flow of vehicle                  grade biomethane and one discharge flow                  containing carbon dioxide. The substantially pure                  hydrogen is subjected to a step of further                  purification to produce vehicle grade hydrogen.                  The heat required for regenerating calcium oxide                  is provided by burning a gas containing a partial                  flow of desulfurized raw biogas, where the heat                  for regeneration of calcium oxide is provided in                  part from a solid oxide fuel cell charged by                  hydrogen from the sorption enhanced reforming                  step, by desulfurized raw biogas or their                  combinations. The additional heat for                  regeneration of calcium oxide is provided by a                  heater charged with hydrogen discharged from the                  sorption enhanced reforming step. The heat medium                  is circulated in a closed loop between at least                  solid oxide fuel cell, heater and regenerator.                  The raw biogas to be upgraded is produced in a                  digestion process based on digestion of a raw                  material chosen from one or more of sewage,                  sludge, municipal waste, domestic waste, animal                  waste, and agricultural waste. The addition of                  hydrogen to the digestion process is arranged at                  least in part as a recycle hydrogen flow from the                  reforming step. Preferred Device: The carbon                  dioxide separation unit is one using a principle                  for separation chosen among water (14) scrubbing,                  physical or chemical absorption using organic                  solvents, pressure swing adsorption, and                  permeation using membranes. The solid oxide fuel                  cell is arranged to receive at least part of the                  gas discharged from the reformer, which is                  subjected to purification in a hydrogen                  purification unit, and charged to a solid oxide                  fuel cell for production of electric energy and                  heat. The part of the gas discharged from the                  desulfurizer is charged to a carbon dioxide                  separation unit to split the gas flow into a                  vehicle grade biomethane and pure carbon                  dioxide.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

AB    NOVELTY - The device has two supporting columns (2)                fixed in a greenhouse shed body (1). Lower sides of                the supporting columns are provided with two                connecting plates (6). Two second connecting plates                are fixedly connected between end parts of the                connecting plates close to each other. Bottom ends                of the second connecting plate are fixed with two                mounting blocks (16). Two sides of four mounting                blocks are rotatably connected with two hollow                communicate (8) for splashing carbon dioxide. The                connecting plate is provided with group of a                spraying mechanism, where the spraying mechanism is                used for irrigating a crop.
   USE - Device for automatically controlling                concentration of carbon dioxide in greenhouse for                greenhouse planting technology field. Can also be                used in agricultural field, industrial field and                residential field.
   ADVANTAGE - The device prevents the greenhouse shed in                different areas of carbon dioxide concentration is                not consistent caused by the concentration of                low-concentration area plant disease resistance and                low yield, so that the quality is reduced. The                device improves the ability of uniformly dispersing                the carbon dioxide in the hollow communicate, so as                to further improve the crop yield.

PT P
PN CN112973477-A
TI Carbonate membrane used in carbon dioxide separation equipment for high-temperature carbon dioxide gas separation, is prepared by adding mixed solution of samarium nitrate and cerium nitrate dropwise to oxalic acid precipitant.
AU CHEN T
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA TECHNOLOGY (UYIM-C)
GA 202172125V
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonate membrane is prepared by adding mixed solution of samarium (III) nitrate and cerium (III) nitrate dropwise to oxalic acid precipitant under stirring, standing for aging, and then drying and calcining aged precipitate to obtain cerium samarium oxide powder namely samaria-doped-ceria (SDC) powder. An appropriate amount of citric acid and ethylene glycol is added to a certain mass ratio of lanthanum (III) nitrate pentahydrate and nickel (II) nitrate hexahydrate mixed solution to prepare viscous gel, and then dried and calcined to obtain lanthanum nickelate (LNO) powder. The formed SDC powder and LNO powder are ball milled with a mass ratio of 4:1, and then dissolved into N-methyl dipyrrolidone with polyethersulfone under stirring to form viscous slurry, which is spun, solidified and calcined to prepare the carbonate membrane support. The formed carbonate membrane support is added to the molten lithium carbonate and sodium carbonate mixture to prepare the carbonate membrane.
   USE - Carbonate membrane used in carbon dioxide separation equipment for high-temperature carbon dioxide gas separation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonate membrane has a more uniform pore diameter, a thin tube wall, a larger carbon dioxide permeation rate and is easy to integrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a membrane reactor for high-temperature carbon dioxide gas separation, which comprises carbonate membrane and nickel/aluminum oxide catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The high-temperature mixed gas is flue gas or synthesis gas from a power plant or a coal gasification plant.

AE TAN J (TANJ-Individual)
   CHANGCHUN PLASTICS CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
   CHANGCHUN PETROCHEM CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201903720P

   USE - Purification method of polybutylene terephthalate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short operating time, low energy consumption and environmental protection, and the industrial value of the cookware.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for purifying polybutylene terephthalate, comprises a polybutylene terephthalate solution supply unit to supply a polybutylene terephthalate solution, the polybutylene terephthalate solution comprises hexafluoroisopropanol, polybutylene terephthalate, and the polyterephthalic acid An oligomer of butylene glycol ester, the polybutylene terephthalate and the oligomer are dissolved in the hexafluoroisopropanol, a carbon dioxide supply unit configured to supply compressed carbon dioxide, a high pressure precipitation tank connecting the polybutylene terephthalate solution supply unit and the carbon dioxide supply unit to receive the polybutylene terephthalate solution and the compressed carbon dioxide, the high pressure precipitation tank is arranged to contact the polybutylene terephthalate solution with the compressed carbon dioxide, purified polybutylene terephthalate is precipitated, and a portion of the oligomer is dissolved in hexafluoroisopropanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The polybutylene terephthalate solution is contacted with the compressed carbon dioxide by forming the polybutylene terephthalate solution into multiple droplets in the compressed carbon dioxide, precipitating multiple polybutylene terephthalate particles, and each of the particles has a particle diameter of from about 0.1 micron to about 0.4. micron, supplying the polybutylene terephthalate solution to a high pressure precipitation tank having the compressed carbon dioxide, and washing the purified polybutylene terephthalate using supercritical carbon dioxide. Preferred Composition: The initial polybutylene terephthalate in the polybutylene terephthalate solution is from 1-8 wt.%. The temperature is from 22-26 degrees C. The pressure is from 1100 psi to 1300 psi. The initial weight of polybutylene terephthalate in the polybutylene terephthalate solution is from 3-6 wt.%.

PN SG10201803773-A1
TI Method of synthesizing cellulose carbonate used for fabricating e.g. films, involves mixing solution comprising dimethyl sulfoxide and cellulose with guanidine-based base, and adding alcohol to precipitate obtained cellulose carbonate.
AU LUO H
   LEE A M X
   HUANG S
AE AGENCY SCI TECHNOLOGY & RES (SGST-C)
GA 201914047J
AB    NOVELTY - Method of synthesizing cellulose carbonate involves mixing a solution comprising dimethyl sulfoxide and cellulose with a guanidine-based organic base (I) in the presence of carbon dioxide to form a cellulose solution comprising cellulose carbonate anions, contacting the cellulose solution with one or more organochlorides in the presence of carbon dioxide to form the cellulose carbonate, and adding an alcohol or water to precipitate the cellulose carbonate.
   USE - Method of synthesizing cellulose carbonate used for fabricating films and fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and environmentally-friendly synthesis of cellulose carbonate with improved yield. The method utilizes common and inexpensive feed stocks such as carbon dioxide and organochlorides instead of expensive and toxic materials such as chloroformate. The method is capable of converting greenhouse gas (carbon dioxide) into cellulose carbonate. The method is carried out under mild reaction conditions i.e. room temperatures of 20-40 degrees C and 1 atm.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of synthesizing cellulose carbonate involves mixing a solution comprising dimethyl sulfoxide and cellulose with a guanidine-based organic base of formula (I) in the presence of carbon dioxide to form a cellulose solution comprising cellulose carbonate anions, contacting the cellulose solution with one or more organochlorides in the presence of carbon dioxide to form the cellulose carbonate, and adding an alcohol or water to precipitate the cellulose carbonate.
   R1-R3 = 1-20C alkyl;and
   R4,R5 = H or 1-20C alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The carbon dioxide in the mixing step and the contacting step has a pressure of at least 1 atm (101325 Pa), preferably 1-20 atm (101325-2026500 Pa). Preferred Composition: The water comprises deionized water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic base (I) comprises 2-tert-butyl-1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine, 1,2,3-triisopropylguanidine and/or 2-butyl-1,3-diisopropylguanidine. The protonated form of the organic base (I) comprises 2-tert-15 butyl-l,l,3,3-tetramethylguanidine-H+. The organochlorides are chosen from benzyl chloride, 4-(trifluoromethyl)benzyl chloride, 4-methylbenzyl chloride, 4-(tert-butyl)benzyl chloride, 4-methoxybenzyl chloride, 3,4-dichlorobenzyl chloride, 2,4-dichlorobenzyl chloride, N-(4-(chloromethyl)phenyl)-4-(trifluoromethyl)benzamide, N-(4-(chloromethyl)phenyl)benzamide, 4-chloro-N-(4-(chloromethyl)phenyl)benzamide, and ethyl chloroacetate. The alcohol comprises methanol or ethanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The mixing step is carried out at a temperature of 20-40 degrees C. The contacting step is carried out at a temperature of 20-40 degrees C for 1-72 hours. Preferred Composition: The cellulose comprises microcrystalline cellulose, amorphous cellulose or cotton. The molar ratio of organic base (I) with respect to cellulose-hydroxide in the mixing step is 0.50-3.00. The cellulose solution further comprises cellulose-O- anions and/or a protonated form of organic base (I). The cellulose carbonate comprises repeating unit of formula (II).R = group chosen from groups of formulae (i)-(v).

PN WO2018063102-A1
TI Automatic ventilation control system for use in split type air-conditioner, has control unit for generating control signal, which propels ventilation fan at rotation rate varying in accordance with concentration of carbon dioxide.
AU CHITIPALUNGSRI S
AE CHITIPALUNGSRI S (CHIT-Individual)
GA 201826026F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a control unit (4) for receiving signal from a carbon dioxide sensor (3.1) and an oxygen sensor (3.2) for controlling propelling of a ventilation fan (1). The control unit generates a control signal, which propels the ventilation fan at rotation rate varying in accordance with concentration of carbon dioxide and varying inversely with the concentration of oxygen to enable ventilation through a ventilation opening when the concentration of carbon dioxide and oxygen detected by the carbon dioxide sensor and the oxygen sensor reaches the determined value.
   USE - Automatic ventilation control system for use in a split type air-conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The system allows the user to select an option best-suited for the operational condition of the control unit from a variety of the installation options. The system reduces energy consumption for air conditioning operation and noises caused by the ventilation fan, keeps cooled air within the premise or the target area when the ventilation system is not in operation and enables display of data for the user.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The control unit is a stand-alone control unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an automatic ventilation control system that includes a control unit.
   Ventilation fan (1)
   Inlet shutter (2)
   Gas sensor (3)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (3.1)
   Oxygen sensor (3.2)
   Control unit (4)

PN US2018058318-A1; WO2018042338-A1; CA3034557-A1; AU2017319585-A1; IN201937007318-A; US10260416-B2; KR2019039819-A; CN109790779-A; BR112019003804-A2; EP3507472-A1; VN63642-A; JP2019532219-W; MX2019002408-A1; GC10861-A; ID201906918-A; EP3507472-B1; EA36885-B1; ES2821408-T3
TI System for providing power production utilizing            transport membrane system combustor, has recuperator            heat exchanger configured for transferring heat from            carbon dioxide-containing stream to working stream            comprising recycled carbon dioxide.
AU ALLAM R J
   BROWN G W
   HOFF B
   ALLAN R J
   GLENN WILLIAM BROWN J
   BROWN J G W
   ALLAM RODNEY J
   BROWN GLENN W
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 201816702C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a fuel source provided for                input of a fuel into a turbine exhaust stream                (209). An ion transport membrane system (ITM)                receives the turbine exhaust stream with input                fuel, and receives an oxygen-containing stream. The                ITM is effective for diffusion of oxygen from the                oxygen-containing stream into the exhaust stream                with the input fuel to combust a portion of the                input fuel and to provide a heated carbon                dioxide-containing stream. A recuperator heat                exchanger transfers heat from the carbon                dioxide-containing stream to the working stream                comprising the recycled carbon dioxide.
   USE - System for providing power production                utilizing an ITM combustor for a gas turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides significant cost savings                in equipment costs and operating costs. The system                performs operation of a power producing turbine at                lower inlet temperature to allow possible                elimination of internal carbon dioxide cooling                streams within turbine blades and an inner turbine                casing with consequent increase in turbine                efficiency and reduction in capital cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for providing power production utilizing an                ITM combustor for a gas turbine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system for providing power production utilizing an                ITM combustor for a gas turbine.
   Turbine exhaust stream (209)
   Direct contact cooler (215)
   Pump (216)
   Indirect water cooler (217)
   Air stream (274)
   Depleted air stream (275)
