PT P
PN CN115072750-A
TI Preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate by            purifying lithium carbonate, involves preparing            industrial grade lithium carbonate, uniformly mixing            industrial lithium carbonate with purified water to            prepare slurry, conveying slurry to primary            carbonization kettle and introducing carbon dioxide            gas.
AU CHEN W
AE SUZHOU XINSI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2022C29161
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate by                purifying lithium carbonate involves preparing                industrial grade lithium carbonate, uniformly                mixing the industrial lithium carbonate with the                purified water to prepare the slurry, conveying the                slurry to a primary carbonization kettle and                introducing carbon dioxide gas for primary                carbonization reaction, clarifying the reaction                liquid to obtain primary lithium bicarbonate                solution, conveying the primary lithium bicarbonate                solution to the secondary carbonization kettle,                introducing carbon dioxide gas to perform secondary                carbonization reaction, clarifying the reaction                liquid to obtain the middle-grade lithium                bicarbonate solution, conveying the medium-grade                lithium bicarbonate solution to the three-stage                carbonization kettle, introducing carbon dioxide                gas to perform three-stage carbonization reaction,                clarifying the reaction liquid to obtain the final                lithium bicarbonate solution.
   USE - Method for preparing battery-grade lithium                carbonate by purifying lithium carbonate, which is                used for preparing ceramic, medicine, catalyst and                lithium ion battery raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively ensures the purity of                the lithium carbonate product, and improves the                quality of the final lithium bicarbonate                product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate by                purifying lithium carbonate involves preparing                industrial grade lithium carbonate, uniformly                mixing the industrial lithium carbonate with the                purified water to prepare the slurry, conveying the                slurry to a primary carbonization kettle and                introducing carbon dioxide gas for primary                carbonization reaction, clarifying the reaction                liquid to obtain primary lithium bicarbonate                solution, conveying the primary lithium bicarbonate                solution to the secondary carbonization kettle,                introducing carbon dioxide gas to perform secondary                carbonization reaction, clarifying the reaction                liquid to obtain the middle-grade lithium                bicarbonate solution, conveying the medium-grade                lithium bicarbonate solution to the three-stage                carbonization kettle, introducing carbon dioxide                gas to perform three-stage carbonization reaction,                clarifying the reaction liquid to obtain the final                lithium bicarbonate solution, preparing the lithium                bicarbonate purification liquid by the lithium                bicarbonate solution, heating and decomposing the                lithium bicarbonate purification liquid, removing                carbon dioxide gas to obtain lithium carbonate                crystal, centrifuging the lithium carbonate                crystal, washing the centrifuged lithium carbonate                crystal, washing and drying to obtain the                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixing proportion                  of the industrial lithium carbonate and the                  purified water is the ratio of the industrial                  grade lithium carbonate to the purified water is                  1:1.1:22-30. The reaction temperature of the                  first-stage carbonization reaction is controlled                  at 25-32&#8451;. The reaction temperature of                  the second-stage carbonization reaction is                  controlled at 28-35&#8451;. The reaction                  temperature of the three-stage carbonization                  reaction is controlled at 30-38&#8451;. The                  preparation of the industrial lithium carbonate                  comprises taking salt lake brine, adjusting the                  pH value to 3-4, adding sulfate solution, barium                  salt solution, stirring, filtering to obtain the                  processing liquid, adjusting the pH value of the                  processing liquid, making the pH value between                  2-4, performing multi-stage extraction, carrying                  out back extraction by hydrochloric acid,                  continuing to adjust the pH value between 6-8,                  filtering then mixing and stirring with sodium                  carbonate solution, filtering and washing, drying                  to obtain the product. The method for preparing                  lithium bicarbonate purifying liquid by lithium                  bicarbonate solution comprises using extraction                  method to remove calcium ion and magnesium ion in                  lithium bicarbonate solution, removing the                  extraction agent the processed lithium                  bicarbonate solution by means of active carbon                  adsorption, removing borate by selective                  adsorption resin, then filling cation exchange                  resin in the resin column, flowing the lithium                  bicarbonate solution through the cation exchange                  resin, obtaining the lithium bicarbonate                  purification liquid.

PN CN114699891-A; CN114699891-B
TI Carbon dioxide capture device for removing carbon            dioxide in gas flow or separating carbon dioxide as gas            product, has circulating pipeline that is provided with            one-way valve on vertical section of pipeline.
AU WANG K
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202291372L
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capture device comprises a                shell (1), a first feeding pipe (2), a second                feeding tube (3), a circulation pipeline (4), a                circulating pipeline, a mixing assembly, a                discharging plate inserted into the first rotating                shaft, and a first motor provided at the center of                the top of the shell. The first motor is provided                with a rotating shaft that penetrates the top                surface of the housing. The mixing assembly is                fixed on the top end of the rotating shaft. A first                unloading plate is inserted in the middle of the                rotation shaft. The bottom of the casing is                connected with a discharge housing (9) through                threads. Multiple supporting legs (91) are provided                between the discharge housing and the ground. A                one-way valve is provided on the vertical section                of the circulation pipeline.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture device for removing                carbon dioxide in a gas flow or separating carbon                dioxide as a gas product during transportation and                sequeetion in an environment-friendly technical                field.
   ADVANTAGE - The circulating pipeline forms the inner                circulation of the upper flow gas, improving the                capture probability. The device has simple                structure, it is convenient to operate and not easy                to happen internal blocking. The circulation                pipeline realizes internal carbon dioxide flow                circulation, avoiding resource accumulation waste.                The second discharging disc increases the                probability of capturing the agent and absorbing                the contact dioxide. The bottom portion of the                discharging plate realizes the discharge of                capturing capture of agent dioxide after absorbing.                The sound insulation cotton isolates the noise                generated by the first motor and the second motor                when working, the noise pollution caused by the                device using environment is avoided, at the same                time, the material of the sound-proof cotton                partially absorbs the vibration energy transmitted                to the shell, it avoids the vibration passing                through the shell to transmit to the bottom part                causing the ground vibration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a semi-sectional view of                the device.
   Shell (1)
   First feeding pipe (2)
   Second feeding pipe (3)
   Circulating pipeline (4)
   Discharging housing (9)
   Support leg (91)

PT P

TI Electrode catalyst layer used in electrolysis cell and carbon dioxide electrolysis apparatus comprises carbonous catalyst carriers, metallic catalyst loaded on carriers and ion-conductive material.

   USE - The layer is useful for carbon dioxide electrolysis cell, electrolysis cell and carbon dioxide electrolysis apparatus (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The layer: in assemble cell to fuel cell, thus becoming to advance smoothly reduction reaction of carbon dioxide; improves diffusibility, to form finer diffusion layer (meso-porous layer) between gas diffusion layer and layer because repellent ability and porosity changed to promote diffusion of gas and/or evacuation of liquid; has pore-diameter distribution, it is presumed that pores of 5-200 mu m diameters contribute toward improvement of current density; and has low chemical reactivity and high electro conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) electrolysis cell (C1) comprising an anode unit having an anode which oxidizes water or hydroxy ions into oxygen, and an anode solution-flow path through which an anode solution is supplied to the anode, a cathode unit having a cathode which is equipped with the catalyst layer and which reduces carbon dioxide into a carbon compound, and a gas-flow path through which carbon dioxide is supplied to the cathode, and a separator separating the anode unit and the cathode unit from each other;
   (2) carbon dioxide electrolysis apparatus comprising the electrolysis cell and an electric power unit supplying electric currents to between the anode and the cathode in the cell; and
   (3) electrolysis cell (C2) comprising the anode unit, the anode solution-flow path, the cathode unit and a cathode solution-flow path through which a cathode solution is supplied to the cathode, and the gas-flow path, and provided that the anode is so placed between the separator and the anode solution-flow path as to be in contact with them.

TI Air conditioning apparatus for adjusting air components in room, has control unit that maintains oxygen and carbon dioxide concentration in room based on detection results of sensors.
AU TOYAMA E
   HASE N
   HATAMOTO T
AE NIPPON DRY CHEM KK (NIDR-Non-standard)
GA 201828486J
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has a control valve (31,32) controls the flow of the first and second gases containing carbon dioxide and inert gas, and containing oxygen. A first sensor (41) detects oxygen concentration in the room. A second sensor (42) detects concentration of carbon dioxide in the room. A control unit (51,52) maintains the oxygen concentration in room within the range of 12 to 14% and maintains the carbon dioxide concentration in the room within range of 3 to 4% by controlling control valve, based on detection results of sensors.
   USE - Air conditioning apparatus for adjusting air components in room.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus maintains an indoor environment in which a human being can breathe and combustible matter can not burn.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The apparatus has a control unit judging whether or not the carbon dioxide concentration and oxygen concentration is equal to or more than a value within a range of 3.5 to 3.9% based on the detection result of the second sensor, and controls the control valve to stop the supply of the first and second gas respectively to the inside of the chamber and supply the second and first gas respectively to the inside of the chamber by controlling the control valve, if the carbon dioxide concentration is 3.5 to 3.9%. The first gas is inert gas that is mixed with 52% nitrogen, 40% argon and 8% carbon dioxide, and second gas is air. The interior portion is maintained at a positive pressure by supplying the first gas. A conduit supplies a first gas containing carbon dioxide and an inert gas into the room. A third sensor detects a difference in atmospheric pressure between the indoor and outdoor. A control unit maintains the atmospheric pressure in the room at the same pressure as the outdoor air pressure or at a positive pressure of 1 to 2000Pa higher than the outside pressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of the air conditioning apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Air conditioning apparatus (1)
   Protection object division (2)
   Control valves (31,32)
   Sensors (41,42)
   Control units (51,52)

AU WANG Y

AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2022C46425
AB    NOVELTY - An oil recovery composition comprises a                dispersion of capsules in critical or supercritical                carbon dioxide. The capsules comprise an aqueous                solution encapsulated by magnetic covalent organic                framework particles containing a crystalline porous                polymer.
   USE - Oil recovery composition is used in                hydrocarbon-bearing formations.
   ADVANTAGE - Supercritical carbon dioxide of the oil                recovery composition, has improved chemical and                physical properties, making it suitable for use in                enhanced oil recovery applications. Supercritical                CO2 being miscible with hydrocarbons, while                contacting hydrocarbon fluid in a reservoir,                displaces the fluid from the rock surfaces and                pushes the fluid toward the production well.CO2                optionally dissolves in the hydrocarbon fluid, to                reduce the fluid viscosity and cause it to swell,                so that the ability to recover hydrocarbons, is                enhanced, and yield of product is improved..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The capsules contained                  in the dispersion of the composition, have                  aqueous solution diameter 10 nm- 100                  &#956;m.The magnetic covalent organic framework                  particles have BET surface area 700- 3700 m2/g. The amount of magnetic covalent organic                  framework particles in the dispersion, is 5.0                  wt.%.Preferred Process: The preparation of                  dispersion involves adding the aqueous solution                  into the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide                  medium, through a pump provided for adding fluids                  at a temperature and pressure greater than that                  of the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide                  medium. The process involves:i) adding aqueous solution and the magnetic                  covalent organic framework particle, at the same                  time, or ii) adding the aqueous solution into the                  critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium                  before adding magnetic covalent organic framework                  particle into the medium, or iii) adding the                  magnetic covalent organic framework particle into                  the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide                  medium before adding the aqueous solution into                  the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide                  medium.Preferred Method: The method of passing a                  dispersion of aqueous solution capsules into                  critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium,                  involves collecting preset amount of the                  dispersion at a surface of the                  hydrocarbon-bearing formation, followed by                  detecting a preset quantity of the dispersion of                  aqueous capsule solution.

   USE - The method is useful for open culture method of oil-producing microalgae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of contamination by miscellaneous bacteria in the open culture of microalgae through a specific culture method, and does not inhibit the growth of microalgae and reduce the carbon fixation efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microalgae are autotrophic single algae or mixed algae that can tolerate nitrogen oxide and low temperature, preferably Ankistrodesmus sp. SS-B7, Chlorella SF-B1, Ankistrodesmus fusiformis HC-DY4, preferably Ankistrodesmus fusiformis HC-DY4.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microalgae medium adopts BG11 medium, SE medium, and/or BBM medium. The volume ratio of the microalgae seed solution to the microalgae culture medium added in the photobioreactor is 1:20-1:5. The nitrogen oxide is nitrous oxide and/or nitrogen dioxide, the gas containing carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide is self-made, or it is flue gas or waste gas, and the flue gas or waste gas is derived from sulfur recovery device incineration tail gas, and/or catalytic cracking regeneration tail gas.Preferred Method: The preparation method of microalgae seed liquid comprises inoculate the microalgae into the microalgae medium, at pH 7-9, temperature 20-35degreesC, light cycle 24 hours, light-dark time ratio 14:10, under the condition that the light intensity is 2000-20000 Lux, the microalgae seed solution is prepared by shaking culture to logarithmic growth phase. In the light-dark alternate cultivation, the light intensity is 2000-20000 Lux, the light-dark period is 24 hours, and the light-dark time ratio is 14:10-10:14. The carbon dioxide-containing gas introduced into the photoreaction has a volume content of carbon dioxide of 5-40%, preferably 5-20%, and does not contain nitrogen oxide. The reaction temperature of the photoreaction is 20-35degreesC, preferably 25-30degreesC. In the dark reaction, the gas containing carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide is introduced into the gas, the volume content of carbon dioxide is 5-40%, and the volume content of nitrogen oxide is 0.03-0.07%. The reaction temperature of the dark reaction is 5-15degreesC, preferably 5-10degreesC. After 3-5 days of alternating light and dark culture, nitrogen oxide was stopped and cultured to the end of the stable growth period.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN113730959-A
TI Removing pesticide residues in pepper oleoresin by            adding pepper volatile oil to pepper oleoresin, heating            and stirring until pepper oleoresin is completely            dissolved and using pepper volatile oil without            pesticide residue as entrainer for supercritical carbon            dioxide extraction.
AU ZHANG J
   LI Y
   WEI Z
   QI L
   JIANG Y
AE CHENGUANG BIOTECH GROUP CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E6554Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing pesticide residues in                pepper oleoresin involves (a) adding pepper                volatile oil to pepper oleoresin, heating and                stirring until pepper oleoresin is completely                dissolved and (b) adding the pepper oleoresin                obtained in step (a) to the supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction device while maintaining the                temperature after the temperature rise, using the                pepper volatile oil without pesticide residue as                the entrainer, and performing supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction to remove pesticide residues in                pepper oleoresin.
   USE - The method for removing pesticide residues in                pepper oleoresin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides good pesticide residue                removing effect, low pesticide residue, simple                technique, high operation feasibility, high product                quality, and suitable for large-scale continuous                industrial production. The method uses pepper                volatile oil as dissolving agent and entrainer,                using supercritical extraction method reaches the                effect of removing pesticide residue in pepper oil                resin, the pepper oil using safety is obviously                improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  adding 30-100 wt.%, 40-80 wt.% or 40-60 wt.%                  pepper volatile oil to pepper oleoresin, heating                  at 75-90degrees Celsius and stirring for 60-90                  minutes. The step (b) involves (i) preheating the                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device at                  50-60degrees Celsius, then adding the pepper                  oleoresin into the supercritical carbon dioxide                  extraction device and controlling the pressure in                  the carbon dioxide extraction kettle at 8-16 MPa,                  temperature at 30-60degrees Celsius and carbon                  dioxide flow rate at 10-15 l/hour, (ii) turning                  on the entrainment pump, adding pepper volatile                  oil at a flow rate of 20 g/hour for entrainment                  extraction, where the rectification column adopts                  the same parameters as the extraction kettle,                  controlling temperature of the separation kettle                  I at 35-40degrees Celsius and pressure at 5-6                  MPa, controlling temperature of the separation                  kettle II at 30-35degrees Celsius and pressure at                  4-5 MPa, controlling the pressure in the carbon                  dioxide extraction kettle at 8-13 MPa or 9-12 MPa                  and temperature of 30-60degrees Celsius,                  performing extraction for 60-180 minutes, 60-120                  minutes or 60-90 minutes and terminating carbon                  dioxide pump and entrainer pump and (iii)                  obtaining pepper oleoresin from extraction                  kettle, and obtaining pepper volatile oil from                  separation kettle I. In the method, the pepper                  oleoresin has pesticide residue content of less                  than or equal to 0.3 ppm.

AU YANG Y
   XIANG S
   MA X
   ZOU Y
   ZHAN T
AE UNIV FUJIAN (UYFJ-C)
GA 2021B2215Y
AB    NOVELTY - A MOFs-zinc material comprises tetrafluoro-terephthalic acid-containing compound (I).
   USE - MOFs-zinc material used as catalyst for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The efficient non-precious MOFs-zinc material realizes the generation of synthesis gas with a controllable carbon-hydrogen ratio at different potentials, and can be prepared by simple and economical method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A MOFs-zinc material comprises tetrafluoro-terephthalic acid-containing compound of formula: (Zn(TFTP)(DAT)2).DMF.2H2O.
   TFTP = 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-terephthalic acid;and
   DAT = 3,5 diamino-1,2,4-triazolyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of MOFs-zinc material, which involves dissolving water-soluble zinc salt, 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-terephthalic acid and 3,5-diamino-1,2,4-triazole in N,N-dimethylformamide and distilled water, mixing, and carrying out hydrothermal reaction at 85 degrees C; and
   (2) use of MOFs-zinc material for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water-soluble zinc salt is zinc nitrate. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of water-soluble zinc salt, 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-terephthalic acid and 3,5-diamino-1,2,4-triazole is 1:1:1. Preferred Conditions: The hydrothermal reaction is carried out for 17 hours. Preferred Process: The use of MOFs-zinc material involves adding and mixing 600 mu L ethanol, 150 mu L distilled water and 250 mu L 5 wt.% Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution, adding MOFs-zinc material to completely dissolve to obtain a mixed solution, spraying 1 mL mixed solution evenly on the carbon paper with a spray gun, and adding carbon paper to the electrode holder, which is the working electrode.

AU SUN Z

GA 202190072G

   USE - The copper complex is useful for preparing electrochemical reduction catalysts (claimed), and electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The copper complex: has high selectivity to carbon dioxide reduction products; and reaches the Faraday efficiency (FE) of methane and ethylene products 20-50% under 1.1V vs. RHE (relative reversible hydrogen electrode) conditions. The method: has simple process, energy consumption, and cheap synthetic raw materials; and is economical, and suitable for large-scale synthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the metal copper complex, comprising (1) adding organic ligand to the dispersant and dissolving it fully, and (2) adding copper source compound to the system, and preparing the system for full dispersion and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complex comprises copper ions and aromatic ligands in a molar ratio of 1:(1-2), preferably 1:1. The dispersant for preparing the metal copper complex is ethanol, water and/or N,N-dimethylformamide in a volume ratio of 1:1:16. The metallic copper complex has crystal diffraction peaks at 2 theta angles of 11.8 degrees , 17.1 degrees and 27.6 degrees . The metallic copper complex has prominent peak of binding energy (BE) near 955.1 eV, which is designated as copper 2 p1/2. The prominent peak with binding energy near 935.4 eV is designated as copper 2 p3/2, and the single peak near 963 eV and the double peak near 945 eV are their respective satellite peaks. The organic ligand is terephthalic acid, isophthalic acid, phthalic acid, 2-aminoterephthalic acid, 5-aminoisophthalic acid and/or trimesic acid. The copper source compound is copper nitrate, copper chloride, copper sulfate, copper acetate and/or hydrates thereof.

PN US2021054970-A1; EP3795994-A1
TI Preparation of dry compressed gas cylinder used to store isotopically prepared mixtures, by passivating compressed gas cylinder, filling passivated/prepared compressed gas cylinder with gaseous carbon dioxide, and emptying cylinder.
AU JACKSIER T
   MATTHEW M C
   SOCKI R A
   SONOBE J
   ISAJI M
   MCHALE J
   MCHALE J L
AE AIRGAS INC (AIRG-Non-standard)
   AMERICAN AIR LIQUIDE INC (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIRGAS INC (AIRG-Non-standard)
   AMERICAN AIR LIQUIDE INC (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202122902Y
AB    NOVELTY - Quality control method for preparation of dry compressed gas cylinder involves (a) passivating and/or preparing the compressed gas cylinder with the technique to be validated, (b) filling the passivated/prepared compressed gas cylinder with gaseous carbon dioxide to a normal working pressure, in which the gaseous carbon dioxide has a known delta18Oxygen isotope ratio, (c) maintaining the pressurized gas cylinder at a first temperature for a first predetermined period of time, and (d) gradually emptying the pressurized gas cylinder, while simultaneously measuring the delta18Oxygen isotopic ratio, where a predetermined variation in the measured isotopic ratio of delta18Oxygen indicates a properly prepared cylinder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of dry compressed gas cylinder, which is useful for storing isotopically prepared mixtures as calibration mixture for calibrating an analyzer, and storing corrosive gas mixtures.
   ADVANTAGE - The method validates cylinders for use in storage of corrosive and/or reactive gas mixtures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The predetermined variation in the measured isotopic ratio of delta18Oxygen of a standard deviation of less than 0.1%, indicates a properly prepared cylinder. The gaseous carbon dioxide has a natural delta18Oxygenisotope ratio.Preferred Method: The first predetermined period of time is 6 hours to 100 days, preferably75 days or greater. The method further involves (c1) inverting and/or agitating the cylinder, thereby ensuring complete mixing, and (c2) heating the cylinder for a second predetermined period of time at a second predetermined temperature based on the metallurgy of the cylinder, preferably less than 177degrees Celsius for an aluminum cylinder and less than 260degrees Celsius for a steel cylinder. The second predetermined period of time is 2-60 days, preferably greater than 5 hours. The compressed gas cylinder internal passivation comprises simultaneously exposing the cylinder to a desiccating temperature and a vacuum for a defined period of time, where the vacuum is less than 20 microns.Preferred Components: The compressed gas cylinder internal passivation comprises a reaction product of a silicon-containing material and an oxygen-containing material and an amount of the reactive gas adsorbed on the reaction product. The silicon-containing material is chosen from a silicon compound of formula: SiR1R2R3R4 (II). The silicon-containing material is chosen from silane, methylsilane, dimethylsilane, trimethylsilane, and/or tetramethylsilane. The oxygen-containing compound is chosen from moisture, molecular oxygen, and/or metal oxides.R1, R2, R3, R4 = H, halo, amine, alkyl, aryl, halogenated alkyl, and halogenated aryl.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111349485-A; CN111349485-B
TI Synthesizing low-sulfur diesel oil lubricity improver by adding unsaturated monohydric alcohol to high-pressure reactor, adding carbon dioxide, stirring, adding eleostearic acid, reacting, and distilling residue.
AU CHENG J
   LI L
   CAO C
   WANG Y
   LI X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 2020633958
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing low-sulfur diesel oil lubricity improver, involves (a) adding unsaturated monohydric alcohol to a high-pressure reactor, and adding carbon dioxide to the reactor to make carbon dioxide in a supercritical state, and (b) stirring, gradually adding eleostearic acid, continuously reacting, distilling the residue under reduced pressure to obtain the improver product.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing low-sulfur diesel oil lubricity improver (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares improver under supercritical conditions with excellent lubricity, low freezing point, low acid value and low dosage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the improver which is prepared by the above-mentioned method, to improve the lubricity of low-sulfur diesel, and when the amount is 150-250 parts per million (ppm), the corrected wear scar diameter of low-sulfur diesel is not greater than 460 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The unsaturated monohydric alcohol comprises allyl alcohol, butenol, pentenol, 2-n-butenol, 3-n-butenol, 2-n-pentenol, 3-n-pentenol, 4-n-pentenol, 3-methyl-3-butenol and/or 3-methyl-2-butenol, where the number of carbon atoms of the unsaturated monohydric alcohol is not less than 3C, preferably 3-5C. The low-sulfur diesel is diesel having a sulfur content of less than 10 ppm and a wear scar diameter greater than 580 mu m. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of unsaturated monohydric alcohol to eleostearic acid is controlled to 0.5:1-3:1, preferably 0.8:1-2:1. In step (a), the carbon dioxide is added to the reactor, the reaction temperature is controlled to 32-60 degrees C, and the pressure is 7.5-13.5 MPa. The pressure of the reduced pressure distillation is 30-150 Pa, preferably 65-120 Pa, and the temperature is 180-220 degrees C, preferably 195-205 degrees C. Preferred Method: The eleostearic acid is prepared by (i) mixing tung oil with alkali, alcohol and water, and homogenously saponifying to remove glycerol to produce fatty acid salt, (ii) acidifying the fatty acid salt, heat-preserving, distilling the alcohol under reduced pressure, and extracting an oil phase, and (iii) washing the oil phase with water, and removing the extractant and water by vacuum distillation to obtain the acid. The step (b) involves dripping eleostearic acid within 1-3 hours, and continuously reacting at 32-60 degrees C for 3-5 hours, where the dripping speed is fast and slow, so that the time for adding 1/2 of the eleostearic acid is not greater than 1/3 of the total dripping time.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PT P
PN CN111303204-A; CN111303204-B
TI New two-dimensional metal-organic complex            containing a thiazole functional group useful in the            reaction of cycloaddition conversion of carbon dioxide            and epoxide to prepare cyclic carbonate.
AU WEI N
   ZHAO Z
AE UNIV SHENYANG NORMAL (USYN-C)
GA 202058083M
AB    NOVELTY - Two-dimensional metal-organic complex                containing a thiazole functional group having                chemical formula of the complex                lead(btdb)(Dimethylformamide), is new.
   USE - The two-dimensional metal organic complex                containing a thiazole functional group is useful in                the reaction of cycloaddition conversion of carbon                dioxide and epoxide to prepare cyclic carbonate                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the two-dimensional metal                organic complex containing a thiazole functional                group; and
   (2) using the two-dimensional metal organic                complex containing a thiazole functional group                comprising taking the epoxide and the                two-dimensional metal-organic complex containing                thiazole functional group in the container, adding                the co-catalyst, stirring and passing carbon                dioxide, making the pressure in the container 1                MPa, reacting at 80 degrees C under high pressure                5-8 hours, where co-catalyst is tetrabutylammonium                bromide in mole ratio, the amount of the catalyst                is 0.5% of epoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): Preparation of the                  two-dimensional metal organic complex containing                  a thiazole functional group comprising using                  4,4'-(benzothiazole-4,7-disubstituted)-dibenzoic                  acid containing thiazole group as the organic                  ligand and metal salt lead(II) nitrate in                  N,N-dimethyl solvothermal reaction in formamide                  solvent forms yellow transparent rod-shaped                  crystals, and obtaining a two-dimensional metal                  organic complex containing a thiazole functional                  group with an unsaturated lead center. Preferred                  Components: The two-dimensional metal organic                  complex crystals containing thiazole functional                  groups belong to the monoclinic system, the space                  group is P21/c, and the structural unit. Each                  lead center is coordinated with four btdb ligands                  and six oxygen atoms of a DMF molecule. Each btdb                  ligand exhibits two coordination modes: bidentate                  bridging and bidentate chelation. Connected to                  the four lead centers to form a two-dimensional                  layered structure. The molar ratio of                  4,4'-(benzothiazole-4,7-disubstituted)-dibenzoic                  acid to Pb(NO3)2 is 1:3-1:4. The epoxide is                  propylene oxide, epoxycyclohexane, allyl glycidyl                  ether, n-butyl glycidyl ether or styrene oxide.                  Preferred Method: The solvothermal reaction                  comprises (a) adding lead(II)                  nitrate,4,4'-(benzothiazole-4,7-disubstituted)-dibenzoic                  acid, N,N-dimethylformamide to the container, and                  stirring at room temperature for 30 minutes, and                  evenly mixing, (b) sealing the container, adding                  it in an oven at 368K for 1 day, (c) slowly                  cooling to room temperature, and allowing to                  stand for at least 1 day, and obtaining yellow                  transparent rod-shaped crystals, and (d) washing                  with solvent N,N-dimethylformamide, filtering and                  drying. In the step (b), the container was sealed                  and placed in an oven at a temperature increase                  rate of 5 degrees C/minutes. In the step (c), the                  cooling rate is slowly cooled to room temperature                  at 5 degrees C/hours, and it is allowed to stand                  at room temperature for at least 24 hours.

PN CN110684907-A; CN110684907-B
TI Leaching uranium from highly mineralized groundwater uranium ore involves analyzing groundwater composition of ore layer, adding carbon dioxide, injecting into underground ore layer, reducing oxygen concentration and adsorbing leachate.
AU LIAO W
   WANG L
   DU Z
AE BEIJING CHEM ENG & METALLURGY RES INST (CNNU-C)
GA 202007397X
AB    NOVELTY - Leaching uranium from highly mineralized groundwater uranium ore involves analyzing groundwater composition of ore layer to determine its bicarbonate content, pH value and concentration of other easily precipitated ions, starting pumping cycle, adding 800-1500 mg/L carbon dioxide to groundwater of ore layer, mixing evenly, injecting into underground ore layer through liquid injection pipeline, reducing groundwater pH of ore layer by 0.1-0.2, controlling time within 15-20 days, adding oxygen and carbon dioxide to leaching agent, mixing, injecting into ore layer for leaching, reducing oxygen injection concentration to 300-500 mg/L, when residual oxygen concentration of leachate reaches 15 mg/L, reducing injected oxygen to 50-200 mg/L, when leaching rate reaches 65% and adsorbing leachate into tower, when uranium concentration of leachate is more than 10 mg/L.
   USE - Leaching uranium from highly mineralized groundwater uranium ore.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The other easily precipitated ions include bicarbonate, divalent calcium, divalent magnesium, sulfate ion, total iron ions and chloride ion. Preferred Composition: The concentration of oxygen and carbon dioxide added to the leaching agent is 650-1000 mg/L and 800-1500 mg/L. When the pH value of the leachate is reduced to 6.5, the carbon dioxide concentration is reduced to 300-500 mg/L, preferably 500 mg/L. If the pH of the leachate continues to decrease, the carbon dioxide concentration is reduced to 100-200 mg/L. If the pH of the leachate decreases to 6.3, the carbon dioxide concentration is reduced to 200 mg/L. The leachate comprises 1-1.2 g/L bicarbonate. If amount of bicarbonate is less than 1 g/L, 200-500 mg/L bicarbonate is added to maintain stable bicarbonate concentration.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: A weakly basic anion exchange resin is used for adsorption. The weakly basic anion exchange resin is JH-1 resin, which is a macroporous structure acrylic copolymer cross-linking skeleton having exchange group of amine and substituted amine (-NHR1). Preferred Composition: The weakly basic anion exchange resin has mass exchange capacity of 2.75 mmol/mL. Preferred property: The weakly basic anion exchange resin has wet apparent density of 0.68-0.76 g/mL and wet true density of 1.05-1.14 g/mL.

PT P
PN CN110195266-A; CN110195266-B
TI Producing functional fiber comprises e. g. adding graphene micro sheet and calcium hydroxide into water and mixing to obtain suspension and introducing carbon dioxide into the suspension for gas-solid reaction.
AU ZHU A
   SHI Q
   XU P
   MAO Z
AE YANGZHOU WEINA COMPOSITE MATERIAL TECHNO (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 201977791A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing functional fiber comprises (i) adding graphene micro sheet and calcium hydroxide into water and mixing to obtain suspension; (ii) introducing carbon dioxide into the suspension for gas-solid reaction, adjusting the pH of the reaction to 7, stopping the carbon dioxide gas, filtering and washing, obtaining submicron calcium carbonate to isolate the dispersed graphene nano-chip filter cake, and drying to obtain graphene nano-chip; (iii) vacuum drying the base resin to remove water and mixing with graphene nano-chip, extruding and granulating by a twin-screw extruder, and obtaining graphene-containing masterbatch; and (iv) mixing the graphene-containing masterbatch with the fiber resin slice, vacuum drying in an oven at 100 degrees C for 12 hours, melting to obtain spinning solution, and spinning the spinning solution to obtain functional fiber.
   USE - The method is useful for producing functional fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has good spinnability, antibacterial and ultra-violet protection function.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of the mixture of the graphene nano-chip and the calcium hydroxide is 6.76:1. In the step (iii), the matrix resin is a polyamide. In the step (iii), the graphene nano-chip comprises 1-5% of the total mass of the matrix resin and the graphene nano-chip after moisture removal. In the step (iv), the fiber resin is a polyamide, the graphene nano-chip accounts for 0.05-0.5% of the total mass of the spinning solution, the graphene nano-chip comprises 0.1-0.25% of the total mass of the spinning solution. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the temperature condition of the gas-solid reaction is 60-70 degrees C. In the step (iii), the heating temperatures of the one-to nine-zone of the twin-screw extruder are respectively, the temperature in one zone is 230 degrees C, the temperature in the second zone is 235 degrees C, and the temperature in the three zone is 235 degrees C, the temperature in the four zones is 240 degrees C, the temperature in the fifth zones is 240 degrees C, and the temperature in the sixth zone is 245 degrees C, the temperature in the seven zone is 240 degrees C, the temperature in the eight zone is 235 degrees C, and the temperature in the nine zone is 235 degrees C, the feeding speed is 18-25 rps, and the extruder speed is 30-38 rps.

PN CN109745828-A; CN109745828-B
TI Monolithic adsorbent for adsorbing oxygen in air, is composite structural adsorbent obtained by stacking dehydrated adsorbent, carbon dioxide adsorbent, nitrogen adsorbent, and argon adsorbent, according to gas flow of raw materials.
AU WANG S
   ZHAO S
   SUN T
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2019445630
AB    NOVELTY - A monolithic adsorbent is a composite adsorbent constructed by a structural adsorbent having a hydraulic equivalent diameter of 15 mm-10 m, length of 50 mm-50 m, and internal diffusion distance of 0.01-0.2 mm. The regular structural adsorbent is assembled by sequentially stacking a dehydrated adsorbent, a carbon dioxide adsorbent, a nitrogen adsorbent, and an argon adsorbent, according to gas flow of the raw materials. The composite adsorbent comprises 20-50 vol.% dehydrated adsorbent, 5-15 vol.% carbon dioxide adsorbent, 0-20 vol.%, preferably 5-10 vol.% argon adsorbent, and remaining nitrogen adsorbent.
   USE - Monolithic adsorbent for adsorbing oxygen in air, and for medical, emergency and industrial oxygen production, in micro-scale, small-scale, medium-scale, and large-scale process (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The monolithic adsorbent efficiently produces oxygen with concentration reaching 30-99%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The monolithic adsorbent adsorbs components which are more easily adsorbed in air according to equilibrium adsorption mechanism, to purify oxygen, has a void ratio of 50% or less, and sequentially removes impurity water, carbon dioxide, nitrogen gas, and argon gas by using various adsorbent combinations. The adsorption oxygen generation process is pressure swing absorption (PSA), vacuum swing absorption (VSA), vacuum pressure swing absorption (VPSA), rapid PSA, rapid VSA, and rapid VPSA. Preferred Composition: The molar concentration of impurity carbon dioxide is less than 2%. The molar concentration of impurity water is less than 5%. Preferred Properties: The structure adsorbent is in the form of a coiled structure made of fiber felt, fiber cloth, and/or paper. The adsorbent has a porosity of less than 50%, preferably less than 40%, of adsorbent module. Preferred Components: The fiber material is a fibrous material having a diameter of 1-100 mu m formed by direct spinning of adsorbent powder, or a supported fiber material formed by adsorbing powder of adsorbent on a fiber carrier. The fiber carrier is a fiber material formed by composite material of silicon oxide and/or aluminum oxide, and has a diameter of 1-100 mu m and 100-1000 degrees C temperature resistance. The dehydrated adsorbent material is activated alumina. The carbon dioxide adsorbent material is sodium-X(NaX). The nitrogen adsorbent is calcium-A(Ca-A), lithium-X(LiX), lithium-LSX(Li-LSX) or calcium-lithium-LSX(Ca-Li-LSX), preferably calcium-A (Ca-A), lithium-X(LiX), and lithium-LSX(Li-LSX). The argon gas adsorbent is silver-A zeolite (Ag-A), silver-X(Ag-X) or silver-lithium-LSX(Ag-Li-LSX), preferably is silver-A(Ag-A) and silver-lithium-LSX (Ag-Li-LSX).

TI Preparing medicinal-grade sodium bicarbonate, comprises e.g. adding sodium bicarbonate, edetic acid or edetate salt and purified water into reaction kettle, stirring, heating to dissolve, infusing carbon dioxide gas and crystallizing.
AU LI Z
   YANG P
   LU Y
   LI Y
AE HENAN JINGKANG PHARM CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2019114018
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing medicinal-grade sodium bicarbonate, comprises adding sodium bicarbonate, edetic acid or edetate salt and purified water into a reaction kettle, stirring and heating to dissolve, infusing carbon dioxide gas for adjusting the pH of the solution, filtering, collecting the filtrate, cooling, stirring and crystallizing, filtering, collecting the filter cake and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing medicinal-grade sodium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes purified water as solvent to remove most of the chloride, edetic acid or edetate can form a complex with an iron salt, and is dissolved in water to remove impurities e.g. iron salts, where the introduction of carbon dioxide can inhibit the decomposition of sodium bicarbonate during the preparation process, simultaneously by controlling the temperature, the decomposition of sodium bicarbonate during the drying process is inhibited, thus produces product with good product quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of sodium bicarbonate, edetic acid or edetic acid salt and purified water is 1:0.001-0.03:4-10. The sodium bicarbonate is commercially available sodium bicarbonate or homemade sodium bicarbonate, where commercially available sodium bicarbonate specifications include industrial grade, chemically pure, analytical grade or food grade. Edetic acid or edetate salt is edetic acid, disodium edetate or calcium disodium edetate. Preferred Method: The stirring and heating dissolving temperature range is 35-80 degrees C. The pH is adjusted so that the pH of the solution is not greater than 8.5, if the pH of the solution is higher than 8.5, the pH can be adjusted by introducing carbon dioxide gas until the pH of the solution is not greater than 8.5. The crystallization temperature in the cooling and stirring crystallizing is 0-20 degrees C. The drying is vacuum drying and/or blowing drying.

PN CN108439383-A
TI Preparing oligo-layered graphite nanosheet by            ultrasonic supercritical carbon dioxide-shear coupling            stripping expanded graphite comprises e.g. mixing            expanded graphite powder, stripping solvent and            dispersing agent and ultrasonicating.
AU CHEN D
   WANG K
   ZHANG R
   LU H
   XU H
   WANG H
   FAN B
   SHAO G
   YANG D
   WANG Z
   WANG J
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 201868226A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oligo-layered graphite nanosheet by                ultrasonic supercritical carbon dioxide-shear                coupling stripping expanded graphite comprises (i)                mixing expanded graphite powder, stripping solvent                and dispersing agent to obtain suspension, (ii)                subjecting suspension to 400-650 w power                ultrasonication for 0.5-3 hours and peeling to                obtain suspension, (iii) placing suspension in                supercritical carbon dioxide neutralization                shearing action for is 1-3 hours to obtain                supercritical suspension, (iv) subjecting                supercritical suspension to 400-650 w power                ultrasound for 0.5-3 hours to obtain graphite                nanosheet suspension, and (v) allowing to stand                graphite nanosheet suspension, fractionating,                centrifuging, and separating to obtain graphite                nanosheet.
   USE - The nanosheet is useful as conductive agent in                super-capacitor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanosheet: has simple and easy preparation                process, strong coupling condition and low cost; is                environmentally friendly, and suitable for                industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                oligo-layered graphite nanosheet is obtained by                above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), dispersing                  5-15 g expanded graphite fine powder in 250-350                  ml solvent, then adding into dispersing agent,                  and ultrasonically dispersing, where mass ratio                  between dispersing agent and expanded graphite is                  1:2-10. In step (iii), reaction pressure is 12-25                  MPa, reaction temperature is 45-55 degrees C, and                  shear rate is 1000 revolution/minute. In step                  (v), grading is divided into three levels,                  centrifugal speed is 3500 revolution/minute, 8000                  revolution/minute and 10000 revolution/minute,                  and centrifugation time for each stage is 45                  minutes. Preferred Components: The stripping                  solvent is 30-70% isopropanol aqueous solution or                  5-50% ethanol solution. The dispersing agent is                  polyvinylpyrrolidone and melamine in a mass ratio                  of 1-3:1-3.

The                piston is driven toward a centerline of the                crankshaft during a power stroke using a connecting                rod (130) and cause the crankshaft to rotate, cause                one power stroke per crankshaft rotation and                produce two power strokes for every single power                stroke that a similar engine is produced if run as                a hydrocarbyl fuel powered internal combustion                engine. The recirculating CO2 system is comprised                with a valve system fluidly coupled with the first                valve of the cylinder head and the second valve of                the cylinder head to collect the used carbon                dioxide. A CO2 storage tank is configured to                receive the used carbon dioxide through the valve                system. A high-pressure gas booster is fluidly                coupled to the CO2 storage tank to pressurize the                used carbon dioxide to a supercritical state                producing scCO2. A working fluid rail is configured                to fluidly connect the scCO2 to the scCO2                injector.
   USE - Closed-loop piston engine system using                recirculating CO2 system with scCO2 as working                fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The scCO2 is expanded very rapidly by the heat                which drives the pistons.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of                single piston.
   Exhaust valves (105,110)
   Injector (115)
   Nozzle region (120)
   Piston (125)

PT P
PN RU2690468-C1
TI METHOD OF REGENERATING SYNTHETIC ZEOLITE WHEN PRODUCING LIQUID CARBON DIOXIDE OF PRIME GRADE FROM UNDERGROUND SOURCES.
AU PASHKEVICH R I
   IODIS V A
AE AS RUSSIA FAR E-GEOTECHNOLOGICAL RES CEN (ASFA-Non-standard)
GA 201959176S
AB    NOVELTY - Invention is intended for industries using high-grade carbon dioxide and can be used in production of liquid or gaseous carbon dioxide. Moist natural carbon dioxide of carbonic mineral water deposits is supplied to gas holder at open valve. From gas holder at pressure of 0.17MPa and temperature of 10C through open valve is supplied to adsorber of moisture with synthetic zeolite of brand KA-SO. At that time the second adsorber is in regeneration. Dry gas is sucked from adsorber by double-stage oil-free carbon-dioxide compressor. After the first compression stage, gas with pressure of 0.4MPa and temperature of 70C enters the intermediate water cooler. Further, the cooled carbon dioxide at 1530C is sucked in by the second stage of the compressor and compressed to pressure of 1.75MPa and temperature of 120130C. Then the main part of gas is pumped into the water cooler, passing, gas is directed for condensation, and a small part of 1.921.95% of the gas flow rate is supplied to the adsorber, through the valve, for zeolite regeneration. Duration of regeneration per day makes 0.921.1 hours. After regeneration and cooling of zeolite in adsorber with duration of 2.83.12 hours, it is included into operation, and working before adsorber is switched to regeneration.
   USE - Chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention makes it possible not to use additional equipment for gas cleaning, which ensures reduction of operating and capital costs.
1 cl, 1 dwg, 5 ex

PT P
PN RU2659839-C1
TI Low-temperature refrigeration machine on carbon dioxide comprises three-stage small-sized compressor, on injection of which there are finned pipelines for cooling vapors after their compression.
AU PORUTCHIKOV A F
AE PORUTCHIKOV A F (PORU-Individual)
GA 201855522L
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to refrigeration technology and can be used in freezing of biomaterials, food products, low-temperature processing of metals and alloys, in technological processes where artificial cooling is required in low temperature range from minus 60 to minus 130 degrees C. Low-temperature refrigerant on carbon dioxide contains three-stage small-sized compressor, on injection of which there are finned pipelines for cooling vapors after their compression, condenser at outlet of compressor third stage, for removing heat from carbon dioxide, followed by its condensation, sequentially located throttle valves and intermediate vessels for lowering pressure, separating steam from steam direction to first and second stages of compressor at intermediate pressures. Process of heat extraction from cooled product located on external surface of heat exchanger-load is carried out during sublimation of carbon dioxide from solid state, due to presence of vacuum pump, providing reduced pressure in hermetic volume of heat exchanger-load. System of throttling and uniform spraying of liquid carbon dioxide followed by its transformation into solid state in form of liquid pipeline with nozzles installed on it throttle. Internal heat exchange surface of heat exchanger-load for uniform distribution of solid carbon dioxide and its sublimation in process of heat extraction from cooled product.
   USE - Refrigerating equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is to improve environmental friendliness, simplify design and achieve lower temperatures while reducing energy consumption.
1 cl, 2 dwg

GA 2022B8569B
AB    NOVELTY - Composite enzyme-assisted supercritical carbon                dioxide method for extracting houttuynin involves                (S1) removing impurities from fresh Houttuynia                cordata, cleaning, air drying, and cutting into                segments, (S2) adding compound enzyme into the                freshly cut H.cordatafor enzymolysis, (S3) freeze                drying after enzymolysis in a freeze dryer, (S4)                pulverizing the freeze-dried H.cordatain a                pulverizer, and (S4) extracting the powder in                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, and                separating in a separation kettle to obtain                volatile oil rich in houttuynin.
   USE - The method is used for extracting vaolatile                oil rich in houttuynin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves yield of H.cordatavolatile                oil and content of houttuynin, and has wide                application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (S1) cleaning                  fresh H.cordata with drinking water, placing the                  cleaned fresh H.cordatain a shady and cool                  environment and air drying surface water, and                  cutting fresh H.cordatain sections with a length                  of 1-5 mm. The pH value of enzymolysis is                  adjusted by using 10 mg/ml citric acid.Preferred Property: In step (S2), adding                  1-3% complex enzyme to the mass of the fresh                  H.cordata.Preferred Conditions: The enzymolysis                  treatment is carried out at 40-50&#8451; for                  1-3 hours and enzymolysis pH is 4.5-6.5. The step                  (S3) involves drying the H.cordatain a freeze                  dryer at -20&#8451; to-40&#8451; for                  12-24 hours. The (S4) involves passing the                  pulverized houttuynia cordata through a 40-mesh                  sieve. In step (S5), the extraction is carried                  out at 40-50&#8451; for 1-3 hours, and with                  the extraction pressure of 15-30 Mpa, and the                  carbon dioxide flow rate is carried out at 20-30                  L/h. In step (S5), the addition amount of the                  freeze-dried Houttuynia cordatapowder in the                  extraction kettle is 100-200 g, the pressure of                  the separation kettle is 5-7 MPa, and the                  temperature of the separation is carried out at                  40-50&#8451;.Preferred Composition: The complex enzyme                  consists of cellulase and pectinase to the mass                  ratio of 1: 1.

PT P
PN CN111589448-A
TI Carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst with ultra-high gas phase selectivity is obtained by adding copper chloride to deionized water, adding sodium hydroxide and sodium borohydride aqueous solutions, filtering, drying and calcining.
AU NING H
   TAN Z
   WU M
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 202085838T
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst with ultra-high gas phase selectivity, is obtained by (a) adding copper chloride to deionized water, adding a sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, and stirring vigorously, (b) adding a sodium borohydride aqueous solution, filtering and drying the resulting solution, and (c) calcining the obtained solid sample in a tube furnace under an air atmosphere and then calcining the sample in a tube furnace under a nitrogen atmosphere to obtain the target material.
   USE - As carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst with ultra-high gas phase selectivity.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst has excellent catalytic effect and high Faraday efficiency performance of the gas phase product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst is obtained by adding 50-80 mg copper chloride to the deionized water, adding 20-30 ml 0.5 mol/l sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, and stirring vigorously, adding 20-30 ml 0.03 mol/l sodium borohydride aqueous solution, suction filtering and drying the resulting solution. Preferred Conditions: The solid sample is calcined in a tube furnace under an air atmosphere at 200 degrees C for 3 hours, and then the sample is calcined in a tube furnace under a nitrogen atmosphere at 300 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain the target material.

AU XIE S
   MA W

   WANG Y
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 202051996T
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst is claimed. The catalyst is                halogen-modified copper electrocatalyst, and the                halogen includes fluorine, chlorine, bromine and/or                iodine.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for electro-reduction                of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to produce                multi-carbon products (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high reactivity and                selectivity, stable catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of catalyst in electro-reduction of carbon dioxide                and carbon monoxide to produce multi-carbon                products comprising reacting in flowing                electrolytic cell using halogen-modified copper                electrocatalyst as cathode, foam nickel or titanium                foam used as anode, saturated silver/silver                chloride electrode used as reference electrode,                passing at least one of carbon dioxide and carbon                monoxide gas into the cathode, and applying                negative potential or negative current for                electrolysis produces multi-carbon products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The copper fluoride                  hydroxide precursor prepared by dissolving                  ammonium bifluoride and copper salt in                  N,N-dimethylformamide, transferring into                  autoclave for solvent heat treatment, washing the                  product and drying to obtain fluorine copper                  hydroxide precursor, the other copper halide                  precursors are prepared by performing ion                  exchange of ammonium halide and copper fluoride                  hydroxide precursor to obtain other copper halide                  precursors. The method comprises maintaining the                  solvent heat treatment at 120-220 degrees C for                  more than 2 hours, washing the product with                  deionized water and ethanol, and subjecting the                  solvent to ion exchange in ethanol solution or                  mixed solution of ethanol and water. Preferred                  Components: The copper halide precursor is                  supported on the gas diffusion layer, and                  carrying out electro-reduction obtains the                  halogen-modified copper electrocatalyst. The                  copper halide precursor includes copper fluoride                  hydroxide precursor and/or other copper halide                  precursors other than the copper fluoride                  hydroxide precursor. The molar ratio between                  ammonium bifluoride and copper salt is not less                  than 0.5; and the molar ratio between ammonium                  halide and fluorine hydroxide copper precursor is                  not less than 1. The copper salt includes                  copper(II) nitrate, copper(II) acetate,                  copper(II) sulfate, and/or copper(II)                  acetylacetonate, preferably copper(II) nitrate;                  the ammonium halide inludes ammonium fluoride,                  ammonium chloride, ammonium bromide and/or                  ammonium iodide. The electric reduction treatment                  potential is -0.2 to -1.5 V vs. reversible                  hydrogen electrode. The carbon dioxide and carbon                  monoxide gas flow rate is 1-100 ml/minute,                  electrolyte includes lithium hydroxide, sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, cesium hydroxide,                  sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, Cesium                  bicarbonate, potassium fluoride, potassium                  chloride, potassium bromide, potassium iodide,                  sodium sulfate and/or potassium sulfate. The                  electrolyte concentration is 0.1-10 M; the                  negative potential is -0.2 to -1.5 V vs.                  reversible hydrogen electrode, the negative                  current is -100 to -2000 mAcm-2. The multi-carbon                  product includes ethylene, ethanol, acetic acid,                  and/or n-propanol.

PN WO2018051198-A1
TI Hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce composition containing oxo-synthesis syngas and methane involves contacting copper-manganese-aluminum mixed metal oxide catalyst with reactant feed comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
AU MAMEDOV A
   REA C
   SHAIKH S
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)
   REA C (REAC-Individual)
   SHAIKH S (SHAI-Individual)
GA 2018232288
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide (CO2) is hydrogenated to produce composition containing oxo-synthesis syngas and methane (CH4) by contacting copper-manganese-aluminum (CuMnAl) mixed metal oxide catalyst with reactant feed comprising hydrogen (H2) and CO2 at H2:CO2 volume ratio of greater than or equal to 0.5:1, where reaction occurs at 550 degrees C and pressure of greater than or equal to 0.5 MPa to produce a product stream comprising H2 and carbon monoxide (CO) at H2:CO molar ratio of less than or equal to 2 and greater than or equal to 5 molar% CH4.
   USE - Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (CO2) to produce composition containing oxo-synthesis syngas and methane (CH4).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: Reaction temperature is 550-650 degrees C, preferably 600-645 degrees C. Reaction pressure is 0.5-6 MPa, preferably 0.5-2 MPa. Reactant feed comprises H2:CO2 volume ratio of 0.5-2, reaction occurs at 570-600 degrees C and 0.5-3 MPa, and syngas-containing composition comprises H2:CO molar ratio of 0.7:1-1.3:1 and 15-25 molar% CH4. Reaction occurs at 550-650 degrees C and 0.5-2 MPa, where syngas-containing composition comprises H2:CO molar ratio of 0.5-2 and 5-35 molar% CH4. Reaction occurs at 635-645 degrees C and 0.5-1.5 MPa, and syngas-containing composition comprises H2:CO molar ratio of 0.9:1-1.3:1 and 25-35 molar% CH4. H2 gas flow rate is 30-70 ml/minute, preferably approximately 50 ml/minute; and CO2 gas flow rate is 30-70 ml/minute, preferably approximately 50 ml/min. Preferred Processes: Syngas-containing composition is used to produce oxo-products. The process further includes removing CO2 from product stream. The process further includes using produced syngas mixture as an intermediate or as feed material in a subsequent synthesis to form oxo-products.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Syngas-containing composition comprises greater than or equal to 30 mol.% CO2. CuMnAl mixed metal oxide catalyst comprises 1-10 wt.% Cu, 1-30 wt.% Mn, and 60-98 wt.% alumina (Al2O3), preferably approximately 5 wt.% Cu, approximately 10 wt.% Mn, and 83-87 wt.% Al2O3. Syngas-containing composition has H2:CO molar ratio of 0.8:1-1.3:1. Syngas-containing composition comprises 5-15 mol.% CO; 55-65 mol.%, preferably 45-55 mol.% CO2; 15-25 mol.%, preferably 25-35 mol.% CH4; and 5-15 mol.%, preferably 10-15 mol.% H2. Preferred Parameters: Catalyst has surface area of 200-300 m2/g, preferably 250-260 m2/g. CO2 conversion is greater than or equal to 30%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Oxo-products comprise greater than or equal to 2C alcohols, alkyl ethers, or dimethyl ether.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

   USE - The method is useful for preparing photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide at a rate of up to 501.8 mu mol/g/hour under the irradiation of the wavelength range of the visible light of a 300W xenon lamp.

Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of PCN-250-Fe2Co and the volume ratio of absolute ethanol is 1:0.1-1:0.2 (mg:ml). In the step (i), the mass ratio of PCN-250-Fe2Co and the volume ratio of ionic liquid is 1:0.4-1:0.6 (mg:ml).

   USE - (I) are useful as thickener in improving                viscosity of supercritical carbon dioxide in                oilfield fracturing.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) improves solubility of thickener in                supercritical carbon dioxide, reduces the amount of                co-solvent, increases viscosity of supercritical                carbon dioxide at low content, reduces fluid loss                coefficient and friction coefficient, and improves                the sand carrying capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Copolymerized branched-chain sesqui-cyclic                siloxane compounds of formula (I) are new.
   R = phenyl, methyl, H, vinyl or ethyl;
   R' = methyl silane moiety of formula                (II);
   R1 = methyl or ethyl or phenyl;
   b = 3-18;
   R2 = methyl, ethyl, phenyl, vinyl or active                hydrogen;
   a = 12-71; and
   R3 = methyl, phenyl, H or vinyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) stirring mixed system of                  cyclosiloxane, end-capping agent, cyclohexane and                  acidic catalyst, filling with nitrogen and                  sealing, and reacting the mixed system at 85                  degrees C for 10-24 hours, cooling the reactant                  to room temperature, removing the cyclohexane and                  low-molecular low-boiling substances to obtain                  side chain functionalized                  co-polydimethylsiloxane, (ii) adding catalyst                  into side chain functionalized copolymerized                  dimethyl siloxane, adding silsesquioxane after                  stirring, heating at 40-60 degrees C and continue                  to stirring for 0.5-2.5 hours, reacting at 80-90                  degrees C for 1.5-11.5 hours, cooling reactant to                  room temperature, adding activated carbon and                  stirring for 1-2 hours, then suction filtering                  and rotatory steaming to obtain final product. In step (i): molar ratio of cyclic                  siloxane to end-capping agent is 7.2-34:1,                  preferably 9.4-26:1; total concentration of                  cyclic siloxane and end-capping agent in                  cyclohexane is 37-86%, preferably 44-78%; molar                  ratio of cyclic siloxane to catalyst is 61-302:1,                  preferably 84-256:1. The dropping rate of silsesquioxane is                  0.2 drops/s-1 to 8 drops/s-1. Preferred Method:                  The step (ii) comprises adding catalyst dropwise                  while stirring, where stirring speed was 300-500                  revolutions/minute, and the dropping rate is                  0.4-1-5.4 drops/s-1.

PT P
PN KR2021043252-A
TI Producing methanol comprises partially removing hydrogen sulfide contained in carbon dioxide-containing methane gas and producing synthesis gas by dry reforming reaction using plasma from carbon dioxide-containing methane gas.
AU LEE Y J
   KWAK G J
   PARKHAEGU
   KANG S
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 2021417942
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol comprises (i) partially removing hydrogen sulfide contained in carbon dioxide-containing methane gas; (ii) producing a synthesis gas by a dry reforming reaction using plasma from the carbon dioxide-containing methane gas obtained from the previous step; (iii) supplying hydrogen to the synthesis gas obtained from the previous step to control the composition of the synthesis gas; and (iv) preparing methanol from the syngas whose composition is adjusted.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high carbon efficiency, and separate process for removing various impurities included in the source gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a diagram schematically showing a process for producing methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (ii), does not require a separate gas composition control. In the step (ii), volatile organic compounds among impurities are converted into syngas, mercaptan compounds and sulfide compounds into hydrogen sulfide, ammonia into nitrogen and hydrogen, and siloxane into silica particles. The step (ii) further comprises (ii.i) removing hydrogen sulfide from the synthesis gas generated by the dry reforming reaction, and (ii.ii) removing solid by-products by filtering, or both of the above two steps are additionally performed. The method for producing methanol from a landfill gas or biogas comprises (a) pre-treating the landfill gas or biogas to dehumidify and partially desulfurize; (b) preparing syngas by dry reforming reaction using plasma at 0.1-5 atm pretreated gas obtained from the previous step; (c) cooling synthesis gas obtained from the previous step to 0-50 degrees C and filtering to remove particulate impurities, and then passing through a dry desulfurization tower to control the sulfur compound content to be less than 0.1 ppm; (d) supplying hydrogen to the synthesis gas obtained from the previous step to adjust the composition of the synthesis gas so that the molar ratio of H2/(2CO+3CO2) is 0.9-1.1; and (e) compressing syngas whose composition is adjusted to 40-100 atmospheres and reacting in a methanol reactor to produce methanol. The step (b), (d), or both are performed using electric energy derived from renewable energy. Preferred Components: The methane gas containing carbon dioxide comprises more than 30% carbon dioxide based on the number of moles of the dry gas of methane. The carbon dioxide-containing methane gas contains more than 500 ppm of at least one impurity of hydrogen sulfide, volatile organic compounds, mercaptan compounds, ammonia, sulfide compounds, and siloxanes. The carbon dioxide-containing methane gas is a landfill gas or biogas. The content of hydrogen sulfide contained in the carbon dioxide-containing methane gas is reduced to less than 50 ppm. In the step (iii), hydrogen supplied is produced by electrolysis of water. In the step (iii), composition of the synthesis gas is controlled so that the molar ratio of H2/(2CO+3CO2) is 0.9-1.1. In the step (d), hydrogen supplied is produced by electrolysis of water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   WANG Y

GA 202058090U
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of triethylene glycol and triethylene glycol monomethyl ether by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises mixing water in mixture of triethylene glycol and triethylene glycol monomethyl ether as retention agent and dispersant, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction for 1-10 hours at 35-100 degrees C under 7.5-25 megapascal (MPa) to obtain triethylene glycol monomethyl ether product, separating triethylene glycol and recovering. TI Preparation of symmetrical urea compound involves carbonylation reacting amine compound with carbon dioxide gas in the co-presence of base and hydrogen silane.
AU YU B
   LIU Z
   YANG G
   ZHAO Y
   ZHANG H
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 202022208G
AB    NOVELTY - A symmetrical urea compound (I) is prepared by carbonylation reacting primary amine compound (II) with carbon dioxide gas in the co-presence of base and hydrogen silane.
   USE - The method is used for preparing symmetrical urea compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The compound contains different functional groups. The method has simple filtration and separation to obtain pure product, provides new reaction system, uses atmospheric carbon dioxide as green non-toxic carbonylation reagent, avoids toxic carbonylation reagents, and does not need to purify and isolate intermediates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A symmetrical urea compound of formula (I) is prepared by carbonylation reacting primary amine compound of formula (II) with carbon dioxide gas in the co-presence of base and hydrogen silane.
   R=cycloalkyl, and optionally substituted alkyl or optionally substituted aryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen silane is alkyl hydrogen silane, phenyl hydrogen silane or polymethylhydrosiloxane. The base is Lewis base. The alkyl hydrogen silane is Et3SiH, Et2SiH2, triethoxysilane or (EtO)2MeSiH. The phenyl hydrogen silane is phenylsilane, Ph2MeSiH or PhMe2SiH. The average molecular weight of polymethylhydrogensiloxane is 200-10000. The Lewis base is potassium tert-butoxide, sodium tert-butoxide, potassium fluoride, cesium fluoride, cesium acetate, tetra-n-butylammonium fluoride or potassium carbonate. The molar ratio of primary amine compound and alkali is 1:1-1:5. The molar ratio of primary amine compound and amino hydrogen silane is 1:1-1:10. Preferred Method: Reaction is done in organic solvent at 30-150 degrees C for 1-36 hours. The pressure of carbon dioxide gas is 0.1-10 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide, dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, acetonitrile, gamma -Valerolactone or diglyme.

GA 201997305R
AB    NOVELTY - An emulsion comprises a continuous phase, a                dispersed phase, a surfactant, and                2-methylimidazole.
