The surfactant is used in an amount of 0.1-0.5                mol/l based on the volume of the continuous phase.                The 2-methylimidazole is used in an amount of 5-20                wt.% based on the total mass of the emulsion.
   USE - The emulsion is useful for adsorbing carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The emulsion used as carbon dioxide adsorbent                can increase the absorption amount and the                absorption rate of carbon dioxide in the                emulsion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the emulsion, which involves mixing                the continuous phase, the dispersed phase, the                surfactant, and 2-methylimidazole, and                ultrasonically stirring to obtain a homogeneous                emulsion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 2-methylimidazole                  is used in an amount of 6 wt.% based on the total                  mass of the emulsion. The surfactant is sodium lauryl sulfate,                  1-dodecyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide and/or                  1-dodecyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride. Preferred                  Method: The method uses the emulsion as an                  adsorbent. Preferred Conditions: The                  ultrasonically stirring step is carried out at                  ultrasonic horn ( phi ) of 20-30 mm, the                  ultrasonic power ratio is 20-40%, the stirring                  time is 2-4 minutes, the ultrasonic treatment                  time is 5-10 seconds, and the ultrasonic time                  interval is 5-10 seconds.

PN CN108410002-A
TI Nano-porous organic framework solid base material by simple solvent heating one-step synthesis useful for carbon dioxide adsorption.
AU LIU Q
AE UNIV SHAOXING (UNSX-C)
GA 2018659109
AB    NOVELTY - Nano-porous organic framework solid base material by simple solvent heating one-step synthesis, the aperture of the material is 5-30 nm, the pore volume is 0.3-1.44 cm3/g and specific surface area is 300-700 m2/g.
   USE - The material is useful for carbon dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; and improves carbon dioxide adsorption performance of existing alkaline material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The one-step synthesis method of the material comprises divinyl benzene is used as cross-linking monomer, azobisisobutyl phthalate is used as initiator, adding basic monomer and organic solvent, and carrying out polymerization under heating conditions, after the end, evaporating the solvent to obtain nano-porous organic skeleton solid alkali material. The basic monomer is vinyl imidazole or vinyl pyridine. The organic solvent is dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, cyclohexane or toluene. The molar ratio of the cross-linking monomer to the basic monomer is 0.5-5, the volume ratio of the cross-linking monomer to the organic solvent is 0.05-0.2, and the mass ratio of the initiator to the cross-linking monomer is 0.01-0.05. The heating condition has a temperature of 140-180 degrees C and the polymerization reaction time is not less than 24 hours.

PT P
PN CN111925880-A
TI Brewing sulfur-free red wine, by freezing fresh            red grape berries to obtain ice peel grape berries,            performing high-density carbon dioxide treatment,            sterilizing, immersing, fermenting by Saccharomyces            cerevisiae, filtering, and filling.
AU XI Q
AE NINGXIA MEIYU WINE BREWING CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2020B5500R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for brewing sulfur-free red wine,                involves (a) freezing the fresh red grape berries                after removing the stems, to freeze the peels to                obtain ice peel grape berries, (b) sending ice peel                grapes into a high-density carbon dioxide treatment                kettle, pumping carbon dioxide for sterilization                and immersion, (c) connecting the treatment kettle                discharge valve, filter, and fermentation tank in                series, opening the discharge valve and putting the                grape juice through the filter into the                fermentation tank, (d) fermenting by taking                Saccharomyces cerevisiae of the grape juice to be                fermented, stirring into sterile warm water,                activating, inoculating the activated S.cerevisiae                to the fermentation tank, fermenting, and when the                sugar content is lower than 4 Brix, the temperature                is lowered to 0-5 degrees C to terminate the                fermentation to obtain red grape fermented wine,                and (e) separately gluing the red grape fermented                wine, filtering, and filling to obtain the finished                product.
   USE - Method for brewing sulfur-free red wine.
   ADVANTAGE - After freezing, the fresh red grape peel is                treated with high-density carbon dioxide, which has                the effect of sterilizing and effectively leaching                the skin components, and no sulfur dioxide is                brought into the process, as compared with the                other methods. Before fermentation, the juice is                separated to separate the skin residue, which                prevents the inferior tannins in the grape seeds                from entering the wine during the fermentation                process, and the process is relatively simple. The                method in which the whole process does not involve                heat treatment, and the product quality is                high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for brewing sulfur-free red wine,                involves (a) peel freezing treatment by passing the                fresh red grape berries after removing the stems,                through a freezer to freeze the peels through a                vibration conveyor belt to obtain ice peel grape                berries, (b) high-density carbon dioxide treatment                by sending ice peel grapes into a high-density                carbon dioxide treatment kettle, pumping carbon                dioxide to maintain a certain pressure and                temperature for sterilization and immersion, (c)                juice separation by sequentially connecting the                high-density carbon dioxide treatment kettle                discharge valve, filter, and fermentation tank in                series, opening the discharge valve and putting the                grape juice through the filter into the                fermentation tank, where the grape juice must be                fermented, (d) fermenting by taking 0.01-0.02 wt.%                of the Saccharomyces cerevisiae of the grape juice                to be fermented, putting it into sterile warm                water, uniformly stirring it, activating at room                temperature for 20-30 minutes, then inoculating the                activated S.cerevisiae to the fermentation tank,                controlling the fermentation temperature at 10-20                degrees C to ferment, and when the sugar content is                lower than 4 Brix, the temperature is lowered to                0-5 degrees C to terminate the fermentation to                obtain red grape fermented wine, and (e)                post-processing by separately gluing the red grape                fermented wine, filtering, and filling to obtain                the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: In step (a), in the                  freezing process of the peel, the freezing is                  performed at -35 degrees C to -30 degrees C, for                  30-60 seconds. In the high-density carbon dioxide                  treatment of step (b), the treatment kettle has a                  pressure of 15-30 MPa, a temperature of 15-30                  degrees C, and a treatment time of 30-60 minutes.                  The filter pore size of the filter in step (c) is                  2-3 mm. In step (d), the S.cerevisiae is a                  commercial dry yeast powder, where the                  temperature of the sterile warm water is 35-38                  degrees C, and the weight ratio of the                  S.cerevisiae to the sterile warm water is                  (1:5)-(1:10). Preferred Components: The red grape                  in step (a) is at least one of Cabernet                  Sauvignon, Merlot, Pinot Noir, Syrah and Cabernet                  Franc.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-E
IP C12G-001/02; C12G-001/022; C12R-001/865

PN WO2019184996-A1; CN110317791-A; AU2019241301-A1; KR2020136463-A; SG11202009826-A1; EP3778880-A1; JP2021519108-W; US2021363497-A1
TI Preparing lentivirus using serum-free suspension            cell, comprises e.g. providing seed solution for            suspension growing packaging cell to produce            lentivirus, and inoculating seed liquid in first            culture solution by placing in culture container.
AU HONG Y
   YAN T
   YING J
   ZHANG H
   ZHANG L
   ZHANG R
AE SHANGHAI CELLULAR BIOPHARM GROUP LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   WUXI CELLULAR BIOPHARM GROUP (WUXI-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI CELLULAR BIOPHARMACEUTICAL GROUP              LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   CELLULAR BIOMEDICINE GROUP HK LTD (CELL-Non-standard)
   CELLULAR BIOMEDICINE GROUP HK LTD (CELL-Non-standard)
   CELLULAR BIOMEDICINE GROUP HK LTD (CELL-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI CELLULAR BIOPHARM GROUP LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   WUXI CELLULAR BIOPHARM GROUP (WUXI-Non-standard)
   CELLULAR BIOMEDICINE GROUP HK LTD (CELL-Non-standard)
GA 201983704F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lentivirus using serum-free                suspension cell, comprises e.g. (a) providing seed                solution for packaging cells i.e. suspension                growing packaging cell to produce lentivirus, (b)                inoculating the seed liquid in first culture                solution by placing in culture container to obtain                first culture which is serum-free cell culture                solution, (c) sub-culturing the first culture with                packaging cells at 30-38 degrees C by monitoring                real-time pH of 6.9-7.4 in first culture, and                controlling dissolved oxygen of 35-55% and/or                carbon dioxide concentration of 2-10% based on                real-time pH, (d) initiating liquid exchange                operation when the liquid exchange trigger                condition is satisfied, and supplementing culture                solution with culture medium to form packaged cell                culture, (e) repeating the steps (c), and (d) for n                times, where, (f) initiating the transfection                operation when the transfection trigger condition                is satisfied, and (g) initiating optionally                translating operation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lentivirus                using serum-free suspension cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lentivirus using serum-free                suspension cell, comprises (a) providing seed                solution for packaging cells i.e. suspension                growing packaging cell to produce lentivirus, (b)                inoculating the seed liquid in first culture                solution by placing in culture container to obtain                first culture (in volume of 3-100 liters) which is                serum-free cell culture solution having seeding                density of 1x 106-5x 106 cells/ml, (c)                sub-culturing the first culture with packaging                cells at 30-38 degrees C by monitoring real-time pH                of 6.9-7.4 in first culture, and controlling                dissolved oxygen of 35-55% and/or carbon dioxide                concentration of 2-10% based on real-time pH, (d)                initiating liquid exchange operation when the                liquid exchange trigger condition is satisfied, and                supplementing culture solution with culture medium                to form packaged cell culture, (e) repeating the                steps (c), and (d) for n times, where n is 1, 2, or                3 times, (f) initiating the transfection operation                when the transfection trigger condition is                satisfied, (g) initiating optionally translating                operation, (h) cultivating the transfected                packaging cells at 30-37 degrees C by real-time                monitoring dissolved oxygen of 35-55%, carbon                dioxide concentration of 2-10% and pH of 6.9-7.4,                (i) initiating the harvesting and liquid-changing                operations when liquid-changing trigger condition                is satisfied, (j) adding culture solution to                culture container to form transfected cell culture,                and repeating steps (h), and (i) for m times, where                m is 1, 2, or 3 times, (k) combining the recycled                liquids to obtain combined virus-containing serum,                and (l) purifying the combined virus-containing                supernatant to obtain purified lentiviral vector.                The culture liquid used in all the above steps is                serum-free cell culture liquid. The liquid exchange                operation is carried out under liquid exchange                triggering condition comprising (i) maintaining                real time pH of less than or equal to 6.9,                preferably less than or equal to 7.05, (ii)                maintaining downward trend of real-time pH by                adjusting concentration of oxygen, air, nitrogen                and/or carbon dioxide, and (iii) maintaining                sub-culturing for less than or equal to 72 hours,                where liquid exchange operation comprises draining                cell-free serum from culture from culture                container, where the volume of culture before clear                liquid is Vq1, and volume of culture after clear                liquid is Vh1, where ratio of Vq1/Vh1 is 3-15. The                transfection operation is initiated under                transfection triggering conditions comprising total                amount of cells of 0.05-2x 1011 cells, cell density                of 0.5-5x 106 cells/ml, cell viability of greater                than or equal to 90%, and total incubation time is                greater than or equal to 72 hours, where the                transfection operation comprises mixing production                plasmid for producing lentivirus with transfection                reagent, then adding to the culture container for                introducing packaging cells to form transfected                packaging cells. The harvesting and liquid-changing                operations are initiated under liquid exchange                triggering condition comprising real time pH of                less than or equal to 6.9, adjusting the oxygen,                air, nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide concentrations,                real-time pH still drops, and sub-culturing for                greater than or equal to 72 hours, where the liquid                changing operation comprises recycling cell-free                virus-containing stock solution located in culture,                where the volume of culture before the recycling of                feed liquid is Vq2, and after recycling of the                clear liquid, the volume of the culture is Vh2,                then the ratio of Vq2/Vh2 is 3-15.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the above steps (c),                  and/or (h), culturing is carried out under                  shaking conditions and by maintaining pH of                  7-7.35. In the steps (c), (d) and (e), culturing                  is carried out for 72-216 hours. In the step (l),                  purification is carried out using ultrafiltration                  and chromatography. Preferred Components: In the                  steps (d), and/or (i), the ratio of Vq1/Vh1 is                  5-10. In the step (f), the multiple plasmid                  transfection comprises three plasmid                  transfections, and four plasmid transfections.                  The culture container is a disposable culture                  container. The total number of viruses contained                  in the combined virus-containing serum is 1x 1012                  Tu. The packaging cell is human embryonic kidney                  epithelial cells.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN US2018200662-A1; US10092875-B2
TI Removing contaminant comprising water from feed gas stream including contaminant, methane and ethane, comprises alternating input of feed gas stream between two beds of adsorbent particle of e.g. zeolite SSZ-36 made from homogeneous mixture.
AU THOMPSON J A
   DAVIS T M
   XIE D
   ZONES S I
AE CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
GA 201855957D
AB    NOVELTY - Removing (M1) contaminant comprising a water from feed gas stream including contaminant, methane and ethane, comprises: alternating input of the feed gas stream between at least two beds of at least one adsorbent particle made from homogeneous mixture and adsorbent particle comprises zeolite SSZ-36, zeolite SSZ-39 and/or zeolite SSZ-45, where the feed gas stream contacts one of two beds at given time by adsorption step and tail gas stream is simultaneously vented from another of two beds by desorption step, contacting in one of two beds occurs at feed pressure of 345-6895 kPa to preferentially adsorb the contaminant from feed gas stream and to produce product gas stream containing carbon dioxide, water, methane recovered from feed gas stream, and ethane recovered from feed gas stream, and feed gas stream is input at feed end of each of two beds, the product gas stream is removed from product end of each of two beds, and the tail gas stream is vented from feed end of two beds.
   USE - The method (M1) is useful for removing contaminant comprising a water from feed gas stream including contaminant, methane and ethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method (M1): removes the contaminants from the gas and selectively retains the methane and the ethane in a product gas stream; utilizes the adsorbent material that produces a product gas stream containing low quantities of carbon dioxide, reduced quantities of water, while retaining significant quantities of the methane and ethane from the feed gas stream and removes the contaminants from a feed gas stream; provides the short total cycle time and reduced specific vacuum power consumption; and is performed in many different types of locations including at refineries, at well heads, and even on offshore platforms or other remote locations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing (M1) a contaminant comprising a water from a feed gas stream including the contaminant, a methane and an ethane, comprises: alternating an input of the feed gas stream between at least two beds of at least one adsorbent particle made from a homogeneous mixture and the adsorbent particle comprises a zeolite SSZ-36, a zeolite SSZ-39 and/or a zeolite SSZ-45, where the feed gas stream contacts one of the two beds at a given time by an adsorption step and a tail gas stream is simultaneously vented from another of the two beds by a desorption step, a contacting in the one of the two beds occurs at a feed pressure of 345-6895 kPa to preferentially adsorb the contaminant from the feed gas stream and to produce a product gas stream containing no greater than 2 mol.% carbon dioxide, at least 10 wppm of the water, at least 65 mol.% of the methane recovered from the feed gas stream, and at least 25 mol.% of the ethane recovered from the feed gas stream, and the feed gas stream is input at a feed end of each of the two beds, the product gas stream is removed from a product end of each of the two beds, and the tail gas stream is vented from the feed end of the two beds. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for removing (M2) a contaminant from a feed gas stream including a methane, an ethane, a carbon dioxide, 20-10000 wppm of water, and 4-1000 wppm of hydrogen sulfide, comprising alternating an input of the feed gas stream between an at least two beds of at least one adsorbent particle made from a homogeneous mixture and the adsorbent particle comprises a zeolite SSZ-36, a zeolite SSZ-39 and/or a zeolite SSZ-45, where the feed gas stream contacts one of the two beds at a given time in an adsorption step and a tail gas stream is simultaneously vented from another of the two beds in a desorption step, a contacting in the one of the two beds occurs at a feed pressure of 345-6895 kPa to preferentially adsorb the contaminant from the feed gas stream, thus producing a product gas stream containing no greater than 2 mol.% of the carbon dioxide, at least 10 wppm of the water, at least 65 mol.% of the methane recovered from the feed gas stream, at least about 25 mol.% of the ethane recovered from the feed gas stream, no greater than 1 wppm of the hydrogen sulfide, and no greater than 1 wppm carbonyl sulfide and the feed gas stream is input at a feed end of each of the at least two beds, the product gas stream is removed from a product end of each of the at least two beds and the tail gas stream is vented from the feed end of the two beds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zeolite SSZ-36 or the zeolite SSZ-39 has a silicon:aluminum mole ratio of 5-100. The zeolite SSZ-45 has a silicon:aluminum mole ratio of 101-400. The zeolite SSZ-36 has a cation as a framework ion and the cation comprises a sodium (preferred), a calcium, a potassium, a lithium, a magnesium or a barium. The zeolite SSZ-39 has a cation as a framework ion and the cation comprises a sodium (preferred), a calcium, a potassium, a lithium, a magnesium, and a barium. The zeolite SSZ-45 has a cation as a framework ion and the cation comprises a sodium, a calcium, a potassium, a lithium, a magnesium, and a barium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The water is adsorbed by the two beds of the adsorbent particle and a carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of the adsorbent particle is lowered by less than 30 mol.%. The two beds of the adsorbent particle are four beds of the adsorbent particle and the product gas stream contains at least 80 mol.% of the methane recovered from the feed gas stream and at least 40 mol.% of the ethane recovered from the feed gas stream. The feed gas stream has a flow rate of 1-100 million standard cubic feet of gas per day (MMSCFD) during the adsorption and the adsorption occurs at an adsorption-temperature from 20-80 degrees C. The method utilizes two beds of the adsorbent particle and further comprising (a) following the adsorption step in one of the two beds and a simultaneous desorption step in the other of the two beds, equalizing a pressure of the one of the two beds and the other of the two beds through the product end of each of the one of the two beds and the other of the two beds at an end of the adsorption and the simultaneous desorption step, and (b) re-pressurizing the other of the two beds having just completed the simultaneous desorption step by sending a slipstream of the product gas stream through the product end of the other of the two beds having just completed the simultaneous desorption step. The two beds of the adsorbent particle are four beds further comprising (a1) following a first adsorption step in a first bed of the four beds, a first equalization step occurs, where the first bed is allowed to pressure equalize with a second bed of the four beds having a lower pressure than the first bed through a first line connecting the product end of the first bed and the product end of the second bed, (b1) following the first equalization step, lowering the feed pressure in the first bed and passing a gas from the first bed to a third bed of the four beds through a second line connecting the product end of the first bed and the product end of the third bed in a providing purge step so that the third bed of the four beds is purged, (c) following the providing purge step, a second equalization step occurs, where the first bed is allowed to pressure equalize with the third bed of the four beds having the lower pressure than the first bed through a third line connecting the product end of the first bed and the product end of the third bed, (d) following the second equalization step, depressurizing a first adsorbent bed to the feed pressure of 6.89-138 kPa through the feed end of the first adsorbent bed in a blowdown step comprising either allowing the gas in the first adsorbent bed to vent to a purge tank, or using a vacuum pump to lower the feed pressure of the first adsorbent bed, (e) following the blowdown step, the first bed is purged in a purging step, where the gas is provided to the first bed through the product end of the first bed from a fourth bed of the four beds while the first bed is at the feed pressure of 6.89-138 kPa and gas is purged through the feed end of the first bed, (f) following the purging step, a third equalization step occurs, where the first bed is allowed to pressure equalize with the fourth bed having a higher pressure than the first bed through a fourth line connecting the product end of the first bed and the product end of the fourth bed, (g) following the third equalization step, a fourth equalization step occurs, where the first bed is allowed to equalize with the second bed having the higher pressure than the first bed through a fifth line connecting the product end of the first bed and the product end of the second bed, (h) following the fourth equalization step, passing a slipstream of a product-gas or a stream of a storage gas from a storage tank through the product end of the first bed to repressurize the first bed to an adsorption-step-pressure in a repressurization step, and (i) following the repressurization step, operating the first bed in an independent adsorption step for period of time for the third bed and the fourth bed to be pressure equalized and the second bed to be depressurized prior to beginning a second adsorption step, where the second bed, the third bed, and the fourth bed are sequenced to cycle through the first adsorption step, the first equalization step, the providing purge step, the second equalization step, the blowdown step, the purging step, the third equalization step, the fourth equalization step and the independent adsorption step in the same order as the first bed. The first adsorption step, the first equalization step, the providing purge step, the second equalization step, the blowdown step, the purging step, the third equalization step, the fourth equalization step and the independent adsorption step occur in a total cycle time of 400-3600 seconds. The method is performed on an offshore platform. The method has a specific vacuum power consumption of 0-1500 kWh/MMSCF of the feed gas stream. 0-50 wt.% Tail gas stream is recycled to the feed gas stream, thus producing the product gas stream containing no greater than 2 mol.% of the carbon dioxide, at least 90 mol.% of the methane in the feed gas stream, and at least 85 mol.% of total hydrocarbons in the feed gas stream. Preferred Components: The feed gas stream comprises the carbon dioxide, 20-5000 wppm of the water, and 0-1000 wppm hydrogen sulfide. The feed gas stream comprises an acid gas comprising the carbon dioxide, a hydrogen sulfide and/or a carbonyl sulfide. The product gas stream comprises at least 20 wppm of the water, at least 95 mol.% of total-methane, and at least 3 mol.% of total-ethane. The product gas stream contains no greater than 50 wppm of hydrogen sulfide. The product gas stream contains no greater than 4 wppm hydrogen sulfide and no greater than 2000 wppm of the water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

AU SERAFIN J

   MORAWSKI A W
AE UNIV ZACHODNIOPOMORSKI TECHNOLOGICZNY (UZAC-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves utilizing improved-type technique.
   USE - Method for obtaining active carbon for adsorption of carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN RU2755541-C1
TI METHOD FOR OBTAINING ZIRCONIUM HYDROXIDE-BASED            RECOVERABLE CARBON DIOXIDE ABSORBER.
AU KHARIN P A
   MIKHALCHENKOVA A N
   BUTYLKIN I P
   KOCHETKOV A A
   TSYGANKOV A S
   IAKUPOVA I S
   SOKOLOVA A G
   MOROZOVA N A
   MOISEEVA A S
AE NIIKHIMMASH CHEM ENG RES & DESIGN INST (NIIK-Non-standard)
GA 2021C13869
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to a method for obtaining a                zirconium hydroxide-based recoverable carbon                dioxide absorber, and can be used in the technology                for producing recoverable carbon dioxide absorbers                for carbon dioxide concentration systems, for                purifying the atmosphere of sealed objects from                carbon dioxide, wherever production of purified                carbon dioxide-free gases is required. The method                for obtaining a zirconium hydroxide-based                recoverable carbon dioxide absorber consists in                mixing basic zirconium carbonate with zinc oxide                and mechanically activating the mixture. Hydrogen                peroxide and a binding agent are then added,                wherein a water dispersion of a carboxylated                copolymer of acrylic and methacrylic acid esters is                used as a binding agent in an amount of 5 to 10%                relative to the dry matter. The granules are                formed, hydrotreated and dried. The basic zirconium                carbonate is pre-ground for at least 30 minutes,                wherein ceramic balls with a diameter of 30 mm are                used as the grinding bodies in a ball drum mill.                The reagents of the mixture are mechanically                activated at a ratio of zirconium ions to hydrogen                peroxide of 1:0.8 and a moisture content of the                absorbent granules of at least 25&#177;2% wt.                The dried mixture is ground through a sieve with a                mesh of 0.3 mm in size. The resulting product is                mixed in the mixing equipment to a homogeneous                condition with a binding agent, wherein a water                dispersion of a carboxylated copolymer of acrylic                and methacrylic acid esters is used as a binding                agent in an amount of 5 to 10% relative to the dry                matter. The granules are then formed in a                granulator with a die of 2.8 mm in size. The                granules are dried to a moisture content of                25&#177;2% wt. and screen on sieves to remove                dust. The dried granules are then loaded into an                autoclave in a layer of up to 10 cm and sustained                over distilled water at a temperature of                120&#176;C for 6 hours. Ammonia is washed off                by boiling in distilled water in a mesh container,                supplying steam from the bottom up through the                granule layer until the ammonia odour is                eliminated. The granules are then redried at a                temperature of 20 to 80&#176;C to a moisture                content of the product of 25&#177;2% wt.
   USE - Chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention allows excluding isolation of                ammonia while maintaining the mechanical properties                of the absorber granules, increasing the dynamic                activity of the absorber with respect to carbon                dioxide, the granule strength, improving the                operational characteristics of the zirconium                hydroxide-based recoverable carbon dioxide                absorber.

PN CN115099508-A
TI Shale gas collecting and conveying pipeline            corrosion rate prediction method, involves using            positive electrode corrosion potential to calculate            positive electrode time of corrosion current            density.
AU HUANG Y
   LIU X
   LI Y
   HE S
   YU J
   GONG J
   PENG X
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2022C4781Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining the                electrochemical reaction in the corrosion process                of sulfuric acid (SRB)/carbon dioxide solution                system corrosion process. The reaction equation of                carbon dioxide corrosion, oxidation reaction                equation, SRB corrosion and reduction reaction                equation are obtained to obtain the total reaction                equation between the SRB and carbon dioxide                corrosion. The total corrosion current density of                each electrochemical reaction is calculated                according to the charge balance of the positive                electrode and negative electrode. The corrosion                potential of the negative electrode is calculated                to obtain a positive electrode corrosion potential.                The time of the corrosion potential is used to                calculate the corrosion current of the time the                negative electrodes are in contact with the                positive electrodes, and the corrosion positive                electrode density is converted into corrosion rate                and corrosion depth, and the corrosion rate is                calculated.
   USE - Shale gas collecting and conveying pipeline                corrosion rate prediction method of SRB and carbon                dioxide coupling.
   ADVANTAGE - The shale gas collecting pipeline corrosion                rate is more scientific, accurate and reasonable,                since the corrosion rate of pipeline in SRB/CO2                co-existing environment is fully predicted.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material is easily softened and heat-sensitive raw material, e.g. paper making black liquor lignin (Kraft lignin). The feeding system is a biomass pulse sample feeder. The lignin is discharged from the hopper through the pneumatic clamp valve. The small amount of lignin particles fall into the horizontal injection pipe. The intermittent nitrogen pulse and the continuous nitrogen carrier gas flow are pushed into the reactor. The pneumatic clamp valve for discharging biomass and the electromagnetic valve for generating pulse flow are synchronously controlled by a programmable logic controller PLC, the pneumatic clamp valve will be in a short time (for example:) 0.35 s (e.g. each 5 s) is opened for onetime, the time setting can be adjusted according to the feeding requirement. The lignin powder through a certain inclined angle of 0-90degrees, feeding from the reactor gas distributor above the bed layer to the reactor at a certain height position, the inclined feeding pipeline comprises a cold water jacket for preventing the lignin powder from reacting at the feeding pipe before entering the reactor. The filter at the top of the reactor is made of a 10 mum wire mesh and a ceramic fiber layer, so that all solids are retained in the reactor, avoiding fine charcoal particle pollution of bio-oil. The condenser 1 is immersed in the oil bath (15) that temperature is controlled to be 80degrees Celsius to collect the pyrolysis vapor of heavy component. The average temperature of the vapor/stream exiting condenser 1 is about 110degreesC.

PN CN113620327-A
TI Preparing small crystalline pseudo-boehmite involves carrying out two-stage carbonation decomposition reaction to sodium aluminate solution and carbon dioxide gas, subjecting pseudo-boehmite carbonization slurry to aging reaction, and performing solid-liquid separation.
AU YAO Y
   CAI J
   KONG J
   HAN H
   WANG S
   LI G
   ZHOU F
AE CHINA ALUMINUM CORP LTD (CNAL-C)
GA 2021D0921D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing small crystalline pseudo-boehmite involves carrying out two-stage carbonation decomposition reaction to sodium aluminate solution and carbon dioxide gas to obtain a pseudo boehmite carbonized slurry, controlling the end-point pH value of the carbonation decomposition reaction at 10-11.5, where the inlet flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is 1.0-5.0 m3/hour, subjecting the pseudo-boehmite carbonization slurry to an aging reaction, and performing solid-liquid separation. The concentration of aluminum oxide in the sodium aluminate solution is 10-50 g/l. The two-stage carbonation decomposition reaction utilizes carbon dioxide. The sodium aluminate solution is obtained by using aluminum hydroxide raw materials and liquid alkali.
   USE - Preparation method of small crystalline pseudo-boehmite used as binder of semi-synthetic rare earth Y-type molecular sieve cracking catalyst.

TI Promoting photosynthetic efficiency of facility gardening crop by zeolite molecular sieve, comprises pre-processing silicalite-1, making fully absorb carbon dioxide and transferring pre-treated sieve to facility cultivation environment.
AU YU J
   LV J
   WANG J
   WANG H
   WANG L
   WANG A
   SHI K
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2021B4391L
AB    NOVELTY - Promoting photosynthetic efficiency of facility gardening crop by zeolite molecular sieve involves pre-processing the Silicalite-1 molecular sieve, making fully absorb carbon dioxide, transferring the pre-processed Silicalite-1 molecular sieve to the facility cultivation environment with carbon dioxide concentration of 480ppm, standing and slowly releasing carbin dioxide by Silicalite-1 molecular sieve; improving the carbon dioxide concentration of facility cultivation environment, promoting facility gardening crop photosynthetic efficiency.
   USE - Method for promoting photosynthetic efficiency of facility gardening crop by zeolite molecular sieve.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple operation, low energy consumption and wide applicability; and improves the photosynthetic efficiency of the facility gardening crop, and the growth of facility gardening crops; is featured with simple and convenient operation safety environment-friendly; and utilizes the silicalite-1 molecular sieve, which does not contain metal ion, and does not require transformation or heating mode at normal temperature and normal pressure, thus it can satisfy different facility horticultural crops, and different growth period of the CO2 requirement, and promotes growth of facilities gardening crops.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pre-treatment method includes filling carbon dioxide into the sealed space filled with Silicalite-1 molecular sieve, sealing and standing for 1-12 hours. The volume ratio of the Silicalite-1 molecular sieve to the carbon dioxide is 0.01-100:1000. The pre-treatment method includes filling carbon dioxide into a sealed space filled with Silicalite-1 molecular sieve, sealing and standing for 1-4 hours and the volume ratio of the Silicalite-1 molecular sieve to carbon dioxide is 0.1-4:1000. The dosage of Silicalite-1 molecular sieve is 30-100 cm^3 per cubic meter of facility cultivation environment. The silicalite-1 molecular sieve is stewed and released for 3 hours, taken out, returned and repeated for 1-10 times. The Silicalite-1 molecular sieve is repeatedly used for 4 times and then dried and regenerated at high temperature, and then continuously used. The high temperature drying regeneration condition is 100-300?oC for 1-48 hours. The facility gardening crop is tomato.

PN CN112516992-A; CN112516992-B
TI Preparation of bismuth vanadate composite material used as photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction material, involves weighing ammonium vanadate, adding ethylene glycol solution, transferring mixed solution to reactor, washing, and drying.
AU HOU D
   HUANG L
   GAN S
   QIAO X
   LI D
AE UNIV CHINA THREE GORGES (UYCR-C)
GA 202131602K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of bismuth vanadate composite material involves weighing ammonium vanadate, adding water, ultra-sonically processing until it is evenly dispersed, recording as solution (A), weighing bismuth nitrate pentahydrate, adding ethylene glycol solution, recording as solution (B), dripping solution (B) to solution (A) until evenly mixing, transferring the uniformly mixed solution to the lining of the PTFE reactor, solvothermally reacting at 150-180 degrees C for 12-36 hours, cooling the reaction product, washing with water and ethanol, and drying to obtain bismuth vanadate-based composite material. The composite material is bismuth vanadate-bismuth oxide composite material.
   USE - Preparation of bismuth vanadate composite material used as photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction material and hexavalent chromium ion reduction material for removing gaseous nitrogen oxide compounds (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces composite material having excellent structure, improved carbon dioxide adsorption performance, catalytic activity in energy and environmental photocatalysis, and realizes the accelerated migration and effective space separation of photogenerated carriers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application of the bismuth vanadate composite material, used as a photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The molar ratio of bismuth nitrate pentahydrate to ammonium vanadate is (1-1.5):1, preferably 1.2:1. Preferred Process: The photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction material is prepared by dispersing composite material in a glass Petri dish containing deionized water under visible light irradiation, drying to form film, placing in a reactor for photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction, adding deionized water to the bottom of the reactor. The hexavalent chromium ion reduction material is prepared by dispersing composite material in a hexavalent chromium ion solution under visible light irradiation, and stirring the suspension in the dark before irradiation to achieve adsorption and desorption equilibrium. The removal method of gaseous nitrogen oxide compounds involves placing the composite material in Petri dish under visible light irradiation, adding deionized water, ultrasonically-processing, drying, and placing in a reactor containing gaseous nitrogen oxide compounds for removal reaction. Preferred Component: The nitrogen oxide compound is nitric oxide.

GA 202126211J
AB    NOVELTY - Cleaning and processing metal thin film circuits on ferrite substrates before electroplating comprises (i) using carbon dioxide snow to clean ferrite substrate once, (ii) soaking ferrite substrate after cleaning with acid solution, (iii) using carbon dioxide snow to perform secondary spray cleaning on ferrite substrate processed in step (ii), (iv) rinsing ferrite substrate after cleaning in step (iii) with pure water, and completing treatment. Preferred Composition: The polyurethane foam comprises a polyol/supercritical fluid microemulsion, preferably polyol/supercritical carbon dioxide microemulsion containing the compound (I).

PT P
PN CN110743606-A; CN110743606-B
TI Catalyst useful for preparing aromatic hydrocarbons by direct hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises metal oxide catalyst composed of metal oxide or metal oxide and carrier, and molecular sieve catalyst.
AU LI C
   QU Y
   LI Z
   WANG J
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202011763N
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises 20-80 %mass, preferably 30-70 %mass metal oxide catalyst (M) and a molecular sieve catalyst (Z), which are physically mixed and expressed as M/Z. The metal oxide catalyst (M) is composed of a metal oxide, or a metal oxide and a carrier. The metal oxide comprises oxides of Group IIIA, IIB, IVB and/or VIB elements. The mass percentage of oxide in the catalyst (M) is 20-100%, preferably 60-100%. The molecular sieve catalyst (Z) is ZSM-5, MCM-22 (both preferred), ZSM-11, ZSM-22 and/or ZSM-23 molecular sieves. The silica-alumina ratio of the molecular sieve used is 20-300, preferably 50-150.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing aromatic hydrocarbons by direct hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high selectivity. During hydrogenation, the carbon dioxide conversion rate can reach 14%, and the aromatic hydrocarbon selectivity (carbon-based selectivity) in hydrocarbon products can reach 80%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for synthesizing aromatic hydrocarbons, which involves using the catalyst to catalyze the reaction of direct hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is oxides of gallium, indium, zinc, cadmium, titanium, zirconium, chromium and/or molybdenum, preferably oxides of gallium, indium, zinc and/or zirconium. The carrier is silicon dioxide and/or aluminum oxide. The molecular sieve catalyst (Z) uses a modified molecular sieve or an unmodified molecular sieve. Preferred Method: The method for preparing the metal oxide catalyst (M) involves an impregnation method, a co-precipitation method, a step-by-step precipitation method, a deposition precipitation method and/or a mechanical mixing method. The impregnation method involves preparing soluble compounds of metal elements required by the metal oxide catalyst (M) into a solution, then mixing with the carrier or the metal oxide and then evaporating to dryness. The co-precipitation method involves using a precipitating agent to uniformly precipitate metal element compounds required by the metal oxide catalyst (M), or uniformly precipitating onto the carrier, or uniformly precipitating on the metal oxides, where the precipitating agent is an alkaline solution, preferably sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, ammonium nitrate or ammonium carbonate. The step-by-step precipitation method involves using a precipitating agent to uniformly precipitate metal element compounds in the metal oxide catalyst (M) respectively, or precipitate onto the carrier or precipitate onto the metal oxides. The deposition precipitation method involves mixing a compound of metal elements required for the metal oxide catalyst (M) with the carrier or the metal oxides to form a suspension, and precipitating the metal element compounds by controlling pH. The mechanical mixing method involves directly and mechanically mixing oxides of various metal elements required by the metal oxide catalyst (M) according to a certain ratio (such as a ball milling method). The method for preparing the metal oxide catalyst (M) further involves: aging at 40-100 degrees C, preferably 80-100 degrees C for 6-24 hours, preferably 15-20 hours; drying at 80-110 degrees C, preferably 100-100 degrees C for 10-20 hours, preferably 15-20 hours; and roasting at 300-800 degrees C, preferably 400-600 degrees C for 2-10 hours, preferably 4-8 hours. During the catalyst precipitation, the pH of the system is controlled at 6-10, preferably 6-8. The molecular sieve (Z) selects modified molecular sieve or unmodified molecular sieve. The modification is performed using zinc, gallium, molybdenum, silver, manganese, phosphorus or silicon elements. The amount of the modifying element is 0.1-20%, preferably 1-5% of the mass of the modified molecular sieve. The modification method mainly uses impregnation method, ion exchange method, physical mixing method or chemical liquid deposition method. The impregnation method involves formulating a soluble compound containing elements of zinc, gallium, molybdenum, silver, manganese and/or phosphorus into a solution, then mixing with the molecular sieve, and directly loading the modified element on the molecular sieve by drying. The ion exchange method involves preparing a soluble compound containing elements of zinc, gallium, molybdenum, silver, manganese and/or phosphorus into a solution, then mixing with the a molecular sieve, and ion-exchanging the modified element onto the molecular sieve. The physical mixing method involves directly and mechanically mixing compound containing elements of zinc, gallium, molybdenum, silver, manganese and/or phosphorus with the molecular sieve in a certain proportion. The chemical liquid deposition method involves mixing a compound containing a modifying element, the molecular sieve and an organic solvent (such as hexane), and loading the modifying element on the molecular sieve. The modification method further involves: drying at 80-110 degrees C for 10-20 hours; and roasting at 300-800 degrees C, preferably 400-600 degrees C for 3-12 hours, preferably 5-10 hours. The mixing method mainly adopts a mechanical mixing method. The mechanical mixing method is a ball milling method. The mixed catalyst M/Z is treated in argon and/or hydrogen for 0.5-4 hours, preferably 0.5-2 hours before reacting. The treatment temperature is a subsequent reaction temperature, and the reaction temperature is 260-450 degrees C, preferably 300-350 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for synthesizing aromatic hydrocarbons involves taking carbon dioxide and hydrogen to serve as raw materials, where the volume ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide is 2-4, and reacting at a temperature of 260-450 degrees C, preferably 300-350 degrees C and a pressure of 0.5-6 MPa, preferably 2-5 MPa in the presence of the catalyst. The raw material space velocity is 200-30000 ml/(g catalyst.hour), preferably 900-10000 ml/(g catalyst.hour). The method further involves optionally adding an inert diluent to the reaction system to make the hydrogenation reaction smooth and easy to control, or to improve selectivity, where the inert diluent is nitrogen and/or argon, and the volume content of the inert diluent in the reaction system is 0-50%, preferably 0-20%.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E10-J02B3; E35; J04-E04B; N03; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-K

PN CN110746749-A
TI Preparing micro-nano-cellulose polyester            microporous foamed sheet involves melt-blending and            extruding micro-nano cellulose and polyester resin            materials into pellets, processing into sheets, and            rolling up.
AU WU M
   HUANG Y
   ZHAO Y
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 202011691B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing micro-nano-cellulose polyester                microporous foamed sheet involves melt-blending and                extruding micro-nano cellulose and polyester resin                materials into pellets, processing into sheets, and                rolling up. The sheet processed is placed in a                pressure-resistant container, and carbon dioxide                gas is passed through to make the                micro-nanocellulose polyester sheet absorb                saturated carbon dioxide. The pressure is released,                discharged the carbon dioxide in the                pressure-resistant container, and taken out the                micro-nano cellulose polyester sheet that absorbs                the saturated carbon dioxide for later use. The                micro-nanocellulose polyester sheet taken out is                unfolded and performed foaming, and after the                foaming is completed, cooled the sheet to room                temperature. The internal microbubbles are shaped                to obtain microcellular foamed micronano cellulose                polyester sheets.
   USE - Method for preparing micro-nano-cellulose                polyester microporous foamed sheet.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare                micro-nano-cellulose polyester microporous foamed                sheet, that has abundant sources of cellulose raw                materials, low prices, simple processes, and                environmental friendliness, and is possible to use                existing equipment for forming and processing                sheets in the industry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyester resin                  material includes polylactic acid, polybutylene                  succinate, polycaprolactone,                  poly3-hydroxyalkanoate or polyethylene                  terephthalate. The micro-nano cellulose includes                  unmodified micro-nano cellulose and modified                  micro-nano cellulose. The unmodified                  micro-nanocellulose includes unmodified                  nanocellulose whiskers, unmodified nanocellulose                  fibers, unmodified lignocellulose or unmodified                  cellulose microfiber. Preferred Conditions: The                  added amount of the micro-nano cellulose to the                  polyester resin material is 0.1-10 wt.%. The                  temperature of the melt blending is 10-40 degrees                  C higher than the melting point of the polyester                  resin material. The pressure of the carbon                  dioxide is 2-30 megapascal. The temperature in                  the pressure-resistant container is (-20)-120                  degrees C. The holding time before releasing the                  pressure after introducing carbon dioxide is                  3-120 hours. The tension during foaming is 0.2-20                  megapascal, the foaming temperature is 80-240                  degrees C, and the foaming time is 5-240                  seconds.

PN CN110541737-A
TI Middle-low temperature surplus heat generate electricity system, has T-carbon dioxide circulation system provided with recycling waste heat resource, where T-carbon dioxide circulation system utilizes cold energy.
AU YU Z
   SU R
   SUN J
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2019A43540
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a Kalina circulation system provided with a first stage recycling waste heat resource. The Kalina circulation system utilizes first stage liquefied natural gas (LNG) cold energy. A T-carbon dioxide circulation system is provided with a second stage recycling waste heat resource. The T-carbon dioxide circulation system utilizes second stage LNG cold energy. A cold fluid outlet of an evaporator is connected with an inlet of a separator. An upper part of the separator is connected to a turbine that is connected with a mixer.
   USE - LNG cold energy-based middle-low temperature surplus heat generate electricity system.

PN CN110526245-A
TI Method for storing carbon dioxide and co-processing radioactive waste, involves placing waste concrete and active waste residue in closed reaction kettle, adding grinding aids and water, and standing still after grinding, adding radioactive.
AU DAI F
   WANG J
   ZHAO R
   ZHANG H
   HUANG H
   YANG W
   WU X
AE CHINA CONSTR READY MIXED CONCRETE CO LTD (CSCE-C)
   CHINA WEST CONSTR CO LTD (CSCE-C)
GA 2019A2993N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for storing carbon dioxide and co-processing radioactive waste, involves placing the waste concrete and active waste residue in a closed reaction kettle, add grinding aids and water, and stand still after grinding, adding radioactive waste and discarded fibers to the closed reaction kettle, and passing CO2 gas into it at a constant rate under stirring conditions to obtain a slurry. After the slurry obtained is transported to a suitable place by a transportation pipeline or a vehicle, it is injected into the formation with specific geological conditions and a specific depth to hydrate and harden.
   USE - Method for storing carbon dioxide and co-processing radioactive waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for storing carbon dioxide and co-processing radioactive waste can achieve permanent solidification of CO2, and solidify the structure of the solidified body while solidifying CO2, which is beneficial to reduce the migration of radioactive waste, and helps to improve the sealing and retention of radioactive waste, has low risk and low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Each component of the active waste residue comprises 15-24 pts. wt. waste concrete, 60-86 pts. wt. active waste residue, 1-2.5 grinding aid, 30-46 pts. wt. water, 10-18 pts. wt. radioactive waste, 0.5-2 pts. wt. waste fiber, 50-75 wt.% slag, 10-15 wt.% steel slag, 15-35 wt.% phosphate slag. The slag, steel slag, and phosphorus slag are all wet slags discharged after water quenching. The slag activity index is /-95%, the steel slag activity index is /-80%, and the phosphorus slag activity index is /-75%. The main components of the waste concrete are cement hydration products and sandy aggregates, and the particle size is less than 4.8 mm. The radioactive waste is selected from fly ash from incineration of medical waste from hospitals, ash from incineration of hazardous waste from factories, sludge from sewage treatment by schools and research institutes, the waste fiber is any one of a waste natural fiber, a glass fiber, or a synthetic polymer fiber, the specific operation of passing CO2 gas is ventilating for 5-8 minutes, stop for 10-15 minutes, and repeat 3-6 times, the grinding aid is a triethanolamine or polyhydric alcohol grinding aid, the closed reaction kettle is a vertical frequency conversion stirring mill.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing a pre-mixture of                primary fresh concrete ingredients in a rotatable                mixing drum (22), where rotatable mixing drum is                mounted on a chassis (12) of a wheeled vehicle. The                internal combustion engine is operated for                producing an exhaust gas containing products of                combustion including carbon dioxide, where internal                combustion engine drives the wheels (14) of the                wheeled vehicle. The exhaust gas is cooled, where                exhaust gas is cooled by passing through a heat                exchanger. The cooled exhaust gas is directed into                the mixing drum. PT P
PN CN109809458-A; CN109809458-B
TI Rugby-shaped calcium carbonate for e.g. food, is            prepared by adding calcium hydroxide slurry to crystal            orientation agent, adjusting initial carbonization            temperature, carbonizing carbon dioxide gas,            dehydrating, pulverizing, sieving.
AU TONG Z
   WANG Q
   LIAO D
   LI L
   ZHONG L
   HUANG S
   ZHOU W
   MENG Y
   LI S
   QIN L
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
   GUANGXI CALCIUM CARBONATE INDUSTRIALIZAT (UYGU-C)
   UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 201948820T
AB    NOVELTY - A rugby-shaped calcium carbonate is prepared                by preparing a calcium hydroxide slurry, adding a                crystal orientation agent to calcium hydride                slurry, thoroughly mixing, adjusting the initial                carbonization temperature, carbonizing the carbon                dioxide gas, dehydrating the calcium carbonate                slurry, drying, pulverizing and sieving. The                preparation of rugby-shaped calcium carbonate                specifically involves (1) digesting the lime,                refining and preparing into calcium hydroxide                slurry, (2) adding a crystal forming agent to                prepared calcium hydroxide slurry, (3) adjusting                the calcium hydroxide slurry to the initial                carbonization temperature, introducing carbon                dioxide gas for carbonization reaction, completing                the reaction, when the pH is lower than 8 and stop                introducing carbon dioxide gas, (4) dehydrating,                drying, pulverizing and sieving.
   USE - Rugby-shaped calcium carbonate for food,                medicine, cosmetics, plastics, rubber, coating,                ink, papermaking and chemical applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The rugby-shaped calcium carbonate does not                require surface treatment, has excellent dispersion                effect, short carbonization time, low production                cost, adjustable particle size of 300-900 nm and                excellent processing fluidity, and is prepared by                simple process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The crystal form is a                  mixed crystal form of a vaterite crystal form and                  a calcite crystal form. The crystal form is a                  football-shaped calcium carbonate, and the mixed                  crystal form is a ball meteorite. The amount of                  crystal form is more than 50 wt.%. The amount of                  calcite crystal form is less than 50 wt.%. The                  particle size of rugby-shaped calcium carbonate                  is 300-900 nm. The amount of crystal forming                  agent is 0-15 wt.%, preferably 1-6 wt.% of solid                  content of calcium hydroxide. Preferred                  Properties: The spherical calcium carbonate is                  irregular, uneven or partially broken due to                  incomplete control of reaction conditions and                  process parameters. Preferred Process: The step                  (1) comprises directly digesting lime with water,                  or mixing the water with methanol or ethanol,                  water or methanol, adjusting the calcium                  hydroxide content in the calcium hydroxide slurry                  to 5-18%, after lime digestion and purification.                  The amount of methanol and/or ethanol 15-80 %mass                  of calcium hydroxide slurry. The step (3)                  comprises adding calcium hydroxide slurry to                  crystal forming agent, stirring uniformly,                  adjusting initial carbonization temperature at                  3-60 degrees C, and introducing carbon dioxide                  gas with concentration of 10-100% to carry out a                  carbonization reaction until the pH is lower than                  8 and introducing the gas to end the                  carbonization, when the agitation state is turned                  on. The carbonization is ended, and corresponding                  operation is carried out according to the gel                  condition, after the carbon dioxide gas is                  stopped. The gel that is formed is dissolved or                  properly heated to accelerate dissolution. The                  gel which is not formed during carbonization                  stirred for gel formation. After the gel is                  formed, the gel is dissolved or properly heated                  to accelerate the dissolution of the gel. The                  calcium carbonate filter cake is obtained by                  filtration or centrifugal dehydration, after the                  gel is melted and depolymerized, and the filter                  cake is dried, pulverized, and sieved. The                  filtrate obtained by dehydrating the calcium                  carbonate slurry in step (4) is subjected to                  precipitation, filtration, and clarification                  treatment, and is recycled.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (2) comprises                  adding heated starch syrup, using                  polyethyleneimine, polyvinylpyrrolidone and/or                  sodium polybenzenesulfonate as crystal forming                  agent to prepared calcium hydroxide slurry.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

TI Three-component mixed working medium useful for waste heat recovery power cycle of an internal combustion engine comprises carbon dioxide and pentane and a refrigerant (difluoromethane).
AU SHU G
   LIU P
   TIAN H
   YU Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201935797C
AB    NOVELTY - Three-component mixed working medium comprises carbon dioxide and pentane and a refrigerant, where the refrigerant is an efficient and environmentally friendly type with a GWP value lower than 1000 and an ODP value of 0, preferably the refrigerant is difluoromethane (preferred), fluoroethane and/or 1,1-difluoroethane. The medium 10-20 pts. wt. comprises pentane, 10-60 pts. wt. difluoromethane and balance of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The working medium is useful for waste heat recovery power cycle of an internal combustion engine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The three-component mixed working fluid has good environmental protection and safety and thermodynamic performance, and the thermal efficiency is higher than 12%; the engine flue gas and the cylinder jacket water residual heat energy can be efficiently recovered at the same time, the flue gas utilization rate is more than 60%, and the cylinder liner water is used. The utilization rate is 100%. The critical temperature of carbon dioxide is obviously improved, and the cooling conditions of the system are relaxed to meet the requirements of the vehicle waste heat recovery system.

TI Process system for removing carbon dioxide from syngas, comprises washing water storage tank, washing water pump, water washing tower, gas-liquid separator, first-stage membrane separator, buffer tank and permeate gas compressor.

AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   NCIG RES INST (NCIG-Non-standard)
GA 201923345K
AB    NOVELTY - A process system comprises a washing water storage tank, a washing water pump, a water washing tower, a gas-liquid separator, a first-stage membrane separator, a buffer tank, a permeate gas compressor, heat exchanger, precision filter group and secondary membrane separator, where the syngas from the gasification tower enters the water washing tower, is counter-flowed with the aqueous solution from the washing water storage tank, and is humidified, separated into a first-stage membrane separator by gas-liquid separation, buffered dehumidification, and the trapped gas is purified by removing carbon dioxide, the permeate gas enter the secondary membrane separator after being pressurized, heat exchanged and precisely filtered by the compressor, the trapped gas of the secondary membrane separator goes to the water washing tower cycle, and the permeate gas is a carbon dioxide-rich acid gas out system.
   USE - Process system for removing carbon dioxide from syngas.

PN DE102017117681-A1
TI Monitoring device for monitoring breathing parameter of patient, has processing device suitable and designed to determine carbon dioxide content of blood based on stored assignment function from detected oxygen content.
AU DELLWEG D
   KERL J
AE FACHKRANKENHAUS KLOSTER GRAFSCHAFT GMBH (FACH-Non-standard)
GA 201913723L
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a sensor device (2) that is arranged with a sensor (12) for detecting oxygen content of the blood and a processing device (3). The processing device is arranged for evaluating the detected oxygen content. The processing device s suitable and designed to determine a carbon dioxide content of the blood based on a stored assignment function from the detected oxygen content. The processing device is arranged to provide an allocation function that describes a reverse proportionality between the oxygen portion and the carbon dioxide portion.

PT P
PN RU2673920-C1
TI Method for creating reactive thrust of manned spacecraft has carbon dioxide released from air is collected, compressed and stored on board spacecraft.
AU GLUKHIKH I N
   TERENTEV I P
AE ENERGIYA KOROLEV ROCKET SPACE CORP STOCK (ENER-Non-standard)
GA 201902132J
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to rocket and space equipment and can be used in the development of jet propulsion units (PU) intended for maneuvering manned spacecraft (SC). Method of creating a reactive thrust of a manned spacecraft, including obtaining of hydrogen and oxygen on board the spacecraft by electrolysis of water with the use of a portion of electrolytic oxygen for breathing, storage of hydrogen and residual oxygen under excessive pressure, direction of these gases at a given time into the combustion chamber of the rocket engine and ignition of these gases, as well as release of carbon dioxide from the air of the inhabited compartments, while the carbon dioxide released from the air is collected, compressed and stored on board the spacecraft, and after ignition of the oxygen-hydrogen mixture in the combustion chamber, the collected carbon dioxide is sent there at a rate not interrupting the combustion of the oxygen-hydrogen mixture. In this case, hydrogen and oxygen are fed into the engine at a weight ratio of about 1:4.
   USE - Astronautics.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention provides an increase in the thrust-to-weight ratio of the manned spacecraft, as well as the possibility of holding longer flights.

AB    NOVELTY - The device has an unloading head whose two                sides are formed with ten directional energy                discharging opening parts in axial direction, where                phase angle of the directional energy discharging                opening parts are not less than eight, spacing                between the directional energy discharging opening                parts are formed as 2 mm, aperture of the                directional energy discharging opening parts is                formed as 3 mm and inner side of the directional                energy discharging opening parts are formed with                three rows.    USE - Sinking carbon dioxide freezing phase change                induced cracking assistant device.

TI Ring-down spectrometer for absolutely measuring carbon dioxide content in atmosphere, has laser coupled to ring-down optical cavity through optical element, where laser forms TEM00 interference mode in ring-down optical cavity.
AU LIN H
   ZHANG J
   FENG X
AE NAT INST METROLOGY CHINA (NAIM-C)
GA 201866672E
AB    NOVELTY - The spectrometer has a gas path system formed in a ring-down optical cavity for supplying gas having a predetermined pressure. An external light path of a semiconductor laser emits a laser. The laser is coupled to the ring-down optical cavity through an optical element. The laser forms a TEM00 interference mode in the ring-down optical cavity. A side of the ring-down optical cavity is formed with an air inlet hole and an air outlet hole. The air inlet hole and the outlet hole are formed on different sides of the ring-down optical cavity, where the optical element comprises a lens and two reflectors.
   USE - Frequency-stabilizing light cavity ring-down spectrometer for absolutely measuring carbon dioxide content in an atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The spectrometer can realize absolute carbon dioxide concentration measurement process without calibration and uncertainty.

PN FR3062163-A1
TI Method for controlling thermal engine of motor vehicle, involves comparing power threshold by pollution control system with minimum threshold when carbon dioxide range is between minimum threshold and maximum threshold.
AU ZUMI M
   FAUQUET P
   LATOUCHENT G
AE PEUGEOT CITROEN AUTOMOBILES SA (CITR-C)
GA 201858092A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves comparing a measured temperature of an exhaust line (1) of a thermal engine (2) with threshold temperature. Duration of temporal threshold is calculated corresponding to predetermined quantity of carbon dioxide. Power threshold is received by a pollution control system and compared with minimum threshold corresponding to activation potential of a specific combustion mode and maximum threshold corresponding to shattering capacity of the exhaust line when the carbon dioxide range is between minimum threshold and the maximum threshold.
   USE - Method for controlling a thermal engine of a motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables receiving and comparing power threshold by the pollution control system with minimum threshold corresponding to activation potential of the specific combustion mode and maximum threshold corresponding to shattering capacity of the exhaust line when the carbon dioxide range is between minimum threshold and the maximum threshold, so that thermal engine can be controlled in an efficient manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a thermal engine.
   Exhaust line (1)
   Thermal engine (2)
   Calculator (8)
   Catalyst (51)
   Particles (52)

GA 2018433428
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing an extracting well. A large aperture drilling process is performed for performing a dense coating process by a natural gas hydrate reservoir. A scraper drill bit cutting groove is formed on the extracting well. An extracting well drilling process is performed for inserting frozen in a sealed drill rod for circulating low-temperature alcohol in a low temperature alcoholic frozen well. A movable working rod is inserted in a natural gas hydrate reservoir cavity for injecting carbon dioxide into a recovery well. An energy extraction process is performed from natural gas. A natural gas hydrate exploitation process is performed by ultra-critical carbon dioxide for generating water.
   USE - Hydrogen-oxygen natural gas hydrate freezing well coordinated mining method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simplifying processing steps, increasing extraction rate and well exploitation range, reducing mining affects a stability of the reservoir, realizes carbon fixation processing in the reservoir, reduces development and utilization of natural gas hydrate effectively and avoiding influence of atmospheric carbon dioxide content.

PT P
PN TW201815479-A
TI Nozzle device capable of ejecting carbon dioxide particles and lubricant includes an outer tube for delivering compressed gas.
AU XU W
   LIU Q
   LIN D
   CAO K
   LIN M
AE HABOR PRECISE INC (HABO-Non-standard)
GA 201859268M
AB    NOVELTY - A nozzle device includes an outer tube for delivering compressed gas, a sleeve tube disposed in the outer tube for delivering compressed gas, a lubricating tube disposed in the outer tube for delivering lubricant, and a cooling tube disposed in the sleeve tube for delivering carbon dioxide. The length of the cooling tube is smaller than the length of the sleeve tube, so that the carbon dioxide entering the sleeve tube can generate particles due to phase change, and is ejected together with the compressed gas of the sleeve tube to the outside. The length of the lubricating tube is less than the length of the outer tube so as to allow the lubricant entering the outer tube to be ejected from the outlet to the outside with the compressed gas in the outer tube. In this way, in the cutting process, not only can carbon dioxide particles and lubricants be ejected to achieve the effects of cooling, cleaning and lubrication, but also a sleeve type design can be used to converge the tubes to increase space efficiency and strengthen the structural stability, and then increase the ejection speed.

   USE - Water vapor and carbon dioxide gas carbon monoxide oxidation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high activity under water vapor and carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of catalyst comprising taking zinc(II) nitrate hexahydrate as zinc source, cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate as cerium source, and copper(II) nitrate trihydrate as copper source, adding hydrofluoric acid, mixing, adding urea at mass ratio of 1:0.1-0.3, roasting under nitrogen atmosphere, urea decomposing, loading platinum nitrate, drying, roasting under air atmosphere, and reducing with hydrazine hydrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst is prepared by taking zinc(II) nitrate hexahydrate as zinc source, cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate as cerium source, and copper(II) nitrate trihydrate as copper source at molar ratio of 0.5-1.5:0.5-1.5:0.1, adding hydrofluoric acid, reacting, depositing, filtering, and drying for 8 hours at 100 degrees C; adding urea at mass ratio of 1:0.1-0.3, adding deionized water, water bath evaporating at 90-100 degrees C, roasting under nitrogen atmosphere at 450 degrees C for 4 hours, and urea decomposing; and soaking in platinum nitrate at room temperature for 12 hours, water bath evaporating at 90 degrees C, drying at 100 degrees C for 8 hours, roasting under air atmosphere at 300 degrees C for 4 hours, reducing with 1 wt.% hydrazine hydrate, washing with deionized water for 4 times, and drying for 8 hours at 100 degrees C.
