PN US2021261408-A1
TI Reactor structure for forming e.g. hydrogen, comprises active piston that changes volume of variable volume reactor, and structure that includes catalyst, sorbent material, and hydrogen permeable membrane, where volume is space between active piston and structure.
AU ANDERSON D M
   FEDOROV A G
AE GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
GA 202198912E
AB    NOVELTY - A reactor structure comprises an active piston that changes a volume of a variable volume reactor and a structure that includes at least one of a catalyst, a sorbent material, and hydrogen permeable membrane. The volume is the space between the active piston and the structure. The variable volume reactor is configured so that products, carbon dioxide and hydrogen are formed due to reaction of fuel. The variable volume reactor is configured so that movement of the active piston sequentially decreases the volume and increases hydrogen partial pressure in the reactor volume, which causes an increase of the rate of reaction and hydrogen permeation through hydrogen membrane and increases the volume and decreases the partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the variable volume reactor, which causes the carbon dioxide to desorb from the sorbent material and removed from the variable volume reactor using an open exhaust valve by movement of the active piston
   USE - Reactor structure used for producing hydrogen and carbon dioxide products and hydrogen-to-carbon products, used as bridge fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor structure provides efficient production of hydrogen in a small-scale, distributed fashion at the point of use, with excellent thermal (energy) efficiency and volumetric yield density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A reactor structure comprises an active piston that changes a volume of a variable volume reactor and a structure that includes at least one of a catalyst, a sorbent material, and hydrogen permeable membrane. The volume is the space between the active piston and the structure. The variable volume reactor is configured so that products, carbon dioxide and hydrogen are formed due to reaction of fuel. The carbon dioxide is adsorbed by the sorbent material, and hydrogen permeates through the hydrogen permeable membrane to outside the variable volume reactor. The variable volume reactor is configured so that movement of the active piston sequentially decreases the volume and increases hydrogen partial pressure in the reactor volume, which causes an increase of the rate of reaction and hydrogen permeation through hydrogen membrane and increases the volume and decreases the partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the variable volume reactor, which causes the carbon dioxide to desorb from the sorbent material and be removed from the variable volume reactor using an open exhaust valve by movement of the active piston to decrease the volume and pushing the carbon dioxide out of the variable volume reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises magnesium aluminum oxide, aluminum oxide, and one or more of nickel, cobalt, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium or platinum, preferably nickel. The sorbent material comprises at least one of calcium oxide, lithium zirconate, hydrotalcite, activated carbon, zeolite, organic-inorganic hybrid, amine-functionalized silica, metal organic frameworks, branched or hyperbranched polymer, or ethylene-amine hyperbranched polymer. The sorbent material is potassium carbonate-promoted hydrotalcite. The weight ratio of the catalyst and sorbent material is 0.1:0.9-0.2:0.5. The selectively permeable hydrogen membrane comprises palladium and silver alloys.

PN CN109892575-A
TI Carbon dioxide microbubble assisted ultrasonic pickling of low-salt smoked ham comprises e.g. preparing carbon dioxide gas pickling solution, mincing the meat into paste, vaccum kneading and ultrasonic processing, filling, and heating.
AU LI S
   TAU S
   MA R
   GUO X
   XIN Q
   QI L
   LIN X
   JI C
   LIANG H
AE UNIV DALIAN POLYTECHNIC (UYDP-C)
GA 201956400N
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide microbubble assisted ultrasonic pickling of low-salt smoked ham comprises (i) preparing carbon dioxide gas pickling solution by adding salt, sodium nitrite, sodium tripolyphosphate, sodium erythorbate in water, and introducing 0.4-0.8 g/l carbon dioxide into the aqueous solution to obtain carbon oxide micro-bubble pickling liquid, (ii) taking pork, and mincing into meat paste, (iii) taking the meat paste and adding pickling solution of step (i) according to 20-30% of meat paste, vacuum kneading, rolling the meat after vacuum packaging, immersing in water for ultrasonic processing to obtain the pickled meat, (iv) filling the pickled meat in casing to obtain semi finished product, (v) heating the semi-finished product to obtain low-salt smoked ham, and (vi) cooling and storing the low-salt smoked ham at 4 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide microbubble assisted ultrasonic pickling of low-salt smoked ham.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: comprises ultrasonic treatment, which increases protein of pork, reduces cooking loss, increases stiffness of the pork; produces meat product has softness with retention of moisture and improves product quality; has simple process and consumes less energy; and is easy to realize original production upgrading.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide microbubbles assisted ultrasonic pickling of low-salt smoked ham comprises (i) preparing carbon dioxide gas pickling solution by adding 5-7% salt, 0.05-0.08% sodium nitrite, 2-3% sodium tripolyphosphate, 0.2-0.3% sodium erythorbate in water, introducing 0.4-0.8 g/l carbon dioxide into the aqueous solution to obtain a carbon oxide micro-bubble pickling liquid, (ii) taking pork, and mincing into meat paste, (iii) taking the meat paste and adding pickling solution of step (i) according to 20-30% of meat paste, vacuum kneading for 15-60 minutes, vacuum degree is -0.09 MPa, rolling the meat after vacuum packaging, immersing in water for ultrasonic processing at 8-10 degrees C for 15-30 minutes, and the ultrasonic intensity is 110 W/cm2, and the ultrasonic processing at 4 degrees C for 12-16 hours to obtain the pickled meat, (iv) filling the pickled meat in casing to obtain semi finished product, (v) heating the semi-finished product at 80-85 degrees C to until it reaches a central temperature of 70-73 degrees C to obtain low-salt smoked ham, and (vi) cooling and storing the low-salt smoked ham at 4 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), is filling the meat in a plastic casing having a diameter of 85-120 mm, and the filling amount in each casing is 1 kg. In the step (vi), cooling process comprises cooling to room temperature, and then cooling to 2 degrees C, and cooling the low-salt smoked ham until it reaches center temperature of 4-6 degrees C. Preparation of carbon dioxide microbubbles assisted ultrasonic pickling of low-salt smoked ham specifically comprises (i) preparing carbon dioxide gas pickling solution by adding 6.25% mass concentration of salt, 0.075% mass concentration of sodium nitrite, 2.5% mass concentration of sodium tripolyphosphate, 0.25% mass concentration of sodium erythorbate in water, stirring and dissolving, introducing 0.4 g/l of carbon dioxide into the aqueous solution to obtain a carbon oxide micro-bubble pickling liquid, (ii) taking pork, and mincing into meat paste, (iii) taking the meat paste and adding pickling solution of step (i) according to 20% of meat paste, vacuum kneading for 20 minutes, vacuum degree is -0.09 MPa, rolling the meat after vacuum packaging, immersing in water for ultrasonic processing at 9 degrees C for 10 minutes, and the ultrasonic intensity is 10 W/cm2, and the ultrasonic processing at 4 degrees C for 16 hours to obtain the pickled meat, (iv) filling the pickled meat in casing to obtain semi finished product , where the diameter cured meat paste is 85 mm and filling the meat 1 kg per each case, (v) heating the semi-finished product at 85 degrees C to until it reaches central temperature of 72 degrees C to obtain low-salt smoked ham, and (vi) cooling low-salt smoked ham to room temperature with running water, then placing at 2 degrees C, cooling to a central temperature of 5 degrees C, and storing in a 4 degrees C refrigerator.

   USE - Producing functionalized organic molecules used for removing harmful gases e.g. carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, methane or its mixtures from an atmosphere, in particular air (all claimed), preferably traffic contaminated air.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is carried out economically under mild conditions and with lower levels of environmental contamination.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is in the form of a multi-phase catalyst, comprises the permanently polarized hydroxyapatite which forms a phase, preferably a main phase, of the catalyst and the brushite and/or the brushite-like material forms a further phase of the catalyst. Preferred Property: The catalyst has a wide angle X-ray scattering pattern and has a Raman spectrum. Preferred Conditions: The contacting process is carried out under a total pressure of 0-250 bar (excluding 0), preferably 0-10 bar (excluding 0) and a partial pressure of the carbon dioxide 0-100 bar (excluding 0), preferably 0-5 bar (excluding 0) and/or under a partial pressure of the methane 0-100 bar (excluding 0), preferably 0-10 bar (excluding 0). The contacting process is carried out under UV irradiation or UV-Vis irradiation, having a wavelength 200-850 nm, preferably of 253.7 nm, and having irradiance of 0.1-200 W/m2, preferably 2-10 W/m2 and at a temperature of 25-250 degrees C, preferably 95 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises permanently polarized hydroxyapatite in a larger proportion than the brushite and/or brushite-like material. The catalyst contains 50-99.9 wt.% , preferably 85-88 wt.% permanently polarized hydroxy apatite, and 0-50 wt.% (excluding 0), preferably 12-15 wt.% brushite and/or brushite-like material with respect to total weight of the catalyst. The volumetric ratio of the catalyst to water, preferably liquid water and/or water vapor is 1000:1-0.01:1, preferably 300:1-0.01:300 and the volumetric ratio of carbon dioxide to methane is 100:1 or 1:100, preferably 1:1. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide to the catalyst is 0-0.5 (excluding 0), preferably 0.3-0.5, and/or with a molar ratio of methane to the catalyst of 0-0.5 (excluding 0), preferably 0.3-0.5.

PN CN111362952-A
TI Preparation of substituted metal phthalocyanine derivative used in e.g. carbon dioxide reduction reaction, involves adding nitrile organic compound to precursor solution comprising metal salt, reacting, purifying, drying, and grinding.
AU LIU M
   CHEN G
   LI J
   CHEN K
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202063642Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of substituted metal phthalocyanine derivative involves adding a nitrile organic compound to a precursor solution comprising a metal salt, completely reacting, purifying the obtained reaction product, drying, and grinding the dried product.
   USE - Preparation of substituted metal phthalocyanine derivative used in electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reaction and carbon dioxide reduction reaction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of substituted metal phthalocyanine derivative having high purity, improved selectivity and yield, less impurity content, and suppressed side reactions and uncertain substitution positions. The substituted metal phthalocyanine derivative enables efficient electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reaction and carbon dioxide reduction reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The precursor solution is prepared by dissolving the metal salt in absolute ethanol as a solvent. The reaction is performed by heating at 100-250 degrees C at a heating rate of 10 degrees C/minute or less for 5-10 hours. The reaction product is purified by washing with deionized water and/or absolute ethanol, and dried at 60 degrees C for 2 hours. Preferred Composition: The nitrile organic compound is phthalonitrile, 2,3-dicyanohydroquinone, 4-nitrophthalonitrile or 4-aminophthalonitrile. The metal salt is metal acetate, preferably cobalt acetate, iron acetate, manganese acetate or nickel acetate. Raw materials of the substituted metal phthalocyanine derivative do not contain impurities that are difficult to remove. The mass ratio of the nitrile organic compound with respect to metal salt is 2.5-5. The usage amount of solvent is 10000-30000 ml with respect to 1 mol of solute in the precursor solution.

The air regeneration system                includes: the box body comprises a first containing                cavity, a second containing cavity and a third                containing cavity; the third containing cavity is                surrounded on the periphery of the second                containing cavity; a potassium super-oxide                medicament feeder, for delivering potassium                super-oxide medicament to the first containing                cavity; a water feeder; the water of the third                containing cavity is conveyed to the first                containing cavity; the top part of the second                containing cavity is provided with an air inlet                passage and an exhaust passage; the air inlet                passage is provided with an air inlet fan. on the                one hand, the whole structure is compact; on the                other hand, cold water stored in the third                containing cavity can be convenient for the second                containing cavity through the potassium super-oxide                medicament and water just after reaction; the                temperature of the potassium hydroxide solution                with high temperature is reduced; the temperature                of the gas flowing out from the second containing                cavity is relatively suitable; a certain degree                reduces the temperature of the gas flowing out from                the second containing cavity.

EA    NOVELTY - System comprises a potassium superoxide                chemical feeder (10); a water feeder (20); and a                mixing reaction oxygen generator (30). The feeding                port of the mixing reaction oxygen generator is                connected with the discharging port of the                potassium super-oxide chemical feeder and the water                feeding port of the water feeder. The top part of the                carbon dioxide absorber is provided with an air                inlet passage (51) comprising an air inlet fan                (53), and an exhaust passage (52). The carbon                dioxide absorber is used to mix potassium hydroxide                solution flowing into potassium hydroxide solution                outlet with the carbon dioxide in the air entering                from the air inlet channel to remove carbon dioxide                from air.

The biogas pretreatment unit comprises an activated carbon desulfurization tower (1), a low-pressure compressor (2), a molecular sieve (3), and a fine filter (4) connected in sequence. The activated carbon desulfurization tower is connected with biogas inlet pipe. The two-stage membrane separation unit includes a multi-stage compressor unit before separation, a first-stage membrane separator (7), a two-stage membrane separator, a first mixer and a multi-stage compressor unit on the permeation side. The air inlet of the multi-stage compressor unit before separation is connected to the outlet of the fine filter. The air outlet of the multi-stage compressor unit before separation is connected to the air inlet of the first-stage membrane separator.
   USE - Used as mixed refrigerant biogas pressure liquefaction system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: has reasonable design and high degree of automation; solves the problems of complex biogas decarbonization processes, harsh decarbonization indicators, and waste of carbon resources in the current biogas liquefaction process; and has good application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for working method of mixed refrigerant biogas pressure liquefaction system, comprising conveying biogas to the activated carbon desulfurization tower from the biogas inlet pipe to remove hydrogen sulfide, then compressing and cooling by the low-pressure compressor and conveying to the molecular sieve for deep dehydration, then removing the fine particles by the fine filter to obtain purified gas, conveying the purified gas to the primary membrane separator after being pressurized by the multi-stage compressor unit before separation, conveying the gas on the retentate side to the secondary membrane separator, conveying permeate gas to the first mixer, conveying the gas on the retentate side of the secondary membrane separator to the heat exchanger for liquefaction, conveying the permeate side gas of the secondary membrane separator to the first mixer, conveying the gas from the first mixer into the multi-stage compressor unit on the permeation side, pressurizing and conveying to the heat exchanger, mixing various refrigerant circulation media by the second mixer and then conveying the mixed refrigerant multi-stage compressor unit to be compressed and conveying the heat exchanger for cooling, throttling through the throttle valve, liquefying methane flow and rich carbon dioxide flow are liquefied in the heat exchanger, introducing liquid methane into the liquid methane storage tank for storage, conveying the carbon dioxide-rich stream to the rectification tower, introducing the high-purity liquid carbon dioxide after rectification at the bottom of the tower into the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank through the reboiler for storage, recovering the methane/carbon dioxide mixed gas at the top of the tower in the heat exchanger, and then burning in the vent torch.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the mixed refrigerant biogas pressure liquefaction system.
   Activated carbon desulfurization tower (1)
   Low-pressure compressor (2)
   Molecular sieve (3)
   Fine filter (4)
   First-stage membrane separator (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The main components of the purified gas are methane and carbon dioxide. The refrigerant circulation media are C1, C2, C3 and N2. The separation pressure difference between the primary membrane separator and the secondary membrane separator is 1-1.2 MPa, after separation by the primary membrane separator and the secondary membrane separator, the purity of the gas on the retentate side of the secondary membrane separator is 98.5-99.5%. The purified gas is boosted to 2 MPa in the multi-stage compressor unit before separation, the primary membrane separator and the secondary membrane separator on the permeate side of the carbon dioxide rich flow through the multi-permeation side multistage compressor unit to pressurize to 2.8 MPa. The purity of the output product of the reboiler is more than 99.95%, and the condensation temperature of the condenser is more than 56 degrees C.

TI Synthesis of methane involves carrying out one-step co-electrolysis of water and carbon dioxide in presence of electrolyte comprising at least one metal hydride superprotonic conducting acid compound, in pressurized electrolytic cell.
AU BJERRUM N J
   CHRISTENSEN E
   PETROUCHINA I M
   NIKIFOROV A
   BERG R W
AE UNIV DANMARKS TEKNISKE (UYRR-C)
   CO2TECHN APS (COTW-Non-standard)
GA 201924187G
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of methane by one-step co-electrolysis of water and carbon dioxide in presence of an electrolyte (40) involves providing a pressurized electrolytic cell (11) comprising an anode (20), a cathode (30), and the electrolyte arranged between the anode and cathode, and producing hydrogen ions at the anode by carrying out anode reaction by reacting 4 molecules of water to obtain 2 molecules of oxygen, 8 molecules of hydrogen ion, and 8 molecules of electrons, and producing methane at the cathode by carrying out cathode reaction by reacting carbon dioxide with the 8 molecules of hydrogen ion and 8 molecules of electrons to obtain methane with 2 molecules of water, and operating the electrolytic cell by providing an electrical current applied across the electrolytic cell to induce the anodic and cathodic reactions, and collecting a cathode product flow from cathodic reaction. The electrolyte comprises at least one metal hydride superprotonic conducting acid compound (I).
   USE - Synthesis of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively produces methane using the electrolytic cell in which decomposition reactions are suppressed and functional electrolyte is maintained. In the electrolytic cell, water does not condense, thus preventing irreversible conversion of the electrolyte or unwanted dissolution of the electrolyte.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of methane by one-step co-electrolysis of water and carbon dioxide in presence of an electrolyte involves providing a pressurized electrolytic cell comprising an anode, a cathode, and the electrolyte arranged between the anode and cathode, and producing hydrogen ions at the anode by carrying out anode reaction by reacting 4 molecules of water to obtain 2 molecules of oxygen, 8 molecules of hydrogen ion, and 8 molecules of electrons, and producing methane at the cathode by carrying out cathode reaction by reacting carbon dioxide with the 8 molecules of hydrogen ion and 8 molecules of electrons to obtain methane with 2 molecules of water, and operating the electrolytic cell by providing an electrical current applied across the electrolytic cell to induce the anodic and cathodic reactions, maintaining the electrolytic cell at an operating water partial pressure of 1-30 bar, maintaining the electrolytic cell at an operating temperature to induce superprotonic conduction in the electrolyte of hydrogen ions produced at the anode, providing a flow of gaseous water as superheated steam as an anode reactant flow to the anode at a flow rate enough to allow the anodic reaction, providing a flow of gaseous carbon dioxide as a cathode reactant flow to the cathode at a flow rate enough to allow the cathodic reaction, collecting a cathode product flow from cathodic reaction, and optionally isolating methane from the cathode product flow. The electrolyte comprises at least one metal hydride superprotonic conducting acid compound of formula: MxHy(AO4)z (I), where x is 1-3, y is 1-5, z is 1 or 2, M is at least one of lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, cesium, thallium, and ammonium, and A is sulfur, selenium, phosphorus, or arsenic. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) electrolytic cell system (10); and
   (2) use of compound (I) as the electrolyte in one-step co-electrolytic conversion of water and carbon dioxide to oxygen and methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the electrolytic cell system.
   Electrolytic cell system (10)
   Electrolytic cell (11)
   Anode (20)
   Cathode (30)
   Electrolyte (40)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (I) is preferably potassium dihydrogen phosphate, cesium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium pentahydrogen bisphosphate, or cesium pentahydrogen bisphosphate. The electrolyte comprises a mixture of at least two of the superprotonic conducting acid compounds, and further comprises a support material for the at least one superprotonic conducting acid compound. The support material comprises a pyrophosphate compound of formula: M't(P2O7) (II), where M't is silicon, germanium, or tin, optionally with a polybenzimidazole fiber reinforcement. The compound (II) is preferably tin pyrophosphate or tin pyrophosphate with the polybenzimidazole fiber reinforcement. The anode comprises at least one oxide of iridium, preferably iridium dioxide and/or iridium trioxide. The cathode comprises at least one of nickel or its alloy, cobalt, ruthenium, and rhodium, preferably nickel.

TI Purifying feed gas stream having dry base carbon            dioxide and impurity of e.g. chlorinated compounds,            comprises e.g. subjecting feed gas stream to catalytic            oxidation to produce gas stream having acidic impurity            of e.g. hydrogen chloride.
AU RODRIGUES G
   ARPENTINIER P
   FRANCO A
   JABES R
   LAGOUTTE F
   PERRU L
   WERLEN E
   VERLAN A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 201915322M
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying feed gas stream comprising dry base                carbon dioxide and impurity of chlorinated, sulfur,                nitrated or fluorinated compounds, comprises: (a)                subjecting feed gas stream to catalytic oxidation                to produce gas stream comprising acidic impurity of                hydrogen chloride, nitrogen oxides (NOx), sulfur                oxides (SOx) or hydrofluoric acid; (b) maintaining                temperature of gas stream from step (a) above                highest value between the dew point of the water                and the dew point of the acid(s) contained in the                gas downstream of the catalytic process; (c)                removing at least a portion of the acidic                impurities by contacting the gas stream from step                (b) in contact with at least one                corrosion-resistant heat exchanger to condense the                acidic compounds while regulating the temperature                of the outgoing gas stream below the dew point of                the water; and (d) separating the acid condensates                from the gas stream from step (c) by means of a                corrosion-resistant separator to produce a gas                stream enriched with carbon dioxide.
   USE - The process and installation are useful for                purifying feed gas stream comprising dry base                carbon dioxide and impurity of chlorinated, sulfur,                nitrated or fluorinated compounds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process and installation: utilize the step                (b) that avoids any creation of acidic liquid which                corrodes the piping and other standard materials;                and utilize the thermal insulation or even an                external temperature maintenance system (electric                or steam) that prevents the creation of cold spots                (temperature locally below the critical dew point)                on the equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying a feed gas stream comprising at                least 90% of dry base carbon dioxide, at least 20%                relative humidity and at least one impurity of                chlorinated, sulfur, nitrated or fluorinated                compounds, comprises: (a) subjecting the feed gas                stream to catalytic oxidation to produce a gas                stream comprising at least one acidic impurity of                hydrogen chloride, nitrogen oxides (NOx), sulfur                oxides (SOx) or hydrofluoric acid, where x is                undefined; (b) maintaining the temperature of the                gas stream from step (a) above the highest value                between the dew point of the water and the dew                point of the acid(s) contained in the gas                downstream of the catalytic process; (c) removing                at least a portion of the acidic impurities by                contacting the gas stream from step (b) in contact                with at least one corrosion-resistant heat                exchanger to condense the acidic compounds while                regulating the temperature of the outgoing gas                stream below the dew point of the water; and (d)                separating the acid condensates from the gas stream                from step (c) by means of a corrosion-resistant                separator to produce a gas stream enriched with                carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for an installation comprising in the                circulating direction of the gas stream (a1) a                catalytic oxidation unit for subjecting the gas                stream to catalytic oxidation to produce a gas                stream comprising the acidic impurity, (b1) a means                of maintaining the temperature of the gas stream                leaving the catalytic oxidation unit above the                acidic dew point, (c1) a corrosion resistant heat                exchanger for condensing the acidic compounds of                the gas stream, and (d1) a corrosion resistant                separator for separating the acidic compounds from                the gas stream to produce a gas stream enriched                with carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heat exchanger and                  the separator comprise materials comprising                  austenitic steel, glass or composite resistant to                  nitric acid, sulfuric or hydrochloric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The installation                  comprises downstream of the separator and in the                  flow direction of the gas stream enriched in                  carbon dioxide, (e1) a liquefier for liquefying                  the gas stream enriched in carbon dioxide, (f1) a                  dryer for drying the liquefied gas, and (g1) a                  cryogenic unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process comprises,                  after step (d), the following successive steps of                  (e) a liquefaction step of the gas stream                  enriched with carbon dioxide, (f) a step of                  drying the liquefied flow, and (g) a step of                  sending the dried stream into a cryogenic unit.                  The catalytic oxidation is carried out by means                  of a sulfur and chlorine-resistant catalytic                  oxidation unit. The feed gas stream subjected to                  the catalytic oxidation is at a temperature of at                  least 300 degrees C, preferably at least 425?Oc.                  The feed gas stream subjected to the catalytic                  oxidation is at a pressure of at least 1 bar                  absolute. The feed gas stream comes from a unit                  of monoethylene glycol, a carbon dioxide                  absorption wash, a monoethylene glycol synthesis                  unit, a biofermentation or any other process that                  generates a flow rich in carbon dioxide. In step                  (c), the water is used as a refrigerant within                  the heat exchanger.
   EXAMPLE - Flow was contacted with a catalyst bed                containing a first layer of platinum catalyst and a                second layer of palladium catalyst, which converts                most of the hydrocarbons into water and carbon                dioxide. On leaving the catalytic reactor, the gas                flow had a temperature above 300 degrees C and                containing hydrogen chloride was cooled to a                temperature above the dew point of water (43                degrees C), to prevent condensation. Minimum                temperature of 55 degrees C, i.e. 12 degrees C                above the theoretical temperature will be                maintained, thus avoiding measuring inaccuracies                and insulation defects of the equipment. The fluid,                at a temperature of 55 degrees C, was then be fed                into a skid comprising a heat exchanger made of a                material resistant to chloridic acid. The cold was                brought into the exchanger with a coolant e.g.                water, at a temperature below 43 degrees C,                preferably below 10 degrees C to minimize the size                of the exchanger. Only condensation of water                combined with a large exchange surface will ensure                the removal of hydrogen chloride contained in the                gas phase due to the very high solubility of                hydrogen chloride in water (720 g/l at 20 degrees                C.) The separation of the liquid droplets                containing the acidic molecules were ensured by a                separator pot (installed directly downstream of the                exchanger) of a material resistant to chlorine                corrosion or more simply by a polymer resin                material. The process gas at the outlet of the                separator pot and the liquid condensed, purged at                the bottom of the separator pot to obtain purified                stream.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

GA 202234559A
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an economizer for heating the CO2 rich solvent transferred from the absorption vessel by heat exchange. The liquid phase under the stripper is a reboiler (L2) to be vaporized by a heating medium. The first CO2 rich solvent stream divided in the separation unit is heated using the gas transferred from the upper portion of the stripper as a heating medium. A stripper interheater heats the semi-lean solvent discharged from the stripper and reflux the stripper using the discharged heating medium and used as the heating medium in the reboiler. By reducing the pressure of the liquid CO2 lean solvent in the reboiler before being transferred to the economizer, the vaporized gas is compressed and transferred to the stripper, and the reduced pressure liquid CO2 lean solvent is transferred to the economizer. A heat recovery unit improves process efficiency by recovering compression heat generated from the CO2 compression unit.
   USE - CO2 capture process system for collecting carbon dioxide, for power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The total renewable energy is reduced by reducing the avoidable fraction of renewable energy. The net power output is improved and the efficiency penalty is reduced. The leveling cost of electricity is reduced through the upgrading process.

The process uses inert gas dry gas stripping to replace steam stripping, which greatly reduces the consumption of steam; and the tower top gas stripping cooling and reflux system, avoids and reduces the occurrence of the blocking and corrosion condition from source, but also saves the circulating water consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Condensed liquid dry gas stripping process for treating components containing carbon dioxide, ammonia and hydrogen sulfide involves carbon dioxide, ammonia and hydrogen sulfide and other component process condensing liquid from the upper portion of the gas stripping tower into the tower, after being distributed by liquid distributor, entering into filler bed layer, using inert gas as gas stripping medium of gas process condensing liquid, the stripping medium enters from the lower portion of the gas stripping tower, after being distributed by the gas distributor, entering into the gas stripping tower packing bed layer, blowing and stripping the process condensing liquid, dissolving the dissolved gas in the process condensing liquid, including but not limited to carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and so on with process condensing liquid, the carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and so on dissolved in the process condensing liquid are separated by stripping medium gas and then enter into the gas stripping medium. The gas medium from the tower top of the gas stripping tower is mixed with the pre-heated inert gas and is burnt or sent to the sulfur recycling device to be uniformly processed, the process condensed liquid after gas stripping is sent out from the bottom of the tower and recycled or sent to the outside.

PN WO2022014554-A1
TI Producing carbonate salt, by performing atomization using alkaline aqueous solution as mist with atomizer, mixing mist of alkaline aqueous solution and exhaust gas, absorbing carbon dioxide of exhaust gas in mist, mixing cations of mist and carbon dioxide, and separating carbonate-containing mist.
AU KAZUO M
   TOSHIHARU C
   TOMOYOSHI N
   YUJI A
   SOICHIRO S
   SHINGO T
AE NANOMIST TECHNOLOGIES CO LTD (NANO-Non-standard)
   SOJITZ CORP (SOJI-Non-standard)
GA 2022167861
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a carbonate salt, involves performing atomization process using an alkaline aqueous solution as a mist with an atomizer, mixing the mist of the alkaline aqueous solution obtained in the atomization step and exhaust gas to form a mixture, absorbing the carbon dioxide of the exhaust gas in the mist, mixing cations of the mist and carbon dioxide to form a carbonate to obtain the mist containing carbonate, and separating the carbonate-containing mist obtained in the mixing step from the exhaust gas.
   USE - The method is used for producing carbonate salt.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbonate production device comprising an atomizer that atomizes an alkaline aqueous solution to make a mist, a mixer that mixes the mist and exhaust gas generated by the atomizer and combines the cations of the mist and carbon dioxide to form a carbonate, and a separator that separates the mist obtained by the mixer from the exhaust gas and recovers the mist containing carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves using a caustic soda solution as an alkaline aqueous solution in the atomization step, reacting the carbon dioxide gas of the exhaust gas and the caustic soda of the mist in the mixing step to form a mist containing sodium carbonate, and separating a mist containing sodium carbonate from exhaust gas in the separation step. The method further involves reacting the sodium carbonate solution obtained in the separation step with calcium hydroxide to obtain calcium carbonate, performing a pretreatment step for separating air pollutants contained in exhaust gas and using the carbonate gas of the exhaust gas from which the air pollutant is separated as a raw material to produce a carbonate, and removing a fine particle substance contained in an exhaust gas, and using the carbonate gas of the exhaust gas from which the fine particle substance is separated as a raw material to produce a carbonate. The reaction step involves mixing calcium hydroxide with the sodium carbonate solution, reacting sodium carbonate and calcium hydroxide to obtain calcium carbonate, and separating and drying a calcium carbonate precipitate obtained in the reaction step. Preferred Conditions: In the atomization step, the atomizer ultrasonically vibrates the alkaline aqueous solution to form a mist, or the atomizer ultrasonically vibrates the alkaline aqueous solution to blow exhaust gas to the surface of a liquid column protruding from the liquid surface to produce a carbonate that mixes mist and exhaust gas, or the atomizer ultrasonically vibrates the alkaline aqueous solution and blows a conveyed gas to the surface of a liquid column protruding from the liquid surface to form a mist mixed gas, in which the mist mixed gas and exhaust gas are mixed in the mixing step, or the atomizer electrostatically atomizes the spray water of an alkaline aqueous solution sprayed from a nozzle to form a mist, or the atomizer blows exhaust gas to an electrostatically atomized mist injected from a nozzle to mix the mist and the exhaust gas, or the atomizer blows a conveyed gas to the mist jetted from the nozzle and electrostatically atomized to form a mist mixed gas that mixes a mist mixed gas and an exhaust gas in the mixing step. Preferred Properties: The average particle size of the mist of the alkaline aqueous solution is less than or equal to 50 mu m, preferably less than or equal to 30 mu m in the atomization step. The average particle size of the mist of the alkaline aqueous solution is greater than or equal to 100 nm in the atomization step. The mist containing the carbonate is separated from the exhaust gas by a cyclone in the separation step. In the mixing step, the mist of the alkaline aqueous solution and the exhaust gas are mixed with a static mixer, and the inside of the mixer is kept below the dew point temperature. Preferred Components: In the atomization step, a caustic soda solution produced from seawater is used in the alkaline aqueous solution as a raw material. The alkaline aqueous solution is an aqueous solution containing an alkali metal or an alkaline earth metal, or a natural product or a waste containing an alkali metal or an alkaline earth metal dissolved in water.

TI Pressurized medicinal composition used in metered            dose inhalers to treat e.g. asthma, has liquid carbon            dioxide, fluid that forms solution with liquid carbon            dioxide, and active ingredient dissolved or suspended            in composition.
AU BUNTING J P
   DEXTER S J
   HODSON P D
   SLOWEY A D
AE KINDEVA DRUG DELIVERY LP (KIND-Non-standard)
   KINDEVA DRUG DELIVERY LP (KIND-Non-standard)
GA 202183120M
AB    NOVELTY - Pressurized medicinal composition comprises                40-98 wt.% first component i.e. liquid carbon                dioxide, 2-60 wt.% second component i.e. a fluid                that forms a solution with liquid carbon dioxide,                and at least one active ingredient dissolved or                suspended in the composition.
   USE - The composition is used in carbon dioxide                based metered dose inhalers for treating                respiratory disorders e.g. asthma or chronic                obstructive pulmonary disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The pressurized medicine composition contains                ethanol or isopropyl alcohol, which increases the                liquid to supercritical phase transition                temperature, hence the inhaler remains stable over                a normal range of use temperatures, and lowers the                pressure within the canister, and/or increases the                temperature of the aerosol spray delivered by an                inhaler.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                metered dose inhaler (10), comprising a metering                valve, a canister (14), and an actuator (12), where                the canister contains the composition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a side view of an inhaler                including a canister containing a valve.
   Metered dose inhaler (10)
   Actuator (12)
   Canister (14)
   Actuator body (16)
   Canister opening (18)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The size of the                  metering valve is 25-100 mu l. The canister                  comprises 1-30 ml composition. The canister                  contains a predetermined number of doses i.e.                  30-200. The pressure of the composition in the                  canister at 20 degrees C is 30-55 bar, and at 40                  degrees C is 40-75 bar. The spray temperature is                  greater than 5 degrees C. The canister is                  equipped with a metering valve, where the                  canister houses a reservoir comprising a                  pressurized carrier fluid mixture for dissolving                  or suspending the active ingredient.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises a carrier fluid and active                  ingredient dissolved or suspended in the carrier                  fluid, where the carrier fluid comprises 40-98                  wt.% first component and 2-60 wt.% second                  component mixed together in a homogeneous                  solution, the first component is liquid carbon                  dioxide, and the second component is liquid at                  room temperature and pressure, or the first                  component is a propellant and the second                  component is a non-propellant, and the first                  propellant component is liquid carbon dioxide.                  The composition preferably comprises at least 40                  wt.% first component, the second component, and                  active ingredient dissolved or suspended in the                  composition, where the liquid to supercritical                  transition temperature of the composition is                  greater than 40 degrees C, preferably 40-120                  degrees C. The liquid carbon dioxide is sole                  propellant. The second component is an alcohol,                  preferably ethanol or isopropyl alcohol. The                  active ingredient comprises bronchodilators,                  corticosteroids or anti-cholinergic agent. The                  composition comprises 2-20 wt.% second                  component.
   ACTIVITY - Antiasthmatic; Respiratory-gen. No biological                data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Solution of beclomethasone dipropionate in                99.5% ethanol was prepared by adding 0.23 g                beclomethasone to 10 ml ethanol. Then 0.87 g                aliquot of the beclomethasone dipropionate in                ethanol solution and 6.09 g carbon dioxide were                added to a refillable 12 g canister.

TI Secondary battery used for charging/discharging process comprises electrolyte consisting of ion-conductive solid oxide arranged between negative and positive electrodes in which carbon dioxide is electrolyzed on negative electrode surface.
AU KAMEDA K
   IHARA M
   HASEGAWA K
AE TOKYO INST TECHNOLOGY NAT UNIV CORP (TOKD-C)
GA 2020206213
AB    NOVELTY - A secondary battery (1) comprises electrolyte (4) consisting of ion-conductive solid oxide arranged between negative electrode (2) and positive electrode (3), in which carbon dioxide is electrolyzed on surface of negative electrode at the time of charge, carbon precipitates to negative electrode side at the surface of negative electrode at the time of discharge. Carbon dioxide is produced by oxidizing carbon and/or carbon monoxide electrochemically.
   USE - A secondary battery used for charging/discharging process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Safety is high, large energy can be stored and the battery can hold down material cost to low. The secondary battery is carbon dioxide emission-free since carbon dioxide generated by discharge can be used as repetition carbon (fuel) source.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for charging/discharging process which involves performing charge and discharge with secondary battery (1).

   Secondary battery (1)
   Negative electrode (2)
   Closed system (2A)
   Positive electrode (3)
   Electrolyte (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Carbon dioxide (CO2) is produced by reduction reaction (R1) of CO2 and negative electrode (2e-) producing carbon monoxide (CO) and oxygen atoms (O2-) (R1). Carbon precipitates to negative electrode side when produced carbon monoxide (2CO) turns into carbon dioxide (CO) and carbon (C) by thermochemistry equilibrium reaction. Negative electrode side is closed system. Closed system is formed of cylindrical shape cell which closes one side or its aggregate. The closed system can store a carbon dioxide in liquid or solid state. The battery charges at low temperature rather than discharge. Charge and discharge are performed at 600-1000 degrees C, preferably 700-1000 degrees C. The charging/discharging process discharges on conditions to which thermochemistry equilibrium reaction performs charge on conditions with incline to right side easily, and inclines to a left side easily. Preferred Components: Positive electrode side is open system. Negative electrode has catalyst which accelerates carbon precipitation. Electrolyte is stabilized zirconia, lanthanum gallate or ceria system solid solution. Negative electrode material is cermet which consists of composite metal oxide or composite metal oxide, and metal. Cermet is nickel (Ni)/yttria stabilized zirconia (YSZ), Ni/gadolinium-doped ceria (GDC), Ni/scandia stabilized zirconia (ScSZ), or Ni/samaria-doped ceria (SDC).
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

AU LANG F

   CHENG P
   PENG H
   MA Y
   HE J
   LI J
   HUANG F
   SONG J
AE WUHAN STEEL CO LTD (WSGC-C)
GA 2018A0334K
AB    NOVELTY - A pipeline steel carbon dioxide corrosion testing method involves preparing pipeline steel sample and subjecting to bending stress loading to obtain a curved pipeline steel sample, preparing a corrosion solution using simulated oil well production fluid, placing bent pipeline steel sample in a closed reaction vessel, adding an etching solution into the closed reaction vessel to introduce carbon dioxide gas, controlling the temperature of the closed reactor at 50-90 degrees C, the partial pressure to 0.9-1.2 MPa and the flow rate to 1.3-1.7 m/s, taking out bent pipeline steel sample after a period of time, placing the bent pipeline steel sample in the pickling liquid for a period of time, taking out the bent pipeline steel sample, washing with deionized water and absolute ethanol, drying and weighing the blown bent steel samples to obtain a corrosion rate R.
   USE - Method for testing carbon dioxide corrosion of pipeline steel under bending stress.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables testing carbon dioxide corrosion of pipeline steel under bending stress in quick and accurate manner.

GA 202265880T
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling temperature of culturing induced pluripotent stem (IPS) cell in carbon dioxide culture box involves setting the temperature preset value SV for different time period according to the cell culture of different types of IPS. The temperature control deviation value ER is calculated between real-time temperature and temperature preset value through the acquired real-time temperature PV inside the carbon dioxide culture box which includes three states, where in state 1 the next set temperature greater than previous progress temperature and ER greater than 0, and in state 2 the next set temperature = last operating temperature, and ER = 0, and in state 3 the next set temperature less than previous operating temperature, and ER less than 0. The state of difference between current temperature and next set temperature is determined, and when ER greater than 0 then controller controls the heater to run in a preset state, and when ER=0 then controller controls the heater to run in a preset state.
   USE - Method for controlling temperature of culturing induced pluripotent stem cell in carbon dioxide culture box.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Controlling temperature of culturing induced pluripotent stem (IPS) cell in carbon dioxide culture box involves setting the temperature preset value SV for different time period according to the cell culture of different types of IPS. The temperature control deviation value ER is calculated between real-time temperature and temperature preset value through the acquired real-time temperature PV inside the carbon dioxide culture box which includes three states, where in state 1 the next set temperature greater than previous progress temperature and ER greater than 0, and in state 2 the next set temperature = last operating temperature, and ER = 0, and in state 3 the next set temperature less than previous operating temperature, and ER less than 0. The state of difference between current temperature and next set temperature is determined, and when ER greater than 0 then controller controls the heater to run in a preset state, and when ER=0 then controller controls the heater to run in a preset state, and when ER less than 0 then controller controls the heater to run in the preset state. The controller controls the operation of the micro air pump, so that low-temperature air enters the incubator and real-time temperature PV inside the carbon dioxide culture box is adjusted to the set value, where temperature SV is the same.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of culturing induced pluripotent stem cell is controlled in carbon dioxide culture box, and when ER greater than 0 and ER greater than 0.5 then SVK=SV+SVasterisk(ER/15)asteriskST, where ST is heating time, heater control output OUT=PID control output and PIDOUTasterisk( 1-(SVPV)), and when 0.1degrees Celsius less than ER less than or equal to 0.5degrees Celsius then other heater control is stopped and bottom heater is turned on, and intermittent heating control is performed according to PID output value, where bottom heater control output BTOUT=PID control output PIDOUTasterisk(1+(SV- PV)) and intermittent time T=128asterisk(SV-PV)-0.663, and when SV-PVless than or equal to0.1degrees Celsius then bottom heater control output BTOUT=0, and when ER=0 then heater control output OUT=PID control output PIDOUT, and when ER less than 0 then 2-position 3-way solenoid valve and micro-motion air pump are opened to input room temperature air into the incubator through the HEPA filter, so that temperature is consistent with the set temperature SV, and when ER less than -0.5, SVK=SV+SVasterisk(ER/15)asteriskheating time ST and heater control output OUT=PID control output PIDOUTasterisk(1-(SV-PV)), and when ER greater than or equal to -0.5degrees Celsius then air pump is turned on, and solenoid valve switch performs PWM intermittent on-off control according to PID output value, OUT=PIDOUTasterisk(1-(SVPV)) and intermittent time T=300asterisk(SV-PV )-0.32, and when the solenoid valve is opened then air enters the incubator, and when solenoid valve is closed and air is emptied through the normally open channel of solenoid valve, and when SV-PV less than =0.1degrees Celsius then solenoid valve and air pump are closed, and air input is stopped.

PN CN112295262-A
TI Blueberry flavor liquid extraction, by picking ripe, non-rotted fresh blueberries, washing, cutting, soaking fruit pieces in phosphate solution, beating, filtering, enzymatically hydrolyzing, extracting and separating anthocyanins.
AU WENCHENG J
AE QINGDAO CHENGQUN XINGSHENG COMMERCE (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2021167165
AB    NOVELTY - Blueberry flavor liquid extraction process, involves (i) picking ripe, non-rotted fresh blueberries, washing and cutting, (ii) soaking the fruit pieces in a phosphate solution, and beating the soaked liquid to obtain a blueberry pulp, (iii) filtering blueberry slurry to remove filter residue, and placing filtrate in a concentration equipment for concentrating, (iv) passing the concentrated blueberry pulp to a reaction device, adding 0.1% pectinase and 0.1% cellulase to the reaction device, and enzymatically hydrolyzing, (v) mixing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid with blueberry pulp, and performing supercritical fluid extraction at 35-45 degrees C for 2-3 hours to obtain a blueberry flavor liquid, and (vi) introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid with blueberry flavor liquid to a separator to separate anthocyanins from the carbon dioxide fluid to obtain the blueberry flavor liquid.
   USE - Blueberry flavor liquid extraction process.
   ADVANTAGE - The process prevents use of heavy equipment which is not easy to handle, uses relatively stable device, which can speed up the extraction rate, prevent uneven mixing of materials and prevent the waste of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid from sticking to the inner wall of the inner reaction tank and not being completely discharged, saves economic costs, facilitates the cleaning of the device after the preparation is completed, can prevent the liquid oil from overflowing due to the added oil, and can prevent the low oil volume from causing poor heating effect of the oil bath.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Blueberry flavor liquid extraction process, involves (i) picking ripe, non-rotted fresh blueberries, washing and cutting, (ii) soaking the fruit pieces in a phosphate solution with a trehalose concentration of 0.7 mol/l trehalose for 3-4 hours, and beating the soaked liquid to obtain a blueberry pulp, (iii) filtering the blueberry slurry obtained in step (ii) to remove the filter residue, and placing the filtrate in a concentration equipment for concentrating, (iv) passing the concentrated blueberry pulp to a reaction device, adding 0.1% pectinase and 0.1% cellulase to the reaction device, and enzymatically hydrolyzing at 51-53 degrees C in oil bath for 2 hours, (v) mixing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid with the blueberry pulp after enzymatic hydrolysis in the reaction device, and performing supercritical fluid extraction at 35-45 degrees C for 2-3 hours to obtain a blueberry flavor liquid, and (vi) introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid with blueberry flavor liquid to a separator to separate anthocyanins from the carbon dioxide fluid to obtain the blueberry flavor liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The reaction device used in step (iv) comprises a device body, and a semicircular sealing cover fixedly installed on the upper end of the device body. A motor is arranged at the center of the upper end of the sealing cover, and three sets of support structures fixedly installed at the lower end of the device main body. A movable roller is fixedly installed on the side of the lower end of the device main body close to the support structure. A threaded connection pipe is installed at one end of the device main body. The one end of the threaded connecting pipe is provided with a connecting pipe parallel to the device main body. An inner reaction tank is provided under the inner sealing cover of the device main body. A movable shaft is movably arranged inside the lower end sealing cover of the motor. The one end of the inner reaction tank penetrates the inner wall of the device main body and is provided with a discharge device below the threaded connecting pipe. The supporting structure comprises a threaded fixing rod, a threaded sleeve and a base. The outer surface of the upper end of the threaded fixing rod is fixedly connected to the outer surface of the lower end of the device main body. The lower end of the threaded fixing rod is threadably connected with the upper end of the threaded sleeve. The threaded fixing rod matches the diameter of the threaded sleeve. The outer surface of the lower end of the threaded sleeve is fixedly connected with the outer surface of the upper end of the base. The one end of the discharge pipe is fixedly connected to the inner reaction tank. The discharge pipe and the inner reaction tank are perpendicular to each other. The first fixing hole penetrates the inside of the middle end of the discharge pipe. The first piston is movably connected with the discharge pipe through the first fixed hole. The first through hole is fixedly connected with the first piston. The upper end of the branch sampling pipe is fixedly connected with the discharge pipe. The branch sampling tube and the device main body are parallel to each other. The second fixed hole is fixedly connected with the branch sampling pipe. The second piston is movably connected with the branch sampling pipe through the second fixed hole. The second through hole penetrates the middle end of the second piston. A screw hole is provided between the threaded connecting pipe and the device main body. The threaded connection pipe is fixedly connected to the inner wall of one end of the device main body through a screw hole. The outer surface of the cleaning rod is movably connected with the inner surface of the inner reaction tank. The connecting tube is made of high light-transmitting material. A stirring rod is provided at the lower end of the middle part of the connecting rod close to the cleaning rod. The outer surface of the upper end of the stirring rod is fixedly connected to the outer surface of the lower end of the connecting rod. The outer side of the stirring rod is provided with stirring blades at equal intervals. The stirring blade is fixedly connected to the outer surface of the stirring rod. The lower end of the motor is fixedly connected with the upper end of the sealing cover.

TI Device used for biogas purification, comprises fan, first filter, biological desulfurization tank, first compressor, buffer tank, filler tower, cold dryer, fixed bed dehydration device, second compressor and purifying gas-storage tank.
AU ZHANG L
   WANG J
AE XIAOGAN HUANYU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (XIAO-Non-standard)
GA 201891038R
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a fan, a first filter, a biological desulfurization tank, first compressor, a buffer tank, a filler tower, a cold dryer, a fixed bed dehydration device, a second compressor, and a purifying gas-storage tank in sequence. The biological desulfurization tank is a tank reactor in which desulfurized bacteria are dispersed inside. The biological desulfurization tank and the first compressor are connected with a deoxidizing device. The deoxidizing device comprises a heater, a fixed bed reactor and a biogas cooler connected in series. The fixed bed reactor is provided with a deoxidation catalyst. The filler tower is filled with carbon dioxide high adsorption capacity particles for carbon dioxide adsorption. The carbon dioxide high adsorption capacity particles are prepared by uniformly mixing terephthalic acid, chromium nitrate, diphenic acid and expanded graphite with water, reacting, washing, performing suction filtration, drying, mixing e.g. polyethyleneimine, and drying.
   USE - The device is useful for biogas purification (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device has high adsorption performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises a fan, a first filter, a biological desulfurization tank, first compressor, a buffer tank, a filler tower, a cold dryer, a fixed bed dehydration device, a second compressor, and a purifying gas-storage tank in sequence. The biological desulfurization tank is a tank reactor in which desulfurized bacteria are dispersed inside. The biological desulfurization tank and the first compressor are connected with a deoxidizing device. The deoxidizing device comprises a heater, a fixed bed reactor and a biogas cooler connected in series. The fixed bed reactor is provided with a deoxidation catalyst. The filler tower is filled with carbon dioxide high adsorption capacity particles for carbon dioxide adsorption. The carbon dioxide high adsorption capacity particles are prepared by uniformly mixing 3-10 pts. wt. terephthalic acid, 8-15 pts. wt. chromium nitrate, 0.3-1.5 pts. wt. diphenic acid, and 10-15 pts. wt. expanded graphite with 140-150 pts. wt. water, reacting at 120-150 degrees C for 30-40 hours, washing, performing suction filtration, drying, mixing dried product with 1-10 pts. wt. polyethyleneimine, 5-15 pts. wt. tetraethylene pentamine, 5-10 pts. wt. porous carbon dioxide capture agent and 100-120 pts. wt. anhydrous ethanol, performing ultrasonic dispersion for 1-5 hours, placing in an oven at 80-85 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, and drying at 105-110 degrees C for 10-15 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The fan is Roots blower.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous carbon dioxide capture agent is prepared by charging 35-40 pts. wt. 1,3,5-triphenylbenzene, 25-30 pts. wt. biphenyl, 18-25 pts. wt. acetaminophen, 36-42 pts. wt. dichloromethane, 20-30 pts. wt. 1,2-dibromopropane, 10-15 pts. wt. anhydrous tin tetrachloride, 3-10 pts. wt. boron fluoride, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. carbonyl tri acetate ammine copper, 1-5 pts. wt. tetrabutyl ammonium triphenyl tin, 0.01-0.08 pts. wt. 1-ethyl-3-mercaptothiourea hydrochloride, 1-5 pts. wt. undecylenic acid amide propylamine oxide in a reaction device, reacting at 20-25 degrees C with magnetic stirring under nitrogen protection and condensing reflux conditions for 12-25 minutes, firstly reacting at constant temperature for 1-5 hours, secondly reacting at 75-80 degrees C for 10-15 hours, filtering to obtain crude product filtrate cake, washing, extracting, and drying under reduced pressure for 20-30 hours.

AE UNIV CHINA PHARM (UYCP-C)
GA 2022834250
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of luteolin ultrafine particles                involves preparing luteolin solution by dissolving                luteolin crude drug in organic solvent to obtain                luteolin solution, introducing carbon dioxide to                crystallization kettle through coaxial nozzle at                certain flow rate, adjusting the temperature and                pressure in crystallization kettle, injecting                carbon dioxide, keeping the temperature and                pressure in crystallization kettle unchanged,                simultaneously pumping luteolin solution to                crystallization kettle from top of crystallization                kettle through coaxial nozzle at certain flow rate                by high-pressure infusion pump, introducing carbon                dioxide for certain time, adjusting flow rate of                carbon dioxide by the rotameter to maintain certain                flow rate, releasing the pressure to standard                atmospheric pressure, removing, opening the                crystallization kettle, and collecting the product,                where the organic solvent is a mixed solvent                prepared from diethyl ether and                2-methyltetrahydrofuran in a volume ratio of                20:1.

PT P
PN IN202221011195-A
TI Molecular catalyst used for reversible conversion of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide, comprises multi-functional redox-active ligand framework around copper core.
AU DUTTA A
   VISHAL V
   MAITI D
   LAHIRI G K
   GURIA S
   DOLUI D
   GHORAI S
   DAS C
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY BOMBAY (IITB-C)
GA 2022555989
AB    NOVELTY - A molecular catalyst for reversible carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide conversion, comprises a multifunctional redox-active ligand framework around a copper core, where ratio of copper to redox-active ligand is 1:1-1:4.
   USE - The copper-based catalyst is used for reversible conversion of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-based catalyst shows high product selectivity. The molecular catalyst exhibits not only carbon dioxide reduction reactivity but also achieves reversible catalytic behavior. The catalyst exhibits energy efficient catalytic response coupled with long-term stability. The catalyst is energy-efficient, robust, and economic catalyst for carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the molecular catalyst comprising (a) preparing the redox-active ligand, (b) adding the redox-active ligand to a solution of copper(II) perchlorate hexahydrate to obtain a mixture solution, (c) evaporating the mixture solution to obtain a residual solid, (d) boiling the residual solid obtained in step (c) in methanol-water mixture, filtering and dissolving the precipitate in methanol for crystallization and (e) obtaining crystals of the molecular catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The redox-active ligand is selected from 2-(phenylazo)pyridine and 6-amino 2(phenylazo)pyridine. The catalyst is bis(2-(phenylazo)pyridine) copper(I) perchlorate or bis(6-amino-2-(phenylazo)pyridine) copper(I) perchlorate. The catalyst exhibits reversible catalysis via both electrochemical and chemical pathways. The solution of copper(II) perchlorate hexahydrate is prepared in methanol/dichloromethane (1:2) solution. The violet crystals are obtained for bis(2-(phenylazo)pyridine) copper(I) perchlorate. The green crystals are obtained bis(6-amino-2-(phenylazo)pyridine) copper(I) perchlorate.
   EXAMPLE - 974.5 mg 2-(phenylazo)pyridine was added dropwise to a methanol/dichloromethane (1:2) blended solution (50 ml) containing 986.3 mg copper(II) perchlorate hexahydrate. The mixture was stirred overnight at room temperature, where a violet solution was obtained. The solution was evaporated under reduced pressure and the residual solid was repetitively washed with n-pentane until the filtrate became colorless. Next, the violet color sticky solid product was boiled in methanol-water mixture (3:1). The resulting precipitate was filtered and dissolved in methanol for crystallization. Violet color crystals appeared next day that were further dried under vacuum to obtain bis(2-(phenylazo)pyridine) copper(I) perchlorate.

AE JINYUN DELI VALVE FACTORY (JINY-Non-standard)
GA 202126494P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing a mud core on a lower parting surface of a casting cavity, and placing the mud core and covering an upper cavity of the casting cavity. The upper and lower cavities are fixed by a flower basket screw rod. A carbon dioxide gas pipe is inserted into a bottom part of a pouring gate and a front flange or a bottom part of a riser farthest from the pouring gate. The riser is covered with a paper board or a cover board. Air flow of the carbon dioxide gas pipe is adjusted. The carbon dioxide gas is blown for 15-25 minutes by the carbon dioxide gas pipe.
   USE - The method is useful for eliminating air hole of stainless steel casting cast by sodium silicate sand.

   FAN Z
   LI R
   QIU Y

GA 202015491D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating flue gas, involves (a) introducing flue gas into a cryogenic unit, cooling the feed gas, sending to a membrane separation unit to facilitate gas permeation, forming a high pressure on a flow side upstream of the membrane, and causing a relatively low pressure on the permeate side to form a gas permeable membrane, and (b) passing the exhaust gas through a pump to a heat exchanger to cool the subsequent flue gas after being treated by the membrane separation unit, while pumping the permeated carbon dioxide rich gas to a cryogenic unit, sublimating the carbon dioxide into a dry ice of solid form in the cryogenic unit, and passing residual gas such as dinitrogen without phase change to achieve carbon dioxide enrichment and storage, where the whole method consists of a low temperature unit, a membrane separation unit and a deep cooling unit.

   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the recovery rate and purity of carbon dioxide, and effectively reduces the energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cold energy required by the low-temperature and cryogenic units is provided by liquefied natural gas. The cold energy of the two gas streams (residual gas and carbon dioxide products) separated by the cryogenic unit is recovered by two other heat exchangers respectively to cool the subsequent inflow gas. Preferred Condition: The temperature of the low temperature unit is set to -20 degrees C. The temperature of the cryogenic unit is set to -120 degrees C, so that the carbon dioxide phase changes.

PN CN110256524-A
TI Extracting sea cucumber saponin comprises performing supercritical extraction, separating carbon dioxide and extract, performing ethanol absorption, subjecting resin adsorption purification and decolorizing.
AU FANG X
   HOU S
   HAN L
AE RONGCHENG HUIHAI CHUANGDA BIOTECHNOLOGY (RONG-Non-standard)
GA 201991794A
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting sea cucumber saponin comprises (i) performing supercritical extraction using sea cucumber by-product, taking supercritical carbon dioxide as extractant, and beeswax as cosolvent for extracting carbon dioxide fluid extract, where extraction temperature is 30-45 degrees C, fluid pressure is 20-40 Mpa and flow rate is 0.10-0.40 ml/minutes for 1-3 hours, separating carbon dioxide and extract in carbon dioxide fluid extract to obtain an extract, performing ethanol absorption to obtain an absorption liquid, (ii) subjecting resin adsorption purification absorption liquid to adsorption purification by a macroporous adsorption resin, analyzing 65-75% sea cucumber saponin by using ethanol as an eluent, collecting active ingredient and collecting sea cucumber saponin eluate, and (iii) decolorizing sea cucumber saponin eluate by adding decoloring agent, filtering and washing to obtain filtrate and washing liquid, and (iv) concentrating, drying filtrate and washing liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting sea cucumber saponin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces high purity of sea cucumber saponin and has high extraction rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for sea cucumber saponin prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the addition amount of ethanol is 2-3 times the volume of the carbon dioxide fluid extract, and the volume concentration is 20-40%. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the separation pressure in separation of carbon dioxide and the extract is 7.8 to 12 MPa, and the temperature is 32.8-55 degrees C. In the step (ii), the addition amount of absorption liquid is the same as resin, the flow rate of the absorption liquid is controlled to be twice the bed volume per hour. In the step (ii), the ethanol elution flow rate is controlled to be 1-1.2 bed volumes per hour. In step (ii), the amount of the sea cucumber saponin eluate is collected to be 6-7 times the weight of the resin. In the step (iii), the decolorizing agent is magnesium oxide. The addition amount of magnesium oxide added per 100 ml of sea cucumber saponin eluate is 1-5 g and decolorizing for 1-1.5 hours. In the step (iv), the concentration temperature is less than or equal to 80 degrees C, the concentration of the vacuum is less than or equal to -0.08 Mpa, and the concentration is concentrated to a relative density of 1.1-1.15.

   JAIN A

AE EXXONMOBIL CHEM PATENTS INC (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL CHEM PATENTS INC (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL CHEM PATENTS INC (ESSO-C)
GA 201922948H
AB    NOVELTY - A polymer is manufactured by (a) polymerizing greater than or equal to 1 monomers in presence of solvent and catalyst to form a reaction product; (b) removing an effluent from reaction product where effluent consists of active catalyst and greater than or equal to 1 unreacted monomers; (c) combining a quench containing carbon dioxide with effluent to form a quenched polymer stream (135) containing carboxyl metal complex; and (d) recovering a polymer from quenched polymer stream.
   USE - Manufacture of polymer used for article (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses carbon dioxide which is excellent quenching agent, particularly in a solution polymerization process. Carbon dioxide has been discovered to prevent insoluble components, such as inorganic precipitates, when used as a quench agent in solution polymerization. Carbon dioxide reacts via an insertion mechanism to form a carboxyl metal complex where the metal is covalently bonded to the polymer chain. Carboxyl metal complex is soluble in the polymerization effluent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the solution polymerization reaction system.
   Polymerization reactor (105)
   Quenched polymer stream (135)
   Polymer-rich stream (145)
   Polymer-lean stream (155)
   Polymer feed stream (180)

Adsorbent bed further comprises greater than or equal to 1 silica adsorbent. Molecular sieve comprises material selected from zeolite X, zeolite Y, zeolite A, faujasite, mordenite, and/or ferrierite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carboxyl metal complex is complex of formula (1). Catalyst is compound of formula (I). Solvent is liquid hydrocarbon. Quench contains solvent or carbon dioxide and solvent. Quench is free of water or methanol.Hf'=catalyst;P'=reaction product;J=divalent bridging group containing C and/or Si;M=group 4 transition metal;X=univalent anionic ligand, or metallocycle ring when 2 Xs joined with M, or chelating ligand, diene ligand or alkylidene ligand when two Xs are joined; andR1-R10=H or 1-10C optionally substituted alkyl.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: Step (d) further includes separating the quenched polymer stream into polymer stream and polymer recycle stream, where polymer stream is separated from polymer recycle stream to recover the polymer; and contacting greater than or equal to 1 portion of polymer recycle stream with adsorbent bed comprising greater than or equal to 2 adsorbents to produce a treated recycle stream. Preferred Components: Monomers comprise 2-40 carbon-containing alpha -olefins or greater than or equal to 1 polyenes. Monomers comprise ethylene, propylene, and diene. Preferred Properties: Polymer has less than or equal to 5% increase in Mooney viscosity (ML 1+4, 125 degrees C) compared to comparative polymer prepared by the process without adding the quench in step (c).
   Preferred Definitions:
   R6,R9=1-4C alkyl;
   R1-R4,R5,R7,R8,R10=Me, Et or H where R1R2, R3R4, R5R6, R7R8 and R9R10 form saturated oro partially saturated cyclic ring structure;
   M=Hf; and
   X=1-3 alkyl or halo.
