PT P
PN CN108728151-A; CN108728151-B
TI Method for preparing alpha -olefin, involves            reacting methane with water vapor under condition of            steam reforming reaction, reacting methane with carbon            dioxide, and re-integrating portion of water vapor into            gas.
AU JIN C
   WU Y
   ZHANG R
   HOU C
   SUN X
   YAN Z
   XIA G
   LI M
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 201887231U
AB    NOVELTY - A alpha -olefin preparing method involves                reacting methane with water vapor under the                condition of steam reforming reaction to obtain                steam reforming synthesis gas, reacting methane                with carbon dioxide under dry reforming conditions                to obtain a dry reforming synthesis gas. A portion                of the water vapor is re-integrated into a gas and                integrated a partial steam reforming Tropsch                synthesis catalyst and Fischer synthesis gas to                prepare a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed.                The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed is                contacted with a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst                at a reaction temperature for producing a alpha                -olefin to obtain a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis                product stream. The alpha -olefin, methane and                carbon dioxide are separated from the                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream, sent the                separated methane to steam reforming synthesis gas                and dry reforming synthesis gas forming steps, and                sent the separated carbon dioxide to dry reforming                synthesis gas forming step.
   USE - Method for preparing alpha -olefin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the alpha -olefin                with effectively reducing the system energy                consumption and greenhouse carbon dioxide                emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                alpha -olefin production system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first metal                  element is selected from the VIII group metal                  element that is selected from iron, copper and                  nickel. The second metal element is manganese.                  The third metal element is selected from lithium,                  sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium, zirconium                  and titanium.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC H04-E04; H04-E05; H04-F02E

PT P
PN CN108224831-A; CN108224831-B
TI Transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump heat exchanger, has first heat exchange component that is fixedly connected to second heat exchange component through first connecting portion.
AU LIU R
   ZHANG B
AE ANHUI HUANQIUXING NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
   ZHONGNENG LVSEJINGLING BEIJING TECHNOLOG (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 201854413B
AB    NOVELTY - The transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump heat exchanger has a first heat exchange component (1), a second heat exchange component (2) and a third heat exchange component (3). The first heat exchange component is fixedly connected to the second heat exchange component through a first connecting portion (4). The second heat exchange component is fixedly connected to the third heat exchange component through a second connecting portion (5). A first clamping plate (601) is fixedly connected to the upper side of a supporting plate (6). The inner wall of the first clamping plate is provided with a second clamping plate (602) arranged in a circle. The clamping portion clamps the third heat exchange component.
   USE - Transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump heat exchanger.
   ADVANTAGE - The transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump heat exchanger can easily realize heat exchange, and the heat exchange effect is obvious.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump heat exchanger.
   First heat exchange component (1)
   Second heat exchange component (2)
   Third heat exchange component (3)
   First connecting portion (4)
   Second connecting portion (5)
   Supporting plate (6)
   First clamping plate (601)
   Second clamping plate (602)

PT P
PN CN108224830-A
TI Transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump gas cooler, has first connecting pipe fixedly connected below water supply current collector, and first connecting pipe fixedly connected to first connecting portion.
AU LIU R
   ZHANG B
AE ANHUI HUANQIUXING NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201854413C
AB    NOVELTY - The transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump gas cooler has a first drain pipe (102) that is drawn from a side of a first heat exchanger sleeve (101). The first drain pipe is connected to a water supply device (4) through a first connecting portion (3). A first connecting pipe (11) is fixed under the supply water flow device. A second drain pipe is connected to a water supply flow splitter (5) through the first connecting portion. A third drain pipe (202) is also drawn on one side of a second heat exchanger sleeve (201). The first connecting pipe is fixedly connected below the water supply current collector. The first connecting pipe is fixedly connected to the first connecting portion.
   USE - Transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump gas cooler.
   ADVANTAGE - The transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump gas cooler is convenient.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump gas cooler.
   First connecting portion (3)
   Water supply device (4)
   Water supply flow splitter (5)
   First connecting pipe (11)
   First heat exchanger sleeve (101)
   First drain pipe (102)
   Second heat exchanger sleeve (201)
   Third drain pipe (202)

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a stationary hydrocarbon production unit installed on a surface of a sea. A liquid separator separates gas produced from a reservoir by a gas compressor, a water pump and a gas mixer. A water mixer flows produced gas by a descending flow pipe and transmits mixture of water with produced gas to the stationary hydrocarbon production unit. A water-gas separator segregates carbonated water from undissolved gas and injects carbonated water into an oil reservoir. A carbonated water pogos guns transports undissolved gas to natural gas treatment units.

PN WO2018031265-A1
TI Reducing immunogenicity of muscle tissue, involves providing muscle tissue from human donor, and exposing muscle tissue to supercritical carbon dioxide to form exposed muscle tissue.
AU JOSLYN A
   WALDEN T
   STILWELL R L
AE ALLOSOURCE (ALLO-Non-standard)
GA 201813124J
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) for reducing immunogenicity of a muscle tissue, involves providing the muscle tissue from a human donor, and exposing the muscle tissue to supercritical carbon dioxide to form an exposed muscle tissue, where the exposed muscle tissue has at least one of a lower immunogenicity or fewer rejection-provoking cells than the muscle tissue before exposure.
   USE - The methods are useful for reducing immunogenicity of a muscle tissue, and regenerating or replacing muscle in a patient (claimed). The method is also useful for preventing adhesions in a patient after surgery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method (M2) for regenerating or replacing muscle in a patient, involves providing a muscle tissue from a human donor, exposing the muscle tissue to a supercritical carbon dioxide to form an exposed muscle tissue, and administering the exposed muscle tissue to the patient; and
   (2) muscle tissue composition, comprising acellular muscle tissue, where the acellular muscle tissue has been treated with a supercritical carbon dioxide, and the muscle tissue composition has fewer rejection-provoking cells than muscle tissue not treated with the supercritical carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further involves contacting the muscle tissue with a decellularization agent. The step of exposing the muscle tissue is performed by exposing the muscle tissue to supercritical carbon dioxide at a temperature of 32-38 degrees C, preferably 35 degrees C for 1.5-4.5 hours, preferably 3 hours. The step of exposing the muscle tissue is performed by exposing the muscle tissue to supercritical carbon dioxide at a pressure of 1350-1475 psi . The method (M1) further involves sterilizing the muscle tissue without supercritical carbon dioxide. The step of exposing the muscle tissue to the supercritical carbon dioxide is performed by exposing the muscle tissue to additives, where the additives comprise peracetic acid, hydrogen peroxide, or ethanol. The step of exposing the muscle tissue involves exposing the muscle tissue to a total volume of supercritical carbon dioxide, where the volume of additives is greater than or equal to 0.001%. The method (M1) further involves adhering mesenchymal stem cells to the exposed muscle tissue. The method (M1) further involves digesting a tissue to form a cell suspension comprising mesenchymal stem cells and non-mesenchymal stem cells, seeding the cell suspension onto the exposed muscle tissue, incubating the exposed muscle tissue seeded with the cell suspension under conditions suitable for adhering the mesenchymal stem cells to the exposed muscle tissue, and removing the non-adherent cells from the exposed muscle tissue. The method (M2) further involves treating the muscle tissue with a decellularization agent, or sterilizing the muscle tissue without supercritical carbon dioxide. Preferred Agent: The decellularization agent comprises sodium dodecyl sulfate, and a surfactant. Preferred Cell: The exposed muscle tissue has a higher ultimate tensile strength than the muscle tissue before exposure. The exposed muscle tissue is less likely to tear than the muscle tissue before exposure. The muscle tissue comprises a skeletal muscle or a smooth muscle. The exposed muscle tissue has fewer contaminants than the muscle tissue before exposure, and the contaminants comprises leukocytes or blood. The mesenchymal stem cells are derived from a tissue processed to form a cell suspension comprising mesenchymal stem cells and non-mesenchymal stem cells that is seeded onto the exposed muscle tissue, where the mesenchymal stem cells are not cultured ex vivo after formation of the cell suspension and before seeding of the cell suspension on the exposed muscle tissue.
   ACTIVITY - Immunosuppressive. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)

PN CN216584264-U; CN114873669-A
TI Biogas slurry decarburization deamination system            comprises ammonia carbon stripping unit comprising            stripping tower, where stripping tower is orderly            provided with tower top outlet from up to down, and            cold material inlet.
AU SHENG C
   LIU X
   CHEN Y
AE JINYI CHUANGDIAN TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY CO (JINY-Non-standard)
GA 202274396R
AB    (CN216584264-U)   NOVELTY - The invention claims a biogas slurry                decarburization deamination system, comprising:                ammonia carbon stripping unit, comprising a                stripping tower, the stripping tower is orderly                provided with a tower top outlet from up to down; a                cold material inlet, a hot material inlet, a side                line discharging port, a steam inlet and a tower                kettle discharging port; wherein the hot material                inlet is used for inputting preheated biogas slurry                to be treated, the steam inlet is used for                inputting steam stripping steam, the cold material                inlet is used for inputting biogas slurry to be                processed, the side wire discharging port is used                for outputting ammonia gas mixed gas, the tower                kettle discharging port is used for outputting the                ammonia liquid, the tower top outlet is used for                outputting carbon dioxide ; ammonia water preparing                unit, for preparing the preset concentration of                ammonia water, connected with the ammonia carbon                stripping unit. The biogas slurry decarburization                deamination system of the invention can realize                efficient removal of ammonia carbon in biogas                slurry, and recovery of carbon dioxide and ammonia                gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Decarbonizing and deaminating biogas slurry                comprises pre-heating the biogas slurry to be                treated and sending to the upper part of the                stripper, sending the stripping steam to the lower                part of the stripping tower, carrying out the                stripping steam and preheated biogas slurry to be                treated to counter-transfer heat and mass transfer,                stripping carbon dioxide and ammonia in the                preheated biogas slurry to be treated into the gas                phase to obtain ammonia-carbon mixed steam, sending                the biogas slurry to be treated and/or the spraying                agent to the upper part of the stripper, carrying                out counter-flow heat and mass transfer by the                biogas slurry to be treated and/or the spraying                agent and the ammonia-carbon mixed steam, removing                the ammonia gas in the ammonia-carbon mixed steam,                so that carbon dioxide is enriched at the top of                the stripper, enriching ammonia in the middle of                the stripper, where the spray agent comprises fresh                water, outputting deammonia biogas liquid from the                tower kettle of the stripping tower, outputting                ammonia gas mixture from the side line, and                outputting carbon dioxide from the top of the                tower, and preparing the ammonia water by using the                ammonia gas mixture.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                biogas slurry decarburization deamination                system.
EA    NOVELTY - Decarbonizing and deaminating biogas slurry                comprises pre-heating the biogas slurry to be                treated and sending to the upper part of the                stripper, sending the stripping steam to the lower                part of the stripping tower, stripping carbon                dioxide and ammonia in the preheated biogas slurry                to be treated into the gas phase to obtain                ammonia-carbon mixed steam, sending the biogas                slurry to be treated and/or the spraying agent to                the upper part of the stripper, removing the                ammonia gas in the ammonia-carbon mixed steam, so                that carbon dioxide is enriched at the top of the                stripper, enriching ammonia in the middle of the                stripper, outputting deammonia biogas liquid from                the tower kettle of the stripping tower, outputting                ammonia gas mixture from the side line, and                outputting carbon dioxide from the top of the                tower, and preparing the ammonia water by using the                ammonia gas mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for decarbonizing and                deaminating biogas slurry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and system can realize the                efficient removal of ammonia carbon in the biogas                slurry and the recovery of carbon dioxide and                ammonia gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preheating process of                  the biogas slurry to be treated specifically                  comprises performing heat exchange with the                  ammonia gas mixture and the deammonia biogas                  slurry in sequence, and obtaining high                  concentration ammonia gas mixture and low                  concentration ammonia water after heat exchange                  between the ammonia gas mixture and the biogas                  liquid to be treated. The process of obtaining                  the stripping steam specifically comprises                  sending primary steam to the ejector to generate                  negative pressure in the ejector, utilizing the                  negative pressure generated by the ejector to                  gasify the deammonia biogas liquid after heat                  exchange with the biogas liquid to be treated,                  injecting the secondary steam obtained by the                  gasification into the ejector, mixing the primary                  steam with the secondary steam to increase the                  pressure in the ejector, i.e. the stripping steam                  is obtained. The preparation of aqueous ammonia                  by using the ammonia gas mixture specifically                  comprises sending the high concentration ammonia                  gas mixture to the lower part of the absorption                  tower, sending the fresh water to the upper part                  of the absorption tower, contacting and mixing                  the high-concentration ammonia gas mixture with                  the fresh water to obtain high-temperature                  ammonia water, and the concentration of the                  high-temperature ammonia water is 15% to 20%,                  using circulating water to cool high temperature                  aqueous ammonia to obtain cooling ammonia water,                  one of which is input to the aqueous ammonia                  storage tank there are two outputs of cooling                  ammonia water, and the other is returned to the                  top of the absorption tower. The low                  concentration ammonia water is sent to the upper                  part of the stripper.

TI Device for preparing pseudoboehmite has carbon dioxide flow controller, air supply pipe, temperature regulator with air pipes and cold-heat exchange system, and reaction tank with pH detector and temperature detector.
AU WANG T
AE SHANDONG JINLIWANG IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG HUIQIAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202262814V
AB    NOVELTY - The device comprises reaction tank (101) matched and connected with feeding component and temperature control component. The feeding component includes carbon dioxide supply mechanism and raw material supply mechanism. The carbon dioxide supply mechanism includes compressed air tank (104), carbon dioxide flow controller and air supply pipe (106). The raw material supply mechanism includes raw material tank (302), feeding hopper (303) and feeding pipe (304). The raw material tank is used for storing sodium meta aluminate raw liquid. The temperature control component comprises temperature regulator (503) including first air pipe, second air pipe (505) and cold-heat exchange system. The reaction tank is provided with pH detector and temperature detector. The temperature regulator is provided with water inlet pipe (508) and water outlet pipe (509). The cold and heat exchange system comprises air cavity and liquid cavity.
   USE - The device is used for preparing pseudoboehmite.
   ADVANTAGE - The device ensures purity and quality of pseudoboehmite after completion of reaction. The device is easy to control, and has simple structure and high reliability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The device comprises reaction tank (101) matched and connected with feeding component and temperature control component. The feeding component includes carbon dioxide supply mechanism and raw material supply mechanism. The carbon dioxide supply mechanism includes compressed air tank (104), carbon dioxide flow controller and air supply pipe (106). The compressed air tank is used for storing the compressed carbon dioxide gas. The air supply pipe has one end connected with the output port of the compressed air tank, and other end connected with the input end of carbon dioxide flow controller. The carbon dioxide flow controller is provided on the top of reaction tank, and has output end connected with the cavity of reaction tank. The compressed air tank is provided with first sensor. The raw material supply mechanism includes raw material tank (302), feeding hopper (303) and feeding pipe (304). The raw material tank is used for storing sodium meta aluminate raw liquid. The feeding pipe has one end connected with the output port of the raw material tank, and other end connected with the feeding hopper. The feeding hopper is provided on the top of the reaction tank. The discharging port of the feeding hopper is connected with the cavity of the reaction tank. The feeding pipe is provided with pump (305). The bottom of raw material tank is provided with second sensor. The temperature control component comprises temperature regulator (503). The temperature regulator comprises first air pipe, second air pipe (505) and cold-heat exchange system. The reaction tank is provided with first side wall and second side wall. The cavity between the first side wall and second side wall forms a temperature control cavity. The first air pipe and second air pipe have one end extending into temperature control cavity. The first air pipe and second air pipe are provided with third sensor. The reaction tank is provided with pH detector and temperature detector. The temperature regulator is provided with water inlet pipe (508) and water outlet pipe (509). The cold and heat exchange system comprises air cavity and liquid cavity. The water inlet pipe and water outlet pipe are connected with liquid cavity. The other end of first air pipe and second air pipe are connected with the air cavity. The cold water or hot water is introduced into the cavity liquid to adjusts the temperature of the gas in the air cavity and complete the process of cold and heat exchange.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a control method of pseudoboehmite preparing device comprising obtaining pH value of reaction solution in reaction tank in real time or obtaining temperature value of reaction solution in reaction tank in real time.

PN CN113996142-A
TI System used for manufacturing zeolite and capturing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas by utilizing fly ash, comprises fly ash zeolite manufacturing system, and fly ash zeolite carbon dioxide capture system.
AU XIAO Y
   YE J
   MU X
   WANG X
   ZHANG D
   YU L
   CHENG Y
   CHENG L
   LI Y
   XU H
AE DATANG HUAINAN LUOHE POWER PLANT (DATA-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG DATANG INT CHAOZHOU POWER GENE (CDAT-C)
   DATANG BOILER PRESSURE VESSEL INSPECTION (DATA-Non-standard)
   CHINA DATANG CORP SCI & TECHNOLOGY RES (CDAT-C)
GA 2022257958
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises fly ash zeolite manufacturing system (1), and fly ash zeolite carbon dioxide capture system (2). The fly ash zeolite manufacturing system comprises a conveying device (11), a washing device (12), a pickling device (14), a hydrothermal reaction device (15), and a drying device (16). The conveying device comprises a rail scale (111) and a grab bucket (112). The grab bucket is connected to the rail scale and can move along the rail scale. The washing device, the pickling device, the hydrothermal reaction device, and the drying device are all located on the moving route of the grab bucket. The fly ash zeolite carbon dioxide capture system comprises a cooling device (22), a first connection pipe (23), a dehydration device (24), a second connection pipe (25), an adsorption device (26), a exhaust gas discharge pipe (27). The cooling device is connected to the dehydration device through the first connecting pipe.
   USE - System used for manufacturing zeolite and capturing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas by utilizing fly ash (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fly ash can be used to produce zeolite and the fly ash zeolite can be used to capture carbon dioxide in exhaust gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for manufacturing zeolite and capturing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas by utilizing fly ash, which involves: (a)separating the fly ash into fine ash by gravity sedimentation, transporting the separated fly ash and fine ash to the pickling device;(b)installing hydrochloric acid in the pickling device, soaking the fly ash in the pickling device to wash away impurity in the fly ash;(c)moving the grab bucket to the top of the pickling device along the track scale, reaching into the pickling device to grab the fly ash, transporting to the washing device;(d)using washing device to wash the pickled fly ash, drying the washed fly ash on the washing device;(e)using the grab bucket to grab the fly ash from the flushing device, moves along the rail balance to the top of the hydrothermal reaction device;(f)putting fly ash into the hydrothermal reaction device simultaneously, adding an additive containing silicon and aluminum is to the hydrothermal reaction device;(g)adding sodium hydroxide solution, fly ash and the added additive to carry out hydrothermal reaction with sodium hydroxide in the hydrothermal reaction cavity to obtain fly ash zeolite, the grab bucket extends into the hydrothermal reaction device to grab the reactive fly ash zeolite, transporting to the washing device, and the washing device washes the fly ash zeolite, drying the washed fly ash zeolite on the rinsing device;(h) using grab bucket to grab the fly ash zeolite from the washing device, moves along the rail scale to the top of the drying device, putting the fly ash zeolite into the drying device for drying;(i) crushing the dried fly ash zeolite, transporting a portion of the crushed fly ash zeolite to the adsorption device sealing the rest, packaging, storing in a warehouse;(j) separating the exhaust gas, so that a portion of the exhaust gas enters the cooling device, and the rest of the exhaust gas enters the chimney and is discharged;(k)using the cooling device to cool the exhaust gas to remove water, and the exhaust gas enters the dehydration device through the first connecting pipe for dehydration;(l) making the dehydrated exhaust gas enters the adsorption device through the second connecting pipe, the fly ash zeolite adsorbs carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas, and making the exhaust gas enters the chimney through the exhaust gas discharge pipe for discharge.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for manufacturing zeolite and capturing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas by utilizing fly ash.
   Fly ash zeolite manufacturing system (1)
   Fly ash zeolite capturing carbon dioxide system (2)
   Conveying device (11)
   Washing device (12)
   Pickling device (14)
   Hydrothermal reaction device (15)
   Drying device (16)
   Track scale (111)
   Grab bucket (112)

TI Cultivating Pleurotus eryngii, by pretreating            thick and fine wood chips, corn cob granules, crude and            fine bagasse, mixing with bran, corn flour, cardamom,            quicklime and light calcium carbonate, inoculating and            controlling temperature.

   SHEN H
AE JIANGSU XIANGRU BIOTECH INC (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201890239J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating Pleurotus eryngii,                involves (a) pretreating thick wood chips, fine                wood chips, corn cob granules, crude bagasse and                fine bagasse, forming the mixture into pile, mixing                with bran, corn flour, cardamom, quicklime and                light calcium carbonate, and obtaining medium                having moisture content of 65-70% and pH of                8.5-9.2, (b) packaging the medium in bags, placing                in sterilization basket, sterilizing the basket,                and placing in 10,000-stage purification cooling                chamber for cooling for 8-10 hours, and (c)                controlling temperature to 18-22 degrees C,                inoculating each strain with vaccination gun in an                amount of 24.3-24.7 cc per kg, placing on culture                rack, transferring to warehouse, maintaining at                21-23 degrees C and humidity of 60-65% for 9-11                days, controlling carbon dioxide concentration to                3000-5000 ppm, harvesting, transferring to                packaging workshop, grading, maintaining at 13-16                degrees C, and storing in refrigerator at 2-5                degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating                P.eryngii.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves taste and quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for cultivating Pleurotus eryngii,                involves (a) preparing culture medium by weighing                8-10 pts. wt. coarse wood chips, 8-10 pts. wt. fine sterilizing the basket, and                placing in 10,000-stage purification cooling                chamber for cooling for 8-10 hours, the medium has                pH of 6-6.2, and (c) controlling temperature to                18-22 degrees C, inoculating each strain with                vaccination gun in an amount of 24.3-24.7 cc per                kg, placing on culture rack, transferring to                warehouse, maintaining at 21-23 degrees C and                humidity of 60-65% for 9-11 days, controlling                carbon dioxide concentration to 3000-5000 ppm, hours,                ventilating to control carbon dioxide concentration                to 3000-5000 ppm, controlling temperature to 14-16                degrees C and humidity to 60-70%, ventilating to                control carbon dioxide concentration to 2500-6000                ppm, controlling temperature to 13-15 degrees C and                humidity to 60-70%, ventilating to control carbon                dioxide concentration to 2500-6000 ppm, controlling                temperature to 13-15 degrees C and humidity to                60-70%, ventilating to control carbon dioxide to 2500-6000 ppm, harvesting,                transferring to packaging workshop, grading,                maintaining at 13-16 degrees C, and storing in                refrigerator at 2-5 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The sterilization is                  performed by maintaining at 99-102 degrees C for                  58-63 minutes, maintaining at 114-116 degrees C                  for 58-63 minutes, maintaining at 122-125 degrees                  C for 158-163 minutes, maintaining at 0.004-0.006                  mPa for 28-33 minutes, and allowing to stand for                  5-8 minutes. TI Recycling waste lead paste comprises reacting            waste lead plaster and ammonium acetate, filtering and            separating, adding ammonia, roasting the undissolved            lead slag, cooling and grinding, separating solid and            liquid, and recycling carbon dioxide gas.
AU WANG X
   XIANG C
   WU X
   DAI S
AE CHILWEE POWER GROUP CO LTD (CHIL-Non-standard)
GA 202283190B
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling waste lead paste comprises (i)                reacting waste lead plaster and ammonium acetate,                and filtering and separating to obtain lead acetate                solution and undissolved lead slag, (ii) adding                ammonia or aqueous ammonia to the lead acetate                solution and introducing carbon dioxide to react to                obtain a lead carbonate mixed solution, filtering                and separating to obtain a high-purity lead                carbonate solid and a mixed solution of ammonium                acetate and ammonium sulfate, (iii) roasting the                undissolved lead slag, cooling and grinding to                obtain crude lead oxide, (iv) reacting crude lead                oxide with the purifying reagent, filtering and                separating impurities remove the crude lead oxide                to obtain pure complex lead liquid, (v) introducing                carbon dioxide gas into the complex lead liquid,                stirring and reacting , separating solid and liquid                to obtain high-purity lead carbonate and complex                solution, (vi) grinding the lead oxide to obtain                the battery level lead oxide product, and recycling                carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Recycling method of waste lead paste used in                lead production enterprises and battery production                enterprises for realizing sustainable development                strategy of lead industry. Can be used for                recycling the lead from the waste lead storage                battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is rationally designed, the lead                oxide prepared by recycling waste lead paste has                high purity, and the whole process is the solid                impurity generated by purifying crude lead oxide,                the other products can be recycled, and it is                environment-friendly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a waste lead paste                recycling flow chart. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

The conversion rate                of solar energy exceeds 2%, which preliminarily                meets the conditions for industrialization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: (1) preparation of high temperature                semiconductor catalyst, which involves (s1)                roasting the semiconductor material at                700&#8451; in air for 2 hours, (s2) dissolving                copper nitrate, ascorbic acid, and                polyvinylpyrrolidone in deionized water, stirring                at 70-80&#176;C for 2-3 hours, and then placing                the reaction system in an ice-water bath to obtain                a copper nanocluster solution, and (s3) mixing the                semiconductor material and the copper nanocluster                solution, evaporating, dispersing the copper                nanoclusters on the surface of the semiconductor                material, and calcining at 500&#176;C for 2                hours in air atmosphere; and (2) application of the                high temperature semiconductor catalyst in the                photoreduction of carbon dioxide.

AU TAN H
   ZHAO Y
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 202217457V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing graphene in bulk, which involves: i. taking graphite raw material and ball mill beads into the high pressure ball mill kettle, pumping carbon dioxide (CO2) into the high pressure ball mill through the high pressure CO2 pump, expel air and reach set pressure to form a supercritical CO2 system, subjecting the graphite raw material to ball milling pretreatment; ii. keeping the ball mill turned on, turn on the high pressure CO2 pump, pretreating graphite raw material in the high-pressure ball mill is purged with supercritical CO2 into the high-pressure shearing kettle, at the same time, the shearing rotor in the high-pressure shearing kettle is turned on for shearing treatment, it ends when the components in the ball mill are transferred, collecting the products in the high pressure shearing kettle.
   USE - Graphene used in the fields of supercapacitors, transistors, flexible displays, new energy batteries, aerospace, electronic computers and the like.
   ADVANTAGE - Method for producing graphene adopts supercritical carbon dioxide ball-milling pre-stripping serial shearing treatment is carried out on the graphite raw material in the atmosphere to prepare the high-quality graphene, operation is simple, environment is protected, efficiency is high, and the continuous stripping, collection and the like of the large-batch high-quality graphene is realized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphene preparation flow chart. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The graphite raw material exists in the form of a dry raw material or a graphite dispersion containing a solvent in the high pressure ball mill. The temperature in the high-pressure ball mill is 33-100 degreesC, the pressure is 8-50 megapascal, the rotational speed is 300-5000 revolutions per minute (rpm), and the ball-milling pretreatment time is 12-72 hours. The pressure in the high-pressure shearing kettle is 8-50 megapascal, the temperature is 33-100 degrees Celsius, and it is consistent with the ball milling kettle, the shearing speed is 500-4000 revolutions per minute (rpm), and the shearing treatment time is 2-72 hours. During the process of transferring the pretreated graphite raw material from the high pressure ball mill to the high pressure shearing kettle, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is controlled to be 5-50 kilogram per hour. During the process of transferring the pretreated graphite raw material from the high pressure ball mill to the high pressure shearing kettle, the graphite raw material carried by carbon dioxide is trapped in the high pressure shearing kettle, and the carbon dioxide (CO2) is returned to the inlet of the high pressure CO2 pump for recycling. The diameter of the ball mill bead is 0.1-20 millimeter. Preferred Components: Graphite raw material is natural graphite, expanded graphite and/or flake graphite. Material of the ball mill bead is stainless steel, zirconia, alumina, agate, polyurethane-clad iron core, and/or hard alloy. Preferred Method: The volume ratio of graphite raw material and high pressure ball mill kettle is controlled to be 10-100 gram per liter. The ratio of the volume of the ball-milling beads to the volume of the high-pressure ball-milling kettle is controlled to be 0.1-0.8:1.

TI Production of hydrogen involves steam-reforming gaseous mixture, purifying crude hydrogen gas stream to provide purified hydrogen gas and fuel gas, and feeding fuel gas as sole fuel to fired heater(s) used to heat stream(s) within process.
AU COTTON W J
   MCKENNA M J
   SADEQZADEH BOROUJENI M
   BOROUJENI M S
AE JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
GA 202205682Y
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrogen involves (i) steam-reforming a gaseous mixture comprising a hydrocarbon and steam in a gas-heated reformer (52) followed by autothermal reforming with an oxygen-rich gas in an autothermal reformer (56) to generate a reformed gas mixture, (ii) increasing the hydrogen content of the reformed gas mixture by subjecting to water-gas shift stage(s) in a water-gas shift unit to provide a hydrogen-enriched reformed gas, (iii) cooling the hydrogen-enriched reformed gas and separating condensed water, (iv) passing the de-watered hydrogen-enriched reformed gas to a carbon dioxide separation unit to provide a carbon dioxide gas stream and a crude hydrogen gas stream, and (v) passing the crude hydrogen gas stream to a purification unit to provide a purified hydrogen gas and a fuel gas. The fuel gas is fed as sole fuel to one or more fired heaters (16,144) used to heat process stream(s) within the process. The ratio of steam and carbon in the gaseous mixture is at least 2.6:1.
   USE - Production of hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently converts hydrocarbons to hydrogen while minimizing production and emission of carbon dioxide, and ensures 97% or more carbon dioxide capture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowsheet of hydrogen production.
   Fired heaters (16,144)
   Saturator (30)
   Gas-heated reformer (52)
   Autothermal reformer (56)
   Pressure swing adsorption unit (134)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The hydrocarbon is a methane-containing gas stream containing more than 50 vol.% methane, and is desulfurized. The ratio of steam and carbon in the gaseous mixture is preferably 2.9:1-3.2:1 or 3.2:1-3.5:1. The oxygen-rich gas comprises at least 90 vol.%, preferably at least 98 vol.% oxygen. Preferred Process: The gaseous mixture comprising the hydrocarbon and steam is formed by contacting the hydrocarbon with water in a saturator (30) to form a saturated gas mixture. The method further involves removing carbon dioxide using a physical wash system or a reactive wash system, preferably an amine wash system. A portion of the crude hydrogen or pure hydrogen is fed to the hydrocarbon. The saturator generates 50-60% or 55-65% steam.

AU LIU T
   XU G
   ZHANG M
   PENG W
   CHEN H
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA ELECTRIC POWER (UYHD-C)
GA 202208102R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a biomass gasification generating system of coal-fired power station coupling the system mainly comprises five parts, a coal-fired power generation system, a biomass gasification system, a high temperature fuel cell system, a gas turbine system and a supercritical carbon dioxide circulation system. wherein the biomass is pyrolyzed and gasified in the biomass gasification furnace, the obtained synthesis gas is used as the fuel of the high temperature fuel cell after being purified. After the electrochemical reaction in the high temperature fuel cell is finished, the flue gas not completely burned out at the anode outlet is mixed with the cathode outlet gas and then enters the combustion chamber to burn. the high temperature flue gas at the outlet of the combustion chamber firstly pushes the gas turbine to apply work, then as the high temperature heat source of the supercritical carbon dioxide circulation system, then used for heating the water supply of the coal-fired power generation system, at last, mixing with the flue gas after processing to obtain the coal-fired boiler outlet, and then discharging it through the chimney. wherein the condensed water of the coal-fired power generation system is used as the cold source of the supercritical carbon dioxide circulating system, the utility model can realize the efficient resource utilization of the solid waste and the coal.

PT P
PN CN112573552-A; CN112573552-B
TI Preparing boehmite using high-silicon aluminum-containing materials by subjecting slurry containing high-silicon aluminum-containing materials and M-carboxylic acid to hydrothermal treatment, performing solid-liquid separation and heating.
AU LI G
   LUO J
   CAO P
   JIANG H
   RAO M
   ZHANG X
   JIANG T
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202137413F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing boehmite using high-silicon aluminum-containing materials involves (i) subjecting the slurry containing high-silicon aluminum-containing materials, lime and M-carboxylic acid to hydrothermal treatment and performing solid-liquid separation to obtain aluminum-containing lye, controlling the concentration of M-carboxylic acid in the aluminum-containing lye to 5-30 g/l, where the molar ratio of M/aluminum is 1-5 and (ii) heating the aluminum-containing lye at 60-160 degrees C, blowning carbon dioxide containing gas into the solution, dripping the pH of the solution to 9.8-11, stopping introducing carbon dioxide and after the reaction is completed and performing solid-liquid separation to obtain boehmite.
   USE - Method for preparing boehmite using high-silicon aluminum-containing materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses low-quality aluminum raw materials to leaching alumina with high selectivity and prepare boehmite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing boehmite using high-silicon aluminum-containing materials (i) subjecting the slurry containing high-silicon aluminum-containing materials, lime, and M-carboxylic acid to hydrothermal treatment and performing solid-liquid separation to obtain aluminum-containing lye, controlling the concentration of M-carboxylic acid in the aluminum-containing lye to 5-30 g/l, where the molar ratio of M/aluminum is 1-5,
   M = 1-5.
   and (ii) heating the aluminum-containing lye at 60-160 degrees C, blowing carbon dioxide containing gas into the solution, dripping the pH of the solution to 9.8-11, stopping introducing carbon dioxide and after the reaction is completed, performing solid-liquid separation to obtain boehmite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The high-silicon aluminum-containing material comprises fly ash, coal gangue, red mud, bauxite, clay, and/or tailings. The carbon dioxide-containing gas comprises pure carbon dioxide gas or mixed gas of nitrogen, oxygen, inert gas and carbon dioxide. The synthetic boehmite comprises porous material with flake, spindle and fiber morphology. Preferred Composition: In the high-silicon aluminum-containing material, the content of aluminum oxide is 10-50%, and the content of silicon dioxide is 20-80%. In the slurry, the molar ratio of calcium oxide/silicon dioxide is 0.5-1. In step (i), the MOH added to the slurry is not less than the theoretical reaction amount, the MOH concentration in the slurry is 1-2 times the MOH concentration at the end of the reaction. In the hydrothermal process, the liquid-solid ratio in the slurry is 5-20 ml/g, the temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is 150-200 degrees C and the hydrothermal reaction time is 0.1-3 hours. The MOH concentration of the aluminum-containing lye is controlled to be 10-20 g/l, preferably 10-15 g/l. In the aluminum-containing lye, the molar ratio of M/aluminum is 1-3.5, preferably 1-1.5. In the gas containing carbon dioxide, the volume percentage of carbon dioxide is 50-100%. The flow rate of the gas containing carbon dioxide is 0.5-20 l/minute. Preferred Method: In step (ii), the method involves performing nucleation reaction in an atmospheric nucleation reaction by performing atmospheric pressure nucleation reaction in open reaction equipment or pressure-resistant reaction equipment, lowering the temperature to 100 degrees C, or 85-95 degrees C or 60-70 degrees C and synthesizing the spindle-type boehmite to obtain boehmite with lamellar structure or performing hydrothermal nucleation reaction in a pressure-resistant reaction vessel at 105-160 degrees C, preferably 120-150 degrees C to synthesize fibrous boehmite. Preferred Conditions: The pH at the end of the reaction is controlled at 9.8-10.5, preferably 9.8-10. The nucleation reaction time is 10-90 minutes, preferably 50-60 minutes.

AU CHENG W
   GOU H
   SU Q

   QIAN W

   ZHANG S
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 2021175105
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen bond donor functionalized polymerized ionic liquid catalyst i.e. imidazolium salt containing polymer compound (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing cyclic carbonate by catalyzing epoxide and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): catalyzes conversion of carbon dioxide and epoxide into cyclic carbonate under optimal reaction conditions, and yield can reaches 99%; combines the hydrogen bond donor and the ionic liquid by free radical polymerization to form heterogeneous catalyst with rich hydrogen bond donor, dispersed active sites, excellent catalytic effect, simple preparation process, excellent cycle performance, simple separation and other advantages has huge potential for industrial application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydrogen bond donor functionalized polymerized ionic liquid catalyst i.e. imidazolium salt containing polymer compound of formula (I) is new.
   R1-R4 = 1-20C alkyl, 6-30C aryl, 3-30C heteroaryl (optionally substituted), H, carboxy, hydroxy, ester, carbony, amino, nitro, nitroso, thio, cyano, aldehyde, sulfonic acid groups, imino, alkoxy, tertiary amine or amide group;
   R5 = H, hydroxy-terminated alkyl, carboxy-terminated alkyl, amino-terminated alkyl, sulfonic acid-terminated alkyl, or mercapto-terminated alkyl;
   R6-R8 = H or methyl;
   R9 = 1-5C alkyl group; and
   X = anion including fluoride ion, chloride ion, bromide ion, iodide ion, tetrafluoroborate, hexafluorophosphate, hydroxide, bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, nitrate, phosphate, dihydrogen phosphate, sulfate, hydrogen sulfate or nitrite.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) preparing cyclic carbonate by catalyzing epoxide and carbon dioxide with (I), where the amount of catalyst used in the carbonylation reaction is 0.1-20 mol.%, the carbonylation reaction is performed at 50-150 degrees C under the reaction pressure of 0.1-3 MPa for 1-24 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) taking imidazole substrates with double bonds and halogenated alkanes (halogenated alcohols, halogenated carboxylic acids and other halogenated substances) as raw materials to synthesize imidazolium-based ionic liquid monomers, or preparing other anionic ionic liquid monomers through ion exchange, and (ii) taking certain amount of the above prepared ionic liquid monomer and certain proportion of the hydrogen bond donor monomer and cross-linking agent to conduct cross-linking polymerization under the induction of the initiator to obtain final product. Preferred Method: The use method comprises placing the epoxy compound and catalyst powder having grain diameter of 10-1000 psi m in a sealed high pressure reaction kettle to mix uniformly, maintaining the temperature of the reaction kettle at 50-150 degrees C, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas in the reaction kettle, maintaining the reaction pressure of the reaction system in the kettle at 0.1-3 MPa, and performing addition reaction for 1-24 hours to obtain cyclic carbonate.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1-R4 = ethyl, propyl, hydroxyethyl, hydroxypropyl, carboxymethyl, carboxyethyl, ethylsulfonate, mercaptoethyl or mercaptopropyl; and
   R5 = hydroxyethyl, hydroxypropyl, carboxymethyl, carboxyethyl, ethylsulfonate, mercaptoethyl or mercaptopropyl.

PN CN111908497-A
TI Preparing cerium dioxide nano powder, comprises e.g. mixing amine and water to prepare carbon dioxide gas trapping agent, and passing carbon dioxide gas, then adding solid cerium salt or aqueous cerium salt solution, and post-processing.
AU ZHANG J
   YU J
   LIU B
   JING P
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA (UNMO-C)
GA 2020B3865S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cerium dioxide nano powder, comprises (i) mixing amine and water in a volume ratio of 1:9-30 to prepare carbon dioxide gas trapping agent, and passing carbon dioxide gas to saturation to prepare liquid precipitant, (ii) adding solid cerium salt or aqueous cerium salt solution to the liquid precipitant and mixing, stirring at 20-150 degrees C for 1-12 hours to react, centrifuging to obtain white precipitate, washing and drying to obtain hydrated cerium oxide powder, and (iii) (a) mixing the step (ii) hydrated cerium dioxide powder with sodium chloride, and performing ball milling, then calcining the obtained mixed solids in tube furnace at 600-1000 degrees C, washing, and drying to obtain spherical cerium dioxide nano powder, (b) calcining the step (ii) hydrated cerium oxide carbonate powder in tube furnace at 300-500 degrees C to obtain fibrous cerium dioxide nano powder.
   USE - The cerium dioxide nano powder is useful for high-end polishing powder, catalyst carrier and nano additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes liquid precipitant, which is more conducive to industrial production and solves the problems of difficult washing and transportation of precipitant; utilizes solid cerium salt can directly added to liquid precipitant to prepare hydrated cerium oxide carbonate, which greatly reduces amount of solvent and ensures recyclability of solvent in the entire process flow; and produces cerium dioxide nano powder with particle size of 30-80 nm. The cerium dioxide nano powder: is spherical, quasi-spherical and fibrous in nature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (ii): supernatant liquid obtained by centrifugal separation is passed into carbon dioxide gas, and repeating step (i) to prepare liquid precipitation agent to ensure recycling of carbon dioxide gas trapping agent; reaction temperature is room temperature to prepare spherical cerium dioxide nano powder; and reaction temperature is 100-150 degrees C to prepared fibrous cerium dioxide nano powder. In step (ii) and step (iii): washing is carried out with water and ethanol. In ball milling process: mass ratio between hydrated cerium dioxide powder and sodium chloride is 1:5-20; milling time is 10-120 minutes; and milling speed is 400 plus minus 50 revolutions/minute. Preferred Components: In step (i): amine is ethylenediamine, diethylamine, ethanolamine or diethanolamine. In step (ii): cerium salt is cerium salt extracted and separated by cerium nitrate, cerium chloride or cerium-rich rare earth chloride, and concentration of aqueous cerium salt solution is 0.03-0.1 mol/l. The mass ratio between cerium salt and liquid precipitant is 1:1-150.

TI Heterogeneous catalyst for hydrogenating carbon dioxide, comprises porous organic polymer, and transition metal compound, in which monomer of polymer comprises frame ligand comprising aromatic ring, and linking group linked to aromatic ring.
AU YOON S H
   GUNASEKAR G H
   PARK K H
   YUNSUNGHO
   HARIYANANDAM G G
   HO P K
AE UNIV KOOKMIN IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYKO-C)
GA 202093796F
AB    NOVELTY - A heterogeneous catalyst comprises a porous organic polymer, and a transition metal compound (I), and coordinated with the polymer. The monomer of the polymer comprises a frame ligand comprising aromatic ring, and a linking group linked by Friedel-Craft reaction to the aromatic ring in the ligand.
   USE - Heterogeneous catalyst for hydrogenating carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heterogeneous catalyst has excellent activity, and utility in continuous process, is manufactured easily.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A heterogeneous catalyst comprises a porous organic polymer, and a transition metal compound of formula: M(L1)x (I), and coordinated with the polymer. The monomer of the polymer comprises a frame ligand comprising aromatic ring, and a linking group linked by Friedel-Craft reaction to the aromatic ring in the ligand.
   M = Ir, Pt, Pd, Ru, Rh, Ni, Fe, Cu, V, Co , Cr, Au, Re, W, Zr, or Mo;
   L1 = anionic ligand or neutral ligand;and
   x = 1-5.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst, which involves forming a porous organic polymer by performing a Friedel Craft reaction with a frame ligand precursor comprising an aromatic ring, and a linker precursor, and coordinating the transition metal compound to the porous organic polymer, in which a linking group precursor comprising a leaving group in an anionic state which is separated from a linking group connected by a Friedel Craft reaction with the aromatic ring included in the frame ligand precursor; and
   (2) hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, which involves reacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the presence of the heterogeneous catalyst to obtain formic acid and/or formate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The unit of the porous organic polymer is of formula (II). The frame ligand is coordinated to the transition metal compound by bond of formula (III). The organic ligand is a monodentate ligand including coordination atom or a multidentate ligand including coordination atoms, preferably of formula: -(L'R1r-L''R2)n-. The frame ligand precursor is of formula: H-(A1-LA-A2)-H, preferably of formula: H-(LA)-H. The linking group is of formula: X1-(R3)-X2, preferably of formula (IV). further comprises a leaving group chosen from chloride, bromide, iodide, tosylate, sulfonate, and mesylate.FL = frame ligand;M(L1)x = transition metal compound;Y' = connector;CM = supercrosslinked polymer unit including functional group capable of crosslinking;A1,A2 = optionally substituted 1-10 aryl or fused ring (when aryl is polycyclyl);LA' = organic ligand comprising coordination bond atom chosen from C, N, O, P, and S, preferably heteroaryl;L',L'' = coordination bond atom chosen from C, N, O, P, and S;R1,R2 = H, 1-3C alkyl, or phenyl (when L' and L'' is N or P) or unshared electron pair (when L' and L'' is O or S);X1,X2,X' = leaving group;R3 = 1-10C alkyl;A4 = aryl compound including 1-20 aryl;l = 2-6;andm = 0 or 1.The supercrosslinked polymer unit includes aromatic ring. Preferred Process: The preparation of the catalyst further involves coordinating the frame ligand precursor, and the transition metal compound, and forming porous organic polymer. The hydrogenation of carbon dioxide further involves injecting and mixing the heterogeneous catalyst, a solvent, and a tertiary amine into a reactor, injecting and heating the carbon dioxide and the hydrogen into the reactor, and removing the heterogeneous hydrogenation catalyst to obtain the formic acid and/or formate.

TI Reducing the carbon dioxide of molten steel at the end point using semi-steel converter steelmaking comprises mixing semi-steel molten steel, adjusting oxygen lance position and performing oxygen blowing and blowing gas supply operation.
AU CHEN G
   CHEN J
   LIANG X
   YANG X
AE PANZHIHUA STEEL GROUP XICHANG NEW STEEL (ANSH-C)
GA 2020630160
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing the carbon dioxide of molten steel at the end point using semi-steel converter steelmaking comprises mixing semi-steel molten steel into the converter, adjusting the oxygen lance position in the early smelting, middle smelting and late smelting respectively and separately performing oxygen blowing operation and bottom blowing gas supply operation on the molten steel in the converter, where the oxygen supply intensity of the oxygen blowing operation in the middle smelting phase is greater than the oxygen supply intensity in the early smelting phase and the later smelting phase, the gun position of the oxygen lance gun in the middle smelting period is smaller than the gun positions in the early smelting period and the late smelting period and the gas supply intensity of the bottom-blowing gas supply operation in the middle smelting period is less than the gas supply intensity in the early smelting period and the late smelting period.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing the carbon dioxide of molten steel at the end point using semi-steel converter steelmaking.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the amount of steel deoxidation alloy; improves the quality of molten steel; reduces the corrosion of high oxidation slag on the lining; and has simple operation and strong feasibility.

TI Method for testing fuel cell e.g. proton exchange membrane fuel cell (PEMFC) on test bench, involves determining concentration of carbon dioxide and concentration of carbon monoxide in exhaust gas using non-dispersive infrared sensor (NDIR).
AU ORASCHE G
   DEHNE T
   WANKER R
AE AVL LIST GMBH (AVLV-C)
GA 202042648K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining the concentration of carbon dioxide and concentration of carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas using an NDIR (9). The determined concentration is used for the development of a fuel cell (1) and for controlling a test bench (20). A volume flow (Q) of the exhaust gas is measured. The concentration of water in the exhaust gas is determined using NDIR. The volume flow of the exhaust gas and determined concentration of water and determined concentration of carbon dioxide and determined concentration of carbon monoxide is converted to a mass flow of the water and mass flow of carbon dioxide and mass flow of the carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas.
   USE - Method for testing fuel cell such as proton exchange membrane fuel cell (PEMFC), phosphoric acid fuel cell (PAFC) or direct methanol fuel cell (DMFC) on test bench.
   ADVANTAGE - The mass flow of various exhaust gas components in the exhaust gas is determined in a simple manner with determined volume flow. The volume flow is determined easily and reliably when the speed of the exhaust gas is detected in an exhaust pipe. The emergency shutdown of the test bench is performed to prevent permanent damage or even destruction of the fuel cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the test bench for the development of the fuel cell.
   Fuel cell (1)
   Volume flow sensor (8)
   NDIR (9)
   Test bench (20)

PN US2019077935-A1
TI Foamed insulation product used in closed cell, extruded polystyrene foamed insulation product, comprises polymeric foam composition comprising polystyrene, blowing agent composition comprising carbon dioxide and co-blowing agent.
AU HAN X
   DELAVIZ Y
   BOUDREAUX C J
   WEEKLEY M Z
AE OWENS-CORNING INTELLECTUAL CAPITAL LLC (OWEN-C)
GA 2019239428
AB    NOVELTY - Foamed insulation product comprises polymeric foam composition comprising polystyrene, blowing agent composition comprising 50 mol.% carbon dioxide and co-blowing agent selected from hydrofluorocarbons, 1,3,3,3-Tetrafluoropropene (HFO-1234ze), trans-1,1,1,4,4,4-Hexafluoro-2-butene (HFO-1336mzz), or combination, and one infrared attenuating agent, where the foamed insulation product has an insulation R-value per inch of between 4 and 7.
   USE - Foamed insulation product used in closed cell, extruded polystyrene foamed insulation product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method foamed insulation product allows to achieve an extruded polystyrene (XPS) foam, has an improved thermal insulation performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) closed cell, extruded polystyrene foamed insulation product comprises polymeric foam composition comprising polystyrene, blowing agent composition comprising 50 mol.% carbon dioxide and one co-blowing agent, where the closed cell, extruded polystyrene foamed insulation product is monomodal, and the closed cell, extruded polystyrene foamed insulation product has an average cell size of from 0.005-0.6 mm; and
   (2) method of manufacturing foamed insulation product, involves introducing a polymeric composition comprising polystyrene into a screw extruder to form a polymer melt; injecting a blowing agent composition into the polymer melt to form a foamed polymeric material, where the blowing agent composition comprises 50 mol.% carbon dioxide and one co-blowing agent; introducing one infrared attenuating agent into the polymeric melt; and extruding the foamable polymeric material through a die under processing pressure of 800-1200 psi into a region of reduced pressure to obtain the foamed insulation product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The foamed insulation product has an insulation R-value per inch of 5 after 60 days agingat the time of manufacture. The foamed insulation product is closed cell, or monomodal. The foamed insulation product has a density of less than 5 lbs/ft3, 1.3- 4.5 lbs/ft3 and compressive strength between 6 and 120 psi. The closed cell, extruded polystyrene foamed insulation product has an average cell size of 0.05-0.2 mm, compressive strength between 6 and 120 psi, insulation R-value per inch between 4 and 7. The extrusion step is operated at a die temperature of 110-130 degrees C. The foamed insulation product has an insulation R-value per inch between 4 and 7.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)

TI Method for drying porous structure within interior chamber of vehicle, involves disconnecting pressure vessel from docking station, depressurizing pressure vessel, and unloading frozen drying material from pressure vessel.
AU GOLFETTO M
AE GOLFETTO M (GOLF-Individual)
GA 2018341440
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves allowing amount of a drying material to flow from first docking station into a pressure vessel (50). The pressure vessel is moved to second docking station apart from the first station. The pressure vessel is connected to the second station. Amount of cooling fluid is flown from the second station to a cooling system of the pressure vessel to freeze the within the pressure vessel. The pressure vessel is disconnected from the second station. The pressure vessel is moved away from the second station. The pressure vessel is depressurized. The material is unloaded from the vessel.
   USE - Method for drying a porous structure within an interior chamber of a vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing cost of pressure vessel construction. The method enables reducing contact angle between liquid carbon dioxide in and around a structure of the porous material. The method enables ensuring that tendency of liquid carbon dioxide injected/pumped into a chamber on a solvent inlet line to flash is reduced. The method enables reducing processing time as the vessel maintains relatively constant hydrostatic pressure throughout the vessel as force of the injected carbon dioxide purges the vessel.

PT P
PN CN107953500-A
TI Preparing microporous polycarbonate composite sheet used in manufacturing suitcase, automobile, electronic and electric industry, involves lining polycarbonate sheet to mold inner surface in advance and selecting polyolefin as raw material.
AU XIE X
   HE H
   LU Q
AE XIANGXING FUJIAN BAG & SUITCASE GROUP CO (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018330543
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing microporous polycarbonate composite sheet involves lining the polycarbonate sheet to the inner surface of the mold in advance, then selecting polyolefin as raw material and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide treatment and injecting additives into the screw extruder during the physical foaming of polyolefin to obtain polyolefin film under high and pressure. The polyolefin film is subjected to supercritical carbon dioxide treatment again and mixed with auxiliary agent in molten state to obtain homogeneous phase mixture with molten polyolefin raw material.
   USE - Method for preparing microporous polycarbonate composite sheet used in manufacturing suitcase, automobile, electronic and electric industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare microporous polycarbonate composite sheet, which shortens the foaming time to obtain micro-porous foaming polycarbonate sheet, reduces polycarbonate material consumption and production cost, has light weight, cold-heat resistance, wear resistance, impact resistance, and obvious economical and social benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing microporous polycarbonate composite sheet involves lining the polycarbonate sheet to the inner surface of the mold in advance, then selecting polyolefin as raw material and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide treatment and injecting additives into the screw extruder during the physical foaming of polyolefin to obtain polyolefin film under high and pressure. The polyolefin film is subjected to supercritical carbon dioxide treatment again and mixed with auxiliary agent in molten state to obtain homogeneous phase mixture with molten polyolefin raw material. The mixture is injected into the mold pre-lined with polycarbonate sheet by pressure relief die and subjecting to foaming and compounding to obtain desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The molten material is injected into the mold, then the mold is flushed with back pressure by connecting to the compressed air tank and when injection is completed, then back pressure is evacuated to to complete foaming and compounding, where back pressure is maintained to 5-10 megapascal (MPa). The amount of stearic acid added is 0-0.5 wt.% by weight of polyolefin raw material.Preferred Components: The auxiliary agent is stearic material as lubricant. The stearic acid material is selected from stearic acid, magnesium stearate, calcium stearate, or zinc stearate. The auxiliary agent comprises filler, reinforcing agent, toughening agent, antioxidant, modifier, and coupling agent, where filler is selected from calcium carbonate, hollow glass beads, mica, kaolin, talcum powder, or montmorillonite and reinforcing agent is selected from glass fiber, carbon fiber, titanium oxide fiber, or nano aluminum oxide. The toughening agent is selected from ethylene vinyl acetate, ethylene propylene diene rubber, polyolefin elastomer, polyhydroxybutyrate, ethylene propylene diene monomer, ESI, or polyethylene-polypropylene copolymers. The antioxidant is selected from antioxidant 1010, antioxidant distearyl thiodipropionate, or antioxidant dilaurly thiodipropionate. The modifier is selected from polyethylene grafted maleic anhydride (PE-g-MAH), polyethylene grafted maleic acid Zinc (PE-g-MAZn), polyethylene grafted glycidyl methacrylate (PE-g-GMA), polyethylene grafted acrylic (PE-g-AA), or polyethylene grafted methacrylic acid (PE-g-MMA). The coupling agent is selected from KH-550, KH-560, KH-570, KH-151, KH-171, KH-602, KH-792, or TTS.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A85 (Electrical applications.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.)

PN WO2022221790-A1; US2022333460-A1
TI Apparatus for controlling fluid injection into            subsurface formations, has flow control valve that is            configured to be positioned downhole in wellbore and to            control flow of injection fluid from fluid conduit into            subterranean formation.
AU FRIPP M L
   MURRAY F
   GRECI S M
AE HALLIBURTON ENERGY SERVICES INC (HALL-C)
   HALLIBURTON ENERGY SERVICES INC (HALL-C)
GA 2022D2229B
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a flow control valve (320)                configured to be positioned downhole in a wellbore                and to control a flow of an injection fluid from a                fluid conduit into a subterranean formation. The                injection fluid comprising carbon dioxide. The flow                control valve comprises a chamber (323) having a                variable volume and filled with a fill fluid                comprising carbon dioxide or an azeotrope of carbon                dioxide. The flow control valve is configured to                actuate to an open configuration to provide a fluid                passageway for the injection fluid to flow from the                fluid conduit into the subterranean formation when                a phase state of carbon dioxide provided as the                injection fluid to the flow control valve is in a                predetermined phase state. The predetermined phase                state is a high density fluid phase.
   USE - Apparatus for controlling fluid injection into                subsurface formations such as wellbores, wellbore                networks, and underground spaces such as rock                formation material or subterranean cavity.
   ADVANTAGE - The valve is positioned to restrict injection                of carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) when the CO 2 is                low-density phase state, and minimally restrict                injection when the carbon dioxide is high- density                phase state. The flow control valve is configured                for use in wellbore fluid injection operations, and                can be used as part of CO 2 sequestration in the                subsurface formation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for controlling fluid injection.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a                flow control valve.
   300Wellbore system
   320Flow control valve
   323Chamber
   328Extended portion
   329Seat
