PN WO2022187733-A1
TI Sorbent polymer composite article used for            adsorbing carbon dioxide from feed stream, comprises            composite region comprising porous polymer and sorbent            material and region of porous polymer positioned            adjacent to side of composite region.
AU CULLY E H
   SCOTTI C M
AE GORE & ASSOC INC W L (GORE-C)
GA 2022B5959D
AB    NOVELTY - A sorbent polymer composite article (20)                comprises a composite region (28) comprising a                porous polymer (22) and a sorbent material (24,                24') and a region (36) of a porous polymer (40)                positioned adjacent to a side of the composite                region (28). The composite region (28) has                hydrophobicity (a1). The region (36) has                hydrophobicity (a2) that exceeds the hydrophobicity                (28).
   USE - Sorbent polymer composite article used for                adsorption for direct air capture of carbon dioxide                from feed stream (claimed) such as air.
   ADVANTAGE - The sorbent polymer composite article can                cycle between adsorbing and desorbing stages                efficiently and with increased durability. The                adsorption of carbon dioxide from feed stream is                efficient method, with maximized amount of carbon                dioxide removed from the atmosphere while                minimizing the energy required in the                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   manufacture of the sorbent polymer composite                article, which involves forming a composite region                (28) comprising the porous polymer (22) and sorbent                material and forming the hydrophobic region (36)                comprising the porous polymer (40) on side of the                composite region (28); and
   method of using the sorbent polymer composite                article, which involves providing the sorbent                polymer composite article, directing a feed stream                comprising carbon dioxide across the sorbent                polymer composite article and adsorbing the carbon                dioxide into the sorbent polymer composite                article.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a elevational view of the                sorbent polymer composite article.
   20Sorbent polymer composite article
   22,40,48Porous polymers
   24,24'Sorbent material
   28,36,38Composite regions
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sorbent material                  is a carbon dioxide adsorbing material and chosen                  from ion exchange resin, zeolite, activated                  carbon, alumina, metal-organic frameworks, and                  polyethyleneimine, preferably polyethyleneimine.                  The composite region (28) further comprises a                  carrier. The sorbent is configured to coat the                  carrier of the first composite layer and does not                  coat the porous polymer of the first composite                  layer. The carrier is silica or a ceramic. The                  third hydrophobic region is a discrete layer from                  the composite region (28). Preferred Process: The                  step of providing the sorbent polymer composite                  article is performed by forming the region of                  porous polymer (48) on the side (b) of the first                  region. The usage method further involves                  desorbing the carbon dioxide by applying at least                  one of water and heat to the sorbent polymer                  composite article and collecting the desorbed                  carbon dioxide using a vacuum.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The sorbent polymer                  composite article further includes a region (38)                  of a porous polymer (48) positioned adjacent to a                  side of the composite region (28). The region                  (38) has hydrophobicity (a3) that exceeds the                  hydrophobicity (a2). The porous polymer (P1)-(P3)                  are the same. The porous polymer (22) of the                  composite region (28) is continuous with the                  porous polymer (22) of the region (36) or with                  the porous polymer (48) of the region (38). The                  porous polymer (22) of the first sorbent                  composite region is expanded                  polytetrafluoroethylene, polytetrafluoroethylene,                  or expanded polyethylene. The porous polymer (22)                  of the region (36) is at least one of                  polytetrafluoroethylene, expanded                  polytetrafluoroethylene, and expanded                  polyethylene. The porous polymer (22) of the                  region (36) and the porous polymer (48) of the                  region (38) are the same. The sorbent polymer                  composite article further comprises at least one                  useful component selected comprising an                  electroconductive material, a thermal conductor                  material, or a hydrophobic material. The second                  hydrophobic region is a discrete layer from the                  composite region (28). Preferred Properties: The                  hydrophobicity of both the regions (36) and (38)                  relative to the composite region (28) is defined                  by the absence of the sorbent material within the                  region (36) and the region (38). The thickness of                  the region (36) is less than the thickness of the                  composite region (28) and the thickness of the                  region (38) is less than the thickness of the                  composite region (28). The thickness of the                  sorbent polymer composite article is 0.1-5.0 mm.                  The composite region (28) has larger pores than                  the region (36) and the region (38). Preferred                  Process: The step of forming the hydrophobic                  region (36) on the composite layer is performed                  by coupling the porous polymer (22) of the                  hydrophobic layer to the side of the porous                  polymer of the composite layer. The method                  further involves forming the hydrophobic region                  (38) comprising the porous polymer (48) on a side                  (b) of the composite layer, connecting the                  hydrophobic region (36) to the hydrophobic region                  (38) by creating points of attachment along the                  length of the sorbent polymer composite article                  such that the composite region (28) is sandwiched                  between the second hydrophobic region and the                  hydrophobic region (38) between adjacent points                  of attachment. The step of forming the                  hydrophobic region (38) on the side of the                  composite region (28) is performed by coupling                  the porous polymer (48) of the hydrophobic region                  (38) to the side of the porous polymer (22) of                  the composite region (28). The coupling step is                  performed by laminating the hydrophobic region                  (36) onto the first side of the composite region                  (28) or laminating the hydrophobic region (38)                  onto the side of the composite region (28). The                  connecting step is performed by using an adhesive                  material. A distance between adjacent points of                  attachment along the sorbent polymer composite                  article is varied. The step of forming the                  composite region (28) is performed by coating the                  porous polymer (22) with a sorbent coating, and                  the step of forming the second hydrophobic region                  on the first side of the composite region (28) is                  performed by applying a surface treatment beyond                  the first side of the composite region                  (28).

PN US2022282613-A1; WO2022187258-A1
TI Method for fabricating sensor module for detecting            carbon dioxide leakage from subsurface reservoir,            involves attaching multiple sensors to rollable sheet            of non-conductive material to form sensor module.
AU AL-YOUSEF A A
   AYIRALA S C
   ALOTAIBI M B
   CHA D K
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2022B51019
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves attaching an anodic                aluminum oxide porous membrane onto a first                conductive electrode. Growth of titanium dioxide                nanotubes is initiated inside the anodic oxide                template using an atomic layer deposition process                and sensors are attached to a rollable sheet of                non-conductive material to form the sensor module.                The sheet has a width of 50 cm-2 m, a height of 50                to 2 m, and a thickness of 5 to 10 cm. The sensor                includes an electrical resistance sensor.
   USE - Method for fabricating a sensor module for                detecting carbon dioxide leakage from a subsurface                formation such as an underground oil-reservoir                environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving capabilities of                carbon dioxide leakage detection between a surface                and an underground environment by installing the                sensor module directly on a drill rig for usage in                shallow wells, thus increasing electrical                resistance with high sensitivity to carbon dioxide                (CO 2 ) leakage. The method allows a large surface                area to allow high sensitivity of electrical                resistance and economical sensing solution, thus                reducing the amount of CO 2 in the atmosphere and                assisting to mitigate high levels of global                warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a                sensor module for detecting carbon dioxide leakage                in a subsurface reservoir comprising: a rollable                sheet of non-conductive material with a rollable                surface; (2) a method for detecting a carbon                dioxide leakage from a subsurface reservoir.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                subsurface reservoir including a sensor                module.

AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)

PT P
PN CN114354843-A
TI Conveniently measuring total mass transfer coefficient of micro-reactor gas-liquid based on chemical system, involves mixing two phases of carbon dioxide and sodium carbonate solution, and calculating change amount of hydroxyl concentration.
AU ZHU W
   REN Y
   WANG H
   ZHANG R
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202258096Y
AB    NOVELTY - Conveniently measuring total mass transfer coefficient of micro-reactor gas-liquid based on chemical system, involves using carbon dioxide and sodium carbonate solution as a gas-liquid two-phase system for measuring the total mass transfer coefficient of the microreactor, after two phases of the carbon dioxide and the sodium carbonate solution flow through the microreactor to be mixed. The pH meter is used for measuring the pH values of the sodium carbonate solution before and after passing through the microreactor. The change of hydroxyl concentration is calculated according to the pH value. The change of the concentration of the carbon dioxide and the mass transfer flux of the carbon dioxide after passing through the microreactor are calculated according to the change of the hydroxyl concentration, and finally calculated total mass transfer coefficient of the microreactor according to a mass transfer formula.
   USE - Method for conveniently measuring total mass transfer coefficient of micro-reactor gas-liquid based on chemical system using carbon dioxide and sodium carbonate solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is more convenient, and has low measuring cost, without using a large device such as chromatograph, and the measuring time is shortened from the hour to 0.5-2 minutes. The method enables to guide the optimization of the micro reactor flow channel structure design, and provides better strengthening the two-phase reaction performance of micro reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Preferred Conditions: The chemical absorption used a sodium carbonate solution or a sodium carbonate-sodium bicarbonate solution, where the pH value of sodium carbonate solution is not higher than 12. The ratio of sodium carbonate-sodium bicarbonate is not higher than 0.98. The gas used in chemical absorption is also carbon dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen and rare gases. The measurement accuracy level of the used pH meter is 0.01-0.1, or used precision pH test strips and acid-base indicators that are accurate to class. The variation of the hydrogen ion concentration is also calculated by the pH value, and then the gas concentration and gas mass transfer flux is absorbed by the liquid, after passing through the microreactor are calculated from the change of hydrogen ion concentration, and finally the total mass transfer coefficient of the microreactor is calculated according to the mass transfer formula.

The step (ii) comprises cooling at 300-500degrees Celsius for 0.5-2 hours. The step (iii) comprises performing replacement reduction at 30-120degrees Celsius for 0.5-5 hours. The step (iv) comprises performing oxidation at 100-300degrees Celsius for 0.5-2 hours.

TI Biological trapping system for collecting carbon dioxide in tail gas of thermal power plant, has flue gas desulfurization tower whose gas outlet is connected to microorganism breeding workshop, and air source heat pump system that is provided to adjust temperature of microorganism breeding workshop.
AU FANG P
   SUN M
   FANG X
AE CHINA NAT ADMINISTRATION COAL GEOLOGY HY (CHNA-Non-standard)
GA 202194055Q
AB    NOVELTY - The biological capture system has a water filter (1), a flue gas desulfurization tower (2), a microbial breeding workshop (3) and an air source heat pump system (4). The gas outlet of the water filter is connected to the gas inlet of the flue gas desulfurization tower through a pipeline. The gas outlet of the flue gas desulfurization tower is connected to the microorganism breeding workshop through a pipeline. The air source heat pump system is provided to adjust the temperature of the microorganism breeding workshop. A microorganism processing workshop (5) is provided for processing microorganisms produced by the microorganism breeding workshop.
   USE - Biological trapping system for collecting carbon dioxide in tail gas of thermal power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide in the atmosphere is effectively reduced through the establishment of microorganisms to collect and store carbon dioxide in the tail gas of the power plant. The produced microorganisms are processed into industrial fuel oil or directly used as raw materials for power plants for power generation. The carbon dioxide recycling is achieved. The physical and chemical capture cost is low. The biological capture system has great economic benefits.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure of the biological trapping system for collecting carbon dioxide in tail gas of thermal power plant.

PN US2021123600-A1; US11162681-B2
TI Burner system for clean combustion in gas turbine, has pipe connected between air outlet and air inlet, and preheating unit receives fuel and recirculated carbon-dioxide from fuel port and recirculated carbon-dioxide port respectively.
AU HABIB M A
   ABDELHAFEZ A
   NEMITALLAH M A
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2021424559
AB    NOVELTY - The burner system has a headend that is connected to the combustor (200) and comprises a second shell surrounding multiple ceramic ion transfer membrane (ITM) tubes equally spaced longitudinally within headend. The headend further comprises a first oxygen depleted air outlet. A preheating unit is connected to headend and has a third shell surrounding a heat exchange tube, fuel port, recirculated carbon-dioxide inlet port, oxygen depleted air inlet and a second oxygen depleted air outlet. A thermally insulated pipe is connected between first oxygen depleted air outlet and oxygen depleted air inlet. The preheating unit receives fuel and recirculated carbon-dioxide from fuel port and recirculated carbon-dioxide port respectively. The fuel and recycled carbon-dioxide are preheated in preheating unit, enters ITM tubes, mixes homogeneously with oxygen, burns as flamelets and generates combustion products in combustor. The combustion products exit the combustor at combustion outlet port.
   USE - Burner system for clean combustion in gas turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The geometric compatibility between tubular ITM and micromixer provides a combined oxy-combustion ITM micromixer. The system recovers the pressure and high temperature energy of the oxygen-depleted air stream and hence increases the overall system efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   a. A method for using an ion transfer membrane micromixer headend for power generation.
   b. A non-transitory computer readable medium storing program for using an ion transfer membrane micromixer headend for power generation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the combustor.
   Combustor (200)
   Closed end (237)
   Mixing plenum (241)
   Screw (243)
   Micromixer headend (250)

GA 202140299Q

   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high selectivity, high activity and excellent carbon dioxide activation ability, low cost and simple preparation method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst, comprising (i) dissolving soluble nickel salt in deionized water, adding molybdenum salt, stirring and heating at 60-100 degrees C for 1-24 hours, and evaporating to obtain solid, (ii) calcining the solid at 500-1000 degrees C for 1-600 minutes, preferably 60-120 minutes to obtain composite nickel molybdenum oxide, and (iii) calcining composite nickel molybdenum oxide in mixed atmosphere of hydrogen and methane at 300-700 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours, and passivating in 1% oxygen/argon atmosphere for 10-24 hours to obtain nickel supported molybdenum carbide catalyst; and
   (2) use method of the nano nickel supported catalyst, comprising (1) adding catalyst and solvent in a mass ratio of 0.001-0.1 into a reaction kettle, (2) replacing air in the reaction kettle using carbon dioxide, and continuously filling carbon dioxide to a specific pressure, and (3) filling hydrogen to react at 100-300 degrees C for 10 minutes-25 hours, where the volume ratio between carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1-30%:70-99%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In step (iii), calcining is carried out by heating to a specific temperature at a heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute, and continuously increasing temperature at a heating rate of 1 degrees C/minute to calcination temperature.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble nickel salt is nickel acetate, nickel nitrate, nickel chloride, nickel sulfate and/or nickel acetylacetonate. The molybdenum salt is molybdic acid, paramolybdic acid, molybdate and/or paramolybdate. In step (1), the solvent is methanol, dimethylformamide, water, cyclohexane, dichloromethane, acetone, chloroform and/or dimethyl sulfoxide. In step (2): the volume ratio between hydrogen and methane is 2-4:1; the mass ratio between catalyst and carbon dioxide is 0.001-1:1; and the pressure is 0.5-15 MPa.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

An outdoor unit (200) is provided for sucking outside air and generating oxygen generated by separating foreign matter, nitrogen and carbon dioxide from the sucked outside air through the indoor unit, and discharging the separated nitrogen and carbon dioxide to the outside. The indoor unit is provided for separating carbon dioxide from the sucked indoor air to supply purified air to the room.
   USE - Oxygen generator for air purification.

The indoor air is purified in the indoor unit, and oxygen generation can be performed in the outdoor unit, thus maximizing the air cleaning function. The indoor unit can be moved and installed relatively freely. The user's convenience can be improved, by compressing the air discharged to the outside and cleaning the dust collecting unit.

PN CN109052437-A; CN109052437-B
TI Preparing flower shape formed mesoporous magnesium oxide nanoparticle comprises e.g. adding distilled water into the reaction vessel, adding magnesium hydroxide and three-part quaternary ammonium salt template, and reacting.
AU ZHANG L
   ZHANG T
   TANG T
   CHEN Q
   FU W
   ZHENG S
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 201900936Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing flower shape formed mesoporous magnesium oxide nanoparticle comprises (i) adding distilled water into the reaction vessel at room temperature, adding magnesium hydroxide and three-part quaternary ammonium salt template, sealing the reactor, removing the air in the kettle by carbon dioxide, and then adding the carbon dioxide gas into the closed reactor, stirring the reaction for 3-6 hours while maintaining the pressure in the autoclave to obtain clear solution, and (ii) removing the carbon dioxide gas in the reactor, dynamically crystallizing the clarified solution at a high temperature, filtering, drying, and calcining to obtain final product.
   USE - The magnesium oxide nanoparticle is useful for adsorption, catalysis, mechanics, optics and ceramics.
   ADVANTAGE - The magnesium oxide nanoparticle has large external surface area and contains large number of basic active sites.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing flower shape formed mesoporous magnesium oxide nanoparticle comprises (i) adding distilled water into the reaction vessel at room temperature, adding magnesium hydroxide and three-part quaternary ammonium salt template, sealing the reactor, removing the air in the kettle by carbon dioxide, and then adding the carbon dioxide gas into the closed reactor, stirring the reaction for 3-6 hours while maintaining the pressure in the autoclave to obtain clear solution, the three-part quaternary ammonium salt template is mono(N1-dodecyl-N3-(3-(dodecyldimethylammonio)-2-hydroxypropyl)-N3,N3-diethyl-2-hydroxy-N1,N1-dimethylpropane-1,3-diaminium) dichloride of formula (I), and (ii) removing the carbon dioxide gas in the reactor, dynamically crystallizing the clarified solution at a high temperature, filtering, drying, and calcining to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide gas described in the step (i) is adding into the closed reaction vessel to a pressure in the reactor of 1-4 MPa. The molar ratio between magnesium hydroxide, three-part quaternary ammonium salt template agent and water is 1:0.04-0.1:150-200. Preferred Method: The dynamic crystallization temperature in the step (ii) is 70-120 degrees C, the dynamic crystallization time is 5-10 hours, and the dynamic crystallization rotation speed is 40-120 revolutions per minute. The calcination temperature in the step (ii) is 350-500 degrees C, the calcination time is 3-4 hours, and the rate of temperature rise during calcination is 1-2 degrees C/minutes.

The compressor (1) is operated at a low load                to fill the main circuit system with the carbon                dioxide working medium. The inlet pressure of the                compressor is reduced through the balance tank, and                the back pressure of the circulation system is                reduced. During the load reduction process, the                excitation, generator parameters are monitored, or                the corresponding load is alternately adjusted to                reduce the load. When the total system load is less                than 10% of the rated load, the brake is manually                switched on to stop. At this time, the first-stage                turbine bypass (4by), the second-stage turbine                bypass (5by) and the compressor bypass (1by) are                all interlocked and opened by not less than                20%.

   FORREST B A
   LU X
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 202287391U
AB    NOVELTY - The calcination system comprises a reactor                (10) that is designed to heat a                carbonate-containing raw material in the presence                of an oxidant to form a decomposition stream                containing solids and carbon dioxide gas. A                separator is designed to separate the decomposition                stream into a gas stream. A heat exchange unit (25)                is designed to receive one or both the gas stream                and the solid stream and withdraw heat to provide a                cooled gas stream including the carbon dioxide and                a cooled solid stream. The reactor and the                separator are combined as a single unit.
   USE - System for calcination of carbonate-containing                raw material e.g. calcium carbonate containing                material such as limestone. Uses include but are                not limited to cement, steel, quicklime, and                electrical power.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing quicklime and                other industrial products that are substantially or                completely carbon dioxide-free, so that quicklime                can be formed in a manner in which no carbon                dioxide or completely free of carbon dioxide is                released to the atmosphere. The quicklime is                available for use in a variety of industries such                as cement, steel, and electrical power generation,                without adding carbon output to the environment.                The sulfur species in the sour gas can be                effectively removed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for calcination of carbonate-containing raw                material.

PN CN114289006-A
TI Preparation method of carbon ball catalyst for lithium carbon dioxide battery, involves subjecting glucose solution to a hydrothermal reaction to obtain porous carbon spheres, and then through washing, the obtained precipitate is carbon spheres with uniform distribution.
AU CHOU S
   LI L
   CHEN J
   QIAO Y
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 202255212S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of carbon ball catalyst for lithium carbon dioxide (Li-CO2) battery using glucose as a carbon source, preparing nano carbon balls by a hydrothermal method, which involves: i. weighing the glucose of set amount, adding water and mixing, obtain homogeneous glucose solution; ii. subjecting glucose solution to a hydrothermal reaction to obtain porous carbon spheres, and then through washing, the obtained precipitate is carbon spheres with uniform distribution, and after drying.
   USE - Preparation method of carbon ball catalyst for lithium carbon dioxide battery (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon sphere catalyst increases the active sites of the material so as to improve the intrinsic activity of the material; the formed nano carbon spheres are used as electrodes for lithium-carbon dioxide electrodes and have lower charge-discharge platforms and catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for porous carbon nanosphere catalysts for lithium carbon dioxide (Li-CO2) batteries, prepared by using the preparation method for Li-CO2 battery carbon ball catalyst and catalyst is to control the morphology and pore size of the porous nano-carbon spherical catalyst particles by controlling the combination of any condition parameters in the concentration of glucose, the type or concentration of detergent, the temperature of hydrothermal reaction, the time and the drying temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a scanning electron microscope image of the glucose carbon ball material for lithium carbon dioxide battery
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), tap water, deionized water or a liquid of tap water and deionized water is used as the water, and the molar volume of glucose in the glucose solution is controlled to be 0.05-0.7 mole per liter (mol/L). In step (2), the volume of glucose solution is not more than 3/4 of the volume of the inner lining of the reactor, and the volume of the glucose solution is not less than 1/4 of the volume of the inner lining of the reactor and is sealed. The temperature of controlling the hydrothermal reaction is 140-220 degreesC, and the reaction times is 6-26 hours, and then the product solution is cooled at room temperature. In step (2), adopt centrifugal washing or suction filtration washing method. In the centrifugal washing step, the detergent is washed with ethanol, deionized water, tap water, or a mixture of water and ethanol, or a hydrochloric acid solution with a concentration not higher than 1 mole per liter. When the mixed solution is used as the detergent, centrifugal washing or suction filtration washing is selected according to the concentration of different solutions. When the concentration of the mixed solution is 0.05-0.1 mole per liter (mol/L), centrifugal washing is used, and washing with suction filtration to obtain precipitates with particles of different sizes. In the step (2), the precipitate can be processed by vacuum drying and freeze drying. Vacuum drying step is drying 8-15 hours at 40-80 degreesC, and freeze-drying is first in the refrigerator of-5-30 degreesC by precipitation agglomeration, then carries out at the temperature of-20-40 degreesC, freeze-drying.

GA 202234833J
AB    NOVELTY - Air conditioner comprises a cooling medium circulating loop which makes the cooling medium circulate in the loop composed of compressor, condenser, expansion valve, evaporator, four-way valve and pressure reducer. The compressor is used to compress low-temperature and low-pressure refrigerant gas into high-temperature and high-pressure refrigerant gas and discharge to the condenser. The four-way valve is used to control the flow direction of the refrigerant in the refrigerant circuit, so that the outdoor heat exchanger and the indoor heat exchanger can be switched between the condenser and the evaporator. An indoor ambient temperature sensor is used to detect indoor ambient temperature. An indoor coil temperature sensor is used to detect indoor coil temperature. Second carbon dioxide sensor is set for acquiring a second carbon dioxide concentration and sending the second carbon dioxide concentration to the controller.
   USE - Used as air conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving room carbon dioxide concentration monitoring accuracy to adjust the fresh air amount of the air conditioner in real time.

PN WO2022036368-A1
TI Individual`s respiration monitoring system, has image processing circuitry determining parameter of carbon dioxide gas being exhaled about mouth of individual based upon thermal image and optical image captured by thermal and optical sensors.
AU STERNBERG E M
   HYDE J N
   MAJDI M S
   RODRIGUEZ J J
   SON Y
   CHEN Y
AE ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
GA 202226723N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a thermal sensor configured to detect carbon dioxide gas. The optical sensor and image processing circuitry is configured to determine a parameter carbon dioxide gas is exhaled about a mouth of the individual based upon a thermal image captured by the thermal sensor and optical image captured by the optical sensor. The processing circuitry is configured to provide an indication of onset of acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) in the individual based upon the parameter. The thermal sensor comprises a camera positioned about the individual. The optical sensor comprises optical camera positioned about the individual. The thermal camera comprises a filter to detect the exhaled carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Non-invasive, remote sensing multi-sensor respiratory imaging system for continuous measurement of respiratory parameters of exhaled respiratory gases of a patient with acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) secondary to corona viruses i.e. COVID-19.
   ADVANTAGE - The system continuously monitors changes in respiratory patterns and carbon dioxide concentration in exhaled respiratory gases, thus allowing for early warnings that can alert healthcare personnel to the beginnings of respiratory failure. The system allows therapeutic interventions to be implemented before respiratory failure has progressed, thus reducing the morbidity, mortality, and severity of acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) secondary to corona viruses such as COVID-19, and hence reducing the chance of healthcare personnel themselves becoming infected with a virus such as SARS-CoV-2, and potentially developing ARDS.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a carbon dioxide sensor and thermal camera.

PN CN113777247-A
TI Convenient and economical method for measuring gas-liquid total mass transfer coefficient of microreactor, comprises measuring gas-liquid total mass transfer coefficient of microreactor, and calculating gas-liquid total mass transfer coefficient for deionized water and carbon dioxide systems.
AU ZHU W
   REN Y
   WANG H
   ZHANG R
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2021E8595L
AB    NOVELTY - Convenient and economical method for measuring the gas-liquid total mass transfer coefficient of a microreactor, comprises using carbon dioxide and deionized water as the gas-liquid two-phase system for measuring the gas-liquid total mass transfer coefficient of the microreactor, mixing the two-phase flow of carbon dioxide and water through the microreactor, taking the mixed solution from the outlet and adding into the strong alkali solution, measuring the pH value of the strong alkali solution before and after adding the mixed solution, and calculating the change in hydroxide concentration by the pH value, calculating the concentration change of carbon dioxide and the mass transfer flux of carbon dioxide gas after passing through the microreactor from the change of hydroxide concentration, calculating the total mass transfer coefficient of the microreactor, and calculating gas-liquid total mass transfer coefficient for deionized water and carbon dioxide systems.
   USE - Convenient and economical method for measuring the gas-liquid total mass transfer coefficient of microreactor used for chemical reaction process of transmission or mixing control and in gas-liquid multi-phase reaction system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is more convenient, the measuring cost is low, without using a large device e.g. chromatograph, and the measuring time is shortened from the hour to 0.5-2 minutes. The method can guide the optimization of the micro-reactor flow channel structure design, and better enhance gas-liquid mass transfer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the gas-liquid total mass transfer coefficient measurement of the microreactor (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method utilizes strong alkali solution which is sodium hydroxide, or potassium hydroxide, hydrogen ion, carbon dioxide, carbonic acid, sodium carbonate, and sodium bicarbonate.

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) comprises a reaction                chamber (118) fluidly connected to an acid tank                (110), a carbonate tank (114), and a carbon dioxide                tank (102), where the reaction chamber has a liquid                byproduct release outlet. A carbonate supply pump acts to regulate                flow of carbonate from the carbonate to reaction                chamber. Acid and carbonate react within the                chamber to produce carbon dioxide gas that flows                into the carbon dioxide. A processor transmits a                control signal to the acid and the acid supply                pumps. The firefighting                apparatus is simple in structure, and can be easily                installed in a simple manner.

PN CN113669121-A; CN113669121-B; WO2022184186-A2; WO2022184186-A3
TI Steam condensing system for thermal power plant,            has condenser medium inlet for inputting liquid carbon            dioxide, and condenser exhaust port connected with            compressor inlet, where gasified carbon dioxide is            introduced into compressor to be cooling medium.
AU WEI H
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
   UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 2021D7364J
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a steam turbine that comprises                a steam turbine outlet used for discharging steam.                A compressor comprises a first compressor inlet and                a compressor outlet. A condenser comprises a                condenser steam inlet, a condenser medium inlet, a                condenser outlet and a condenser exhaust port. The                hot steam inlet is connected with the steam turbine                outlet. The condenser medium inlet is connected                with the compressor outlet. The condenser medium                inlet is used for inputting liquid carbon dioxide.                The condenser exhaust port is connected with the                first compressor inlet. The liquid carbon dioxide                is used as the cooling medium. The heat is                exchanged with the steam discharged from the steam                turbine in the condenser. The steam is condensed.                The gasified carbon dioxide is introduced into the                compressor to be compressed to be the cooling                medium to enter the condenser after being                compressed to be liquid circularly.
   USE - Steam condensing system for thermal power                plant, coal-fired power plant and electric power                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses carbon dioxide as cooling                medium, which can fundamentally solve the problem                of large use amount of circulating water in power                plant, complex anti-corrosion and anti-scaling                technique, high waste water treatment cost and                environmental pollution. The power consumption is                reduced, thus saving energy, circulating waste                water zero discharge, saving external purchase                cost, reducing the water resource consumption, and                protecting the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                electric power plant condensing method.

PT P
PN CN113481536-A
TI Preparation of electrocatalytic carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst for converting carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide , involves stirring palladium chloride, copper chloride, cobalt nitrate and L-glutamic acid, adjusting pH, adding carbon nanotubes, ultrasonically processing, and reacting.
AU YANG L
   BAI Z
   MIAO R
   ZHANG Q
   WANG C
   CHANG F
AE UNIV HENAN NORMAL (UYNR-C)
GA 2021B9976Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of alloy cubic hollow shell structure electrocatalytic carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst involves mixing 0.5-1.5 mL of 2 mg/mL palladium chloride aqueous solution, 0.245-1.334 mL of 6 mg/mL copper chloride dihydrate aqueous solution and 250 muL of 6 mg/mL cobalt nitrate solution, dispersing in ethylene glycol to obtain solution (A), adding 16.67 mg L-glutamic acid to solution (A) to obtain solution (B), mixing, adjusting pH of solution to 11 with 8 wt.% potassium hydroxide and ethylene glycol solution, stirring to obtain solution (C), adding 5 mg commercial multi-walled carbon nanotubes, ultrasonically processing for 30 minutes, stirring for 2 hours to obtain suspension, adding suspension to 25 mL PTFE-lined autoclave, sealing, heating to 160degrees Celsius at rate of 5degrees Celsius/minute for 6 hours, leaving still until it is cooled to room temperature, centrifuging, washing the product with secondary water for 3-4 times and vacuum drying at 40degrees Celsius for 24 hours.
   USE - Preparation of alloy cubic hollow shell structure electrocatalytic carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst for converting carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and organic compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple and carried out under mild reaction condition to produce product with high efficiency, and consumes less energy. The electroreduction catalyst has excellent electronic effect and synergistic effect between palladium, copper and cobalt, which effectively increases the Faraday efficiency of electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.

AU XI Z

The group of CO2 condensers and containers are used to liquefy the CO2 gases. The group of boilers are used for combustion of CO2-neutral fuels such as plant straw and wood in winter, in order to heat CO2 fluids with their combustion heat before they enter the heat engines. The S storage system is used to store CO2 liquids in order to absorb the CO2 liquids condensed from CO2 gases during the cold period and in winter. The S storage system and C storage system are used for heat or cold transfer with hot water or water ice as the working medium which play role for heat or cold storage from summer to winter times or from winter to summer times to heat or cool carbon dioxide in winter or summer times.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide based groove type heat collecting device used in field of solar high temperature heat utilization, has cooling unit whose outlet is connected with inlet of compression unit.
AU HAN W
   JIN H
   LIU Q
   HONG H
   WANG Z
   XUE X
AE INST ENG THERMOPHYSICS CHINESE (CAET-C)
GA 2020716736
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a groove-type heat collection unit that converts solar energy into thermal energy. Heat exchange unit is used to transfer the heat in the heat collection unit to the heat user. A heat recovery unit is used to transfer heat to the heat transfer working fluid. A cooling unit is used to reduce the temperature of supercritical carbon dioxide working fluid. A compression unit increases the pressure of the heat transfer working medium and overcomes the circulation resistance of the heat transfer working medium. The outlet of heat collection unit is connected with the inlet of heat exchange unit, such that working fluid flowing out of the heat exchange unit reaches the cooling unit after passing through the heat recovery unit. The outlet of cooling unit is connected with the inlet of the compression unit, so that the working fluid flowing out of the compression unit enters the heat collection unit to complete the cycle after being heated by the regenerative unit.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide based groove type heat collecting device used in field of solar high temperature heat utilization (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide-based groove type heat collecting device uses supercritical carbon dioxide working fluid, which has stable physical properties, to increase the heat collection temperature, and to meet the needs of users with different loads.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the supercritical carbon dioxide based groove type heat collecting device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Heat exchanger (2)

PN US2020176791-A1; WO2020112774-A1; KR2021094074-A; CN113228361-A; US11424469-B2
TI Method for elevated pressure operation of molten            carbonate fuel cell with enhanced carbon dioxide            utilization, by operating fuel cell at a cathode            pressure of 1.0 kilo-Pascals-gram or more, and a            transference of 0.97 or less.
AU ROSEN J
   SHEN E B
   GEARY T C
   ROTH J
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
   FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
   EXXONMOBIL TECHNOLOGY & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202048921K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves operating a molten                carbonate fuel cell (120) comprising an anode (127)                and a cathode (129) at an operating condition                comprising a cathode pressure of 1.0                kilo-Pascals-gram or more, an average current                density of 60 milli-amperes per square centimeter                or more, a transference of 0.97 or less, and with a                cathode input stream comprising a carbon dioxide                content of 5.0 vol.% or less, to generate                electricity, an anode exhaust (125) comprising                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and carbon doxide, and a                cathode exhaust comprising a carbon dioxide content                of 1.0 vol.% or less.
   USE - Method for elevated pressure operation of                molten carbonate fuel cell with enhanced carbon                dioxide utilization.
   ADVANTAGE - Increasing the operating pressure of the                molten carbonate fuel cell when using a low carbon                dioxide-content cathode input stream can provide                unexpectedly large increases in operating voltage                while also reducing or minimizing the amount of                alternative ion transport and/or enhancing carbon                dioxide utilization. The increased operating                voltage can be beneficial in a variety of ways,                such as reducing the waste heat generated by the                fuel cell and/or allowing for trade-off of                operating voltage for increased current                density.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic diagram which                shows a reaction system for operating a fuel cell                array of molten carbonate fuel cells in conjunction                with a chemical synthesis process.
   Reforming stage (110)
   Molten carbonate fuel cell (120)
   Anode exhaust (125)
   Anode (127)
   Cathode (129)
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC L03-E04C; X16-C02

TI Sterilizing carbon dioxide heat pump locomotive air conditioning cooling machine unit, has evaporation condenser connected to suction end of R134a compressor, and ozone generator inserted into outlet of air supply fan of air conditioner.
AU HE M
   GOU S
   TAO W
AE GANSU YIDE NEW ENERGY EQUIP CO LTD (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 2020259448
AB    NOVELTY - The unit has a regenerator (6) connected to a R744 gas-liquid separator (13) that is connected to a suction end of a carbon dioxide compressor (1) i.e. R744 trans-critical compressor to form a loop. The exhaust end of the R134a compressor (2) is connected to a combined air conditioner external unit (4). The combined air conditioner external unit is connected to a R134a accumulator (12). The R134a accumulator connected to an evaporation condenser (5). The evaporation condenser is connected to a suction end of the R134a compressor. An ozone generator (21) is inserted into an outlet of an air supply fan (14) of a combined air conditioner (3).
   USE - Sterilizing carbon dioxide heat pump locomotive air conditioning cooling machine unit.
   ADVANTAGE - The unit is convenient to popularize and use, and can purify air in a better manner, and has high heat exchange performance, and achieves energy-saving function in an environmental friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a sterilizing carbon dioxide heat pump locomotive air conditioning cooling machine unit operating method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic view of a sterilizing carbon dioxide heat pump locomotive air conditioning cooling machine unit.

GA 202019896E
AB    NOVELTY - The fire extinguishing system has a composite inert gas ratio analysis system, a composite inert gas preparation system (2), and a composite inert gas injection system (3). The compound inert gas ratio analysis system is provided with an inert gas storage tank, and a miniature static gas mixer is connected downstream of the inert gas storage tank, and a goaf simulation device is set downstream of the miniature static gas mixer. A gas chromatograph is provided downstream of the goaf simulation device. The composite inert gas preparation system is provided with a carbon dioxide tank truck (20) and a nitrogen generator (21). The carbon dioxide tank truck and the nitrogen generator are connected together through a static gas mixer. A composite inert gas injection system is connected downstream of the static gas mixer.
   USE - Fire extinguishing system for injecting compound inert gas such as carbon dioxide (CO2) and nitrogen (N2) into coal mine.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures its fire extinguishing effect, also effectively improves the applicability and feasibility of the inert gas in the prevention of spontaneous combustion of large-scale and long-term residual coal in the goaf. The safety of inert gas in large area and long-term residual coal casing spontaneous combustion is improved, the maintenance cost is reduced, and the beneficial effect of effectively preventing fire or extinguishing open flames is achieved.

PN DE102018116852-A1; WO2020011468-A1; CN112368516-A; EP3821175-A1; US2021270477-A1
TI Device for regulating carbon dioxide content in interior, has bioreactor which is provided with inlet for admitting air from interior, outlet for exhausting air from bioreactor and living being for converting carbon dioxide into dioxide.
AU NEESE C
   AMSHOFF B
AE MIELE & CIE KG (MIEL-C)
   MIELE GMBH & CO KG (MIEL-C)
   MIELE & CIE KG (MIEL-C)
   MIELE & CIE KG (MIEL-C)
GA 202005803L
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a bioreactor (3) which is provided with an inlet for admitting air from an interior, an outlet for exhausting air from the bioreactor and one living being (4) designed to partially convert carbon dioxide contained in the air into dioxide by photosynthesis. The living being performing photosynthesis is alga. A lighting device (5) is arranged coaxially in the bioreactor. The bioreactor is designed as a plate reactor.
   USE - Device such as household appliance or commercial device for regulating carbon dioxide content in interior. Uses include but are not limited to private household such as children room and public facility such as school, kindergarten, classroom, conference room, living room and office environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The lighting device is arranged in the bioreactor to ensure optimal and direct illumination of the bioreactor. The light energy entered by the lighting device correlates directly with carbon dioxide to be converted to enable optimal operation. The device maintains and/or improves the well-being, concentration and performance of person staying in the interior. The strong fumigation of the living being in the bioreactor is avoided by using the membrane module. The single living being is made to come to a reactor surface for a short time and allowed to enjoy light. The bioreactor is formed from multi-wall sheets which are provided with one membrane module through which the interior air is flowed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the cross-sectional view and top view of the device for regulating carbon dioxide content in interior.
   Casing (1)
   Pump (2)
   Bioreactor (3)
   Living being (4)
   Lighting device (5)

PT P
PN CN110679821-A
TI Platform for preparing custom carbonated beverage, has electronic control unit that is connected to parameter detection unit volume, and analysis device which is configured to calculate gas volume based on carbon dioxide gas concentration.
AU YOU X
AE YOU X (YOUX-Individual)
GA 202007510X
AB    NOVELTY - The platform has a carbon dioxide gas tank which is used to store a compressed carbon dioxide gas in advance. A quantitative driving device is provided in which one end is connected to a vent pipe, and the other end is connected to a bottom opening of the soda water modulation container. A soda-water modulation container is configured to pre-fill a fixed volume of drinking water before receiving the released carbon dioxide gas. A real-time video recording device is configured to perform the real-time video recording operation on the environment of the current user to obtain a live video frame corresponding to the current moment. An instant recording device is provided with a parameter detection unit and an electronic control unit which is connected to the parameter detection unit. A volume analysis device is configured for calculating the gas volume based on the carbon dioxide gas concentration and the fixed capacity.
   USE - Platform for preparing custom carbonated beverage such as water, cola, sprite, and soda.
   ADVANTAGE - The custom carbonated beverage preparation platform has a simple principle and is convenient and practical. Since the carbon dioxide beverage can be selected to be customized according to the preference of the current user for the manufacture of carbonated beverages, the device structure is simplified while meeting the needs of different populations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a custom carbonated beverage preparation method.

TI Method for reinforcing carbon dioxide by solid waste by using salty wastewater, involves collecting solid raw material after gas-solid separation into bag filter and returning to entrained flow reactor through second screw feeder.
AU LIU R
   WANG X
   WANG Q
   HE Z
   XIAO T
AE CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2019048043
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves humidifying mineralized solid waste by the raw material warehouse (1) and entering dryer (2). The pretreated mineralized solid waste is transported into wind main pipe through the first screw feeder (3) and carried by wind into entrained flow reactor (6). The salty wastewater for moisture distribution is injected into the gas mixer (5). The amount of moisture in bed reactor is adjusted by changing the flow rate of the water metering pump (15). The flue gas (13) after removing carbon dioxide at top of entrained flow reactor and the raw materials after the reaction are first cooled, and entered into the bag filter (10) for gas-solid separation. The solid raw material after gas-solid separation is collected and collected into a bag filter, and returned to the entrained flow reactor through second screw feeder (4) to continue the reaction. The flue gas analyzer is used to analyze the concentration of carbon dioxide in the flue gas after carbon dioxide removal.
   USE - Method for reinforcing carbon dioxide by solid waste by using salty wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The flow-bed reactor is used to improve the reaction contact efficiency. The method effectively improves the utilization of waste and reduce the CO2 emission of power plants.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for enhancing carbon dioxide of solid waste by using salty wastewater.
   Raw material warehouse (1)
   Dryer (2)
   First screw feeder (3)
   Second screw feeder (4)
   Gas mixer (5)
   Entrained flow reactor (6)
   Bag filter (10)
   Flue gas (13)
   Water metering pump (15)

PN US2018230010-A1
TI Heat generating and producing methane, comprises e.g. exothermically reacting hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the reactor to form methane, water and heat and separating the methane from the water.
AU KING F A
AE KING F A (KING-Individual)
GA 2018634329
AB    NOVELTY - Heat generating and producing (M1) methane, comprises (a) providing hydrogen and carbon dioxide to a reactor, (b) exothermically reacting the hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the reactor to form methane, water and heat, (c) separating the methane from the water, and (d) at least one of (i) using the heat from the reactor for an industrial process comprising generating power in a turbine and heating, (ii) burning the methane for an industrial process comprising generating power in a turbine and heating, and (iii) oxidizing the methane of step (b) in an exothermic reaction to produce at least carbon dioxide and hydrogen, at least one of which is used to repeat step (a), and heat, where the heat used for an industrial process comprising generating power in a turbine and heating.
   USE - The method is useful for heat generating and producing methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides: an improved heat producing and heating systems for industrial environments; an improved method of producing methane from hydrogen and carbon dioxide; and an improved method of cyclically performing exothermic reactions in an effort to greatly enhance the heating capability from methane in environments, preferably when oxygen and/or hydrogen can be relatively inexpensively obtained.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for heat generating and producing (M2) methane comprising (s1) oxidizing methane in an exothermic reaction to produce heat and at least carbon dioxide and hydrogen, where the heat used for an industrial process generating power in a turbine and heating, and the steps (a)-(e) as per se.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (d)(iii) is performed and the oxidation step further produces carbon monoxide and water, with at least one of the carbon dioxide and hydrogen separated from the carbon monoxide. The step (d)(iii) is performed and both the carbon dioxide and hydrogen are used to repeat step (a). The step (d)(iii) is performed and both the carbon dioxide and hydrogen are used to repeat step (a). The method further comprises: a heat exchanger receiving output of the reactor, where heat exchanger used for an industrial process comprising generating electricity and heating. The carbon dioxide provided for step (a) is a waste product from one of combustion, and) fermentation, generated from dissolution of water and an acid, generated from an amine process from fossil fuels, and obtained from a natural emission from one of geysers, hot springs, or volcanoes; step of providing a heater, where heater initially heating the reactor to at least 300 degrees C to begin the exothermic reaction step, and then securing the heater while continuing the exothermic reaction step. The hydrogen provided for step (a) is generated from the step of electrolysis of water. The step of electrolysis performed generates oxygen, and the oxygen is used in step (d)(iii). The reactor has a heat exchanger for use with step (d)(i). The reactor has a catalyst comprising nickel ruthenium and alumina, and the exothermic reaction step utilizes the catalyst to assist in performing the reaction. In the method (M2) both the hydrogen and the carbon dioxide are provided to the reactor from the oxidizing step. The step (s1) is performed and the oxidation step further produces carbon monoxide and water, with at least one of the carbon dioxide and hydrogen separated from the carbon monoxide. The steps (s1)-(e1) are performed repeatedly in a cycle. The method (M2) further comprises: a heat exchanger receiving output of the reactor, where the heat exchanger used for an industrial process comprising generating electricity and heating; and providing a heater, where heater initially heating the reactor to at least 300 degrees C to begin the exothermic reaction step, and then securing the heater while continuing the exothermic reaction step. The carbon dioxide provided for step (s1) is a waste product from one of combustion, and fermentation, generated from dissolution of water and an acid, generated form an amine process from fossil fuels, and obtained from a natural emission from one of geysers, hot springs, and volcanoes, and the reactor has the heat exchanger.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Sleep monitoring system for monitoring sleep of            subjects e.g. couple sharing bed, has processor that            derives sleep pattern information from registered            carbon dioxide concentration during presence of subject            based on degree of crosstalk.
AU HILBIG R
   BOUMA P H
   SU J
   JING SU S
AE KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
GA 201836328W
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a processor (31) that                monitors a carbon dioxide concentration from sensor                data produced by the carbon dioxide sensors                (21,21') in a sleeping region to detect a presence                of a subject. The processor registers the monitored                carbon dioxide concentration, and determines a                degree of crosstalk between the sensors upon                detecting the presence of the subject. The                processor derives the sleep pattern information                comprising an indication that the subject is awake                and/or asleep, during the presence of the subject                from the registered concentration, based on the                degree of crosstalk.
   USE - Sleep monitoring system for monitoring sleep                of pair of subjects e.g. couple sharing bed in                bedroom.
   ADVANTAGE - The active monitoring of the carbon dioxide                concentrations in the space is facilitated when the                space is occupied by the pair of subjects, thus the                efficiency of monitoring sleep of the pair of                subjects is improved. The data collection during                calibration is repeated a number of times so as to                improve the accuracy of the extracted                thresholds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for monitoring the sleep of the                pair of subjects in the different sleeping regions                within a space; and
   (2) a computer program product for monitoring                the sleep of the pair of subjects in the different                sleeping regions within the space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                sleep monitoring system.
   Sleep monitoring system (10)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (21,21')
   Computing device (30)
   Processor (31)
   Data storage device (33)

AU LAI D
AE DAYAO MAWANG KEHUA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DAYA-Non-standard)
GA 202028266K
AB    NOVELTY - Hemp phenol extraction involves screening and predrying hemp, crushing, carrying out secondary drying, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, to obtain primary extract of diphenol, carrying out molecular distillation of the extract to cannabidiol enrichment, separating and purifying cannabidiol by chromatography, and irradiating UV light on the separated and purified cannabidiol.
   USE - Hemp phenol extraction method.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is carried out with high purification efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The drying is carried out at 125-145 degrees C until the water content is less than 6.5%. The hemp is crushed to 65-95 mesh. The secondary drying process is carried out at 125-145 degrees C until the moisture content is less than 4.5%. The extraction is carried out at 35-45 degrees C under pressure of 15-25 MPa for 1.5-3.5 hours. The molecular distillation is carried out at 80-200 degrees C under vacuum degree of 0.01-15 torr. The chromatographic process is carried out by diluting the extract to obtain diluted solution with solid content of 1.1-1.8% and carrying out chromatographic column gradient elution using large pore absorption resin.

AU YU H

   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is mild in reaction conditions and high in specific selectivity, can be recycled and is environment-friendly, and the cleanness of industrial reaction is improved. The manufacturing cost and the generation of three wastes are reduced, and the environment-friendly pressure is reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The dimethylbiphenyl diisocyanate is prepared by mixing o-toluidine, a polyoxometalate and a solvent in a reaction vessel, adding benzene silane, an acid binding agent and a dehydrating agent, mixing uniformly, and introducing gaseous carbon dioxide, and setting the reaction temperature to 0-50 degrees C, and stirring the reaction for 6-36 hours, separating and purifying. The polyoxometalate catalyst after the reaction is recovered by using an organic solvent is recycled. Preferred Components: The acid binding agent is pyridine or triethylamine. The dehydrating agent is dimethyl sulfoxide or phosphorus oxychloride. The solvent is selected from the group consisting of anhydrous acetonitrile or N,N-dimethylformamide Preferred Condition: The reaction temperature is set to 0-40 degrees C, and the reaction time is 6-24 hours. The reaction temperature is set to 30 degrees C and the reaction time is 12 hours. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the polyoxometalate, the o-toluidine, the benzene silane, the acid binding agent and the dehydrating agent is 0.02:2:4:1:2, and the ratio of the solvent to the o-toluidine is 3 mL/mmol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyoxometalate uses a polyoxometalate of the M-Anderson type where M is iron, nickel, copper, zinc, manganese, aluminum, chromium, carbon monoxide.

PN CN108976085-A
TI Preparing special fertilizer for tea tree germination by mixing biomass charcoal, mixture of aqueous ammonia and humic acid, introducing carbon dioxide, collecting solid material and drying collected material.
AU LI H
   ZHANG S
   FU X
AE BEIJING SANJU LVNENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SJEP-C)
GA 2018A26264
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing special fertilizer for tea tree germination involves mixing biomass charcoal, mixture of aqueous ammonia and humic acid, and introducing carbon dioxide or gas containing carbon dioxide, collecting solid material and drying the collected material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing special fertilizer for tea tree germination.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared fertilizer has high stability and exhibits slow release effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The method further involves agitating the mixed slurry, when the carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide-containing gas is introduced. The method involves intermittently adding humic acid to the mixed slurry at an interval of 0.5-3 minutes, while carbon dioxide or a gas containing carbon dioxide is introduced. The biomass charcoal is obtained by carbonizing biomass at 450-530 degrees C to obtain pyrolysis gas and biomass carbon. The method further involves burning the pyrolysis gas to obtain flue gas, crushing the biomass charcoal, mixing with mixture of aqueous ammonia and humic acid to obtain mixed slurry, introducing flue gas, mixing uniformly, collecting solid material and drying the solid material. The method further involves condensing or spraying the pyrolysis gas to obtain biomass gas, performing combustion, and collecting the flue gas obtained by the combustion, and separating the condensate or spraying aqueous phase onto biomass charcoal, and then preparing the mixed slurry. The method involves collecting liquid, which is used as mother liquor for recycling.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.)

PT P
PN EP3696145-A1; WO2020165445-A1; CA3121602-A1; CN113260593-A; BR112021011628-A2; EP3924300-A1; IN202117036794-A; US2022106194-A1; JP2022520927-W
TI Producing nano-precipitated calcium carbonate comprises simultaneously or consecutively carrying out simultaneously mixing and milling calcium oxide containing material with water and adding under simultaneously mixing and milling.
AU PEDROSO L
   SPIEGEL H
   SKRZYPCZAK M
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 202079451J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing nano-precipitated calcium carbonate (nano-PCC) comprises: (a) providing a calcium oxide containing material, (b) providing water in an amount of up to 200 wt.%, based on the total dry weight of the calcium oxide containing material, (c) providing a carbon dioxide source, (d) preparing nano-PCC by simultaneously or consecutively carrying out (i) simultaneously mixing and milling the calcium oxide containing material of step (a) with the water of step (b), and (ii) adding under simultaneously mixing and milling the carbon dioxide source of step (c) in an amount which corresponds to a mole ratio of the calcium oxide in the calcium oxide containing material of step (a) to the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide source of step (c) of 1:1-1:3.5.
   USE - The process is useful for producing nano-PCC which is used in paper, paper products, paper coatings, ink, paint, coating, plastics, polymer compositions, adhesives, building products, sealants, foodstuff, agricultural products, cosmetic products or pharmaceutical products, preferably in paper, paper coatings, plastics, paint, adhesives and sealants and most preferably in adhesives and sealants (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing nano-precipitated calcium carbonate (nano-PCC) comprises: (a) providing a calcium oxide containing material, (b) providing water in an amount of up to 200 wt.%, based on the total dry weight of the calcium oxide containing material, (c) providing a carbon dioxide source, (d) preparing nano-PCC by simultaneously or consecutively carrying out (i) simultaneously mixing and milling the calcium oxide containing material of step (a) with the water of step (b), and (ii) adding under simultaneously mixing and milling the carbon dioxide source of step (c) in an amount which corresponds to a mole ratio of the calcium oxide in the calcium oxide containing material of step (a) to the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide source of step (c) of 1:1-1:3.5, with the proviso that when steps (i) and (ii) are carried out consecutively, in the first step (i) the calcium oxide containing material of step (a) is simultaneously mixed and milled with an amount of the water of step (b), which corresponds to a mole ratio of the calcium oxide in the calcium oxide containing material of step (a) to water of 1:1-1:1.5, and in the second step (ii) the remaining amount of water of step (b) is added under simultaneously mixing and milling. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) nano-PCC obtained by the process; and
   (2) system for producing a nano-PCC comprising at least one dry mill, comprising (A) at least one inlet suitable for feeding calcium oxide containing material into at least one dry mill, where the inlet is arranged so that it does not come into direct contact with the milling beads within the at least one dry mill during milling, (B) at least one inlet suitable for feeding water into at least one dry mill, (C) at least one inlet suitable for feeding a carbon dioxide source into at least one dry mill, where the inlet is arranged such that it is located below the liquid inlet level in the at least one dry mill and (D) at least one outlet for removing the nano-PCC from at least one dry mill, where the outlet comprises a sieve and is arranged so that it is in direct contact with the milling beads within the dry mill during milling and is at least partially located below the liquid inlet level in the at least one dry mill.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises (e) separating the nano-PCC from the mixture obtained from step (d). The process does not involve a drying step and preferably the nano-PCC obtained in step (d) has a residual total moisture content of 0.1-10 wt.%, based on the total dry weight of the nano-PCC, preferably 0.2-8 wt.%, more preferably 0.2-5 wt.%, and most preferably 0.2-3 wt.%. The calcium oxide containing material provided in step (a) has a minimum calcium oxide content of at least 75 wt.%, preferably at least 90 wt.%, and most preferably 95 wt.%, based on the total weight of the calcium oxide containing material and/or has a weight median particle size d50 of 1-300 mm, preferably of 2-200 mm, more preferably of 4-100 mm, and most preferably of 6-80 mm. The carbon dioxide source of step (c) comprises 4-99.8 vol.% carbon dioxide, more preferably 5-95 vol.% carbon dioxide, even more preferably 6-40 vol.% carbon dioxide, even more preferably 7-30 vol.% carbon dioxide and most preferably 8-25 vol.% carbon dioxide, based on the total volume of the carbon dioxide source. The steps (i) and (ii) are carried out consecutively and in the first step (i) the calcium oxide containing material of step (a) is simultaneously mixed and milled with an amount of the water of step (b), which corresponds to a mole ratio of the calcium oxide in the calcium oxide containing material of step (a) to water of 1:1.01-1:1.40, preferably in a mole ratio of 1:1.02-1:1.20 and most preferably in a mole ratio of 1:1.03-1:1.08. The step (d) the mole ratio of the calcium oxide in the calcium oxide containing material of step (a) to the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide source of step (c) is 1:1-1:2, preferably the mole ratio is 1:1.2-1:1.8, more preferably the mole ratio is 1:1.3-1:1.7 and most preferably 1:1.4-1:1.6. The step (d) the steps (i) and (ii) are carried out consecutively and the amount of water added in step (ii) is 1-140 wt.%, based on the total dry weight the calcium oxide containing material, preferably 20-130 wt.%, more preferably 30-100 wt.% and most preferably 40-80 wt.%. The step (d) the steps (i) and (ii) are carried out simultaneously and the amount of water added in step (ii) is 1-180 wt.%, based on the total dry weight the calcium oxide containing material, preferably 20-150 wt.%, more preferably 50-120 wt.% and most preferably 70-90 wt.%. The nano-PCC obtained in step (d): has a specific surface area of 5-80 (preferably 7-40, more preferably 8-20 and most preferably 10-15) m2/g, measured using nitrogen and the BET method according to ISO 9277:2010; and/or is in form of particles having a number-based median particle size d50 of below 900 nm, preferably 1-800 nm, more preferably 40-700 nm, even more preferably 70-500 nm and most preferably 100-400 nm; and/or is in form of particles having a number-based top cut particle size d98 of below 1000 nm, preferably 1-950 nm, more preferably 40-900 nm, even more preferably 70-850 nm and most preferably 100-700 nm; and/or has a residual total moisture content of 0.1-10 wt.%, based on the total dry weight of the nano-precipitated calcium carbonate, preferably 0.2-8 wt.%, more preferably 0.2-5 wt.%, and most preferably 0.2-3 wt.%. The process is performed in at least one dry mill and preferably in at least a dry sand mill. Preferred Components: The system comprises one dry mill, which has inlets (A), (B) and (C) and outlet (D). The system comprises at least two serially arranged dry mills, where the first dry mill at least has inlet (A) and the second dry mill at least has inlets (B) and (C) and outlet (D) and the serially arranged dry mills are connected to each other for transporting the product of the first dry mill to the second dry mill. The first dry mill also has inlet (B).
   EXAMPLE - Calcium carbonate containing material, water and carbon dioxide source were feed to the dry sand mill. Calcium carbonate containing material was fed through inlet at the top of the mill, while water was fed through inlet at a lateral hole in the mill and calcium dioxide source was fed through inlet at a lateral hole in the mill. The obtained precipitated calcium carbonate leaves the mill by the bottom screen and was collected.
