PN WO2020157585-A1; CA3126782-A1; AU2020215838-A1; CN113614025-A; EP3917875-A1; US2022112150-A1
TI Production of acetic acid and dimethyl ether used in e.g. oil application, involves reacting, through catalytic partial oxidation reaction and reactant mixture containing oxygen and feeding portion of hydrogen-lean syngas and dimethyl ether.
AU RAJAGOPALAN V
   PANT A
   NARAYANASWAMY R
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
GA 202074003X
AB    NOVELTY - Production of acetic acid (65) involves (a) reacting, through a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reaction, and a CPO reactant mixture (5) in a CPO reactor (10) to produce a hydrogen-lean syngas (15), (b) feeding at least a portion of the hydrogen-lean syngas and dimethyl ether (DME) to a DME carbonylation unit (30) to produce methyl acetate and a hydrogen-enriched syngas, and (c) feeding at least a portion of the methyl acetate and water to a methyl acetate hydrolysis reaction zone (60) to produce acetic acid and a methanol stream. The CPO reactant mixture comprises hydrocarbons, oxygen, and optionally carbon dioxide and/or steam. The hydrocarbon comprises 3 mol% or more 2C or more alkane. The CPO reactor comprises a CPO catalyst. The hydrogen-lean syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and unreacted hydrocarbons. The hydrogen-enriched syngas (31) comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and unreacted hydrocarbons.
   USE - Production of acetic acid and dimethyl ether (claimed) used in oil, gas, coal fracking of shale, biomass and landfill gas application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of acetic acid having reduced energy requirements, and excellent selectivity and overall carbon efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of acetic acid involves (a) reacting, through a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reaction, and a CPO reactant mixture in a CPO reactor to produce a hydrogen-lean syngas, (b) feeding at least a portion of the hydrogen-lean syngas and dimethyl ether (DME) to a DME carbonylation unit to produce methyl acetate and a hydrogen-enriched syngas, and (c) feeding at least a portion of the methyl acetate and water to a methyl acetate hydrolysis reaction zone to produce acetic acid and a methanol stream. The CPO reactant mixture comprises hydrocarbons, oxygen, and optionally carbon dioxide and/or steam. The hydrocarbon comprises 3 mol% or more 2C or more alkane. The CPO reactor comprises a CPO catalyst. The hydrogen-lean syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and unreacted hydrocarbons. The molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the hydrogen-lean syngas is 0.7-1.3. The hydrogen-enriched syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and unreacted hydrocarbons. The molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the hydrogen-enriched syngas is 1.4-2.2. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of dimethyl ether (DME), which involves (a) reacting, through a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reaction, and a CPO reactant mixture in a CPO reactor to produce a hydrogen-lean syngas, (b) feeding at least a portion of the hydrogen-lean syngas to a dimethyl ether (DME) reactor to produce a DME reactor effluent comprising DME, methanol, water and carbon dioxide, (c) separating at least a portion of the DME reactor effluent into a DME stream, a methanol stream, a water stream, and a carbon dioxide stream, (d) optionally recycling at least a portion of the methanol stream to the DME reactor, and (e) optionally recycling at least a portion of the carbon dioxide stream to the CPO reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of system for production of acetic acid from a hydrogen-lean synthesis gas.
   CPO reactant mixture (5)
   CPO reactor (10)
   Hydrogen-lean syngas (15)
   Compressor (20)
   DME carbonylation unit (30)
   Hydrogen-enriched syngas (31)
   Methyl acetate stream (35)
   Methanol synthesis unit (40)
   DME reaction zone (50)
   Methyl acetate reaction zone (60)
   Acetic acid (65)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The CPO reactor is CPO pressure. The DME carbonylation unit is DME carbonylation pressure. The CPO pressure is same as the DME carbonylation pressure. At least a portion of the hydrogen-lean syngas is compressed in a compressor (20) to yield a compressed syngas. At least a portion of the compressed syngas is fed to the DME carbonylation unit in step (b). The common reactor comprises both methyl acetate hydrolysis reaction zone and the DME synthesis reaction zone. Preferred Process: The compressor size is smaller than the size of a compressor used for compressing hydrogen-lean syngas in an otherwise similar process involves (i) employing a hydrogen recovery unit for producing a hydrogen-lean syngas and/or (ii) producing a hydrogen-lean syngas from hydrocarbons comprising less than 3 mol% 2C or more alkane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon comprises methane, natural gas, natural gas liquids, liquefied petroleum gas, associated gas, well head gas, enriched gas, paraffins, shale gas, shale liquids, fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) off gas, refinery process gases, refinery off gases, stack gases, and fuel gas from a fuel gas header. The 2C or more alkane comprises ethane, propane, and butane. The CPO reactor comprises at least one CPO operational parameter chosen from CPO reactant mixture at 100-500 degrees C, a CPO pressure of 20-80 barg and a CPO contact time for 0.001 milliseconds-5 seconds. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of total moles of carbon in the hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture to the total moles of oxygen in the reactant mixture is 0.5:1-3:1. The molar ratio of the total moles of water in the reactant mixture to the total moles of carbon in the hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture is less than 0.6:1. The molar ratio of the total moles of carbon dioxide in the reactant mixture to the total moles of carbon in the hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture is 0.5:1 or more. Preferred Process: The method further involves introducing at least a portion of the hydrogen-lean syngas to a hydrogen recovery unit to decrease the amount of hydrogen in the hydrogen-lean syngas, and feeding at least a portion of the methanol stream and/or at least a portion of the another methanol stream to a DME synthesis reaction zone to produce a DME stream and feeding at least a portion of the DME stream to the DME carbonylation unit in step (b).

TI Methane production apparatus for manufacturing            methane from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, has control            unit that controls hydrogen supply unit for supplying            hydrogen to reactor, and source gas supply unit.
AU IMAGAWA H
   SAKAI M
   ITO Y
   SAYAMA S
   BABA N
   TANAKA H
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
GA 2019A3704L
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has a reactor (10) that                accommodates a catalyst (11). A source gas supply                unit (31) supplies the source gas supplied from a                supply source of source gas (30) containing carbon                dioxide and nitrogen oxide to the reactor. A                control unit (70) controls a hydrogen supply unit                (41) for supplying hydrogen to the reactor, and the                source gas supply unit. The hydrogen supply unit is                controlled, after supplying a predetermined amount                of the source gas to the reactor. The control unit                supplies the reactor supplied with the source gas                with hydrogen in the number of moles satisfying                first predetermined formula relating number of                moles of hydrogen supplied, number of moles of                carbon dioxide stored in the catalyst in the                reactor supplied with hydrogen, number of moles of                nitrogen oxides stored in the catalyst in the                reactor to which hydrogen is supplied, and number                of moles of hydrogen used to reduce the catalyst in                the reactor supplied with hydrogen.
   USE - Methane production apparatus for manufacturing                methane from carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the amount of carbon dioxide in the gas                phase without being stored in the catalyst is                reduced. The amount of unreacted carbon dioxide                contained in the reaction gas mixture is reduced                after the methanation reaction, and the purity of                the produced methane is improved. The raw material                gas is continuously supplied to the reactor.                Therefore, the production efficiency of methane is                improved, and the production cost of methane is                reduced. The purity of the produced methane is                improved by separating the hydrogen contained in                the reaction gas mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The apparatus satisfies second predetermined                formula relating number of moles of hydrogen used                to reduce the catalyst in the reactor supplied with                hydrogen and number of moles of oxygen supplied to                the reactor. A third predetermined formula is                satisfied relating number of moles of hydrogen used                to reduce the catalyst in the reactor supplied with                hydrogen and number of moles of metal in the                catalyst in the reactor to which hydrogen is                supplied. The reactor is provided with an outlet                (14) for taking out gas inside the reactor, and an                outlet concentration measuring unit for measuring                the carbon dioxide concentration at the outlet of                the reactor. The control unit controls the source                gas supply unit to stop the supply of the source                gas when a measurement value by the outlet                concentration measurement unit is larger than a                predetermined value of 20-400 ppm. The source gas                supply unit switches the supply destination of the                source gas. The control unit controls the source                gas supply unit. The supply destination of the                source gas is switched to another reactor, when a                predetermined amount of the source gas is supplied                to a specific reactor among several reactors. A                hydrogen separation unit (80) separates hydrogen                from the reaction gas mixture taken out from the                reactor. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a control method of methane production                apparatus; and
   (2) a methane production method involves                supplying a source gas containing carbon dioxide                and nitrogen oxide. The catalyst is caused to store                carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide. The supply of                the source gas to the reactor is stopped. Hydrogen                in the number of moles satisfying the predetermined                formula is supplied to the reactor supplied with                the source gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                methane production apparatus. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)

PN JP2018202274-A; JP6882076-B2
TI System for collecting carbon dioxide, has effluent-segregation portion that supplies production liquid as drainage liquid with impurities reduced by effluent-segregation membrane as diffusion suppression liquid.
AU MONIWA S
   UTATSU M
   NAGANO K
   KATO Y
   HODOTSUKA M
   KITAMURA H
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS KK (TOKE-C)
GA 2019004781
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an absorption portion (11) for absorbing carbon dioxide in the combustion exhaust gas (1) into the absorption liquid. A reproducing portion (15) releases the carbon dioxide from the absorption liquid. A diffusion suppressing portion (12) collects the absorption liquid accompanying the absorption portion exhaust gas with a diffusion suppressing liquid. A diffusion suppressing liquid holding portion supplies the diffusion suppressing liquid to the diffusion suppressing portion, collects the diffusion suppressing liquid from the diffusion suppressing portion, and discharges the diffusion suppressing liquid as a drainage liquid. An effluent-segregation portion (27) is provided with an effluent-segregation membrane (27a) that separates impurities derived from the absorption liquid from the drainage liquid, and supplies the production liquid as the drainage liquid with the impurities reduced by the effluent-segregation membrane as the diffusion suppression liquid.
   USE - System for collecting carbon dioxide from exhaust gas discharged from combustion system such as thermal power plant, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The system efficiently uses the diffusion suppressing liquid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system comprises a drainage liquid filtration unit (25) that removes solid substance in the drainage liquid by filtration and supplies the drainage liquid from which the solid substance is removed to the effluent-segregation portion. A disinfection unit (26) disinfects the drainage liquid and supplies the disinfected drainage liquid to the effluent-segregation portion. The effluent-segregation portion is configured to discharge the concentrated liquid obtained by concentrating the impurities without passing the drainage liquid through the effluent-segregation membrane. A heating purification unit (28) purifies the concentrated liquid by heat treatment. Several measuring devices measure physical quantities of one of drainage liquid, production liquid, and concentrated liquid. A control unit controls processing of drainage liquid based on the physical quantity measured by the measuring devices. An acidic gas supply unit supplies an acidic gas to the waste liquid and supplies the drainage liquid to which the acidic gas is supplied to the effluent-segregation portion. A gas purification unit (17) removes a predetermined component from the reproducing unit exhaust gas discharged from the reproducing unit. A drainage liquid supply flow path and supplies the purification portion drainage liquid which is discharged from the gas purification unit and contains the predetermined component to the effluent-segregation portion. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for collecting carbon dioxide involves absorbing carbon dioxide in the combustion exhaust gas into the absorption liquid by an absorption portion. The carbon dioxide is released from the absorption liquid that absorbed the carbon dioxide by a reproducing portion. The absorption liquid accompanying the absorption portion exhaust gas discharged from the absorbing portion with a diffusion suppressing liquid is collected by a diffusion suppressing portion. The diffusion suppressing liquid is supplied to the diffusion suppressing portion, the diffusion suppressing liquid is collected from the diffusion suppressing portion, and the diffusion suppressing liquid is discharged as a drainage liquid by a diffusion suppressing liquid holding portion. The impurities derived from the absorption liquid are separated from the drainage liquid by an effluent-segregation membrane. The production liquid is supplied as the drainage liquid with the impurities reduced by the effluent-segregation membrane as the diffusion suppression liquid by an effluent-segregation portion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for collecting carbon dioxide.

   Effluent-segregation membrane (27a)
   Heating purification unit (28)

AU CHEN C
   YANG H

   LI Y
   ZENG W
   DING K
   WANG K
   LIU Y
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 201895496E
AB    NOVELTY - Production of cyclopentanone comprises                subjecting furfural and hydrogen to heterogeneous                hydrogenation under the action of carbon dioxide                and catalyst.
   USE - Production of cyclopentanone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved cyclopentanone                selectivity, avoids attachment of furfural and/or                furfuryl alcohol polymer to the surface of the                catalyst, and prolongs the service life of the                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst comprises                  0.1-0.6 wt.%, preferably 0.3-0.5 wt.% main active                  component, 0.01-0.1 wt.%, preferably 0.05-0.075                  wt.% auxiliary catalytic component and a carrier.                  The main active component is gold (Au), palladium                  (Pd), platinum (Pt) and/or ruthenium (Ru). The                  auxiliary catalytic component is nickel (Ni),                  cobalt (Co), iron (Fe) and/or copper (Cu). The                  carrier is titanium dioxide (TiO2), alumina                  (Al2O3), silicon dioxide (SiO2), and/or molecular                  sieve. The catalyst is Au-Cu/SiO2, Pt-Fe/TiO2,                  Pt-Co/Al2O3, Ru-Cu/molecular sieve, and/or                  preferably Au-Ni/TiO2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The reaction is                  conducted at 120-160 degrees C, preferably                  130-150 degrees C and 1-5 MPa, preferably 2-4                  MPa. The furfural concentration in the reaction                  system is not greater than 0.01 wt.%. The                  furfural feed rate is not greater than 4 g                  furfural/(g catalyst.hour).

TI Sorbent polymer composite article used to receive            and adsorb carbon dioxide, comprises first region            having solid sorbent and first porous polymer having            pores, and portion of solid sorbent immobilized within            pores of first porous polymer.
AU CULLY E H
   SCOTTI C M
AE GORE & ASSOC INC W L (GORE-C)
GA 2022B5914D
AB    NOVELTY - Sorbent polymer composite article (A1) (20)                comprises a first region (28) having a solid                sorbent (24) and a first porous polymer (22)                comprising many pores (32), where the first region                has at least a portion of the solid sorbent                immobilized within at least some of the pores of                the first porous polymer, and the first region is                configured to receive carbon dioxide through the                first porous polymer and adsorb the carbon dioxide                onto the solid sorbent.
   USE - The articles are useful for receiving carbon                dioxide through the first porous polymer and                adsorbing the carbon dioxide onto the solid sorbent                (claimed) and absorbing direct air capture (DAC) of                carbon dioxide from a dilute feed stream, e.g. air,                used in other adsorbent methods and                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The articles have retained solids are solid                sorbent materials, which enables the article to                adsorb and separate at least one desired substance                from a source stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   combining a solid sorbent and a first porous                polymer comprising providing a first porous polymer                having the pores, providing a solid sorbent,                combining the sorbent and the first porous polymer                so that a portion of the sorbent is disposed within                the pores of the first porous polymer, and                immobilizing the solid sorbent within the pores of                the first porous polymer; and
   entrained polymer composite article (A2)                comprising a first porous polymer comprising many                nodes, many fibrils that connect adjacent nodes,                and the pores defined by the nodes and the fibrils,                where the first porous polymer has a first state in                which the fibrils are substantially straight, and a                second state in which the fibrils are substantially                wavy or bent and the pores are smaller in size than                in the first state, and many solid particles are                retained in the pores in the first state and                immobilized in the pores in the second state.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates elevational view of                sorbent polymer composite article.
   20Sorbent polymer composite article
   22First porous polymer
   24Sorbent material
   28First composite region
   32Pores
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The combining step                  includes: delivering a slurry comprised of the                  sorbent to the first porous polymer; and applying                  the sorbent in a dry particle form to the first                  porous polymer under a forced air flow. The                  immobilizing step further comprises: applying a                  solvent to the combination of the first porous                  polymer and the sorbent; and evaporating the                  solvent; applying heat to the first porous                  polymer and the sorbent; and attaching a second                  region to the first porous polymer having the                  sorbent material. The evaporating step shrinks a                  pore size of the pores of the first porous                  polymer. The applying the heat to the first                  porous polymer and the sorbent shrinks a pore                  size of the pores of the first porous polymer.                  The process of attaching includes laminating the                  second region to the first porous polymer having                  the sorbent material. Preferred Components: The                  solid sorbent is an ion exchange resin, zeolite,                  activated carbon, alumina, metal-organic                  frameworks, or polyethyleneimine. The article                  further comprises a second region having a second                  porous polymer and a third region having a third                  porous polymer, where the second porous polymer                  of the second region and the third porous polymer                  third region is hydrophobic. The first porous                  polymer, the second porous polymer, and the third                  porous polymer are the same. The first region has                  a thickness that is less than 5 mm. The first                  porous polymer of the first region is expanded                  PTFE, PTFE, or expanded polyethylene. The tensile                  strength of the first porous polymer after the                  solid sorbent is immobilized within some of the                  pores of the first porous polymer remains                  approximately equal to an original tensile                  strength of the first porous polymer before the                  solid sorbent is immobilized within some of the                  pores of the first porous polymer. The second                  region is arranged on a first side of the first                  region and the third region is arranged on a                  second side of the first region. The second and                  third porous polymer of the second region and the                  third region, respectively, are one of at least                  PTFE, expanded PTFE and expanded polyethylene.                  The slurry further comprises a carrier. The solid                  particles comprise: a carrier; and a sorbent                  material covering the carrier. The solid                  particles have an average particle size of                  0.1-100 &#956; m. The article (A2) further                  comprises: a second porous polymer region; a                  third porous polymer region, where the first                  porous polymer is sandwiched between the second                  and third porous polymer regions; and an                  end-sealing region that extends between the                  second and third porous polymer regions, where                  the second porous polymer region, the third                  porous polymer region, and the end-sealing region                  cooperate to protect the solid particles in the                  first porous polymer.

PN CN114991897-A
TI Multi-stage expansion liquid carbon dioxide            mixture energy storage system, has condenser whose hot            side outlet is communicated with inlet of            low-temperature liquid mixture storage tank.
AU ZHANG X
   QIAO Y
   WU S
   HAN W
   LI K
   GAO W
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2022C0021B
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a low-temperature liquid                mixture storage tank (1) whose outlet is connected                with a cold side inlet of an evaporator (2). A cold                side outlet of the evaporator communicates with an                inlet end of a compressor (3). An outlet end of the                compressor communicates with a high temperature                mixture storage pool (4). A hot side of a condenser                (6) communicates with the low temperature liquid                mixture tank. The outlet of a low temperature heat                dissipation tank (1) is provided with a water inlet                and a water outlet, where the water inlets are                connected with each other. The water outlet is                fixed on the lower side of the water storage                tank.
   USE - Multi-stage expansion liquid carbon dioxide                mixture energy storage system for use in solar                photo-thermal power generation field. Can also be                used in wind power and solar photovoltaic power                generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The system adopts the compressed liquid carbon                dioxide mixture energy storage technology, so that                the mixture near the ambient temperature can be                cooled and condensed into liquid, without cooling                at low temperature, thus storing the                low-temperature and low-pressure carbon dioxide                mixture in a liquid form with large density, and                the storage space is small. The working medium                pressure in the high temperature mixture storage                tank of the energy storage system is reduced along                with the time, thus the system uses the design of                multi-stage turbine grading expansion, when the                system pressure is gradually reduced, the turbine                is in the sliding pressure running state, and when                the pressure is reduced to a certain range, closing                the high pressure channel, turn on low pressure                channel and only operating the low pressure                turbine, stopping the high-pressure turbine, which                can greatly improve the turbine efficient operation                range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                pressure adjusting method of multi-stage expansion                liquid carbon dioxide mixture energy storage                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic flow diagram of                the multi-stage expansion liquid carbon dioxide                mixture energy storage system.
   1Low-temperature liquid mixture storage                tank
   2Evaporator
   3Compressor
   4High temperature mixture storage pool
   6Hot side of a condenser

AB    NOVELTY - The chip has multiple sensing elements located                on a square base body and provided with two heat                measuring elements as breath flow and flow                direction sensing element and a heat conducting                element as a carbon dioxide sensing element. A portion of                the carbon dioxide sensing element is covered with                an inert coating. Another portion of the carbon                dioxide sensing element is extended in air, where                the sensing elements respectively transmits                collected signal to a receiving terminal through a                predetermined signal path. A chip layer is provided                with multiple signal wires. A chip main body is                fixed in a sampling tube, where the sampling tube                is made of Nafion material.

   ADVANTAGE - The chip is convenient to process, and has                small volume and high sensitivity, and monitors                short distance of respiration situation in real                time, changes temperature distribution of a chip                surface by respiratory air flow, locates the four                heat measuring elements at the chip surface in a                specific air flow passage, utilizes average                temperature difference and corresponding position                to determine positive direction and reverse                direction of the air flow, provides a quantity heat                sensor with a micro processing thermal stack, so as                to realize passive monitoring, and monitors a                thermal conductivity change condition of the air                flow by the heat conducting element located at                center of the chip, so as to obtain carbon dioxide                content in the respiratory air flow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for manufacturing a MEMS                respiration monitoring sensing chip; and

the lower part of the inner part of the box body is provided with a fire extinguishing component, the fire extinguishing component comprises a carbon dioxide storage tank, the air outlet end of the carbon dioxide storage tank is provided with a pulse electromagnetic valve, the air outlet end of the pulse electromagnetic valve fixedly connected with main pipe, the air outlet end of the main pipe fixedly connected with branch pipe; the top end of the branch pipe fixedly connected with with a spray head; by setting the fireproof isolation plate, the fireproof isolation plate divides the box body into two spaces, two spaces are used independently, then by setting two monitoring components and a fire extinguishing component, using the monitoring component to monitor the smoke information, using the fire extinguishing component to extinguish the fire in the space, the fire in the single space can be extinguished separately, at the same time, the fire extinguishing component adopts carbon dioxide fire extinguishing method, carbon dioxide can quickly isolate oxygen, the purpose of extinguishing fire is reached, in addition, when the liquid carbon dioxide is sprayed and gasified, it will absorb a large amount of heat, further realizing the purpose of extinguishing and protecting the internal device, at the same time, the monitoring component is set above, the fire extinguishing component is set on the lower part, when extinguishing, the exhaust fan on the monitoring component can quickly pump the lower carbon dioxide to the upper part of the box body, so as to accelerate the speed of extinguishing the fire.

TI Method for abating hydrogen sulfide and carbon            dioxide from non-condensable gas (NCG) mixture, by            producing water stream enriched with dissolved hydrogen            sulfide and carbon dioxide to aid in pH-regulation of            water stream in reservoir.
AU SIGFUSSON B
   ARNARSON M P
   GUNNARSSON I
   GUNNARSSON T
   EINARSSON J G
   SIGFESSON B
   ARNARSON M I
   ZIPUSSONBERGUR
   MAGNUSPO A
   YINGBEE G

   JOHANGARDAR E

AE ORKUVEITA REYKJAVIKUR (ORKU-Non-standard)
   CARBFIX (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBFIX (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBFIX (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBFIX (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202130931J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pressurizing the NCG                mixture, encompassing hydrogen sulfide (H2S) and                carbon dioxide (CO2) and at least also one of H2,                N2, CH4 and/or argon, to a pressure between 3 and                20 bar, and contacting a stream of the pressurized                NCG mixture with a stream of water. The method also                involves the absorption of at least a portion of                the H2S and CO2 from the pressurized NCG mixture,                thus separating the at least a portion of the H2S                and CO2 from at least a portion of the at least one                of H2, N2, CH4 and/or argon and producing a stream                of water enriched with dissolved H2S and CO2                comparable to the water stream and a stream of                pressurized gas, which has been depleted of H2S and                CO2 compared to the NCG mixture.
   USE - Method for abating hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide from non-condensable gas (NCG) mixture,                such as a geothermal NCG mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method meets the need for an                environmentally friendly method for separating,                capturing and preparing H2S and CO2 from NCG for                later storage, or for later use relying solely on                the absorption of the relatively soluble CO2 and                H2S gas from the NCG mixture into the liquid steam                condensate (or any other water stream) without                relying on e.g. the addition of chlorine for                oxidizing the sulfur to a higher oxidation state                and at the same time leaving the relatively poorly                soluble H2, N2 and CH4 gasses in the NCG                mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The water stream enriched with dissolved H2S                and CO2 is transferred to an injection well for                injecting the water stream into a geological                reservoir, or to a system for injection of a water                stream into a geological reservoir for use of the                water stream as an aid for pH-regulation of the                water stream. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included                for a system for abating hydrogen sulfide and                carbon dioxide from a non-condensable gas                mixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic diagram which                shows a system for abating hydrogen sulfide and                carbon dioxide from a non-condensable gas                mixture.

PT P
PN EP3284827-A1; WO2018034565-A1
TI Using gaseous feed stream comprising carbon dioxide as feed stream for growing algae, by contacting gaseous feed stream with absorption liquid comprising carbonic anhydrase, and contacting obtained liquid feed stream with algae culture.
AU GOETHEER E L V
   KOENST P M
   HERNANDEZ MIRELES I
   VAN OS P
   VAN DER STEL R W
   KONST P M
AE NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
GA 201813369R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for using a gaseous feed stream comprising carbon dioxide as a feed stream for growing algae, involves (a) contacting the gaseous feed stream with an absorption liquid comprising carbonic anhydrase in an absorption module to produce a purified gas stream and a liquid feed stream comprising bicarbonate, (b) contacting portion of the liquid feed stream and/or the gaseous feed stream comprising carbon dioxide with microorganisms to generate carbonic anhydrase, and (c) contacting portion of the liquid feed stream with an algae culture to produce algae-containing biomass.
   USE - The method is useful for using a gaseous feed stream comprising carbon dioxide as a feed stream for growing algae. The apparatus is useful for sequestrating carbon dioxide (claimed), where algae is used for biomass production. The method is useful for converting the bicarbonate into valuable compounds such as beta -carotene, anti-oxidants, lipids and fatty acids by the algae. The valuable compounds are used in applications such as biofuel and cosmetics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, reduces the necessity for further carbonic anhydrase addition, and increases product yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for sequestration of carbon dioxide using algae, by the above-mentioned method, which comprises (i) a first fluid circulation system comprising an absorption module comprises a gas inlet, and downstream of the absorption module, a first bioreactor comprising a filter adapted to retain microorganisms in the first bioreactor, and (ii) a second fluid circulation system, that is connected to the first circulation system and extents from the absorption modules or downstream of the absorption module and upstream of the first bioreactor to upstream of the absorption module or to the first circulation system downstream of the first bioreactor and upstream of the absorption module, where the second fluid circulation system comprises a second bioreactor that is adapted to culture algae, and the second bioreactor comprises an outlet comprising a filter adapted to enable a liquid outflow while retaining the algae in the second bioreactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further involves oxidation of nitrogen oxide with a catalyst to form an oxidized gaseous feed stream comprising nitrogen oxide and carbon dioxide that is contacted with the absorption liquid in step (a). The step (b) further involves producing a stream of carbonic anhydrase that is fed to the absorption module, where the steps (b) and (c) are performed in separate bioreactors, i.e. step (b) is performed in a first bioreactor and the step (c) is performed in a second bioreactor. The pH of the absorption liquid is controlled by adding a basic liquid stream that is additionally produced in step (c). The pH of the algae culture is controlled by the outflow of the basic liquid stream. The step (c) is performed in parallel bioreactors comprising algae. The method involves adding additional water and nutrients to the liquid feed stream before contacting with the algae. Preferred Components: The gaseous feed stream further comprises nitrogen oxide and the liquid feed stream further comprises nitrite and nitrate. The gaseous feed comprises flue gas, preferably flue gas originating from an electrical power plant. Preferred Apparatus: The apparatus further comprises an oxidation module comprising a catalyst module adapted for oxidizing nitrogen oxide connected to the gas inlet of the absorption module through a gas transport system such as a tube. Preferred Microorganism: The microorganism comprises cyanobacteria and/or algae.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. TI Liquid trans-critical carbon dioxide energy            storage system, has liquid storage tank whose outlet is            connected to inlet of high pressure air storage tank            through valve.
AU ZHANG D
   SUN L
   TANG B
   XIE Y
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)

GA 202263527W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a liquid storage tank stored                with liquid carbon dioxide. An outlet of the liquid                storage tank is connected to an inlet of a high                pressure air storage tank (9) through a first valve                (101), an evaporation assembly and an energy                storage assembly. An outlet of the high pressure                air storage tank is connected to an inlet of an air                storage tank through a second valve (102), an                energy releasing assembly and a condensing                assembly.
   USE - Liquid trans-critical carbon dioxide energy                storage system for use in solar power generation                stable grid-connected operation of energy storage                technology. Uses include but are not limited to                terrestrial heat, solar energy and industrial waste                heat.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has compact structure, rational                design, and fully utilizes heat energy and cold                energy for reducing scale of the storage system,                increases flexibility of the system, thus utilizing                different types of heat source, such as terrestrial                heat, solar energy and industrial waste heat and                other enhanced system power generation performance.                The system has easy phase change, effectively                reducing energy storage stage system power                consumption, so as to increase system output power,                and effectively improve compactness and flexibility                of the system. The system adopts simple and                effective way to liquefy and gasify the carbon                dioxide, so that the low pressure carbon dioxide is                stored in the liquid storage tank in liquid                form.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a liquid                trans-critical carbon dioxide energy storage                method.

TI Energy-saving carbon dioxide recycling device, has            low-temperature washing tower whose inner portion is            equipped with sleeve, where inner portion of sleeve is            provided with solid adsorbent filter plate that is made            of carbon-based carrier.
AU QIU G
AE NANJING YITAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202252563X
AB    NOVELTY - The energy-saving carbon dioxide recycling                device has a base (1) whose top portion is provided                with a low-temperature washing tower (2). A top                portion of the base is connected with a foam                removing washing tower (3). A top end of a                compressor (4) is connected to the base. A bottom                portion of a shell (8) and a first liquefying and                purifying device are connected through a pipeline.                Two sides of the low temperature washing tower are                connected with circulating pumps (10) and shielding                pumps (11) on both sides. A sleeve is arranged                inside the foam removing and washing tower, and a                solid adsorbent filter plate is provided inside the                sleeve. A carbon-based carrier is made of the solid                adsorption filter plate.
   USE - Energy-saving carbon dioxide recycling                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has small floor area, simple                process flow, low energy consumption index, low                cost, solves problem of high cost of the existing                carbon dioxide recovery device, realizes chemical                and physical characteristics of various toxic and                harmful substances in the carbon dioxide gas                according to alcohol fermentation, removes volatile                organic matter from chemical reaction according to                mol weight of the substance, removes heavy, light,                according to the principle of micro-solution and                mutual solubility of different substances,                condensing and degrading, and boiling point                principle of the different substances to realize                new method for producing food-grade carbon dioxide                without adsorption dry the system, and saves large                amount of washing water.

PN CN113803038-A; CN113803038-B
TI Simulation device of shale oil pyrolysis and            spitting integration, comprises reaction chamber for            containing shale oil core, where reaction chamber is            provided with first and second air inlet and            outlet.
AU WAN T
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 202202855E
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide air inlet                assembly connected with a first gas inlet. The                carbon dioxide inlet component is provided with a                carbon inlet pipeline and a first heating                component. A first pressurizing component is                arranged on the carbon dioxide gas inlets. A water                vapor intake component is connected with the second                gas intake pipeline. A second pressurization                component is set on a second gas injection                pipeline. The second gas infusion pipeline is                arranged with a second valve at an upstream side of                the second air injection pipeline, and a product                collection component is in communication with an                outlet.
   USE - Used as simulation device of shale oil                pyrolysis and spitting integration.
   ADVANTAGE - The shale oil is pyrolyzed by carbon dioxide                and steam after heating and pressurizing, and it is                swallowed, and the pyropyrolysis and huff                integration of the shale oil can be realized. The                device can realize the integration of huff and puff                integration of shale oil without using the                underground igniter.    CHEN P
   DONG K
   JIANG J
   ZHU R
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 2021B77897
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide that is used as supplementary gas for pulverized coal silo, fluidized gas from coal injection tank. A mixture of carbon dioxide, oxygen and air is used as the pulverized coal conveying gas to control the oxygen content of the conveying gas. The oxygen equivalent of the conveying gas is increased, and a mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen is used as the joint gas of the coal injection gun. The oxidative and ablation effect of the annular joint gas on the coal injection gun is reduced. The operation in different modes increases the pulverized coal burn-out rate and improves the pulverized coal combustion effect depending on the amount of coal injection. The newly added carbon dioxide gas source requires a flow rate that ensures the use of carbon dioxide gas as supplementary gas for pulverized coal silo, fluidized pressurized gas of coal injection tank, pulverized coal carrier gas and annular joint gas at the same time.
   USE - Coal injection system for blast furnace iron-making.
   ADVANTAGE - The original annular joint gas oxygen gas source requires a flow rate that ensures an increase of 150Nm3/h in the oxygen flow rate of a single coal injection gun. The system increases the oxygen equivalent of the conveying gas and ensures that the oxygen content in the conveying gas is within the safe range.

PT P
PN CN108067180-A; CN108067180-B
TI Magnesium-calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent useful for adsorption and separation of carbon dioxide generated in chemical chain reforming enhanced hydrogen production process, comprises calcium oxide, yttrium oxide and magnesium oxide.
AU QIN C
   HE D
   RAN J
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 201842365K
AB    NOVELTY - Magnesium-calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises 40-80 wt.% calcium oxide, yttrium (III) oxide and magnesium oxide (balance), where the content difference between yttrium (III) oxide and magnesium oxide is not greater than 5%.
   USE - The magnesium-calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent is useful for adsorption and separation of carbon dioxide generated in chemical chain reforming enhanced hydrogen production process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has high adsorption efficacy and good circulation stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing magnesium-calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprising (1) dropwise adding soluble yttrium salt solution and soluble magnesium salt solution into soluble calcium salt solution, stirring to mix uniformly, where the soluble yttrium salt solution, soluble magnesium salt and soluble calcium salt solution concentration is same, i.e. 2.4-2.6 mol/l, and dropwise adding metahydrate citric acid solution into the mixed solution, (2) dropwise adding mixed solution of step (1) to monohydrate citric acid solution, stirring at 80~90 degrees C for 5-7 hours until a gel is formed, where the concentration of the citric acid monohydrate solution is 4-6 mol/l, the amount of citric acid monohydrate solution is determined based on the molar amount of metal cation/molar amount of citric acid monohydrate is 1.6-1.8, (3) placing the gel obtained in the step (2) in air at room temperature for 20-24 hours, heating at 80-100 degrees C for 6-7 hours, heating at 105-115 degrees C for 40-48 hours, until dry gel forms, (4) placing dry gel in muffle furnace, calcining at 800-900 degrees C for 1-2 hours until the organic matter is fully combusted and the nitrate is completely decomposed, and (5) grinding and sieving the burnt particles to obtain the magnesium-calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The magnesium-calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises 60 wt.% calcium oxide, 20 wt.% yttrium (III) oxide and magnesium oxide. In the step (1), the soluble yttrium salt solution, soluble magnesium salt and soluble calcium salt solution concentration is 2.5 mol/l. In the step (2), the concentration of the citric acid monohydrate solution is 5 mol/l.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier is PVP-K30. The mass ratio of indapamide to carrier is 1:2-1:4. In the step (a), the mass concentration of the indapamide-carrier mixed solution is 3-11 mg/ml.Preferred Method: In the step (b), maintaining the crystallization kettle at 35-65degreesC under 9-21 Mpa. In the step (c), the flow rate at which the solution is sprayed into the crystallization kettle is 0.4-1.4 ml/minute. In the step (d), the flow rate of continuously feeding carbon dioxide is 3plus minus0.2 l/minute and carbon dioxide is introduced for 100-150 minutes.

PN WO2021252166-A1; US2021389391-A1
TI Method for subsurface reservoir monitoring, involves generating vertical current flow penetrating reservoir and corresponding time-domain magnetic B-field response to fluids in rock formation at vertical electric bipole source.
AU ZHDANOV M S
   COX L H
   GRIBENKO A
   BAN H
   CHIBA H
AE TECHNOIMAGING LLC (TECH-Non-standard)
   JAPAN OIL GAS & METALS NAT CORP (NIOI-C)
   JAPAN OIL GAS & METALS NAT CORP (NIOI-C)
   TECHNOIMAGING LLC (TECH-Non-standard)
GA 2021E3540G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating vertical current flow penetrating a reservoir (5) and a corresponding time-domain magnetic B-field response to fluids in a rock formation at a vertical electric bipole source. Volume images of electromagnetic (EM) parameters of the rock formation are determined using a three-dimensional EM inversion technique applied to the acquired time- domain B- field data. The changes of the volume images are correlated with known geological formations for subsurface reservoir monitoring. The reservoir is formed by hydrocarbon bearing rocks, geothermal resources bearing formations and carbon dioxide capture and storage.
   USE - Method for subsurface reservoir imaging and monitoring using surface and borehole geophysical data for hydrocarbon bearing reservoirs. Can also be used for monitoring carbon dioxide injections in deep reservoir in carbon capture and storage (CCS) projects or geothermal field study.
   ADVANTAGE - The presence of low salinity aquifers further reduces the resistivity contrast, and thus limits the applicability of conventional induction coils magnetic or electric receivers. The volume of the reservoir rocks penetrated by the injected current may be characterized by significant anomalies in the distribution of the magnetic field measured by the ground or borehole-based array of magnetic SQUID sensors at different time moments after the electric source pulse.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a reservoir monitoring system for measuring the magnetic B(t) field response from the reservoir rocks using an actual vertical electric current source.
   First electrode (1)
   Second electrode (2)
   Magnetic field receivers (3)
   Borehole (4)
   Reservoir (5)

PN CN113754410-A
TI Preparation of low-carbon microporous            magnesia-carbon brick used in refining, involves adding            binder to raw material comprising specified amount of            recycled magnesia-carbon brick, nanocarbon black,            magnesium lactate and aluminum titanate, mixing            uniformly, and press-molding under preset            conditions.
AU WEI Z
   XU N
   WU Q
AE JIANGSU JINNAI NEW MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202202493J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of low-carbon microporous                magnesia-carbon brick involves adding 2.2-3.4 wt.%                of binder to raw material comprising 96-98 wt.%                recycled magnesia-carbon brick, 0.9-1.7 wt.%                nanocarbon black, 0.7-1.1 wt.% magnesium lactate                and 0.4-1.2 wt.% aluminum titanate, mixing                uniformly, and press-molding the resulting mixture                at 160-180degreesC for 5-9 hours under the                condition of 15-20 MPa.
   USE - Preparation of low-carbon microporous                magnesia-carbon brick (claimed) used in refining                and continuous casting of molten steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method environmental-friendly provides                low-carbon microporous magnesia-carbon brick having                high mechanical strength, low thermal conductivity,                and long service life with reduced energy                consumption, by a simple and economical process.                The magnesium lactate used in the preparation of                brick decomposes to produce carbon dioxide in a                high-temperature environment, and the carbon                dioxide atmosphere in the matrix structure prevents                the nanocarbon black from oxidizing and failing,                thereby improving the thermal shock resistance of                magnesia carbon bricks. The magnesium oxide                obtained by dehydroxylation of the periclase                effectively inhibits the decomposition of aluminum                titanate and optimize the stability of brick during                high-temperature use. The magnesia-carbon brick has                compressive strength of 35.5-42 MPa, apparent                porosity of 3.3-4.4% and thermal conductivity of                3.22-3.55 W/(m.K).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The recycled                  magnesia-carbon brick comprises 85 wt.% or more                  magnesium oxide, 1.1 wt.% or less calcium oxide,                  1.5 wt.% or less silicon dioxide, 0.7 wt.% or                  less ferric oxide, and 3.9 wt.% or less lime. The                  content of carbon in nanocarbon black is 97.9                  wt.% or more. The content of magnesium lactate is                  98 wt.% or more. The aluminum titanate comprises                  49.1 wt.% or more aluminum oxide and 47.5 wt.% or                  more titanium dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The binder is                  polymethylmethacrylate, polycarbonate and/or                  propylene oxide.

AU ZHU H
   ZHANG G
   NI D
AE YUNNAN ZHIKONG ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2022658846
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an integrated device of intelligent security system, relating to the technical field of security device, comprising a box body, a fire extinguishing mechanism and a sealing mechanism; one side of the outer wall of the box body is provided with a box door; the outer wall of the box door is provided with a handle; the top end of the box body is provided with a controller. The utility model is provided with a smoke sensor, a controller, an electromagnet, a connecting spring; a sliding rod and a sealing film; when the fire hazard occurs in the box body, the smoke sensor senses the smoke in the box body, and transmits the signal to the controller; the controller processes the signal to power off the electromagnet; the electromagnet releases the iron block; under the elastic contraction action of the connecting spring, drives the spring plate to move upward and reset; so as to pull the sliding rod to move upwards and bursting the sealing film; the carbon dioxide gas in the air storage tank is conveyed into the box body through the vertical air pipe and the transverse air pipe, increasing the proportion of carbon dioxide gas in the box body and reducing the proportion of oxygen, so as to reach the purpose of extinguishing fire.

PN CN113648343-A
TI Medicine useful for treating chronic urticaria, prepared by cleaning fresh mint, spraying sodium glycerophosphate solution on surface, freeze-drying crushed mint, adding extracting agent, filtering, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and adjusting ratio of mint extract and solvent.
AU LI W
AE LI W (LIWW-Individual)
GA 2021D6417G
AB    NOVELTY - Medicine comprises 70-90% mint extract and 10-30% solvent.
   USE - The medicine is useful for treating chronic urticaria and relieving itching.
   ADVANTAGE - The medicine has an excellent antipruritic effect on chronic urticaria, can quickly relieve the discomfort of patients, and mint extract is a natural irritant substance that can be used for a long time without adverse side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing medicine, comprising (a) selecting fresh mint, cleaning, and drying the surface water naturally; (b) selecting the mint which is naturally dried, and spraying a sodium glycerophosphate solution on the surface of the mint, air drying for 1-2 hours, adding cellulase solution, and crushing the mint; (c) freeze-drying the crushed mint, adding an extracting agent for extraction, separating, filtering, and concentrating to obtain extract A and mint residue; (d) adding grinding agent and cellulase solution into the mint residue, and grinding the mint residue until the volume of the mint residue is reduced to 20-50% of the original volume, adding ethanol aqueous solution for extraction, filtering under reduced pressure, and concentrating the filtrate to obtain extract B; (e) combining extract A and extract B, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain a mint extract; and (f) adding a solvent into the mint extract and adjusting the ratio of the mint extract and the solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The mass fraction of the mint extract is 80-90%. The mint extract is menthol. The mint extract is L-menthol. The solvent is water or ethanol. The volume fraction of the sodium glycerophosphate solution is 0.1-1% and the mass ratio of the sprayed amount of the sodium glycerophosphate solution and the mint is (0.5-1):1. The mass fraction of the cellulase solution is 1-5% and the mass ratio of the cellulase solution and the mint is 1:(15-20).Preferred Method: In the step (c), the freeze-drying process includes freezing the mint at -10degrees Celsius to 0degrees Celsius for 2-3 hours, heating to room temperature at a heating rate of 1-5degrees Celsius/minute, freezing the treated mint at -20degrees Celsius to -10degrees Celsius for 2-3 hours at a heating rate of 1-5degrees Celsius/minute, heating to 1-5degrees Celsius, refrigerating for 0.5-1 hours, and heating to room temperature at a heating rate of 0.5-1degrees Celsius/minute. The extraction includes adding ethanol aqueous solution and dilute hydrochloric acid into the mint, adjusting the pH of the solution system to 3-4, refluxing and extracting for 1-2 hours, where the temperature of reflux extraction is 40-50degrees Celsius, and the volume fraction of the ethanol aqueous solution is 75%.
   ACTIVITY - Antiallergic; Dermatological; Antipruritic.

AB    NOVELTY - The energy circulation system (10) comprises a pure oxygen circulating fluidized bed combustion device (100) of a dry exhaust gas recirculation method that burns fuel to generate electricity. A renewable energy unit (12) produces electricity through renewable energy. A water electrolysis unit (14) separates water into hydrogen and oxygen by using the electricity produced through the renewable energy unit for water electrolysis. A pure oxygen supply unit (15) receives pure oxygen from the water electrolysis unit or the outside and supply it to the pure oxygen circulating fluidized bed combustion device. A methanation unit (16) combines carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide produced through the pure oxygen circulating fluidized bed combustion device with hydrogen separated through the water electrolysis unit to produce methane.

PT P
PN US2021291124-A1
TI Forming additive body for mixing with            supersaturated body to form beverage with regulated            nucleation, comprises determining pH level of            supersaturated body, adjusting pH level of additive            body and adjusting viscosity of additive body.
AU MONTOYA K
AE MONTOYA K (MONT-Individual)
GA 2021B0090C
AB    NOVELTY - Forming an additive body for mixing with a                supersaturated body to form a beverage with                regulated nucleation, comprises: (A) providing a                supersaturated body; (B) determining the pH level                of the supersaturated body; (C) providing an                additive body; (D) adjusting the pH level of the                additive body; and (E) adjusting the viscosity of                the additive body.
   USE - The method is useful for: forming an additive                body for mixing with a supersaturated body to form                a beverage with regulated nucleation (claimed);                controlling the nucleation of gas from                supersaturated liquids; controlling the nucleation                of carbonated bodies when combined with additive                bodies that may cause an undesirable amount of                nucleation; and controlling the foam created when                adding cocktail mixers to carbonated liquids e.g.                beer and other types of carbonated beverages.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: minimizes the carbon dioxide foam                that may result when mixing a carbonated body with                an additive body; slows the accumulation (the                nucleation) of the carbon dioxide at the                pre-existing nucleation sites NSE by increasing the                viscosity of the additive body; and slows the                nucleation process by adding an anti-caking agent                (e.g. silicon dioxide) to the additive body (e.g.                to a dry ingredient additive body) to encapsulate                pre-existing nucleation sites NSE within the                additive body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                beverage mixer for controlling nucleation when                mixing with a supersaturated body to form a                beverage, comprising 59% water, 8.5% tomato paste,                25% lime juice, 4% vinegar, 0.2% maltodextrin, 0.6%                citric acid and 2.7% other ingredients.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pH level of the                  additive body is adjusted in (D): based on the pH                  level of the supersaturated body determined in                  (B); or to substantially match the pH level of                  the supersaturated body determined in (B). The pH                  level of the supersaturated body determined in                  (B) is 2.5-5 and the pH level of the additive                  body is adjusted in (D) to be 2.5-5. The pH level                  of the supersaturated body determined in (B) is                  3-4.2 and the pH level of the additive body is                  adjusted in (D) to be 3-4.2. The pH level of the                  additive body is adjusted in (D) to be                  3.11+/-0.2. The pH level of the additive body is                  adjusted in (D) by adding an ingredient to the                  additive body comprising acetic acid, adipic                  acid, ammonium aluminum sulfate, ammonium                  bicarbonate, ammonium citrate, ammonium                  hydroxide, ammonium phosphate, calcium acid                  pyrophosphate, calcium carbonate, calcium                  chloride, calcium citrate, calcium fumarate,                  calcium gluconate, calcium hydroxide, calcium                  lactate, calcium oxide, calcium phosphate,                  calcium sulfate, citric acid, cream of tartar,                  fumaric acid, gluconic acid,                  glucono-delta-lactone, hydrochloric acid, lactic                  acid, magnesium carbonate, magnesium fumarate,                  magnesium hydroxide, magnesium phosphate,                  magnesium sulfate, malic acid, manganese sulfate,                  metatartaric acid, phosphoric acid, potassium                  acid tartrate, potassium aluminum sulfate,                  potassium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate,                  potassium chloride, potassium citrate, potassium                  fumarate, potassium hydroxide, potassium lactate,                  potassium phosphate, potassium pyrophosphate,                  potassium sulfate, potassium tartrate, potassium                  tripolyphosphate, sodium acetate, sodium acid                  pyrophosphate, sodium acid pyrophosphate, sodium                  aluminum phosphate, sodium aluminum sulfate,                  sodium bicarbonate, sodium bisulfate, sodium                  carbonate, sodium citrate, sodium fumarate,                  sodium gluconate, sodium hexametaphosphate,                  sodium hydroxide, sodium lactate, sodium                  phosphate, sodium potassium hexametaphosphate,                  sodium potassium tartrate, sodium potassium                  tripolyphosphate, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid,                  or tartaric acid. The pH level of the additive                  body is adjusted in (D) by adding citric acid to                  the additive body in an amount of: greater than                  0.25% of the total volume of the additive body;                  or 0.6 g/118 ml of additive body. The viscosity                  of the additive body is adjusted in (E) by adding                  an ingredient to the additive body: comprising                  vegetable gum, starch, sugar, pectin, protein,                  glyceride, or synthetic; and in the amount of                  0.01-0.1% of the total volume of the additive                  body. The viscosity of the additive body is                  adjusted in (E) by: adding maltodextrin to the                  additive body in an amount of 0.2 g/118 ml of                  additive body; adding tomato paste to the                  additive body in an amount of 5-9% of the total                  volume of the additive body; and adding tomato                  paste to the additive body in an amount of 10                  g/118 ml of additive body.Preferred Components: The supersaturated                  body includes a carbonated body. The                  supersaturated body includes beer and the                  additive body includes Michelada mixer. The pH                  level of the beverage mixer is 2.5-5, preferably                  3-4.2. The pH level of the beverage mixer is                  3.11+/-0.2.
   EXAMPLE - Michelada beverage mixer was created using 59%                filtered water, 8.5% tomato paste, 2.5% lime juice,                white vinegar, 1.3% sea salt, 0.01-% dustless black                pepper, 0.1725% chili-lime seasoning, 0.1725%                maltodextrin, 0.3% monopotassium glutamate, 0.05%                yeast extract, 0.75% natural flavorings, 0.004%                Capsicum, 0.1% cayenne pepper, 0.55% citric acid,                and 0.05% smoked sweet paprika. Each of these                ingredients were chosen to do at least one of                nucleation site reduction, acidify, basify,                increase viscosity, and/or dissolve, unless                otherwise noted. The Michelada beverage mixer was                then added to a beer to form a Michelada beverage.                1 part Michelada beverage mixer was added to 3                parts beer (a 3:1 ratio of beer to Michelada mixer)                to form a Michelada beverage. 118 ml Michelada                mixer was added to 354 ml (12 ounces, or one                12-ounce beer) to form a Michelada beverage. 70.009                ml/118 ml Filtered water prevents the introduction                of additional nucleation sites by ensuring minerals                and particulates are removed before formulation. To                making up the majority of the mixture, filtered                water dissolves ingredients and was more basic than                any other ingredient. The other ingredients work to                acidify this disparity in pH level. 10.03 g/118 ml                Tomato paste was used to measure and control the                sodium content of the mixture and the resulting                nucleation as well as increase the viscosity of the                mixture. The tomato paste also works to acidify the                mixture and add flavor. 29.5 g/118 ml Lime Juice                serves as the primary flavoring for the mixture                while providing acidification to the solution.                4.425 g/118 ml White vinegar-12% commercial-grade                high-acidity concentration was chosen to acidify                the solution. This ingredient also amplifies the                beverage's tang without the need for more lime                juice and its accompanying nucleation causing                particulates. 1.534 g/118 ml Fine sea salt and                0.0118 g/118 ml dustless black pepper were used                because of the small size of the grind and the                resulting reduction of nucleation. Keeping the                coarse surface area of the ingredients to a minimum                reduces the size of nucleation sites relative to a                coarse grind which would act as a larger nucleation                site. 0.1725 g/118 ml Maltodextrin was used as a                thickening agent and increases the viscosity of the                solution, which leads to a delayed carbon dioxide                aggregation and the subsequent nucleation. 0.354                g/118 ml Monopotassium glutamate was non-sodium                alternative to monosodium glutamate. Monopotassium                glutamate was used to reduce sodium and associated                nucleation while also enhancing flavor. 0.0590                g/118 ml Yeast extract was an extract used for                flavoring, in lieu of monopotassium glutamate, to                reduce sodium and associated nucleation. 0.885                g/118 ml Natural flavorings and 0.0047 g/118 ml                Capsicum aqueous and high dissolving formulas were                chosen to reduce the impact of nucleation sites in                the solution. 0.649 g/118 ml Citric acid was used                to lower the acidity of the solution while adding a                sour taste. 0.1725 g/118 ml Chile lime seasoning,                0.059 g/118 ml smoked sweet paprika and 0.118 g/118                ml cayenne pepper add flavor. 12 oz/4 oz (118 ml)                Beer contains alcohol which has foam negating                effects.

TI Fire suppression method for aircraft cargo compartment, involves initiating halocarbon into surrounding environment subsequent to initiation of inert gas and carbon dioxide.
AU FAZZIO M P
   HAGGE H
   CHATTAWAY A
   SIMPSON T
AE KIDDE TECHNOLOGIES INC (KIDD-C)
   KIDDE TECHNOLOGIES INC (KIDD-C)
GA 202195391E
AB    NOVELTY - The fire suppression method (22) involves detecting (24) fire stimulus with a sensor in the environment surrounding the sensor. A discharge consisting of inert gas and/or carbon dioxide, is initiated (26) into the surrounding environment. Another discharge e.g. halocarbon such as iodocarbon, trifluoroiodomethane, is initiated (28) subsequent to the initiation of the former discharge. The two discharges are in gaseous state, liquid state and foam state. The former discharge reduces the temperature of the environment to less than or equal to about 315 degree Celsius prior to initiation of latter discharge.
   USE - Fire suppression method for of aircraft interior (claimed), such as cargo compartment. Can also be used for other fire protection applications such as computer rooms and machinery spaces.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing a fire suppression system that is an alternative to halon 1301 systems for the protection of aircraft cargo compartments. The method allows a controller to initiate the discharge of the halocarbon into the surrounding environment in response to detecting the fire stimulus in the environment surrounding the sensor, so that the controller can initiate the second discharge into the environment, thus reducing the risk of fire hazards such as aerosol can explosions, in an effective manner. The controller can control deep-seated aircraft fire hazards in an efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a fire suppression system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is the flowchart of the fire suppression process.
   Fire suppression method (22)
   Detecting fire stimulus (24)
   Initiating discharge (26)
   Initiating another discharge (28)
   Initiating third discharge (30)

   ZHANG X
   LUO G
AE FUJIAN VOCATIONAL COLLEGE BIO ENG (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202164030P
AB    NOVELTY - Production method of olive juice beverage involves washing green olive, draining, picking green olive after draining, selecting green olive with hardness of greater than 220 N/g, soaking green olive into alcohol, taking out, putting in sealing bag filled with carbon dioxide gas for 12-16 hours, soaking cooked green olive raw material in salt water added with lime for 3-4 hours, cleaning and draining olive, adding fixative, uniformly stirring, pressing green olive to extrude fruit core, squeezing, filtering and separating to obtain juice and fruit residue, extracting fruit residue and mixing with juice to obtain raw liquid, adding stabilizer and auxiliary material, uniformly mixing, quantifying each barrel of material liquid, adding essence, homogenizing, and sterilizing.
   USE - The method is used for producing olive juice beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The size of the compression force of the sorting machine compressing device is adjustable, so that the device can according to the size of the green olive of different quality groups and the hardness of the detector; the green olive after pressing by the compressing device; the hardness of the olive with hardness greater than 220 N/g is kept complete; the green olive with hardness less than 220N/g is flattened; the second conveying belt is obliquely arranged; the shape of the olive and the flattened green olive are sorted out.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an olive hardness selecting machine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The alcohol concentration is 80%. The weight ratio of malic acid and citric acid is 1:2. Preferred Method: The environment temperature of green olive in sealing bag is kept at 18-26 degrees C. The leaching liquor is mixed with juice, and further adding malic acid and citric acid and adjusting pH value to less than 4.0.
