PT P
PN CN112569896-A
TI Calcium oxide-based bimetallic composite material useful in high-temperature carbon dioxide adsorption and in-situ catalytic conversion, comprises adsorption active component, catalytically active component and auxiliary catalytic component.
AU HU J
   SHAO B
   SUN Z
   LIU H
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202137418K
AB    NOVELTY - Calcium oxide-based bimetallic composite material comprises adsorption active component, catalytically active component and auxiliary catalytic component; where the adsorption active component is calcium oxide, the catalytically active component includes metal iron oxide, the auxiliary catalytic component includes cobalt, nickel, and/or manganese oxides.
   USE - The composite material is useful in high-temperature carbon dioxide adsorption and in-situ catalytic conversion (claimed); and used in capture and conversion of CO2 in flue gas of large industrial processes e.g. coal-fired power plants, steel manufacturing, cement manufacturing, and ethylene manufacturing.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material: preparation adopts simple sol-gel one-step synthesis; has high CO2 adsorption capacity and stability, and high catalytic activity sites.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the calcium oxide-based bimetallic composite material comprising (1) sequentially adding the calcium salt and the metal salt as catalytically active component and the auxiliary catalytic component to aqueous solution, fully dissolving, adding an organic template to obtain mixed solution, the molar ratio between the calcium salt and the organic template is 1:1-2; (2) stirring and fully dissolving the mixed solution obtained in the step (1), heating in a water bath to continue stirring to obtain well-dispersed translucent sol; (3) heating and drying the semi-transparent sol obtained in the step (2) to obtain dry gel; (4) grinding and crushing the dry gel obtained in the step (3), transferring it to muffle furnace for calcination, grinding, tableting, and crushing to obtain composite material with particle size of 0.2-0.3 mm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite material has particle size of 0.2-0.3 mm and has hierarchical pore structure of mesopores and macropores. The composite material has formula (Fe(x)M(y)CaO(n))(I),M = cobalt, nickel and/or manganese metal oxides; andx, y, n = mass content of iron, M and O respectively., the mass ratio the adsorption active component calcium oxide to addition of catalytically active component and auxiliary catalytic component is 1:0.05-0.3, the weight ratio the catalytically active component Fe and auxiliary catalytic component M is 1:0.2-1, which can form part of alloy phase. In the step (1), the calcium salt is calcium chloride, calcium nitrate and/or calcium acetate; the metal salt includes Fe salt and cobalt, nickel or manganese salt, and the salt form is chloride, nitrate and/or acetate; the organic template is citric acid, ammonium citrate, oxalic acid, ammonium oxalate, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, and/or P123. Preferred Method: In the step (2), the heating and stirring temperature of the water bath is 80-100 degrees C, and the heating time is 4-6 hours; in the step (3), the heating and drying temperature of the translucent sol is 110-130 degrees C, and the heating time is 12-16 hours; in the step (4), the muffle furnace calcination temperature is 700-900 degrees C, and the calcination time is 4-6 hours. The high-temperature flue gas carbon dioxide adsorption and in-situ catalytic conversion by using the calcium oxide-based bimetallic composite material, comprises (i) filling the calcium oxide-based bimetallic composite material in a fixed bed reactor, and reducing at 500-600 degrees C and hydrogen atmosphere for 1-2 hours; (ii) adjusting the temperature to the specified adsorption conversion temperature, starting to capture carbon dioxide in the flue gas, and reaching the penetration time; and (iii) keeping the adsorption temperature constant, switching the hydrogen gas flow, the adsorbed carbon dioxide starts to be converted into syngas, at the same time, regenerating the adsorption active site calcium oxide in composite material. In the same fixed bed reactor, carbon dioxide adsorption and conversion are carried out at the same temperature; between steps (ii) and (iii), the time of the adsorption and conversion process is matched by adjusting the flow rate of the flue gas and the flow rate and content of hydrogen.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN112443316-A; CN112443316-B
TI Liquid carbon-dioxide (CO2) logging isolation            fluid preset method involves isolating fluid in well,            and ensuring fluid in well does not move up, and stop            replenishing at appropriate time during logging until            end of logging.
AU XU H
   WANG Y
   LU Y
   GAO M
   XIAO Y
   WAN S
   GUO L
   HUO H
   YANG J
   LIU X
AE DAQING OILFIELD CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   DAQING OILFIELD LTD CO (CNPC-C)
GA 202126008P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining the well                pressure data, liquid CO2 injection volume, bottom                hole depth, oil layer position and string structure                and other relevant data before testing the liquid                CO2 gas well. The test valve is slowly opened at                the upper end of the Christmas tree, and the                pressure change is observed while opening it. The                logging tool goes down slowly after the test valve                is fully opened, and the isolation fluid is preset                successfully. The contact surface between the well                liquid and the preset liquid in the logging blowout                pipe will move up after the logging tool goes down                smoothly. The volume space occupied by the logging                tool is filled. The isolation fluid is added to                fill this space intermittently and timely. The                contact liquid level is kept under the Christmas                tree, and the fluid is continuously isolated in the                well. The fluid is ensured in the well does not                move up, and stop replenishing at an appropriate                time during logging until the end of logging.
   USE - Liquid carbon-dioxide (CO2) logging isolation                fluid preset method.
   ADVANTAGE - The labor intensity is reduced, the logging                efficiency and safety are improved, and the logging                instruments to go down smoothly is enabled.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                liquid CO2 logging isolation fluid preset                method.

   High-pressure grease injection pump                (2)
   Liquid level hole (3)
   Drain valve (4)

TI Economical test method for stress corrosion resistance of hydrogen sulfide carbon dioxide oil well pipes, involves evaluating stress corrosion resistance of oil well pipe materials, and evaluating corrosion resistance of oil well pipes.
AU ZHONG B
   CHEN Y
   GAO P
   LI L
   AI F
   SAN H
   SU X
   SHA K
   XIAO Y
AE ANGANG STEEL CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2020B9405S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves observing the surface morphology of the sample and measuring the corrosion depth, processing the sample to a suitable size, preprocessing the surface, calculating the surface area and original mass, and performing a four-point bending load at a certain ratio according to the nominal yield strength. The surface of the non-failed sample is treated with rust removal, the quality after the test is recorded, and the corrosion rate analysis is performed after the test cycle is completed. The stress corrosion resistance of oil well pipe materials is evaluated by observing the corrosion morphology and calculating the depth of corrosion pits, and the corrosion resistance of oil well pipes is evaluated in combination with the corrosion rate. The test solution contains bicarbonate.
   USE - Economical test method for stress corrosion resistance of hydrogen sulfide (H2S) carbon dioxide (CO2).
   ADVANTAGE - The economical test method conducts stress corrosion resistance test in service environment or simulated actual environment for oil well pipe materials and evaluates the stress corrosion resistance performance of oil well pipes by observing corrosion morphology and measuring the depth of corrosion pits. The quantifiable rating is performed on the specimens that have not failed under bending loading, and the corrosion resistance of the oil well pipe material in a specific working environment is evaluated, and the selection of materials and equipment maintenance are guided.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the surface morphology of the sample after cleaning.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a front-stage heater (H1), a front-stage turbine (T1), a final-stage heater (H2) and a final-stage turbine (T2) that are connected in series in sequence. The exhaust port of compressor in the compressor unit communicates with the turbine unit through a branch pipe (P1). The communication position between the branch pipe and the turbine unit is located downstream of the suction port of the front-stage turbine. An auxiliary regenerator (AR) and an auxiliary heater (H3) are provided on the branch pipe, and the auxiliary regenerator is located upstream of the auxiliary heater.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A water-soluble 1-benzyl-7-(((((1-benzylquinolin-1-ium-2-yl)amino)carbamothioyl)amino)amino)quinolin-1-ium dichloride of formula (I), is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the compound (I), which involves mixing (i) dihydrazinoquinoline and a solvent (s1), increasing the temperature to 120-145 degrees C, dripping carbon disulfide and an accelerator into the obtained mixture, and performing vulcanization reaction for 8-12 hours, cooling (ii) the obtained mixture, dripping benzyl chloride into the mixture, and performing water-soluble modification at 85-95 degrees C for 8-12 hours, and removing (iii) the solvent, extracting using an extractant, and removing the extractant from the extract phase; and
   (2) use of the compound (I) in acidification treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The accelerator is potassium hydroxide, and the usage amount of the accelerator is 1-5 %mass of the dihydrazinoquinoline. The concentration of acid in the acid solution is 15-20 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solvent (s1) is methanol, ethanol and/or isopropanol. The mass ratio of dihydrazinoquinoline with respect to solvent (s1) is 50:(20-50). The molar ratio of dihydrazinoquinoline, carbon disulfide and benzyl chloride is 40:(20-25):(40-45). The extractant is ether, benzene and/or toluene. The addition amount of the compound (I) with respect to acid solution is 0.1-2 %mass in acidification treatment. Preferred Process: In the process (iii), the solvent is removed by decompression distillation method.

PN KR2131564-B1
TI Manufacturing sleeve, comprises mixing raw            materials, molding mixture by shooting mold with air            pressure, curing by spraying carbon-dioxide gas to            molded mixture, demolding cured mixture from mold and            drying demolded mixture.
AU HONG Y F
AE TAE KWANG METECH CO LTD (TAEK-Non-standard)
GA 202063458L
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing sleeve, comprises mixing the raw                materials to obtain a mixture; molding the mixture                by shooting the mold with air pressure; curing by                spraying carbon-dioxide gas to the molded mixture;                and demolding the cured mixture from the mold and                drying the demolded mixture, where the raw material                includes heating materials, feedstocks, hardened                raw materials and lightweight materials, and the                heating material includes at least one of aluminum                powder and aluminum hexafluoride, and the feedstock                includes at least one of silicon dioxide and                hematite, the curing raw material includes soda                silicate (water glass) and urea resin, the                lightweight material includes at least one of rice                husk ash (RHA), cenosphere and pearlite.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing                sleeve.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can prevent shrinkage defects of                cast steel casting, high improved productivity and                heat generation or heat retention, improved working                environment because no waste water is generated;                can be used to reduce the brittleness of the                elongated sleeve by gas injection hardening, and                can improve the light weight and heat generation;                high carbon content promotes the exothermic                reaction more, and the particle size is larger than                other rice husks, promotes good exothermic                properties and can further reduce the amount of                urea resin used; curing efficiency can also be                improved to shorten curing time; improves the                impact resistance of the sleeve to prevent it from                being broken; is economical; and has low                hygroscopicity, and the effect of reducing the                drying time and cost and preventing cracks from                occurring inside the sleeve.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                sleeve, manufactured as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw material                  further comprises a pitch-based carbon fiber. The                  raw material further comprises an acrylic                  emulsion. Preferred Method: The mixing step                  comprises mixing 20-26 pts. wt. aluminum powder,                  2.5-3.5 pts. wt. aluminum hexafluoride, 20-26                  pts. wt. silicon dioxide, 20-26 pts. wt.                  hematite, 17-25 pts. wt. soda silicate (water                  glass), 2.5-3.5 pts. wt. urea resin, 4-6 pts. wt.                  RHA, 4-6 pts. wt. cenosphere and 4-6 pts. wt.                  pearlite. In the drying step, the demolded                  mixture is dried at 100-120 degrees C for 30                  minutes to 2 hours. The mixing step further                  comprises mixing 2.5-3.5 pts. wt. pitch-based                  carbon fiber. The method further comprises                  spraying a urea mixture solution on the surface                  of the demolded mixture after the demolding step.                  The urea mixture solution is prepared by mixing                  urea resin and water in a weight ratio of                  1:1.

PN CN111298734-A; CN111298734-B
TI Preparation of sodium bisulfite/ethyl cellulose microcapsule by adding ethyl cellulose solution with milled sodium bisulfite powder, adding cyclohexane, precipitating ethyl cellulose from ethyl cellulose solution, and vacuum filtering.
AU ZHENG C
   YAN C
   HUANG Z
   ZHANG T
   WANG J
   WU Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202058190G
AB    NOVELTY - A sodium bisulfite/ethyl cellulose microcapsule is prepared by adding ethyl cellulose into N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, stirring to completely dissolve, adding milled sodium bisulfite powder, rapidly stirring, fully dispersing sodium bisulfite in ethyl cellulose solution, dropwise adding cyclohexane, precipitating ethyl cellulose from ethyl cellulose solution, coating on sodium bisulfite particle surface, vacuum filtering to obtain filter cake, freeze-drying to obtain sodium bisulfite/ethyl cellulose microcapsule, adding filtrate to water, introducing carbon dioxide, performing protonation on N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, dissolving in water, separating to obtain cyclohexane and N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine protonated aqueous solution, collecting and recycling, introducing nitrogen into N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine protonated aqueous solution, separating N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, collecting N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, and recycling.
   USE - The method is used for preparing sodium bisulfite/ethyl cellulose microcapsule.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A sodium bisulfite/ethyl cellulose microcapsule is prepared by adding ethyl cellulose into N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, stirring to completely dissolve to obtain ethyl cellulose solution, adding milled sodium bisulfite powder, rapidly stirring, fully dispersing sodium bisulfite in ethyl cellulose solution, dropwise adding cyclohexane under fast stirring condition, precipitating ethyl cellulose from ethyl cellulose solution, coating on sodium bisulfite particle surface to prepare microcapsule suspension, vacuum filtering to obtain filter cake, freeze-drying to obtain sodium bisulfite/ethyl cellulose microcapsule, adding filtrate to water, introducing carbon dioxide, performing protonation on N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, dissolving in water, separating to obtain cyclohexane and N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine protonated aqueous solution, collecting and recycling, introducing nitrogen into N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine protonated aqueous solution, separating N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, collecting N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, and recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of ethyl cellulose and sodium bisulfite is 8:17. The total concentration ethyl cellulose and sodium bisulfite in system is 10%. The mass ratio of N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine and cyclohexane is 1:1. Preferred Method: Reaction is done at 40-50 degrees C. Stirring is done at speed of 200-400 revolutions/minute (rpm) and 300-400 rpm. The introduction rate of carbon dioxide per liter of system solution is 120-150 mL/minute. The flow rate of nitrogen per liter of N,N dimethylcyclohexylamine protonated aqueous solution is 100-120 mL/minute.

The airtight container includes a first accommodating portion that accommodates target object, and a second accommodating portion that accommodates the ozone hydrate. A connection pipe connects first and second accommodating portions. An on-off valve is provided in the connection pipe.

   CHENG G
AE UNIV FOSHAN (UYFS-C)
GA 202033693S
AB    NOVELTY - Solid oxide fuel cell includes an anode composed of catalyst and metal-ceramic base material, a cathode and electrolyte for matching the anode located between the anode and the cathode. The catalyst includes a metal support with a composition and nano-oxide carrier with fluorite crystal phase structure.
   USE - Solid oxide fuel cell used for converting hydrocarbon fuel into hydrogen and carbon monoxide rich fuel, preferably gasoline-type alkanes to hydrogen and carbon monoxide rich forms (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The solid oxide fuel cell has excellent catalytic effect on complex hydrocarbon fuels (such as gaseous alkanes such as methane, propane, and liquid alkanes such as gasoline), and can efficiently convert these hydrocarbons into hydrogen-rich and carbon monoxide-rich fuel gas.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Solid oxide fuel cell includes an anode composed of catalyst and metal-ceramic base material, a cathode and electrolyte for matching the anode located between the anode and the cathode. The catalyst includes a metal support with a composition formula of Ni1-xMox and nano-oxide carrier with fluorite crystal phase structure of general formula Ce1-yAyO2- delta .
   x=0-0.5;
   y=0.01-0.4; and
   A=an element selected from calcium and zirconium.

The cathode is lanthanum strontium manganese oxide, lanthanum strontium cobalt iron oxide, barium strontium cobalt iron oxide having a perovskite crystal phase structure.

PT P
PN CN110833841-A
TI Micro-nanoized monodisperse phosphorus-ruthenium catalyst prepared by uniformly dispersing ruthenium phosphide on surface of hydroxyapatite in monodisperse form, used for formylation of amine with carbon dioxide.
AU CUI D
   ZHAO K
   TONG Q
   CAI T
   YUAN J
   JIN C
AE SHANGHAI NAT ENG RES CENT NANOTECHNOLOGY (NERC-C)
GA 202018858N
AB    NOVELTY - Micro-nanoized monodisperse phosphorus-ruthenium catalyst is claimed. The catalyst is prepared by uniformly dispersing ruthenium phosphide as active component on the surface of hydroxyapatite as carrier in a monodisperse form.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for formylation reaction of amine with carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high loading quantity, uniform dispersion and single active component. The method is simple and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) dissolving calcium source and phosphorus source in a molar ratio of 5:3 in deionized water until the concentration of the calcium source and the phosphorus source is 0.2-0.5 mol/l, adjusting pH of reaction solution to 2-4 using acid solution, mixing the calcium source and phosphorus source under vigorous stirring conditions, adjusting pH of the reaction solution to 10-12 using aqueous ammonia under stirring conditions, reacting in a water bath at 40-90 degrees C for 4-10 hours, then allowing to stand, aging for 18-24 hours, filtering, washing with deionized water, and freeze-drying to obtain hydroxyapatite carrier, and (ii) adjusting pH of ruthenium chloride solution to 7 using aqueous ammonia, dispersing hydroxyapatite in the ruthenium chloride solution in a mass ratio of 0.1-5 %, stirring at room temperature for 2-5 hours, centrifuging, washing, drying at 50-100 degrees C, performing temperature programmed reduction in hydrogen atmosphere at 400 degrees C for 2 hours, and heating continuously at 600-800 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium source is calcium nitrate and/or calcium chloride. The phosphorus source is ammonium hydrogen phosphate, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, ammonium phosphate and/or phosphoric acid. The acid solution is nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid and/or phosphoric acid. The hydrogen atmosphere is 5-10% hydrogen-argon mixture. The concentration of ruthenium chloride solution is 0.1-10 mmol/l.

PT P
PN US2020015721-A1
TI Method for detecting respiratory blockage, involves comparing determined change pattern of collected non-dissolved carbon dioxide gas to stored change patterns and signaling alarm when difference from result is beyond preset threshold.
AU CHENG L
   CHENG C T
AE CHENG L (CHEN-Individual)
   CHENG C T (CHEN-Individual)
GA 202005961B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves attaching a housing with a carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor to a region of skin of a person. The region person skin is warmed to a preset temperature. The non-dissolved CO2 gas emitted from region of skin of the person is collected. The change pattern of collected non-dissolved CO2 gas is determined by a processing circuit by analyzing its concentration waveforms and slopes without relying on its absolute CO2 concentration values. The determined change pattern of collected non-dissolved CO2 gas is compared to a set of stored personalized CO2 change patterns. An alarm is signaled when the difference from compared result is beyond a preset threshold.
   USE - Method for detecting respiratory blockage.
   ADVANTAGE - The changing pattern helps to identify the early sign of respiration problem. The CO2 monitor system creates a personalized database for each user and use advanced pattern recognition techniques to quickly detect an abnormal CO2 level change. The personalized pattern is supplemented by taking into account the heart rate change of the user. The system can select the appropriate pattern category such as quiet, mild or vigorous category for comparison. Miniaturizing the monitor system allows a person to wear on any part of its body without noticing it.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for detecting respiratory blockage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of a CO2 monitor system applied to human skin.
   CO2 monitor system (100)
   Skin-mount pad (101)
   Vacuum pump (103)
   Signal processing circuit (104)
   Temperature control (105)

TI Acidification retarder capable of adsorbing carbon dioxide to form isolation layer, comprises monomer solution and initiator, where monomer solution comprises ammonium sulfate and initiator comprises potassium persulfate.
AU SHI H
   LI X
   DING L
   ZHANG Y
   LIU X
   MENG L
   WANG L
   ZU K
   FU P
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2019A0401U
AB    NOVELTY - Acidification retarder capable of adsorbing carbon dioxide (CO2) to form isolation layer, comprises monomer solution and an initiator. The monomer solution comprises 4.0-6.0 wt.% acrylamide, 8.0-10.0 wt.% 2-acrylamide-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid , 0.1-1.0 wt.% perfluoroalkylamide propyl dimethylallyl ammonium chloride, 1.0-10.0 wt.% ammonium sulfate, 5-25 wt.% of 10% sodium hydroxide solution, and balance is water. The initiator consists of a redox initiation system consisting of 2.0-6.0 wt.% of 1.0% potassium persulfate and 1.0-4.0 wt.% of 1.0% 2,3,5,6-tetrahydroxy-2-hexenoic acid-4-lactone.
   USE - Acidification retarder capable of absorbing CO2 to form an isolation layer used in the acidification and production increase operation of carbonate reservoirs in oil and gas wells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The acidification retarder reduces reaction rate, effectively reduces the H + diffusion and achieves the effect of deep acidification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a preparing acidification retarder, which involves;
   (A) formulating acrylamide, 2-acrylamide-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid, perfluoroalkylamidepropyldimethylallylammonium chloride and ammonium sulfate into an aqueous solution in proportion;
   (B) adjusting aqueous solution with a sodium hydroxide solution with a concentration of 10% by mass so that the pH value is 5.5-6.5 to obtain a monomer solution; and
   (C) purging monomer solution with nitrogen for 30 minutes, and adding an initiator to the polymerization reaction to obtain an acidification retarder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The acidification retarder is represented by formula (I).X, Y, and Z=number of moles of the corresponding unit, where ratio of the number of moles of X, Y, and Z is (6-10): (4- 6): (0.01-0.3).. The polymerization reaction has a polymerization temperature of 35-50 degrees C and a polymerization time of 5- 10 hours. The apparent viscosity of the acidification retarder is not less than 200cps, and the intrinsic viscosity is 1.5-3.5dL / g. The acidification retarder is added with a concentration of 3.0% / 100mL to the acidification retarder with a concentration of 15% by weight of HCl, the static dissolution rate of carbonate rocks within 60 minutes is reduced from 0.0216-0.0019 g / second..

PT P
PN CN110327879-A; CN110327879-B
TI Preparing biomass porous carbon adsorbent product suitable for flue gas carbon dioxide adsorption, by mixing and grinding biomass powder and potassium hydroxide, heating, grinding and crushing carbonized product, and modification treatment.
AU LIU J
   WU D
   SHEN F
   DONG Y
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019877201
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomass porous carbon adsorbent product suitable for flue gas carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorption, involves: (S1) selecting biomass as a carbon source, washing and drying by using water, and then crushing and sieving to obtain powder with the granularity of 50-200 meshes; (S2) mixing and grinding the biomass powder and potassium hydroxide (KOH) serving as an activating agent in a mass ratio of 1:0.2-1:1; (S3) transferring the mixture into tubular furnace, heating from room temperature to 600-1000 degrees C at rate of 2-10oC/minute under an inert gas atmosphere, and carbonizing for 0.5-4 hours; naturally cooling to room temperature in inert gas atmosphere, and using 8-10 wt.% diluted HCl to remove impurities, washing and drying; and (S4) grinding and crushing the carbonized product, then carrying out modification treatment on the carbonized product by grinding and crushing in an air atmosphere for 0.5-30 minutes by adopting working voltage of 20-60 kV at low-temperature plasma.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing biomass porous carbon adsorbent product suitable for flue gas CO2 adsorption from coal-fired power plant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The high-quality porous carbon adsorbent can be prepared from the biomass raw material. The preparation method is simple, the cost is low, the influence on the environment is small, and the industrial application is easy to implement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a biomass porous carbon adsorbent product is prepared by using the method above, used for capturing flue gas CO2 of a coal-fired power plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: In step (S3), the inert gas is preferably nitrogen, and the gas flow rate of the inert gas is preferably set to be 50-200 ml/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In step (S4), a low-temperature plasma with a working voltage of 30-40 kV is preferably adopted for carrying out modification treatment. Preferred Components: The biomass is preferably one or a combination of tree bark, tree leaves and wood chips. Preferred Parameters: In step (S1), a carbon source is preferably crushed and sieved to 80-150 mesh powder. The mixture is preferably heated at 700-900 degrees C from room temperature at a rate of 3-8 degrees C/minute in an inert gas atmosphere, and is carbonized for 0.5-4 hours. Preferred Composition: In step (S2), the biomass powder and the activating agent are preferably mixed and ground according to a mass ratio of 1:0.4-1:0.8.

TI Method for calibrating mass flow meter in constant volume sampling (CVS) exhaust gas analysis system, involves determining second carbon dioxide mass by first gas analyzer and determining mass flows of two mass flow meter by gas analyzer.
AU SEEWALD G
AE AVL LIST GMBH (AVLV-C)
   AVL LIST GMBH (AVLV-C)
   AVL LIST GMBH (AVLV-C)
GA 201985399W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing exhaust gas in an exhaust passage (20) which opens into a mixing channel (14). A dilution air duct (12) is conveyed in the mixing channel. The exhaust-air mixture flows in the mixing channel. The mass flow of the exhaust air mixture in the mixing channel of the exhaust gas analysis system is measured. The mass flow of the air sucked into the exhaust gas analysis system is measured. The mixing channel is measured continuously and discontinuously over sample bag (32) for a defined measuring cycle. The calibration factor for the second mass flow meter is continuously measured by exhaust gas channel. The second carbon dioxide mass is determined by first gas analyzer (34). The mass flows of the two mass flow meter (36,42) is determined by gas analyzer (40).
   USE - Method for calibrating mass flow meter in constant volume sampling exhaust gas analysis system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method with high accuracy in the determination of the exhaust gas mass flow is ensured without additional component and complexity by errors due to inaccuracies in the measured values the two mass flow meters are minimized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure of exhaust gas analysis system for carrying out method for calibrating mass flow meter in CVS exhaust gas analysis system.
   Dilution air duct (12)
   Mixing channel (14)
   Exhaust passage (20)
   Sample bag (32)
   First gas analyzer (34)
   Mass flow meters (36,42)
   Gas analyzer (40)

PN RU2702571-C1
TI METHOD OF PRODUCING A COMPOSITION FOR TREATING CELLULOSE AND PAPER PRODUCTS AND A DEVICE FOR CARRYING OUT SAID METHOD.
AU KOPITSYN D S
   NOVIKOV A A
   KOTELEV M S
   GUSHCHIN P A
   IVANOV E V
   VINOKUROV V A
   VERKHOVSKII R G
   VINOGRADOV M N
   SIVER V V
AE SVD SOFT CO LTD (SVDS-Non-standard)
GA 201990004X
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining carbonated magnesium alcoholate is carried out by contacting metallic magnesium with alcohol in an atmosphere of inert gas at temperature of +20C to boiling temperature of alcohol. Initial content of magnesium in reactionary sweep from 0.01 wt%. up to 5.0 wt%. Obtained suspension is concentrated to the content of magnesium alcoholate in suspension of 2 wt%. up to 40 wt%, but not less than 2 times by distillation of excess alcohol, which is directed for recycling for contact with metal magnesium. Concentrated suspended magnesium alcoholate suspension is dissolved by contacting with carbon dioxide at temperature of +20C to boiling temperature of alcohol to obtain a solution of carbonated magnesium alcoholate in alcohol. Target product a composition for processing cellulose and paper products is obtained by mixing a solution of a carbonated magnesium alcoholate in alcohol with an easily volatile solvent selected from halogen-substituted hydrocarbons. Content of carbonated magnesium alcoholate in the end product is 3.0 wt% up to 25 wt% Method is carried out in a flow type reactor consisting of a container for producing magnesium alcoholate 1, divided into sections for fractionating products and reagents by size of granules, containers for concentration of magnesium alcoholate 2, magnesium alcoholate dilution tanks under action of carbon dioxide 3, connected by products controlled by mass transfer lines and reagents.
   USE - Pulp and paper industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Disclosed group of inventions increases efficiency and efficiency of the process of producing a carbonated magnesium alkoxide and prevents contamination of the end product with unreacted magnesium particles.
6 cl, 5 ex

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing cooling process to cool a combustion tube (14). PN CN110235934-A
TI Air conditioning fresh-keeping method of fruit and            vegetable comprises pre-cooling harvested fruits and            vegetables, cold storing, adjusting oxygen, carbon            dioxide and nitrogen gas in storage environment,            sealing, packing and transporting.
AU XIAO C
AE LIUYANG YUYOU AGRIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV (LIUY-Non-standard)
GA 201982039P
AB    NOVELTY - Air conditioning fresh-keeping method of fruit                and vegetable comprises (a) carrying out                pre-cooling treatment after harvesting fruits and                vegetables as soon as possible before                transportation or storage for quickly removing heat                of the fruit, (b) completing the treatment after                the fruit and vegetable are picked, (c) adding                fruits and vegetables into cold storage for cooling                or carrying out forced air cooling, (d) maintaining                fruits and vegetables in a relatively sealed                storage environment, changing and adjusting the                proportion of oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen                gas in storage environment, sealing and storing,                (e) taking the fruit with appropriate maturity in a                plastic bag, adjusting the atmosphere of the bag                with a gas distribution device at 0 degrees C, and                (f) packing ripen fruit with non-toxic                fresh-keeping bag, adding in packing box for                packaging, transferring into fruit box,                transporting by using refrigerated transport truck,                and lightly handling the fruit when loading and                unloading.
   USE - Used as air conditioning fresh-keeping method                of fruit and vegetable.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can extend the preservation time                by the fresh MAP preservation method, thus reducing                the burden of rapid sales, and has filled the gap                in the off-season of fruit and vegetable production                that increases the output and income of fruit                farmers, also makes the consumers taste early in                two months, reduces the cost of fruit and vegetable                e-commerce business, and has broad development                prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (e) comprises                  placing the weighted fruit in the groove portion                  of air-conditioning fresh-keeping vacuum                  packaging machine, covering the plastic box with                  a film, and configuring the internal environment                  gas. The step (e) further comprises generating an                  inert gas mixing multiple gases including oxygen                  by a modified atmosphere vacuum packaging machine                  in a storage environment configuration, filling                  the crisper, and prolonging the storage time by                  reducing the respiratory strength of the fruits                  and vegetables. The fruit and vegetable in step                  (e) are maintained at low temperature in each                  step of transportation, and the fruit cannot be                  placed in the unloading dock without                  refrigeration equipment. The fruit boxes on the                  transport vehicle in step (f) are placed at a                  distance from each other to ensure that the air                  around the fruit box is freely circulated in all                  directions. The fresh-keeping bag in step (f)                  comprises non-toxic, non-polluting and low-cost                  materials, and the plastic box and the box board                  used for packaging are clean, non-toxic and                  odor-free materials.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

TI Device for controlling package pressure and gas flow for e.g. vegetables packaged good, has film formed with first opening for exposure to inner side of package, and channel extended between first opening and second opening.
AU KLANN K
AE LASERSHARP FLEXPAK SERVICES LLC (LASE-Non-standard)
GA 201964325G
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a film (300) i.e. multilayer film, formed with a first opening for exposure to an inner side of a package and a second opening for exposure to an outer side of a package. A channel (340) i.e. laser-ablated channel, extends between the first opening and the second opening, where dimensions of the channel are utilized to control rate of flow of carbon dioxide from the inner side of the package to the outer side of the package and control oxygen transmission rate (OTR) from the outer side of the package to the inner side of the package such that flow of carbon dioxide/OTR ratio is between about 0.1 and about 100.
   USE - Device for controlling package pressure and gas flow for a packaged good i.e. vegetables or fruits packaged good.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces package weight and shipping cost and manufacturing costs due to minimal material requirements of the device, and restricts placement of valves to areas of packaging to receive flexing or stretching so as to limit freedom of package design and handling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for controlling package pressure and gas flow for a packaged good.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a channel formed from translated laser light between two layers of a multilayer film.
   Film (300)
   High absorbency layer (310)
   Channel (340)
   Translated part (400)

AU LI H
AE LI H (LIHH-Individual)
GA 2019536735

TI Manufacturing gas separation membrane by            dissolving poly(ether-b-amide) copolymer in solvent,            casting polymer solution into mold, evaporating            solvent, submerging film in crosslinking solution            comprising diisocyanate polyether, and drying.
AU YANG J
   HARRIGAN D
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201919651R
AB    NOVELTY - A gas separation membrane is manufactured by                dissolving poly(ether-b-amide) (PEBA) copolymer in                solvent to form polymer solution; casting polymer                solution into a mold; evaporating solvent to form a                film; submerging film in a crosslinking solution to                form crosslinked film, where crosslinking solution                comprises diisocyanate polyether (I); and drying                the film to form the gas separation membrane.
   USE - Manufacture of gas separation membrane                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The membranes achieve high carbon dioxide                permeability, high thermal stability, resistance to                plasticization, and high carbon dioxide/methane                pure gas selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A gas separation membrane is manufactured by                dissolving poly(ether-b-amide) (PEBA) copolymer in                solvent to form polymer solution; casting polymer                solution into a mold; evaporating solvent to form a                film; submerging film in a crosslinking solution to                form crosslinked film, where crosslinking solution                comprises diisocyanate polyether of formula (I);                and drying the film to form the gas separation                membrane.
   R6=alkyl or -H;
   x=1-200; and
   R1-R11=-C=N=O, alkyl, or -H, provided that 1                or 2 of R1-R5 is -C=N=O, and 1 or 2 of R7-R11 is                -C=N=O.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) gas separation method which involves                flowing gas stream through gas separation membrane,                and separating the gases via the gas separation                membrane; and
   (2) gas separation membrane comprising                crosslinked poly(ether-b-amide) copolymer which                comprises urethane crosslinks that is the reaction                product of poly(ether-b-amide) copolymer and                diisocyanate polyether (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Poly(ether-b-amide)                  copolymer comprises 25-80 wt.% polyether segment                  and 75-20 wt.% polyamide segment. Polymer                  solution comprises 1-99 wt.% poly(ether-b-amide)                  copolymer. Crosslinking solution comprises 2-40                  wt.% crosslinker (I). Preferred Process:                  Crosslinking solution is heated to 35-85 degrees                  C. Preferred Condition: Gas separation membrane                  is dried at 35-90 degrees C. Preferred Property:                  Gas separation membrane has thickness of 30-70 mu                  m.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R6=Me or -H;
   R4, R8= C=N=O; and
   R3, R9=Me.

AU MLYNAR G

AE ZIPPE GMBH & CO KG (ZIPP-Non-standard)
GA 201891162U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing (01) the glass raw materials in the form of a Ge-amount. The batch is mixed (02). The batch is agglomerated to form pellets. The water glass is added (03) as a binder to the Ge amount. The mixture is cross-linked with the water glass contained in a vacuum atmosphere. The curing is taken place in the vacuum atmosphere during or after the formation of pellets. The vacuum atmosphere has an absolute pressure which is lower than the vapor pressure of a medium contained in the mixture, in water.
   USE - Method for producing glass batch pellets.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide connection or flue gas connection in order to be able to carry out the vacuumization of the glass batch pellets in a carbon dioxide-containing process atmosphere. The vacuum is constructed in the vacuum device is in principle arbitrary and can be done simply by using vacuum pump, with which the gas located in a process chamber together with the glass quantity pellets can be conveyed out of the process chamber.

   Step for mixing batch (02)
   Step for adding water glass (03)
   Step for transferring produced mixture into shaping facility (05)
   Step for melting mixture pellets with liquid glass melt (09)

PN FR3066258-A1
TI Natural gas stream processing system used to treat natural gas stream implementing treatment system, comprises regenerative adsorption purification unit, cryogenic nitrogen rejection units and means.
AU TERRIEN P
   COSTA D B P
   MARTY P
   RENOU E
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 201889886H
AB    NOVELTY - Natural gas stream processing system comprises: at least one regenerative adsorption purification unit of the transportation security administration (TSA) type making it possible to eliminate at least partly at least one impurity including water, carbon dioxide or heavy hydrocarbons from the natural gas stream; at least one cryogenic nitrogen rejection units (NRU) nitrogen extraction unit, for treating the natural gas stream from the TSA unit and making it possible to produce a methane-enriched B gas which is depleted in nitrogen; and means for conveying at least one part B1 of the gas B to the unit TSA so that it can be used as the regeneration gas of the unit TSA.
   USE - The system is useful for treating a natural gas stream implementing the treatment system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for treating a natural gas stream implementing the treatment system, comprising (a) adsorption purification of the natural gas stream by means of the TSA-type adsorption purification unit so as to eliminate at least partly at least one impurity including water, carbon dioxide or heavy hydrocarbons from the gas natural, (b) extraction of at least a portion of the nitrogen contained in the natural gas from step (a) by cryogenics via the cryogenic NRU nitrogen extraction unit, (c) recovery at the outlet of the NRU unit of a gas B enriched in methane, and (d) regeneration of the TSA unit by means of at least a part B1 of the gas B.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system comprises at least a first and a second adsorption purification unit TSA type arranged in series, and the NRU cryogenic unit is used to treat the natural gas from these two TSA units. The first TSA unit makes it possible to eliminate at least part of the water from the natural gas and the second unit TSA makes it possible to eliminate at least partly the carbon dioxide of the natural gas stream. The second TSA unit makes it possible to eliminate at least partly the heavy hydrocarbons or the cryogenic unit NRU makes it possible to eliminate at least partly the heavy hydrocarbons. The installation comprises means for conveying at least a portion B1 of the gas B to the first and second TSA unit so as to be able to be used in series as regeneration gas of the first and second TSA unit. The NRU cryogenic unit makes it possible to produce a nitrogen enriched gas A, where the installation comprises: means for conveying gas A to the first or second TSA unit so that it can be used as a regeneration gas; and means for conveying at least one part B1 of the gas B to the unit TSA which has not received the gas A so that it can be used as the regeneration gas of the unit TSA. In the system: the NRU unit comprises at least one distillation column and an exchange line, and means for conveying the gas B1 leaving the TSA unit to the exchange line; or the unit NRU makes it possible to produce a cold gas at a temperature of below 15 degrees C, where the system comprises an exchanger external to the NRU, and means for conveying the gas B1 leaving the TSA unit up to the exchanger and means for conveying the cold product to the exchanger. The system comprises means for conveying gas B1 leaving the TSA to an external cold source at a temperature of below 15 degrees C. Preferred Process: In the step (a), the gas is purified by passing the flow of natural gas in series through a first and a second TSA unit. The step (c) is recovered at the outlet of the NRU unit a gas B enriched with methane and a nitrogen-enriched gas A. The impurities adsorbed in the step (a) at least one heavy hydrocarbon or aromatic derivatives and the process comprises a step (e) adjusting the dew point in hydrocarbons of the gas B1 resulting from step (d). The NRU unit comprises at least one distillation column and an exchange line for cooling at least a portion of the natural gas stream and adjusting the dew point to hydrocarbons by cooling the gas B1 from step (d) at a temperature of below 20 degrees C in the exchange line of the NRU unit. The adjustment of the hydrocarbon dew point of the gas B1 from step (d) is effected by cooling the gas B1 from the step (d) to a temperature of below 20 degrees C in an exchanger external to the NRU unit with a cold product at a temperature of below 15 degrees C. In the method, the cold product is liquefied natural gas possibly mixed with a part of the methane-enriched gas B, or any other gas at a temperature of below 15 degrees C from the NRU. The adjustment of the hydrocarbon dew point of the gas B1 from step (d) is effected by cooling the gas B1 to a temperature of below 20 degrees C by means of an external cold source to the treatment system. The process comprises reinstating at least a portion of the B1 gas having undergone a dew point adjustment to hydrocarbons in the B gas. The process comprises reintegrating at least a portion of the gas B1 resulting from TSA in the gas B, in the impurities adsorbed in step (a) at least carbon dioxide and during the entire duration of the process, the sum of the carbon dioxide and nitrogen contents in the gas B is maintained downstream of the reintegration of at least a portion of the gas B1 of 2-6% by adjusting the parameters the NRU.

PN US2018318774-A1; WO2018204580-A1; KR2019142781-A; CN110799260-A; SG11201909033-A1; EP3618944-A1; JP2020518446-W; US10960361-B2; CN110799260-B
TI Forming crosslinked polymer membrane, comprises reacting, in solution, silane with polymer of pendant nucleophile in weak acid catalyst, casting an the solution, and heating the cast solution to elevated temperature for crosslinking period.
AU VAUGHN J T
   SUNDELL B J
   SANDORE B J
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   VAUGHN J T (VAUG-Individual)
   SUNDELL B J (SUND-Individual)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   VAUGHN J T (VAUG-Individual)
   SUNDELL B J (SUND-Individual)
GA 201887000F
AB    NOVELTY - Forming (M1) a crosslinked polymer membrane, comprises: reacting, in solution, a silane with a polymer comprising a pendant nucleophile in the presence of a weak acid catalyst, thus grafting the cellulose acetate with at least a portion of the silane; casting an amount of the solution; and heating at least a portion of the cast solution to an elevated temperature for a crosslinking period, thus forming a polymer membrane comprising crosslinks formed from the silane.
   USE - The method is useful for forming a crosslinked polymer membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides crosslinked polymer membrane with improved carbon dioxide permeability, selectivity, and plasticization resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) modifying (M2) a preformed polymer membrane, comprising immersing a preformed polymer membrane in a solution comprising a weak acid catalyst and a silane, where the preformed polymer membrane comprises a polymer repeat unit comprising a pendant nucleophile, and maintaining the solution, where the membrane is immersed, at a crosslinking temperature of at least 100 degrees C but less than the glass transition temperature of the membrane for a crosslinking period, thus crosslinking the membrane with at least a portion of the silane; and
   (2) an asymmetric crosslinked polymer membrane, comprising a porous support layer, and a dense selective layer comprising a polymer comprising a pendant nucleophile and many crosslinks comprising silanes, where a carbon dioxide permeability through the membrane is at least 25 Barrer when measured in pure gas flow at 25 degrees C and 100 psia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the method (M1), the step of reacting comprises maintaining the solution at a temperature of at least 100 degrees C for a grafting period of at least 5 minutes. The casting step comprises: immersing the amount of the solution in a nonsolvent; and spinning the amount of the solution into a hollow fiber. The crosslinking period is at least 10 minutes. The solution comprises a solvent. The method (M1) further comprises: mixing the silane and the solvent, where the silane is a dry silane and the solvent is dry solvent; mixing the weak acid catalyst with the mixture of the silane and the solvent, where the weak acid catalyst is a dry weak acid catalyst; and mixing the polymer with the mixture of the weak acid catalyst, silane, and solvent while stirring, where the polymer is a dry polymer. The method (M2) further comprises: removing the membrane from the solution, and drying the membrane at a drying temperature of at least 100 degrees C; and solvent exchanging the membrane with a non-solvent after removing the membrane from the solution prior to the drying step, where a weight ratio of the silane to the weak acid catalyst in the solution is at least 5:1 and a molar ratio of the silane to the polymer in the solution is at least 1:1. Preferred Components: The ratio of the weak acid catalyst to the solvent is 1-30 vol.%. The ratio of the silane to the solvent is 5-15 vol.%. The ratio of the polymer to the solvent is 1-30 vol.%. The weak acid catalyst is a carboxylic acid or acetic acid. The polymer comprises pendant nucleophile is cellulose acetate and the silane is vinyltrimethoxysilane. The crosslinking period is at least 5 minutes. The polymer repeat unit comprises a pendant nucleophile is cellulose acetate and the silane is vinyltrimethoxysilane. The membrane comprises a polymer comprising a member including a polyimide, a polysulfone, a polyamide, a poly benzoxazole, a polybenzimidazole, polyphenyl ether, a polyketone, a polynorbornene, and an ester of cellulose, preferably cellulose acetate, where the membrane comprises crosslinks formed from vinyltrimethoxysilane. The carbon dioxide/methane selectivity across the membrane is at least 20 as determined from measurements in pure gas flow at 25 degrees C and 100 psia. The carbon dioxide permeability measured in pure gas flow at 25 degrees C and 500 psia is not greater than 2.5 times carbon dioxide permeability measured in pure gas flow at 25 degrees C and 25 psia. The portion of the crosslinks is oligomeric. The porous support layer and the dense selective layer form a continuous asymmetric structure. The asymmetric crosslinked membrane is a hollow fiber or thin film, preferably a thin film composite membrane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108759494-A
TI Metal smelting tail gas desulfurizing            dust-removing method, involves supplying pure oxygen to            oxygen feeding port and circulating pipe for enrichment            combustion, and smelting natural gas to complete metal            smelting tail gas discharge process.
AU SHANG X
   SHANG S
AE JIESHOU JINGHUA TECHNOLOGY INFORMATION (JIES-Non-standard)
GA 201889570H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a drying and                dehydrating machine (41) in a gas collecting                chamber (4) to dry tail gas. The dried tail gas is                introduced into an air compressor (5) for gas                compression. Liquid carbon dioxide is separated by                using the air compressor by adopting a gas                compression separation technology. A residual gas                (6) is compressed and discharged by using the air                compressor. The residual gas discharged by the air                compressor is returned to a smelting furnace (1)                through a circulating pipe (11) to recycle. Pure                oxygen is supplied to an oxygen feeding port (12)                and the circulating pipe for enrichment combustion.                A natural gas is smelted to complete metal smelting                tail gas discharge process.
   USE - Metal smelting tail gas desulfurizing                dust-removing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing smelting                treatment and recycling of the tail gas in a closed                system without discharging the tail gas to air to                realize zero discharge of pollutant to an                atmosphere, reducing tail gas processing                difficulties to ensure economical clean and                environment-friendly process so as to ensure better                greenhouse effect and eliminate carbon dioxide                emission in metal smelting process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a smelting                furnace. '(Drawing includes non-English language                text)'
   Smelting furnace (1)
   Gas collecting chamber (4)
   Air compressor (5)
   Residual gas (6)
   Circulating pipe (11)
   Oxygen feeding port (12)
   Drying and dehydrating machine (41)

PT P
PN CN108497294-A
TI Rice flour composition useful for preparing dumplings comprises rice flour, acetylated corn powder obtained by mixing corn powder and chitosan in acetic acid solution system, and spray-drying, modified cassava powder, and stabilized starch.
AU GUO S
   ZHU T
AE ANHUI JINTAIYANG FOOD CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201872520R
AB    NOVELTY - Rice flour composition comprises rice flour, acetylated corn powder, modified cassava powder, and stabilized starch, the acetylated corn powder is obtained by mixing corn powder and chitosan in acetic acid solution system, and spray-drying to obtain the powder; the modified cassava powder is obtained by spraying acetic acid into the cassava, performing heat treatment at high temperature and cooling.
   USE - The rice flour composition is useful for preparing dumplings (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The rice flour composition comprises modified cassava powder having higher viscosity used to replace the existing glutinous rice flour; have more nutrients; can enrich the dumpling material and changes the taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing rice flour composition comprising (i) cleaning the rice and removing impurities, and soaking, taking out and grinding into rice powder slurry, and pumping the rice powder slurry into a filter press for filter pressing to obtain wet filter cake, (ii) adding acetylated corn powder, modified cassava powder and stabilized starch in wet filter cake, and primarily stirring to obtain the mixture, using a vacuum pump to form negative pressure in the pressure resistant stirring barrel, taking the mixture, filling pressure-resistant mixing tank with high-density carbon dioxide to a pressure of 20 MPa, returning to normal pressure after maintaining for 10-15 minutes, repeating filling the high density carbon dioxide and releasing pressure 2-3 times, wrapping the rice dumpling, and directly preparing rice dumpling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The rice flour composition comprises 100-150 pts. wt. rice flour, 100-150 pts. wt. actylated corn powder, 10-30 pts. wt. modified cassava powder, and 10-20 pts. wt. stabilized starch. Preferred Method: In the step (i), controlling the rice soaking solution water temperature at 20-25 degrees C, the pH value of the soaking liquid is 5.2-8. In step (i), rice is subjected for rough, fine grinding treatment to make the crushed rice powder particle more than 80 meshes. In step (i), the pressure of the press is 15-25 MPa, the pressing time is more than 30 minutes.

PN KR2018092686-A
TI Equipment for cultivating ornamental plant in e.g. home, has case forming internal space to fix plant, and control unit controlling lighting part, wind fan and water supplying part based on measured light quantity, humidity and temperature.
AU WOO Y J
   KIM J R
   KWON W J
   KWAK S H
   KIM Y N
AE UNIV DONG-A RES FOUND IND-ACAD COOP (UYDS-C)
GA 2018662118
AB    NOVELTY - The equipment (100) has a case forming an internal space to fix a plant. The case is formed with transparent material. A sensing part (110) measures light quantity, humidity, temperature and carbon dioxide concentration of the internal space. A lighting part (120) emits light to the internal space. A wind fan (130) inflows outer side air to the internal space. A water supplying part (140) supplies moisture to the internal space. A control unit (150) controls the lighting part, the wind fan and water supplying part based on the measured light quantity, humidity and temperature. The control unit controls the lighting part to emit the light to the internal space when the measured light quantity is less than predetermined value. The control unit controls the wind fan to flow the outer side air into the internal space when the measured carbon dioxide concentration and measured temperature are greater than the predetermined value.
   USE - Equipment for cultivating a plant i.e. ornamental plant in a home and an office.
   ADVANTAGE - The equipment adaptively controls an environmental parameter for the plant cultivation so as to perform efficient plant cultivation without intervention of human.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a plant cultivating equipment. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Plant cultivating equipment (100)
   Sensing part (110)
   Lighting part (120)
   Wind fan (130)
   Water supplying part (140)
   Control unit (150)

TI Refrigerant scroll compressor for use within heat pump with carbon dioxide as refrigerant, has main refrigerant outlet provided in wall of compressor housing and facing spirals in center of stationary spiral, which is always open.
AU KOZALLA P
   KLOTTEN T
AE HANON SYSTEMS (HACL-C)
GA 201863395N
AB    NOVELTY - The compressor (1) has compression chambers designed as a suction pressure chamber for suction of refrigerant, which is divided into an inner suction chamber and an outer suction chamber, a middle compression chamber that is divided into an inner middle compression chamber and an outer middle compression chamber and a refrigerant outlet chamber in which two inner end regions (3a) of a stationary spiral (3) and a mobile spiral face one another. Refrigerant outlets (4.1, 4.2) are arranged in a wall (2a) of a compressor housing (2), which is attached with a front portion of the spirals. Valves are used for opening and closing the refrigerant outlets. A main refrigerant outlet (5) in the wall of the compressor housing faces the spirals in a center of the stationary spiral, which is always open and does not comprise a valve.
   USE - Refrigerant scroll compressor for use within a heat pump with carbon dioxide as refrigerant.
   ADVANTAGE - The main refrigerant outlet is arranged in the wall of the compressor housing, and faces the spirals in the center of the stationary spiral, which is always open and does not comprise the valve such that the heat pump has high efficiency in an air conditioning mode.

PN JP2018103092-A
TI Manufacture of separation membrane for treating gas containing acidic gas, involves immersing inorganic porous support into liquid mixture and coating at preset immersion time and coating speed based on active length of porous support.
AU HASEGAWA H
   KOYAMA A
   WATANABE K
AE TOYO RUBBER IND CO LTD (TOYF-C)
GA 201852671J
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of separation membrane involves preparing a liquid mixture of an alkoxysilane, an acid catalyst, water, and an organic solvent, immersing an inorganic porous support in the liquid mixture, pulling the support from the liquid mixture to coat the surface of the support with the liquid mixture, and heat treating the porous support to form a separation layer having a polysiloxane network structure on the surface of the support. The inorganic porous support has active length (L0) of 40-60 mm. When immersion time in immersion process is (T0) and coating speed in coating process is (V0), and when active length (L1) of the porous support is 2L0-4L0, the coating speed (V1) is defined by preset relation and immersion time (T1) is equal to (T0), and when active length (L2) of porous support is 4L0-25L0, the coating speed (V2) is equal to (V0) and immersion time (T2) is defined by preset relation.
   USE - Manufacturing of separation membrane used for treating gas containing acidic gas e.g. nitrogen and carbon dioxide discharged from manufacturing equipment of town gas, hydrogen supply installation of fuel cell, sanitation facility of industrial fume, and liquefied-carbon-dioxide manufacturing equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces separation membrane having excellent gas separation performance and gas throughput based on the relationship between the size of the inorganic porous support and immersion time of the metal alkoxide solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The coating speed (V1) is defined by a relation: V1=V0x L1/L0. The immersion time (T2) is defined by a relation: T2=T0x L2/L0.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid mixture further comprises a metal salt having an affinity for an acidic gas. The metal salt is lithium, sodium, potassium, cesium, magnesium, calcium, nickel, iron or aluminum acetate, nitrate, carbonate, borate, or phosphate. The inorganic porous support comprises alpha -alumina as a main component. Preferred Property: The inorganic porous support has micropores having pore volume distribution of 100-300 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkoxysilane comprises 20-100 wt.% hydrocarbon group-containing trialkoxysilane.

TI Container for preserving fermented food e.g. bean paste, has vent hole that is set so that length of each vent hole of sealing object is set to specific range, so that inflow of oxygen to vent hole is blocked from exterior.
AU WATANABE T
AE W KK (WWWW-Non-standard)
   W KK (WWWW-Non-standard)
GA 2018536427
AB    NOVELTY - The container has a container main unit (1) which accommodates a fermented food. A cover unit (2) is provided to seal the opening portion of the container main unit. A sealing object (3) is provided. A strip-shaped vent hole (3B) is provided in the junction portion (3A) of the sealing object and the carbon dioxide in a container main unit is emitted. The vent hole is set so that the length of each vent hole of the sealing object is set to L and become the values 10-200 of L/W by making a width, so that inflow of oxygen to the vent hole is blocked from the exterior.
   USE - Container for preserving fermented food such as bean paste and kimchi.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the carbon dioxide levels in the container main unit are discharged according to the fermentation extent of the foodstuff preserved, the possibility of damaging even preserving the fermented food for a long period of time is eliminated. Since inflow of oxygen from the outside is blocked, the vent hole formed in the junction portions of the sealing object is enabled to maintain the quality of the fermented food for a long period of time.

PT P
PN CN108051550-A; CN108051550-B
TI Determining main acidic substance in a cell culture medium, involves before the start of culturing, measuring pH of the cell culture medium, and taking a medium from a bioreactor in which cells are cultured, and measuring the pH of medium.
AU ZHU C
   CHEN H
   LI L
   JIANG X
   ZHANG T
   FENG Y
   WANG Y
   LIU J
   CENG X
   WU Q
   YU P
   GU L
   WANG R
   WANG B
   ZHAO B
   CUI K
   ZENG X
AE JILIN GUANJIE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 2018408529
AB    NOVELTY - Determining main acidic substance in a cell culture medium, involves: (a) before the start of culturing, measuring pH of the cell culture medium and recording as A value; (b) in the course of culturing, taking a medium from a bioreactor in which cells are cultured, and measuring the pH of medium and recording as B value; (c) shaking the cell culture medium; after the gas has overflowed, measuring pH value of culture medium, denoted as C value; and (d) comparing A value, B value, and C value to determine the main species of the acidic substance. If the C value - B value is not greater than 0.2, and the A value - C value is not greater than .2, then carbon dioxide and lactic acid are the main acid substances in the cell culture medium. If the C value - B value is not greater than 0.2, and the A value - C value is less than or equal to 0.2, carbon dioxide is used in the cell culture medium as main acidic species. If the C value - B value is less than or equal to 0.2, and the A value - C value is not greater than 0.2, lactic acid is the main acidic species in the cell culture medium.
   USE - The method is useful for determining main acidic substance in a cell culture medium (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can rapidly and accurately determine the types of main acidic substances in the culture medium. It has improved efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: Determining main acidic substance in cell culture medium further involves: (e) according to the analysis result of step (d), adopting different measures to adjust the pH value in the cell culture medium: when the main acidic substance in the cell culture medium is carbon dioxide, compressed air is blown into the cell culture medium to remove carbon dioxide from the cell culture medium; when the main acid substance in the cell culture medium is lactic acid, an alkaline substance is added to the bioreactor to neutralize the lactic acid; when the main acid substances in the cell culture medium are carbon dioxide and lactic acid, compressed air is introduced into the bioreactor, and an alkaline substance is added. The compressed air is introduced from the bottom layer of the cell culture medium; preferably, the compressed air is also introduced from the surface layer of the cell culture medium; more preferably the pressure of the compressed air introduced from the bottom layer and/or the compressed air introduced from the surface layer is 0.015-0.02 MPa; most preferably, the surface layer continues to pass in compressed air, and the bottom layer is analyzed according to the result of step (d). The method repeats steps (b)-(d) while introducing compressed air and/or adding a basic substance, until the C value - B value is less than or equal to 0.2 and/or A value - C value is less than or equal to 0.2. It also involves: (f) monitoring the atmospheric pressure of the bioreactor and removing the gas in time so as the bioreactor gas pressure is maintained at a fixed value; preferably, monitoring is automated monitoring; more preferably, the automated monitoring is real-time monitoring; preferably, the gas is discharged through an exhaust valve, more preferably, the exhaust valve is an automatic exhaust valve; most preferably, the opening degree of the automatic exhaust valve is adjustable; preferably, the fixed pressure value of the bioreactor is 0.005-0.01 MPa. The method further involves condensing the gas prior to venting the gas; and exhaust gas treatment of the exhausted gas. The pH value in step (a), step (b) and/or step (c) passes through a pH meter or pH test paper is measured. Preferred Device: The bioreactor is a large-scale bioreactor, preferably, the large-scale bioreactor has a volume of 3000-6000 L. Preferred Cell: The cells are plant cells or animal cells; preferably, the cells are animal cells; more preferably, the cells are animal cells used to produce antigens or viruses.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PN CN108002409-A
TI Preparation of injection-level medical sodium bicarbonate comprises preparing sodium crude product, dissolving in distilled water, stirring, filtering, vacuum concentrating, cooling, crystallizing, filtering, washing and drying.
AU LIU Q
   TAN Z
   GUO B
   WU N
   WU Z
   WU T
AE TIANSHENG PHARM GROUP CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201837515V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of injection-level medical sodium bicarbonate comprises adding sodium carbonate powder in distilled water, stirring until completely dissolved, filtering, where concentration of sodium carbonate in filtrate is 0.10-0.50 g/mL, discharging air in reaction device, introducing carbon dioxide gas, stirring, filtering, separating white crystal slurry and washing to obtain sodium crude product; adding obtained crude sodium bicarbonate in distilled water, stirring until completely dissolved, filtering, vacuum concentrating, cooling, crystallizing, filtering, washing and drying to product.
   USE - Method for preparing injection-level medical sodium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces sodium bicarbonate with yield of more than 80% and content of 99.5-100.5%, which meets Chinese Pharmacopoeia 2015 injection grade pharmaceutical sodium bicarbonate standard. The product has uniform particle size, good liquidity and low production cost and is easy to filter and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of injection-level medical sodium bicarbonate comprises adding sodium carbonate powder in 25-35 degrees C distilled water, stirring at 25-60 revolutions/minute until completely dissolved, filtering, where concentration of sodium carbonate in filtrate is 0.10-0.50 g/mL, discharging air in reaction device, introducing 0.01-0.03 MPa carbon dioxide gas, stirring at 25-35 degrees C for 1-5 hours, filtering, separating out large amount of white crystal slurry and washing to obtain sodium crude product; adding obtained crude sodium bicarbonate in 25-35 degrees C distilled water, stirring at 25-60 revolutions/minute until to completely dissolved, filtering, vacuum concentrating at 30-35 degrees C under - 0.04-- 0.07 MPa, cooling, crystallizing, filtering, washing and drying to product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sodium carbonate powder contains 96-98% sodium carbonate. Preferred Method: The drying process is carried out at 28-35 degrees C for 4-10 hours.

PN CN107988796-A; CN107988796-B
TI Preparation of plastic preservative film by washing polymethyl methacrylate microsphere with ethanol solution, drying, putting in mixed solution of hydrogen peroxide and sulfuric acid, heating, naturally cooling, and washing.
AU CHEN Q
   GUAN D
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
   CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM ENG SCI & TECHNIC (XKLI-C)
GA 2018393785
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of plastic preservative film includes washing polymethyl methacrylate microsphere with ethanol solution, drying, putting in a mixed solution of hydrogen peroxide and sulfuric acid, heating, naturally cooling, washing with distilled water, adding polyvinylidene chloride, emulsifier and water, magnetically stirring until forming uniform and stable composite emulsion, performing corona treatment on surface of nonwoven fabric, uniformly coating the surface of nonwoven fabric with the composite emulsion using a coating machine, and infrared drying.
   USE - Preparation of plastic preservative film used for kiwi fruit packaging (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has low energy consumption, and low cost, and provides plastic preservative film, which has high carbon dioxide/oxygen permeation ratio and low carbon dioxide concentration, maintains good gas adjusting effect under low-oxygen environment, and prolongs the shelf life of fruit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of plastic preservative film comprises washing polymethyl methacrylate microsphere with ethanol solution, drying, putting in a mixed solution of hydrogen peroxide and sulfuric acid, heating, naturally cooling, washing with distilled water, adding polyvinylidene chloride, emulsifier and water, magnetically stirring until forming uniform and stable composite emulsion, performing corona treatment on surface of nonwoven fabric, uniformly coating the surface of nonwoven fabric with the composite emulsion using a coating machine, infrared drying, laser irradiating the coating layer of nonwoven fabric, so that the polymethyl methacrylate and polymer molecular chain of polyvinylidene chloride in the coating layer are partially broken, increasing the gas permeability of the coating layer, and obtaining the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Method: The laser is carbon dioxide laser, disc laser or optical fiber laser having laser frequency of 4.5-6x 1014 Hz and radiation distance of 0.2-0.5 m. The laser irradiation is conducted for 5-10 seconds.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the mixed solution of hydrogen peroxide and sulfuric acid, the volume ratio of hydrogen peroxide and sulfuric acid is 3:7-4:7.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The coating machine is automatic whole plate coating machine or automatic selection type coating machine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The emulsifier is sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, lauric acid monoglyceride or monopalmitate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The particle diameter of polymethyl methacrylate microsphere is 30-100 -mm. The heating step is conducted at 80-90 degrees C for 50-70 minutes. The composite emulsion comprises (based on the total weight of 100 pts. wt.) 10-20 pts. wt. polymethyl methacrylate microsphere, 25-30 pts. wt. polyvinylidene chloride, 1-2 pts. wt. emulsifier, and 48-64 pts. wt. water. The nonwoven fabric is Terylene (RTM: polyester), polypropylene fiber, polyamide, urethane elastic fiber or Orlon (RTM: acrylic fiber). The corona treatment is conducted at 5000-10000 V/m2. The coating thickness is 0.2-0.5 mm.
