PN CN107970884-A
TI Preparation of lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve loading filler, involves scattering surface-treated lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve in solvent, mixing with bonding component and additive, distributing and molding.
AU QIAN Z
   GONG Q
AE SUZHOU SIMEITE SURFACE MATERIAL TECHNOLO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201835208D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve loading filler, involves (i) surface-treating powdery lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve with a silane coupling agent to change its surface affinity where surface amination enhances carbon dioxide adsorption capacity and surface alkylation increases hydrophobicity and enhances affinity for organic phase, (ii) scattering surface-treated lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve in solvent, then adding bonding component and additive, stirring and distributing into a more stable slurry-like dispersion system and (iii) molding.
   USE - Preparation of lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve loading filler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the space occupation and reduces energy consumption by pressure swing adsorption oxygen production technology. The loading packing ensures that the air treatment capacity per kg of molecular sieve is not less than 100 L/minute, and oxygen output per kg of molecular sieves is not less than 10L/minute, and unit power consumption is not higher than 0.5 kWh/Nm3.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve loading filler, involves (i) surface-treating powdery lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve with a silane coupling agent to change its surface affinity where surface amination enhances carbon dioxide adsorption capacity and surface alkylation increases hydrophobicity and enhances affinity for organic phase, (ii) scattering surface-treated lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve in solvent, then adding bonding component and additive, stirring and distributing into a more stable slurry-like dispersion system, (iii) molding slurry-like dispersion system through a loaded packing to obtain lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve having a loaded packing structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (ii), the bonding component is selected from inorganic bonding components or organic bonding components. The bonding component accounts for 5-80% of the total solid content in the slurry. The inorganic bonding component is selected from a group consisting of silica sol, aluminum sol, kaolin and/or attapulgite. The inorganic bonding component occupies 5-20% of the solid content and 10-80% of the solid content. In step (2), the additive is selected from ethanol, glycerin, lithium chloride and/or potassium chloride, preferably ethanol or glycerol, or lithium chloride and potassium chloride. The additive is added in an amount of 0-10 %mass of slurry. Preferred Process: In step (iii), carrier load molding is performed by selecting porous substrate as a carrier, cleaning, immersing in the slurry-like dispersion system, taking out and drying, repeating foregoing immersing-drying process until the desired load is reached and finally heating load carrier and drying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In step (i), the lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve is dispersed in aromatic hydrocarbon solvents prior to surface treatment of lithium-type low-silicon molecular sieve, and then silane coupling agent is added for surface treatment. Preferred Components: The solvent is N-methylpyrrolidone, dichloromethane, ethylene glycol, ethanol and/or water, preferably water. Preferred Composition: The solvent accounts for 20-90% of the mass fraction of the slurry. The solvent is selected from a group consisting of N-methylpyrrolidone, dichloromethane, ethylene glycol and ethanol organic solvent. The organic bonding component is selected accounting for 10-40% of the mass of the slurry. The mass ratio of molecular sieve powder and organic polymer is 1:1-1:4.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The organic bonding component is selected from polyamideimide, polyamino aromatic hydrocarbon and/or polymethylmethacrylate. Preferred Process: In step (iii), carrier-free self-forming is carried out film-forming slurry-like dispersion in step (ii) by coating, extruding into hollow fibers or adding into other suitable structures, immersing in water for several days to remove residual materials and vacuum drying.

PN CN107827138-A
TI Preparation of super-fine active calcium            carbonate, involves placing quicklime into digestion            container, adding hot water to fully digest, making            calcium hydroxide slurry, and adding calcium hydroxide            slurry to reaction container.
AU LI T
   LI G
   CAO J
   ZHAN Z
AE ANHUI XINTAO NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018251071
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of super-fine active calcium                carbonate involves placing quicklime into a                digestion container, adding hot water to fully                digest, making calcium hydroxide slurry, adding the                calcium hydroxide slurry to a reaction container,                reducing the temperature of the slurry, inletting                carbon dioxide into the container, carbonizing,                adding crystal modifier, continuously reacting,                adding dispersant, continuously carbonizing,                heating the slurry, adding coating agent to calcium                hydroxide slurry for surface activation coating,                dehydrating the reaction slurry, drying, and                crushing.
   USE - Preparation method of super-fine active                calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of super-fine                active calcium carbonate with improved carbonizing                efficiency, accelerated preparation speed, improved                quality, and reduced agglomeration phenomenon, by                economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of super-fine active                calcium carbonate involves (1) placing quicklime                into a digestion container, adding hot water to                fully digest, and making calcium hydroxide slurry                with concentration of 7-13%, (2) adding the calcium                hydroxide slurry to a reaction container, reducing                the temperature of the calcium hydroxide slurry to                20-35 degrees C, inletting carbon dioxide into the                reaction container to carbonize, carbonizing for                5-10 minutes, adding crystal modifier, continuously                reacting for 20-30 minutes, adding dispersant, and                continuously carbonizing for 15-25 minutes to                complete the carbonization process, (3) heating the                slurry at 65-80 degrees C, and adding coating agent                to calcium hydroxide slurry for surface activation                coating, where the coating time is 1-2 hours, and                (4) dehydrating the reaction slurry, drying, and                crushing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of                  carbon dioxide is 1-5 L/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The crystal modifier                  comprises a mixture of citrate and glucose in                  ratio of 3:2. The crystal modifier is added in an                  amount of 0.1-2 wt.% of the slurry. The                  dispersant comprises a mixture of sodium                  dodecylbenzenesulfonate and sulfate in ratio of                  1:2. The dispersant is added in an amount of                  0.5-2.5 wt.% of the content of calcium carbonate                  in the slurry. The coating agent comprises a                  mixture of stearic acid and sorbitan fatty acid                  in ratio of 5:1. The coating agent is added in an                  amount of 1-3 wt.% of the amount of calcium                  carbonate in the slurry.

PT P
PN US2018057341-A1; US10196254-B2
TI Portable container for transporting and dispensing beverages such as beer, has lid that is pivotally attached to lower receiver by axle having open position, closed position and recess for receiving faucet when handle is in down position.
AU KINCAID D A
   KALBAS T
   WIRTZ D
   BROUGHTON A
   MCLAREN S
   MACKAY I
AE PIK SIX LLC (PIKS-Non-standard)
   KINCAID D A (KINC-Individual)
   KALBAS T (KALB-Individual)
   WIRTZ D (WIRT-Individual)
   BROUGHTON A (BROU-Individual)
   MCLAREN S (MCLA-Individual)
   MACKAY I (MACK-Individual)
GA 2018163906
AB    NOVELTY - The container has a lower receiver with an internal compartment that has bottom and circular sidewalls extending from bottom to rim at top of lower receiver and pair of wheels. A retaining ring with cylindrical interior surface, top side, bottom side and exterior surface frictionally engages internal compartment of lower receiver. The retaining ring has compartment for receiving carbon dioxide (CO2) container and regulator with control valve. A lid has open position, closed position and recess for receiving faucet (190) which dispense liquid when handle (150) is in down position.
   USE - Portable container for transporting Sixth Barrel or Cornelius kegs (RTM: Stainless steel container for home brewed beer) and dispensing beverages such as beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The portable container and dispenser are specifically designed to hold securely in place Sixth Barrel or Cornelius keg. The faucet tucks into groove on the top of the container providing protection during transport when placed in the down position. The regulator and carbon dioxide are attached on the outside of the container for easy access and are protected by hinged door that opens and closes. The sidewalls are insulated to minimize the temperature differential between the internal compartment and the exterior and to minimize air exchange.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of placement of Sixth Barrel or Cornelius kegs inside the portable container.
   Handle (150)
   Locking slot (180)
   Faucet (190)
   Hand slot (220)

PT P
PN CN107673379-A; CN107673379-B
TI Preparing high-purity magnesium carbonate involves preparing magnesium hydroxide solution, ultrasonic atomizing magnesium hydroxide solution into magnesium hydroxide mist, and placing magnesium hydroxide atomized material.
AU FAN Y
   TANG M
   ZHANG R
AE MIANYANG YUANDA NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (MIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201814530E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity magnesium carbonate involves preparing magnesium hydroxide solution with a mass fraction of 40-60%, ultrasonic atomizing magnesium hydroxide solution into magnesium hydroxide mist. The magnesium hydroxide atomized material is placed into the carbonization tower, passed carbon dioxide to carbonization reaction, controlled carbonization reaction temperature to 65-85 degrees C for 3-5 hours to obtain intermediate magnesium carbonate material.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity magnesium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-purity magnesium carbonate which has high purity, greatly improves production efficiency in the industrial production process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-purity magnesium carbonate involves preparing magnesium hydroxide solution with a mass fraction of 40-60%, ultrasonic atomizing magnesium hydroxide solution into magnesium hydroxide mist. The magnesium hydroxide atomized material is placed into the carbonization tower, passed carbon dioxide to carbonization reaction, controlled carbonization reaction temperature to 65-85 degrees C for 3-5 hours to obtain intermediate magnesium carbonate material. The intermediate magnesium carbonate material is heated at 80-90 degrees C, added water, added into a stainless steel spherical container, added curing stabilizer, sealed, placed spherical container on a four-axis grinding apparatus, and four-axis grinding apparatus is started, where stainless steel spherical container randomly rotate for 2-3 hours. The feed port of the stainless steel spherical container is sealed by a screw cap, and the screw cap is sealed and connected to be flush with the surface of the stainless steel spherical container. The solids and liquids are separated and the solid obtained is dried and calcined to obtain high-purity magnesium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ultrasonic atomization power is 1-1010 kW, the frequency is 1.8-2.4 MHz, and atomization rate is 80-200 kg/h. The magnesium hydroxide atomized matter is mixed with carbon dioxide, and carbon dioxide is used as the carrier gas flow of the magnesium hydroxide atomized substance, passed into the carbonization tower. The carbon dioxide content in the carbon dioxide gas is 99%, and the pressure at the top of the carbonization tower is controlled at 1.2-1.5 MPa. The stainless steel spherical vessel has a thermal insulation layer, rotational speed of the active shaft of the four-axis polishing device is 100-150 rpm, and the random transformation frequency is 30-60 second. The crystal form control agent is atomized by crystal form control agent through ultrasonic atomization, crystal form control agent is formed. The atomized matter is passed into the carbonization tower, where ultrasonic atomization power is 5-10 kW, the frequency is 1.8-2.4 MHz, and the atomization rate is 1-5 kg/h. Preferred Composition: The crystal form control agent comprises 20-25 pts. wt. vegetable oleic acid, 5-8 pts. wt. nonylphenol, 1-3 pts. wt. 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride, 1-3 pts. wt. triethanolamine, 1-3 pts. wt. guar gum, 2-5 pts. wt. alkyl glycosides, and 80-100 pts. wt. water. The amount of the curing stabilizer is 0.01-0.05 times of the intermediate magnesium carbonate material. The aging stabilizer is mixture of mannitol, cellulose ether and polyacrylic acid in a weight ratio of 1:2:1. The dry roasting is performed in a roasting furnace at 150-200 degrees C at rotary roaster speed of 5-10 revolutions per minute.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); V06 (Electromechanical Transducers and Small Machines)
MC A10-E05B; A12-W11; E11-P; E34-B02; V06-V01N
IP C01F-005/24

PN JP2018016614-A
TI Production of hydrocarbon-based compound used in hydrocarbon fuel, involves mixing water and liquid material, and applying impact force to produce mixture comprising higher amount of hydrocarbon-based compound than that of liquid material.
AU IMANAKA T
   TAKEMOTO T
AE SI ENERGY KK (SIEN-Non-standard)
GA 201809890Y
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrocarbon-based compound involves (a) preparing functional water (A) comprising carbon dioxide-containing nanobubbles and active oxygen, and (b) mixing the functional water and a liquid material (B) comprising a hydrocarbon-based compound, and applying an impact force. The content of the hydrocarbon-based compound in the mixture to which the impact force is applied, is higher than the content of the hydrocarbon-based compound in the liquid material.
   USE - Production of hydrocarbon-based compound used in hydrocarbon fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical, and efficiently produces hydrocarbon-based compound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus (30) for producing hydrocarbon-based compound, which consists of a tank (31) comprising carbon dioxide-containing nanobubbles and functional water, a tank (33) comprising the liquid material, a reaction tank (41) for reacting the functional water with the liquid material, a supply line (37) connected to the tanks (31) and (33), a nozzle (39) arranged in the reaction vessel at a tip portion, and a supply line (s2) for applying the impact force by colliding with a wall surface (41a) of the reaction tank. The functional water is supplied from the tank (31) and the liquid material is supplied from the tank (33). The functional water and the liquid material are discharged from the nozzle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of the apparatus for producing the hydrocarbon-based compound.
   Apparatus (30)
   Tanks (31,33)
   Premixer (35)
   Supply line (37)
   Nozzle (39)
   Reaction tank (41)
   Wall surface (41a)
   Separation tank (45)
   Functional water (A)
   Liquid material (B)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The supply line comprises a premixer (35) for premixing the functional water and the liquid material, before discharging the functional water and the liquid material from the nozzle. The apparatus further consists of separation tank (45) for separating the mixture into a water phase and a phase comprising hydrocarbon-based compound, a functional water production unit for producing the functional water and supplying it to the tank (31), a supply unit (u1) for supplying air or carbon dioxide in the form of nanobubbles in water, a supply unit (u2) for supplying oxygen to water, and a light irradiating unit for irradiating light on water. The functional water production unit further consists of a supply unit (u3) for supplying carbon dioxide to water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The functional water is prepared by (a1) supplying carbon dioxide-containing nanobubbles to water, and (a2) supplying oxygen to water and light irradiating water in the presence of a photocatalyst to generate active oxygen. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-containing nanobubbles are air nanobubbles or carbon dioxide nanobubbles. The water is pure water. Preferred Composition: The functional water comprises 10 ppm or more dissolved oxygen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid material comprises one or more components chosen from 5-27C saturated hydrocarbon, light oil, kerosene oil, gasoline, and jet fuel. Preferred Properties: The average carbon number of the hydrocarbon-based compound in the mixture to which the impact force is applied, is within plus minus 20% of the average carbon number of the hydrocarbon-based compound in the liquid material.

PN CN107641188-A
TI Epoxide-terminated polyurethane material comprises polyester thermoplastic polyurethane , polyether glycol, diisocyanate, modified hollow glass microspheres, silicone resin oligomer, white carbon, foaming agent, and foam stabilizer.
AU KE G
   KE Y
AE XIAMEN TONGXINYUAN PACKAGING PROD CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
GA 201810887T
AB    NOVELTY - Epoxide-terminated polyurethane (ETPU) material comprises 70-80 pts. wt. polyester thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU), 20-30 pts. wt. polyether glycol, 20-30 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 5-10 pts. wt. modified hollow glass microspheres, 10-20 pts. wt. silicone resin oligomer, 5-8 pts. wt. white carbon, 5-8 pts. wt. foaming agent, and 8-10 pts. wt. foam stabilizer.
   USE - The ETPU material is useful for preparing sole pad (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ETPU material: has light weight, high elasticity, and improves the ability of materials to absorb impact loads.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the epoxide-terminated polyurethane material, comprising (i) taking polyester TPU, polyether glycol, diisocyanate, silicone resin oligomer, and white carbon black, mixing uniformly by high mixer to obtain mixed raw material, (ii) taking resulting mixed feedstock from the main feed port obtained in the step (i) into a twin-screw extruder, taking modified hollow glass microspheres from the screw extruder side feed opening into the extruder, extruding, and granulating to obtain modified TPU particles, (iii) adding foaming agent and foam stabilizer in the reactor, accessing to carbon dioxide, creating carbon dioxide atmosphere, taking modified TPU obtained in step (ii) into the reactor, setting the pressure to 8-9 MPa, temperature at 100-105 degrees C, and reaching the set value insulation pressure for 1 hour, (iv) finishing insulating and packing, quickly opening the relief discharge valve to obtain ETPU particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyether diol is polytetramethylene ether glycol, polyoxypropylene glycol and polyoxypropylene-ethylene oxide copolyol. The modified hollow glass microspheres are hollow glass microspheres through sodium hydroxide caustic washing, hollow glass microspheres are modified by silane coupling agent. The silane coupling agent is gamma -aminopropyl triethoxysilane, gamma -(2,3-epoxy-propoxy) propyltrimethoxysilane, gamma -(methacryloyloxy) propyltrimethoxysilane and vinyl triethoxysilane. The silicone oligomer comprises 6 pts. wt. dimethyl diethyl silane, 3 pts. wt. methyl triethylsilane, 3 pts. wt. phenyl triethylsilane and 2 pts. wt. diphenyldiethylsilane. The foaming agent is prepared by mixing water and ethanol according to the mass ratio of 1:1. In the step (iii), providing with a stirrer, the speed of the stirrer in the heating period is set to 500 revolution/minute (rpm), and insulating dwell time speed is set to 250 rpm.

PN CN107569416-A
TI Preparing sunscreen cream by adding e.g. water to melon skin, sonicating, filtering, adding e.g. inoculant to filtrate, fermenting, adding e.g. cactus stem, crushing, extracting, distilling, stirring e.g. residue and concentrating mixture.
AU WANG L
   LU L
AE CHANGZHOU YUCHUN CHEM CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201805535Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sunscreen cream, involves (a) collecting watermelon skin, adding e.g. water, stirring, adding the mixture into a container, adjusting pH and standing, (b) sonicating container, filtering, adding e.g. inoculant to filtrate and fermenting, (c) mixing fermented mixture with e.g. cactus stem, crushing, subjecting the crushed mixture to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, distilling the extract and collecting the distillation residue, and (d) adding e.g. distillation residue and lanolin to a stirrer, stirring and concentrating the mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sunscreen cream.
   ADVANTAGE - The sunscreen cream is effective for a long duration, causes no stimulation to human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing sunscreen cream, involves (a) taking watermelon, removing the pulp, collecting skin, adding additive and water to the skin, stirring, adding the mixture to a container, adjusting the pH to 5-6 using hydrochloric acid and allowing to stand, (b) sonicating container, filtering, adding soybean meal and inoculant to the filtrate and fermenting in a fermenter, (c) mixing the fermented mixture with cactus stem and ethanol solution, crushing, subjecting the crushed mixture to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, distilling the resulting extract and collecting the distillation residue, and (d) adding 60-65 pts. wt. distillation residue, 20-25 pts. wt. sodium alginate, 15-18 pts. wt. lanolin, 4-6 pts. wt. titanium dioxide and 1-2 pts. wt. hydroxylysine to a stirrer, uniformly stirring and concentrating the stirred mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The additive comprises tannin and polyvinyl pyrrolidone mixed in a mass ratio of 4-(6:1). The inoculant comprises Aspergillus oryzae and yeast mixed in a mass ratio of 3:5. In step (a), the melon skin, additive and water have a mass ratio of (4-6):3:(9-11). In step (b), the filtrate, soybean meal and inoculant have a mass ratio of (6-8):(3-5):(1-2). In step (b), the fermentation is carried out at 37-42 degrees C for 1-2 days using carbon dioxide for protection. In step (c), the fermented mixture, cactus stem and ethanol solution have a mass ratio of (4-6):(5-8):(9-11).

PN CN107541718-A; WO2019041489-A1; CN107541718-B
TI Preparation of hydrotalcite based magneto-optical thin film material involves placing PTFE substrate into cathode plate, adding e.g. metal ion, placing in vacuum chamber, heating, reacting with mixed gas, washing, drying and curing.
AU DAI X
AE SUZHOU YUNSHU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
   SUZHOU YUNSHU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201803776P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hydrotalcite based magneto-optical thin film material involves placing polytetrafluoroethylene substrate into the groove of the cathode plate, adding metal ion solution and urea solution, placing in a closed and evacuated vacuum chamber, heating the cathode plate and anode plate, starting the plasma generator power supply of vacuum chamber and introducing mixed gas of sulfur, hydrogen and argon, reacting to obtain magneto-optical film material precursor, placing the film precursor in carbon dioxide atmosphere, washing with deionized water, drying, and curing the precursor.
   USE - Preparation of hydrotalcite based magneto-optical thin film material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and provides uniform and compact hydrotalcite based magneto-optical thin film material having excellent electrical performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of hydrotalcite based magneto-optical thin film material involves placing polytetrafluoroethylene substrate into the groove of the cathode plate, adding metal ion solution and urea solution of volume ratio 1:1, placing in a closed and evacuated vacuum chamber at 103-104 Pa, heating the cathode plate at 550-650 degrees C and anode plate at 260-350 degrees C, starting the plasma generator power supply of vacuum chamber and introducing mixed gas of sulfur, hydrogen and argon of ratio 1:2:1, reacting for 12-14 hours to obtain hydrotalcite-based magneto-optical thin film material precursor, placing the film precursor in carbon dioxide atmosphere, washing with deionized water until it gets neutral, drying for more than 12hours at 64 degrees C, and curing the precursor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal ion comprises magnesium, nickel, calcium, copper, manganese, aluminum, chromium, gallium, lanthanide, iron and/or vanadium. The concentration of urea solution is 2.5 mol/L.

PN CN114815927-A
TI Large power supply temperature control system for            power distribution station, has heater that is provided            for absorbing heat and converting into carbon dioxide,            and finally returned to carbon dioxide compression pump            through four-way valve.
AU ZHANG J
   JIANG Z
   ZHOU H
AE TAIZHOU POWER SUPPLY BRANCH STATE GRID (SGCC-C)
GA 2022A3464F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an internal heat exchanger (10)                and an external heat exchanger (8) that are                connected with an external loop that is                sequentially connected with a first throttle valve                (7) and a third interface (3c) of a four-way valve                (3). The four-way valve is provided for controlling                the communication between a first interface (3a)                and the third interface in heating mode, while a                second interface (3b) communicates with a fourth                interface (3d) to switch the system to heating                mode. The high-temperature supercritical carbon                dioxide is passed through the internal heat                exchanger and the external heat exchanger in turn                to dissipate heat to power module and is converted                into two-phase carbon dioxide, and then passed                through the first throttle valve for throttling and                pressure relief, and passed through air exchange. A                heater (4) is provided for absorbing heat and                converting into carbon dioxide, and finally                returned to a carbon dioxide compression pump (1)                through the four-way valve.
   USE - Large power supply temperature control system                for power distribution station of distribution                network facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The large power supply temperature control                system is simple in structure, convenient to                control, by using supercritical carbon dioxide                medium self-heat absorption characteristics                matching flow and control of the heat exchange                structure, at the same time, combined with four-way                valve for reversing adjustment, and realizes the                fast switching and stable operation of                refrigerating and heating double-model.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                large power supply temperature control system of                power distribution station. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)

TI Method for testing carbon dioxide (CO2) oil            displacement asphaltene precipitation distribution            mode, involves carrying out multi-point sampling to            long core after oil displacement, and obtaining            distribution rule of precipitation at macroscopic            layer.
AU YANG S
   SHEN J
   WANG Q
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2021D5574H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining 12 blocks of low                permeability reservoir standard rock core, testing                before displacement, porosity, Amott-Harvey wetting                index. A front long rock core salt water saturation                and distribution test is performed. A carbon                dioxide displacement is determined after                permeability reducing detection. A CO2 displacement                wetting indicator is determined. A distribution                characteristic detection is performed after                absorbing asphaltene precipitation. A blockage hole                throat of the carbon dioxide precipitation                distribution characteristic is detected. A benzene                + alcohol reverse displacement is performed to                remove adsorption state of the precipitation.                Multi-point sampling is performed to the long core                after oil displacement. Distribution rule of the                asphaltenes precipitation at a macroscopic layer is                obtained.
   USE - Method for testing carbon dioxide (CO2) oil                displacement asphaltene precipitation distribution                mode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quantitatively evaluating                the comprehensive change index of reservoir rock                wettability after carbon dioxide (CO2) oil                displacement, and the influence of asphaltene                precipitation on the reservoir seepage capability                and fluid distribution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for testing carbon dioxide oil displacement                asphaltene precipitation distribution mode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system for testing carbon dioxide oil displacement                asphaltene precipitation distribution mode.
   Core holder (1)
   Confining pressure pump (2)
   Vacuum pump (3)
   Injecting pump (4)

TI Carbon dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane dispersion comprises poly(carbonate oxime ether)polyol, diisocyanate, hydrophilic chain extender, small molecule chain extender and neutralizer.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHAO Q
   WANG X
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2018596372
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane dispersion comprises 80-110 pts. wt. poly(carbonate oxime ether)polyol, 19-32 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 6-9 pts. wt. hydrophilic chain extender, 3-7 pts. wt. small molecule chain extender and 2.3-3.5 pts. wt. neutralizer.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane dispersion.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyurethane dispersion has initial viscosity of 12-17, viscosity of 26.5-32 hours, and peel strength of 32.5-42.5 N/20 mm at 180 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane dispersion comprises 80-110 pts. wt. poly(carbonate oxime ether)polyol, 19-32 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 6-9 pts. wt. hydrophilic chain extender, 3-7 pts. wt. small molecule chain extender and 2.3-3.5 pts. wt. neutralizer, where the poly(carbonate oxime ether)polyol of formula (I).
   x, y, z = less than or equal to 1-50; and
   m, n, p = less than or equal to 1-80.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing carbon dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane dispersion comprising (i) adding poly(carbonate-ether)polyol and diisocyanate into the reaction vessel under nitrogen gas atmosphere, adding solvent, mixing and reacting uniformly to obtain first intermediate, (ii) adding hydrophilic chain extender into the first intermediate, adding small molecule chain extender, catalyst and solvent and reacting to obtain second intermediate, and (iii) neutralizing the second intermediate with a neutralizing agent, adding water to emulsifying, and distilling under reduced pressure to obtain final product; and
   (2) carbon dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane pressure-sensitive adhesive comprising 100-240 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane dispersion, 24.5-37.5 pts. wt. heavy calcium carbonate, 0.3-1.25 pts. wt. meteorological white carbon black, 0.08-0.41 pts. wt. aqueous dispersant, 0.05-022 pts. wt. aqueous antifoaming agent, 0.45-1.02 pts. wt. aqueous thickening agent and 4.85-14.8 pts. wt. aqueous curing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction temperature of the first step is 85-90 degrees C, and the reaction time is 2-4 hours. The reaction temperature of the second step is 60-65 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1.5-3 hours. Preferred Components: The diisocyanate is isophorone diisocyanate, 1,6-hexamethylene diisocyanate, 4,4'-dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate, hexylene diisocyanate, 1,4-cyclohexane diisocyanate, tetramethyl meta-xylylene diisocyanate, cyclohexane dimethylene diisocyanate, norbornane diisocyanate or methylcyclohexyl diisocyanate. The hydrophilic chain extender is dimethylolpropionic acid, dimethylol butyric acid, N-methyldiethanolamine, N-butyl diethanolamine, 2,2'-iminodiethanol or trihydroxyethylamine. The small molecule chain extender is ethylene glycol, butylene glycol or diethylene glycol. The aqueous dispersant is BYK-034, BYK-085 and BYK-182; and the aqueous antifoaming agent is BYK-024, BYK-020 and BYK-028.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The preparing poly(carbonate-ether)polyol by heating the reaction kettle at 50-80 degrees C, evacuating and charging with carbon dioxide, and cooling to room temperature, adding double metal cyanide catalyst and citric acid into the reactor under the protection of carbon dioxide and reacting to obtain mixture, heating the reaction kettle at 50-80 degrees C, evacuating and charging with carbon dioxide, and cooling to room temperature, adding above mixture and epoxy compound monomer into the reaction kettle, stirring and passing carbon dioxide into the kettle through a carbon dioxide pressure regulator, placing the reaction kettle in a constant temperature bath for polymerization.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A81 (Adhesives and binders - including chipboard.); G03 (Adhesives - excluding dispensers. PN CN113463131-A; CN113463131-B
TI Preparation of copper monoatomic catalyst used in            electrode for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon            dioxide to methane, involves mixing copper source,            reducing agent and water, adding capping agent, mixing,            adding carbon powder, mixing, heating, and            pickling.
AU CHEN G
   WANG J
   LI F
   LIN H
   SU L
   LIN S
   LUO S
AE ZHONGSHAN POWER SUPPLY BUREAU GUANDONG (CSPG-C)
   GUANGDONG POWER GRID CO LTD (CSPG-C)
GA 2021C00785
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of copper monoatomic catalyst                involves (1) mixing copper source, reducing agent                and water to obtain solution of light blue                transparent solution, (2) adding capping agent to                the light blue transparent solution, mixing, adding                carbon powder, mixing, heating, and pickling.
   USE - Preparation of copper monoatomic catalyst used                in electrode for electrocatalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide to methane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation and excellent                expansibility, and is economical and suitable for                large-scale production. The copper monoatomic                catalyst has high selectivity and current density                for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to                methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                electrode, which comprises electrode component and                the copper monoatomic catalyst coated on the                electrode component.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The copper source is                  copper(II) chloride dihydrate, anhydrous cupric                  chloride and/or copper sulfate.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  the copper source to the reducing agent is                  1:10-1:20. The molar ratio of the capping agent                  to the copper source is 4:1-8:1. The mass ratio                  of copper in the copper source to the carbon                  powder is 1:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is                  glucose and/or 1,2-hexadecanediol. The capping                  agent is hexadecylamine and/or oleylamine. The                  reagent used in the pickling process is acetic                  acid.Preferred Property: The concentration of the                  acetic acid is 10-100 %mass.

AB    NOVELTY - Methane formation apparatus comprises culture                tank (1) which cultures a methane producer by a                culture solution, gas supply apparatus (2) which                supplies the carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas which                the methane producer requires for the culture tank,                and generated gas extracting device (3) which                extracts the methane which the methane producer                produces.

PT P
PN CN113426472-A; CN113426472-B
TI Cobalt-based catalyst useful in preparing carbon            monoxide by catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide,            comprises Santa Barbara amorphous-15 substrate and            cobalt active center loaded on the Santa Barbara            amorphous-15 substrate.
AU QIN Y
   LIANG H
   ZHANG B
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 2021B46584
AB    NOVELTY - Cobalt-based catalyst comprises Santa Barbara                amorphous-15 (SBA-15) substrate and cobalt active                center loaded on the SBA-15 substrate. The cobalt                active center is obtained by reducing cobalt oxide                deposition layer loaded on the SBA-15 substrate,                and cobalt oxide deposition layer is formed by                atomic layer deposition.
   USE - The cobalt-based catalyst is useful in                preparing carbon monoxide by catalytic                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cobalt-based catalyst has excellent                catalytic activity, selectivity and                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing carbon monoxide by catalytic                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprising                reacting raw gas under the action of the                cobalt-based catalyst to obtain carbon monoxide and                methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass percentage of                  cobalt element in the cobalt-based catalyst is                  2-30%. The cobalt in the cobalt active center                  exists in one valence state of divalent cobalt or                  the coexistence of three valence states of                  divalent cobalt, trivalent cobalt and zero-valent                  cobalt. The number of repetitions is not greater                  than 100 times, and the cobalt in the cobalt                  active center in the cobalt-based catalyst is in                  a valence state of divalent cobalt; and number of                  repetitions is not less than 300, and the cobalt                  in the cobalt-based catalyst is in the form of                  coexistence of three valence states: divalent                  cobalt, trivalent cobalt, and zero-valent                  cobalt.Preferred Method: The temperature during                  reduction reaction is 250-450degrees Celsius. The                  formation of the cobalt oxide deposition layer by                  atomic layer deposition comprises (i) pulsing                  cobalt precursor on surface of the SBA-15                  substrate in atomic layer deposition vacuum                  reaction chamber, and introducing gas so that                  cobalt precursor adsorbed on the surface of the                  SBA-15, and then removing unadsorbed cobalt                  precursor by pumping, (ii) pulse the oxidant and                  introducing gas to make the oxidant react with                  the adsorbed cobalt precursor on the surface of                  the SBA-15 in step (i), and then pumping the gas                  to remove the unreacted oxidant, and (iii)                  repeating steps (i) and step (ii) in sequence to                  form a cobalt oxide deposition layer on the                  surface of SBA-15. In steps (i) and (ii), the                  temperature is 200-300degrees Celsius, the                  pressure is 10-200 Pa during atomic layer                  deposition; and volume ratio of the used carrier                  gas to the atomic layer deposition vacuum                  reaction chamber is 1:5-50 min-1. In step (iii),                  number of repetitions is 50-1000 times. The                  temperature during the reaction is 200-600degrees                  Celsius, the pressure is 0.1-5.0 MPa, and the                  flow rate of raw material gas passing 1 g of the                  cobalt-based catalyst per 1 hour is 12000-25000                  ml.

PT P
PN WO2021180567-A1; EP3878885-A1; CN115190893-A
TI Producing polyether carbonate polyol to produce            polyurethane plastic, preferably polyurethane foam,            comprises adding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide into            H-functional starter substance in presence of double            metal cyanide catalyst.
AU LANGER M
   POHL M
   SUBHANI M A
   VOR D E L
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO GERMANY AG (CVST-C)
GA 2021A65462
AB    NOVELTY - Producing polyether carbonate polyol,                comprises adding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide                into H-functional starter substance in presence of                double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst or metal                complex catalyst based on metals cobalt and/or                zinc. The alkylene oxide contains 5-50 wt.%                ethylene oxide, based on the total weight of the                alkylene oxide used, and the addition of the                ethylene oxide is carried out in an atmosphere                containing carbon dioxide.
   USE - The polyether carbonate polyol is useful for                producing polyurethane plastics, preferably                polyurethane foams (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyether carbonate polyol has increased                proportion of primary hydroxy groups.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                polyether carbonate polyol prepared by the above                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises removing H-functional starter substance                  or a suspending agent and optionally water and/or                  other highly volatile compounds by increased                  temperature and/or reduced pressure (''drying''),                  the catalyst of the H-functional starter                  substance or the suspending agent before or is                  added after drying, if necessary to activate the                  DMC catalyst, a partial amount (based on the                  total amount of the amount of alkylene oxide used                  in the activation and terpolymerization) of the                  alkylene oxide is added to the mixture resulting                  from step (a), this addition of a partial amount                  of alkylene oxide optionally can take place in                  the presence of CO2, and then waiting for the                  temperature peak (''hotspot'') and/or a pressure                  drop in the reactor that occurs due to the                  following exothermic chemical reaction. Preferred                  Components: The suspending agent includes                  4-methyl-2-oxo-1,3-dioxolane, 1,3-dioxolan-2-one,                  toluene, xylene, ethylbenzene, chlorobenzene                  and/or dichlorobenzene. In the step (g) it                  contains 5-40 wt.%, preferably 12-32 wt.%                  ethylene oxide based on the total weight of the                  alkylene oxide. In the step (g) carbon dioxide is                  used at a pressure of 2.5-100 bar, preferably                  8-30 bar. At least one compound includes ethylene                  glycol, propylene glycol, 1,3-butanediol,                  1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, 2                  -Methylpropane-1,3-diol, neopentyl glycol,                  1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, diethylene                  glycol, dipropylene glycol, glycerol,                  trimethylolpropane, pentaerythritol, sorbitol and                  polyether polyol with molecular weight of                  150-4500 g/mol, and functionality of 2-3.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.)

PT P
PN CN113198478-A; CN113198478-B
TI Preparing MXene/nickel aluminum layered double            metal hydroxide composite material useful in carbon            dioxide reduction involves electrostatically            self-assembling mixed reaction systems of e.g. lithium            fluoride and nickel nitrate hexahydrate.
AU YU Y
   CHEN K
   LI Q
   CHEN F
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202191934S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing MXene/nickel aluminum layered double                metal hydroxide composite material, comprises                etching a mixed reaction system containing MAX                powder, lithium fluoride and hydrochloric acid by                mechanical stirring, ultrasonically stripping                obtained reaction product to obtain MXene two                dimensional material, hydrothermally reacting a                mixed reaction system containing nickel nitrate                hexahydrate, aluminum nitrate nonahydrate and                methenamine to prepare nickel-aluminum layered                double-metal hydroxide, and mechanically stirring                and electrostatically self-assembling the mixed                reaction systems to obtain the composite                material.
   USE - The composite material is useful in                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material promotes the separation                of photo-generated electron-hole pairs, exhibits                good photocatalysis performance on reduction of                carbon dioxide, and is environment friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the                  MAX powder is 1-2 g titanium aluminum carbide                  powder, and the dosage of lithium fluoride is 1-2                  g. In the step (b), the dosage of hydrochloric                  acid is 20-40 ml, the oil bath temperature is 30                  degrees C, the stirring speed is 500                  revolutions/minute. In the step (c), the dosage                  of the black powder B is 0.5-1 g, the dosage of                  the deionized water is 90-180 ml. In the step                  (g), the capacity of the reaction kettle is 100                  ml. The method comprises (a) adding MAX powder                  and lithium fluoride into a plastic beaker to                  obtain a mixed powder A, (b) dissolving the mixed                  powder A in hydrochloric acid, mechanically                  stirring for 72 hours under the oil bath,                  centrifuging and separating, washing, and drying                  to obtain a black powder B, (c) dissolving the                  black powder B in deionized water to obtain black                  solution C, (d) ultrasonically dispersing the                  black solution C , then centrifuging and                  collecting the upper solution to obtain a black                  solution D, (e) freezing and drying the black                  solution D to obtain a black powder E, (f)                  dissolving nickel nitrate hexahydrate, aluminum                  nitrate nonahydrate and methenamine in de-ionized                  water to prepare a solution F, (g) performing                  hydrothermal reaction of the solution F in a                  reaction kettle at 140 degrees C for 24 hours,                  cooling the solution with the furnace,                  centrifuging and separating, washing and drying                  to obtain a green powder G, (h) ultrasonically                  dissolving the black powder E obtained in the                  step (e) and the green powder G obtained in step                  (g) in deionized water, mechanically stirring for                  1 hour to obtain a solution H, and (i)                  centrifuging and separating the solution H,                  washing and drying to obtain a black powder I. In                  the step (d), the ultrasonic dispersion process                  is carried out for 60 minutes, andthe upper layer                  solution is 1/2 of the total volume of the                  solution. In the step (f), the adding sequence is                  firstly adding nickel nitrate hexahydrate,                  aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, at last adding                  methenamine, where the molar ratio of nickel                  nitrate hexahydrate, aluminum nitrate nonahydrate                  and methenamine is 3:1:4.2, and the dosage of                  deionized water is 60-120 ml. The step (h)                  comprises adding 5-20 mg black powder E, 50 mg                  green powder G and 40 ml deionized water and                  ultrasonically dissolving for 10-30                  minutes.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E11-P; E11-Q02C; E11-W; E31-N05A; E31-N05C; E35-W; J04-E01; J04-E04A; J04-E04C; A12-L04; A12-W11K

TI Preparing calcium carbonate whisker involves mixing industrial active lime and hydroxyapatite, adding water, raising the temperature, continuing to pass carbon dioxide gas and stirring to carry out the carbonation reaction.
AU HUANG H
   XU K
AE HUANGSHI HAINA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (HUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020241131

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare calcium carbonate whisker with improved strength and wear-resistant and temperature-resistant performance.

The drying is performed under the condition of 100-120 degrees C, and the drying time is controlled at 2-3h. The length of the prepared calcium carbonate whiskers is 38-48 mu m, and the aspect ratio is 30-36.

PT P
PN JP2020037493-A
TI Producing concrete useful as structural material, involves forming mixture comprising cement and water, adding carbon dioxide to mixture, and curing mixture.
AU FUJIKURA Y
   KOBAYASHI K
   MASUDA K
   NAKAMURA Y
   KATO T
   TAKEUCHI M
AE FUJITA KK (FUKG-C)
GA 202020543H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing concrete involves forming a first mixture comprising cement and water, forming a second mixture by adding carbon dioxide to the first mixture, and curing the second mixture, where the weight of the water is adjusted so that an unhydrated cement remaining in the concrete is 0-50%.
   USE - The method is useful for producing concrete, which is useful as structural material.
   ADVANTAGE - The concrete is effectively immobilized with carbon dioxide, and has excellent strength through out the concrete and weather resistance, has high density and prolonged service life, and suppresses intrusion of contaminants such as oxygen and water from outside.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a concrete comprising a hydrated cement and greater than or equal to 0.1% calcium carbonate based on the weight of the concrete.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Method: The method involves adjusting weight, so that the unhydrated cement does not remain in the concrete. The method involves adding the carbon dioxide as fine particles. The method further involves adding a porous material that reversibly adsorbs carbon dioxide to the first mixture or the second mixture. The method involves adding the porous material after adsorbing the carbon dioxide on the porous material. Preferred Components: The first mixture further comprises aggregates, and admixtures. The concrete further comprises aggregates, the porous material that reversibly adsorbs carbon dioxide, 0.1-20 wt.%, preferably 1-10 wt.% thickener based on the weight of the hydrated cement and 0-50 wt.% unhydrated cement based on the total weight of the concrete.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous material comprises silicon oxide, zirconium oxide, aluminum oxide, aluminum silicate, an oxide of a lanthanoid metal, or carbon. The porous material has an organic group comprising an amino or carbamate group on a surface.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The thickener comprises a cellulose derivative or polyacrylamide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 201933007N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for purifying and crystallizing lithium carbonate, involves (i) mixing and pulping lithium carbonate and water, (ii) introducing carbon dioxide or mixed gas of carbon dioxide and inert gas to the slurry obtained in the step (i), leaving still and taking out the supernatant, (iii) mixing the supernatant obtained in the step (ii) with an aqueous solution of ethanol or ethanol in a microreactor to obtain a mixed solution and separating out solids, and (iv) drying the obtained solids.
   USE - Method for purifying and crystallizing lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves stable product quality, reduces the requirement of purification of the raw material, has mild operating conditions, is economical and solvent circulation amount is small.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of ethanol in the aqueous ethanol solution in step (iii) is 80-100 vol.%. The amount of ethanol in the mixed solution in step (iii) is 50-90 vol.%.

AB    NOVELTY - Treating carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide                gas synthesis compounds by low temperature plasma                dual electric field involves introducing reaction                gasses into the reactor. The reactor comprises the                corona discharge electric field. The double                electric field comprises firstelectric field and                the second electric field. The first electric field                being a positive corona discharge electric field,                or an alternating corona discharge electric field,                or other energy source capable of providing                sufficient energy to react gas molecules decomposed                into electric field sources of atoms, ions,                radicals, etc., the second electric field is a                negative corona discharge electric field.
   USE - Method for treating carbon dioxide and/or                carbon monoxide gas synthesis compounds by low                temperature plasma dual electric field.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reactive gas is a                  gas comprising carbon monoxide and/or carbon                  dioxide, such as flue gas, water gas, syngas,                  and/or automobile exhaust. Preferably, the                  reaction gas is specifically: flue gas and water                  vapor, or water gas or syngas optionally mixed                  with hydrogen. The different organic and                  inorganic compounds are prepared by adjusting the                  electric field strength of the plasma double                  electric field, the electric field strength                  passing between the electric field center                  electrode and the opposite electrode distance,                  whether the dielectric is added and its                  dielectric constant is adjusted. the double                  electric field is a weak electric field, and the                  gas containing carbon monoxide and/or carbon                  dioxide is reformed into a gas mixture product                  containing ethanol and methanol by the action of                  the plasma double electric field; Alternatively,                  the double electric field is a strong electric                  field, and a gas containing carbon monoxide                  and/or carbon dioxide passes through the plasma                  double electric field. It is an organic compound                  containing aliphatic hydrocarbons, high carbon                  ethers, high carbon alcohols, high carbon esters,                  or inorganic compounds such as nitrogen, oxygen,                  sulfuric acid, and ammonia. The reaction chamber                  or the metal tubular reaction chamber structure                  is arranged in parallel to form a cylinder or                  tubular tube group. The reformed gas mixture is                  treated to obtain a gas phase product and a                  liquid phase product, and the treatment may be                  carried out by a gas-liquid separator or a                  distiller. , for example, a water cooled                  condensing separator.

   XUE S
AE XINJIANG ACAD AGRIC SCI STORAGE & PROCES (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 201844763L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for packaging and preserving green walnuts in modified atmosphere, involves pre-cooling post-harvest walnuts, opening oxygen and carbon dioxide cylinder valve, blending high oxygen ratio and low carbon dioxide ratio gas, packaging the walnuts in bags, weighing into bag-type air-conditioning packaging machine, discharging gas in package, filling the packages with configured mixture according to size of bags, loading with half-fiber absorbent paper, sealing bags filled with chopped walnuts and mixed gases, performing cryopreservation, and maintaining the mixed gases in bags to maintain freshness of product.
   USE - The method is useful for packaging and preserving green walnuts in modified atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces soft rot rate to 43.44-79.47%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In pre-cooling step, the green walnuts are physiologically mature at flowering stage of 110-120 days. The pre-cooling is performed at 5 degrees C. The discharge set time is 10 seconds. The inflation setting time is 5 seconds. The half-fiber absorbent paper has G07 250x 380 mm, and maximum absorption rate of 82.75 g. The sealing time is set to 1 second. The storage is performed at 0-2 degrees C. The walnut varieties are Wen 185 and Xinxin 2.

TI Synergistic catalyst system for preparing cyclic carbonates from alkylene epoxide and carbon dioxide, comprises wood ash, and trialkanolamine, and prepared by adding alkylene epoxide in vessel, mixing wood ash and trialkanolamine.
AU LOPINTI K
   SHARMA M
   MONDAL S
   ARORA A K
   PURI S K
   MAZUMDAR S K
   SURESH R
AE INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
GA 201806401S
AB    NOVELTY - Synergistic catalyst system comprises wood ash, and trialkanolamine.
   USE - Synergistic catalyst system for preparing cyclic carbonates from alkylene epoxide and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The synergistic catalyst system is eco-friendly, and prepared in cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing cyclic carbonates, which involves:
   (A) adding alkylene epoxide in vessel;
   (B) mixing wood ash and trialkanolamine with alkylene epoxide;
   (C) adding carbon dioxide gas in mixture and heating;
   (D) cooling reaction mixture to room temperature;
   (E) recycling wood ash catalyst by filtering of reaction mixture;
   (F) washing with alcoholic solvent followed by drying; and
   (G) separating trialkanolamine to obtain color less pure form of cyclic carbonates.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of wood ash is 5-15 wt.% of alkylene epoxide. The concentration of triethanolamine is 0.005-0.05 molar equivalents with respect to alkylene epoxide. The amount of cyclic carbonates are obtained is 70-85%. Preferred Process: The wood ash is obtained by calcining wood blocks at 500-900 degrees C. Preferred Components: The trialkanolamine is selected from triethanolamine, triisopropanolamine, and triisobutanolamine. The alkylene epoxide is selected from ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1,2-butyl epoxide, 1,2-pentyl epoxide, 1,2-hexyl epoxide, 1,2-heptyl epoxide, 1,2-octyl epoxide, 1,2-nonyl epoxide, and 1,2-decyl epoxide. The alcoholic solvent is selected from methanol, ethanol, propanol, and butanol. Preferred Condition: The heating is carried out at 60-140 degrees C and maintaining carbon dioxide gas pressure of 10-60 bars for 5-20 hours. The drying is carried at 80-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Performing home-brewing process involves providing raw materials and active dry yeast, mixing uniformly raw material and active dry yeast to carry out anaerobic fermentation, carrying out anaerobic fermentation.
AU GE W
AE MIANYANG XINBAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202247974U
AB    NOVELTY - Performing home-brewing process involves providing raw materials and active dry yeast. The raw material and active dry yeast are mixed uniformly to carry out anaerobic fermentation, the anaerobic fermentation is carried out under the set fermentation temperature and fermentation pressure value to obtain an alcohol-containing mixture, the set fermentation pressure value allows carbon dioxide to dissolve in the mixture meet carbonation requirements. The anaerobic fermentation is stopped, the anaerobic fermentation is stopped by cooling after the alcohol content in the mixture reaches the set alcohol content value.
   USE - Method for performing home-brewing process.
   ADVANTAGE - The process improves the fermentation efficiency and ensures the flavor. The carbon dioxide generated during the fermentation process can carbonate the wine products without adding carbon dioxide, and the taste is better, and the raw materials are easily available, the operation is simple, and the non-professional personnel can execute it after simple learning. The variety of tastes can be achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing fruit wine by brewing process, and a method preparing beer by brewing process, which involves: (a) adding fruit juice, glucose, sucrose, flavor seasoning and active dry yeast into the fermentation tank and mix uniformly; (b) putting the fermentation tank into the brewer for fermentation treatment, set the temperature of the fermentation start-up stage to be 15-40degrees Celsius, the temperature of the fermentation growth stage to be 30-40degrees Celsius, and the pressure value of the entire fermentation stage to be set to 0.2-0.4 megapascal (MPa), when the set pressure value is reached, controlling the pressure by releasing portion of the carbon dioxide, and at the same time, dissolving portion of the carbon dioxide produced by fermentation in the wine under this pressure; (c) testing the alcohol concentration produced at intervals, and stop the fermentation after the fermentation reaches the required alcohol degree; and (d) cooling the product to complete the brewing, after the fermentation is stopped.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an alcohol concentration detection curve diagram. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

AE UNIV JIANGSU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJG-C)
GA 2022375902
AB    NOVELTY - A low alloy material resistant to carbon                dioxide corrosion comprises 0.03-0.15 wt.% carbon,                0.15-0.6 wt.% silicon, 0.6-1.3 wt.% manganese,                0.7-0.9 wt.% chromium, 0.2-0.3 wt.% molybdenum,                0.01-0.04 wt.% aluminum, 0.01 wt.% or less                phosphorus, 0.005 wt.% or less sulfur, 0.03-0.2                wt.% titanium, is 0.04-0.2 wt.% vanadium, 0.02-0.1                wt.% niobium, 0.02-0.1 wt.% zirconium and remaining                iron. The microstructure of the material is                tempered martensite and phase (II) particles.

PN DE102019007264-A1
TI Pipeline system for carrying heterogeneous component mixture from gas processing unit to flare, has impulse lines that are placed in predetermined orientation to flow direction, and pipes that are coated with non-stick coating.
AU BAUER H
   FLUEGGEN R
   HARFF M
   PESCHEL A
AE LINDE GMBH (LIND-Non-standard)
GA 2021414237
AB    NOVELTY - The pipeline system (10) has pipes (11,12) and fittings or valves (13). The pipes are formed in a way that the pipes not fall below a critical diameter or a critical bending radius. The main portion is operated in a way that the flow rate not falls below a minimum. The critical diameter, critical bending radius or minimum flow velocity is specified in a way that the pipes are not laid with the carbon dioxide present in the solidified state. The impulse lines (15) are placed in an orientation of 90 degrees to the flow direction. The pipes are coated with a non-stick coating. The component mixture comprises less than 5 mol% carbon dioxide in partially solidified state and otherwise predominantly or exclusively methane, ethane or ethylene and optionally components with lower boiling than methane. The non-stick coating comprises PTFE, enamel and ceramic material.
   USE - Pipeline system for carrying heterogeneous component mixture from gas processing unit to flare.
   ADVANTAGE - The fittings or valves do not have any dead spaces. The blowing off is performed at safe location. The safe blow-off points are relatively far away from the system to ensure a sufficient distance to potentially explosive zones. The deposit of solid carbon dioxide is avoided. The isentropic expansion in the valve is avoided. The impulse lines are prevented from laid, so that pressure, flow and level are reliably monitored in measuring devices connected to corresponding pulse lines. The adherence of carbon dioxide particles to the pipe wall is prevented, which otherwise acts as a starting point for the formation of larger carbon dioxide blocks or plugs. The blockage of the pipeline system or corresponding flare system is safely avoided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for guiding heterogeneous component mixture from gas processing unit to flare.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the arrangement that includes a pipeline system for carrying heterogeneous component mixture from gas processing unit to flare.

   Pipes (11,12)
   Valve (13)

   Gas processing unit (20)

AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of urea from ammonia and carbon                dioxide involves synthesizing urea in a urea                synthesis section (S1) including urea reactor (1),                where section (S1) operating at a urea synthesis                pressure (P1) and delivering a reaction effluent                (E1) containing urea, synthesizing urea in a urea                synthesis section (S2) including urea reactor (2),                where section (S2) operating at a urea synthesis                pressure (P2) lower than pressure (P1) and                delivering a reaction effluent (E2) containing                urea, stripping the reaction effluent (E1), which                is performed in a stripping section including                stripper operating at a stripping pressure lower                than the pressure (P1), and obtaining a                urea-containing liquid stripper effluent and a                gaseous phase containing vapors of ammonia and                carbon dioxide, where the reaction effluent (E2)                and stripper liquid effluent are sent to a recovery                section where a carbamate-containing recycle                solution is produced, and the recycle solution is                sent partly to the reactor (1) and (2).
   USE - Synthesis of urea used for synthesizing                melamine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is inexpensive, and enables                synthesis of urea with high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a plant,                which comprises a urea synthesis section (S1)                including urea reactor (1) operated at a synthesis                pressure (P1), where urea is synthesized from                ammonia and carbon dioxide delivering a reaction                effluent (E1) containing urea, a urea synthesis                section (S2) including urea reactor (2) operated at                a synthesis pressure (P2), where urea is                synthesized from ammonia and carbon dioxide                delivering a reaction effluent (E2) containing                urea, a stripper (4) connected to the reactor (1)                to receive the reaction effluent (E1), where the                stripper is operated at a stripping pressure lower                than the synthesis pressure (P1), and the stripper                delivers a urea-containing liquid effluent and a                gaseous phase containing ammonia and carbon                dioxide, a recovery section (13), feeding unit to                feed the reaction effluent (E2) and the stripper                liquid to the recovery section, and unit to feed a                carbamate-containing recycle solution, which is                produced in the recovery section, partly to the                reactor (1) and reactor (2).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                urea plant.
   Reactors (1,2)
   Carbamate condenser (3)
   Stripper (4)
   Recovery section (13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves sending the gaseous phase withdrawn from                  the stripping section to carbamate condenser (3)                  and sending the condensate effluent of the                  condenser to the reactor (2), where the gaseous                  phase from the stripping section is condensed                  only partially, so that the condensate effluent                  is a biphasic stream still comprising ammonia                  and/or carbon dioxide in a gaseous state, mixing                  an amount (A1) of fresh ammonia feed with recycle                  ammonia and feeding to the reactor (1), mixing an                  amount (A2) of fresh ammonia feed with recycle                  ammonia and feeding to the reactor (2), feeding                  an amount (B1) of fresh carbon dioxide feed to                  the reactor (1), feeding an amount (B2) of fresh                  carbon dioxide feed to the reactor (2). The                  stripping involves performing thermal stripping                  without the addition of a gaseous stripping                  medium. The majority of the urea collectively                  contained in the reaction effluent (E2) and in                  the stripper liquid effluent is synthesized in                  the urea synthesis section (S1). The reactor (1)                  is operated with nitrogen to carbon ratio of                  3.5-4, and hydrogen to carbon ratio of 0.3-0.7.                  The reactor (2) is operated with nitrogen to                  carbon ratio of 3.3-3.8 and hydrogen to carbon                  ratio of 0.5-1.0.16. Preferred Apparatus: The                  plant further comprises a high-pressure                  condenser, unit to send the gaseous phase from                  the stripper to the condenser, and unit to send a                  condensate effluent of the condenser to the                  reactor (2). The plant is an integrated                  urea-melamine plant and a urea section and a                  tied-in melamine section, where portion or all of                  the urea synthesized in the urea section is used                  in the melamine section to produce melamine.                  Preferred Composition: The amount of carbon                  dioxide feed sent to the reactor (1) is at least                  80%. The amount of recycle solution sent to the                  reactor (1) is at least 75%. The gaseous phase                  withdrawn from the stripping section has nitrogen                  to carbon molar ratio of 4 or more. Preferred                  Conditions: The reactor (1) is operated at a                  pressure of 200 bar or greater, preferably                  220-240 bar. The reactor (2) is operated at a                  pressure of 120-180 bar, preferably 140-160                  bar.

PN CN111595689-A; CN212391350-U
TI Carbon dioxide saturation and water head reverse pressure saturation dynamic triaxial test instrument for tailing dam, has glass protective cover placed with test sample, and confining pressure loading device connected to switch valve.
AU WANG G
   CHEN Z
   WANG X
   LIU W
   YANG Y
   HUANG J
   SUI S
   ZHANG C
   TIAN S
   NIE W
   XU H
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2020863598
AB    NOVELTY - The instrument has a hollow sample lifting platform whose inner portion is sleeved with a hollow vibrator. The test sample is placed above the lifting platform aerator (43) and is installed in a glass protective cover (13) equipped in glass protection cover device. A stress loading device includes a back pressure loading device (4), a confining pressure loading device (5), and a confining pressure back pressure regulator (12). The bottom portions of confining pressure loading device and the back pressure loading device are respectively connected to the confining pressure back pressure regulator through data lines. The confining pressure back pressure regulator is provided with a confining pressure back pressure regulator. The top of the confining pressure loading device is connected to the confining pressure switch valve (15) through a hose, and the top of the back pressure loading device is connected to the back pressure opening valve (22) through a hose.
   USE - Carbon dioxide saturation and water head reverse pressure saturation dynamic triaxial test instrument for tailing dam.
   ADVANTAGE - The experimental samples are placed in the glass protection cover for carbon dioxide saturation and water head back pressure saturation when performing experiments. The dynamic loading device is able to simulate the liquefaction of tailings during earthquakes for the whole experimental sample. The stress loading device provide load confining pressure and back pressure for the experimental sample. The test instrument is moved more conveniently. The carbon dioxide saturation and water head reverse pressure saturation dynamic triaxial test precision of tailing dam is improved. The sample experimental cost is reduced. The simple operation, convenient installation and accurate data analysis of test instrument are ensured.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide saturation and water head reverse pressure saturation dynamic triaxial test instrument for tailing dam.
   Back pressure loading device (4)
   Confining pressure loading device (5)
   Confining pressure back pressure regulator (12)
   Glass protective cover (13)
   Confining pressure switch valve (15)
   Back pressure opening valve (22)
   Lifting platform aerator (43)
