PN CN111058947-A; CN111058947-B
TI Gas turbine exhaust utilization system has fan and            first helical gear which is fixed is fixed at lower end            and upper end of first rotating shaft and when hot            airflow rises fan is rotated to drive airflow cooling            device to operate.
AU INVENTOR U
AE TAIZHOU HUANGYAN KANGBAI ENVIRONMENTAL (TAIZ-Non-standard)
   ZHEJIANG TEFU DEV CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020365949
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a second frame (16) which is                provided with a second gas pipe (20) connecting a                carbon dioxide compressor (17) and a first gas pipe                (18). A third air pipe (31) is provided between the                right wall of the first and second gas pipe. A                second baffle (33) is provided between the front                and rear walls of a second baffle cavity, and upper                end of first gas pipe is closed when second baffle                is turned to lower limit position. The right end of                second gas pipe is closed when the second baffle is                turned to the upper limit position, and an airflow                cooling device (103) is provided in the second                frame. The airflow cooling device includes a fixed                block (40) arranged between walls of the third gas                pipe, and is provided with a first rotating shaft                (41). A fan (42) and a first helical gear (43) is                fixed at the lower and upper end of the first                rotating shaft. The fan is rotated to drive the                airflow cooling device to operate when the hot                airflow rises.
   USE - Gas turbine exhaust utilization system.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas turbine exhaust utilization system can                effectively muffle the exhaust gas of the gas                turbine to reduce noise, and compress the carbon                dioxide in the exhaust gas to effectively use the                exhaust gas. At the same time when the exhaust gas                temperature rises due to insufficient combustion of                the gas turbine, the gas cooling device can be                automatically turned on to efficiently cool down                the hot air stream, so as not to affect the working                efficiency of the carbon dioxide compressor, and                achieve low carbon dioxide emissions of the gas                turbine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the gas                turbine exhaust utilization system.

   Fixed block (40)
   First rotating shaft (41)

PT P
PN US2019368786-A1; EP3578903-A1; CA3044714-A1; BR102019011251-A2; EP3578903-B1; ES2789173-T3; MX2019006298-A1; US11397032-B2
TI Carbon dioxide refrigeration system i.e. vapor            compression refrigeration system for cooling display            devices e.g. cases, has conditioning unit configured to            transfer heat from coolant within first fluid conduit            into second fluid conduit.
AU NEWEL J E
   BITTNER J D
   HAYES N M
AE HILL PHOENIX INC (DOVE-C)
   HILL PHOENIX INC (DOVE-C)
GA 2019A1638F
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a heat sink (34)                configured to remove heat from coolant circulating                within a coolant circuit (37), where the heat sink                comprises an inlet fluidly coupled to a coolant                outlet of a heat exchanger (31) through which the                coolant enters the heat sink, and an outlet fluidly                coupled to the coolant inlet of the heat exchanger                through which the coolant exits the heat sink. A                magnetocaloric conditioning unit (32) is configured                to transfer heat from the coolant within a first                fluid conduit (35) of the coolant circuit into the                coolant within a second fluid conduit (36) of the                coolant circuit, where the first fluid conduit                fluidly couples the coolant outlet of the heat                exchanger to the inlet of the heat sink, and the                second fluid conduit fluidly couples the outlet of                the heat sink to the coolant inlet of the heat                exchanger.
   USE - Carbon dioxide refrigeration system i.e. vapor                compression refrigeration system for cooling                temperature controlled display devices e.g. cases                and merchandiser devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The system controls amount of heat transferred                from the Carbon dioxide refrigerant to the coolant                by adjusting temperature of coolant supplied to the                heat exchanger without requiring use of a control                valve. The system operates the control valve to                minimize amount of heat gained by the coolant in                the heat exchanger to prevent MRS 30 from                overwhelmed. The heat exchanger is positioned                between a gas cooler/condenser and a high pressure                valve to provide additional cooling for the                refrigerant. The system includes the control valve                operable to control a flow of the coolant through                the heat exchanger, and a controller configured to                operate the control valve to maintain ultra-heat of                the refrigerant exiting the heat exchanger at a                predetermined setpoint by varying an amount of heat                removed from the compressed refrigerant in the heat                exchanger.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                magnetic refrigeration system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                carbon dioxide refrigeration system with a magnetic                refrigeration system.
   Heat exchanger (31)
   Magnetocaloric conditioning unit (32)
   Heat sink (34)
   Fluid conduits (35,36)
   Coolant circuit (37)
   Carbon dioxide refrigeration system                (100)

PT P
PN KR1990059-B1
TI High purity methane production device comprises e.g. anaerobic digestion tank to purify biogas comprising methane and carbon dioxide to discharge high purity methane, gas collector coupled to anaerobic digestion tank, and hydrogen cylinder.
AU YOON Y M
   KANG S
   YANG S M
   SHIN H S
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 201987540V
AB    NOVELTY - High purity methane production device comprises an anaerobic digestion tank (160) for purifying the biogas comprising methane and carbon dioxide to discharge high purity methane (170) and decomposing methane-producing bacteria (acetoclasticmethanogens) using methane-producing bacteria and acetic acid and acetic acid, hydrogen and carbon dioxide contained in the organic waste introduced from the outside and hydrogenotrophic methanogens using hydrogen and methane producing bacteria using acetic acid (acetoclastic methanogens), a gas collector (150) connected to the anaerobic digestion tank, a hydrogen cylinder (120) for supplying hydrogen to the biogas to generate the feeding gas (feeding gas), and anaerobic medium (basal anaerobic medium) (140) connected to the anaerobic digestion tank. The methane-producing bacteria are cultured using the hydrogen.
   USE - Used as high purity methane production device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device increases biogas production and methane content of biogas, shortens microbial concentration time, and improves utilization rate of gas circulation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High purity methane production device comprises an anaerobic digestion tank (160) for purifying the biogas comprising methane and carbon dioxide to discharge high purity methane (170) and decomposing methane-producing bacteria (acetoclasticmethanogens) using methane-producing bacteria and acetic acid and acetic acid, hydrogen and carbon dioxide contained in the organic waste introduced from the outside and hydrogenotrophic methanogens using hydrogen and methane producing bacteria using acetic acid (acetoclastic methanogens), a gas collector (150) connected to the anaerobic digestion tank, a hydrogen cylinder (120) for supplying hydrogen to the biogas to generate the feeding gas (feeding gas), and anaerobic medium (basal anaerobic medium) (140) connected to the anaerobic digestion tank. The methane-producing bacteria are cultured using the hydrogen. The biogas is collected and the gas is circulated by providing an injection gas to the anaerobic digestion tank. The anaerobic digester is the dominant species of methane-producing bacteria (acetoclastic methanogens) using the acetic acid. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing high purity methane using the above device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device.
   Hydrogen cylinder (120)
   Anaerobic medium (140)
   Gas collector (150)
   Anaerobic digestion tank (160)

PT P
PN CN110194557-A
TI Treating of fluorine and chlorine ions in acidic wastewater solution involves contacting acidic wastewater solution with heat generated by combustion, evaporating fluorine and chlorine ions, neutralizing, discharging, and recrystallizing.
AU LI T
   SUN X
   HE Y
   XIA S
   YANG G
AE UNIV YANTAI (UNYT-C)
GA 2019778210
AB    NOVELTY - Treating of fluorine and chlorine ions in acidic wastewater solution involves (1) pumping acidic wastewater solution containing fluorine chloride ions into submerged combustion evaporator, and directly contacting acidic wastewater solution with the heat generated by combustion of combustible gas and oxidizing agent, concentrating wastewater evaporation solution, and evaporating fluorine and chlorine ions in wastewater in the form of hydrogen fluoride, hydrogen chloride, water, oxygen, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide gases, (2) self-pressing distilled gases into acid-base neutralization tower, spraying alkaline solution, neutralizing by countercurrent contacting of hydrogen fluoride and hydrogen chloride gas, converting fluorine-containing and chloride ion solution, and discharging oxygen, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide gases, (3) and pumping fluorine-containing and chloride-based solutions into fluorochloride separation column, and performing recrystallization separation.
   USE - Treating of fluorine and chlorine ions in acidic wastewater solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy to operate and economical, solves the problem of difficulty in handling fluorine and chlorine ions and high energy consumption of multiple-effect evaporator in existing acidic wastewater treatment of metallurgical industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating of fluorine and chlorine ions in acidic wastewater solution involves (1) pumping acidic wastewater solution containing fluorine chloride ions into submerged combustion evaporator, and directly contacting acidic wastewater solution with the heat generated by combustion of combustible gas and oxidizing agent, concentrating wastewater evaporation solution, and evaporating fluorine and chlorine ions in wastewater in the form of hydrogen fluoride, hydrogen chloride, water, oxygen, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide gases, (2) self-pressing distilled hydrogen fluoride, hydrogen chloride, water, oxygen, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide gas into acid-base neutralization tower, spraying down alkaline solution to top of the column, rising, carrying out neutralization reaction by countercurrent contacting of hydrogen fluoride and hydrogen chloride gas, converting fluorine-containing and chloride ion solution, and discharging oxygen, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide gases outside the tower, (3) and pumping fluorine-containing and chloride-based solutions obtained from the acid-base neutralization column into fluorochloride separation column, carrying out recrystallization separation using two different solubility to obtain solid fluoride salt and chloride salt. The apparatus used comprises a submerged combustion evaporator, an acid-base neutralization tower and a fluorochloride separation tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The submerged combustion evaporator comprises common industrial gas or oil burner, civil inflamer, special burner, and high temperature flue gas generated by combustion of the combustible gas and oxidizing agent with acidic wastewater solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic wastewater solution is a mineral acid comprising sulfuric acid or nitric acid or mixed solution of organic acids comprising formic acid or acetic acid. The combustible gas is a mixture of one or more of methane, ethane, dimethyl ether, propane, butane, and hydrogen. he oxidizing agent is pure oxygen, air or a mixture of pure oxygen and air. The alkaline solution is one or more of sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, and calcium hydroxide. The fluoride salt is sodium fluoride or calcium fluoride. The chloride salt is sodium chloride or calcium chloride. Preferred Process: The acidic waste water solution is evaporated to obtain a concentrated liquid and an acid gas containing fluorine and chlorine. Preferred Composition: The fluoride ion in acidic wastewater solution in step (1) has concentration of 50-5000 mg/L. The chloride ion in acidic wastewater solution has concentration of 300-10000 mg/L. The acidic wastewater solution after evaporation and concentration comprises less than 50 mg/L fluoride ion content and less than 300 mg/L chloride ion content.

PT P
PN CN109595734-A; CN109595734-B
TI Method for controlling fresh air system, involves calculating spray frequency of water spray device according to cold load and temperature of cooling water, and controlling cooling water spray device in accordance with spray frequency.
AU CAO L
AE GD MIDEA AIR-CONDITIONING EQUIP CO LTD (MEDE-C)
GA 201934942J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves periodically obtaining (S101) a concentration of carbon dioxide in a room. The determination is made (S102) whether the concentration of the carbon dioxide is less than a preset concentration threshold. A cooling water spray device is controlled (S103) to stop spraying if the second humidity is equal to or greater than a sum of the first humidity and the preset difference humidity. A gear position is determined (S104) when the fan is turned on according to the concentration of the carbon dioxide. A cold load required for fresh air cooling of the fresh air system is obtained (S105) according to a gear position when the fan is turned on. A temperature of the cooling water is obtained (S106). A spray frequency of the cooling water spray device is calculated (S107) according to the cold load and the temperature of the cooling water. The cooling water spray device is controlled (S108) in accordance with the spray frequency.
   USE - Method for controlling fresh air system.
   ADVANTAGE - The phenomenon that a large amount of water mist adheres to the bottom of the ventilation duct is avoided. The mold is prevented from occurring in the ventilation tube. The fresh air supply temperature is accurately reduced. The realization is simple, and cost is low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a ventilation system;
   (2) an electronic device; and
   (3) a non transitory computer-readable storage medium storing program for controlling fresh air system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for controlling fresh air system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for periodically obtaining concentration of carbon dioxide in room (S101)
   Step for determining whether concentration of carbon dioxide is less than preset concentration threshold (S102)
   Step for controlling cooling water spray device to stop spraying (S103)
   Step for determining gear position when fan is turned on (S104)
   Step for obtaining cold load required for fresh air cooling of fresh air system (S105)
   Step for obtaining temperature of cooling water (S106)
   Step for calculating spray frequency of cooling water spray device (S107)
   Step for controlling cooling water spray device in accordance with spray frequency (S108)

PT P
PN CN108455608-A
TI Light hydrocarbon conversion for increasing content of carbon monoxide in syngas, comprises e.g. mixing light hydrocarbon, water vapor and recycled decarburization regeneration gas, preheating, carrying out conversion reaction and heating.
AU WANG L
AE SICHUAN TIANYI SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CNCC-C)
GA 2018691702
AB    NOVELTY - Light hydrocarbon conversion for increasing content of carbon monoxide in syngas, comprises (i) mixing light hydrocarbon, water vapor and recycled decarburization regeneration gas and preheating to obtain the mixed raw material gas A; (ii) conveying the premixed mixed raw material gas A into the reforming furnace to carry out conversion reaction; (iii) recovering reformed gas B by heating, cooling and separating from the process condensed water to obtain the reformed gas C; (iv) separating the reformed gas C by the decarbonization system to obtain the syngas D and carbon dioxide-rich decarburization regeneration gas E; (v) conveying syngas D to the subsequent chemical production unit as raw material; and (vi) pressurizing the carbon dioxide-rich decarburization regeneration gas E by a compressor and returning to the light hydrocarbon raw material used as a conversion medium.
   USE - The method is useful for light hydrocarbon conversion for increasing content of carbon monoxide in syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prouduces syngas using chemicals e.g. ethylene glycol which can increase the yield by about 5-50% compared with the traditional light hydrocarbon conversion method and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Light hydrocarbon conversion for increasing content of carbon monoxide in syngas, comprises (i) mixing light hydrocarbon, water vapor and recycled decarburization regeneration gas and preheating to obtain the mixed raw material gas A; (ii) conveying the premixed mixed raw material gas A into the reforming furnace to carry out conversion reaction; (iii) recovering reformed gas B by heating, cooling and separating from the process condensed water to obtain the reformed gas C; (iv) separating the reformed gas C by the decarbonization system to obtain the syngas D and carbon dioxide-rich decarburization regeneration gas E; (v) conveying syngas D to the subsequent chemical production unit as raw material; and (vi) pressurizing the carbon dioxide-rich decarburization regeneration gas E by a compressor and returning to the light hydrocarbon raw material used as a conversion medium, where the method carried out direct treatment of light hydrocarbons as raw materials, direct removal carbon dioxide from the reformed gas, returning to a reforming furnace as an intermediate for light hydrocarbon conversion and a reforming furnace.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide inside the device is closed in a closed loop, and no external carbon source is required, and the carbon monoxide shift reaction is inhibited by the circulating carbon dioxide content of carbon monoxide in the decarbonation conversion gas. The light hydrocarbons in the step (i) comprise natural gas, refinery dry gas, oilfield associated gas, coke oven gas, and dry distillation gas. The reforming furnace used in the step (ii) is a steam reforming furnace, an auto-thermal reforming furnace or a steam reforming furnace or a two-stage auto-thermal reformer. Preferred Method: The preheating temperature of the mixed raw material gas in the step (i) is 400-660 degrees C. The temperature of the reformer reformed gas B in the step (ii) is 800-1050 degrees C. The operation pressure of the reforming furnace in the step (ii) is 0.5-8 MPa. The decarburization system of the reformed gas C in the step (iv) is a liquid medium absorption method or a solid medium pressure swing adsorption method. The syngas D in the step (v) is used as a raw material for subsequent chemical production. A decarburized regeneration gas E in the step (vi) must be returned to the light hydrocarbon feedstock used as a medium for conversion reaction.

General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)

PT P
PN US2018056234-A1; US10668428-B2
TI Environmental control system (ECS) for removing            concentration of carbon dioxide in occupied spaces e.g.            homes, has gas-liquid contactor-separator that includes            rotating porous bed to provide heat/mass transfer            surface.
AU WENG D
   YATES S
   BERSHITSKY A
   BONK T
   HENSON P
AE HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)
   HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)
GA 201816406Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has an air conditioning                subsystem (103), and the downstream of a mix                manifold (104) of the air conditioning subsystem                and upstream of an environment (105) to be                conditioned. The downstream of a contaminant                removal subsystem (111) of the environment is to be                conditioned. The contaminant removal subsystem                includes a gas-liquid contactor-separator which                includes rotating porous bed that provides                heat/mass transfer surface for contact between the                contaminated air from environment and the liquid                absorbent. The contactor-separator produces cleaned                air and used liquid absorbent.
   USE - Environmental control system (ECS) for                removing concentration of carbon dioxide in                occupied spaces such as homes, buildings,                transportation vehicles, aircraft and                spacecraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The contaminant removal subsystem uses a                liquid-based architecture, ionic liquid contaminant                absorbents, spray gas-liquid contactors, and/or                centrifugal separators to provide performance,                reliability, and safety, while lowering the size,                weight, power, thermal load and amount of                consumables needed. The liquid is durable and last                without deterioration for the life of the mission.                The compounds have flexibility as the liquid                absorbent for carbon dioxide removal system with                ability to remove contaminants, because very large                number of ionic liquids exists, and both the cation                and the anion are tailored to obtain desired                characteristics. The dilution of the ionic liquids                with water is expected to increase the mass                transfer rate without greatly decreasing the ionic                liquid capacity. The system is a low-maintenance,                high-reliability system, since the ionic liquid                system is not having corrosion problem. The system                reduces the mass of consumables and reduces the                launch costs associated with the use of the ionic                liquid system. The combination of direct liquid                contact and novel ionic liquids provides integrated                carbon dioxide, humidity and trace contaminant                removal system with significantly lower size,                weight and power. The sorbent is completely                regenerable, without the use of space vacuum or                high temperatures, and thus provides excellent                option for closed-loop carbon dioxide removal on                existing and future vehicles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                environmental control system.
   ECS (100)
   Air conditioning subsystem (103)
   Mix manifold (104)
   Environment (105)
   Contaminant removal subsystem (111)

TI Method for producing polymer-grade ethylene from            dry gas, involves introducing dry gas into an            absorption tower for absorption treatment to obtain a            tower kettle product containing carbon dioxide,            introducing tower bottom product of the absorption            tower into demethanizer for demethanization.
AU LI D

AB    NOVELTY - Amino-bridged hexacarboxylic acid ligand i.e.                4',4'',4'''-(1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triyl)tris(azanediyl)tribiphenyl-3,5-dicarboxylic                acid (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in amino-modified metal organic                framework material for selective adsorption and                separation of carbon dioxide/methane and/or carbon                dioxide/nitrogen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has characteristics of easy                availability of synthetic raw materials, mild                synthetic conditions, simple operation, few                by-products and easy mass preparation. The                amino-modified metal-organic framework material has                excellent thermal stability and can keep porous                framework structure unchanged after guest molecules                are removed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Amino-bridged hexacarboxylic acid ligand i.e.                4',4'',4'''-(1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triyl)tris(azanediyl)tribiphenyl-3,5-dicarboxylic                acid of formula (I) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are                also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) amino-modified metal organic framework                material of formula ((Cu3(C45H24N6O12))n),                amino-modified metal organic framework material                belongs to tetragonal crystal system having I4/m                space group, and the unit cell parameters are: a, b                is 34.2168(11) Angstrom , c is 48.6142(19) Angstrom                , alpha is 90.00 degrees , beta is 90.00 degrees ,                and gamma is 90.00 degrees ; and
   (3) preparing amino-modified metal organic                framework material, comprising performing                coordination reaction of (I) and copper(II) sulfate                pentahydrate in presence of solvent, where solvent                is at least one of dimethylformamide, dimethyl                sulfoxide, water, and acetonitrile, preferably                dimethylformamide and dimethyl sulfoxide,                amount-to-volume ratio of the dimethylformamide and                dimethyl sulfoxide is 1:0.8-1.2, preferably the                amount of dimethylformamide is 0.1-0.3 ml per 1 mg                of ligand (I), amount-mass ratio of the ligand (I)                and copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate is 1:2-8,                coordination reaction satisfies at least the                following conditions: the reaction temperature is                70-120 degrees C, and the reaction time is 48-120                hours; and
   (4) selective adsorption and separation of                carbon dioxide/methane and/or carbon                dioxide/nitrogen comprising (a) activating                amino-modified metal-organic framework material to                obtain activated amino-modified metal-organic                framework material, (b) contacting the activated                amino-modified metal organic framework material                with gas containing carbon dioxide/methane and/or                carbon dioxide/nitrogen, where in step (a), the                amino-modified metal-organic framework material is                amino-modified metal-organic framework                material.
   n = positive integer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) reacting dimethyl                  4'-aminobiphenyl-3,5-dicarboxylate (II) and                  cyanuric chloride in a 1,4-dioxane solution to                  obtain reaction product, and (ii) subjecting                  reaction product to hydrolysis reaction under                  alkaline conditions, acidifying, filtering,                  washing with water until neutral, washing with                  hot methanol 2-3 times, and drying to obtain                  ligand (I), where molar ratio of (II) to amount                  of cyanuric chloride is 1:0.3-0.4. Preferred                  Method: In step (i): conditions of the reaction                  at least satisfy: the temperature is 70-120                  degrees C, and the time is 6-12 hours; in step                  (ii), the conditions of the hydrolysis reaction                  at least satisfy: the temperature is 40-90                  degrees C, and the time is 4-10 hours. The method                  further comprises before performing the                  coordination reaction, contacting solvent, ligand                  (I), and copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate reaction                  system with acidic substance at no higher than 35                  degrees C for acidification, and then heating                  acidified reaction system to carry out the                  coordination reaction, where acidic substance is                  at least one of fluoroboric acid, hydrochloric                  acid, and phosphoric acid, preferably fluoroboric                  acid. In step (i), steps of activation treatment                  include soaking treatment and degassing                  treatment, where soaking treatment is carried out                  in solution containing anhydrous acetone, and the                  time of the soaking treatment is 70-90 hours; In                  the immersion treatment, amount of anhydrous                  acetone used is 0.1-0.5 ml per 1 mg of the                  amino-modified metal organic framework material;                  conditions of the degassing treatment at least                  satisfy: the temperature is 80-130 degrees C, and                  the time is 5-20 hours.

TI Carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst            rich in oxygen vacancies used in electrochemical            reduction reaction, comprises catalyst which is metal            nanoparticles loaded on nitrogen-doped graphene by            adsorption.
AU FAN X
   HE Q
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 202050523R
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction                catalyst rich in oxygen vacancies comprises                catalyst which is metal nanoparticles loaded on                nitrogen-doped graphene by adsorption and                hydrothermal self-assembly of one or more metal                salt precursors and graphene oxide, and the surface                of the metal nanoparticles is rich in metal oxide                phases with oxygen vacancies, where the catalyst                comprises 2-10 pts. wt. metal, 80-92 pts. wt.                carbon, 2-6 pts. wt. nitrogen, and 3-8 pts. wt.                oxygen.
   USE - The oxygen-rich vacancy-rich carbon dioxide                electrochemical reduction catalyst is used in an                electrochemical reduction reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction                catalyst has excellent carbon dioxide                electrochemical reduction performance, is stably                dispersed on the carbon substrate, and exhibits                excellent carbon dioxide reduction reaction                performance. The preparation method is carried out                in a simple and cost-effective manner in a short                period.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the oxygen-rich vacancy carbon                dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst,                comprising (a) ultrasonically dispersing graphene                oxide in deionized water, and performing ultrasonic                treatment for 28 hours to prepare a uniform                suspension with a concentration of 1-5 mg/ml,                denoted as dispersion A, (b) adding one or more                metal salt solutions of nickel chloride                hexahydrate, ferric nitrate hexahydrate and zinc                nitrate hexahydrate into the dispersion liquid A,                performing ultrasonic dispersion for 14 hours,                placing in a hydrothermal kettle for heating                reaction, where the reaction temperature is carried                out at 140-220&#176; C for 10-16 hours, and                drying the obtained reaction product in a freeze                dryer for 5-20 hours to obtain a dried composite,                (c) setting the furnace temperature to                700-1100&#176; C, where the gas flow rate of                argon is 100&#177; 50 sccm, ammonia is                50&#177; 20 sccm, putting the dried composite                prepared in the step (b) into a tubular furnace                center, and carrying out high-temperature heat                treatment for 0.5-3 hours to obtain the metal                nanoparticles loaded on the nitrogen-doped                graphene, and (d) keeping the furnace temperature                unchanged, changing the gas flow rate to                100&#177; 50 sccm argon, 10&#177; 5 sccm                hydrogen gas, and performing high-temperature heat                treatment for 10-60 minutes to obtain the oxygen                vacancy-rich carbon dioxide electrochemical                reduction catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt                  precursor is a mixture of any one or more of any                  one or more of nickel chloride hexahydrate, iron                  nitrate nonahydrate, and zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate. Preferred Method: In step (b), a                  metal salt solution of one or more of nickel                  chloride hexahydrate, ferric nitrate hexahydrate                  and zinc nitrate hexahydrate is added to the                  dispersion liquid A, and the metal salt solution                  is added in an amount of 1-10% of the mass                  percentage of the metal oxide graphene. The                  hydrogen in the step (d) is hydrogen which                  carries water vapor first through first-stage                  water.

PT P
PN CN107999002-A; CN207838956-U
TI Micro reactor device for synthesizing 3,6-dichlorosalicylic acid used as dicamba intermediate, comprises carbon dioxide gas cylinder, raw material storage tank, where carbon dioxide gas cylinder is set with raw material storage tank.
AU ZHANG Z
   SU Y
   DU G
   LV L
   ZHAO H
   WAN Y
AE JIANGSU CHANGQING AGROCHEMICAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018375958
AB    NOVELTY - Micro reactor device comprises carbon dioxide gas cylinder (1), raw material storage tank (2), a pre-heating mixer, micro-reactor (3) and a cyclone (4), where carbon dioxide gas cylinder is connected with the raw material storage tank through gas pipeline (6). The pre-mixer is connected with the pre-heating mixer. The liquid pipeline mixer is connected with the pre-heating mixer. The pre-heating mixer is connected to the micro-reactor. The micro-reactor and the cyclone are connected with the bottom of the swirler which is provided with a water receiving tank.
   USE - Micro reactor device for synthesizing 3,6-dichlorosalicylic acid of dicamba intermediate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The micro reactor device has lower cost, higher yield and better selectivity, reduces waste water, and improves the utilization ratio of the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing 3,6-dichlorosalicylic acid, which involves using 2,5-dichlorophenol and liquid potassium to obtain 5-dichlorobenzene potassium solution, using micro-reactor device system, adding 2,5-dichlorobenzene potassium solution into raw material storage tank, and keeping the temperature, starting the conduction oil, heating at 150 degrees C, where temperature of the hot oil to 140 degrees C, starting heating micro reactor, where inside temperature of the micro-reactor is 140 degrees C, starting compression pump, pressurizing 2,5-dichlorobenzene potassium solution, entering pre-mixer at 140 degrees C, then feeding into micro-reactor with 2,5-dichlorobenzene solution, opening vent valve of the carbon dioxide bottle, adding carbon dioxide gas into preheating mixer, then opening the feeding valve of the micro-reactor with 6.0-7.0 megapascal pressure, reacting in micro-reactor through back pressure to discharge the reacted material, mixing with water into cyclone, discharging unreacted carbon dioxide through the exhaust valve, using reaction generated material mixer to dissolve in water, using xylene to separate water from mixer and cyclone, entering oil layer receiving tank, dissolving the material in water, entering into receiving tank with water layer, taking water layer receiving aqueous solution tank for analyzing product content from lower opening of the swirler, and entering aqueous solution into next working procedure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of micro reactor device.
   Carbon dioxide gas cylinder (1)
   Raw material storage tank (2)
   Micro-reactor (3)
   Cyclone (4)
   Gas pipeline (6)

PN WO2018019699-A1; EP3487937-A1; US2019161619-A1; JP2019529299-W; US11168219-B2; US2022017753-A1; US2022025190-A1; US2022025189-A1; EP3487937-B1
TI Production of surface-reacted calcium carbonate            used as e.g. preservative, involves treating calcium            carbonate-containing material with hydronium ion donor            and carbon dioxide in aqueous medium, and adding            water-soluble metal cation source.
AU GERARD D E
   RENTSCH S
   WELKER M
   URWYLER S
   GLAUBITZ J
   KNUPFER M E
   GANE P A C
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2018090400
AB    NOVELTY - Production of surface-reacted calcium                carbonate involves providing a calcium                carbonate-containing material, at least one                hydronium ion donor, and at least one water-soluble                metal cation source, treating the calcium                carbonate-containing material with the hydronium                ion donor and carbon dioxide in an aqueous medium                to form an aqueous suspension of surface-reacted                calcium carbonate, and adding the water-soluble                metal cation source during step (d). The carbon                dioxide is formed in situ by hydronium ion donor                treatment. The carbon dioxide is supplied from an                external source.
   USE - Production of surface-reacted calcium                carbonate used as metal cation releaser such as                micronutrient delivery agent and plant protection                product, and as preservative for controlling odor,                and enhancing and mediating antimicrobial activity                and electrical conductivity of articles e.g. paper                products, engineered wood products, plasterboard                products, polymer products, hygiene products,                medical products, healthcare products, filter                products, woven materials, nonwoven materials,                geotextile products, agricultural products,                horticultural products, clothing, footwear                products, baggage products, household products,                industrial products, packaging products, building                products, and construction products, for polymer                applications, paper coating applications,                papermaking, paints, coatings, sealants, printing                inks, adhesives, food, feed, pharmaceuticals,                concrete, cement, cosmetics, and water treatment                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and                efficiently produces biodegradable surface-reacted                calcium carbonate. The surface-reacted calcium                carbonate provides article with excellent water                resistance, antimicrobial activity and electrical                conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of surface-reacted calcium                carbonate involves (a) providing a calcium                carbonate-containing material, (b) providing at                least one hydronium ion donor, (c) providing at                least one water-soluble metal cation source, (d)                treating the calcium carbonate-containing material                with the hydronium ion donor and carbon dioxide in                an aqueous medium to form an aqueous suspension of                surface-reacted calcium carbonate, and (e) adding                the water-soluble metal cation source during step                (d). The carbon dioxide is formed in situ by                hydronium ion donor treatment. The carbon dioxide                is supplied from an external source. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) surface-reacted calcium carbonate;                and
   (2) composition, which comprises the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate and an additional                surface-reacted calcium carbonate, which is a                reaction product of natural ground calcium                carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate with                carbon dioxide and the hydronium ion donor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium                  carbonate-containing material is natural ground                  calcium carbonate and/or precipitated calcium                  carbonate, preferably natural ground calcium                  carbonate. The natural ground calcium carbonate                  is chosen from marble, chalk, dolomite and/or                  limestone. The precipitated calcium carbonate is                  chosen from precipitated calcium carbonates                  having aragonitic, vateritic and/or calcitic                  crystal form. The hydronium ion donor is chosen                  from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous                  acid, phosphoric acid, citric acid, oxalic acid,                  acidic salt such as dihydrogen phosphate which is                  at least partially neutralized by a cation chosen                  from lithium, sodium and/or potassium, hydrogen                  phosphate which is at least partially neutralized                  by a cation chosen from lithium, sodium,                  potassium, magnesium and/or calcium, acetic acid                  and/or formic acid, preferably phosphoric acid.                  The water-soluble metal cation source is chosen                  from metal salt, transition metal complex, metal                  hydroxide and/or metal oxide, preferably copper                  nitrate, copper sulfate, copper acetate, copper                  chloride, copper bromide, copper iodide, zinc                  nitrate, zinc sulfate, zinc acetate, zinc                  chloride, zinc bromide, zinc iodide and/or their                  hydrates. Preferred Properties: The calcium                  carbonate-containing material is in form of                  particles having a weight median particle size                  d50 of 0.05-10 mu m, preferably 0.6-1.2 mu m                  and/or a weight top cut particle size d98 of                  0.15-55 mu m, preferably 3-15 mu m. The                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate has a specific                  surface area of 15-200 m2/g, preferably 30-140                  m2/g, as measured using nitrogen by BET method.                  The surface-reacted calcium carbonate has a                  volume determined median particle size d50 of                  1-75 mu m, preferably 5-15 mu m, and/or a volume                  determined top cut particle size d98 of 2-150 mu                  m, preferably 10-30 mu m. The surface-reacted                  calcium carbonate has an intraparticle intruded                  specific pore volume of 0.1-2.3 cm3/g, preferably                  0.6-1.6 cm3/g, as measured by mercury porosimetry                  measurement. The surface-reacted calcium                  carbonate has an intraparticle pore size of                  0.004-1.6 mu m, preferably 0.007-1.0 mu m, as                  measured by mercury porosity measurement.                  Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the                  hydronium ion donor to the calcium                  carbonate-containing material is 0.01-4,                  preferably 0.1-0.58. The method involves                  providing 0.01-60 wt.%, preferably 0.5-10 wt.%                  water-soluble metal cation source, based on the                  total weight of the calcium carbonate-containing                  material. Preferred Process: The calcium                  carbonate-containing material is treated with a                  solution (s1) comprising a portion (p1) of the                  hydronium ion donor, and with a solution (s2)                  comprising the remaining portion of the hydronium                  ion donor and the water-soluble metal cation                  source. The method further involves (f)                  separating the surface-reacted calcium carbonate                  from the aqueous suspension, (g) drying the                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate at a                  temperature of 60-600 degrees C, until the                  moisture content of the surface-reacted calcium                  carbonate reaches 0.01-5 wt.%, based on the total                  weight of the dried surface-reacted calcium                  carbonate. Preferred Condition: The step (d) is                  carried out at a temperature of 20-90 degrees C,                  preferably 60-70 degrees C.
DC A60 (Additives and Compounding Agents If the usage is very restricted it may be classified under the individual polymer or process involved.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); F07 (Other textile applications - include mechanical treatment of fabrics (D06C, F, G, H, J, L, M).); G01 (Inorganic pigments and non-fibrous fillers (C09C).); ; D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); B06 (Inorganics - including fluorides for toothpastes etc.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. TI Preparing p-toluic acid by catalyzing carbon dioxide and toluene, comprises e.g. reacting catalyst material composed of Lewis acid main catalyst, zeolite carrier and chlorosilane promoter, toluene basic raw material and organic solvent medium in reactor.
AU CHEN Y
   JIANG O
   WU T
   YANG S
   ZHANG W
   ZHENG X
   SHI L
   PENG X
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021E7237R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing p-toluic acid by catalyzing carbon dioxide and toluene, comprises (i) adding the catalyst material composed of Lewis acid main catalyst, zeolite carrier and chlorosilane promoter, toluene basic raw material and organic solvent medium into the reactor, and (ii) reacting at 20-260degrees Celsius and a reaction pressure of normal pressure to 7.5 MPa, and introducing carbon dioxide for catalytic reaction to prepare the p-toluic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing p-toluic acid by catalyzing carbon dioxide and toluene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has the highest atomic utilization rate of 100%, demonstrates the environmental and economic advantages of energy conservation and emission reduction and the sustainability of industrial applications.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chlorosilane is phenyltrichlorosilane, phenyldimethylchlorosilane, diphenylmethylchlorosilane, triphenylchlorosilane, methyltrichlorosilane, dimethyl tert-butylchlorosilane, trimethylchlorosilane, or 1H, 1H, 2H, 2H-perfluorooctyltrichlorosilane. The zeolite carrier is ZSM, BEA and/or HY aluminosilicate zeolite. The Lewis acid main catalyst is aluminum trichloride, aluminum bromide, titanium tetrachloride, and/or tin tetrachloride. The organic solvent is toluene, n-hexane, cyclohexane, dichloromethane, dichloroethane, carbon disulfide, tetrahydrofuran, dimethyl sulfoxide, and/or methylpyrrolidone. The mass ratio of the Lewis acid main catalyst, the zeolite carrier and the chlorosilane promoter is (0.1-1):1:(0.1-3), and the addition amount of the catalytic material is 10-200 wt.% of the substrate mass. The reactor is a tank reactor or a tower reactor. The addition amount of dichloromethane is 1-10 times the mass of the substrate.Preferred Method: The reaction temperature is preferably 30-100degrees Celsius, the reaction pressure is preferably 1.5-6 MPa, and the reaction time is 7.5-8.5 hours. The method also includes a separation process of p-toluic acid as follows: after the catalytic reaction is completed, adjusting the reaction mixture to pH 6-7 with a hydrochloric acid solution, and adding dichloromethane, obtaining the organic phase through solid-liquid and liquid-liquid separation, and washing the organic phase to neutrality with pure water, concentrating the organic phase by evaporation under reduced pressure, and finally obtaining the p-toluic acid product by column chromatography for separation, purification and crystallization.

   ADVANTAGE - Invention provides higher efficiency of growing photosynthesizing microorganisms and increases the amount of absorbed carbon dioxide.

PN CN110105197-A
TI Supercritically purifying alpha-linolenic acid useful in medicine field, comprises adding edible peony seed oil into extraction kettle, performing two-stage supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, recovering and separating carbon dioxide.
AU PENG W
   YANG Y
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV HENAN AGRIC (UYHR-C)
GA 201970684S
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritically purifying alpha -linolenic acid comprises adding edible peony seed oil into an extraction kettle, performing two-stage supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 20-60 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours with a pressure of 15-25 MPa, subjecting the obtained extract to second extraction at 20-60 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours with a pressure of 30-50 MPa, transferring the extract into a separation kettle, recovering and separating carbon dioxide to obtain purified alpha -linolenic acid.
   USE - The alpha -linolenic acid useful in medicine field (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate; and provides 60-95 wt.% alpha -linolenic acid, maintains the biological activity of alpha -linolenic acid, and utilizes the resulting product as a medicinal raw material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The separation and purification are carried out at 10-50 degrees C under pressure of 5-15 MPa. The first extraction pressure is 15-20 MPa, and the extraction temperature is 35-40 degrees C. The second extraction pressure is 35-45 MPa, and the extraction temperature is 40-45 degrees C. The carbon dioxide flow rate is 0.4-1 l/hour per kg of peony seed oil.

TI Linde type A zeolite adsorbent useful for            adsorbing carbon dioxide, comprises zeolite skeleton            comprising an aluminosilicate zeolite structure            including fine pores and mesopores.
AU HEE C K
   CHUL Y H
   NAM K J
   SUP H S
   TAE B H
   WOO C D

   YOON H C
   KIM J N

   BEUM H
   CHO D W
   PARK J H
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 201937194A
AB    NOVELTY - LTA (linde type A) zeolite adsorbent comprises                a zeolite skeleton comprising an aluminosilicate                zeolite structure including fine pores and                mesopores. The adsorbent has high carbon dioxide                selectivity compared to methane.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for adsorbing carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has high carbon dioxide                selectivity compared to methane, increases                adsorption/desorption rate, and increases                utilization rate of entire micropores.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing LTA zeolite adsorbent, comprising (a)                adding alumina-based raw material, silica-based raw                material, sodium hydroxide, and surfactant into a                solvent to prepare an aqueous solution, (b)                hydrothermally synthesizing the aqueous solution,                (c) calcining the LTA zeolite adsorbent formed by                hydrothermal synthesis, and (d) exchanging a part                of the sodium ions in the zeolite with other                cations by using alkali metal, alkaline earth metal                or metal salt aqueous solution in the calcined LTA                zeolite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mesopores are                  connected to outside of the LTA zeolite                  adsorbent, where at least a part of the                  micropores are directly connected to the outside,                  and at least a portion of the micropores is                  connected to the outside through the mesopores.                  The micropores has size of 0.33-0.37 nm. The                  mesopores have size of 2-50 nm. The total                  specific surface area ratio of the fine                  pores:mesopores is 20:1-1:1. The volume ratio of                  the micropores: mesopores is 10:1-1:5. The                  pressure during the adsorption of the carbon                  dioxide to the LTA zeolite adsorbent is between                  0.01-3000 psi. The reaction temperature for                  adsorbing the carbon dioxide to the LTA zeolite                  adsorbent is from -20 degrees C to 100 degrees C.                  The carbon dioxide adsorption-desorption curve                  for the LTA zeolite adsorbent has the same                  profile. The alumina-based raw material includes                  sodium aluminate, aluminate sulfate, aluminum                  hydroxide, and aluminum chloride, alumina powder,                  colloidal alumina, alumina powder and/or aluminum                  alkoxide. The silica-based raw material includes                  water glass, sodium silicate, sodium                  metasilicate, silica powder, colloidal silica                  and/or silicon alkoxide. The molar amount of                  silica in the silica-based raw material is 1-3                  times the molar amount of alumina in the                  alumina-based raw material. The sum of the molar                  amount in terms of sodium oxide (Na2O) and the                  molar amount of sodium oxide (Na2O) contained in                  the alumina-based raw material and silica-based                  raw material is 2-6 times. The zeolite adsorbent                  comprises .01-70% of sodium ions which are                  ion-exchanged with alkali metal and/or alkaline                  earth metal ion. The alkali metal and alkaline                  earth metal ions include monovalent cation of                  potasium, silver, chromium, caesium, divalent                  cation of barium, beryllium, calcium and/or                  magnesium. The concentration of the alkali metal,                  alkaline earth metal or metal salt aqueous                  solution is 0.1-70 wt.%. Preferred Method: The                  hydrothermal synthesis is carried out at 70-200                  degrees C for 30 minutes to 72 hours. The step of                  exchanging a portion of the sodium ions in the                  zeolite with another cation is carried out at                  0-100 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant                  includes                  (3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)octadecyldimethylammonium                  chloride,                  (3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)hexadecyldimethylammonium                  chloride,                  (3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)-dodecyldimethylammonium                  chloride,                  (3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)octylammonium                  chloride, N- (3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)aniline,                  3-(2-(2-aminoethylamino)ethylamino)propyltrimethoxysilane,                  N-(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)-N'-(4-vinylbenzyl)ethylenediamine,                  triethoxy-3-(2-imidazolin-1-yl)propylsilane,                  1-(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)urea,                  N-(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)-ethylenediamine,                  (3-(diethylamino)propyl)trimethoxysilane,                  (3-glycidyloxypropyl)trimethoxysilane, 3-                  (trimethoxysilyl)propyl methacrylate,                  (2-(cyclohexenyl) ethyl)triethoxy-silane,                  dodecyltriethoxysilane,                  hexadecyltrimethoxysilane,                  (3-aminopropyl)trimethoxy-silane,                  (3-mercaptopropyl)trimethoxysilane and/or                  (3-chloropropyl)trimethoxysilane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU YANG Q
   JI A
   LI X
AE JIANGSU PROVINCE METALLURGICAL DESIGN (BJSW-C)
GA 2018374805
AB    NOVELTY - Method for increasing reaction rate of melting furnace involves placing sponge iron in a melting furnace, heating, blowing argon gas to the bottom of the melting furnace, blowing the mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide at 0.1-1.0 MPa for 100-500 L/minute to the top of the furnace and stopping blowing oxygen and carbon dioxide mixed gas when the sponge iron in the melting furnace starts to boil.    ADVANTAGE - (A): can forms polymerization reaction, which                is characterized by infrared spectroscopy and solid                carbon nuclear magnetic resonance; high specific                surface area, excellent void structure and high                thermal stability (under calculation of nitrogen                adsorption and desorption curves); and has                adsorption capacity of compound (I) for carbon                dioxide and nitrogen is 24 and 1.2 cm3g1                respectively and adsorption capacity of compound                (II) for carbon dioxide and nitrogen is 16 and 1.2                cm3g1 respectively under the pressure of 1 bar and                temperature of 298 K.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (A), comprises (i) adding                  4-(2,4,5-tris(4-formylphenyl)-3,6-dimethylphenyl)benzaldehyde                  (III) and three-node amine into a reaction vessel                  containing organic solvent, ultrasonicating for                  20-30 minutes to make uniformly mixed, then                  adding aqueous acetic acid solution as catalyst,                  and carrying out "freezing-evacuating-thawing to                  room temperature" under argon atmosphere and                  liquid nitrogen, and cycling                  "freezing-evacuating-thawing to room temperature"                  for 2-5 times, sealing the reaction vessel under                  vacuum conditions, and reacting at                  100-140degreesC for 5-7 days after the reaction                  vessel is returned to room temperature, and (ii)                  cooling to room temperature after completing step                  (i) reaction, collecting solid product by                  filtration, washing with tetrahydrofuran and                  acetone, then extracting with tetrahydrofuran for                  10-15 hours, and vacuum-drying at 80-120degreesC                  for 10-24 hours to obtain final product. The                  three-node amine is                  N1,N1-bis(4-aminophenyl)benzene-1,4-diamine or                  4-(3,5-bis(4-aminophenyl)phenyl)aniline.Preferred Components: In step (i): organic                  solvent is dioxane, or mixed solvent of                  o-dichlorobenzene and n-butanol in a volume ratio                  of 5-9:1; ratio between organic solvent and (III)                  is 1-1.5:0.02 ml/mmol, and molar ratio between                  (III) and three-node amine is 1:1-1.5; molar                  ratio between (III) and three-node amine is                  1:1.35; concentration of aqueous acetic acid                  solution is 3-9 mol/l, preferably 6 mol/l; and                  volume ratio between organic solvent and aqueous                  acetic acid solution is 6-12:1, preferably                  10:1.

PT P
PN WO2021234717-A1
TI Converting carbon dioxide into methane, methanol, formic acids or other fuels or chemicals, by using magnesium.
AU POLSHETTIWAR V
   RAWOOL S
   BELGAMWAR R
AE TATA INST FUNDAMENTAL RES (TTTA-C)
GA 2021D8438P
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbon dioxide into methane, methanol, formic acids or other fuels or chemicals, by using magnesium.
   USE - The process is useful for converting carbon dioxide into methane, methanol, formic acids or other fuels or chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is carried out without any external energy source e.g. thermal, light or electric energy source, any sacrificial reagent or any cocatalysts; can also be used to produce high yield of hydrogen; has a significant scientific and technological impact to achieve the dream of Net-zero emissions energy systems; utilizes nano and bulk magnesium which are air-stable and can be used in open environment, although non-catalytic and stoichiometric could serve as the basis of an economical and sustainable carbon dioxide utilization strategy, which could be a step towards magnesium civilization; and provides very fast reaction with a maximum yield of products which is achieved in less than 15 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process: magnesium chemisorbs and then activates the carbon dioxide molecules by electron transfer to facilitate the reduction, using water as a hydrogen source; the conversion of carbon dioxide is carried out at low to atmospheric pressure and at -100 degrees C to room temperature; magnesium nanoparticles or bulk magnesium is used to carry out the conversion process of carbon dioxide; and the conversion of carbon dioxide into 100 mu mol/g methane using magnesium nanoparticles takes place in less than 15 minutes.
   EXAMPLE - Carbon dioxide conversion reaction was carried out by taking 50 mg magnesium (or other metal/alloy) in 350 ml round bottom (RB) flask as a batch reactor. To protect the oxidation of magnesium surface, magnesium nanoparticles were stored in a glove box with inert (argon) atmosphere and loaded in RB flask in the glove box. The RB flask was purged with carbon dioxide gas (99.995% pure) for 30 minutes with a flow rate of 250 ml/minute using a mass flow controller. After that 5 ml deionized water was added in ~20 seconds. Reaction progress was monitored with time, and gas sampling was done after every 5 minutes. Methane and hydrogen were quantified by on-line Agilent Technologies 490 micro GC, with molecular sieve column and thermal conductivity detector. Methanol and formic acid were identified and quantified by proton NMR. Carbon dioxide conversion by magnesium nanoparticles was reproduced for more than 15 times.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.)

AU LUO Y
   HE R
   YI Z

AE SICHUAN SHUHAN ZHIBO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202195525G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silver nanowires involves adding nano-silver wire to supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, soaking at 35-40 degrees C and pressure of 10-18 MPa for 60-120 minutes, and releasing pressure. Silver nanowires are added to isopropanol (A), ultrasonically processed for 15-30 minutes, surface treatment solution is added, ultrasonically processed for 30-45 minutes, followed by heating at 30-40 degrees C, introducing nitrogen into surface treatment solution for 1-2 hours, separating solid and liquid, washing solid, and vacuum drying to obtain surface-modified nano silver wires.
   USE - Method for preparing nano-silver wire for conductive silver paste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the nano silver wire with high conductive performance and improved electric performance of a battery sheet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The surface treatment solution is prepared by adding citric acid, malic acid and amino acids to isopropanol B to stirring at a speed of 300-500 rpm for 30-45min to obtain a surface treatment solution.    ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the disulfide bond-containing carbon dioxide/nitrogen and redox stimulus-responsive worm micelle system in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with high viscosity and have great application prospects in the fields of fracturing fluid, material preparation, and drug release.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is dithiothreitol, glutathione, sodium dithionite, vitamins C, sodium borohydride, potassium borohydride, lithium aluminum hydride, and sulfurous acid. The oxidizing agent is benzoyl peroxide, hydrogen peroxide, potassium permanganate, potassium dichromate, potassium permanganate, oleum, lead dioxide, sodium bismuth, periodic acid, cobalt trifluoride, sodium ferrate, sodium hypochlorite, sodium persulfate, potassium persulfate or their mixtures.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anionic surfactant is sodium lauryl sulfate, sodium dialkylbenzene sulfonate or sodium cetyl carboxylate. The cystamine bicarbonate is cystamine bicarbonate, N,N,N',N'-tetramethylcystamine bicarbonate, N,N,N',N'-tetraethylcystamine bicarbonate, N,N,N',N'-tetrapropyl cystamine bicarbonate and N,N,N',N'-tetrabutylcystamine bicarbonate. The nitrogen is introduced into the worm-like micelle and heated at 40 degrees C. A reducing agent is added to the worm micelle system to decompose the worm-like micelles.

PT P
PN CN112679663-A; CN112679663-B
TI Preparing supercritical carbon dioxide thickening agent, comprises subjecting ethoxytrimethylsilane and vinyltriethoxysilane to hydrolysis condensation exchange reaction, condensing and refluxing, rotary steaming, washing, and drying.
AU WANG Y
   LIU B
   LI Q
   LIANG L
   GONG J
   TANG L
   ZHANG C
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2021456957
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing supercritical carbon dioxide thickening agent, comprises (i) subjecting ethoxytrimethylsilane and vinyltriethoxysilane to hydrolysis condensation exchange reaction at a temperature of 50-90 degrees C and pH of 2.5-3.5, condensing and refluxing, performing rotary steaming on reaction product to remove low-boiling point impurities, washing and drying to obtain vinyl tris(trimethylsiloxy)silane, where the molar ratio of vinyltriethoxysilane and ethoxytrimethylsilane is 1:1-5; and (ii) subjecting vinyl tris(trimethylsiloxy)silane and ethylene glycol dimethacrylate to perform free radical polymerization addition reaction in the presence of initiator to obtain a polymer which is supercritical carbon dioxide thickening agent, where the molar ratio of the vinyl tris(trimethylsiloxy)silane to ethylene glycol dimethacrylate is 1:1-3.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing supercritical carbon dioxide thickening agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The weight average molecular weight of the obtained polymer supercritical carbon dioxide thickening agent is 7000-11000, preferably 7500-8000. The initiator is azobisisobutyronitrile with the amount of 10-60 ppm, preferably 30-50 ppm. The molar ratio of vinyltriethoxysilane and ethoxytrimethylsilane is 1:2-4.5. The molar ratio of the vinyl tris(trimethylsiloxy)silane to ethylene glycol dimethacrylate is 1:1-2. The mass concentration of the polymer in supercritical carbon dioxide fracturing fluid is 0.5-1 wt.%. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the reaction comprises adjusting the pH using reagent hydrochloric acid, hydrolysis condensation exchange reaction is carried out for 2-6 hours, and washing is carried out with sodium bicarbonate and de-ionized water. In the step (ii), the free radical polymerization addition reaction is carried out at 80-95 degrees C, preferably 80-90 degrees C for 2-24 hours, preferably 4-8 hours. In the step (i), the hydrolysis condensation exchange reaction is carried out at 60-70 degrees C for 2-3 hours.

   ADVANTAGE - The oil-displacement agent has good stability,                delay the time of gas breakthrough, has good                injection performance, low cost and simple                operation method, improves the sweep coefficient                and recovery rate, reduces the gas channeling of                carbon di oxides, and does not increases the                interfacial tension,.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing a oil-displacement agent, which                involves:
   (A) mixing the water and surfactant uniformly                and adding to the stirred tank;
   (B) vacuumizing the stirred tank and                connecting the tank with liquid carbon                dioxide;
   (C) pouring liquid carbon dioxide into the                stirred tank; and
   (D) stirring continuous to form a stable                emulsion is the oil-displacing agent.

The speed of continuous stirring                  is 500-1000 r/min, and the stirring time is                  greater than or equal to 2 hours. The speed of                  continuous stirring is 1000r/min, and the                  stirring time is 2 hours. The pressure in the                  stirred tank is greater than 10 MPa, and the                  temperature in the stirred tank is less than 30                  degrees C during continuous mixing. The                  temperature of the oil reservoir is higher than                  50 degrees C, the oil reservoir is a crude oil                  reservoir, and the viscosity of the crude oil is                  10-10000 mPa s.

PN CN109621890-A
TI Preparation of whole process industrialized            spherical calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent,            involves selecting calcium-based adsorbent and inert            carrier powder, spray-drying precursor solution to            obtain precursor powder and calcining.
AU TONG X
   LIU W
   XU M
   YANG Y
   LI Q
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019380087
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of whole process                industrialized spherical calcium-based carbon                dioxide adsorbent involves (a) selecting a                calcium-based adsorbent and an inert carrier powder                for wet mixing, obtaining a uniformly mixed                precursor solution, spray-drying the precursor                solution to obtain a calcium-based adsorbent-inert                carrier powder, that is a precursor powder, (b)                calcining precursor powder to obtain a porous                calcium oxide powder, hydrating and drying calcium                oxide powder to obtain calcium hydroxide powder,                (c) subjecting calcium hydroxide powder to                extrusion spheronization.
   USE - Preparation of whole process industrialized                spherical calcium-based carbon dioxide                adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The pore structure of the adsorbent is                improving, which is beneficial to the gas-solid                reaction. The preparation of industrial                high-performance spherical calcium-based carbon                dioxide adsorbent meets the requirements of                industrial scale preparation and industrial                circulating fluidized bed applications.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium-based                  adsorbent in step (a) is preferably calcium                  nitrate, calcium chloride, calcium bromide,                  calcium iodide, calcium formate, calcium acetate,                  calcium propionate, calcium gluconate, calcium                  lactate, calcium tartrate and/or calcium malate.                  The inert carrier in step (a) is preferably                  aluminum oxide, magnesium oxide, manganese oxide,                  ytterbium oxide, yttrium oxide, cerium oxide,                  lanthanum oxide, neodymium oxide, praseodymium                  oxide, titanium dioxide and/or zirconium dioxide.                  Preferred Composition: The calcium oxide                  adsorbent powder has a mass fraction of calcium                  oxide greater than 50% in step (b). Preferred                  Conditions: The drying in step (a) is preferably                  spray drying, the peristaltic pump speed is                  preferably from 1-10 mL/minute, and the inlet air                  temperature is preferably from 160-220 degrees C.                  The calcination temperature in step (b) is                  preferably from 850-950 degrees C, and the                  calcination time is 30-90 minutes. The rotation                  speed of the extruder when the extrusion is                  rounded in step (c)is preferably 20-50 rpm, and                  the rotation speed of the spheronizer is                  preferably 1000-1400 rpm. Preferred Process: The                  preparation of whole process industrialized                  spherical calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent                  in step (b) is carried out by hydrating calcium                  oxide, obtaining wet material of calcium                  hydroxide, preferably adding 5-10 wt.% of a pore                  former to the calcium hydroxide wet material,                  thermally interpreting the evolved gas by pore                  former during the precalcination of the                  calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent in                  industrial applications, and improving the pore                  structure of the adsorbent which is beneficial to                  the gas-solid reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The pore former                  preferably uses urea, polyethylene glycol 6000,                  cellulose, starch, and rice husk.

PN EP3381298-A1; EP3381298-B1; EP3381298-B8
TI Producing carbonic acid in drinking water, comprises utilizing a mixture of a cation exchanger with a solid formed as carbonate, in which the carbonate is ion exchanged in an aqueous environment in the presence of the cation exchanger.
AU JOHANN J
AE BWT AG (BWTA-Non-standard)
   BWT HOLDING GMBH (BWTH-Non-standard)
GA 201877063Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbonic acid in drinking water, comprises utilizing a mixture of a cation exchanger with a solid formed as carbonate, where the carbonate is ion exchanged in an aqueous environment in the presence of the cation exchanger which is at least partially in the hydrogen ion form and dissolved to form carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbonic acid in drinking water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces carbon dioxide in drinking water in a simple manner, and provides favorable carbon dioxide footprint.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cartridge or pad, comprising at least one cation exchanger and at least one solid, preferably a salt formed as a carbonate which liberates carbon dioxide with the release of a cation to the cation exchanger.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: A further salt, preferably an initiator salt, is used, which accelerates the formation of carbon dioxide, preferably an initiator salt having a solubility in water of 200 g/l at 20 degrees C. The solid is present in the mixture in a proportion of at least 5 wt.%, preferably more than 20 wt.%. The mixture is introduced into the drinking water in a cartridge, in a pad or as a loose filling. A strongly acidic or weakly acidic cation exchanger is used, which is loaded with more than 10%, preferably more than 80% of its capacity with hydrogen ions. The solid includes magnesium and/or calcium carbonate. The process is carried out in a pressure bottle. The cartridge or the pad is divided into segments, where the segments are each filled with a mixture of cation exchanger and solid, preferably salt. The cartridge or the pad is filled with an initiator salt.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q32 (Container/closure types, special packaging features and transit packaging (B65D)); Q34 (Types of goods packaged/bottled/bound/labelled/unpacked (B65B, C, D))
