PN KR2022120906-A
TI Manufacture of S-allyl-L-cysteine for e.g.            treating e.g. Alzheimer disease, involves reacting            dehydrated sulfur and supercritical synthesis-carbon            dioxide obtained using methyl alcohol, nitrogen and            carbon dioxide, collecting sulfur, and freeze-drying            sulfur.
AU HYUN S
AE HYUN S (HYUN-Individual)
GA 2022B2242A
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of SAC (S-allyl-L-Cysteine)                involves (1) adding sulfur power to sodium                hydroxide aqueous solution and stirring, (2)                discarding the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution,                adding aqueous acetic acid solution, and stirring,                (3) discarding acetic acid aqueous solution reacted                with stirring, (4) dehydrating the sulfur, putting                the dehydrated sulfur into a supercritical reactor,                (6) charging methyl alcohol and nitrogen in carbon                dioxide in a supercritical synthesis reactor and                treating to yield supercritical synthesis-carbon                dioxide, (7) charging supercritical                synthesis-carbon dioxide to a supercritical                synthesis containing dehydrated sulfur and reacting                with stirring, (8) reacting the sulfur reacted with                stirring to -120&#176; C rapid nitrogen cooling                device, (9) spraying and collecting sulfur reacted                by spraying on the rapid nitrogen cooling device to                a collecting device, (10) treating the collected                sulfur by spraying the collecting device, and (11)                freeze-drying the sulfur.
   USE - Manufacture of S-allyl-L-cysteine for                preventing, improving and treating degenerative                brain diseases e.g. Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, Lou                Gehrig's and Huntington's disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces 99% or more of                S-allyl-L-cystine, which reduces oxidative stress                in HT22 cells to improve cell viability and reduces                nitrite, an inflammatory mediator induced by the                addition of LPS in BV cells, to treat degenerative                brain diseases without side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of SAC (S-allyl-L-Cysteine) for                preventing, improving and treating degenerative                brain diseases e.g. Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, Lou                Gehrig's, Huntington's disease involves (1) adding                sulfur power to 50 L (10%) sodium hydroxide aqueous                solution in 20 kg pts. wt. and stirring for 3                hours, (2) discarding the sodium hydroxide aqueous                solution, adding 40 L of 5% aqueous acetic acid                solution, and stirring for 2 hours, (3) discarding                the 5% acetic acid aqueous solution reacted with                stirring, (4) dehydrating the sulfur discarding the                5% acetic acid aqueous solution, putting the                dehydrated sulfur into a supercritical reactor                (capacity: 300L class, pressure 400 bar, neodymium                stirrer shaft Dia (&#937; )50 equipped), (6)                charging 2L of methyl alcohol and 200 ml/L nitrogen                in 20 L of carbon dioxide in a supercritical                synthesis reactor and treating at a temperature of                70&#176; C and a pressure of 450 bar to yield                supercritical synthesis-carbon dioxide, (7)                charging supercritical synthesis-carbon dioxide at                a pressure of 250 bar to a supercritical synthesis                containing dehydrated sulfur and reacting with                stirring at 95&#176; C for 2 hours, (8)                reacting the sulfur reacted with stirring to                -120&#176; C rapid nitrogen cooling device at                -85&#176; C or less, (9) spraying and                collecting sulfur reacted by spraying on the rapid                nitrogen cooling device to a collecting device,                (10) treating the collected sulfur by spraying the                collecting device at a pressure of 400 bar in a                high-pressure homogenizer, and (11) freeze-drying                the sulfur treated at the 400 bar pressure at                -20&#176; C in a freeze drying device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sulfur is                  synthesized with carbon dioxide, methyl alcohol                  and nitrogen as a co-solvent by treating                  supercritical synthetic carbon dioxide. Preferred                  Composition: The sodium hydroxide aqueous                  solution comprises 5-60% sulfur. The acetic acid                  aqueous solution comprises 5-60% sulfur.                  Preferred Process: The method involves reacting                  sulfur at -120&#176; C rapid nitrogen cooling                  device at -85&#176; C or less.
   ACTIVITY - Neuroprotective; Nootropic; Antiparkinsonian;                Anticonvulsant.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

AB    NOVELTY - Sewage treatment system comprises e.g. a sewage treatment device of supercritical carbon dioxide, extracting liquid separator, separation system, and oxygen-enriched combustion system, where the space division system is connected to liquefied natural gas (LNG) source through first heat exchanger, for separating to obtain liquid oxygen and gaseous nitrogen to the nitrogen and oxygen in the pressurized air under action of cold energy of original LNG. The LNG is obtained by using natural gas by first grade absorbing and oxygen-enriched combustion system.
   USE - The sewage treatment system is useful in liquefied natural gas cold energy (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves the structure of the key components, solves the limited problem of natural cold energy sewage treatment, and has utilization of the sewage treatment system with the use of oxygen-enriched combustion modules, LNG cold energy, achieved and zero carbon emissions and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sewage treatment system comprises a sewage treatment device of supercritical carbon dioxide (12), extracting liquid separator (13), separation system (9), and oxygen-enriched combustion system (10), where the space division system is connected to the liquefied natural gas (LNG) source (1) through the first heat exchanger, for separating to obtain liquid oxygen and gaseous nitrogen to the nitrogen and oxygen in the pressurized air under the action of cold energy of the original LNG. The LNG is obtained by using natural gas by the first grade absorbing. The oxygen-enriched combustion system is provided with a second heat exchanger. The second heat exchanger is used for heat processing the liquid oxygen to gaseous oxygen. The oxygen-enriched combustion system is used for the action of the gaseous oxygen in the oxygen-enriched combustion flue gas carbon dioxide (CO2). The supercritical carbon dioxide sewage treatment device is provided with a third heat exchanger. The third heat exchanger is used for the primary effect of the natural gas using the gas CO2 is converted into supercritical CO2. The supercritical carbon dioxide sewage processing device connected with the oxygen-enriched combustion system and used for extracting the sewage under the condition of extracting agent in the supercritical CO2 to obtain clean water of sewage impurity reduces. and the sewage impurity enriched extracting liquid, after the first level using natural gas through the second stage heat to obtain the secondary natural gas; the supercritical carbon dioxide by sewage treatment device is connected with the extraction liquid separator. The extract liquor separator for CO2 the sewage impurity in the extracting liquid to be separated, the separated CO2 into circulation oxygen enrichment combustion reaction in the oxygen-enriched combustion system. The whole sewage treatment system step using LNG cold energy is the original LNG is first-level heat to obtain the one-stage utilization of natural gas and the one-stage using natural gas through the secondary endothermic gas obtained after the secondary utilization, generating and using the intermediate supercritical CO2 achieves the cyclic utilization of processing and the CO2 of the sewage.

The carbon capture system is used to capture the liquid CO2 and achieves the carbon capture. The sewage processing system further comprises a freezing sewage treatment device. The freezing water treatment device comprises a compressor, a fourth heat exchanger, freezing tank, coil pipe and a mechanical ice breaking assembly, where the compressor is connected with the LNG source for the original LNG to the LNG source output for pressurizing. TI Solar thermal power generation system for use in space environment, comprises energy storage systems and carbon dioxide transcritical cross-flow circulation power generation system, where energy storage system contains solar concentrator.
AU WANG Y
   MA W
   ZHANG Z
   ZHU Q
   LIU X
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 201958389H
AB    NOVELTY - A solar thermal power generation system comprises energy storage systems and carbon dioxide transcritical cross-flow circulation power generation system, where the energy storage system comprises energy storage medium storage tank, first valve, second valve and solar concentrator, specific connection relationship is outlet of the energy storage medium storage tank is connected to energy storage medium inlet of the solar concentrator through the first valve and energy storage medium outlet of the solar concentrator is connected to inlet of the energy storage medium storage tank through the second valve to form energy storage system circuit and the carbon dioxide transcritical cross-flow circulation power generation system includes solar concentrator, turbine, regeneration system, radiant cooling system, compressor and cryopump.
   USE - Solar thermal power generation system for use in space environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The solar thermal power generation system has efficiency, which is increased to more than 10% under same conditions and comprises radiant cooling system, which utilizes amount of cold contained in space to cool carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A solar thermal power generation system comprises energy storage systems and carbon dioxide transcritical cross-flow circulation power generation system, where the energy storage system comprises energy storage medium storage tank, first valve, second valve and solar concentrator, specific connection relationship is outlet of the energy storage medium storage tank is connected to energy storage medium inlet of the solar concentrator through the first valve and energy storage medium outlet of the solar concentrator is connected to inlet of the energy storage medium storage tank through the second valve to form energy storage system circuit, the carbon dioxide transcritical cross-flow circulation power generation system includes solar concentrator, turbine, regeneration system, radiant cooling system, compressor and cryopump, specific connection relationship is solar concentrator outlet is connected to turbine inlet, outlet of the turbine is connected to inlet of the regeneration system, outlet of the regeneration system is connected to inlet of the radiant cooling system, outlet of the radiant cooling system is connected to compressor (E) inlet, outlet of the compressor is connected to secondary inlet of regeneration system, the regeneration system is connected to outlet of the radiant cooling system, outlet of the radiant cooling system us connected to cryopump inlet, outlet of the cryopump is connected to heat absorption side inlet of the regeneration system and outlet of the regeneration system is connected to solar concentrator inlet to form carbon dioxide transcritical cross-flow circulation power generation system and working medium utilized in the carbon dioxide transcritical cross-flow circulation power generation system is carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for operating the solar thermal power generation system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The regeneration system includes multiple heat exchangers. The radiant cooling system includes several heat exchangers.

PN WO2022140827-A1
TI Zwitterion base synthesis process involves e.g.            reacting hydroxy-benzaldehyde-type compound with            primary amine, cyclizing using ammonium salt and            vicinal dialdehyde, N-alkylating by alkylating agent,            deprotonating phenolic hydroxy group.
AU FERREIRA DO NASCIMENTO J
   DUPONT J
   CABRAL DE MENEZES S M
   DOS SANTOS F P
   MARIN G
   DE LEMOS PINTO AYDOS G
   EBELING G
AE PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
   UNIV FEDERAL DO RIO GRANDE DO SUL (URGR-C)
GA 202288004M
AB    NOVELTY - Zwitterion base synthesis process (P1)                comprises: (a) promoting the reaction between a                hydroxy-benzaldehyde-type compound with a primary                amine to form the corresponding imine, and then                cyclizing the imine by adding ammonium salt and a                vicinal dialdehyde in presence of solvent at -10                degrees C and 80 degrees C for 10 minutes to 24                hours to obtain a compound of the type                2-(hydroxy-phenyl)-imidazole; (b) promoting the                N-alkylation reaction between the obtained                2-(hydroxy-phenyl)-imidazole compound and                alkylating agent for formation of                2-(hydroxy-phenyl)-imidazolium salt in presence of                solvent at 40-100 degrees C for 10 minutes and 24                hours; and (c) promoting the deprotonation of the                phenolic hydroxy group of the obtained                2-(hydroxy-phenyl)-imidazolium salt through the                reaction of aqueous solution of this component with                a strongly basic ion exchange resin at 10-80                degrees C for up to 60 minutes to obtain formation                of the inner or zwitterionic salt of                2-(oxy-phenyl)-imidazolium type.
   USE - The method is useful for zwitterion base                synthesis process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method minimizes main drawbacks inherent                in large-scale carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide                capture processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMs are also included                for:
   (1) zwitterionic bases, is obtained by having                in their structure an imidazolium cation covalently                bonded, through the C-2 carbon, to a phenolate                anion 2-phenylimidazolidine cation of formula                (IV);
   (2) carbon dioxide capture process (P2) using                zwitterionic base solutions, comprising promoting                contact of a gas stream containing acid gases with                a zwitterionic base sorbent solution for the                removal of at least part of these acid gases under                sorption conditions to obtain a purified gas stream                and a solution with sorbed acid gases, and in                parallel, to promote the regeneration of the                sorbent solution under desorption conditions to                obtain a zwitterionic base solution and a stream of                acid gases; and
   (3) use of the carbon dioxide capture process,                is applied in industrial segments e.g. in the                energy sector to capture carbon dioxide from                exhaust gases, in the chemical sector to remove                carbon dioxide from gaseous process streams                catalysts in which carbon dioxide can poison                catalysts and especially in the oil and gas sector                for the purification of natural gas.
   R1, R9 = alkyl, aryl, alkylether or 1-10C                alkylalcohol group;
   R7, R8 = imidazolium cation comprise hydrogen                or alkyl, aryl, alkylether or 1-10C alkylalcohol                groups; and
   R2a, R3a, R4a, R5a, R6a = benzene ring                (substituents on the benzene ring is the O- group),                -O-, H, hydroxy, 1-10C alkoxy groups, halide,                phenyl or 1-10C alkyl group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the process (P1):                  the hydroxy-benzaldehyde-type compound contains                  in its aromatic ring at least one hydroxy group                  and the other substituents of the benzene ring                  are hydrogen, 1-10C alkoxy groups, halides,                  phenyl or 1-10C alkyl group; the compound of                  hydroxy-benzaldehyde type is                  3-hydroxy-benzaldehyde or 4-hydroxy-benzaldehyde;                  the primary amine is of formula (R1a-NH2) (I);                  the ammonium salt is of formula (NH4)nY1) (II);                  and the alkylating agent is of formula (R9a-X)                  (III). In the process (P2): the zwitterionic base                  containing the quaternary ammonium cation is of                  formula (V); the zwitterionic base containing the                  quaternary phosphonium cation is of formula                  (VI)l; the zwitterionic base containing                  pyridinium is e.g. of formulae (VII)-(X); the                  gaseous feed stream is composed of a gaseous                  mixture containing acid gases e.g. carbon dioxide                  and hydrogen sulfide; the zwitterionic base is an                  internal organic salt formed by the covalent                  association of a quaternary ammonium cation,                  quaternary phosphonium, pyridinium, pyrrolidinium                  or imidazolium, with the phenolate anion; the                  zwitterionic solution is a solution of inner salt                  1,3-dimethyl-2-(3-oxyphenyl)-imidazolium or                  1,3-dimethyl-2-(4-oxy-phenyl)-imidazolium; the                  zwitterionic solution using water, methanol,                  ethanol, isopropanol, aliphatic alcohol having                  1-18C alkyl chain as solvent, diols having 2-5                  atoms of carbon or 3-6C polyols, ethylene glycol                  monoalkyl ethers of formula (H(OCH2CH2)nOR2b)                  (XI) and ionic liquids functionalized with the                  hydroxy group.R1a = alkyl, aryl, alkylether or 1-10C                  alkylalcohol group;Y1 = bromide, chloride, sulfate, acetate,                  carbonate or bicarbonate anions;n = 1 or 2;R9a = alkyl, arylalkyl, alkylether or 1-10C                  alkylalcohol group;X = halide, alkylsulfonate and alkylsulfate                  group in which the 1-5C alkyl group;A = phenolate anion (Oph-) with independent                  substituents comprising H, halides, phenyl, 1-10C                  alkoxy or 1-10C alkyl;n = 1-4; andR2b = 1-12C alkyl group.The primary amine is methylamine. The                  primary amine is in content of 0.5-2 equivalents                  in relation to the hydroxy-benzaldehyde-type                  compound. The ammonium salt is in a content of                  0.3-2 equivalents in relation to the compound of                  the hydroxy-benzaldehyde type. The dialdehyde is                  glyoxal or its analogues with alkyl, aryl, alkyl                  ether or 1-10C alkyl alcohol substituents. The                  dialdehyde is in content of 0.5-2 equivalents in                  relation to the hydroxy-benzaldehyde-type                  compound. The solvent of the first step is water,                  acetonitrile, ethyl acetate and 1-10C aliphatic                  alcohols in the alkyl chain in content of 1-100                  times the mass of the hydroxy-benzaldehyde-type                  compound. The solvent is content of 10 times the                  mass of the hydroxy-benzaldehyde-type compound.                  The alkylating agent is iodomethane or                  methanesulfonyl chloride. The solvent of the                  stage (b) is water, acetonitrile, ethyl acetate                  and aliphatic alcohols in the 1-10C alkyl chain                  in content of 1-20 times the mass of the                  hydroxy-benzaldehyde-type compound. The solvent                  is in a content of 5 times the mass of the                  hydroxy-benzaldehyde-type compound. The                  zwitteritterionic bases are solids soluble in                  water and polar organic solvents e.g. alcohols,                  polyalcohols, acetonitrile and acetone. Preferred                  Method: The step (b) is carried out at 70 degrees                  C under agitation for 1 hour. The step (c) is                  carried out at 30 degrees C under agitation for                  10 minutes. In the process (P2): the absorption                  condition is 0.5-5 M concentration of                  zwitterionic base solution, temperature is 0-60                  degrees C and pressure of 1-150 bar absolute; the                  zwitterionic solution of the absorption stage                  having a temperature of 40 degrees C and a                  pressure of 1.3-3 bar absolute; the desorption                  condition is: temperature of 60-150 degrees C and                  a pressure of 0-10 bar absolute; and the                  zwitterionic solution of the desorption step                  having a temperature of 100 degrees C and a                  pressure of 1 bar absolute.
   EXAMPLE - 42 mmol Methylamine (40% aqueous solution) was                added over a solution of 40 mmol                3-hydroxy-benzaldehyde in 20 ml methanol at room                temperature were added in a reaction flask. The                exothermic reaction causes a slight heating of the                reaction mixture and then the precipitation of the                corresponding imine is observed. The suspension was                stirred for a further 10 minutes and then 2.88 g                ammonium carbonate was added. The resulting mixture                was cooled to 0 degrees C with the aid of an                external ice-water bath. Glyoxal (40% aqueous                solution, 5.8 g; 40 mmol) dissolved in 15 mL of                CH3OH was added slowly with stirring. The reaction                mixture was stirred for another 30 minutes at 0                degrees C, generating a gradual dissolution of the                solids. The ice bath was removed and the reaction                was stirred for another 24 hours at room                temperature, observing the precipitation of the                desired reaction product. At the end, 15 ml water                was added to the suspension, the solid was filtered                on a Buchner funnel, washed with ice-cold 1:1                methanol/water and dried under reduced pressure.                2.69 g Slightly yellowish solid were obtained                (38.6% yield). 40 ml Acetonitrile, 35.6 mmol                1-methyl-2-(3-hydroxy-phenyl)-imidazole and 53.4                mmol iodomethane were added into a Schlenk flask.                The flask was closed with a rubber septum and the                suspension was heated at 70 degrees C under                stirring with the aid of a magnetic bar, for 1                hour. The resulting brownish solution solvent was                evaporated to a solid which was washed with ethyl                acetate and then dried under reduced pressure. 10 g                Brownish solid were obtained (88.9% yield). 14 ml                The exchange resin was measured ionic Ambersep 900                hydroxy was added to a beaker. The resin was washed                three times with distilled water. Then, 10 mmol the                resin was added to a solution of                1,3-dimethyl-2-(3-hydroxy-phenyl)-imidazolium                iodide (3.16 g,) in 10 ml water. The reaction was                stirred at room temperature in a beaker for 10                minutes. After that time the ion exchange resin was                filtered off and the water from the solution was                evaporated under reduced pressure, resulting in 1.8                g hygroscopic brown solid (96% yield) i.e.                1,3-dimethyl-2-(3-oxy-phenyl)-imidazolium inner                salt.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); E11 (Containing P and/or Si.); ; H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E07-D09A; E11-A01B; E11-F03; E11-F07A; E11-F07D; E11-Q02B; E31-N05C; H01-X; J04-E01; J04-E09; A12-H; A12-M; A12-W11

PT P
PN CN111744015-A
TI Treating solid tumor cancer by injecting carbon            dioxide gas into tumor cells, expanding cell volume,            causing membrane of tumor cell to cause cancer cells to            die, overcoming inflammatory environment, and            inhibiting tumor blood vessel growth.
AU YAO Q
   CHEN Y
   SONG K
   LV T
   DIAO Y
AE UNIV QINGDAO AFFILIATED HOSPITAL (UNQD-C)
GA 2020A1081V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating solid tumor involves (a)                improving low oxygen environment by injecting                hyperbaric oxygen into tumor microenvironment,                improving hypoxic state of tumor microenvironment,                making the cells in tumor microenvironment escape                from the hypoxic state, repairing damaged normal                cells, spraying anti-tumor drugs, injecting                compressed oxygen into tumor microenvironment to                inhibit growth of tumor cells, injecting hypoxic                gas into tumor tissue, and spraying anti-tumor                drugs into tumor microenvironment, inhibiting                growth of tumor cells and hindering normal                development of tumor cells, (b) improving                high-pressure environment, (c) using low PH                environment by injecting carbon dioxide gas into                solid tumor cells, expanding the volume of tumor                cells by carbon dioxide gas and eventually causing                the cell membranes of tumor cells to rupture and                cause cancer cells to die, (d) overcoming                inflammatory environment of immunosuppression, and                (e) inhibiting tumor blood vessel growth.
   USE - The method is useful for treating solid tumor,                inhibiting tumor angiogenesis and tumor blood                vessel growth, and preventing tumor growth,                metastasis and recurrence.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for treating solid tumor by regulating                tumor microenvironment involves (a) improving low                oxygen environment by injecting hyperbaric oxygen                into tumor microenvironment, improving hypoxic                state of tumor microenvironment, making the cells                in tumor microenvironment escape from the hypoxic                state, repairing damaged normal cells, spraying                anti-tumor drugs, injecting compressed oxygen into                tumor microenvironment to inhibit growth of tumor                cells, injecting hypoxic gas into tumor tissue                before the tumor cells enter the proliferation                stage, and spraying anti-tumor drugs into tumor                microenvironment along with the hypoxic gas,                inhibiting growth of tumor cells and hindering                normal development of tumor cells, (b) improving                high-pressure environment by using tyrosine kinase                inhibitors to inhibit the biochemical reactions                mediated by PDGF and SCF, inhibiting expression of                V-ab1 and the proliferation of ber-ab1 cells,                reducing high interstitial fluid pressure of tumor                tissue, improving blood flow condition of tumor                site, and increasing delivery of drugs to the tumor                site, (c) using low PH environment by injecting                carbon dioxide gas into solid tumor cells,                expanding the volume of tumor cells by carbon                dioxide gas and eventually causing the cell                membranes of tumor cells to rupture and cause                cancer cells to die, (d) overcoming inflammatory                environment of immunosuppression by using of cyclic                dinucleotide-like interferon gene stimulating                protein agonists that triggers spontaneous                anti-tumor T cell responses and overcoming                spontaneous T cell inflammatory tumor                microenvironment, and (e) inhibiting tumor blood                vessel growth by using vascular inhibitors to                combat angiogenesis and block the nutritional                supply of tumors, prevent tumor growth, metastasis                and recurrence, where the carbon dioxide gas cannot                dissolve in the water inside tumor cells due to the                higher PH in the tumor microenvironment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  boosting hyperbaric oxygen to 1.8-2 ATA at a                  constant rate within 15 minutes before the input,                  stabilizing voltage at 1.8-2 ATA for 20-30                  minutes, reducing it to normal pressure at a                  constant rate within 15 minutes. Preferred                  Component: The anti-tumor drug is one of                  5-fluorouracil, roxithromycin and doxorubicin.                  The low-oxygen gas is one of carbon dioxide,                  nitrogen, helium, and argon, and the low-oxygen                  gas flows out of the body with the blood. The                  tyrosine kinase inhibitor is one of imatinib,                  dasatinib, and nilotinib. The vascular inhibitor                  is one of YH-16, AGS, TWIN, hydroxytarinine                  extract, and avastin.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Angiogenesis inhibitor.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Tyrosine kinase inhibitor.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); B03 (Other heterocyclics.)

PT P
PN EP3670443-A1; DE102019200245-A1
TI Producing liquid fuel, comprises e.g. introducing carbon dioxide and hydrogen into first reactor, adding carbon monoxide, producing methanol using catalyst, producing synthesis gas, and adding methanol into second reactor.
AU SCHEMME S
   TSCHAUDER A
   SAMSUN D R C
   PETERS P R
   STOLTEN P D
   SAMSUN R C
   PETERS R
   STOLTEN D
AE FORSCHUNGSZENTRUM JUELICH GMBH (KERJ-C)
   FORSCHUNGSZENTRUM JUELICH GMBH (KERJ-C)
GA 2020561554
AB    NOVELTY - Producing liquid fuel, comprises (a) introducing carbon dioxide and hydrogen into a first reactor (1), (b) adding carbon monoxide from a reformer (8) into the first reactor, (c) producing methanol from hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the first reactor using catalyst and/or producing synthesis gas in the first reactor and/or producing dimethyl ether in the first reactor, (d) adding methanol and/or synthesis gas and/or dimethyl ether from the first reactor into a second reactor (2), (e) producing hydrocarbons from the methanol and/or synthesis gas and/or dimethyl ether, (f) adding hydrocarbons into a separation station (3), (g) separating liquid fuel in the separation station and leading the separated liquid fuel out of the separation station, (h) adding by-product from the separation station into the reformer, and (i) producing carbon monoxide in the reformer from the by-product.
   USE - The method is useful for producing liquid fuel using device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces liquid fuel, preferably gasoline, diesel and kerosene, improves high efficiency and high yield of liquid fuel, and maximizes content of middle distillates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for carrying out the above method comprising a first reactor, an inlet for introducing carbon dioxide and hydrogen into the first reactor, a catalyst provided in the first reactor for catalytic conversion of the mixture of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen into methanol, a catalyst in the first reactor for catalytic conversion of the mixture of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen into methanol, a reformer, a connection provided between the first reactor and the reformer for feeding carbon monoxide from the reformer into the first reactor, a second reactor, a connection provided between the first reactor and the second reactor for feeding methanol from the first reactor into the second reactor, a catalyst in the second reactor for catalytic conversion of methanol into gaseous and liquid hydrocarbons in the second reactor or into dimethyl ether, a separation station, a connection between the second reactor and the separation station for feeding gaseous and liquid hydrocarbons into the separation station, a separation device in the separation station in which liquid fuel is separated in the separation station and in which liquid fuel is led out of the separation station, a connection between the separation station and the reformer in which a by-product from the separation station is fed to the reformer, and a reforming device in the reformer in which carbon monoxide is produced from the by-product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device for producing liquid fuel.
   First reactor (1)
   Second reactor (2)
   Separation station (3)
   Reformer (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The first reactor has a gas outlet and a control device. The control device controls addition of the by-products into the reformer and to lesser extent to the first reactor. The first reactor is an RWGS reactor. The second reactor is a reactor for carrying out a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis. A heat transport device supplies heat from the reformer into at least one rectification columns and/or to the first reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon monoxide is produced from by-product with the aid of the reformer using oxygen and water. The carbon monoxide is produced from the by-product through autothermal reforming. The method comprises passing hydrogen, carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide from the reformer into the first reactor; introducing water vapor which is produced in the first or second reactor into the separation station for carrying out the separation and/or into the reformer for carrying out the reforming. The gas is periodically led out of the first reactor. The method comprises introducing hydrogen generated by electrolysis into the first reactor and/or carbon dioxide obtained by deposition on air into the first reactor. The hydrogen is introduced into the second reactor in ratio of 1.6-2.2.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

PN CN114806137-A
TI Carbon dioxide-based thermoplastic polyurethane            (TPU) degradable material for rubber screen, comprises            TPU particles, antioxidant, degradation agent and epoxy            resin.
AU DONG Y
   ZHOU N
   ZHANG K
AE SUZHOU BEICAI NANO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2022A33997
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based thermoplastic                polyurethane (TPU) degradable material comprises                75-100 wt.% TPU particles, 0.1-2 wt.% antioxidant,                0.1-10 wt.% degradation agent, and 1-10 wt.% epoxy                resin.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-based TPU degradable material                for rubber screen. Uses include but are not limited                to medical health, electronic appliance, industry                and sports.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based TPU degradable                material is more environment-friendly. The                degradation agent in the screen is the fungi and                bacteria in the surrounding environment, yeast and                other active strain metabolism the rest plastic is                disintegrated into micro-fragments, will not                generate new pollution source, compared with the                traditional incineration treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation method of the TPU degradable material                comprising (S1) mixing the TPU particles,                antioxidant, degradation agent and epoxy resin in a                reaction kettle, high speed stirring in the kettle                at 125-135 &#176;C, fully reacting for 8-10                minutes to obtain the TPU mixed material; (S2)                placing the TPU mixed material into the mixer,                mixing under the environment of 140-145&#176;C;                and (S3) conveying the mixed TPU mixed material to                the granulator, extruding and granulating the TPU                mixed material by pelletizer to obtain the TPU                degradable material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The TPU particles are                  diphenylmethane diisocyanate or toluene                  diisocynate and other isocyanate molecules and                  macromolecular polyalcohol, the chain extenders                  react with each aggregate to form the high                  molecular material particles. The chain extender                  is low molecular polyalcohol. Antioxidant is one                  of aromatic amine antioxidant and hindered phenol                  antioxidant. The degradation agent is                  carbohydrate e.g. starch.

TI Catalyst used for producing methane, comprises alumina as carrier, nickel as active metal, and basic oxide as additive catalyst, where basic oxide comprises lanthanum oxide, yttrium oxide, and/or praseodymium oxide.
AU OHASHI A
   MORITA K
   KATO Y
   NAKAO K
   SUZUKI K
AE NIPPON STEEL ENG KK (YAWA-C)
GA 202284567H
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises alumina as carrier, nickel as active metal, and basic oxide as additive catalyst, where the basic oxide comprises lanthanum oxide, yttrium oxide, and/or praseodymium oxide.
   USE - The catalyst is used for producing methane, and in the methanation reaction for methanation of carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst forms high methane yield than before at a relatively low temperature even if the reaction is carried out at a high gas treatment rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing carbon dioxide methanation catalyst, involving preparing catalyst carrier by preparing salt of basic oxide to aqueous solution and soaking with alumina to form a catalyst carrier, drying and heating obtained catalyst carrier, performing catalyst mixture production process by soaking dried and heated catalyst carrier with salt of nickel in aqueous solution to form a catalyst mixture, and drying and heating obtained catalyst mixture, where the basic oxide comprises lanthanum oxide, yttrium oxide, and/or praseodymium oxide; and
   (2) a method for producing methane, involving contacting carbon dioxide methanation catalyst with mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen at greater than or equal to 200 degrees C to form methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carrying ratio of active metal nickel is 10-30 %mass based on total amount of catalyst. The carrying ratio of basic oxide comprising lanthanum oxide or yttrium oxide is 4-30 %mass based on total amount of catalyst. The carrying ratio of basic oxide comprising praseodymium oxide is 10-30 %mass based on total amount of catalyst. Preferred Method: The method involves performing catalyst mixture production process for uniformly mixing salt of basic oxide and salt of nickel to an aqueous solution, impregnating aqueous solution with alumina to obtain catalyst mixture, drying and heating obtained catalyst mixture.

PN CN114163679-A
TI Microfoamed diffusion plate has light polystyrene (PS) layer and two polystyrene layers respectively located at top and bottom of the light PS layer, and carbon dioxide bubbles provided in the light PS layer and the two PS layers.
AU WU K
AE CHANGZHOU AOZHI POLYMER NEW MATERIAL CO (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202241555W
AB    NOVELTY - Microfoamed diffusion plate comprises a light polystyrene (PS) layer (1) and two PS layers (2) respectively located at the top and bottom of the light PS layer, where the two light PS layers are compounded by three-layer co-extrusion process, the light PS layer and the two PS layers are provided with carbon dioxide bubbles (3).
   USE - Used as a microfoamed diffusion plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The diffusion plate: has specific gravity reduced to 0.85-1.05 g/cm3 by supercritical carbon dioxide microfoaming process, thus reducing the material cost; the same light transmittance and thickness of diffusion plate can reduce the dosage of the diffusion agent because of the refractive index difference of the foam core and the base material; has uniform and good diffusion effect; and is processed by three-layer co-extrusion process taht ensures surface hardness and the optical effect of secondary diffusion; and ensures improved bending strength and rigidity as tension exists between the diffusion plate internal material interface bubbles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the cross-sectional view of the micro-foaming diffusion plate.
   Light PS layer (1)
   PS layer (2)
   Carbon dioxide bubble (3)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The light PS layer and the two PS layers are made up of polystyrene and extruded and moulded by a plastic extruding machine; the polystyrene is added with filler and special auxiliary agent, where the ratio of the polystyrene, filler and special auxiliary agent is 10000:1:8. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide bubbles in the light PS layer and two PS layers are formed by carbon dioxide micro-foaming process comprising (a) injecting supercritical carbon dioxide liquid into a pressure container, dissolving a part of polystyrene raw material in supercritical carbon dioxide liquid to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide swelling PS liquid, (b) processing the first section extrusion, mixing the remaining amount of polystyrene and diffusion particle master batch, extruding by single screw to obtain a solution, (c) processing second section extrusion, injecting supercritical carbon dioxide through the injection hole after the solution enters the second section of single screw to melt and expand PS liquid, and (d) slowly foaming in the screw after the solution is mixed with supercritical carbon dioxide, where the middle layer foaming rate is controlled by precisely controlling the injection amount of supercritical carbon dioxide. In the step (d), the solution after foaming is extruded simultaneously through different auxiliary screw rods, and the plate is extruded by three-layer in mold co-extrusion mold and cooled to form the diffusion plate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The filler is rutile titanium pigment.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The special auxiliary agent is organic silicone micro powder.

TI Heterojunction composite material used as            photocatalyst, comprises oxygen-rich vacancy hollow            cerium oxide and molybdenum diselenide scattered on            surface of oxygen-rich vacancy hollow cerium oxide, and            molybdenum diselenide and oxygen vacancy-rich hollow            cerium oxide form heterojunction structure.
AU LIU T
   WANG X
   JIANG J
   GUO H
AE UNIV YUNNAN (UYUN-C)
GA 2022399618
AB    NOVELTY - Heterojunction composite material comprises                oxygen-rich vacancy hollow cerium oxide and                molybdenum diselenide scattered on the surface of                the oxygen-rich vacancy hollow cerium oxide, and                the molybdenum diselenide and oxygen vacancy-rich                hollow cerium oxide form a heterojunction                structure.
   USE - The heterojunction composite material is used                as a photocatalyst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen-rich hollow cerium oxide has a                uniform hollow structure, improves specific surface                area of the heterojunction composite material, has                strong carbon dioxide adsorption capacity, improves                utilization efficiency of visible light, is                conducive to the capture of electrons by carbon                dioxide by the introduction of oxygen vacancies,                promotes photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                by the heterojunction composite, increases                absorption range of visible light and the                separation efficiency of photo-generated carriers,                and improves catalytic activity of the                heterojunction composite for reducing carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing heterojunction composite material,                comprising mixing hollow cerium oxide, molybdenum                source, selenium, reducing agent and water,                carrying out hydrothermal reaction, obtaining                heterojunction composite precursor, and calcining                the heterojunction composite material precursor in                a hydrogen atmosphere to obtain heterojunction                composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mass fraction of the                  molybdenum diselenide is 12.3-63.5%. The particle                  size of the oxygen vacancy-rich hollow cerium                  oxide is 100-500 nm, and the thickness of the                  shell layer is 7-20 nm. The preparation of the                  hollow cerium oxide is as follows: mixing                  non-porous silicon dioxide, polyvinylpyrrolidone,                  water, hexamethylenetetramine and water-soluble                  cerium salt, carrying out hydrothermal reaction,                  and calcining to obtain silicon dioxide loaded                  cerium oxide, placing in strong alkaline solution                  for removing silicon dioxide to obtain hollow                  cerium oxide. In the method, the mass ratio of                  cerium in the non-porous silicon dioxide and                  water-soluble cerium salt is 1:2171-2181, the                  temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is                  95-100degrees Celsius, the time is 100-120                  minutes, the calcining temperature is                  450-600degrees Celsius, the calcining time is 100                  minutes, and the concentration of the strong                  alkali solution is 3-5 mol/l. In the method, the                  mass ratio of the hollow cerium oxide and                  selenium is 1:0.45-0.5, the molar ratio of                  molybdenum and selenium in the molybdenum source                  is 1:2-3, the reducing agent comprises hydrazine                  hydrate and/or sodium borohydride, and the molar                  ratio of the selenium and reducing agent is                  1:1-2. In the method, the temperature of the                  hydrothermal reaction is 180-200degrees Celsius,                  and the time is 20-24 hours. In the method, the                  calcining temperature is 450-550degrees Celsius,                  and the time is 30-40 minutes.

PN CN112916213-A; CN112916213-B
TI Process method for processing carbon neutral minerals, involves replacing sulfuric acid by carbon dioxide generated by limestone roasting and passing sulfuric acid into ore slurry for carbon neutralization, using lime.
AU HE Z
AE BGRIMM TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (BEJM-C)
GA 202165682Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting up the roaster at the processing site of polymetallic sulfide minerals for limestone roasting. The carbon dioxide generated by the limestone roasting replaces sulfuric acid and is passed into the ore slurry for carbon neutralization, using the lime produced by the roasting of the limestone as a pH adjuster to process polymetallic sulfide minerals, so that the carbon dioxide is converted into carbonate. The polymetallic sulfide mineral processing process using lime as the pH adjuster is a flotation process or a cyanide leaching-flotation process.
   USE - Process method for processing carbon neutral minerals.
   ADVANTAGE - The grade and recovery rate of recoverable minerals are basically equivalent to the existing lime-sulfuric acid method and the carbon dioxide generated in the process of calcining lime is effectively utilized with calcium carbonate. The need to add sulfuric acid to reduce the pH of the slurry is eliminated, the impact of the use of sulfuric acid in the existing lime-sulfuric acid method on equipment corrosion and pipeline blockage are effectively avoided. The production safety is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the carbon neutral mineral processing process method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

AU SHARIFF A M
   SHAIKH M S
   BUSTAM M A
AE UNIV PETRONAS TEKNOLOGI (UYPE-Non-standard)
GA 2022A0752W
AB    NOVELTY - The solvent has a blend of potassium carbonate                and glycine. Ratio of weight of the potassium                carbonate to the glycine is 5:1. Viscosity of the                solvent is reduced with the weight ratio when                surface tension and carbon loading are increased at                operating temperature of 25 to 30 degrees Celsius                and pressure of 60 bar psi. The solvent contains 10                weight percent of the pottasium carbonate and 2                weight percent of glycine. Human irritation                equivalent score of the solvent is in range from                0.34 to 0.48. The corrosion rate of the solvent is                up to 2.708 milli-inches per year at 30 degree                Celsius for stainless steel grade SS-304 in carbon                dioxide saturated solvent.
   USE - Solvent i.e. amine-based solvent, for removing                carbon dioxide from gas stream i.e. natural gas,                from a ground or offshore reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The solvent is eco-friendly, less viscous,                less corrosive and economical for high-pressure                applications.

The methane producing device comprises a shunting chamber (3), a methane chamber (4), an anode chamber (5), a cathode chamber. A separation chamber further comprises a cathode plate and the gap between the cathode plate is a cathode chamber. The waste gas outlet of the cooling dust remover is connected with the air inlet of the n-type pipe. The gas outlet of the n-type pipe is connected with the water inlet of the cooling dust remover. The gas outlet of the n-type pipe is connected with the air inlet of the shunt chamber. The waste gas rises in the shunting chamber.

PN WO2020249441-A1; WO2020249441-A4; EP3942098-A1; CN113993617-A; US2022220639-A1
TI Production of carbon fiber material used for separating carbon dioxide or ammonia from gas mixture, involves producing solution of polyacrylonitrile in solvent, electrospinning, drying, crosslinking, and carbonizing obtained fiber material.
AU TEMPEL H
   KRETZSCHMAR A K G
   SELMERT V O
   WEINRICH H
   KUNGL H
   EICHEL R
   EICHEL R A
AE FORSCHUNGSZENTRUM JUELICH GMBH (KERJ-C)
   FORSCHUNGSZENTRUM JUELICH GMBH (KERJ-C)
   FORSCHUNGSZENTRUM JUELICH GMBH (KERJ-C)
GA 2020C70582
AB    NOVELTY - Production of carbon fiber material involves producing a solution of 5-10 wt.% polyacrylonitrile in a suitable organic solvent, electrospinning the obtained solution and drying the obtained fiber material, crosslinking the fiber material at 150-350 degrees C in an air or oxygen atmosphere for 1-30 hours, and carbonizing the obtained fiber material in a protective gas atmosphere at a temperature of 500-2500 degrees C. The polyacrylonitrile solution is free of silicon, sulfur, metal compounds, intermetallic compounds, silicon compounds and/or sulfur compounds. Stabilization or surface modification steps are carried out with the fiber material by treatment with chemical reagents and/or under the action of tensile stress.
   USE - Production of carbon fiber material used for separating carbon dioxide or ammonia from gas mixture e.g. exhaust gas from internal combustion engines, natural gas, biogas or air (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical production of carbon fiber material with improved selectivity for carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon fiber material, which has content of ultra-micropores having of size of less than 0.4 nm of 0.01-10 cm3/g, measures by carbon dioxide adsorption test.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic solvent is at least one chosen from dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, acetone, methyl ethyl ketone, and ethanol, preferably dimethylformamide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polyacrylonitrile is a polyacrylonitrile homopolymer or a polyacrylonitrile copolymer. Preferred Properties: The polyacrylonitrile has a molecular weight of 50000-200000 g/mol.

An output                end of the first heat exchange sub-path is                connected with the input end. The output end is                connected with an input end of the second heat                exchange sub-path. A heat absorption path and a                work output path are orderly connected together. An                output end of the working output path is connected                with an input end of the first heat exchange                sub-path. The working output path is provided with                a turbine. A flow bypass unit is provided with a                turbine bypass valve.
   USE - Ultra-critical carbon dioxide re-compression                cycle power generation system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                ultra-critical carbon dioxide re-compression cycle                power generation system operating method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram                of an ultra-critical carbon dioxide re-compression                cycle power generation system.

PT P
PN CN110353075-A; CN110353075-B
TI Inhibiting non-enzymatic browning of orange peel by cutting fresh orange peel, placing in closed reaction kettle, filling kettle with carbon dioxide under pressure, releasing pressure, boiling orange peel with sucrose solution and baking.
AU HU W
   ZHANG J
   YANG Z
   PENG B
   XU X
   PAN S
AE UNIV HUAZHONG AGRIC (UHZA-C)
GA 201988932E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for inhibiting non-enzymatic browning of orange peel by high-pressure carbon dioxide, involves (i) cutting fresh orange peel into pieces, placing in a closed reaction kettle, filling the reaction kettle with carbon dioxide until the pressure in the kettle is increased to 3-20 MPa, controlling the temperature in the kettle at 25-50 degrees C, maintaining the pressure for 10-60 minutes, rapidly relieving the pressure, releasing the carbon dioxide and taking out the orange peel, and (ii) boiling the orange peel with a sucrose solution at a mass concentration of 30-70% for 2-10 minutes, stopping heating, continuously immersing in the sucrose solution for 6-24 hours, taking out the orange peel, draining the surface moisture and baking in an oven to obtain the processed orange peel.
   USE - The method is useful for inhibiting non-enzymatic browning of orange peel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method inhibits the non-enzymatic browning of the orange peel at a relatively low temperature, reduces the influence of hot processing on the physical and chemical properties of orange peel, and maintains the nutrient composition and sensory quality of the orange peel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The pressure in the kettle is 8-12 MPa, the temperature is 30-35 degrees C and the pressure is maintained for 20-30 minutes.

GA 2019703110

   ADVANTAGE - The kit has high specificity, and can be prepared in accurate, convenient and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing a carbon dioxide detecting kit, which involves adding 20-50% purified water to the container, adding tris hydroxymethyl aminomethane according to the concentration, stirring evenly to dissolve completely, and adding phosphoenolpyruvate, phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase, malate dehydrogenase, reduced coenzyme I, surfactant and stabilizer, stirring evenly to completely dissolve, adding purified water to the container to the total amount, filtering and filling in a reagent bottle to obtain a reagent, adding 20-50% purified water to the container, adding carbonate according to the concentration, stirring evenly to dissolve completely, adding purified water to the container to the total preparation amount, filtering and filling in a reagent bottle to obtain a calibrator.

TI Method for valorization of gas effluents resulting            from alcoholic fermentation, involves filtering or            decantating insoluble alkaline bicarbonate suspension            in order to obtain wet alkaline bicarbonate residue and            aqueous phase.
AU RICARD J
AE SEDE ENVIRONNEMENT SAS (SEDE-Non-standard)
   SEDE ENVIRONNEMENT SAS (SEDE-Non-standard)
   SEDE ENVIRONNEMENT SAS (SEDE-Non-standard)
   SEDE ENVIRONNEMENT SAS (SEDE-Non-standard)
GA 201920722Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves producing a fermented                matter and a gas which includes carbon dioxide. The                stage of production of an alkaline bicarbonate                suspension includes a partly low introduction of                the first effluent of the compartment gas                compressed. The alkaline bicarbonate precipitation                in the first compartment by carbon dioxide reaction                is dissolves with ions carbonates, in order to                obtain an insoluble alkaline bicarbonate                suspension. The insoluble alkaline bicarbonate                suspension of the first compartment is recovered,                followed by a stage of filtration or decantation of                the insoluble alkaline bicarbonate suspension in                order to obtain a wet alkaline bicarbonate residue                and an aqueous phase.
   USE - Method for valorization of gas effluents                resulting from alcoholic fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas effluent advantageously has a flow                ranging between 30 and 100 m3/h. The compressed gas                effluent is introduced partly low first compartment                into the first compartment by plates with fine                bubbles to ensure a large surface of gas exchange                and to privilege the mass transfer of gas carbon                dioxide carbon dioxide dissolves in aqueous                solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                installation of the apparatus for alcoholic                fermentation.

   Storage tank (300)
   Compressor (400)
   Filtration unit (500)
   Drying unit (600)

TI Anti-folding type anesthesia monitor carbon dioxide sampling tube, has L-shaped pipe extended with multiple elastic pieces along specific direction, where diameter of L-shaped pipe is gradually increased.
AU XIE Y
   YE Y
AE SUZHOU MUNICIPAL HOSPITAL (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2019201271
AB    NOVELTY - An anti-folding type anaesthesia monitor carbon dioxide sampling tube, comprising a three-way tube, first end of endotracheal tube of the three-way pipe and the second end, the third end of the three-way pipe connecting through a linker to one end of an L-shaped pipe; the other end of the L-shaped tube connecting with one end of a soft PVC tube through the joint, the other end of the soft PVC pipe connected with carbon dioxide sampling port through joint; said anti-folding anesthesia monitoring carbon dioxide sampling tube further comprises a folding structure. the bending structure adopts the following proposal from at least one of the first of the soft PVC pipe is inserted with a spiral spring, the second one of the L-shaped pipe is extended with multiple elastic piece towards the direction of the soft PVC pipe towards the end part of the soft PVC pipe, the third for the soft PVC pipe from root part of the L-shaped pipe away from the L-shaped pipe diameter of the root part is gradually increased. When this utility model is used, the joint will not bend.

PN CN109076837-A
TI Intelligent small greenhouse, has humidity sensor inserted into flowerpot soil, touch display screen, temperature sensor, humidity sensor, Carbon dioxide sensor, ventilator, heater, Bluetooth module and water pump connected with controller.
AU LIU H
AE UNIV HUAINAN NORMAL (HNUV-C)
GA 2019017019
AB    NOVELTY - The green house has a humidity sensor inserted into a flowerpot soil. Multiple drop irrigation branch pipes (11) are connected with a water source water pipe (10) through a connection pipe head. An outer through water pump is connected with the water source water pipe. A touch display screen (5), a temperature sensor (6), a humidity sensor, a Carbon dioxide sensor (7), a ventilator (8), a heater (9), a Bluetooth module, a water pump and a controller are connected with a storage battery. The touch display screen, the temperature sensor, the humidity sensor, the Carbon dioxide sensor, the ventilator, the heater, the Bluetooth module and the water pump are connected with a controller.
   USE - Intelligent small greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The greenhouse realizes automatic control green plant growth environment so as to ensure green plant is grows vigorously according to the growth requirement of green plant watering, and is convenient to manage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of an intelligent small greenhouse.
   Touch display screen (5)
   Temperature sensor (6)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (7)
   Ventilator (8)
   Heater (9)
   Water source water pipe (10)
   Drop irrigation branch pipes (11)
